PT P
PN CN113336207-A
TI Combined production system of yellow phosphorus            and synthesis gas comprises discharge port of coal            powder preparation unit connected with coal powder            inlet of phosphorus coal gasification furnace for            inputting coal powder, and air outlet.
AU MEI Y
   DI C
   NIE Y
   XIA J
   XIE D
   ZHU Y
   LONG P
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2021A4561C
AB    NOVELTY - Combined production system of yellow                phosphorus and synthesis gas comprises phosphorus                coal gasification furnace. The discharge port of                the coal powder preparation unit is connected with                the coal powder inlet of the phosphorus coal                gasification furnace for inputting the coal powder                into the phosphorus coal gasification furnace. The                air outlet of the air separation unit is connected                with the air inlet at the bottom of the phosphorus                coal gasification furnace, for inputting oxygen                into the phosphorus coal gasification furnace. The                mineral material forming unit is connected to the                feeding port on the top of the phosphorus coal                gasification furnace, for conveying raw materials                for production into the phosphorus coal                gasification furnace. The furnace gas outlet of the                phosphorus coal gasification furnace is connected                with the air inlet of the separation washing unit,                and one output port of the separation washing unit                is the yellow phosphorus product output port.
   USE - Used as combined production system of yellow                phosphorus and synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: improves the efficiency of yellow                phosphorus production, yellow phosphorus production                capacity and synthesis gas output; and reduces                carbon dioxide emissions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a functional block diagram                of the combined production system of yellow                phosphorus and synthesis gas (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The phosphorus coal                  gasifier is fixed-bed reactor, the lower part of                  which is injected through the burner combustion                  air separation unit with pure oxygen and coal                  powder input from the pulverized coal preparation                  unit to form high-temperature coal gasification                  combustion zone exceeding 1600 degrees C, the                  upper part is the massive phosphate rock, coke,                  silica or spherical mixture of the three input by                  the mineral material forming unit with this                  combustion zone as the boundary, the lower part                  is the melting zone for the reduction of                  phosphate rock, the reduction reaction occurs at                  high temperature to produce yellow phosphorus,                  the coal gasification carbon monoxide and                  dihydrogen and phosphorus vapor gradually rise                  from the bottom of the phosphorus coal gasifier                  to the top, sending from the air duct to the                  separation washing unit, the furnace gas                  temperature is 300-700 degrees C, and the                  operating pressure is 1.5-3.5 MPa. The air                  separation unit is used to produce oxygen and                  nitrogen, and conveying the oxygen to the                  phosphorus coal gasifier to provide pure oxygen                  to the burner.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
MC E31-A01; E31-K04; H09-C
IP C01B-025/027; C01B-003/02; C01B-003/50

TI Open pulse oxygen supply bronchoscope has electromagnetic valve whose air outlet is connected with air inlet of pressure relief valve, where air outlet of pressure relief valve is connected to oxygen supply pipe in handle.
AU HA D
   WU R
   SHU N
AE HA D (HADD-Individual)
GA 2021A0396P
AB    NOVELTY - The open pulse oxygen supply bronchoscope has a bronchoscope main portion, and a camera that is arranged at the front end of the bronchoscope main portion. A handle (1) of the bronchoscope is provided with a display screen (5) connected with the camera. The air inlet of an electromagnetic valve (6) is connected with an oxygen source. The air outlet of the electromagnetic valve is connected with the air inlet of a pressure relief valve (7). The air outlet of the pressure relief valve is connected to the oxygen supply pipe in the handle. The electromagnetic valve, the camera and the display screen are all electrically connected to the power supply.
   USE - Open pulse oxygen supply bronchoscope used for observation of pulmonary lobe, section and sub-section bronchus lesion, biopsy sampling, full anesthesia trachea cannula, bacteriology, cytological examination in medical industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The electromagnetic valve and the air release valve generate pulse, so as to make the open expansion of the lung rhythm. The carbon dioxide is discharged through the open exhaust port, thus avoids the occurrence of hypercholenic acid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the open pulse oxygen supply bronchoscope.
   Handle (1)
   Display screen (5)
   Electromagnetic valve (6)
   Pressure relief valve (7)
   Oxygen source interface (8)

Carbon monoxide emission alarm information of an enterprise whose carbon monoxide emission amount exceeds the first preset threshold is generated in response to the determined carbon monoxide emission exceeding a first preset threshold value. A discharge port of the enterprise is set with a carbon monoxide separating device and a carbon monoxide processing device. Carbon monoxide is introduced in predetermined time to a container filled with a metal oxide by using a processing device to obtain carbon dioxide and metal simple substance. The container is provided with a predetermined temperature.
   USE - Method for generating carbon monoxide emission alarm information in steel production process using an electronic device (claimed).

AB    NOVELTY - Air pollution reduction device using microalgae comprises outer case (10) having air intake port formed on one side and air outlet port formed on other side. The guide unit which flows air sucked in through air inlet of outer case by blowing unit in zigzag direction in vertical direction and guides air to air outlet of outer case. The culturing unit for culturing microalgae that generate oxygen by consuming carbon dioxide in air sucked into outer case. The spraying unit pressurizes culture solution of culture unit and sprays from upper side of guide unit toward air flow in guide unit.

PN KR2021052039-A
TI Inorganic composite pigment as white pigment in paints, coatings, plastics, papers and fibers, comprises core comprising titanium dioxide particles, shell layer with calcium carbonate particles fixed on core and surface treatment layer.
AU YEON K S
   KANG K H
   AHN J H
   JUN M G
AE KCC CORP (KCCK-C)
GA 202149500E
AB    NOVELTY - Inorganic composite pigment comprises a core comprising titanium dioxide particles; a shell layer in which calcium carbonate particles are fixed on the surface of the core; and a surface treatment layer comprising at least one of polyacrylic acid and polyacrylate on the shell layer.
   USE - The inorganic composite pigment is useful as white pigment in paints and coatings, plastics, papers and fibers.
   ADVANTAGE - The inorganic composite pigment has an effect of scattering light efficiently due to a particularly high refractive index, controlled particle size and dispersibility and simplified manufacturing method, excellent whiteness and/or hiding power, thus it is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing inorganic composite pigment, comprising preparing a mixed slurry containing calcium hydroxide and titanium dioxide; lowering the pH of the mixed slurry by bubbling a gas containing carbon dioxide in the mixed slurry in the presence of a shear force; forming composite particles having a core/shell layer structure by fixing calcium carbonate particles on the surface of titanium dioxide particles from the mixed slurry having a lower pH; and performing surface treatment on the outermost surface of the composite particle having the core/shell layer structure using at least one of polyacrylic acid and polyacrylate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The titanium dioxide particles are surface-treated with at least one of inorganic acid and inorganic acid salt of 0.1-10 wt.% based on the total weight of the titanium dioxide particles. The surface treatment layer is included in an amount of 0.1-20 wt.% based on the total weight of the inorganic composite pigment. The weight ratio of titanium dioxide particles and calcium carbonate particles is 1-49:51-99. Preferred Method: The bubbling of the carbon dioxide-containing gas is performed so that the pH of the mixed slurry is 7-12. The shear force is a shear rate of 20000-150000/second.
   EXAMPLE - 5.5 g of titanium dioxide having an average particle size of 300 nm was added to the calcium hydroxide slurry, and water was added to obtain a mixed slurry of titanium dioxide and calcium hydroxide having a solid content of 5%. A high speed mixer generating shearing force (shearing speed 36000/second) was added with 33% concentration of carbon-dioxide mixed gas (50 cc/minute carbon-dioxide, 100 cc/minute nitrogen) was bubbled into the titanium dioxide and calcium hydroxide mixed slurry, and the pH was lowered to 7.5 or less. The mixed gas input was stopped. The inorganic composite thus obtained was surface-treated by a wet treatment method using a dispersion in which polyacrylic acid was dissolved, and then heat-treated at 80 degrees C to obtain an inorganic composite pigment in which 1.01 g of polyacrylic acid was formed as a surface treatment layer. The resulting inorganic composite pigment was subjected to thermogravimetric analysis (TGA) under 10 degrees C/minute conditions using a thermogravimetric analyzer. As a result, the weight ratio of titanium dioxide particles and calcium carbonate particles in the additive composite pigment composition was confirmed to be 38:62.

PT P
PN CN112410299-A; CN112410299-B
TI Performing enzymatic hydrolysis for acute            isolation of mammalian hippocampal cells involves using            papain and collagenase to digest hippocampal tissue            fragments.
AU YANG Y
AE UNIV SICHUAN WEST CHINA HOSPITAL (USCU-C)
GA 2021271145
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing enzymatic hydrolysis for                acute isolation of mammalian hippocampal cells                involves using papain and collagenase to digest                hippocampal tissue fragments for 15-25 minutes,                where the working concentration of papain and                collagenase is 1-3 mg/ml.
   USE - The method is useful for performing enzymatic                hydrolysis for acute isolation of mammalian                hippocampal cells (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is performed in a time-saving and                efficient manner, isolates cells with high quality                and improves success rate of the patch clamp                experiment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for acute isolation of mammalian hippocampal cells,                which involves (a) sectioning by taking the                hippocampus tissue and cutting the hippocampus                tissue at 0-4 degrees C, (b) digesting with 1-3                mg/ml papain for 15-25 minutes, sucking out the                papain, digesting with 1-3 mg/ml collagenase for                15-25 minutes and continuously passing in 95%                oxygen and 5% carbon dioxide mixed gas during the                digestion process, (c) terminating the digestion,                washing away the enzyme solution, and transferring                to a Neurobasal (RTM: Basal medium designed for                long-term maintenance and maturation of pure                post-natal and adult brain neurons) mixture, (d)                gently pipetting using the glass tube, letting to                stand and adding the upper suspension to the Petri                dish, and (e) adhering the cells to the wall for                30-45 minutes at room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The collagenase is                  type I collagenase. The papain concentration is 2                  mg/ml. The collagenase concentration is 2 mg/l.                  Preferred Method: The digestion time is 20                  minutes. During the cutting process of step (a),                  the hippocampus is located in Hank's balanced                  salt solution (HBSS) or Dulbecco's modified Eagle                  medium (DMEM) medium. The step (a) involves                  cutting the tissue into portion of 1 mm2 in size.                  The animal is a mouse. The method further                  involves (f) incubating and storing at room                  temperature.

PT P
PN IN202141003102-A; IN381244-B
TI Low cost process for producing biodiesel from high free fatty acid (FFA) coconut distillate oil involves filtering coconut fatty acid distillate (CFAD) oil for removal of impurities and solid particles, pre-treating, and esterifying.
AU DEVAN P
   RAJESH K
   DEVAN P K
AE DEVAN P (DEVA-Individual)
   RAJESH K (RAJE-Individual)
   DEVAN P K (DEVA-Individual)
GA 2021131170
AB    NOVELTY - A low cost process for producing biodiesel from high free fatty acid (FFA) coconut distillate oil involves (i) filtering coconut fatty acid distillate (CFAD) oil for removal of impurities and solid particles to obtain a filtrate and retentate; (ii) pre-treating the filtered CFAD oil for reducing FFA to below a pre-determined threshold level; (iii) subjecting pre-treated CFAD oil to an esterification process upon determining the FFA content beyond the pre-determined threshold level; (iv) separating the water from esterification reaction product through gravity separation; (v) subjecting the pre-treated CFAD oil directly to transesterification process upon determining the FFA content below the pre-determined threshold level; (vi) separating the glycerine from transesterification reaction product through gravity separation; and (vii) purifying the product for obtaining a short chain saturated fatty acid of coconut fatty acid distillate biodiesel containing 3 wt.% oxygen.
   USE - A low cost process for producing biodiesel from high free fatty acid (FFA) coconut distillate oil.
   ADVANTAGE - Properties of produced biodiesel resembling the commercial diesel are ascertained. Spending quantity of fuel on premixed combustion phase is low. Improved oxidation of soot particles leads to formation of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, thus, reducing smoke emission.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The pre-treatment process includes subjecting the filtered CFAD to heat at 110-120 degrees C for the removal of moisture from the oil. Esterification process is performed for 90 minutes by (i) introducing methanol in the ratio of 10:1 of the volume of oil, (ii) mixing the oil, methanol mixture with a catalyst of concentrated sulfuric acid at 2.5 vol.% oil, (iii) subjecting to a hot temperature of 60 degrees C, (iv) providing a continuous stir operation at the rate of 600 rpm, (v) filtering the treated oil, methanol and catalyst combination through a separation funnel for 8 hours, and (vi) draining the glycerine and separating the moisture through gravity separation technique. Esterification process is performed in CFAD upon determining the FFA content above the pre-determined threshold of 2.5%. Esterification process is repeated until the FFA content is below the pre-determined threshold value. Transesterification process is performed for 90 minutes by (i) introducing methanol in the ratio of 8:1 of the volume of oil, (ii) mixing the oil, methanol mixture with a catalyst of potassium hydroxide (KOH) at 1.5 vol.% oil for obtaining the methoxide solution, (iii) subjecting to a hot temperature of 60 degrees C, (iv) providing a continuous stir operation at the rate of 600 rpm, (v) filtering the treated oil, methanol and catalyst combination through a separation funnel for 8 hours, (vi) draining the glycerine and separating the methyl ester of the CFAD through gravity separation technique. The CFAD is double processed through esterification, and/or transesterification to obtain the purified form of biodiesel containing 3 wt.% oxygen, high content of methyl laurate, higher cetane number, shorter combustion delay, lesser carbon deposition, low iodine content, lesser unsaturated fatty acids and good blend with diesel. Preferred Parameters: The CFAD biodiesel comprises higher oxidative stability, and lesser nitrogen oxide emission. Preferred Process: The CFAD biodiesel comprises maximum blend ratio of 20% CFAD biodiesel and 80% commercial diesel at 30 degrees C ambient temperature.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)
MC D10-A01; H06-B04A; H06-P
IP C10L-001/02

TI Manufacture method for making gas sensor based on Schottky barrier control, involves detecting gas by changing reactivity of gas based on height of Schottky barrier formed at boundary between conductive layer and electrode on substrate.
AU KIM H J
   KIM Y J
   PARK J
AE UNIV YONSEI IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIA-C)
GA 202106186B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves forming a conductive layer on a substrate. An electrode is then deposited on the conductive layer. Gas is selectively detected by changing the reactivity of the gas based on the height of a Schottky barrier formed at the boundary between the conductive layer and electrode.
   USE - Manufacture method for making a gas sensor (claimed) based on Schottky barrier control.
   ADVANTAGE - Provides a gas sensor manufacture method that can confirm a change in resistance due to the difference in the Schottky barrier between the transition metal chalcogenide (TMD) and the metal electrode. Confirms it there is a difference in the response degree, thus improving reactivity the the nitrogen dioxide gas, carbon dioxide gas, and carbon monoxide gas. Enables reaction of the non-reactive gas according to electrode selection. Provides a two-dimensional (2D) TMD gas sensor capable of operating at low temperature. Ensures that materials suitable to replace the oxide-based gas sensor can be used. Improves the performance of the gas sensor by controlling the Schottky barrier.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a gas sensor formed based on Schottky barrier control.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the gas sensor manufacture method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Conductive layer forming process (S12)
   Electrode depositing process (S14)

PN CN112191028-A
TI Preparing low wind resistance filter cloth useful for purifying environment, by adding foaming agent and nano-manganese dioxide to ceramic precursor sol, adding emulsifier, spraying, brushing on glass fiber skeleton cloth and drying cloth.
AU CHEN Q
   ZAN H
   SI W
   BAI T
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM ENG SCI & TECHNIC (XKLI-C)
GA 2021069128
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing low wind resistance filter cloth involves (a) adding foaming agent and nano-manganese dioxide to ceramic precursor sol and uniformly mixing to obtain a mixed sol, (b) adding emulsifier to oil phase, uniformly mixing, then adding deionized water and mixed sol, spraying the mixture through the microfluid reactor to form water-in-oil dispersible particles, sintering the water-in-oil dispersible particles at high temperature for generation of pores by action of the foaming agent to obtain complex microsphere particles with porous ceramic microspheres supporting manganese dioxide and (c) uniformly mixing the complex microsphere particles with deionized water, then adding the adhesive, continuing stirring to form a uniform coating, then evenly brushing the coating on both sides of the glass fiber skeleton cloth and drying to obtain low wind resistance filter cloth.
   USE - The method for preparing low wind resistance filter cloth is useful for purifying environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares filter cloth which has high safety, sphericity and specific surface area, small wind resistance, synergistic adsorption performance of the porous carrier and prolonged effect, catalyzes and decomposes formaldehyde into carbon dioxide and water at normal temperature, is easy to absorb impurities and environment-friendly and overcomes the defect of saturated absorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: In step (a), the mass ratio of the ceramic precursor sol, foaming agent, and nano-manganese dioxide is 100:0.5-1:15-30, where the ceramic precursor sol is a sol containing 10% aluminum hydroxide and 15% silica and the foaming agent is sodium bicarbonate. In step (a), the mass ratio of the emulsifier, oil phase, deionized water, and mixed sol is 1-2:15-20:50-80:40-60, where the oil phase is white oil and the emulsifier is polyglycerol-3-diisostearate. The step (b) involves sintering at 1200-1250 degrees C for 1-2 hours. In step (c), the mass ratio of composite microsphere particles, deionized water, and adhesive is 20-50:80-90:3-6, where the adhesive is 15 % mass polytetrafluoroethylene emulsion.

TI Preparing self-draining type concrete building block involves selecting cement, fly ash, fine sand, silicon micro powder, bentonite, waterproof agent, preservative, coagulation accelerator, and then adding water into each raw material.
AU XIA R
   GONG C
   XIA Y
AE XIA R (XIAR-Individual)
   HONG Q (HONG-Individual)
GA 202101415A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing self-draining type concrete building block involves selecting cement with a certain mass ratio, fly ash, fine sand, silicon micro powder, bentonite, waterproof agent, preservative, coagulation accelerator as raw material, adding water into each raw material, mixing and stirring to obtain concrete casting material, pre-cleaning the casting mold, drying, the inner portion of the casting mold is embedded with an opening forming device. The concrete pouring material is introduced into the casting mold, the mold is formed, after cooling, demolding to obtain the building block main body with self-draining function. The building block main body is placed in the curing box for carbon dioxide steam curing at 40-60 degrees C cooling the building block main body, after curing, and coated anti-corrosion paint coating on the building block main body.
   USE - Method for preparing self-draining type concrete building block.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing reciprocating telescopic of ramming rods so as to vibrate concrete casting material and effectively improving quality of the concrete building block.

   L-shaped connecting plate (6)

The raw materials are pulverized to prepare powdered raw materials before being mixed and stirred. The water is used for preparing the self-draining concrete building block. Preferred Components: The cement is silicate cement. The casting mold comprises a mold main body (1) and a lower mold seat (3) fixedly connected to the bottom end of the mold main body. The inner portion of mold main body is provided with a cavity. The left and right ends of the lower mold seat are fixedly connected with an L-shaped bearing frame (4). TI Radio-based measurement, analysis, reporting and documentation system for showing potential hazards in rooms and buildings, enables transmission of measurement values to Internet-based cloud system by radio communication unit.
AU UNSENOS D L
   PERAGLIE T
AE ISIS IC GMBH (ISIS-Non-standard)
GA 2020C5811A
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has integrated sensors (2) for measuring gases and other environmental parameters. The measurement results are evaluated, and the measurement results interpreted according to configurable default values are displayed. The transmission of the measurement values to a central point such as an Internet-based cloud system (8) is performed by a radio communication unit (7). An algorithm is configured for regulating the air exchange of the room air environment and the sensor housing, and a timely detection of increased and decreased changes in carbon dioxide content is reached.
   USE - Radio-based measurement, analysis, reporting and documentation system for measuring gases and other environmental parameters and showing potential hazards in rooms and buildings.
   ADVANTAGE - The hazard warning takes place sooner if higher situational room occupancy is noticed, in comparison to stored previous lower room occupancy. The system is thus able to determine the change in room occupancy and to issue a corresponding warning or display earlier if necessary, especially in epidemic or pandemic risk situations.

PN AT522533-A1
TI Generating hydrogen comprises e.g. filling water tank with distilled water, converting water vapor into plasma, generating saturated steam, obtaining hydrogen-rich gas as permeate, feeding carbon-rich gas to hydraulic piston compressor.
AU GRUBER S
AE GS-GRUBER-SCHMIDT (GSGR-Non-standard)
GA 2020B4989B
AB    NOVELTY - Generating hydrogen, comprises filling water tank with distilled water, regulating the volume flow of distilled water via the control valve to 10-100 l/min, converting water vapor into plasma by additively reinforcing the incoming and reflected waves in the reactor core in the reactor. The water tank has a minimum capacity of 100-5000 l. The temperature of the distilled water has 5-85 degrees C. The water tank is depressurized. The pump, driven electrically by an electric motor, generates a minimum pressure of 1-5 bar. The evaporator generates saturated steam with a temperature of minimum 111-145 degrees C. The saturated steam pressure is given with pressure of 1.5-4 bar. The saturated steam obtained in the separation bottle is superheated via a superheater to a temperature of 120-175 degrees C. The dibutyl ether is stored in the fuel tank without pressure. The fuel in tank is stored at a temperature of 5-50 degrees C. The fuel is compressed by hydraulically driven pump to a pressure of 1-5 bar.
   USE - The method is useful for generating hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and scalable, and has the separation and storage of carbon dioxide and water as fundamental property.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The fuel is evaporated via the evaporator to a temperature of 150-175 degrees C. The vaporous fuel is introduced into the reactor. The superheated water vapor is converted into water vapor plasma in a plasma reactor. The water vapor plasma is generated with the help of microwaves with a frequency of 0.5-10 GHz. The microwaves are generated with the help of a magnetron and a piston for regulation of the wave reflection is set. The electrical power of a magnetron has a value of minimum 1 KW, maximum 10 kW. The microwave generators are arranged in sequence along the reactor, a minimum of one microwave generator, a maximum of 10 microwave generators can be used to generate a high-density water vapor plasma with a minimum number of charged particles of 103-1027 N/m3. The microwave generators are arranged in sequence along the reactor, a minimum of one microwave generator, a maximum of 10 microwave generators are used to generate a high-density water vapor plasma in which the charged particles have a temperature of 1-100 eV. The water vapor plasma is brought together with the vaporous fuel in a reactor to make steam reforming possible. The reactor is surrounded by coils that generate a magnetic field with a minimum magnetic flux density of 0.5-10 T to chemically react the water vapor plasma with the fuel. At the end of the reactor there is a catalyst bed made of pellets with a diameter of at least 3-5 mm to increase the chemical reaction conversion by 5-30%. A carrier made of ceramic aluminum oxide with nickel oxide on the surface is used as the catalyst, and has a layer thickness of 10-100 mu m. The water is obtained as condensate from the reformed gas at a temperature of 10-25 degrees C. The reformed gas consists of a gas mixture of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and methane. The reformed dry gas is compressed via a hydraulic piston compressor to a pressure of 4-20 bar. The gas is separated into hydrogen and carbon-containing gases in a gas separation stage. The gas separation stage has a membrane with a pore diameter of at least 1-3 A degrees . The hydrogen-rich gas obtained as permeate has a fraction of carbon-containing gases of at least 1- 5%. The carbon-rich gas is fed to a hydraulic piston compressor which compresses the gas to a pressure of 50-70 bar. The carbon dioxide contained in the gas is deposited as liquid condensate at a pressure of 50 bar, at a temperature of 10 degrees C, at a pressure of 70 bar, with a temperature of 25 degrees C. The carbon dioxide-free gas is returned to the reactor via a control valve to be recycled again.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC E11-N; E11-P; E31-A02A; H04-E06; N07-G

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - COMPUTING AND CONTROL - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: not utilizing steam primarily as a heat and/or energy transfer medium throughout the chemical production; and not producing steam with heat provided by combustion of a fuel; providing at least 25 MW of electricity; and utilizing for a majority, most, or at least 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80 or 90% of the electricity, electricity from a renewable source. The method comprises providing substantially all of the net energy needed for compressing from electricity, where the providing step comprises compressing via an electric motor-driven compressor or a turbine-driven compressor driven by electrically produced steam; and providing a majority, greater than 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80, or 90%, or substantially all of the net energy utilized by the method from electricity. The step of providing a majority of the net energy needed for powering, pumping, heating, cooling, compressing, separating, or their combinations utilized for one or more of the preparing, the reacting, the separating, the recycling, or their combinations from a non-carbon based energy source, from a renewable energy source, and/or from electricity further comprises regenerating one or more guard beds, adsorbers, process gas dryers, strippers, and/or some catalysts via electrical heating, the addition of electrically-compressed gases, and/or the addition of electrically-generated steam. Preferred Components: The renewable source comprises hydroelectric, solar, wind, geothermal, nuclear, wave, tide, or their combinations.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AE BYEON G H (BYEO-Individual)
GA 202090615B
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of functional traditional brick involves mixing clay, ocher, kaolin, silica sand, and functional materials, molding according to shape of the traditional brick to be manufactured, drying to remove moisture, and baking at 1000-1300 degrees C. The baking process is divided into baking process (a) performed at 1000-1100 degrees C, and the baking process (b) is sealed to block oxygen, carburizing the carbon dioxide, baking to 300 degrees C and natural cooling. The baking process involves minimizing the contact of the traditional brick with external air or external oxygen by moving it to the baking process (b) using a separate moving unit which is sealed after taking it out of the baking process kiln. In the baking process (a), the traditional brick turns red, and when the process (b) is completed, it turns silver or silver gray. The baking process kiln (i) and (ii) are manufactured as an integral type, but have a separable configuration inside, and the moving unit moves inside the integrated kiln.
   USE - Manufacture of functional traditional brick (claimed) such as clay bricks and cement brick used as structural materials for buildings.

The functional material further includes carbon nanotubes (CNT) to increase strength, improve thermal resistance by including silica, improve physical properties by using bentonite or volcanic ash, and contain nutrients inside so that plants adhere and grow. PN CN111606344-A
TI Preparation of nano calcium carbonate with high specific surface area by adding urea into calcium hydroxide suspension, stirring, adjusting pH value using ammonia water, heating, adding composite modifier, filtering, and freeze-drying.
AU WEI M
AE GUANGXI XIAYANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202087248M
AB    NOVELTY - A nano calcium carbonate with high specific surface area is prepared by adding urea into calcium hydroxide suspension, stirring and mixing, adjusting pH value using ammonia water to 8-9 to obtain slurry A, constant temperature heating to 35-40 degrees C, introducing mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen with volume ratio of 1:2, stopping introducing mixed gas when pH value of slurry A is 6.5-7, continuously stirring for 1-2 hours to obtain slurry B, constant temperature heating under stirring condition to 80-90 degrees C, adding composite modifier for heat preservation for 2-5 hours to obtain nano calcium carbonate suspension, filtering, and freeze-drying. The adding amount of urea is 3-5% of mass of calcium hydroxide suspension. The adding amount of composite modifier is 3-5% of mass of slurry B. The composite modifier is obtained by mixing stearic acid, sodium palmitate and starch.
   USE - The method is used for preparing nano calcium carbonate with high specific surface area.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves that crystal form of nano calcium carbonate is difficult to control in producing nano calcium carbonate and small specific surface area of product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of calcium hydroxide in calcium hydroxide suspension is 1-2 mol/L. The ammonia water is 25-30 wt.% ammonia water. The composite modifier comprises 10-20 pts. wt. stearic acid, 10-20 pts. wt. sodium palmitate and 30-40 pts. wt. starch. Preferred Method: Freeze-drying is done by constant temperature cooling filtered nano-calcium carbonate solid to 20-25 degrees C for 0.5-1 hour at speed of 3-5 degrees C, constant temperature cooling to - 20 to - 30 degrees C for 2-3 hours at speed of 3-5 degrees C, constant temperature cooling to - 50 to - 70 degrees C for 2-3 hours at speed of 1-2 degrees C/minute, controlling pressure at 0.05-0.08 MPa, and heating to 25-35 degrees C for 3-5 hours at speed of 1-3 degrees C/minute.

TI Synthesizing full-silicon and high-silicon            molecular sieve membranes, comprises e.g. carrying out            high temperature calcining of CHA-type molecular sieve            membrane to remove template agent, and cooling.
AU ZHANG Y
   QIU H
   KONG X
AE UNIV SHANGHAI ENG & TECHNOLOGY (USES-C)
GA 202080100Y
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing full-silicon and high-silicon                CHA-type molecular sieve membranes, comprises (i)                uniformly coating the full-silicon CHA-type                molecular sieve crystal seed on the porous carrier,                and then drying, (ii) mixing the silicon source,                template agent, alkali, water and aluminum source,                preparing to obtain mother liquid, (iii) soaking                the porous carrier coated with full-silicon                CHA-type molecular sieve seed crystal into the                mother liquid, then carrying out crystallization                reaction to obtain CHA-type molecular sieve film,                and (iv) carrying out high temperature calcining of                CHA-type molecular sieve membrane to remove                template agent, and cooling to obtain final                product.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing                full-silicon and high-silicon CHA-type molecular                sieve membranes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is controllable; synthesizes                full-silicon and high-silicon CHA-type molecular                sieve membranes in a dilute solution of about 30                water/silicon dioxide; is also called sol-free                synthesis; avoids the use of a large amount of                mother liquor in traditional hydrothermal                synthesis; improves the utilization rate of raw                materials; and reduces the preparing cost of the                membrane. Full-silicon and high-silicon CHA-type                molecular sieve membrane has the thickness of below                1 mu and excellent separation performance of carbon                dioxide-methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The coating method                  comprises brush coating, dip coating, spray                  coating or spin coating, and the coating                  thickness is 20-2000 nm; the dip coating                  comprises dispersing full-silicon CHA-type                  molecular sieve seeds in water to form dispersion                  with a concentration of 0.01-1 wt.%, and then dip                  coating. The step (iii) comprises soaking the                  porous carrier in the mother liquor for 5-600                  seconds, taking out the porous carrier                  impregnated with mother liquor, placing in an                  empty crystallization kettle, then placing the                  mother liquor with a ratio of 0.002-0.5g/ml to                  the volume of the crystallization kettle, and                  carrying out crystallization reaction. The                  crystallization temperature is 80-220 degrees C,                  the time is 1-240 hours, the temperature of                  high-temperature calcining is 370-700 degrees C,                  and the time is 2-8 hours, and the heating and                  cooling rate is 0.5-2 K/minute. Preferred                  Components: The full-silicon CHA-type molecular                  sieve seeds are full-silicon CHA-type molecular                  sieve seeds with average particle size of less                  than 100 nm after ball milling or CHA-type                  molecular sieve seed crystals that are not ball                  milled and whose particle size is less than 300                  nm; the shape of the porous carrier includes                  single-channel tube, multi-channel tube, flat                  plate or hollow fiber tube, and the materials                  include ceramics, stainless steel, aluminum                  oxide, titanium dioxide, zirconium dioxide,                  silicon dioxide, silicon carbide or silicon                  nitride, and the pore size is 2-2000 nm. The                  templating agent includes                  N,N,N-trimethyl-1-adamantylammonium hydroxide;                  the alkali includes ethylenediamine,                  triethylamine, dipropylamine, cyclohexylamine                  and/or aqueous ammonia; and the aluminum source                  includes aluminum isopropoxide, aluminum                  hydroxide and/or sodium metaaluminate. The                  silicon source includes ammonium                  hexafluorosilicate, and the molar ratio between                  ammonium fluorosilicate,                  N,N,N-trimethyl-1-adamantylammonium hydroxide,                  ethylenediamine, water and aluminum oxide in the                  mother liquor is 1:0-2:0-8:10-100:0-0.01                  (preferably 1:0.3-1:4-8:20-30:0-0.01). The                  silicon source includes silica sol, silica                  aerosol or a mixture of ethyl orthosilicate and                  ammonium fluoride or hydrofluoric acid, and the                  molar ratio between silicon dioxde, fluorine,                  N,N,N-trimethyl-1-adamantylammonium hydroxide,                  ethylenediamine, water and aluminum oxide in the                  mother liquor is 1:3-9:0-2:0-8:10-100:0-0.01                  (preferably 1:6:0.3-1:4-8:20-30:0-0.01).
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Photocatalyst preparation involves disperse mobil composition of matter molecular sieve in water to obtain solution A by ultrasonic waves, and then dissolving barium chloride and vanadium chloride in water to obtain solution B.
AU TAO F
AE TAO F (TAOF-Individual)
GA 2020519963
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalyst preparation involves disperse mobil composition of matter 41 (MCM-41) molecular sieve in water to obtain solution A by ultrasonic waves, dissolving barium chloride and vanadium chloride in water to obtain solution B, and then dissolving solution B into the solution A dropwise under hydrothermal reaction to obtain barium-vanadium mixed doped MCM-41 molecular sieve. The titanium sulfate is dissolved in water, added to the barium-vanadium mixed doped MCM-41 molecular sieve with stirring to perform hydrothermal reaction, and then centrifuged, filtered, dried in air atmosphere and ground to obtain barium-vanadium mixed doped molecular sieve supported photocatalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing photocatalyst for catalytic degradation of organic pollutants.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the photocatalyst for catalytic degradation of organic pollutants in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with effective inhibition of recombination of photogenerated electrons and holes, strong photocatalytic degradation activity for organic pollutants, and good mineralization of different organic pollutants into carbon dioxide, water or less toxic organic matter. The prepared photocatalyst play the role of catalytic degradation of organic pollutants under the action of ultraviolet light, shows a good catalytic degradation effect on organic pollutants under visible light and infrared light compared with the existing conventional photocatalysts to greatly improve the utilization of sunlight.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The barium chloride and the vanadium chloride are provided in a molar ratio of 1-5:0.5-1. The MCM-41 molecular sieve mixed with titanium sulfate and barium-vanadium are provided in a molar ratio of 1:1-5.

AE LEE K T (LEEK-Individual)
GA 202050232K
AB    NOVELTY - Processing meat, comprises (a) introducing the slaughtered meat into a processing space, (b) deboning the slaughtered meat, (c) wrapping and freezing the half-cut and deboned meat with a secondary wrapper, (d) shredding the meat, (e) packaging the shredded meat 3 times using tertiary wrapping paper having an oxygen permeability of less than 10000 cc/m2/day and refrigerating at 0-2 degrees C for 10-24 hours for aging, (f) removing the tertiary wrapping paper and maintaining the temperature of 0-1 degrees C, exposing the meat to oxygen for 30 minutes to 2 hours to change the meteoglobin to oxymioglycine to develop meat, and (g) removing oxygen while injecting nitrogen into the 4th wrapping paper, and finally packing the meat in vacuum by inhaling nitrogen. The method is carried out by cutting, crushing, and shredding slaughtered meat in a processing space having a temperature of -2 to 5 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for processing meat.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents growth of microorganisms due to frictional heat generated during cutting by spraying liquefied carbon dioxide to the cut portion of meat in the deboning or shredding step.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for processing meat (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN111196951-A
TI Extracting essential oil from orange peel involves washing the fresh orange peel and soaking in water, then putting into sieve blue and immersing in liquid nitrogen, and then lifting up and putting at room temperature.
AU DONG Y
AE NANJING XIBOEN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202048654H
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting essential oil from orange peel involves washing the fresh orange peel and soaking in water, then putting into sieve blue and immersing in liquid nitrogen for 30-60 minutes, then lifting up and putting at room temperature for 5-10 minutes, then immersing the sieve blue in whole 30-60 minutes in liquid nitrogen. The orange peel is dried, crushed and passed through a 5-30 mesh sieve at low temperature to obtain orange peel peel powder. The orange peel skin powder is mixed with the solvent uniformly and put into the extraction kettle, and then passed supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) into the extraction kettle. The extraction kettle temperature is controlled to 40-50 degrees C and the extraction pressure to 30-45 megapascal. The CO2 containing the extract flows out of the extraction kettle and enters the separation kettle for reduced-pressure separation to obtain orange peel essential oil.
   USE - Method for extracting essential oil from orange peel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to extract essential oil from orange peel, that avoids considering the loss of active ingredients during the extraction process of orange peel, and improves the quality and yield of orange peel essential oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The orange peel skin powder is placed in the extraction kettle and mixed with 0.01-0.08 times of the weight of the biological enzyme and mixed for 30-60 minutes. The orange peels are vacuum dried at low temperature, and process involves stacking the orange peels in a vacuum drying oven, controlling the vacuum degree to 0.009-0.36 megapascal, and the vacuum drying temperature to 40-52 degrees C. The orange peel is dried to a moisture content of 5% or less. The pressure of the separation kettle is 7-9 megapascal and the temperature is 25-30 degrees C. Preferred Components: The biological enzyme is one of beta -glucosidase, pectinase and cellulase. The solvent is a low boiling point solvent, preferably the solvent is at least one of acetone, ethanol, ether, methanol and dichloromethane.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC D10-A05

   USE - As skin repairing solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The skin repairing solution avoids safety issues associated with transplantation of live cells and cell proliferation such as immune-compatibility, tumorigenicity and propagation of infectious diseases compared to directly applying mesenchymal stem cells to the treatment of skin wounds, adopts the conditional culture solution that can be evaluated for safety, dosage, and efficacy in a manner similar of the traditional medicines, can be produced and manufactured easily, can be stored by freeze-drying, is easy to package, transport and use and does not require time-consuming resuscitation and expansion of stem cells before treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the repairing solution involving (i) using a culture flask to culture human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells in a carbon dioxide incubator to achieve a density of 90-95%, (ii) washing cells with an alpha - minimum essential medium (MEM) culture medium, adding a new alpha -MEM culture medium, continuously culturing in the incubator, culturing for 48-96 hours and obtaining supernatant in the culture flask and (iii) respectively pouring the collected supernatant into an ultrafiltration centrifuge tube, centrifuging, filtering by a filter membrane to obtain the conditional culture solution of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell and placing the culture solution in a refrigerator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves using the culture flask which is a T-175 culture flask, where the culture medium is alpha -MEM culture medium and the culture conditions comprising temperature of 37 degrees C, carbon dioxide concentration of 5% and saturation humidity.    ACTIVITY - Dermatological.

PN CN110498434-A; CN110498434-B
TI Recycling lithium ion battery positive active material comprising calcining positive active material of lithium ion battery, subjecting leaching reactions, filtering, reacting tetra filtrate with carbonate and filtering.
AU PENG C
   YAN X
   TANG Y
   WEN D
   ZHANG Y
AE CHANGSHA JIANA LITHIUM IND TECHNOLOGY CO (CHAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGDONG JIANA ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGDONG JIANA ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   QINGYUAN JIAZHI NEW MATERIALS RES INST (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2019A3961G
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling lithium ion battery positive active material, comprising lithium cobaltate, lithium nickelate and lithium manganate. The recycling method comprises calcining positive active material of the lithium ion battery under a hydrogen atmosphere to obtain a calcined product, subjecting calcined product to a first leaching reaction with water, filtering to obtain a first filter residue and a first filtrate, performing second leaching reaction of the first filter residue with water, adding acid to adjust the pH value to be less than 6 at the same time, during the second leaching reaction, filtering to obtain second filter residue and second filtrate, filtering to obtain a third filter residue and a third filtrate, mixing first filtrate and second filtrate and adjusting the pH value to be plus minus 12 for reaction, filtering to obtain a fourth filter residue and a fourth filtrate, reacting fourth filtrate with carbonate in air or carbon dioxide atmosphere and filtering.
   USE - The recycling lithium ion battery positive active material is useful for lithium ion battery anode (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The recycling method realizes the recovery and reuse of positive active materials in waste lithium-ion batteries, and recovers industrial lithium carbonate required for the synthesis of positive active materials suitable. The method produces no waste water, is environmentally friendly, has low environmental pressure and lithium recovery rate is more than 90%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The recovery method further comprises adding water to the third filter residue and adjusting pH value to 0.5-1.5 by adding acid, and filtering to obtain a fifth filter residue and a fifth filtrate, concentrating fifth filtrate and cooling to obtain anode activity material precursor, pulverizing the lithium ion battery positive active material to obtain a powder; before performing the calcination reaction and particle size of the powder is 80-100 meshes. The calcination reaction is performed under the action of a catalyst, where the temperature of the calcination reaction is 630-700 degrees C and calcination reaction time is 2-4 hours, preferably, the catalyst is sodium bisulfate. The first leaching reaction, second leaching reaction, and third leaching reaction are all performed at room temperature, and reaction time is 1-3 hours. The method comprises adding a surfactant after the first filtrate and second filtrate are mixed. In the reaction process in which the third filter residue is added with water and adjusted to a pH value of 0.5-1.5 by adding acid and the amount of water is 2-7 times the weight of the third filter residue. The reaction temperature is room temperature, and the reaction time is 2-4 hours. The recovery method further comprises adding water into the third filter residue and adding an acid to adjust the pH value to 0.5-1.5, and sequentially adding an oxidant and adjusting the pH with the fourth filter residue to 4.5-6 for the reaction. The reaction process of the fourth filtrate with carbonate in the atmosphere of air or carbon dioxide comprises reacting at room temperature for 2-4 hours, heating to 70-90 degrees C, and reacting for 1-3 hours. Preferred Components: The amount of the catalyst is 0.01-0.05% of the positive active material of the lithium ion battery. The addition amount of water in first leaching reaction is 0.5-1 times the weight of the calcined product. The addition amount of water in the second leaching reaction is 0.5-1 times weight of the first filter residue. The addition amount of water in the third leaching reaction is 0.5-1 times the weight of the second filter residue. The acid is sulfuric acid. The amount of the surfactant is 0.005-0.01% of the total mass of the first filtrate and second filtrate. The surfactant is an alkyl sulfonate, a fatty alcohol ether sulfate, a tricarboxylate, a propylnaphthalenesulfonate, a butylnaphthalenesulfonate, a ligninsulfonate, and/or a heavy alkylbenzene sulfonates. The mass ratio of the first filtrate and second filtrate is 1:0.8-1.5. The third filter residue is further added with a reducing agent during a reaction process in which water and acid is added to adjust the pH value to 0.5-1.5, preferably, the 0.005 to 0.01% reducing agent is hydrogen peroxide (total mass of water and acid). The air or carbon dioxide gas is passed in an amount of 10-35 m3/hours. The amount of the carbonate is 0.2-1.15 times the weight of the fourth filtrate.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PT P
PN CA3044223-A1; US2019358606-A1; US11376560-B2
TI Synthesis of alumina aerogel used as e.g. sorbent in low-temperature capture of carbon from flue gas, involves dissolving alumina alkoxide in mixed aromatic and alcoholic solvent, adding water to form aerogel, aging and supercritical-drying.
AU KARAMI D
   BARARPOUR S T
   MAHINPEY N
AE UTI LP (UTIU-C)
   UTI LP (UTIU-C)
GA 2019992620
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of alumina aerogel involves dissolving an alumina alkoxide in a mixed aromatic and alcoholic solvent to form an alumina alkoxide solution, adding 2-5 mol (with respect to 1 mol of alumina alkoxide) water to the alumina alkoxide solution to form a liquid aluminum hydroxide aerogel, mixing and aging the aluminum hydroxide aerogel for specific time period to form an aged aerogel, supercritical-drying the aged aerogel to form an alumina aerogel having a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller surface area of 1500 m2/g or more.
   USE - Synthesis of alumina aerogel e.g. potassium carbonate impregnated alumina or nickel oxide-and/or-ferric oxide impregnated alumina used as highly-active sorbent in low-temperature capture of carbon from flue gas, and as oxygen carrier in chemical-looping combustion (all claimed) of methane at high temperature.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables synthesis of alumina aerogel having improved Brunauer-Emmett-Teller surface area and bulk density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) low-temperature carbon capture, which involves exposing potassium carbonate impregnated alumina to carbon dioxide; and
   (2) chemical-looping combustion, which involves using nickel oxide-and/or-ferric oxide as an oxygen carrier.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The alumina aerogel has Brunauer-Emmett-Teller surface area of preferably 2000 m2/g or more. Preferred Process: The process further involves thermally-dehydrating the alumina aerogel at 400 degrees C or more to form a dehydrated alumina aerogel, calcining the dehydrated alumina aerogel at 1000 degrees C or more to form a calcined alumina, and adsorbing a metal carbonate, preferably potassium carbonate on the calcined alumina to form potassium carbonate impregnated alumina, or adsorbing a metal oxide, preferably nickel oxide and/or ferric oxide on the calcined alumina to form nickel oxide-and/or-ferric oxide. The thermal dehydration is partially performed under a dehydrating vacuum pressure. The aerogel aging period is more than 1 day, preferably 1-7 days, more preferably 5 days.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alumina alkoxide is aluminum triisoproxide (Al((CH3)2CHO)3). The alumina alkoxide solution is clear and colorless. The liquid aluminum hydroxide aerogel is clear and colorless. The mixed aromatic and alcoholic solvent comprises a mixture of methanol and toluene. The ratio (volume/volume) of methanol/toluene in the mixed aromatic and alcoholic solvent is more than 1/10 to less than 1/2, preferably 1/5.

TI Bimodal high-density polyethylene nanopore foaming material obtained by foaming bimodal high-density polyethylene composition comprising double peak high-density polyethylene.
AU WANG Z

PN CN110104851-A
TI Processing zero-emission treatment of            desulfurization wastewater of magnesia power plant            comprises e.g. first softening the wastewater to remove            magnesium ion and heavy metal ions, and mixture enters            first high-density precipitation unit.
AU LI H
   WANG J
   SHENG Y
   LI Y
AE BEIJING SECCO KANGLUN ENVIRONMENTAL PROT (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201970691U
AB    NOVELTY - Processing zero-emission treatment of                desulfurization wastewater of magnesia power plant                comprises first softening the wastewater to remove                magnesium ion and heavy metal ions, and the                first-stage softened muddy water mixture enters the                first high-density precipitation unit, and the                obtained supernatant enters the secondary softening                unit to remove calcium ion, and the second-stage                softened muddy water mixture enters the second                high-density precipitation unit and the supernatant                is treated by the membrane system, and recycling                the fresh water, and introducing the concentrated                water into the chelating resin unit to deeply                remove calcium and magnesium and heavy metal ions,                the water entering the bipolar membrane                electrodialysis unit, processing to obtain a                concentration of 10% sodium hydroxide solution and                8% hydrochloric acid solution.
   USE - The method is useful for processing                zero-emission treatment of desulfurization                wastewater of magnesia power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can realizes the recycling of the                magnesium hydroxide slurry, greatly reduces the use                amount of the flue gas desulfurization magnesia,                adopts the bipolar membrane electrodialysis to                produce the acid-base process, and simultaneously                utilizes the carbon dioxide in the power plant flue                gas to prepare the soda ash, thus realizing the                salt in the wastewater, resource utilization, and                no need to buy softeners.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INDUSTRIAL STANDARDS - Preferred Method: In the first level                  softening is added with a sodium hydroxide                  solution, the mol ratio of the sodium hydroxide                  solution to the magnesium ion is 2:1-3:1, and the                  staying time is 10-30 minutes. In the first level                  softened muddy water mixture enters the first                  high-density precipitation unit to precipitate,                  and the obtained magnesium hydroxide precipitated                  slurry is used for flue gas desulfurization, and                  the sulfur dioxide in the power plant flue gas is                  absorbed, and the precipitation time is 2-5                  hours. In the first high-density precipitation                  supernatant enters the secondary softening unit,                  adding the sodium carbonate solution, and the mol                  ratio of the sodium carbonate solution to the                  calcium ion is 1:1-1.5:1, and staying time is                  10-30 minutes. In the two-stage softening                  mud-water mixture enters the second high density                  sedimentation unit, obtaining calcium carbonate                  sludge into the sludge treatment unit, and                  settling time is 2-5 hours. In the second high                  density deposition unit supernatant water by                  adding hydrochloric acid solution to adjust the                  pH to 7-8 and then enters the membrane treatment                  system. In the method, after entering the                  membrane treatment system, the concentrated                  sodium sulfate-rich water produced by the                  nanofiltration is used for flue gas                  desulfurization, and the sodium chloride-rich                  fresh water is introduced into the membrane                  concentration unit; the fresh water produced by                  the membrane concentration unit as recycled                  water, the concentrated water produced enters the                  chelate resin unit, and the heavy metal ions e.g.                  calcium and magnesium are deeply removed, and so                  the sum of heavy metal ions in the effluent is                  not less than 0.1 mg/l. The chelating resin                  effluent entering the bipolar membrane                  electrodialysis unit, preparing the sodium                  hydroxide solution with concentration of 10% and                  8% hydrochloric acid solution, the sodium                  hydroxide solution can be reused in the primary                  softening unit and two-stage softening unit or                  used to produce soda ash. Preferred Components:                  The film processing system comprises orderly                  connected ultra-filtration, nano-filtration and                  membrane concentration unit. The membrane                  concentration unit comprises combination of                  reverse osmosis and/or electrodialysis.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))
MC A12-M05; A12-W11J; D04-A01; D04-B05A; D04-B07D; Q42-D
IP B01D-053/96; C01B-007/01; C01D-001/04; C01D-007/07; C02F-009/06

PN JP2019116596-A
TI Purification of resin composition used for forming molded products, involves contacting resin composition of preset volume density with super/subcritical fluid at preset temperature with respect to composition glass transition temperature.
AU ARIURA E
   MIYAZAWA S
AE NIPPON ZEON KK (JAPG-C)
GA 201962572P
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of resin composition, involves contacting the resin composition having a volume density of 0.6-0.8 g/cm3 with a supercritical or subcritical fluid at a temperature glass transition temperature ( degrees C) of resin composition or more to (glass transition temperature of resin composition+100) degrees C or less and at a pressure of 10 MPa or more.
   USE - Purification of resin composition used for forming molded products e.g. conveyance containers of semiconductors e.g. wafers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method fully suppress blocking in the resin composition obtained, and reduces the amount of high-boiling-point volatile organic compound contained in the resin composition, which enables molding and heating of the resin composition into desired shapes having excellent mechanical strength, without thermal deterioration. The purified resin composition enables efficient manufacture of molded products without contaminating the metal mold and biaxial kneader. Since discharge of volatile organic compounds from conveyance container formed using the purified resin composition is reduced, organic substance contamination of the accommodated wafer is prevented and yield of semiconductor product manufacture is improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the purifier used for purifying the resin composition.
   Reactor (10)
   Carbon dioxide tank (20)
   Heat exchanger (21)
   Entrainer tank (30)
   Purifier (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The resin composition contains 500 ppm or less volatile component having a boiling point of 250 degrees C or more. The resin composition is crystalline.

PN JP2019111476-A
TI Separation membrane used in e.g. water treatment, comprises polymer as main component and has preset hole diameter change rate in thickness direction of separation membrane.
AU SAKAEMURA H
   TAKADA K
   KOBAYASHI A
AE TORAY IND INC (TORA-C)
GA 2019604812
AB    NOVELTY - A separation membrane comprises polymer as a main component and has hole diameter change rate in thickness direction of separation membrane. The membrane has region (A) which is the aggregation of the region where the hole diameter change ( alpha i) is -0.1 to 0.1, and a region (B) adjacent to the region (A). The average hole diameter (Ra) of the region (A) is less than the average hole diameter of the region (B).
   USE - Separation membrane used in water treatment as microfiltration membrane, ultrafiltration membrane, manufacture of industrial water or drinking water from sea water, saline water, sewage or wastewater. Can also be used for kidney and plasma skimming, fruit juice concentration, exhaust gas or carbon dioxide gas or fuel cell separator, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The separation membrane has compatible permeation performance and removal performance in long time filtration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of separation membrane which involves preparing resin composition preparation by melt-kneading 15-90 %mass polymer and 10-85 %mass structure formation agent, discharging the resin composition from a discharge nozzle and forming a resin molding, impregnating the film with solvent (S1) in which the polymer has solubility parameter of 25 or less, and impregnating the film with solvent (S2) in which the polymer has solubility parameter of 35 or less and is larger than solvent (S1).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer is a hydrophilic polymer and comprises cellulose ester and/or nylon. Preferred Properties: The separation membrane has average hole diameter (Ra) of 1-1000 nm and (Rb) of 1-3000 nm. The separation membrane region (A) has thickness of 10-500 mu m and the ratio of thickness of regions (A) and (B) is 1 or less. The ratio of (Ra) with respect to Rb is 0.5 or less. The separation membrane has a breaking strength of 6 MPa or more.

TI Apparatus for operating coherent optical receiver,            has optical receiver comprising laser, and            power-control unit for controllably setting intensity            of local-oscillator signal based on power-control            settings stored in electronic controller.
AU VAN VEEN D
   HOUTSMA V
AE NOKIA SOLUTIONS & NETWORKS OY (OYNO-C)
   NOKIA USA INC (OYNO-C)
   NOKIA SOLUTIONS & NETWORKS OY (OYNO-C)
GA 201957620C
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a coherent optical receiver                (114) comprising a laser, an optical power-control                unit, an optical mixer, and one or more                photo-detectors. The optical mixer mixes an optical                input signal and an optical local-oscillator signal                and applies one or more resulting mixed optical                signals to the photo-detectors. The optical                power-control unit is connected between the laser                and the optical mixer. An electronic controller                (118) stores a set of power-control settings                corresponding to a set of remote optical                transmitters (112). The optical power-control unit                controllably set intensity of the optical                local-oscillator signal based on power-control                settings stored in the electronic controller.
   USE - Apparatus for operating a coherent optical                receiver.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus allows a passive optical network                (PON) to reduce amount of a fiber, a carbon dioxide                equipment, and an active traffic-management                equipment compared to that required for                point-to-point architectures, so that idle                intervals of a burst-mode optical transmitter can                be relatively short guard intervals configured to                prevent collision and/or interference of data                bursts transmitted to a same optical receiver by                different optical transmitters.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a PON                system.
   PON system (100)
   Processor (102)
   Remote optical transmitters (112)
   Coherent optical receiver (114)
   Electronic controller (118)

TI Active energy ray-curable composition uses in e.g.            coating agent, printing ink, photoresist, adhesive            agent or sealant, comprises anionically polymerizable            acrylate-based compound, and amidine-based compound as            photobase generator.
AU ARIMITSU K
   OOMURA K
AE UNIV TOKYO SCI FOUND (UNTS-C)
   TOA GOSEI KK (TOAG-C)
   TOA GOSEI KK (TOAG-C)
   UNIV TOKYO SCI FOUND (UNTS-C)
   TOA GOSEI KK (TOAG-C)
   UNIV TOKYO SCI FOUND (UNTS-C)
   UNIV TOKYO SCI FOUND (UNTS-C)
   TOA GOSEI KK (TOAG-C)
   TOA GOSEI KK (TOAG-C)
GA 201951877Q
AB    NOVELTY - An active energy ray-curable composition                comprises an anionically polymerizable                acrylate-based compound (I) and an amidine-based                compound (II) as a photobase generator.
   USE - Active energy ray hardening-type composition                is uses in coating agent, printing ink,                photoresist, adhesive agent, or sealant for forming                cured material (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has improved storage stability                prior to active energy ray irradiation. The                composition is capable of rapidly carrying out                photopolymerization reaction without generation of                carbon dioxide gas by active energy ray                irradiation, and maintaining a constant quality for                a long time without any increase in viscosity due                to gelation. The composition provides cured                material having improved quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An active energy ray-curable composition                comprises an anionically polymerizable                acrylate-based compound of formula (I) and an                amidine-based compound of formula (II) as a                photobase generator.
   EWG = electron-withdrawing group;
   X = single bond or -O-;
   R = n-valent organic group;
   n = 1-6;
   ring A = aryl or heteroaryl (both optionally                substituted);
   R1,R2 = H or alkyl;and
   R1+R2+N = optionally substituted ring.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                manufacture of active energy ray-curable                composition, which involves mixing the anionically                polymerizable acrylate-based compound (I) and                amidine-based compound (II) as a photobase                generator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The compound (I) is                  at least one chosen from dialkyl 2-methylene                  malonate, alkyl 2-acyl acrylate, alkyl 2-cyano                  acrylate, and 5-methylene-1,3-dioxane-4,6-dione                  compound.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1+R2+N = aziridinyl, azetidinyl,                pyrrolidinyl, piperidinyl, morpholinyl, imidazolyl,                pyrazolyl or 1,2,3,4-tetrahydroisoquinolinyl.

PT P
PN CN109650430-A; CN109650430-B
TI Preparing nano-calcium carbonate by high magnesium            content limestone, comprises adding disodium edetate in            calcium carbonate slurry and reacting to obtain            suspension, adding sodium stearate solution, drying and            crushing modified suspension.
AU LIU L
   ZHU Y
   LAN Q
   LIAO J
AE GUANGXI HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI HUANA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019394004
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-calcium carbonate by high                magnesium content limestone, comprises (i)                crushing, calcining, sieving, digestion and aging                high-magnesium according to a common method to                obtain refined lime milk, (ii) adjusting specific                gravity of the refined lime milk to 1.060-1.08, and                introducing refined lime milk into bubbling                carbonation reaction kettle, controlling reaction                temperature of lime milk, carbonizing by                introducing carbon dioxide mixed gas for 45-60                minutes, and adding sodium hydroxide and sodium                polyacrylate to carbonize pH to 7 at end point to                obtain calcium carbonate slurry, (iii) adding                disodium edetate in the calcium carbonate slurry,                keeping temperature at 60-80 degrees C for 30                minutes to obtain a calcium carbonate suspension,                then adding sodium stearate solution to the                suspension and stirring for 30 minutes to obtain a                modified calcium carbonate suspension, and (iv)                drying and crushing the modified calcium carbonate                suspension to obtain nano-calcium carbonate                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                nano-calcium carbonate by high magnesium content                limestone.
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-calcium carbonate: has regular                appearance, cubic shape, good dispersibility, low                oil absorption value and low pH value; expands                application range of low-quality and high-magnesium                content limestone; and improves utilization                efficiency of resources.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The magnesium content                  of the high-magnesium limestone is controlled to                  be 1.5-4.4%. The amount of the sodium hydroxide                  and sodium polyacrylate is 1.5-3% and 0.2-0.5% of                  the dry basis mass of calcium carbonate,                  respectively. The amount of disodium edetate is                  0.5-1 wt.% of the dry basis mass of calcium                  carbonate. The concentration of carbon dioxide in                  the carbon dioxide mixed gas is 30-35%. The lime                  milk has a reaction temperature of 20-30 degrees                  C. The sodium stearate solution is added in an                  amount of 2.5-3.5 wt.% of dry basis mass of                  calcium carbonate. The concentration of the                  sodium stearate solution is 10-15%.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Preparing volcanic rock catalyst useful for preparing a methanol synthesis gas from biomass gasification gas comprises e.g. mixing volcanic rock carrier with nickel salt solution, drying to obtain precursor, pre-calcining and calcining.
AU XU X
   JIANG E
   SUN Y
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA AGRIC (USCG-C)
GA 201925441C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing volcanic rock catalyst comprises (i) uniformly mixing volcanic rock carrier with nickel salt solution and drying to obtain precursor; (ii) pre-calcining the precursor to decompose the nickel salt and calcining at 700 degrees C for 4-8 hours to obtain volcanic rock catalyst, the mass fraction of the volcanic rock carrier is 80-95 pts. wt., mass of the nickel salt is 5-20 pts. wt. and the nickel salt is in terms of nickel oxide.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing a methanol synthesis gas from biomass gasification gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst maintains high activity under complex atmosphere of biomass gasification gas, is extremely active when preparing methanol synthesis gas by carbon dioxide combined with steam reforming technology and has high methane conversion rate and hydrogen and carbon monoxide are extremely selective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for:
   (1) volcanic rock catalyst prepared by the preparation method as mentioned above; and
   (2) preparing the methanol synthesis gas by using a biomass gasification gas comprising introducing a biomass gasification gas and a supplementary gas into a catalyst bed containing the volcanic rock catalyst and reacting to form methanol synthesis gas and the make-up gas is a mixture of water vapor or methane/steam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The space velocity of the reaction is 2500-3200 mL.g-1.h-1. The mass ratio of volcanic rock carrier to nickel salt in terms of nickel oxide is 8.5-9.5:1. The mass ratio of volcanic rock carrier to nickel salt in terms of nickel oxide is 9:1. Preferred Method: The method in step (ii) after pre-calcining calcining at 800-900 degrees C for 4-8 hours to obtain volcanic rock catalyst. The reaction temperature is 750-900 degrees C.
