The structure of the utility model is simple, the operation cost is low, and the treating efficiency is high, the final water can meet the requirement of recycling in the steel industry.

AB    NOVELTY - A composite zinc powder graphene coating                comprises base material and curing agent. The base                material comprises 12-18 pts. wt. flaky zinc                powder, 2-8 pts. wt. spherical zinc powder, 5-10                pts. wt. graphene ceramic composite material, 10-20                pts. wt. active filler, 15-30 pts. wt. epoxy resin,                10-30 pts. wt. thinner and 1-10 pts. wt. additive.                The curing agent is an amine curing agent.
   USE - Composite zinc powder graphene coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite zinc powder graphene coating has                high electrical conductivity, high sheet bonding                properties and excellent corrosion resistance,                forms excellent conductive network, improves                synergistic effect of flake zinc powder and                graphene microchips, and achieves excellent                cathodic protection under low zinc                conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of graphene ceramic composite material,                involves magnesium powder and aluminum oxide                powder, placing obtained mixed powder in a reactor,                inducing electricity in carbon dioxide atmosphere,                spreading the combustion reaction and cooling to                obtain graphene ceramic composite material, mixing                graphene ceramic composite material, epoxy resin                and diluent, adding active filler, stirring evenly,                grinding to fineness of less than or equal to 50 mu                m, adding flake zinc powder and spherical zinc                powder, stirring to obtain composite zinc powder                graphene coating base material, mixing composite                zinc powder graphene coating base material and                curing agent and stirring uniformly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of base                  material to curing agent is (3-8):1. Graphene                  ceramic composite is graphene, magnesium                  aluminate spinel and alumina. The active filler                  is talc powder, lithopone, mica powder,                  precipitated barium sulfate, zinc phosphate, and                  nano silica. Preferred Process: Graphene ceramic                  composite material is prepared by carrying out                  combustion synthesis reaction using magnesium                  powder, alumina powder and carbon dioxide as raw                  materials.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diluent is xylene,                  n-butanol, cyclohexanone, isophorone, methyl                  isobutyl ketone, ethylene glycol monobutyl ether,                  propylene glycol methyl ether acetate, and benzyl                  alcohol. The additive is leveling agent, coupling                  agent, dispersing agent, and defoamer. Preferred                  Properties: The particle size of flake zinc                  powder in the base material is 10-20 mu m. The                  particle diameter of spherical zinc powder is 2-4                  mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The epoxy resin is                  E-51 epoxy resin, E-44 epoxy resin, E-20 epoxy                  resin, DER331 (RTM: Liquid epoxy resin) epoxy                  resin, DER671 (RTM: Liquid epoxy resin) epoxy                  resin, and CYD011 epoxy resin.

PN IN201731017393-A
TI Extracting water soluble eggshell membrane protein, comprises e.g. preparing eggshell membrane (ESM) powder, and ultrasonication of powder ESM for many times with and without autoclaving pretreatment for protein concentration.
AU SAH M K
   RATH S N
   ROOPAVATH U K
   PANIGRAHI B B
AE MAHESH K S (MAHE-Individual)
   SUBHA N R (SUBH-Individual)
GA 201895069M
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting (M1) water soluble eggshell membrane protein (SEP), comprises: preparing eggshell membrane (ESM) powder; ultrasonication of powder ESM for many times with and without autoclaving pretreatment for protein concentration; estimating total protein concentration of extracted SEP; characterizing total extraction with sodium dodecyl sulfate-polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis (SDS-PAGE); and making many studies for studying the physico and thermal properties of lyophilized SEP from supernatant and ESM for pellet partition.
   USE - The method is useful for: extracting water soluble eggshell membrane protein; and cells culturing in homogenized ESM fibers (claimed), which is used for tissue regeneration. No biological data given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for cells culturing (M2) in homogenized ESM fibers, comprising examining cytotoxicity and biocompatibility of ESM derived samples with human adipose tissue derived mesenchymal stem cells (hMSCs), seeding hMSCs samples onto weighed samples, culturing the seeded samples in Dulbecco's modification of eagle medium (DMEM) supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum, 1% L-glutamine and 1% penicillin-streptomycin antibiotics, maintaining cultures at 37 degrees C in a humidified atmosphere containing 5% carbon dioxide replacing media every 2 days, studying cell seeded samples for a period of 3d, 7d, 14d, and 21d, fluorescein diacetate/stain propidium iodide staining for live-dead cell assay, and adherencing cells by field emission scanning electron microscopy (FESEM).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The ultrasonication of powder ESM for protein concentration includes: autoclaving powder ESM samples; preparing aqueous mixture of 2wt%; controlling non-autoclaved samples of powder ESM; ultrasonicating both samples (autoclaved and non-autoclaved) by sonic ruptor 250 ultrasonic homogenizer; locating a micro tip probe of ultrasonic homogenizer at 2cm below a top surface of mixture all the time; placing both samples in an ice bath for maintaining a temperature well below 30 degrees C; performing ultrasonication at 15 kHz for extraction of SEP at many times; performing all the sonication in triplicate; centrifuging the sample at 8000g for 10 minutes; and determining the concentration in the supernatant. The total protein concentration of extracted SEP supernatant from ESM extract is estimated by a bicinchoninic acid assay protein assay kit (Thermo fisher). The total protein content of extract SEP from supernatant characterized with SDS-PAGE, includes: running SEP solution samples on 12% homogeneous gels; dissolving the protein SEP solution in sample buffer in the presence of 5 wt/vol.% para-mercaptoethanol; heating for 5 minutes at 95 degrees C in an Eppendorf Thermomixer (RTM: Device for heating, cooling, and mixing almost any of lab vessels) loading solution onto the gel at a concentration of 15 mu g per electrophoresis well; and running the gels at a constant current (15 mA/slab gel) for 40 minutes. The studies to determine physicochemical and thermal properties of lyophilized SEP from supernatant and ESM from pellet partition, includes: FESEM; X-ray diffraction (XRD); Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR); and thermal gravimetric analysis (TGA), where to perform FESEM observation at a 15 kV accelerating potential and observe the behavior of ESM and lyophilized SEP towards cells. In the method (M1): many time periods with and without autoclaving for the extraction of SEP includes 0.5 hours, 4 hours, 8 hours, and 12 hours; to perform a series of measurements on the freeze-dried powder samples or lyophilized SEP obtained during extractions to determine changes in their crystallinity by X-ray diffraction on a diffractometer; FTIR spectra analysis of 1 g of many ESM sample is performed with FTIR spectrometer system for spectral acquisition and instrumental control; to analyze the thermal behavior of 20 mg of many ESM sample by TGA test using thermal analyzer; and the un-autoclaved samples of thermal stability of extracted SEP of ESM are more. In the method (M2): fluorescein diacetate/stain propidium iodide staining for live-dead cell assay includes using fluorescent probes to label the viable and nonviable cells, washing cell-seeded biopolymeric samples with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), incubating the sell seeded biopolymeric samples at 37 degrees C for 15 minutes in a carbon dioxide incubator with 2 mu g/ml fluorescein diacetate in PBS (once), rinsing them twice in PBS to remove the unstained fluorescein diacetate and placing in 20 mu g/ml propidium iodide solution for 2 minutes at room temperature, keeping specimens in PBS and viewing under a fluorescent microscope, and labeling viable cytoplasm of the cells are green and labeling non-viable cells are red; and FESEM for cellular adherence includes washing cell-seeded biopolymeric samples with PBS (once), fixing samples with 2.5% glutaraldehyde for 4 hours at room temperature, dehydrating the fixing samples in a graded ethanol series, air drying and coating with gold using a sputtering unit, viewing the samples with FESEM, and analyzing the external morphology of the surfaces and their behavior towards the attachment of cells.
   ACTIVITY - Vulnerary.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparing ruthenium-based catalyst useful in            Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction, comprises e.g.            extruding and molding the alumina precursor into strip,            drying molded product to obtain carrier semi-finished            product, and roasting.
AU SUN X
   HOU C
   XIA G
   WU Y
   JIN C
   YAN Z
   LI M
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
GA 201895694P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ruthenium-based catalyst comprises                (1) extruding and molding the alumina precursor                into strip, drying the molded product to obtain                carrier semi-finished product, and roasting carrier                semi-finished product; (2) supporting the active                metal component ruthenium on the alumina carrier                obtained in the step (1); where the method of                molding the extruded strip and selection of alumina                precursor make the grain size of carrier                semi-finished product larger than that of alumina                precursor, and is characterized by X-ray                diffraction, where the amount of increase Delta d                is at least 0.1 nm, Delta d=d1-d2, d1 is the grain                size of the alumina precursor, and d2 is the grain                size of the carrier semi-finished product.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in Fischer-Tropsch                synthesis reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple. The catalyst: has                excellent performance when applied to                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction; and has high                C5+ hydrocarbon selectivity, high CO conversion                rate, low carbon dioxide and methane                selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) ruthenium-based catalyst is prepared by                above mentioned method; and
   (2) Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction                comprising contacting synthesis gas with catalyst                under Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction                conditions, where the catalyst is ruthenium-based                catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of increase                  Delta d is 0.1 to 2 nm, preferably 0.7-1.4 nm.                  The alumina precursor is pseudo-boehmite; the                  pseudo-boehmite has grain size of 3-5.2 nm; the                  pseudo-boehmite contains no sulfur-containing                  compounds. Preferred Method: In the method,                  molding and extruding the alumina precursor                  comprises mixing the alumina precursor, water,                  extrusion agent and peptizer to obtain mixture,                  extruding obtained mixture in extruder, where the                  weight ratio of water to alumina precursor is                  0.5-1.8:1, preferably 0.55-1.5:1; based on 100                  pts. wt. alumina precursor, used amount of                  extrusion agent is 0.1-6 pts. wt., preferably 2-4                  pts. wt., extrusion agent of peptizer is 0.1-6                  pts. wt., preferably 0.6-4 pts. wt.; preferably,                  the extruder is screw extruder. In the method,                  molding and extruding at 40-90 degrees C,                  preferably 45-80 degrees C. In the step (1),                  drying comprises drying the molded product within                  0-10 hours, preferably within 0-5 hours after                  extruding; drying temperature is 80-300 degrees                  C, preferably 140-260 degrees C. In the step (1),                  roasting at 350-750 degrees C, preferably 450-660                  degrees C for 1-12 hours, preferably 2-5 hours.                  In the step (2), the alumina carrier and the                  active metal component ruthenium are used in                  amount such that the ruthenium content in terms                  of oxide is 0.1-15 wt.%, preferably 0.2-12 wt.%                  based on total weight of catalyst. In the method,                  introducing auxiliary agent into catalyst after                  the step (1), where the auxiliary agent                  comprising lanthanum, zirconium, cerium, silicon,                  tungsten , molybdenum, copper, manganese,                  rhenium, rhodium, palladium, osmium, iridium,                  platinum, silver and/or gold; preferably, the                  amount of the auxiliary agent is such that the                  amount of the auxiliary agent based on the oxide                  is 0.01-10 wt.% based on the total weight of                  catalyst. In the method, introducing auxiliary                  agent into the catalyst prior to step (2).

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of zinc oxide (ZnO)-zirconium                dioxide (ZrO2)-alumina (Al2O3)-ZSM-5 double-shell                catalyst comprises stirring ammonium carbonate,                zirconium nitrate pentahydrate and zinc nitrate                hexahydrate at molar ratio of 2.2:1.0:0.2, forming                precipitate, aging at 70 degrees C for 2 hours,                cooling, filtering, washing, drying at 110 degrees                C for 4 hours, baking at 500 degrees C for 3 hours                to obtain ZnO-ZrO2 powder, coating Al2O3 on surface                of ZnO-ZrO2 at weight ratio of 1:2-1:9, drying at                105 degrees C for 1 hour, baking at 600 degrees C                for 3 hours, milling into powder, stirring                tetraethyl orthosilicate, sodium aluminate,                tetrapropylammonium hydroxide and water at mol                ratio of 40-360:1:19:4015, aging at room                temperature for 3 hours to form sol system, adding                powder, stirring, hydrothermally reacting in                homogeneous reactor at 170-190 degrees C for 36-48                hours, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging,                washing by deionized water and anhydrous ethanol,                drying at 120 degrees C for 12 hours and baking at                500-600 degrees C for 3 hours.

PN WO2018125879-A1; TW201827394-A
TI Treating organic carbonate stream to remove contaminants, involves passing organic carbonate stream through fixed bed comprising weak base to remove contaminants and produce less contaminated stream.
AU YU K
   VAPORCIYAN G G
AE SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
GA 201852489D
AB    NOVELTY - Method (I) of treating an organic carbonate stream involves passing the organic carbonate stream through a fixed bed to remove contaminants and produce a less contaminated stream, where the fixed bed comprises a weak base.
   USE - The method is used for treating an organic carbonate stream to remove contaminants, where the organic carbonate, e.g. ethylene carbonate is used for the production of acyclic dialkyl carbonate and monoalkylene glycol, the alkylene carbonate is used as solvents and diluents in industrial processes, used as dyes, fibers, plastics and batteries and as raw materials for commercial products such as cosmetics and pharmaceuticals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the total halogens to a very low content and produces less contaminated steams. The diphenyl carbonate has a very stringent halogen specification of less than 300 parts per billion by weight (ppbw).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method (II) of producing diphenyl carbonate involving (a) reacting carbon dioxide and ethylene oxide to form ethylene carbonate, (b) reacting the ethylene carbonate with ethanol by a transesterification reaction to form diethyl carbonate and mono-ethylene glycol, (c) reacting the diethyl carbonate with phenol by a transesterification and disproportionation reaction to form diphenyl carbonate and (d) passing the diphenyl carbonate stream through the fixed bed to remove contaminants and produce a less contaminated stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weak base is chosen from alkali, alkaline earth metal, an oxide (calcium oxide or magnesium oxide), a hydroxide (sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, lithium hydroxide, cesium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, barium hydroxide, magnesium hydroxide or strontium hydroxide), a carbonate (sodium hydrogen carbonate, potassium hydrogen carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate or calcium carbonate), a hydride (sodium hydride or calcium hydride), an amide (lithium diisopropyl amide), an alcoholate (sodium methoxide, sodium ethoxide, lithium methoxide, lithium ethoxide, potassium methoxide or potassium ethoxide), or a phenolate of an alkali or alkaline earth metal (sodium phenoxide, potassium phenoxide, lithium phenoxide, calcium phenoxide, disodium salt of bisphenol A, or dilithium salt of bisphenol A), a hydride of boron (sodium borohydride, tetramethylammonium borohydride or tetrabutylammonium borohydride), a hydride of aluminum (lithium aluminum hydride), a salt of an inorganic acid (sodium acetate, potassium acetate, lithium acetate, calcium acetate, sodium stearate, potassium stearate, lithium stearate, calcium stearate, sodium benzoate, potassium benzoate or lithium benzoate), a phosphate or a borate of an alkali or alkaline earth metal (disodium hydrogen phosphate, dipotassium hydrogen phosphate, dilithium hydrogen phosphate, disodium phenylphosphate, dipotassium phenylphosphate, dilithium phenylphosphate or sodium borate) and their derivatives, and a quaternary ammonium hydroxide (tetramethylammonium hydroxide, tetraethylammonium hydroxide, tetrapropylammonium hydroxide, tetrabutylammonium hydroxide, benzyltrimethylammonium hydroxide or hexadecyltrimethylammonium hydroxide). The contaminants comprise halogens (organic chlorides) or ethyl oxitol. The fixed bed comprises a bed of extrudates or other low pressure drop solids. Preferred Composition: The less contaminated stream has less than 50% of the contaminants in the organic carbonate stream. The organic carbonate stream contains less than 0.01 wt.% halogens. Preferred Method: The method (I) further involves regenerating the fixed bed to remove contaminants. The method (I) comprises using two fixed beds, where one can be regenerated while the other is contacted with the organic carbonate stream. The method (II) further involves passing the diphenyl carbonate to a purification step after the diphenyl carbonate is passed through the fixed bed, where the purification step comprises a distillation column.

PT P
PN CN108239894-A; CN108239894-B
TI Constant-temperature extrusion molding system has heat exchange tubes that are arranged in predetermined chamber, and heat exchange tube in which each of two ends is in communication with two prescribed chambers.
AU LIN Z
AE LIN Z (LINZ-Individual)
   LIN Z (LINZ-Individual)
GA 2018539360
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has temperature-increasing roller that is arranged at a relative position of film housing co-extruded with pressure roller (10). A roller housing is formed with a temperature equalizing chamber provided with a pair of partitions to partition the temperature equalizing chamber into a separate first chamber, a second chamber and a third chamber room. The rotating shafts are respectively formed on the two side plates of the roller housing. The flow channels (221) are respectively in communication with the first chamber and the third chamber. The heat exchange tubes are arranged in second chamber. Each of the two ends of the heat exchange tube is in communication with first chamber and third chamber. The second chamber is a closed space and is provided with supercritical fluid. The supercritical fluid is carbon dioxide, water, methane, ethane, propane, ethylene, propylene, methanol, ethanol or acetone.
   USE - Constant-temperature extrusion molding system.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat is quickly and evenly spread and transmitted, so that the uniform heating or heat dissipation is produced. Thus, the product quality and yield are improved effectively.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the constant-temperature extrusion molding system.
   Pressure roller (10)
   Temperature equalizing roller structure (20)
   Film (30)
   Constant-temperature extrusion molding system (100)
   Flow channel (221)

PT P
PN KR2018070096-A
TI Mushroom grower has control unit that controls unit cooler, intake unit, and exhaust unit on basis of growth information stored in crop growth database and value measured by sensor.
AU LEE H G
   LIM H S
   KIM N H
   CHO Y S
   SEO C S
   ROH C R
AE HERIT CO LTD (HERI-Non-standard)
   GREEN MARITIME CO LTD (GREE-Non-standard)
   KT CORP (KTKT-C)
GA 201852362B
AB    NOVELTY - The mushroom grower has a unit panel which is made of high-density expanded polystyrene (EPS) and having a certain curvature. A dome structure is formed by connecting the unit panels. A unit cooler (630) is located above the interior of the dome structure. An intake unit (640) is positioned above the interior of the dome structure. An exhaust unit (650) is located on the lower side of the inside of the dome structure. A sensor (660) measures the carbon-dioxide concentration, humidity or temperature inside the dome structure. Crop growth database stores mushroom growth information. A control unit (600) controls the unit cooler, the intake unit, and the exhaust unit on the basis of the growth information stored in the crop growth database and the value measured by the sensor.
   USE - Mushroom grower.
   ADVANTAGE - The mushroom grower has excellent durability, warmth, and workability. Mushroom grower reduces the cooling and heating cost because it is possible to suppress the thermal efficiency loss of radiation and convection inside the cultivation due to the blocking of the direct sunlight input.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block view of the mushroom grower. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Control unit (600)
   Unit cooler (630)
   Intake unit (640)
   Exhaust unit (650)
   Sensor (660)

PN WO2018110341-A1; KR2019086663-A; CN110062747-A; IN201917016345-A; US2019315630-A1; EP3556730-A1; JP2018556583-X; EP3556730-A4; JP2020186166-A; KR2021027501-A; KR2238500-B1; JP6862473-B2; JP6906090-B2; KR2305363-B1; US2021323835-A1; CN114394617-A; US11352265-B2; CN110062747-B
TI Potassium titanate powder used in friction            modifier used in resin composition, comprises columnar            potassium titanate particles having preset range of            average long diameter, average short diameter and            aspect ratio.
AU HIOKI T
   MORI H
   ITO Y
   SEN H
AE OTSUKA CHEM CO LTD (SAKB-C)
   OTSUKA CHEM CO LTD (SAKB-C)
   OTSUKA CHEM CO LTD (SAKB-C)
   OTSUKA CHEM CO LTD (SAKB-C)
   OTSUKA CHEM HOLDINGS CO LTD (SAKB-C)
   OTSUKA CHEM CO LTD (SAKB-C)
GA 201849493V
AB    NOVELTY - A potassium titanate powder comprises columnar                potassium titanate particles (I) having an average                long diameter of 30 mu m or more, an average short                diameter of 10 mu m or more, and an aspect ratio of                1.5 or more.
   USE - Potassium titanate powder is used in friction                modifier used in resin composition for forming                molded article e.g. material for friction component                (all claimed) e.g. brake lining, disc pad and                clutch facing for vehicle and industrial                machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The molded article formed using the resin                composition containing the potassium titanate                powder has excellent mechanical strength,                fade-proof property and abrasion resistance while                avoiding safety and health concerns, and high                friction coefficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The columnar potassium titanate particles are                of formula: K2TinO2n+1 (I).
   n = 5.5-6.5.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) resin composition, which comprises the                potassium titanate powder and a thermosetting                resin; and
   (2) manufacture of potassium titanate powder,                which involves preparing a pulverized mixture by                mechanically pulverizing titanium dioxide or a                compound that generates titanium dioxide by heating                and potassium carbonate, preparing potassium                dititanate by heating and calcining (i) the                pulverized mixture under an atmosphere having a                carbon dioxide concentration of 1-60 vol.%, and                heating and calcining (ii) potassium dititanate                after eluting the potassium content from the                potassium dititanate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of                  particles having average long diameter of 30 mu m                  or more and average short diameter of 10 mu m or                  less is 50 vol.% or more. The content of fibrous                  particles is 0.3 vol.% or less. Preferred                  Property: The powder has a specific surface area                  of 0.3-3 m2/g. Preferred Process: The calcining                  (i) is performed at 800-1000 degrees C. The                  elution of the potassium content is performed by                  mixing an acid with the aqueous slurry of the                  potassium dititanate to adjust the pH of the                  aqueous slurry to 11.5-12.5. The calcining (ii)                  is performed at 600-1300 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The resin composition                  further comprises 0.5 %mass or less copper                  component.

The                  aluminosilicate zeolite has a mean crystal size                  of at least 100 nm. The aluminosilicate zeolite                  has crystal size of 0.1-7 mu m, preferably 0.5-6                  mu m. The aluminosilicate zeolite comprises at                  least 95%,, preferably 97% phase pure AEI                  framework. The aluminosilicate zeolite is                  substantially free of medium and large pore                  frameworks. The aluminosilicate zeolite is                  substantially free of FAU-framework. The                  aluminosilicate zeolite further comprises an                  alkaline earth metal. The AEI zeolite crystals                  are at least 90% phase pure. The AEI zeolite                  crystals have an SAR of 20-50. The AEI zeolite                  crystals have a mean crystal size of at least 100                  nm. At least one zeolite is the aluminum source.                  The zeolite is the silica and aluminum source.                  The zeolite is zeolite-Y. The zeolite comprises                  at least two zeolite-Y having different                  silica-to-alumina ratios. The synthesis gel                  comprises an alkaline metal source with a molar                  ratio of alkaline metal to alumina is not greater                  than 2. The synthesis gel has molar ratios of:                  silica:alumina of 20-60; disodium oxide:alumina                  of 0.5-2; SDA2O:alumina of 3-10; water:alumina of                  600-2500, and hydroxyl ion:silica of 0.4-0.6. The                  SDA comprises 2,6-N,N-diethyl-cis                  2,6-dimethylpiperidium. Preferred Method: The                  reacting step is performed at a temperature of                  120-180 degrees C for 2-15 days. The relative                  yield on silica is at least about 30%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN US2018087019-A1
TI System comprises tank or pond configured to grow microorganisms, high shear device configured to process a gas consumable by microorganisms in a liquid medium, and a fluid line configured to extract medium containing microorganisms.
AU HASSAN A
   BORSINGER G G
   ANTHONY R G
AE HRD CORP (HRDH-C)
GA 201824686U
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a tank or pond configured to grow microorganisms; a high shear device (200) configured to process a gas consumable by the microorganisms in a liquid medium operating at a shear rate of not greater than 1000000/s to form a gas-super-saturated (GSS) liquid stream and feed the GSS stream into the tank or pond for microorganism growth, where the GSS stream maintains a GSS level for greater than or equal to 10 minutes; and a fluid line configured to extract a medium containing microorganisms from the tank or pond.
   USE - The system is useful for generating products from microorganisms, including ethane, ethylene, sugars, acids, lipids, ketones, alcohols, amino acids, proteins, or its combinations.
   ADVANTAGE - The process and system are able to reduce or eliminate the bottleneck of insufficient consumable carbon dioxide (CO2) during microorganism culture and thus for deriving valuable products. The method and system are capable to reducing this hindrance and significantly improve efficiency for microorganism culture and product recovery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a longitudinal cross-section view of a multi-stage high shear device.
   High shear device (200)
   Generators (220, 230, 240)
   Stators (227, 228, 229)
   Drive shaft (250)
   Arrow (260)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred System: The system comprises a nutrient source consumable by the microorganisms, where the nutrient source is co-fed to the tank or pond for microorganism growth. The nutrient source comprises municipal waste; sewage waste; paper pulp; chemical and petrochemical; vegetable including grain, sugar; farm discharge; animal farm discharge including beef, pork, poultry; canning discharge, fishing discharge; farming discharge; food processing discharge. The system comprises a pretreating unit for the nutrient source, where the pretreating unit comprises a high shear device. The pretreating unit is configured to eliminate undesirable pathogens via gas-assisted high shear lysing of pathogen cells. It is configured to increase the bio-availability of nutrient in the nutrient source. The system further comprises a product recovery unit configured to receive the microorganism-containing medium from the fluid line. The product recovery unit comprises another high shear device configured to disintegrate cell walls of and intracellular organelles in the microorganisms to release products. The system comprises a pressurized vessel to enhance the disintegration. It also comprises a conversion system configured to convert at least one of the products to biodiesel. The system is modular. It is integrated with an existing facility.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.)

PT P
PN WO2018052937-A1; US2018128770-A1
TI Solid contact ion-selective electrode for sensor, has electron-conducting substrate, polymeric sensing membrane, and hydrophobic conductive polymer film layer disposed between electron- conducting substrate and polymeric sensing membrane.
AU LINDNER E
   GUZINSKI M
AE LINDNER E (LIND-Individual)
   GUZINSKI M (GUZI-Individual)
   LINDNER E (LIND-Individual)
   GUZINSKI M (GUZI-Individual)
GA 201823204J
AB    NOVELTY - The solid contact ion-selective electrode (2) comprises electron-conducting substrate (20), polymeric sensing membrane (30), and highly hydrophobic conductive polymer film layer (40) disposed between electron-conducting substrate and polymeric sensing membrane, such that polymer film layer comprises Tetradecyl-substituted polyethylenedioxythiophene (PEDOT-C14) or its homologues, in combination with highly hydrophobic counter ion. The polymeric sensing membrane comprises plasticized or unplasticized PVC, polyurethane, methacrylate, or silicon rubber films.

   ADVANTAGE - The solid contact ion-selective electrode is robust, more durable, maintenance free and easier to miniaturize, has excellent potential stability and short hydration time, and attains improved response characteristics, thus enabling device with sensors to be more accurate, precise and faster. Eliminates carbon dioxide (CO2) interference in pH sensor response.

TI Preparation of nitrogen gas, by compressing air, obtaining preconditioned air, deoxidizing, feeding air into membrane separation system, trapping nitrogen, carbon monoxide and argon on permeate side of membrane and purifying.
AU GUO T
   WU C
   YANG J
   KANG L
   HUANG Z
AE HEFEI TRANSCENDENT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 201818340Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nitrogen gas involves compressing air, storing in buffer tank, cooling air, passing air through heater to remove large amount of water vapor in air and prevent water vapor from entering mold, obtaining preconditioned air, deoxidizing air, passing deoxygenated air into molecular sieve adsorption bed, feeding air into membrane separation system, trapping nitrogen, carbon monoxide and argon on permeate side of membrane to obtain purified nitrogen and subjecting purified nitrogen to re-adsorption and purification in nitrogen-producing facility.
   USE - Preparation of nitrogen gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of nitrogen gas with high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of nitrogen gas involves compressing air by air compressor, storing in buffer tank, cooling air by a condenser, passing air through a heater to remove large amount of water vapor in the air and prevent water vapor from entering mold, obtaining preconditioned air, passing preconditioned air through a deoxygenation reactor for deoxidation treatment, passing deoxygenated air into molecular sieve adsorption bed so that residual water vapor, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons in the air can be removed, feeding air into membrane separation system, trapping nitrogen, carbon monoxide and argon on the permeate side of the membrane to obtain purified nitrogen and subjecting purified nitrogen to re-adsorption and purification in nitrogen-producing facility.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The molecular sieve adsorption bed is double-layered bed containing upper layer of molecular sieve, lower layer of activated alumina having improved moisture adsorption rate. The upper molecular sieve adsorbs moisture, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons. The membrane separation system is made of tubular fiber membrane module including polyimide fiber membrane module, polypropylene fiber membrane module, polyvinylidene fluoride fiber membrane module and cellulose acetate fiber membrane module.

AU DAI W
AE NANJING JINGYUN CHEM CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 201808890J
AB    NOVELTY - Traditional Chinese medicine eye mask comprises 20-30 pts. wt. Clerodendrum fortunatum, 15-25 pts. wt. Mosla dianthera, 10-20 pts. wt. Lepidium apetalum and 6-10 pts. wt. Desmodium triflorum.
   USE - Traditional Chinese medicine eye mask is useful in the preparation of moisturizing cosmetics (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The traditional Chinese medicine eye mask is fine and stable in performance, does not cause irritation to skin, is environmentally-friendly, and is convenient to use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the method for preparing the traditional Chinese medicine eye mask, which involves crushing each raw materials, adding the crushed materials to an extraction kettle, preheating the extraction kettle and a separation kettle, cooling a storage tank, opening the gas supply of carbon dioxide gas cylinder when the preset temperature is reached, boosting a high-pressure pump, carrying out constant-temperature and constant-pressure extraction at a separation pressure of 5-6 MPa and a separation temperature of 50 degrees C for 1 hour, discharging the mixture to obtain a volatile oil, subjecting the supercritical extraction medicine residue to ultrasonic extraction using 10-20 times ethanol, filtering, concentrating the filtrate at 55 degrees C to obtain a concentrated solution with a relative density of 1.20-1.25, drying the concentrated solution to obtain an extract powder, combining the volatile oil and extract powder, adding additive materials, and uniformly mixing to obtain the eye mask.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The hydrogel                  composition further comprises alginate, and                  pancreatic cells. The hydrogel                  composition is gelled by binding a CTP terminal                  end and an alginate end. The hydrogel composition                  comprises chlorophyll at a concentration of                  101/ml to 1010/ml, and insulin secreted by the                  pancreatic cell. Preferred Method: The method                  further comprises encapsulating the pancreatic                  cell in the gelled hydrogel. The step of                  irradiating the light is performed in an oxygen                  concentration of 101/ml to 1010/ml, and performed                  over 1-5 times at 20-40&#176; C for 10-50                  minutes at intervals of 10-50 minutes.

A degree                to which the particles are carbonated is                determined, where the water is contained in a                watertight or substantially watertight vessel, and                the vessel is open to atmosphere. The water is                completely immersed in the water. Carbonated water                is caused to move through the water by contacting                unconfirmed RCA with the water in such a way that                the water percolates down through the un confluent                RCA in a uniform or substantially uniform manner,                emerging at a bottom of the unconfined RCA, and                introducing the water at or near a surface of the                water, in which the water to flow through the                immersed RCA to an exit site that is at the                bottom.

The molecular weight of the plasticizer B is 10000-20000. The extrusion granulation temperature of the twin-screw extruder in (S1) is 80-160degrees Celsius. The extrusion granulation temperature of the twin-screw extruder in (S2) is 140-230degrees Celsius. The extrusion temperature of the sheet extruder in (S3) is 160-220degrees Celsius, the cooling roller adopts a mirror roller, and the cooling temperature is kept at 5-15degrees Celsius. The thickness of the prepared sheet is 25-30 mum and the width is 40 cm.

TI Method for recovering scandium in red mud using aqueous ammonia in coking plant, involves adding concentrated sulfuric acid to scandium-rich waste liquid, mixing aqueous ammonia with acidified scandium-rich waste liquid, ventilating and calcining scandium raw materials.

   ZHOU L
   ZHANG S
   HUANG T
AE CHANGSHU INST TECHNOLOGY (CHGS-C)
GA 2021E6199M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recovering scandium in red mud involves (1) mixing the red mud and the sodium carbonate aqueous solution, stirring evenly, injecting carbon dioxide gas into the slurry, and filtering, to obtain scandium-rich waste liquid, (2) adding concentrated sulfuric acid to the scandium-rich waste liquid to obtain acidified scandium-rich waste liquid, (3) mixing aqueous ammonia of coking plant with the acidified scandium-rich waste liquid, stirring uniformly to obtain the mixed liquid, subjecting the mixed liquid to low-temperature plasma irradiation, cooling to room temperature and filtering, drying obtained solid portion to obtain the primary recovered scandium raw material, (4) ventilating and calcining newly recovered scandium raw materials at a temperature of 1000-1200degrees Celsius for 3-6 hours, and cooling obtained sintered structure to room temperature.
   USE - Method for recovering scandium in red mud using aqueous ammonia in coking plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and can effectively recycle the scandium element in the red mud and provides the prepared Scandium oxide with high purity. The method can realize the recovery of 63% scandium in the mud, and the prepared scandium oxide purity can reach 92%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The initial concentration of the sodium carbonate aqueous solution is 5-25:100g/mL. The solid-liquid ratio of red mud and sodium carbonate aqueous solution is 1:3-5g/mL. The concentration of sulfate in the medium-rich scandium waste liquid is 5-10 M. The volume ratio of aqueous ammonia and acidified scandium-rich waste liquid of the China Coking Plant is 0.5-2.5:1. The aqueous ammonia of China Coking Plant includes total ammonium of 3684-9879 mg/L, volatile ammonium of 2347-8642 mg/L, carbon dioxide of 113-2631 mg/L, hydrogen sulfide of 227-2540 mg/L, and hydrogen cyanide of 9-162 mg/L.Preferred Properties: The voltage of low-temperature plasma is 5-75 kV.

PN CN113559833-A
TI Hollow sodium titanate nano-sheet assembly used for photocatalysis, has a hollow rod-shaped structure formed by uniformly assembling sodium titanate nano-sheets with specific length and width.
AU XIONG J
   ZHU X
   LI S
   CHENG G
AE UNIV WUHAN TEXTILE (UWTX-C)
   WUHAN INST TECHNOLOGY (WUHT-C)
GA 2021C53145
AB    NOVELTY - Hollow sodium titanate (Na4TiO4) nano-sheet assembly has a hollow rod-shaped structure formed by uniformly assembling Na4TiO4 nano-sheets with length of 50-150 nm and width of 10-40 nm to form a diameter of 300-500 nm and a length of 1.5-2.5 microm.
   USE - Hollow Na4TiO4 nano-sheet assembly used for photocatalysis (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The Na4TiO4-based nanosheet assembly and H2Ti2O5H2O nanosheets have photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction activity, and TiO2 nanosheets have excellent photocatalytic hydrogen evolution activity. The synthesis method is simple, easy to operate, cost effective, in line with actual production needs, and has good application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. a method for preparing hollow Na4TiO4 nano-sheet assembly, which involves adding titanium source into ethylene glycol, refluxing, reacting to obtain the titanium dioxide (TiO2) precursor, centrifuging the product, drying, dispersing product in water, adding sodium hydroxide solution to the mixture, stirring uniformly and subjecting to hydro-thermal reaction to obtain the hollow Na4TiO4 nano-sheet assembly; and
   2. a nanosheet derivative, which is prepared by sequentially acidifying and calcining to obtain H2Ti2O5H2O having a length of 50-150 nm, width of 10-30 nm and a TiO2 nanosheet derivative with a length of 50-150 nm and a width of 5-20 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The titanium source is tetrabutyl titanate or titanium isopropoxide. Preferred Conditions: The volume ratio of titanium source to ethylene glycol is 2-8:100-200. The concentration of sodium hydroxide in the hydrothermal reaction system is 0.6-1.2 mol/L. The solid-to-liquid ratio of the dried TiO2 precursor to the total volume of water and sodium hydroxide solution is 0.3-0.7 g:40-60 mL. The reflux reaction time is 0.5-1.5 hours and the reflux temperature is 100-140degrees Celsius. The hydrothermal reaction time is 1-3 hours and the hydrothermal temperature is 160-200degrees Celsius. The acidification method includes adding the hollow Na4TiO4-based nanosheet assembly to hydrochloric acid solution, stirring, centrifuging and drying to obtain the H2Ti2O5H2O nanosheet derivative. The concentration of hydrochloric acid solution is 0.5-1.5 mol/L. The stirring reaction time is 6-18 hours. The calcination temperature is 300-600degrees Celsius and time is 1.5-4.5 hours.

GA 2021C4784A

The rock core is made of carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide. The endothermally reaction or the exothermal reaction is used to heat-preserve rock core. The reactant of the chemical reaction is composed of a solid medicine that can generate endothermic reaction or exothermic reaction when meeting water. The solid medicine is mixture of solid barium hydroxide and solid ammonium chloride, or solid medicine is calcium oxide, or solid medicine is iron powder.

The method allows making the reaction and the rock core to enter into the pressure maintaining cabin almost at the same time. The structure design of the method is smart, scientific and reasonable, and the operation is very convenient.

PN US11140912-B1
TI Producing yeast with increased levels of amino            acids and complex carbohydrates by propagating seed            yeast in propagator, providing propagation medium,            sugar source, air and nutrients to fermenter,            fermenting, and drying yeast cream.
AU ZEECK J R
AE AGVAULT LLC (AGVA-Non-standard)
GA 2021B5951K
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a yeast with increased levels of                amino acids and complex carbohydrates including                mannan oligosaccharides involves providing a seed                yeast, a sugar source, air, and nutrients to at                least one propagator; propagating the seed yeast in                the propagator creating a propagation medium;                providing at least a portion of the propagation                medium, the sugar source, the air and the nutrients                to at least one fermenter; fermenting the                propagation medium in the fermenter creating a                fermentation medium, where the fermenting produces                carbon dioxide (CO2) and a ratio of oxygen to CO2                in a top portion of the fermenter is maintained at                2-6; separating a yeast cream from the fermentation                medium; and drying the yeast cream to produce a                yeast product. The sugar source comprises 70-80                wt.% DP1 sugar, 5-15 wt.% DP2 sugar, 5-15 wt.% DP3                sugar, and 1-10 wt.% DP4 sugar.
   USE - The process is useful for producing a yeast                with increased levels of amino acids and complex                carbohydrates including mannan oligosaccharides                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process can produce a yeast strain with                increased mannan oligosaccharides, amino acids, and                complex carbohydrates. The yeast strains can                improve feeding economics, supplant antibiotics and                other medications, and improve animal health and                growth performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The propagating proceeds                  until yeast in the propagation medium comprises                  40-75% budding and 60-90% viability and the                  propagator is at least about 80% full. The air is                  filtered and compressed. The process further                  involves monitoring a sugar profile in the                  fermentation medium with high-performance liquid                  chromatography, monitoring complex carbohydrate                  and protein production, oxygen and nitrogen                  saturation, and/or yeast cell mass with                  near-infrared analysis, and monitoring yeast cell                  viability and budding count by grid smear                  examination. Fermenting proceeds until                  fermentation reaches peak growth stage.                  Fermenting proceeds until the fermenter is 75-90%                  full. The process involves multiple propagators                  and multiple fermenters. The process operates as                  a portion of an existing dry mill ethanol                  facility.Preferred Components: The sugar source                  comprises glucose, maltose, maltotriose, and                  dextrin. The nutrients comprise one or more of a                  nitrogen source, a phosphorus source, minerals,                  vitamins, and one or more of phosphoric acid,                  anhydrous ammonia, sulfuric acid, naturally                  occurring organic acids from the sugar source,                  and/or combinations. The sugar source is added to                  the propagator and fermenter at a rate of about                  1-80 gallons per minute.Preferred Device: The propagator comprises a                  stainless steel tank and is 10-30% of the                  capacity of the fermenter. The propagator                  comprises a cooling system to maintain a                  temperature inside the propagator at 82-90degrees                  Fahrenheit, and the fermenter comprises a cooling                  system to maintain a temperature inside the                  fermenter at 86-90degrees Fahrenheit. The                  propagator and the fermenter comprise at least                  one agitator and at least two baffles. The                  propagator comprises an air vent and maintains a                  pressure in the propagator at 5-15 psi, where the                  fermenter comprises an air vent and maintains a                  pressure in the fermenter at 5-10 psi. The                  fermenter comprises a stainless steel tank having                  a volume of 50000-200000 gallons. The fermenter                  is configured to adjust sugar source, air,                  nutrients, and pressure to maintain yeast                  viability and yeast budding levels.Preferred Parameters: The fermentation                  medium has a pH of 4-6. The fermentation medium                  comprises oxygen saturation levels of 50-95%                  where the fermentation medium comprises dissolved                  oxygen levels of 8-25 ppm.

PN CN113440967-A
TI Production of highly dehydrated back-suction            oxygen comprises e.g. preheating demineralized water,            passing high-purity oxygen to maintain pressure,            passing cooled and softened water into water            electrolysis system, and performing dehydration            detection of electrolytic oxygen.
AU ZHUANG J
AE ZHUANG J (ZHUA-Individual)
GA 2021B5648V
AB    NOVELTY - Production of highly dehydrated back-suction                oxygen comprises (1) preheating the demineralized                water to 95-100degrees Celsius, where the                preheating time is 10-30 minutes, passing in                high-purity oxygen to maintain the pressure, the                pressure is 0.02-0.04 MPa, and lowering the                temperature to room temperature while the pressure                is maintained, (2) passing the cooled and softened                water into a water electrolysis system for                electrolysis to produce electrolyzed oxygen, and                passing through a dehydrogenation catalytic tower                to remove hydrogen and methane, (3) pressurizing                electrolytic oxygen into the dual-temperature                molecular sieve tower to remove moisture and carbon                dioxide, performing dehydration detection of the                electrolytic oxygen, and absorbing the remaining                moisture before falling off, and (4) filtering                electrolyzed oxygen after dehydration detection                through a filter to obtain pure oxygen after                removing impurities.
   USE - The method is useful for production of highly                dehydrated back-suction oxygen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can reduce the temperature of the                oxygen by the molecular sieve before absorbing                water; improves the adsorption effect of the                molecular sieve to the water; can prompt the                technical personnel to control the flow speed of                the oxygen; ensures the efficient dehydration of                oxygen; and reduces water content of the                oxygen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (2), the                  working temperature of the dehydrogenation                  catalytic tower is at 160-200degrees Celsius. The                  temperature of the upper space of the molecular                  sieve is 120-150degrees Celsius, and the                  temperature of the lower space of the molecular                  sieve is 10-20degrees Celsius.Preferred Components: In the step (3), the                  middle part of the dual-temperature molecular                  sieve tower is filled with molecular sieve, and                  the molecular sieve is 13X APG molecular sieve.                  The magnetic suspension ball comprises a                  liquefied spherical shell and a magnetic inner                  ball. The magnetic inner ball is located at the                  inner side of the liquefied spherical shell. The                  inner end of the liquefied spherical shell is                  connected with a plurality of cold insulation                  capsule and uniformly distributed. One end of the                  cold insulation capsule close to the magnetic                  inner ball is connected with matched inner                  pushing bag. The liquefied spherical shell is                  embedded with many uniformly distributed water                  guiding fiber. One end of the inner pushing bag                  is close to the magnetic inner ball and connected                  with a magnetic separating sheet. The magnetic                  separating sheet and the magnetic inner ball are                  connected with an elastic guide wire. The                  magnetic separating sheet is made of high                  magnetic conductivity material. The magnetic                  separating sheet are combined into a whole ball                  shell. The inner guide cooling sheet is made of                  hard heat conducting material. The outer thermal                  preservation sleeve is made of hard heat                  insulation material. The outer expansion film is                  made of elastic thermal insulation material. The                  liquefied spherical shell is made of glass steel.                  The surface of the glass steel is coated with a                  nano hydrophilic coating. The cold storage                  capsule is filled with a phase change cold                  storage material. The phase change cold storage                  material is in direct contact with the liquefied                  spherical shell.

PT P
PN CN113340504-A; CN113340504-B
TI Method for obtaining residual stress distribution from fused silica hypothetical temperature distribution, involves inputting stress-strain equation as internal strain, and obtaining residual stress distribution of fused silica.
AU ZHANG C
   LIAO W
   CHEN J
   JIANG X
   FANG Z
   ZHANG L
   WANG H
   ZHU Q
AE LASER FUSION RES CENT CHINA ACAD ENG PHY (CAEP-C)
GA 2021A4417U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves realizing hypothetical temperature distribution of fused silica. A hypothetical temperature initial freezing state curve and a volume change curve are obtained when a hypothetical temperature state decreases from freezing temperature to room temperature. Volume shrinkage is calculated when the hypothetical temperature state of a position decreases from the initial freezing temperature to the room temperature. A reference virtual temperature state is selected to calculate relative volume shrinkage of the virtual temperature state of the position relative to the reference virtual temperature state when the initial freezing temperature drops to the room temperature. Distribution of relative volumetric strain is obtained. The distribution of the relative volume strain is converted into linear strain distribution to input a stress-strain equation as internal strain. Residual stress distribution of the fused silica is obtained by finite element numerical simulation.
   USE - Method for obtaining residual stress distribution from fused silica hypothetical temperature distribution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables obtaining the residual stress distribution of the fused silica after action of Carbon dioxide laser and obtaining residual stress of a silica optical fiber in a convenient and flexible manner so as to realize important application value in a fused silica processing field.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graph view representing a relationship between initial freezing temperature and room temperature of fused silica. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Method for preparing hollow expanded polyethylene particles and producing foam, involves pressurizing prepared foam beads with compressed air to generate internal pressure in cells of beads, and mixing prepared polyethylene foam beads and adhesive according to certain ratio.
AU ZHANG Z
   JI H
   WANG Y
AE NANJING FORCE NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021950294
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing the raw materials including the compound polyethylene resin, compound nucleating agent, nano-calcium carbonate and auxiliary agents. The material is sucked by the feeder into the twin-screw extruder for mixing and conveying, homogenized by the melt pump, and extruded into the mold, and pelletized by high-speed underwater pelletizing equipment to prepare sheet-shaped reinforced modified polyethylene particles. The autoclave is filled with carbon dioxide and nitrogen mixed gas to pressurize. The prepared foam beads are pressurized with compressed air to generate internal pressure in the cells of the beads. The prepared polyethylene foam beads and adhesive are mixed according to a certain ratio, added to the molds of various shapes, positioned for a certain period of time, placed on the platform shelf and solidified by natural or ventilated heating.
   USE - Method for preparing hollow expanded polyethylene particles and producing foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides environmental protection, sound absorption, lightweight and cost reduction solutions in the fields of home furnishings, building materials, industrial parts and auto parts. PN CN113072092-A
TI Preparing crystal plane coupled cuprous oxide used            as flow electrolytic cell, by mixing copper salt,            polyvinylpyrrolidone and water, and heating, and in            stirred mixed solution, adding sodium hydroxide            solution to react, and adding ascorbic acid            solution.
AU GAO M
   WU Z
   GAO F
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 202186182R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing crystal plane coupled                cuprous oxide involves (a) mixing copper salt,                polyvinylpyrrolidone and water, and heating to                50-60 degrees C to obtain a mixed solution, where                the dosage ratio of the copper salt and                polyvinylpyrrolidone is 1 mmol:0.56-2.78 g, and (b)                in the stirred mixed solution, first adding sodium                hydroxide solution to react, and adding the                ascorbic acid solution to react to obtain cuprous                oxide coupled with crystal planes.
   USE - Method used for preparing crystal plane                coupled cuprous oxide used as flow electrolytic                cell (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses fewer reaction raw materials,                realizes the adjustment of the morphology of the                cuprous oxide, adjusts the ratio between the                obtained cuprous oxide crystal faces, has uniform                size, excellent carbon dioxide electrocatalytic                performance and high Faraday efficiency and current                density of multi-carbon products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a flow                electrolytic cell comprising a cathode electrode,                an anode electrode, a diaphragm, an anolyte and a                catholyte, where the cathode electrode comprises a                carbon paper layer, a cuprous oxide layer                compounded on the carbon paper layer, a PTFE layer                compounded on the cuprous oxide layer, and the                composition of the cuprous oxide layer comprises                the crystal plane coupled cuprous oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (b) further                  involves adding the ascorbic acid solution,                  completing the reaction, cooling to 20-40 degrees                  C, centrifuging, washing and drying. The method                  for preparing the cathode electrode involves (i)                  mixing the cuprous oxide, ethanol, and Nafion                  (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based                  fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution coupled with                  the crystal planes by ultrasonic to obtain a                  dispersion, (ii) spraying the dispersion liquid                  on hydrophobic carbon paper, and after drying,                  obtaining sprayed carbon paper, and (iii) soaking                  the sprayed carbon paper in the PTFE dispersion                  for 1-3 minutes, taking out, and drying to obtain                  a cathode electrode. Preferred Conditions: The                  heating rate of the heating in step (a) is 4-6                  degrees C/minute. The temperature after the                  heating in step (a) 55 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: In step (A), the copper salt is                  chosen from copper chloride, copper sulfate                  and/or copper nitrate, the dosage ratio of the                  copper salt to water is 1 mmol:80-120 ml. The                  dosage ratio of the copper salt and                  polyvinylpyrrolidone is 1 mmol:1.11 g. In step                  (b), the concentration of the ascorbic acid                  solution is 0.4-0.8 mol/l, and the drip rate of                  the ascorbic acid solution is 1-3 ml/minute. The                  dosage ratio of the ascorbic acid solution to the                  copper salt is 5-15 ml:1 mmol. The cuprous oxide                  coupled with the crystal plane is the coupling of                  the cuprous oxide crystal plane and the cuprous                  oxide crystal plane. The anode electrode is                  nickel foam, the diaphragm is a bipolar membrane,                  the anolyte is a 1 mol/l potassium bicarbonate                  solution, and the catholyte is a 1 mol/l                  potassium bicarbonate solution.

Preferred Components: The cerium compound                  comprises cerium chloride, cerium oxide, and/or                  cerium-zirconium solid solution. Preferred                  Composition: The mass ratio of the formic acid to                  the pseudo-boehmite (in terms of alumina) is                  (0.5-0.8):1. The mass ratio of the magnesium                  oxide to the pseudo-boehmite (measured in terms                  of alumina) is (0.3-0.5):1. The mass ratio of the                  cerium compound (measured in terms of cerium                  oxide) to the pseudo-boehmite (measured in terms                  of alumina) is (0.04-0.25):1. The mass ratio of                  the ammonium metavanadate (measured in terms of                  vanadium pentoxide) to the pseudo-boehmite                  (measured in terms of alumina) is (0.03-0.07):1.                  The mass ratio of the ferric chloride (measured                  in terms of ferric oxide) to the pseudo-boehmite                  (measured in terms of alumina) is                  (0.01-0.04):1.

PN CN112940700-A
TI New nitrogen-containing corrosion inhibitor used as gas-liquid two-phase corrosion inhibitor in anti-corrosion of gas field equipment pipelines in gas field development process.
AU LI S
   YUAN X
   XIAO J
   MAO T
   CHEN W
   MO L
   TIAN Y
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2021708954
AB    NOVELTY - Nitrogen-containing corrosion inhibitor (I) is new.
   USE - Nitrogen-containing corrosion inhibitor of formula (I) used as gas-liquid two-phase corrosion inhibitor in anti-corrosion of gas field equipment pipelines in gas field development process, where corrosive environment is acidic corrosive environment formed by hydrogen sulfide (H2S) and carbon dioxide (CO2) (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen-containing corrosion inhibitor of formula (I) enables to prepare gas-liquid two-phase corrosion inhibitor, which has proper oil-water distribution performance; has good corrosion protection effect (corrosion inhibition rate is greater than or equal to 90%), and provides stable corrosion inhibition performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Nitrogen-containing corrosion inhibitor of formula (I) is new..
   R=1-20C alkyl, alkenyl, aryl, ring alkyl;
   x=integer from 0-20;
   y=integer from 0-20; and
   z=integer from 0-20.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing nitrogen-containing corrosion inhibitor of formula (I);
   (2) a gas-liquid two-phase corrosion inhibitor, which contains 10-30 wt.% nitrogen-containing corrosion inhibitor of formula (I), and 70-90% wt.% compound; and
   (3) a method for preparing gas-liquid two-phase corrosion inhibitor, which involves heating main nitrogen-containing corrosion inhibitor of formula (I) to 50-80 degrees C, and then adding compound to main nitrogen-containing corrosion inhibitor for compounding gas-liquid two-phase corrosion inhibitor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The method for preparing nitrogen-containing corrosion inhibitor of formula (I) involves mixing organic acid, organic polyamine and thiourea and reacting at 140-175 degrees C; and obtaining nitrogen-containing corrosion inhibitor after reaction is completed. Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of organic acid, organic polyamine and thiourea is 0.8-1.2:1-1.2:0.5-1, preferably 0.8-1:1-1.2:0.5-1. Preferred Components: The organic acid is alkyl acetic acid (R-COOH), where R is selected from 1-20C alkyl, alkenyl, aryl, and cycloalkyl; organic polyamine is NH2((CH2)XNH)Y(CH2)ZNH2, where X is integer of 0-20, Y is integer of 0-20, and Z is integer of 0-20. The reaction time is controlled by amount of water produced in reaction process, and reaction is determined to be completed when reaction water is completed. The surfactant includes one or two of SP-80 and isopropanol. The solvent includes washing oil. The temperature of main nitrogen-containing corrosion inhibitor is raised to 50-80 degrees C, and then surfactant and solvent in compound are sequentially added to main nitrogen-containing corrosion inhibitor for compounding. Preferred Composition: The gas-liquid two-phase corrosion inhibitor comprises compound which contains 1-5 wt.% surfactant and 95-99 wt.% solvent; 10-30 wt.% nitrogen-containing corrosion inhibitor of formula (I), 1-5 wt.% surface active agent and 65-89 wt.% washing oil.
DC E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); M14 (Other chemical surface treatments - including etching, brightening, forming non-metallic layers, passivation, cathodic protection and corrosion inhibitors, but not processes specifically for semiconductor production (C23F, C25). This Section also covers multistage processes.)

PN CN112915777-A
TI Blast furnace gas dechlorination and            desulfurization purification process comprises removing            dust from blast furnace gas, spraying deacidification            powder adsorbent and reacting hydrogen chloride, cyanic            acid and activity components in gas.
AU ZHANG X
   ZHENG F
   WANG Z
   WANG Y
   JIN J
   CHEN K
   ZHOU Z
   QU J
   WANG G
AE WUHAN KELIN CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 202165776Y
AB    NOVELTY - Blast furnace gas dechlorination and                desulfurization purification process comprises (a)                removing dust from blast furnace gas, and spraying                deacidification powder adsorbent into the pipe,                reacting the hydrogen chloride, cyanic acid and                activity components in the gas and removing, and                collecting the deacidified saturated powder                adsorbent; (b) passing blast furnace gas into                deoxygenation reactor, under the action of the                deoxygenation catalyst, reacting the oxygen in the                gas with hydrogen or carbon monoxide to form water                or carbon dioxide to remove oxygen; (c) passing                deoxygenated blast furnace gas into waste pressure                turbine power plant to recover the waste pressure                and waste heat for power generation, under the                organic sulfur hydrolysis catalyst, combining the                carbonyl sulfide and carbon disulfide in the blast                furnace gas with water vapor to undergo a                hydrolysis reaction and converting into                easy-to-remove hydrogen sulfide; and (d)                desulfurizing.
   USE - The method is useful for blast furnace gas                dechlorination and desulfurization                purification.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not affect the dry dust                removing, recovers residual pressure residual heat                to generate electricity, can effectively remove                chloride, cyanide and sulfide in blast furnace gas,                has high removing precision, simple flow, small                pressure drop, simple device, low investment, low                operation cost, and small floor area.

PN WO2021102451-A1
TI Method of separating essential oil from medicinal herb used for separating cannabidiol from plant material, involves preparing mini oven for subsequent use by removing residue of medicinal herb which is mostly biochar.
AU MCNEIL M
AE MCNEIL M (MCNE-Individual)
GA 202159350N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves opening a mini oven (30). A personal amount of the medicinal herb is positioned in the mini oven. The mini oven is closed. The medicinal herb in the mini oven is pyrolysized which includes displacing oxygen from the mini oven with carbon dioxide responsive to decarboxylation of the medicinal herb. The gasified essential oil out of the mini oven is moved through an outlet orifice (35). The gasified essential oil is condensed into an aerosol cloud (67). The aerosol cloud of condensed essential oil is moved to a mouthpiece. The mini oven is prepared for a subsequent use by removing residue of the medicinal herb which is mostly biochar from the mini oven.
   USE - Method of separating essential oil from medicinal herb used for separating cannabidiol from plant material.
   ADVANTAGE - The medicinal herb is pyrolysized in the mini oven by generating heat with heating element while starving the pyrolysizing cooking space of oxygen accomplished by limiting or eliminating flow of air into pyrolysizing cooking space.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a personal essential oil extractor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a partial sectioned front view of a personal essential oil extractor.
   Mini oven (10)
   Outlet orifice (35)
   Inside space (46)
   Aerosol cloud (67)
   User (90)

PT P
PN WO2021099679-A1; FI201905993-A; FI129441-B1; IN202247033991-A; KR2022098007-A; CN114651098-A; EP4061997-A1; BR112022006300-A2
TI Controlling discharges of nitrogen compounds in            production of cellulose carbamate, comprises e.g.            producing dissolving pulp and/or kraft pulp from            cellulosic fibrous material and activating it to            decrease degree of depolymerization of pulp.
AU RAMARK H
   VALTA K
   VANHATALO K
   RARNARK H
   BALTA Q
   HANNU M
AE ANDRITZ OY (ANDZ-C)
   ANDRITZ OY (ANDZ-C)
GA 202157401D
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling discharges of nitrogen compounds                in the production of cellulose carbamate (CCA) (2),                comprises producing dissolving pulp and/or kraft                pulp (4) from cellulosic fibrous material at a                chemical pulp mill and activating it to decrease a                degree of depolymerization of the pulp, and                reacting the activated dissolving pulp or activated                kraft pulp with urea (3) to produce CCA, thus                releasing ammonia, where the CCA production is                integrated into the pulp mill having a flue gas                system so that carbon dioxide from the flue gases                are reacted with released ammonia to produce urea,                which is used in the carbamate production.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling                discharges of nitrogen compounds in the production                of cellulose carbamate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the: ammonia or                ammonia-containing wastewater from the carbamate                production that can be utilized to neutralize                prehydrolysate from dissolving pulp production                and/or acidic wastewater from the pulp mill; and                nitrogen-containing wastewater from the CCA                production that can be led to an evaporation plant                and concentrated to produce dry nitrogen                fertilizers, or can be conveyed to a wastewater                treatment plant of the pulp mill to be used as                nutrient.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of                cellulose carbamate production.
   Cellulose carbamate (2)
   Urea (3)
   Dissolving pulp and/or kraft pulp (4)

GA 2021288235
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an air conditioner circulating phase change refrigerating system and air conditioner, the air conditioner circulating phase change refrigerating system, comprising: a pressure air source; at least two storage tanks arranged in parallel; the storage tank is respectively connected with the pressure air source; the pressure in the storage tank is controlled by alternately introducing high pressure gas; an air conditioner heat exchanger; the air conditioner heat exchanger is respectively connected with the storage tank, for alternately receiving the liquid cooling medium provided by the storage tank for heat exchange refrigeration; wherein the storage tank alternately receives the gaseous cooling medium after heat exchange of the air conditioner heat exchanger; and it is converted into liquid cooling medium for recycling under the pressurizing and heat exchange effect of the high pressure gas.
