PT P
PN CN112410288-A
TI Extracting human secretory gland stem cells in vitro with hair follicle regeneration function by e.g. rinsing human exocrine gland tissue, cutting, digesting exocrine gland tissue with type I collagenase, culturing cells, and passing.
AU XIONG Z
   WANG H
   LIU W
   WANG C
AE ANHUI BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021271545
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting human secretory gland stem cells in vitro with hair follicle regeneration function comprises (1) rinsing human exocrine gland tissue, and cutting into pieces; (2) digesting the exocrine gland tissue with type I collagenase from the shredded tissue; (3) collecting the digested cells; (4) culturing the cells and passing them 3-5 times; (5) adding the cultured cells to phosphate buffered saline, homogenizing, and adding cell suspension to the stem cell separation liquid at a ratio of 2:1; (6) performing density gradient centrifugation at 1500 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 15 minutes in a centrifuge; (7) carefully absorbing the middle cell layer rich in the nucleated cell; (8) adding mononuclear cells into high-sugar Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium containing 10% calf serum, inoculating in the culture bottle, continuously culturing in an incubator at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide condition, and (9) using fibronectin solution with a concentration of 10 mu g/ml to screen stem cells.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting human secretory gland stem cells in vitro with hair follicle regeneration function.
   ADVANTAGE - The method extracts a large amount of cells, which has high survival rate, easier to adhere to the wall in the culture flask, and good cell activity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The separation liquid includes 3-8 pts. wt. diatrizoate meglumine, 3-10 pts. wt. hydroxymethyl cellulose, 0.01-1 pts. wt. cesium chloride, 0.01-10 pts. wt. polysucrose, 0.01-8 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol, mixing the above ingredients and adding 0.01 mol/l phosphate buffer to prepare the concentrated solution of the stem cell separation solution; diluting the concentrated solution with 0.01 mol/l phosphate buffer to the required specific gravity, the specific gravity range is 1.090-1.120 g/ml. The human exocrine gland tissue is repeatedly washed by sterile phosphate buffered saline until there is no blood in the human external secretory gland tissue. The screening method involves using fibronectin phosphate buffered saline solution to cover the bottom of the container, placing at 4 degrees C, absorbing the liquid after 12 hours; using human mesenchymal stem cell complete culture medium to clean the container; absorbing the culture medium to obtain the single cell suspension prepared by the 5th generation cell with fusion rate of 80 %; placing the single cell suspension into the container of fibronectin, keeping constant temperature at 37 degrees C; absorbing the liquid after 30 minutes; then adding human mesenchymal stem cell complete culture medium; the cell attached to the bottom of the container paved with fibronectin is human in vitro secretory gland stem cell. The human secretory gland stem cell in vitro is stored in liquid nitrogen at -196 degrees C. The culture is to culture the human mesenchymal stem cells in the complete medium of human mesenchymal stem cells at 37 degrees C under the condition of carbon dioxide concentration of 5%, after the cell fusion reaches 70-80%, and passaging five times. The method for each passage of the passage is: (1) cell washing: taking out the human secretory gland stem cells to be passaged in a sterile environment, and rinsing them with buffer for 2-4 times; (2) cells segmentation: dividing human secretory gland stem cells into multiple small pieces; (3) inoculation: adding complete human mesenchymal stem cell medium to terminate the digestion, centrifuging for 5 minutes, and discarding the supernatant; and (4) cultivation: immediately sealing with sterile cellophane tape, placing in an incubator with a temperature of 36-38 degrees C, and a humidity of 60-80% to continue incubating.
DC A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC A03-A04A; A05-H03; A12-W11L; B04-B04D4; B04-C02; B04-C03C; B04-F02B; B04-H20A; B04-L05C1; B05-A01B; B05-B02A3; B05-C04; B05-C07; B10-A07D; B10-C04C; B11-B03A; C04-B04D4; C04-C02; C04-C03C; C04-F02B; C04-H20A; C04-L05C1; C05-A01B; C05-B02A3; C05-C04; C05-C07; C10-A07D; C10-C04C; C11-B03A; D05-A02C; D05-H10; D05-H13

PN RU2740462-C1
TI METHOD OF PREVENTING FORMATION OF ASPHALTENE, RESINOUS PARAFFIN DEPOSITS (ARPD) IN LIFT PIPES DURING GAS-LIFT OPERATION OF OIL WELLS.
AU NGUEN V T
   ROGACHEV M K
   ALEKSANDROV A N
   KHASANOV A A
AE UNIV ST PETERSBURG MINING (LEMI-C)
GA 2021125725
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to oil industry, in particular to methods of preventing deposit formation on oil-producing equipment of paraffin and other components of hydrocarbon material, for example, asphaltene-resin-paraffin compositions during its production and transportation. Proposed method comprises pumping working fluid into annular space of the well. Initial data are obtained on component compositions of formation fluid and associated petroleum gas, which is used as a working agent, then it is purified from hydrogen sulphide and carbon dioxide, then, purified associated petroleum gas is pumped without changing its hydrocarbon composition and temperature of oil saturation with paraffin is determined and its formation depth in the well taking into account the oil component composition change during pumping of associated petroleum gas, further, the ratio of light and heavy fractions of injected associated petroleum gas is changed and the temperature of oil saturation with paraffin and the depth of its formation for a given ratio of light and heavy fractions are determined. Then the factors are compared and the optimal version of the required amount of working agent consumption is selected at the lowest temperature of oil saturation with paraffin.
   USE - Oil and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Improving operating efficiency of gas lift wells complicated by asphaltene-resin-paraffin deposits, increasing overhaul period of their operation.
1 cl, 2 tbl, 4 dwg
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

PN KR2020145521-A
TI Ion separation method using kelvin water dropper, involves making metal tank in cylindrical model to confine water separated by ions and separated sources, insulated and sealed with structure tap in form of tank.
AU MOON Y
AE MOON Y (MOON-Individual)
GA 202102279T

A plastic bowl and faucet (4) is composed of a needle type to maximize instability when dropping water from the water base to the electrode ring. The metal tank is made in a cylindrical model to confine water separated by ions and separated sources, insulated and sealed with the structure tap in the form of a tank so that only H+ ions separated from ions. A hydrogen tank (7) consists of a structure connected by structure and line so that only hydrogen gas is collected. A hose (9) is composed of a typical hose made of rubber polymer material to connect the electric wire composed of electric wire and hydrogen tank.

Preferred Components: The base material in the step (a) is 45 steel of 100 mmx 50 mmx 10 mm, which is sanded with sandpaper before pretreatment and cleaning with acetone. The carboxylic acid molecule is an acetonitrile solution of perfluorooctanoic acid at a concentration of 10 mmol/l. The contact angle between the glass phase high temperature slag and the surface of the nickel-based cladding layer is 120-150 degrees .

PN CN111439759-A
TI Producing food-grade potassium sulfite by adding potassium hydroxide to potassium metabisulfite mother liquor, adding heavy metal impurity remover to solution, precipitating, aging, adding potassium hydroxide to filtrate, and spray-drying.
AU XU D
   SUN J
   MA Z
   HE G
AE ZIBO CHANGCHENG CHEM FACTORY (ZIBO-Non-standard)
GA 202072457U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing food-grade potassium sulfite, involves (a) adding potassium hydroxide to potassium metabisulfite mother liquor under stirring, and adjusting pH of the solution to 4-8, (b) adding heavy metal impurity remover to the solution, and stirring at 60-90 degrees C for 20-40 minutes to generate small particles of heavy metal groups, (c) continuously adding additive agent to the solution under agitation to adsorb and coagulate the small particles of the heavy metal groups into large particles to precipitate, leaving still in a heat-preserving state to precipitate, and aging, (d) filtering the solution to obtain a filtrate, adding potassium hydroxide to the filtrate to make the potassium ions content in the solution to reach 200-500 g/l, reacting with sulfur dioxide to a pH value of 8-10 to obtain potassium sulfite solution, and (e) spray-drying the solution under the protection of inert gas to obtain a food-grade potassium sulfite product.
   USE - The method is useful for producing food-grade potassium sulfite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not require heating to purify carbon dioxide, does not require heating and evaporation at the later stage of production, and reduces the production energy consumption and production cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The heavy metal impurity remover comprises inorganic heavy metal impurity remover comprising potassium sulfide, sodium sulfide, ammonium sulfide or hydrogen sulfide, where the addition amount of the inorganic heavy metal impurity remover is 0.003-0.008 %mass or organic heavy metal impurity remover comprising thioacetamide, dithiocarbamate or trisulfonate, where the addition amount of the organic heavy metal impurity remover is 0.001-0.005 %mass. The additive agent comprises calcium sulfate, calcium sulfite, diatomaceous earth, activated clay, activated carbon, polyacrylamide and/or sodium polyacrylate, where the addition amount of calcium sulfate, calcium sulfite, diatomaceous earth, activated clay and activated carbon is 0.02-0.05 %mass, and the addition amount of polyacrylamide and sodium polyacrylate is 0.0001-0.0005 %mass. The inert gas is nitrogen, where the atmosphere condition comprises 90-95 vol.% nitrogen content in step (e). Preferred Method: The step (c) involves adding the additive agent at 60-80 degrees C, stirring for 15 minutes, and heat-preserving at 60-80 degrees C for 45-60 minutes. The step (d) involves reacting at 60-90 degrees C. The step (e) involves spray-drying at an inlet temperature of 200-280 degrees C, and outlet temperature of 105-125 degrees C under the protection of inert gas. The spray-drying is carried out by (i) heating nitrogen, conveying the heated nitrogen into a spray-dryer, where the air inlet volume is 2100-2300 m3/hour, and the air output volume is 2800-3200 m3/hour, and (ii) turning on a centrifugal nozzle and feed pump to control the feed rate of potassium sulfite solution to 160-200 kg/hour, when the inlet air temperature reaches 200-280 degrees C, and the outlet temperature reaches 105-125 degrees C, and feeding the material into the spray-dryer for drying.

AE BAYER AG (FARB-C)
GA 202065692D
AB    NOVELTY - Active compound combination comprises: (a) 2-(6-(4-bromophenoxy)-2-(trifluoromethyl)-3-pyridyl)-1-(1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)propan-2-ol; (b) spiroxamine; and (c) at least one further active compound comprising (i) inhibitors of the ergosterol synthesis, and (ii) inhibitors of the respiratory chain at complex I or II, where the further active compound (c) is different from compound (a) and compound (b).
   USE - The combination is useful for: controlling harmful microorganisms in crop protection and in the protection of materials by applying to the harmful microorganisms and/or their habitat; treating a transgenic plant, and seed; and coating the seed (all claimed), where the harmful microorganism are fungi and bacteria. Test details are described but no results given.
   ADVANTAGE - The combination: provides the plants with nutrient use efficiency, especially nitrogen (N)-use efficiency, phosphorus-use efficiency, water use efficiency, improved transpiration, respiration and/or carbon dioxide assimilation rate, better nodulation, improved calcium-metabolism; and prevents adverse effects including rotting, decay, discoloration, decoloration or formation of mould.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) composition comprising a content of an active compound combination, in addition to at least one carrier and/or surfactant; and
   (2) seed coated with the active compound combination, or the composition.

The compound (c) is prothioconazole, tebuconazole, bixafen, fluopyram, inpyrfluxam, and isoflucypram. The compound combination is: 2-(6-(4-bromophenoxy)-2-(trifluoromethyl)-3-pyridyl)-1-(1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)propan-2-ol, spiroxamine and prothioconazole; 2-(6-(4-bromophenoxy)-2-(trifluoromethyl)-3-pyridyl)-1-(1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)propan-2-ol, spiroxamine and tebuconazole; 2-(6-(4-bromophenoxy)-2-(trifluoromethyl)-3-pyridyl)-1-(1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)propan-2-ol, spiroxamine and bixafen; 2-(6-(4-bromophenoxy)-2-(trifluoromethyl)-3-pyridyl)-1-(1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)propan-2-ol, spiroxamine and fluopyram; 2-(6-(4-bromophenoxy)-2-(trifluoromethyl)-3-pyridyl)-1-(1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)propan-2-ol, spiroxamine and 3-(difluoromethyl)-1-methyl-N-(1,1,3-trimethyl-2,3-dihydro-1H-inden-4-yl)-1H-pyrazole-4-carboxamide; or 2-(6-(4-bromophenoxy)-2-(trifluoromethyl)-3-pyridyl)-1-(1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)propan-2-ol, spiroxamine and N-(5-chloro-2-isopropylbenzyl)-N-cyclopropyl-3-(difluoromethyl)-5-fluoro-1-methyl-1H-pyrazole-4-carboxamide. The weight ratio compound (a) to compound (b) is 100:1-1:100. The weight ratio compound (a) to compound(s) (b) is 100:1-1:100. The combination comprises exactly 1 compound (c). The combination comprises a further active compound comprising insecticides and plant growth regulators.
   ACTIVITY - Plant Protectant; Fungicide; Antibacterial.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Application of the combination is 0.001-15 (preferably 0.01-5) g/kg of seed, or 0.1-10000 (preferably 50-300) g/ha by watering or dripping.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN111390349-A
TI Gas electric welding inclined plate comprises e.g. groove processing front surface, adjusting position of welding gun, providing gas pipe and water-cooled slider, and starting control equipment to weld inclined plate during welding process.
AU LI Y
   LIU Y
   PAN Q
   XI B
   XIE L
   XU R
   ZHU G
   GU Z
   WANG Y
AE SHANGHAI WAIGAOQIAO SHIPBUILDING CO LTD (CHSB-C)
GA 202066779M
AB    NOVELTY - Gas electric welding inclined plate comprises (i) groove processing the front surface of the inclined plate to be welded, setting a gasket on the reverse side of the inclined plate, and forming a joint between the two inclined plates; (ii) adjusting the position of the welding gun so that the nozzle of the welding gun is aligned with the center line of the pair of seams and the angle between the welding wire and the inclined plate in the welding gun is 65-85 degrees ; (iii) providing a gas pipe and a water-cooled slider, where the gas pipe conveys carbon dioxide gas is connected to the welding place, the water-cooled slider is connected with a water-cooled pipe, and the water-cooled slider moves with the welding torch; and (iv) starting the control equipment to weld the inclined plate during the welding process, and the welding gun swings back and forth in the groove.
   USE - The method is useful for gas electric welding inclined plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method stabilizes the joint quality, realizes automatic welding, improves the production efficiency and production quality, and reduces labor intensity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for gas electric welding inclined plate (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN DE102018222537-A1; WO2020127402-A1; CN113196345-A; KR2021099650-A; EP3899879-A1; US2022068054-A1
TI Method for typing motor vehicles, involves            determining characteristic value assigned to individual            vehicle and typifying individual vehicle depending on            size of characteristic value by assignment to profiles            of classification.
AU ZEREY M
   KIRST C
   JOCHEM S
AE ZF FRIEDRICHSHAFEN AG (ZAHF-C)
   ZF FRIEDRICHSHAFEN AG (ZAHF-C)
   ZF FRIEDRICHSHAFEN AG (ZAHF-C)
GA 202056589K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating the average                values as well as the size and frequency of                deviations from the average values using the                recorded transmission data. A classification (10)                has a standard profile (10.3) and a further profile                (10.1) to the standard profile is created for the                average values on the basis of the average values                and the size and frequency of the deviations. The                transmission data is recorded by a data acquisition                device (6) over a specific operating interval and a                specific mileage of the individual vehicle (50).                The characteristic value assigned to the individual                vehicle from the transmission data recorded in the                individual vehicle is determined. The individual                vehicle is typified depending on the size of the                characteristic value by assignment to one of the                profiles of the classification.
   USE - Method for typing motor vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - The recorded transmission data can be used by                the manufacturer to optimize the transmission for                its further production. Efficiency-oriented                transmission parameterization can achieve low                emissions of carbon dioxide and nitrogen                oxides.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for typing motor vehicles.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a system diagram of an                inventive method for typing motor vehicles.
   Data acquisition device (6)
   Classification (10)
   Further profile (10.1)
   Standard profile (10.3)
   Individual vehicle (50)

PN CN111204771-A
TI Preparing high-purity spherical nano-silica with            onion structure involves mixing high-purity silica and            high-purity carbon uniformly in heating reactor, and            carrying out carbothermal reduction reaction.
AU WEI K
   MA W
   WANG J
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202049446F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity spherical nano-silica                with onion structure involves mixing high-purity                silica and high-purity carbon uniformly in a                heating reactor, and carrying out a carbothermal                reduction reaction at a temperature of 1500-2200                degrees C to obtain a mixed gas of silicon dioxide                and carbon di-oxide and bottom silicon melt. The                gas trapping device is used to introduce silicon                dioxide and carbon di-oxide mixed gas into                condensing pipe, and passed excess oxygen into                condensing pipe to react with silicon dioxide and                carbon di-oxide mixed gas at a uniform speed to                generate high-purity spherical nano-silica core and                carbon-dioxide. The temperature gradient of                condensing pipeline is controlled, and adjusted                number and particle size of high purity spherical                nano-silica core outer layer silica growth to                obtain onion structure of high purity spherical                nano-silica, and then performed gas-solid                separation of high-purity spherical nano-silica and                carbon di-oxide gas with onion structure.
   USE - Method for preparing high-purity spherical                nano-silica with onion structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare high-purity                spherical nano-silica with onion structure which                has short process flow, simple operation, high                economic benefit, energy saving and environmental                protection, and also provides a reliable technical                approach for the large-scale and                cost-effective.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of                  high-purity silica and high-purity carbon is                  (1-2):1. The molar amount of oxygen introduced is                  1.05-1.15 times the molar amount required for the                  reaction of oxygen with silicon dioxide and                  carbon mono-oxide. The temperature gradient of                  the condensing pipeline is 10-100 degrees C per                  meter.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC E11-W; E12-A13; E31-N05C; E31-P01

PN RU2720632-C1
TI Development method of super viscous oil deposit includes successive pumping of steam into formation with creation of steam chamber.
AU BEREGOVOI A N
   RAKHIMOVA SH G
   KNYAZEVA N A
   RAZUMOV A R
   AMERKHANOV M I
AE TATNEFT SHASHIN PUBLIC STOCK CO (TATN-C)
GA 202049945U
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the oil-producing industry. Method of super-viscous oil deposit development includes successive pumping of steam into formation with creation of steam chamber, fringe of 5-30% aqueous solution of carbamide, process pause of 2-5 hours duration, pumping of hydrocarbon solvent rim and extraction of product. Volume of fringe of water solution of carbamide is calculated by formula Vsol=(0.1 divide by 0.4).Vo/VCO2fc , where (0.1 divide by 0.4) is coefficient, which is selected based on well injection capacity; Vo is volume of produced oil, m3; VCO2fc is volume of carbon dioxide released in formation conditions, m3. Volume of rim of injected liquid hydrocarbon solvent is calculated by formula VS(l)=(0.1 divide by 0.4).0.53.Vo/Caf, where (0.1 divide by 0.4) is coefficient, which is selected based on well injection capacity; 0.53 is coefficient characterizing the ratio of the volumes of liquid and vaporized solvent; Vo is the amount of oil produced, m3; Caf is content of aromatic fraction in solvent, %.
   USE - Oil, gas and coke-chemical industries.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result is high efficiency of steam-thermal action during development of super-viscous oil deposit, elimination of irrational flow of heat carrier, carbamide solution and hydrocarbon solvent, reduced corrosion of oil equipment, reduced material costs.
1 cl, 2 tbl, 1 ex

   LI H
   LI X
   PENG M
AE LY STEEL CO LTD (LYST-Non-standard)
GA 202038770M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining blast furnace                gas with certain volume. The blast furnace gas is                sequentially introduced in first reaction solution                and second reaction solution, where the first                reaction solution is water soluble zinc compound                and the second reaction solution is silver nitrate                solution. The blast furnace gas is fully reacted                with the first reaction solution for performing                filtering process to obtain filter liquid. Chlorine                ion mass filter liquid is detected in potential                titration. Chlorine content in the blast furnace                gas is calculated, where the chlorine content is                concentration of chloride ion in the blast furnace                gas per unit volume. A frame container is connected                with a potential titrimeter that is fixed on a                silver electrode.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bionic rice leaf lattice anisotropic                superhydrophobic surface material comprises (i)                cutting polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) polymer                material to the required size according to the                design requirements, placing in container                containing toluene, absolute ethanol and deionized                water, washing with an ultrasonic washer for 5 -15                minutes, removing various organic and inorganic                pollution impurities on the surface of the material                and drying in constant temperature blast oven at                40-90 degrees C; (ii) drawing dot matrix                anisotropic graphics using marking mate drawing                software of high-speed laser marking machine and                drawing bionic rice leaf anisotropic lattice                structure suitable for processing the surface of                the substrate; and (iii) processing anisotropic                microstructure surfaces using a carbon dioxide gas                laser, turning on the laser engraving machine,                placing the PTFE polymer material substrate                processed on the sample stage of the laser                equipment and controlling laser to engrave.

PN CN110846101-A; CN110846101-B
TI Anti-foam methanol automobile engine lubricating oil, prepared by e.g. reacting low-molecular weight polymer of perfluoro n-octyl acrylate compound-containing palladium ions and methanol fuel engine lubricating oil.
AU LIU H
   WANG L
   WANG H
AE LIAONING SANTE PETROCHEMICAL CO LTD (LIAO-Non-standard)
GA 2020203245
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-foam methanol automobile engine lubricating oil, is claimed. The anti-foam methanol automobile engine lubricating oil is obtained by reacting low-molecular weight polymer of perfluoro n-octyl acrylate compound (I)-containing palladium ions and methanol fuel engine lubricating oil produced according to the corporate standard Q/LSY0052018 of Liaoning Sante Petrochemical Co., Ltd..
   USE - Used as anti-foam methanol automobile engine lubricating oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The lubricating oil can quickly convert formic acid and formaldehyde diffused to the secondary surface of methanol fuel engine into carbon dioxide and water, and quickly discharge them out of the lubricant layer of the engine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Anti-foam methanol automobile engine lubricating oil, is claimed. The anti-foam methanol automobile engine lubricating oil is obtained by reacting low-molecular weight polymer of perfluoro n-octyl acrylate compound of formula (I)-containing palladium ions and methanol fuel engine lubricating oil produced according to the corporate standard Q/LSY0052018 of Liaoning Sante Petrochemical Co., Ltd..
   n=degree of polymerization (2-5);
   Z=perfluoro n-octyl; and
   E=reaction product after initiation reaction of peroxy or azo initiator including after initiation reaction of p-methylbenzoyl peroxide, azobisisobutyronitrile or tert-butyl hydroperoxide.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises taking perfluoro n-octanol as raw material A, taking palladium(II) nitrate catalyst as raw material B, adding both into a colloid mill to prepare colloidal solution, adding acrylic acid as raw material C, controlling the reaction temperature under vacuum and stirring conditions, reacting by stirring, discharging the generated water as by-product to obtain perfluorooctyl acrylate containing palladium ions as product D, controlling the reaction temperature under vacuum conditions, adding initiator into product D under stirring conditions to cause a polymerization reaction to obtain polymerized product P, taking product P and synthesizing product P with the methanol fuel engine lubricating oil produced according to the corporate standard Q/LSY0052018 of Liaoning Sante Petrochemical Co., Ltd., controlling the reaction temperature under vacuum conditions, and stirring for a period of time to obtain final product. Preferred Components: The controlled molar ratio between raw material A and raw material B is 1000:1-100000:1, and the controlled molar ratio between raw material A and raw material C is 2:1-1:2. The calandria cooler and by-product water receiver is further provided with a vacuum balance pipe (13). The vacuum balancing pipe is connected with tubular cooler and by-product water receiver. Preferred Method: The reaction temperature of colloidal solution and raw material C is 20-100 degrees C, and the reaction pressure is 133 Pa-760x133 Pa (sic), and the reaction time is greater than or equal to 2 hours. In the method, the product D is added with initiator at reaction temperature of 40-130 degrees C, the controlled molar ratio between product D and initiator is 100:1-10000:1, and the polymerization time is greater than or equal to 2 hours. The mass ratio between product P and methanol fuel engine lubricating oil produced according to the enterprise standard Q/LSY005-2018 of Liaoning Sante Petrochemical Co., Ltd. is 1:1000-1:100000, and the synthesis reaction time is greater than or equal to 1 hour. The method comprises adding colloidal solution into a reactor 1 to react with raw material C. The reactor 1 is tank reactor. The reactor 1 comprises a kettle reactor main body (1), calandria cooler (2) and by-product water receiver (3). The kettle reactor main body is provided with a feeding port (4) and a discharging port (5). The kettle reactor main body is inserted with a stirrer (6). The stirrer (6) is connected with a motor (7). The outside of the kettle reactor main body is provided with a jacket (8). The jacket (8) is provided with a heat carrier inlet (9) and heat carrier outlet (10). The inside of the kettle reactor main body connected with calandria cooler through pipeline. The by-product water receiver is connected to the lower end of the tubular cooler (2). The calandria cooler is provided with a cooling water outlet (11) and the cooling water inlet (12). The upper end of the tubular cooler is connected with the vacuum system. In the method, the product D is polymerized in the reactor 2, and the product P and the methanol fuel engine lubricating oil are produced according to the corporate standard Q/LSY0052018 of Liaoning Sante Petrochemical Co., Ltd. in the reactor 2 by synthesis reaction. The reactor 2 is a kettle reactor. The reactor 2 comprises kettle reactor main body (1) is provided with a feeding port (4) and a discharging port (5). The kettle reactor main body (1) is inserted with a stirrer (6). The stirrer (6) is connected with a motor (7). The outside of the kettle reactor main body is provided with a jacket (8). The jacket (8) is provided with a heat carrier inlet (9) and heat carrier outlet (10). The kettle reactor main body inner wall is distributed with a cooling pipe (14). The cooling pipe (14) of the cooling water inlet (15) and cooling water outlet (16) out from the side wall of the kettle reactor main body. The kettle reactor main body is further connected with a vacuum system by a pipeline.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); H07 (Lubricants and lubrication - this excludes self-lubricating surfaces e.g. PTFE coated surfaces and lubrication systems in general. PN WO2020041535-A1; KR2021037727-A; CN112601599-A; EP3840862-A1; US2021310392-A1; JP2021535552-W; US11326497-B2
TI Energy recovery converter for purifying exhaust gas from mechanical engine, comprises membrane electrode assembly that has first and second electrode, and oxidation catalyst that is used to promote electrochemical reaction.
AU JOHNSON L G
AE JOHNSON IP HOLDING LLC (JOHN-Non-standard)
   JOHNSON IP HOLDING LLC (JOHN-Non-standard)
   JOHNSON IP HOLDING LLC (JOHN-Non-standard)
GA 202017560C
AB    NOVELTY - The energy recovery converter (20) comprises a membrane electrode assembly that has a first electrode (14) and a second electrode (16). An oxygen ion conductive membrane (18) is sandwiched between the first and second electrodes. The first and second electrodes includes an oxidation catalyst that is used to promote an electrochemical reaction. A second molecular oxygen content is being higher than a first molecular oxygen content. An oxidation catalyst is a platinum or palladium catalyst. A reduction catalyst is selected from the group of rhodium and platinum. The exhaust gas includes hydrocarbons, nitrogen oxides and water in a first chamber (12). The hydrocarbons and water in the exhaust gas are converted to water, carbon dioxide, nitrogen and carbon monoxide by the rhodium catalyst.
   USE - Energy recovery converter used for purifying exhaust gas from a mechanical engine.
   ADVANTAGE - A catalytic converter provides an improved device and method to oxidize and reduce toxic gasses in exhaust gas of a mechanical engine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for purifying exhaust gas from a mechanical engine by using a energy recovery converter.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the energy recovery converter.
   First chamber (12)
   First electrode (14)
   Second electrode (16)
   Ion conductive membrane (18)
   Energy recovery converter (20)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC E10-J02D; E11-N; E11-Q02A; E31-H02; H06-C03A; J01-E02D; J04-E04A; J04-E09A; N07-G; X16-F02

PT P
PN WO2020039031-A1; EP3841186-A1
TI Production of hydrocarbons used as green diesel, involves hydrogenating and deoxygenating feedstock containing unsaturated fatty acids and its derivatives in presence of hydrogen-donor and metal-free heteroatom-doped carbon material.
AU MIKKOLA J
   VANKLINT K
   SILJEBO W
   KONWAR L J
   SAMIKANNU A
AE ECO-OIL MILJOEBRAENSLEN I SVERIGE AB (ECOO-Non-standard)
   ECO-OIL MILJOEBRAENSLEN I SVERIGE AB (ECOO-Non-standard)
GA 202017596X
AB    NOVELTY - Production of 6-20C hydrocarbons involves providing a feedstock (F1) comprising saturated or unsaturated fatty acids and its derivatives, and hydrogenating and deoxygenating the feedstock in the presence of hydrogen-donor and a metal-free hydrogenation-decarboxylation catalyst under low-pressure hydrothermal conditions at 350-400 degrees C, and pressure of 10-30 bar. The hydrogenation-decarboxylation catalyst is a metal-free heteroatom-doped carbon material.
   USE - Production of 6-20C hydrocarbons used as green diesel for diesel engines.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables direct production of sulfur-free liquid hydrocarbons from a single or mixed feed without additional hydrocracking/isomerization steps. The hydrocarbon produced has zero net emission of carbon dioxide. Since a metal-free catalyst is used, the production cost is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) system for production of the hydrocarbons, which consists of a single reactor (R) comprising a bed of the metal-free catalyst comprising heteroatom-doped carbon (oxygen-doped active carbon and nitrogen doped active carbon); and
   (2) use of heteroatom-doped carbon for hydrogenating and/or decarboxylating the fatty acid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a plant comprising the system.
   Back pressure regulator (BPR)
   Feedstocks (F1,F2)
   Heating oven (H)
   Reactor (R)
   Gas liquid separator (S)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The system further comprises a back pressure regulator (BPR) unit, which maintains the pressure of the system, a heating oven (H) unit, which maintains the temperature in the reactor, and a gas liquid separator (S), which separates liquid hydrocarbons from gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogenation-decarboxylation catalyst comprises at least one chosen from nitrogen-doped carbon material and an oxygen-doped carbon material, and is derived from a biogenic source. The hydrogen-donor is at least one chosen from carboxylic acid and 1-4C alcohol, preferably at least one chosen from formic acid, acetic acid, methanol, ethanol and glycerol. The hydrogenation-decarboxylation catalyst further comprises a carbon material chosen from oxygen-doped active carbon and nitrogen-doped active carbon. The carbon precursor is of biomass origin. The feedstock is obtained from at least one chosen from plants (including algae) and animals. The feedstock comprises at least one chosen from 8-18C saturated and unsaturated fatty acids, fatty glycerides (mono-, di-, and tri-glycerides) and fatty alkyl esters of 8-18C fatty acids, preferably octanoic acid, palmitic acid, stearic acid, oleic acid, linoleic acid, ( gamma )-linolenic acid, and tristearin, tall oil fatty acids (TOFA), coconut oil, rapeseed oil, sunflower oil, and jatropha oil. Preferred Process: The process further optionally involves selectively hydrogenating and deoxygenating a feed comprising unsaturated fatty acids and/or its derivatives by contacting the feedstock with the hydrogenation catalyst in the presence of water, resulting in production of feedstock of saturated fatty acids and/or its derivatives. Preferred Conditions: The contact time of the feedstock with the catalyst is 180-250 minutes, when operating in batch mode and the weight hourly space velocity (WHSV) is 0.05-10/hour, when operating in continuous mode. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of feedstock to water is 1:4-1:10 and the weight ratio of hydrogen-donor to feedstock is less than 0.77.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)
MC H04-A; H04-E08; H04-F02A; H04-F02E; H04-F06; H06-B04
IP B01J-021/18; C10G-003/00

PT P
PN CN110812411-A
TI Topical essential oil useful for antiallergic treatment of skin eczema and promoting e.g. blood circulation, comprises essential oils of Sophora flavescens, cortex phellodendri, radix scutellariae, honeysuckle, peppermint and licorice.
AU MIAO M
   FENG S
   LV Z
AE UNIV HENAN CHINESE MEDICINE (HECO-C)
GA 202018625F
AB    NOVELTY - Topical essential oil comprises 5-7 ml Sophora flavescens essential oil, 5-7 ml cortex phellodendri essential oil, 3.5-4.5 ml radix scutellariae essential oil, 3.5-4.5 ml honeysuckle essential oil, 3.5-4.5 ml peppermint essential oil, 3.5-4.5 ml pepper essential oil, 5-7 ml wormwood essential oil, 5-7 ml licorice essential oil and 3.5-4.5 g borneol, where the S.flavescens essential oil is prepared by crushing S.flavescens through a 10-20 mesh sieve to a coarse powder, subjecting supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction in a weight-to-volume ratio of 1:(10-20) at 42-50 degrees C, an extraction pressure of 20-25 MPa, a separation temperature of 50-60 degrees C and a separation pressure of 4.5-5.5 MPa for 1.5-2.5 hours, and the preparation method of essential oils of the remaining raw material are by the same as the S.flavescens essential oil.
   USE - The topical essential oil is useful for antiallergic treatment of skin eczema, and promoting blood circulation, detoxification, dampness, antipruritic and antiallergic effects.
   ADVANTAGE - The topical essential oil is rich in raw materials, prepared by simple method, convenient to use and economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The topical essential oil comprises 6 ml S.flavescens essential oil, 6 ml cortex phellodendri essential oil, 4 ml radix scutellariae essential oil, 4 ml honeysuckle essential oil, 4 ml peppermint essential oil, 4 ml pepper essential oil, 6 ml wormwood essential oil, 6 ml licorice essential oil and 4 g borneol.
   ACTIVITY - Antiallergic; Dermatological; Antiinflammatory; Vasotropic; Antidote; Antipruritic.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

TI Small animal direct current breathing loop, has right-angle joint whose horizontal joint is connected to anesthetic suction structure, and three-way joint whose upper interface is connected to air bag, where side interface of joint is connected with valve.
AU PENG J
   PENG X
AE SHANGHAI MAIBEN MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020178571
AB    NOVELTY - the lower interface of the utility model relates to animal medical appliance technology field, especially relates to a small animal direct breathing loop, through the vertical interface of the upper interface is h-shaped joint of the right-angle joint is connected, is located at the right side is connected with the anesthesia machine by suction, are connected by the lower interface of expiration threaded pipe and three-way joint at the lower interface of the left side, and the horizontal interface of the right angle joint connected to animal anesthesia face mask or cannula, the upper interface of the three-way joint is connected to an airbag and balanced so as to ensure the pressure in the loop. and is connected to too large can automatically release the airway pressure through side interface and the safety valve is three-way joint, and is further provided with a detecting device with ETCO2 bypass interface, so as to carry out solid powder of carbon dioxide in exhaled breath loop in the anesthesia operation monitoring on the right-angle joint, so as to ensures anaesthesia safety of small animal anesthesia during surgery.

PT P
PN CN210068318-U
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide integrated electricity generating system, has heat regenerator whose air inlet is connected with air outlet of expander, where heat regenerator is connected with heat exchanging medium output by low-temperature methanol washing device.
AU FANG J
   LIU M
   ZHU X
   SUN L
AE ENN SCI & TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (XINA-C)
GA 2020160330
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a supercritical CO2 integrated generate electricity system, the system comprising a first expander, a first regenerator, the air inlet of the first regenerator is connected with air outlet of the first expander for heat exchange medium output receiving the first expander; the first air inlet is the outlet of the first regenerator and the first expander; the liquid outlet of the low temperature methanol washing device, a low temperature methanol washing device is connected with the cold source inlet of the first regenerator, CO2 and heat exchanging medium output by the low temperature methanol washing device in the first regenerator to exchange heat. In the utility model, the temperature CO2 from the temperature of the supercritical CO2 fluid of low temperature methanol washing device preparation is much lower than the cooler, can to greater degree of heat exchange with the heat exchange medium, the temperature difference between exhaust gas then enters the flue gas and system will increase between the first expander discharge, that is, it improves the temperature difference of the system to high temperature flue gas and exhaust so as to bring great heat transfer driving force, improves the system generate electricity efficiency.
DC Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M))
MC Q52-A; Q52-C09
IP F02C-003/22; F02C-003/30; F02C-006/00; F02C-006/18

PT P
PN CN110761751-A; CN110761751-B
TI Multipoint gas sampling device for soil in aerated zone with background comparison function, has fixed ring and active ring whose outer side are respectively provided with multi-point gas collection devices.
AU ZHANG X
   ZHOU L
   ZHU Y
   LI Z
   LIU P
   MU Q
   JI Z
   ZHANG H
   YANG H
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 202013724M
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an active ring (3) that is arranged in the fixed ring (1). A servo motor is set in the active ring. The servo motor is connected with the fixed ring for driving. The outer side of the fixed ring and the active ring are respectively provided with multi-point gas collection devices that realize the change between the sample monitoring state and the reference calibration state. The multipoint gas collection device comprises a gas extraction pipe interface (4) and an air suction pipe interface (6). The gas extraction pipeline interface is connected to the active ring through the suction passage (5).
   USE - Multipoint gas sampling device for soil in aerated zone with background comparison function.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas sampling device can quickly and conveniently measure the carbon dioxide flux in the soil layer at different depths at the same location and at the same depth in different locations, and the measurement results high accuracy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the multipoint gas sampling device for soil in aerated zone with background comparison function. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Fixed ring (1)
   Active ring (3)
   Gas extraction pipe interface (4)

AU TAO L
   TANG Z
   TANG J
AE TAO L (TAOL-Individual)
GA 2019A75557
AB    NOVELTY - Performing double-kiln and double-base metallurgical method involves performing preliminary reduction of metal oxide-containing minerals in a flame-proof rotary kiln, re-reduction in an internal combustion rotary kiln, and separating the reductant. The material outlet of the flame-proof rotary kiln and the inlet of the internal combustion rotary kiln are sealedly connected. The flame-proof rotary kiln control temperature is 900-1000 degrees C, and internal combustion rotary kiln control temperature is 1100-1350 degrees C. The particle reducing agent is used, and added flame-proof rotary kiln by air injection. The internal combustion rotary kiln adopts speed-regulating screw machine to add airtight from the inlet of internal combustion rotary kiln.
   USE - Method for performing double-kiln and double-base metallurgical method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve double-kiln and double-base metallurgical method, that adopts a reduction metallurgy method combining a flame-proof rotary kiln and an internal combustion-type rotary kiln, which accurately controls the mixing and reduction temperature of materials, can effectively avoid the phenomenon of ring formation in the flame-proof rotary kiln, and reduces energy consumption compared with the traditional single rotary kiln reduction method, and carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions are reduced by 40-50%, and there is no nitrogen oxide emissions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The flame-proof rotary kiln and the internal combustion-type rotary kiln are both horizontal, and the bottom surface of the flame-proof rotary kiln is higher than the top surface of the internal-combustion rotary kiln and is installed horizontally. The internal combustion rotary kiln is tilted 2.5-5 degrees horizontally, the end of the inlet is higher than the end of the outlet. The flame-proof rotary kiln is 30-40 meters long, and internal combustion rotary kiln is 8-16 meters long. The flame-proof rotary kiln includes a thermal insulation outer wall and a heat-resistant cylinder, and a reducing agent spray gun is provided at the end. The reducing agent spray gun is located at the axial center of the flame-proof rotary kiln, and the end portion extends into the heat-resistant cylinder. An injection burner is provided at the end of the internal combustion rotary kiln, the injection burner extends into the internal combustion rotary kiln, and a burner is provided on the top. The particle reducing agent has a volatile content of 25-40%, greater than or equal to 45 wt.% fixed carbon, less than or equal to 15 wt.% ash content, less than or equal to 0.5 wt.% sulfur (S), thermal stability temperature greater than or equal to 680 degrees C, ash melting point is greater than 1200 degrees C, and water content is 8-12 wt.%, particle size is 5-20 mm. The amount of particle reducing agent in the flame-proof rotary kiln is 30-32% by weight of minerals, and the amount of air is 20-60 newton cubic metres/ ton of minerals, which are continuously injected at a pressure of 24.5-29.4 kilopascal. The filling factor of the flame-proof rotary kiln is 25-50%, the rotation speed is 0.3-0.6 revolutions/minute, and the initial reduction time is 2.3-2.8 hours. The filling coefficient of the internal combustion rotary kiln is 8-15%, the rotation speed is 0.3-2.5 revolutions/minute, and the reduction time is 0.3-0.5 hours. The re-reduced material is separated by wet magnetic separation or dry magnetic separation. Preferred Components: The particulate reducing agent is any one of long bituminous coal, lignite, or biomass particles. The mineral is any one of refractory iron ore, iron concentrate, or iron-containing solid waste containing nickel oxide and manganese oxide.

TI Core-shell structure silicon dioxide/silicon            carbide material useful as wave absorbing stealth            material and photocatalyst comprises silicon dioxide            core and a silicon carbide shell layer wrapped on the            outer surface of silicon dioxide core.
AU CAO H
   WANG D
   WANG H
   XUE J
   ZHENG Y
AE WUHAN INST TECHNOLOGY (WUHT-C)
GA 2019A5235U
AB    NOVELTY - Core-shell structure silicon dioxide/silicon                carbide material comprises silicon dioxide core and                a silicon carbide shell layer wrapped on the outer                surface of the silicon dioxide core.
   USE - The core-shell structure is useful as wave                absorbing stealth material or photocatalyst                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, adopts an organic                binder as a carbon source and silicon dioxide                particles as a silicon source to synthesize                core-shell structure silicon dioxide/silicon                carbide material in situ, and economical and easily                available raw materials, and which is conducive to                industrial production and has great potential                application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing core-shell structure silicon                dioxide/silicon carbide material comprising (1)                using silica particles as a silicon source and an                organic polymer binder as a carbon source to obtain                a mixed dispersion of silica and organic polymer                binder, (2) heating the mixed dispersion to obtain                a silica/ organic polymer binder precursor, (3)                carbonizing the precursor and drying the precursor                to obtain carbon dioxide-coated silica particles,                (4) performing calcination of the carbon-coated                silica particles at 1200-1400&#176; C under                vacuum conditions for 2-5 hours to obtain the                initial product, and (5) performing calcination and                decarbonizing the primary product to obtain a                core-shell structure silicon dioxide/silicon                carbide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle diameter                  of the silicon dioxide core is 50 nm to 2                  &#956; m. The silica particles are amorphous                  silicon dioxide, and the particle size of the                  silicon dioxide particles is 50 nm to 2                  &#956; m. Preferred Method: In the step (2),                  heating and stirring the mixed dispersion at                  80-100&#176; C for 0.5-2 hours to obtain a                  silicon dioxide/organic polymer binder precursor.                  In the step (3), drying the precursor at                  80-110&#176; C, and carbonizing at                  450-600&#176; C for 0.5-3 hours. In the step                  (5), performing calcination of the primary                  product at 600-700&#176; C for 2-5 hours,                  removing unreacted carbon to obtain core-shell                  structure silicon dioxide/silicon carbide                  material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The dispersion solvent                  in the mixed dispersion is ethanol and water, and                  the organic polymer binder is                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyacrylamide and/or                  polyvinyl alcohol. The molar ratio of carbon                  atoms and silicon atoms in the mixed dispersion                  is (3-5): 1.

PN CN110295074-A; CN210287253-U
TI System for comprehensively utilizing hydrogen resources in coke oven gas, comprises coke oven gas pretreatment unit, coke oven gas purification unit, coke oven gas separation unit and hydrogen utilization unit.
AU SUN X
   LI K
   QIU Y
   LIU G
   DONG X
AE BEIJING PETROCHEMICAL ENG CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 201985632S
AB    NOVELTY - System for comprehensively utilizing hydrogen resources in coke oven gas, comprises a coke oven gas pretreatment unit, a coke oven gas purification unit, a coke oven gas separation unit and a hydrogen utilization unit. The coke oven gas removes heavy impurities in the coke oven gas pretreatment unit, and then enters the coke oven gas purification unit to remove carbon dioxide (CO2) and hydrogen sulfide (H2S) gas impurities to obtain purified coke oven gas. The coke oven gas separation unit comprises a liquefaction separation unit and pressure swing adsorption (PSA) hydrogen extraction unit. The purification coke oven gas unit enters the liquefaction separation unit to extract liquefied natural gas (LNG), hydrogen-rich gas after LNG extraction and residual portion of purification coke oven gas are mixed and then enter the PSA hydrogen extraction unit, and industrial hydrogen is extracted. The hydrogen utilization unit is a caprolactam combined device.
   USE - The system or method is useful for comprehensively utilizing hydrogen resources in coke oven gas (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for comprehensively utilizing hydrogen resources in coke oven gas which involves carrying out treatment and separation on the coke oven gas to obtain LNG product and industrial hydrogen, where the industrial hydrogen enters the caprolactam combined device to carry out caprolactam production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The PSA gas, after the PSA hydrogen extraction unit extracts industrial hydrogen, is rich in methane gas, and is compressed by the compressor and then sent out to be used as a fuel gas. Preferred Process: Step of treating and separating the coke oven gas involves sequentially entering coke oven gas to a coke oven gas pretreatment unit, a coke oven gas purification unit and a coke oven gas separation portion; mixing hydrogen-rich gas after LNG extraction and residual portion of purified coke oven gas, and then entering the PSA hydrogen extraction unit to extract industrial hydrogen. Removing heavy impurities in the coke oven gas pretreatment unit involves, in the coke oven gas pretreatment unit, the coke oven gas is subjected to gas holder buffering and sedimentation and fractionation of tar, then subjected to primary boosting and removing heavy impurities, and the pretreated gas enters a coke oven gas purification unit after secondary boosting.

TI Beverage comprises fructus cannabis protein powder, calcium lactate, vitamin D, water and stabilizer comprising linseed gum, xanthan gum and sucrose fatty acid ester.
AU WANG M
   YANG J
   WANG Y
   ZHU S
AE INST BAST FIBER CROPS CHINESE ACAD AGRIC (CAGS-C)
GA 201992086E
AB    NOVELTY - Beverage comprises 1-5 %mass fructus cannabis protein powder, 0.1-0.2 %mass calcium lactate, 0.25-0.9 %mass stabilizer, 1x 10-6-2x 10-6 %mass vitamin D and 94-98.6 %mass water. The stabilizer comprises linseed gum, xanthan gum and sucrose fatty acid ester in a mass ratio of (1-2):(1-5):(0.5-2).
   USE - As beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage has high comprehensive nutrition and supplements calcium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the beverage, involving (i) shelling hemp seed to obtain fructus cannabis, carrying out physical squeezing and deoiling to obtain seed meal, subjecting the seed meal to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction and degreasing to obtain fructus cannabis protein powder, (ii) putting the obtained fructus cannabis protein powder in water, boiling, homogenizing and filtering to obtain fructus cannabis protein emulsion, (iii) putting linseed gum, xanthan gum and sucrose fatty acid ester in hot water, stirring until completely dissolved to obtain a stabilizer solution, and (iv) sequentially adding calcium lactate, fructus cannabis protein emulsion, stabilizer solution, vitamin D and other additives, uniformly mixing, homogenizing, supplementing water until the proportion of the beverage is met, adding lactic acid to adjust the pH value, uniformly mixing, bottling and sterilizing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The beverage further comprises 0.01-0.02 %mass polylysine salt, 0.01-0.02 %mass D-sodium erythorbate, 0.01-0.02 %mass stevioside, 0.05-0.15 %mass ethyl lactate and 0.01-0.05 %mass ethyl maltol. The protein content of the fructus cannabis protein powder is 65-70%. In step (ii), the mass ratio of fructus cannabis protein powder and water is (1-5):(40-60). In step (iii), the mass ratio of linseed gum, xanthan gum, sucrose fatty acid and water is (1-2):(1-5):(0.5-2):(92-98). Preferred Condition: The beverage further comprises lactic acid, where the pH value of the beverage is adjusted to 6.3-6.8. Preferred Method: In step (ii), the boiling time is 10-30 minutes, the homogenization is carried out at a speed of 10000-20000 rpm for 5-10 minutes, and the filtering is carried out through a 200 mesh filter cloth. In step (iv), the temperature of the cannabis protein emulsion is 70-90 degrees C, the mass ratio of fructus cannabis protein powder and stabilizer is (1-5):(0.25-0.9), calcium lactate, vitamin D and other additives are added, the homogenization is carried out at a rotation speed of 10000-20000 rpm for 5-10 minutes, and the pH value of the beverage is adjusted to 6.3-6.8 by adding lactic acid, where the other additives comprises polylysine salt, D-sodium erythorbate, stevioside, ethyl lactate and ethyl maltol. Preferred Condition: The temperature of the hot water is 90-95 degrees C.

PN CN110257250-A
TI Culture medium composition for Chaetoceros muelleri comprises sodium nitrate, monopotassium phosphate, silicate, ferric citrate, manganese chloride, copper sulfate, zinc sulfate, sodium molybdate, cobalt chloride, and EDTA-disodium.
AU ZHANG B
   ZHU X
   XIONG J
   XIE D
   ZHAO Y
   CHEN X
   WEI P
   CHEN T
   PENG J
   HE P
AE GUANGXI ZHUANG AUTONOMOUS REGION INST (GUAG-C)
GA 2019920346
AB    NOVELTY - Culture medium composition for Chaetoceros muelleri comprises 75-90 mg/L sodium nitrate, 5-10 mg/L monopotassium phosphate, 35-50 mg/L silicate, 3.9-5 mg/L ferric citrate, 1.5-1.8x 10-1 mg/L manganese chloride, 0.5-1.5x 10-3 mg/L copper sulfate, 2-3x 10-2 mg/L zinc sulfate, 7-7.5x 10-3 mg/L sodium molybdate, 1-2x 10-2 mg/L cobalt chloride, 4-5x 10-3 mg/L EDTA-disodium, 0.05-0.15 mg/L vitamin B1 (VB1), and 4.5-5.5x 10-4 mg/L vitamin B12 (VB12). Culturing involves placing composition into a photobioreactor, the full aeration is 10-15 minutes, followed by addition of algae solution algae to make seeding density of 0.5-1.4x 106 cell/ml, controlling the photobioreactor material temperature at 25-34 degrees C, the salinity is 5-40%, pH is 7.7-8.3, light intensity is 3500-7000 lux, introducing air and carbon dioxide into water, and harvesting culture after 3-5 days.
   USE - The culture medium composition is useful for Chaetoceros muelleri (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition or method can improve growth speed and the culture density of Chaetoceros muelleri.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: Preparation of the composition involves: (a) preparing mother liquor A: sequentially adding sodium nitrate, potassium dihydrogen phosphate, and sodium silicate into double distilled water, until the starting material is completely dissolved and then placing in a next feedstock, and sterilization to obtain mother liquor A; (b) preparing mother liquor B: adding sodium molybdate and ferric citrate in double distilled water, heating with stirring, filtering the solution, cooling and sterilizing using a filter paper, to give a mother liquor B; (c) preparing mother liquor C: adding manganese chloride, copper sulfate, zinc sulfate, cobalt chloride and EDTA-disodium into double distilled water, completely dissolving after sterilization, to obtain mother liquor C; (d) preparing mother liquor D: taking sterilized distilled water, after cooling, adding VB1 and VB12 to obtain mother liquor D; and (e) precipitation for 5-7 days after filtering, ozone disinfection, UV disinfection, and then uniformly mixing the mother liquor A, mother liquor B, mother liquor C, and mother liquor D to give the composition. It also involves introducing air and carbon dioxide into the material, and controlling air rate at 3-5 L/minute; or introducing the air and carbon dioxide into the material, with volume ratio of air and carbon dioxide of 8-12:1. Preferred Parameters: The salinity of material is 20-30%. The photobioreactor control temperature is 28-31 degrees C.

TI Method for diffusing fluid flow within modular extraction vessel for performing supercritical fluid extraction, involves passing extracting solvent through vessel chambers, and diffusing flow path of extracting solvent along extraction path.
AU FOGWILL M O
   FADGEN K
   KELLEY S
   MICHIENZI J D
AE WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
   WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)

   FADGEN K (FADG-Individual)
   KELLEY S (KELL-Individual)
   MICHIENZI J D (MICH-Individual)
GA 201977962P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves assembling a modular extraction vessel (201) including a set of extraction vessel chambers, where each extraction vessel chamber is associated with a diffuser. An extracting solvent is introduced into the modular extraction vessel (203), where the solvent is a compressed carbon-dioxide. The extracting solvent is passed through the set of extraction vessel chambers. A flow path of the extracting solvent is diffused along an extraction path (205) using the diffusers associated with the extraction vessel chambers, where positioning of the extraction vessel chambers and the diffusers redistributes flow of the extracting solvent along the extraction path more equally than a single extraction vessel.
   USE - Method for diffusing fluid flow within a modular extraction vessel for performing supercritical fluid extraction for separating components in solid or semisolid matrix.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables customizing the modular extraction vessel to a desired volume depending on number of extraction vessel chambers used to allow for more thorough diffusion of the extracting solvent when coupled with diffusers associated with each extraction vessel chamber.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for diffusing fluid flow within a modular extraction vessel.
   Step for assembling modular extraction vessel (201)
   Step for introducing extracting solvent into modular extraction vessel (203)
   Step for diffusing flow path of extracting solvent along extraction path (205)

PT P
PN CN110157658-A
TI Inducing growth of retinal ganglion cell by formulating stem cell to obtain single cell suspension, counting and diluting single cell suspension with differentiation medium, proliferating embryoid body and performing differentiation culture.
AU CHEN Z
AE RONS CELL BIOTECHNOLOGY GUANGZHOU CO LTD (RONS-Non-standard)
GA 2019770980
AB    NOVELTY - Method for inducing growth of retinal ganglion cells by stem cells in vitro involves (a) formulating stem cell to prepare a single cell suspension, (b) counting and diluting the single cell suspension with a differentiation medium to a dry cell suspension of 10-5-10-3 cells/ml, and inoculating into a culture dish to obtain a embryoid body and (c) proliferating and transferring the embryoid body into an induction differentiation medium, and performing differentiation culture at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide and saturated humidity. The induced differentiation medium comprises 10 ng/ml basic fibroblast growth factor (bFGF), 20 ng/ml brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF), 20 ng/ml ciliary neurotrophic factor (CNTF), 1 ng/ml N2 supplement, 1 ng/ml non-essential amino acid, 2 -mg/ml heparin, and remainder of Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium/nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/F12) medium. The DMEM/F12 medium is prepared by mixing DMEM medium and F12 medium in a volume ratio of 1:1.
   USE - The method is useful for inducing growth of retinal ganglion cells for alleviating deterioration of patients after optic nerve injury, promoting the recovery of visual function and restoring of damaged optic nerve structure and function of patients.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Medium: The medium further comprises 1-5 ng ginkgolides. Preferred Method: The ginkgolide is obtained by extracting the Ginkgo biloba extract. The Ginkgo biloba extract is prepared by (a) adding 90% ethanol to the G.biloba leaves in a mass ratio of 1:10-12, extracting for 2-4 hours to obtain the first extract, adding 60% ethanol to the first extract in a mass ratio of 1:7-10, extracting for 2-4 hours to obtain the second extract, adding 40% ethanol to the first extract in a mass ratio of 1:3-5 and extracting for 1-3 hours to obtain the third extract, (b) concentrating the extract to half of the original volume, extracting with an equal volume of ethyl acetate 3 times, extracting and concentrating the separated organic phase to obtain an extract and then diluting with deionized water in a volume ratio of 1:80 to obtain a mixed solution and (c) uniformly mixing ADS-17 column on the mixture, washing with water until the effluent is colorless, then eluting with ethanol, collecting the eluate, recovering the ethanol, concentrating to obtain the extract, and crystallizing with 70% ethanol to obtain G.biloba extract. The ginkgolides is prepared by (a) gradually adding diatomaceous earth to ethyl acetate under continuous stirring, adding G.biloba extract, heating under reflux at 70-80 degrees C for 4-5 hours and concentrating by filtration to obtain a concentrate, where the diatomaceous earth, ethyl acetate and G.biloba extract are in a mass ratio of 0.8-1.2:20-30:1, (b) adding deionized water to the concentrated solution, allowing to stand to separate the layers, passing the ethyl acetate layer through an acidic alumina column, and eluting the mixture with water and saturating with ethyl acetate to collect an eluate and (c) concentrating the eluate under reduced pressure, dissolving in anhydrous ethanol, then adding with deionized water until alcohol content is 50-60%, allowing to stand to obtain crystals, and drying to obtain ginkgolides, where the diatomaceous earth is modified diatomaceous earth. The modified diatomaceous earth is prepared by (a) adding diatomaceous earth to a nitric acid solution having a pH of 0.3-0.5, formulating into a slurry having a concentration of 20-50 wt.%, boiling, cooling, filtering, washing, drying at 60-120 degrees C and calcining at 500-600 degrees C for 6 hours to obtain first pre-modified diatomaceous earth, (b) adding the first pre-modified diatomaceous earth to 25-30 wt.% ferric nitrate solution, formulating into slurry having a concentration of 40-50 wt.%, drying and calcining at 500-600 degrees C for 10 hours to obtain a second pre-modified diatomaceous earth, (c) uniformly mixing nano-zirconium dioxide and the second pre-modified diatomaceous earth in a gas flow to obtain a modified diatomaceous earth, where the mass ratio of the second pre-modified diatomaceous earth and the nano zirconium dioxide is 1:0.01-0.05 and the nano-zirconium dioxide is modified nano-zirconium dioxide. The modified nano-zirconium dioxide is prepared by weighing 1-2 parts mass, preferably 1.5 parts mass cerium nitrate, 4-10 parts mass, preferably 6 parts mass sodium hydroxide, 40-50 parts mass, preferably 45 parts mass deionized water, 0.8-1 part mass, preferably 0.9 part mass nano-zirconium dioxide, dissolving cerium nitrate in 20-25 parts mass deionized water, dissolving sodium hydroxide in 20-25 parts mass deionized water, uniformly mixing the two solutions and reacting at 140 degrees C for 48 hours in a high pressure environment, discarding half of the supernatant, adding equal volume of deionized water, uniformly mixing and reacting at 140 degrees C for 48 hours, filtering the solid matter, washing, drying and calcining at 500-600 degrees C for 3 hours in an air atmosphere to obtain a modified nano-zirconium dioxide.

PN CN110156052-A
TI Process for co-production of liquid ammonium chloride fertilizer and calcium carbonate involves using salt brine as raw material, and adopting ammonia aspiration, carbonization, calcination and lime steaming ammonia series process.
AU PENG S
AE CHINA LIGHT IND INT ENG CO LTD (POYG-C)
GA 201977167D
AB    NOVELTY - The process for co-production of liquid ammonium chloride fertilizer and calcium carbonate involves (1) directly using salt brine as raw material, adopting ammonia aspiration, carbonization, calcination and lime steaming ammonia series process to obtain product soda ash and steaming ammonia waste liquid, (2) evaporating and concentrating ammonia waste liquid at 50-150 degrees C, and clarifying to obtain salt calcium liquid and waste residue, (3) evaporating salt calcium solution at 50-150 degrees C to obtain solution of sodium chloride and calcium chloride, (4) carrying out ammonia absorption or carbonization reaction of calcium chloride solution at 25-80 degrees C for 1-5 hours, adding to ammonium bicarbonate to obtain product calcium carbonate and liquid ammonium chloride fertilizer, (5) using calcium carbonate as product or recycling as raw material for producing lime and carbon dioxide, (6) recycling sodium chloride as a raw material for producing soda ash.
   USE - Process for co-production of liquid ammonium chloride fertilizer and calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables co-production of liquid ammonium chloride fertilizer and calcium carbonate which has high yield of 100%, low comprehensive energy consumption, calcium ion recycling effect, excellent energy conservation and emission reduction effect, and does not discharge waste liquid, by economical and environmentally-friendly process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The sodium chloride content in salt brine is 300-330 g/l. The ammoniated waste liquid contains 95-140 g/l calcium chloride and 40-100 g/l sodium chloride. The ammoniated waste liquid further contains calcium sulfate, calcium carbonate, magnesium hydroxide, silicon dioxide, calcium oxide, iron oxide, and aluminum oxide. The content of each component in salt calcium solution comprises 252-350 g/l calcium chloride and 121-131 g/l sodium chloride. The content of each component in calcium chloride solution comprises 2652-1151 g/l calcium chloride and 7.31-27 g/l sodium chloride. The content of each component in liquid ammonium chloride fertilizer comprises 628-1109 g/l ammonium chloride, and 7.31-27 g/l sodium chloride. The salt brine is brine of sodium chloride-water, sodium sulfate-water, sodium chloride-sodium sulfate-water, sodium chloride-calcium sulfate-water system.

PN CN110078082-A
TI Method for recovering silicon residue by using intermediate frequency furnace, involves smelting silicon residue raw material in intermediate frequency furnace, adding desiliconizing agent, reacting and pouring liquid into vibrating machine.
AU YANG S
   ZHOU X
AE XINJIANG TAIYUDA ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOG (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 201969240P
AB    NOVELTY - The method for recovering silicon residue by using intermediate frequency furnace, involves (a) smelting silicon residue raw material in an intermediate frequency furnace, preparing desiliconizing agent (I), (b) controlling the temperature in intermediate frequency furnace at 1430-1510 degrees C, adding desiliconizing agent (I) to the furnace, making the concentration of carbon dioxide in the furnace to 5-10%, stirring, (c) pouring residue liquid into vibrating machine for stirring the residue after the reaction in the furnace is completed, and placing desiliconizing agent (II) into vibrating machine, (d) filtering the product in vibrating machine to recover the solid phase. The total weight of desiliconizing agent (I) is less than 0.2 times of weight of silicon residue raw material. The sum of weight of desiliconizing agent (I) and desiliconizing agent (II) is 0.2 times of weight of silicon residue raw material.
   USE - Method for recovering silicon residue by using intermediate frequency furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables recovery of silicon residue with high recovery rate, and which solves the problem that a large amount of alumina is contained in the product of the silicon residue recovery treatment so as to reduce the content of aluminum in the recovered product and improve silicon content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The desiliconizing agent (I) and (II) comprise iron oxide, lime, fluorite and water glass with weight ratio of 7:1:1.5:0.5. The ratio of desiliconizing agent (I) and desiliconizing agent (II) is 2:1. The amount of silicon residue raw material is less than or equal to 200 kg. Preferred Process: The desiliconizing agent is prepared by crushing lime and fluorite into a powder, adding water glass and stirring to form a paste and coating the surface of the iron oxide block. The recovery process of silicon residue is carried out by soaking recovered solid phase with cold water for 0.5-1 hour and draining the generated gas. The silicon content is determined by sampling when the silicon residue is completely melted into the residue liquid in step (a), and the filtered residue liquid is sampled in step (d) to determine the silicon content. Preferred Properties: The filtration is carried out through sieve having pore size of 0.1 mm.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   WEI Y (WEIY-Individual)
GA 201960609U

   ADVANTAGE - The plant antipruritic essential oil is pure and natural, without any chemical addition, convenient use, has high safety, quick antipruritic effect, mild and comfortable, and the plant has excellent stability and high extraction rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for compound extraction method for oil, which involves (1) selecting, removing and freeze-drying german chamomile, grass, honeysuckle, Sophora flavescens and fructus cnidii, (2) respectively pulverizing German chamomile, grass, honeysuckle, S.flavescens and fructus cnidii at low temperature and passing through sieve of 80-100 mesh to obtain single raw material powder, (3) compounding and premixing single raw material powder of german chamomile, grass, honeysuckle, S.flavescens and fructus cnidii obtained in step (2) according to weight ratio of 1:(1-100):(1-100):(1-100):(1-100) to obtain composite raw material powder, (4) subjecting the composite raw material powder obtained in step (3) to carbon dioxide supercritical fluid extraction to obtain crude extract of plant and (5) adding yellow-brown extract obtained in step (4) to distillation pot, introducing water vapor and performing steam distillation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: In step (3), the weight ratio of single raw material powder of german chamomile, grass, honeysuckle, S.flavescens and fructus cnidii is 1:10:5:2:3. Preferred Process: In step (3), the single raw material powder of german chamomile, grass, honeysuckle, S.flavescens and fructus cnidii is compounded and mixed in a conical mixer, and the weight fraction is small, two single raw material powders are added to the conical mixer for mixing, and other single raw material powders are added. Preferred Conditions: In step (4), the extraction temperature of carbon dioxide supercritical fluid extraction is controlled at 32-42 degrees C, the carbon dioxide fluid pressure is controlled at 11-38 MPa, the carbon dioxide flow rate is controlled at 10-25 L/hour, and the extraction time is 40-80 minutes. In distillation stage (I), the pressure of water vapor is controlled at 0.3-0.6 MPa for 3-10 minutes. In distillation stage (II), the pressure of water vapor is controlled at 0.05-0.2 MPa for 70-120 minutes. The temperature of distillation extraction is controlled at 90-110 degrees C.
   ACTIVITY - Antipruritic.
