   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the adsorbent, comprising (i) washing and drying the straw, (ii) placing straw in tube furnace, introducing mixed gas composed of air and carbon dioxide, heating to 700 degrees C at heating rate of 15-18 degrees C/minutes, carbonizing and activating for 75-100 minutes, cooling and grinding through 20 meshes sieve to obtain straw biochar, (iii) dissolving magnesium chloride hexahydrate in water to obtain a magnesium chloride solution, (iv) immersing the straw biochar in the magnesium chloride solution, (v) dropping sodium hydroxide solution in the mixture of the straw biochar and the magnesium chloride solution, reacting in a water bath at 50-70 degrees C for 1 hour, precipitating, washing precipitate with distilled water, filtering and drying to obtain modified biochar, and (vi) placing modified biochar in a quartz boat, placing in a tube furnace, igniting for 1-2 hours at an air flow rate of 0.2 l/minutes, heating rate of 15 degrees C/minute, and 350-500 degrees C and cooling; and
   (2) use method of magnesium oxide-loaded straw biochar sulfur dioxide adsorbent, comprising wetting sulfur dioxide adsorbent with water and subjecting to a sulfur dioxide adsorption operation at 25 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The straw biochar is made of straw, and the straw is rice straw, wheat straw or corn straw. The powdered magnesium oxide is made of magnesium chloride hexahydrate as a raw material. In the step (ii), the the mixed gas composed of air and carbon dioxide, the volume ratio of air to carbon dioxide is 1:4. In the step (iii), the mass percentage concentration of magnesium chloride in the magnesium chloride solution is 20-25%. In the step (iv), the mass ratio of the straw biochar to the magnesium chloride solution is 1:50. In the step (v), the concentration of sodium hydroxide is 0.5 mol/l and the dropping is stopped after the pH of 11. The moisture content of the sulfur dioxide adsorbent after wetting with water is 100% of the mass of the sulfur dioxide adsorbent itself.

TI High-pressure solid high-capacity lithium iron            phosphate preparation involves weighing, mixing and            adding iron phosphate, lithium carbonate, polyethylene            glycol and sucrose into alcohol for wet grinding to            obtain slurry.
AU JIANG Y
AE BAO J (BAOJ-Individual)
GA 2019556204
AB    NOVELTY - High-pressure solid high-capacity lithium iron                phosphate preparation involves weighing, mixing and                adding iron phosphate, lithium carbonate,                polyethylene glycol and sucrose into alcohol for                wet grinding to obtain a slurry having a particle                size of 400-500 nm. The obtained slurry is                spray-dried, and then pulverized into a particle                size of 3-5 mu m pulverized material, where the                alcohol vapor is condensed to obtain alcohol. The                obtained pulverized material is placed in a roller                furnace for sintering under heating section, a heat                preservation section, a decarburization section and                a cooling section. The nitrogen and carbon dioxide                are introduced into the decarburization section and                the cooling section to obtain a calcined material.                The obtained calcined material is crushed, sieved                to remove iron and then vacuum-packed to obtain the                high-pressure high-capacity lithium iron                phosphate.
   USE - Method for preparing high-pressure solid                high-capacity lithium iron phosphate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the high-pressure solid                high-capacity lithium iron phosphate in a simple,                cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with high                compaction density and good electrical                performance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic scanning                electron microscope image of the prepared                high-pressure solid high-capacity lithium iron                phosphate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The iron phosphate,                  lithium carbonate, polyethylene glycol and                  sucrose are provided in a molar ratio of                  1:1.01-1.02:0.05-0.1:0.1-0.15. The wet grinding                  is carried out by using a sand mill, where the                  zirconia balls are placed in the sand mill. The                  pulverizing is performed by using a jet mill. The                  ferrous salt, urea, hydrogen peroxide and                  phosphoric acid are provided in a molar ratio of                  1:1.5-2:0.55-0.6:1.02-1.05.

PN KR2019047882-A
TI Composition used to produce polyurethane rigid foams for thermal insulation material of membrane type liquefied natural gas carrier cargo window, comprises polyol, isocyanate, foaming agent and glass hollow spheres.
AU LEE H B
   KIM D Y
   BAE H J
   SONG E H
   SEO J G
   KIM H S
   AN B H
   LIM D W
AE HYUNDAI HEAVY IND CO LTD (HHIH-C)
   DONGSUNG FINETEC CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 201941430D
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises 80-120 pts. wt. polyol, 100 pts. wt. isocyanate, 0.5-10 pts. wt. foaming agent and 0.5-15 pts. wt. glass hollow spheres based on 100 pts. wt. polyol. The polyol is mixed polyol containing (a) 15-35 wt.% polyol obtained by adding propylene oxide and ethylene oxide to sorbitol, (b) 30-50 wt.% polyol obtained by adding propylene oxide and ethylene oxide to sucrose and 25-50 wt.% polyol obtained by adding diethylene glycol or propylene glycol to phthalic anhydride. The isocyanate is polymeric methylene diphenyl diisocyanate having functionality of 2.6-3. The foaming agent includes 1,1,1,3,3-pentafluoropropane, trans 1-chloro,3,3,3-trifluoropropene, cis 1,1,1,4,4,4-hexafluoro-2-butene and/or carbon dioxide.
   USE - The composition for is useful to produce polyurethane rigid foams for thermal insulation material (all claimed) of membrane type liquefied natural gas carrier cargo window.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has compressive strength of 5-12% equivalent to that of rigid polyurethane foam reinforced with conventional glass fibers, and produces rigid polyurethane foam which does not have loss in thermal conductivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for rigid polyurethane foam produced using the above composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition further comprises 0-15 pts. wt. crosslinking agent and 0-15 pts. wt. flame retardant based on 100 pts. wt. mixed polyol. Preferred Components: The glass hollow spheres: have density of 0.4-0.6 g/cc, strength of 16000-30000 psi and hollow spheres average wall thickness of 0.6-0.8 mu m; comprise 20-30 vol.% glass and 70-90 vol.% internal gas; and contains 0-10% particles having size of 8-15 mu m, 0-50% particles having size of 15-25 mu m and 0-90% particles having size of 20-35 mu m, where maximum particle size is 30-35 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed polyol has average hydroxy value of 350-550. The isocyanate has average isocyanate content of 29-32%. The reaction ratio of isocyanate of the isocyanate to hydroxy of the mixed polyol is 1-1.3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The rigid polyurethane foam has density of 60-120 kg/m3.
   EXAMPLE - Polyol mixture containing 36 wt.% polyol (sucrose and propylene oxide/ethylene oxide) having average hydroxy value of 350-550, 30 wt.% sorbitol and propylene oxide/ethylene oxide and 34 wt.% phthalic anhydride and diethylene glycol, polymeric methylene diphenyl diisocyanate having average isocyanate content of 29-32%, and 1,1,1,3,3-pentafluoropropane were mixed to produce composition.
DC A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

TI Osteoinductive composition comprises brain-derived            neural nutrition factor, nerve growth factor, epidermal            growth factors, fibroblast growth factors and N2 and            B27 cell culture additive, salidroside and Astragalus            polysaccharide.
AU TIAN N
   ZHANG S
   WANG X
AE JILIN TUOHUA BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 2019417489
AB    NOVELTY - Osteoinductive composition comprises                brain-derived neural nutrition factor, nerve growth                factor, epidermal growth factors, fibroblast growth                factors and N2 cell culture additives, a B27 cell                culture additive, salidroside and Astragalus                polysaccharide.
   USE - The osteoinductive composition/induction                differentiation culture solution is useful for                preparing medicine for preventing and treating                nervous system diseases.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple; enhances differentiation                of nerve cell activity and reduces inducement                differentiation time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIM are also included for: (1)                inducing human mesenchymal stem cells are induced                into a differentiation culture medium comprising                5-30 ng/ml brain-derived neural nutrition factors,                5-30 ng/ml nerve growth factor, 0.5-4 wt.%                epidermal growth factor, 10-50 ng/ml fibroblast                growth factor, 1-4 wt.% N2 cell culture additives,                10-40 mu g/l salidroside and 10-40 mg/l Astragalus                polysaccharide. TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The mesenchymal stem                  cells comprises human umbilical mesenchymal stem                  cell, human bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells,                  human adipose mesenchymal stem cells, human                  epidermal mesenchyme stem cell or human embryonic                  mesenchymal stem cells. The differentiated nerve                  cells comprise astrocyte, oligodendrocyte and                  nerve cells. The compound nerve cell comprises                  0.5x 106 to 8x 106 cells/ml differentiated nerve                  cells. The salidroside is water extract Rhodiola                  rosea, Astragalus polysaccharide is water extract                  of Astragalus membranaceus.
   ACTIVITY - Neuroprotective.

PT P
PN US2019107522-A1; FR3072173-A1; CA3019988-A1; CN109633119-A; BR102018070296-A2; GB2570023-A; FR3072173-B1; MX2018012081-A1; AR113286-A1; RU2018134770-A; US10830752-B2; GB2570023-B; CN109633119-B
TI Method for determining parameter representative of            quantity of hydrocarbon-containing compound present in            free form, involves determining parameter that is            representative of quantity of hydrocarbon-containing            compound present in free form.
AU ROMERO-SARMIENTO M
   YOUSSOUF S
   LETORT G
   ROMERO-SARMIENTO M F
   ROMERO S M F
   YUSSUF S
   LETOR G
AE IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
GA 2019321652
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves heating the sample                according to a first heating sequence under an                inert atmosphere, and continuously measuring a                representative quantity of the                hydrocarbon-containing compounds released during a                portion of the first heating sequence with a                representative quantity of carbon monoxide (CO) and                a representative quantity of carbon dioxide (CO2)                is released during the first heating sequence. A                residue of the sample originating from the first                heating sequence is heated according to a second                heating sequence under an oxidizing atmosphere, and                measuring a representative quantity of CO and a                representative quantity of CO2 released during                second heating sequence. The parameter                representative of the quantity of                hydrocarbon-containing compounds present in free                form is determined within the rock from                measurements from the first and second                samples.
   USE - Method for determining parameter                representative of quantity of                hydrocarbon-containing compounds present in free                form within sedimentary rock.
   ADVANTAGE - The sample is prepared to eliminate the                impurities and representative sample of the                isolated total organic matter originating from the                sedimentary rock which is obtained from a portion                of the sample of sedimentary rock, from which the                mineral fraction is eliminated by a procedure of                hot acid attack and drying of samples.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical representative                of a method.
DC S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC S03-E14E7

PN CN109437434-A
TI Treating phenol cyanide wastewater comprises e.g. subjecting phenol cyanide waste water for first-level breaking process under alkaline condition, oxidizing cyanide in wastewater to cyanate, and treating wastewater with activated carbon.
AU LIU X
   LI Z
   LI D
   WU C
   ZHENG Y
   ZHOU X
AE WISDRI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION ENG TECH (WISD-Non-standard)
GA 201924523N
AB    NOVELTY - Treating phenol cyanide wastewater comprises (i) subjecting the phenol cyanide waste water for first-level breaking process under alkaline condition, and oxidizing cyanide in wastewater to cyanate, (ii) based on the alkaline environment of the first- level cyanogen-treated wastewater, treating the wastewater with activated carbon to adsorb and remove the phenolic pollutants, and (iii) subjecting the wastewater treated by the modified activated carbon to second- level cyanogen treatment to oxidize and decompose the cyanate in the wastewater into carbon dioxide and nitrogen.
   USE - The method is useful for treating phenol cyanide wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can synergistically remove cyanide and phenolic pollutants in the phenol cyanide-containing wastewater, and improves the adsorption effect using modified activated carbon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for phenol cyanide-containing wastewater treatment system, comprising first grade breaking mechanism, active carbon adsorption mechanism and second grade cyanide breaking mechanism, where the first stage cyanide breaking mechanism is connected to waste water supply pipe, the wastewater inlet of the activated carbon adsorption mechanism is connected to wastewater outlet of first stage cyanide breaking mechanism, and the waste water outlet of activated carbon adsorption mechanism is connected with wastewater inlet of second stage cyanide breaking mechanism.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structural view of phenol cyanide-containing wastewater treatment system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: In the step (i), adding sodium hydroxide into the phenol cyanide-containing wastewater, adjusting the pH value of phenol-containing cyanide wastewater to 10-11, the oxidation-reduction potential of the controlled wastewater is 300-350 mv, carrying out sodium chloride removing reaction by adding sodium hypochlorite to the phenol cyanide-containing wastewater. In the step (iii), adding hydrochloric acid into the wastewater, adjusting pH value of the wastewater to 6.5-8, the oxidation-reduction potential of the controlled wastewater is 550-600 mv, adding and then sodium chloride into the phenol cyanide-containing wastewater to carry out cyanogen breaking reaction. In the step (ii), the alkaline activate carbon is used. The entire process is carried out in sealed environment. The first stage cyanogenation mechanism comprises mutually connected first pH regulating tank and first cyanide breaking tank, the waste water supply pipe is connected to first pH adjusting tank, the waste water outlet of first cyanide breaking pool is connected with the waste water inlet of active carbon absorption mechanism. The second grade waste water breaking mechanism comprises mutually connected second pH regulating tank and second cyanide breaking tank, the second pH adjustment tank waste water inlet and outlet is connected with active carbon absorption mechanism. The first grade breaking mechanism, the active carbon absorption mechanism and the second breaking mechanism is combined into a movable modularized structure. The modular structure is totally enclosed structure.

PN CN108486047-A; CN108486047-B
TI Preparing bone marrow mesenchymal stem cell            extract comprises e.g. culturing embryonic bone marrow,            collecting 3-5th generation of bone marrow mesenchymal            stem cells, culturing, adding retinol and maltose,            filtering and concentrating.
AU ZHOU X
AE GREAT BEAUTY BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GREA-Non-standard)
   GREAT BEAUTY BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (GREA-Non-standard)
GA 2018712708
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bone marrow mesenchymal stem cell                extract comprises (i) collecting embryonic bone                marrow, preparing cell suspension, culturing to                more than 85% of cell fusion, sub-culturing, and                collecting 3-5th generation of bone marrow                mesenchymal stem cells, (ii) using serum-free                medium without phenol red, when bone marrow                mesenchymal stem cells proliferate to passages 3-5,                culturing a certain time, adding retinol and                maltose, placing in a carbon dioxide incubator,                collecting the cell supernatant, and filtering and                removing impurities, and (iii) concentrating the                filtrate by ultra-filtration to obtain                ultra-filtrate.
   USE - The extract is useful in medical dressing                (claimed). The medical dressing is useful                preventing bacterial infection; accelerating wound                healing; controlling and absorbing wound exudates;                protecting new tissue; avoiding bleeding and pain;                promoting skin regeneration; replenishing moisture;                and diminishing inflammation and                detumescence.
   ADVANTAGE - The dressing has good biocompatibility, strong                gas permeability and no toxicity and side effects;                protects new tissue; utilizes natural raw                materials; adopts freeze-dried form; maintains                activity of cytokines and proteins; can be stored                and transported at room temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) bone marrow mesenchymal stem cell extract                prepared by the above method; and
   (2) medical dressing, comprising the bone                marrow mesenchymal stem cell extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The medical dressing                  comprises 0.1-0.5% bone marrow mesenchymal stem                  cell extract, 0.05-0.25% collagen lyophilized                  powder, where the mass ratio of the stem cell                  extract to collagen is 2:1, 1-4 % sodium alginate                  powder, 5-15% vitamin C powder, 80.25-93.85%                  saline, where the powder is mixed with                  physiological saline to form a gelatinous liquid.                  Preferred Method: In the step (i), the process                  comprises collecting the embryonic bone marrow,                  adding high glucose DMEM medium with 10% fetal                  bovine serum, mixing and centrifuging to remove                  the supernatant, preparing the cell suspension in                  DMEM-F12 medium containing 10% fetal bovine                  serum, inoculating in DMEM-F12 medium containing                  10% fetal bovine serum at a suitable inoculum                  size, placing in a carbon dioxide incubator,                  passing more than 85% of cell fusion, and                  collecting the third to fifth generation bone                  marrow stem cells. In the step (i), the process                  comprises using serum-free medium without phenol                  red, when the bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells                  proliferate to the 3-5th generation, adding 1 mu                  mol/l retinol and 1 mu mol/l maltose, incubating                  at 37 degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator,                  allowing to stand for 48 hours, collecting the                  cell supernatant, and filtering the bacteria and                  other impurities by a vacuum filtration system.                  In the step (iii), the solution filtered in step                  (ii), and the molecular weight cutoff of the                  ultra-filtration membrane is 3000 kDa. The                  process further comprises preparing the                  ultra-filtrate into a lyophilized powder, and                  adding 40 mM trehalose as a freeze-drying                  protective agent. The preparation of the collagen                  lyophilized powder comprises (i) thawing fish                  skin, adding water, crushing and grinding, and                  adding ultrasound while crushing and grinding to                  obtain slurry, (ii) mixing the slurry with a                  sodium chloride solution, stirring uniformly and                  soaking for a period of time, and centrifuging                  and removing supernatant to obtain first                  precipitate, (iii) mixing the first precipitate                  with the citric acid solution in a ratio of                  1:5-9, soaking for a while, and centrifuging and                  removing the supernatant to obtain second                  precipitate, (iv) mixing the second precipitate                  with water in a ratio of 1:20-40 and adding with                  2-6% papain for enzymatic hydrolysis, subjecting                  to enzymatic hydrolysis at 35-38 degrees C,                  controlling the pH of the reaction solution to                  4.5-5.5, centrifuging and taking the supernatant                  to obtain crude extract, (v) The supercritically                  degreasing the crude extract with carbon dioxide,                  and (vi) salting out the product of step (v),                  centrifuging and removing the precipitate,                  dissolving, desalting the solution and                  concentrating by ultra-filtration, and                  freeze-drying ultra-filtration retentate to                  obtain white, non-scented collagen lyophilized                  powder.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial; Vulnerary; Antiinflammatory;                Analgesic; Dermatological.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).)

PN CN108315296-A; WO2019161591-A1
TI Isolating and culturing mesenchymal stem cells, by replacing primary culture of mesenchymal stem cells with serum-free Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium nutrient solution, neutralizing, subculturing and separating mesenchymal stem cells.
AU YANG T
   SUI
   SUI Y
AE SHENZHEN ATOP BO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
   SHENZHEN ZHIBO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201863733X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for isolating and culturing mesenchymal stem cells, involves obtaining primary cells of mesenchymal stem cell, culturing and expanding primary cells of mesenchymal stem cells by replacing primary culture of mesenchymal stem cells with serum-free Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (DMEM) nutrient solution after 5-10 days, removing culture supernatant, adding first digestive solution, digesting for 0.5-2 minutes, neutralizing, centrifuging, adding complete medium to resuspend, culturing for 4-5 days, when cells were 65-75% confluent, subculturing in ratio of 1:6-8, and separating mesenchymal stem cells by collecting cells in P2 or P3 logarithmic growth phase, digesting, neutralizing, and collecting desired mesenchymal stem cells after washing.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating and culturing mesenchymal stem cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for cryopreservation and resuscitation of mesenchymal stem cells, which involves resuspending the mesenchymal stem cells in cell cryopreservation solution, placing in a container filled with isopropyl alcohol, incubating overnight at -90 degrees C to -70 degrees C, transferring to liquid nitrogen tank, immediately thawing the frozen mesenchymal stem cells in a 40-45 degrees C water bath, adding to complete medium, mixing uniformly, removing supernatant after centrifugation, resuspending in complete medium, and culturing at 35-40 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The first digestive solution comprises 0.25% trypsin-EDTA. The centrifugation is performed at 800-1200 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 6-10 minutes. The mesenchymal stem cell primary cell is primary cell of bone marrow-derived mesenchymal stem cell, primary cell of autologous adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cell or primary cell of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell. The primary cells of mesenchymal stem cell are obtained by (a) extracting bone marrow solution with syringe containing 500 U/ml heparin in phosphate buffered saline (PBS), diluting with PBS containing 20 U/ml heparin, adding to lymphocyte separation solution with a specific gravity of 1.073 g/ml, purifying mononuclear cell population by centrifugation, washing with DMEM culture solution to obtain a precipitate, suspending the precipitate in serum-free culture solution, inoculating at a density of 5x 106 cells/ml, culturing at 35-40 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide, after first 3 days of culture, changing liquid for first time, removing nonadherent cells, changing culture solution once every 3 days, and obtaining primary cells of bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells by co-culturing for 6 days, (b) extracting autologous fat particles excluding visible microvessels and muscle tissue, washing with PBS, cutting to less than 1 mm3, adding second digestive solution, after digestion, collecting filtrate, neutralizing using low-sugar DMEM medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum, centrifuging, recovering precipitate, adding serum-free medium to precipitate at a density of 1x 106 cells/ml, incubating at 35-40 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide, after first 3 days, changing medium, removing nonadherent cells, changing medium once every 3 days, and obtaining primary cells of autologous adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells by co-culturing for 6 days, the second digestive solution is serum-free DMEM containing 0.1% collagenase type I and 0.05% trypsin, or (c) cutting umbilical cord to length of greater than 15 cm, washing with physiological saline, removing blood stain with 2 times saline, removing blood vessels and outer membrane, stripping Wharton's jelly, cutting into 0.5-1 mm3, applying the Wharton's jelly to Petri dish, adding serum-free complete medium, incubating at 35-40 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide and obtaining primary cells of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells by culturing for 7-10 days. The cryopreservation solution comprises 90% fetal bovine serum and 10% dimethyl sulfoxide.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).)

TI Aqueous composition useful in fire extinguishers,            comprises aqueous solution comprising potassium salt,            film forming agent comprising fluorinated silane or its            derivative, foaming agent comprising a non-ionic            surfactant and water.
AU YAN H
   XU J
   CHO C M
   TAN A Y X
AE AGENCY SCI TECHNOLOGY & RES (SGST-C)
GA 201852494U
AB    NOVELTY - Aqueous composition comprises: aqueous                solution comprising at least one potassium salt;                film forming agent comprising a fluorinated silane                or its derivative; at least one foaming agent                comprising a non-ionic surfactant; and water.
   USE - The composition is useful in fire                extinguishers (claimed) and in fire-fighting and                anti-burning system that is capable of                extinguishing fires effectively in harsh conditions                (hot and cold climates) and in sprinkler systems,                cargo ships or freighters, car fire extinguishers,                fire extinguisher balls and kitchen                extinguishers.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: is more stable so that it can                be diluted with tap water and/or sea water; can be                stored below 0 degrees C without freezing; is                non-toxic and has low viscosity mixture with                excellent fire extinguishing performance, which can                be used to extinguish fires arising from polar                and/or non-polar organic solvent; provides a                cooling effect due to the intrinsic thermal                properties of a cold material which cools a fire                when injected into the flames; is decomposed to                release potassium ions, hydrogen, water and carbon                dioxide in fire, thus helping to extinguish the                fire; and is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing aqueous composition comprising adding                foaming agent to aqueous solution and adding film                forming agent to the mixture.

The step                  of adding the foaming agent comprises adding an                  alkoxylated ammonium alkyl sulfate and the glycol                  based non-ionic surfactant in an amount which                  forms 0.05-13 wt.% of the aqueous composition and                  the glycol based non-ionic surfactant is ethylene                  glycol. The step of adding the film forming agent                  comprises adding the film forming agent                  comprising the fluorinated silane or its                  derivative in an amount which forms 2-13 wt.% of                  the aqueous composition. The method further                  comprises stirring the mixture at 25-35 degrees C                  for 2 hours to 6 hours after adding the film                  forming agent comprising the fluorinated silane                  or its derivative and diluting the mixture with                  water.
   EXAMPLE - 100 g Potassium formate was added to 500 ml of                tap water and stirred to dissolve completely.                Subsequently, 0.5 g ammonium lauryl ether sulfate,                100 ml ethylene glycol and 100 ml fluorinated                silane were added. The resulting suspension was                stirred for 4 hours, and then diluted for further                use.

TI Human limbal epithelial stem cell culture media used to culture limbal stem cells used in human transplantation, comprises isoproterenol, human epidermal growth factor, and antibiotic, where media does not contain cholera toxin.
AU DENG S X
   GONZALEZ S
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
GA 201849455K
AB    NOVELTY - Human limbal epithelial stem cell culture media comprises isoproterenol, human epidermal growth factor (EGF), and an antibiotic, where media does not contain cholera toxin and/or dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO).
   USE - Human limbal epithelial stem cell culture media used to culture LSCs used in human transplantation for patients suffering from limbal stem cell deficiency.
   ADVANTAGE - The human limbal epithelial stem cell culture media minimizes risk of cross-contamination and toxic effects when LSCs are transplanted to the patient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for growing human limbal epithelial stem cells, which involves disposing cells in a cell culture media at a temperature and under carbon dioxide concentrations sufficient for the human limbal epithelial stem cells to grow; and
   (2) a transport system for cultured limbal stem cells, which comprises transport container, a screw-cap that forms a tight seal with the transport container, a ring attached to the cap adapted to stabilize cultured limbal stem cells (cLSCs), and a cell transport media comprising Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium: nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/F12), 4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid (HEPES), human EGF, human AB serum, hydrocortisone, penicillin-streptomycin, gentamicin, and amphotericin B, where media does not contain cholera toxin and/or dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The cell culture media comprises 20% (v/v) human serum and 0.5-2 mu g/mL isoproterenol, 0.4-10ng/mL, preferably not more than 0.4ng/mL human epidermal growth factor (EGF), 0.4-5 mu g/mL hydrocortisone, and one of penicillin, streptomycin, gentamicin or amphotericin B. Preferred Components: The cell culture media further comprises insulin, transferrin, selenite, progesterone, and putrescine. The cell culture media further comprises a denuded amniotic membrane and human limbal epithelial stem cells. Preferred Conditions: The human limbal epithelial stem cells are disposed within limbal tissue explant. The media is free of xenobiotic supplements. The human limbal epithelial stem cells growing in media comprise greater than 3% p63a bright cells, preferably 3% p63a bright human limbal epithelial stem cells. The preponderance of the human limbal epithelial stem cells do not differentiate after being disposed in the media. The differentiation state of the cell is observed by observing cell morphology. The differentiation state of cell is observed by observing expression of a polypeptide in the human limbal epithelial stem cells. The temperature for disposing the cells in a cell culture media is 35-38 degrees C, and the carbon dioxide concentration is 4-10%.
   ACTIVITY - Immunosuppressive. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable examples given.

TI Preparing water-absorbent resin having high liquid absorption rate involves obtaining aqueous solution containing unsaturated acrylic monomer and crosslinking agent, and mixing blowing agent particles and/or blowing agent solution.
AU GU Z
   HU J
   XU L
   XU K
   HANG Y
AE YIXING DANSON TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YIXI-Non-standard)
GA 2018491987
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing water-absorbent resin having high liquid absorption rate involves obtaining an aqueous solution containing an unsaturated acrylic monomer and a crosslinking agent, mixing the blowing agent particles and/or the blowing agent solution with the aqueous solution of the unsaturated acrylic monomer and the crosslinking agent at a temperature of -10-120 degrees C and a surface activity of 1000 ppm by mass of the unsaturated monomer in the presence of the agent, preparing a foaming agent particle monomer solution having a particle diameter of 0.05-50 micrometer, and dissolving 0-10 pts. wt. foaming agent in 100 pts. wt. monomer solution, blowing agent releases one or more of nitrogen, carbon dioxide, oxygen, ammonia or steam gas by chemical and/or physical reaction to cause aqueous monomer solution is polymerized to form a porous gel-like crosslinked polymer at a temperature of 200-200 degrees C.
   USE - Method for preparing water-absorbent resin having high liquid absorption rate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare water-absorbent resin having high liquid absorption rate does not affect the liquid absorption property of water-absorbent resin, and has high absorption rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The hydrogel cross-linked polymer is granulated into gel particles during or after the polymerization, and drying, fine granulating, and sieving to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The aqueous solution of the unsaturated acrylic monomer and the crosslinking agent is a water-soluble salt of acrylic acid and/or acrylic acid as a main component mixed solution of ingredients. The foaming agent forms a turbid liquid in a monomer aqueous solution at a temperature of 10-120 degrees C. The surfactant and foaming agent before the unsaturated acrylic monomer and crosslinking agent aqueous solution are prepared, and dispersed to an aqueous monomer solution. The surfactant and the foaming agent are added to the initiator before, during or after the addition of the monomer solution. The foaming agent is produced during polymerization, during pulverization of the hydrogel polymer, or during heating and drying, and gas forms a porous structure crosslinked polymer. Preferred Composition: The amount of the foaming agent is 0.005-20 wt.% based on the unsaturated monomer. Preferred Components: The crosslinking agent is selected from compound containing multple vinyl groups in the molecule, vinyl compound in the molecule, and one compound capable of reacting with a carboxyl group on the unsaturated monomer, or one or more compounds having multiple functional groups capable of reacting with a carboxyl group on the unsaturated monomer in the molecule. The foaming agent is selected from ammonium carbonate, ammonium hydrogencarbonate, sodium carbonate, sodium hydrogencarbonate, calcium carbonate, and potassium carbonate. , potassium hydrogencarbonate, magnesium carbonate, diguar acid ester, azobisisobutyronitrile, azobisisoheptanenitrile, azobisisovaleronitrile, azodicarbonamide, azobisisobutylphosphonium hydrochloride, diisopropyl azodicarboxylate, dinitrosopentamethylenetetramine, p-toluenesulfonylhydrazide, p-toluenesulfonyl ketone oxime, 4,4'-oxobisbenzenesulfonyl hydrazide, 2,4 toluene disulfonyl hydrazide, sodium percarbonate, hydrogen peroxide, and benzoyl peroxide. The surfactant is selected from anionic surfactant, nonionic surfactant, cationic surfactant, amphoteric surfactant, fluorine-based surfactant, and organometallic surfactant.

PN US2018163009-A1; US10227467-B2
TI Manufacturing low density foamed article e.g. cushion or cushioning element for article of footwear involves combining in extruder a molten thermoplastic ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer with chemical blowing agent and supercritical fluid.
AU BAGHDADI H A
AE NIKE INC (NKEN-C)
GA 201846689D
AB    NOVELTY - A low density foamed article is manufactured by combining in extruder (12) a molten thermoplastic ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer with greater than or equal to 15 wt.% chemical blowing agent and supercritical fluid to form a mixture where supercritical fluid contains 0.1-5 wt.% supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) of 0.1-4 wt.% supercritical nitrogen gas (N2), injecting mixture into a mold (28) at injection pressure of 110-207 MPa, and molding mixture to form low density foamed article where mold consists of porous tool, and low density foamed article has density of less than or equal to 0.3 g/cm3.
   USE - Manufacture of low density foamed article e.g. cushion or cushioning element for article of footwear, protective equipment or clothing (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Low density foamed article has desired cushioning degree. Wiping increases rapid mixing of supercritical fluid additive and polymeric material in extruder resulting in distribution of relatively finely divided, isolated regions of supercritical fluid additive in polymeric material immediately upon injection.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the injection molding system used in manufacturing low density foamed article.
   Extruder (12)
   Polymer processing screw (14)
   Control system (25)
   Mold (28)
   Melting device (39)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Supercritical fluid is 0.5-3 wt.% supercritical CO2 or 0.4-2.5 wt.% supercritical N2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: Porous tool is porous metal or metal alloy. Porous tool is an insert having dimensions of mold cavity. Porous tool consists of inserts spaced within the mold cavity.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Molten thermoplastic ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer contains 25-50 wt.% vinyl acetate. Mixture contains 3-12 wt.% chemical blowing agent and crosslinker. Preferred Parameters: Molten thermoplastic ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymers have melt index of 0.5-50 g/10 minute (at 190 degrees C, 8.7 kg). Porous tool has pore size of 3-20 mu m.
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A83 (Clothing, footwear.)
MC A04-G07; A08-B01; A11-B; A11-B06A; A12-C00C; A12-C02; A12-C04; A12-S04A2

TI Oxygen carrier/carbon carrier based biomass chemical chain gasification device, has first cyclone separator connected to calcination reactor, where bottom of calcination reactor and gasification reactor are formed into hydrogen dioxide.
AU LIAO Y
   LIU G
   WU Y
   MA X
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201835132D
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an air reactor (1), two isolators (5-1,5-2), a calcination reactor (3) and a gasification reactor (2) that are orderly connected together by a pipeline. A top of the calcination reactor is connected with an inlet of a first cyclone separator (4-3). An exhaust pipeline of the first cyclone separator is connected to the calcination reactor through a valve. An inlet of the gasification reactor and a second cyclone separator (4-2) are connected with the second isolator. Bottom of the calcination reactor and the gasification reactor are formed into hydrogen dioxide by the pipeline.
   USE - Oxygen carrier/carbon carrier based biomass chemical chain gasification device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device performs biomass chemical chain gasification absorption with calcium oxide chain gasification process so as to improve energy utilization efficiency of the biomass and effectively separate carbon dioxide generated by a chemical chain gasification process reaction.

PT P
PN EP3296736-A1; WO2018054905-A1; EP3296736-B1; US2019219556-A1; ES2742830-T3
TI Method of measuring energy content of gas such as liquefied natural gas flowing in pipeline, involves correlating measured speed of sound and relative permittivity to derive energy content of gas.
AU BUEKER O
   BUKER O
AE SP TECH RES INST SWEDEN (SPTE-Non-standard)
   SP TECH RES INST SWEDEN (SPTE-Non-standard)
   SWEDEN SP TECH RES INST (SWSP-Non-standard)
GA 201821840C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring the speed of sound in the gas by using a speed sensor (42). The relative permittivity of the gas is measured by using a guided wave radar (GWR) sensor (44). The measured speed of sound and relative permittivity are correlated to derive the energy content of the gas. The energy content of the gas is derived by using the measured pressure and temperature parameters of the gas. The measured speed of sound, relative permittivity, and carbon dioxide content are correlated to derive the energy content of the gas.
   USE - Method for measuring energy content of gas such as liquefied natural gas (LNG), natural gas (NG), compressed natural gas (CNG) or biogas flowing in pipeline using guided wave radar (GWR) device.
   ADVANTAGE - A robust correlation measurement technique is provided and the lifecycle cost is reduced. The method allows the energy content of LNG to be extracted from existing flow and level measurements made during the transportation of LNG, such as during loading and/or offloading of carrier vessels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for measuring the energy content of a gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of a system for determining the energy content of a gas.
   Pipeline (40)
   Speed sensor (42)
   GWR sensor (44)
   Pressure sensor (48)
   Temperature sensor (50)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); W06 (Aviation, Marine and Radar Systems)

TI Device for conversion of first hydrocarbon-based feedstock comprising aromatic compounds, has fractionation train extracting cut comprising benzene, one cut comprising toluene, and ethylbenzene from feedstock, xylenes separating unit for treating cut, and isomerization unit for treating raffinate.
AU FEUGNET F
   JOLY J
AE IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
GA 202246000A
AB    NOVELTY - Device has a fractionation train suitable for extracting cut comprising benzene, one cut comprising toluene and one cut comprising xylenes and ethylbenzene from the first hydrocarbon-based feedstock (2). A xylenes separating unit treats the cut comprising xylenes and ethylbenzene and produces an extract comprising para-xylene and a raffinate comprising ortho-xylene, meta-xylene and ethylbenzene. An isomerization unit treats the raffinate and for produces an isomerate enriched in para-xylene, which is sent to the fractionation train. A pyrolysis unit treats a second hydrocarbon-based feedstock for producing pyrolysis effluent comprising hydrocarbon-based compounds of 6-10 carbon atoms partially feeding the hydrocarbon-based feedstock. A pyrolysis gas (32) comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The reverse water gas shift (RWGS) reaction section treats the pyrolysis gas and produces an RWGS gas enriched in carbon monoxide and in water.
   USE - Device for conversion of a first hydrocarbon-based feedstock comprising aromatic compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The device increases the production of aromatics, and notably of para-xylene, produced by a process of pyrolysis of hydrocarbon-based compounds, ensures conversion of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide which are byproducts of the pyrolysis, and total amount of the carbon i.e. biobased carbon thus being able to be upgraded.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device has a fractionation train suitable for extracting cut comprising benzene, one cut comprising toluene and one cut comprising xylenes and ethylbenzene from the first hydrocarbon-based feedstock. A xylenes separating unit treats the cut comprising xylenes and ethylbenzene and produces an extract comprising para-xylene and a raffinate comprising ortho-xylene, meta-xylene and ethylbenzene. An isomerization unit treats the raffinate and for produces an isomerate enriched in para-xylene, which is sent to the fractionation train. A pyrolysis unit (13) treats a second hydrocarbon-based feedstock for producing pyrolysis effluent comprising hydrocarbon-based compounds of 6-10 carbon atoms partially feeding the hydrocarbon-based feedstock. A pyrolysis gas comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The reverse water gas shift (RWGS) reaction section treats the pyrolysis gas and produces an RWGS gas enriched in carbon monoxide and in water.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for the conversion of a first hydrocarbon-based feedstock comprising aromatic compounds which involves: (a) fractionating the first hydrocarbon-based feedstock in a fractionation train in order to extract cut comprising benzene;
   (b) separating the cut comprising xylenes and ethylbenzene in xylenes separating unit and producing an extract comprising para-xylene and a raffinate comprising ortho-xylene, meta-xylene and ethylbenzene;
   (c) isomerizing the raffinate in an isomerization unit and producing an isomerate enriched in para-xylene;
   (d) sending the isomerate enriched in para-xylene to the fractionation train;
   (e)treating a second hydrocarbon-based feedstock in a pyrolysis unit to produce pyrolysis effluent comprising hydrocarbon-based compounds containing from 6-10 carbon atoms feeding;
   (f) treating the pyrolysis gas in an RWGS reverse water gas shift reaction section to produce an RWGS gas enriched in carbon monoxide and in water;
   (g) treating the RWGS gas enriched in carbon monoxide and in water in a fermentation reaction section to produce ethanol; and
   (h) converting the ethanol into a mixture of aromatic and paraffinic compounds in an aromatization reaction section, mixture of aromatic and paraffinic compounds feeding the first hydrocarbon-based feedstock.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of conversion device.
   Feedstock separation unit (1)
   First hydrocarbon based feedstock (2)
   Aromatics (3)
   Pyrolysis unit (13)
   Pyrolysis gas (32)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fermentation reaction section is suitable for recycling the carbon dioxide present at the fermentation outlet to the inlet of the RWGS reaction section. The fractionation train is suitable for extracting 9-10C monoaromatics cut from the first hydrocarbon-based feedstock. The device further comprises a disproportionating unit suitable for partly treating the cut comprising toluene and for producing a xylene-enriched cut, which is recycled to the isomerization unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pyrolysis unit comprises reactor used under operating conditions of an absolute pressure of 0.1-0.5 MPa and hourly space velocity (HSV) of 0.01-10 hour-1, a temperature of 400-1000degrees Celsius, a zeolite catalyst comprising zeolite socony mobil-5 (ZSM-5), ferrierite, zeolite beta, zeolite Y, mordenite, ZSM-23, ZSM-57, EU-1 and ZSM-11, and preferably the catalyst is a catalyst comprising only ZSM-5. The RWGS reaction section comprises reactor used under operating conditions of temperature of 400-800degrees Celsius, pressure of 0.1 and 10 MPa, space velocity of the gas entering the reactor of 5000-20000 mL/gcata/h, catalysts based on iron or alkali metals. The aromatization reaction section comprises reactor used under operating conditions of a catalyst based on ZSM-5 zeolite containing metal compounds, reaction temperature of 300-500degreesC, pressure of 0.1-1.0 MPa, weight hourly space velocity (WHSV) of 0.5-3.5 hour-1, the WHSV being the ratio of the mass flow rate of feedstock to the weight of catalyst used.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN KR2222623-B1
TI Producing elastic filler in artificial turf for reducing carbon and collecting fine dust, comprises first mixing composition, second mixing first mixture for injection into extruder, and introducing second mixture into extruder and cooling.
AU SIN S D
AE JMCGR CO LTD (JMCG-Non-standard)
GA 202123554E
AB    NOVELTY - Producing elastic filler comprises (a) first mixing the composition; (b) second mixing the first mixture so as to be suitable for injection into an extruder; (c) introducing second mixture into extruder and extruding; and (d) cooling and cutting extruded filler. The elastic filler is based on biomass synthetic resin containing diatomaceous earth that is porous and has function of adsorbing, decomposing, and washing formaldehyde and volatile organic compounds in air and contributing to prevention of global warming by suppressing increase in total amount of carbon dioxide. The elastic filler composed of styrene-ethylene-butadiene-styrene or polyvinyl butyral polymer containing biomass synthetic resin. The mixture comprises 5-20 pts. wt. diatomaceous earth powder in the form of 150 mesh size, 20-60 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 0.5-10 pts. wt. inorganic pigment, 0.5-3 pts. wt. process oil, 0.5-3 pts. wt. stearic acid emulsifier, 0.1-3 pts. wt. colorant, and 0.1-3 pts. wt. anti-aging agent.
   USE - The elastic filler is useful in artificial turf for reducing carbon and collecting fine dust (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The elastic filler has excellent adhesion between filler particles, elasticity and elastic recovery, dimensional stability, and durability, has low thermal conductivity and thermal expansion coefficient, excellent melting point, prevents agglomeration due to low shrinkage and thermal shock resistance under high temperature in summer, and hardly stiffens at low temperature in winter, prevents phenomenon of breaking, creates environmentally friendly playground that can be used all year round, constructs environmentally friendly turf field with complex function of reducing carbon dioxide and fine dust, provides pleasant environment to the user, and contributes in stadium to prevention of warming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing elastic filler comprises (a) first mixing the composition; (b) second mixing the first mixture so as to be suitable for injection into an extruder; (c) introducing the second mixture into an extruder and extruding; and (d) cooling and cutting the extruded filler. The elastic filler is based on biomass synthetic resin containing diatomaceous earth that is porous and has the function of adsorbing, decomposing, and washing formaldehyde and volatile organic compounds in the air and contributing to the prevention of global warming by suppressing the increase in the total amount of carbon dioxide. The elastic filler composed of styrene-ethylene-butadiene-styrene or polyvinyl butyral polymer containing biomass synthetic resin. The mixture comprises 5-20 pts. wt. diatomaceous earth powder in the form of 150 mesh size, 20-60 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 0.5-10 pts. wt. inorganic pigment, 0.5-3 pts. wt. process oil, 0.5-3 pts. wt. stearic acid emulsifier, 0.1-3 pts. wt. colorant, and 0.1-3 pts. wt. anti-aging agent. The biomass synthetic resin is biomass polyethylene containing 20-25 wt.% biomass complex. The biomass polyethylene is composed of polymer containing linear low-density polyethylene and medium-density polyethylene in weight ratio of 4:1. The diatomaceous earth is composed of freshwater diatomaceous earth containing 93 wt.% silica, 2.7-4.1 wt.% aluminum oxide, 0.5-1.5 wt.% ferric oxide, 0.1-0.3 wt.% magnesium oxide, 0.05-0.6 wt.% calcium oxide, 0.01-0.08 wt.% potassium oxide, 1.5-3.5 wt.% sodium oxide, and 0.05-0.17 wt.% titanium oxide. The inorganic pigment is environmentally friendly by using composition of an elastic filler, which is composed of iron oxide or cobalt oxide, and is easy to collect fine dust and reduce carbon.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for producing elastic filler useful in artificial turf for reducing carbon and collecting fine dust (claimed). (Drawing includes non-English language text).
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); Q41 (Road, rail, bridge construction (E01))

PT P
PN CN111925979-A
TI Constructing animal and cell model for artemisinin to improve inflammation of rosacea comprises constructing animal model and cell model for artemisinin to improve the inflammatory phenotype of rosacea.
AU YUAN X
   LI J
   ZHANG Y
AE GUIZHOU PROVINCIAL PEOPLES HOSPITAL (GZPH-C)
GA 2020B8358F
AB    NOVELTY - Constructing animal and cell model for artemisinin to improve the inflammation of rosacea comprises (a) taking a 7-week-old BALB/C mouse as an animal model of artemisinin to improve the inflammatory phenotype of rosacea, giving artemisinin for seven consecutive days, shaving the back skin of mice, injecting LL37 polypeptide, (b) purifying amino acid sequence by HPLC and identifying by mass spectrometry, culturing in complete DMEM medium, dividing into groups, (c) plating HaCaT cells in a 24-well plate containing cell slides, culturing cells, dividing into groups, adding LL37, stimulating and (d) setting the groups, placing chemotactic chamber in the 24-well plate, planting HUVECs cells in the chamber, adding artemisinin, washing 24-well plate, fixing and staining with 0.5% crystal violet, washing and drying the cell, observing the cells attached to the lower membrane of the cell under a microscope and photographing and counting.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing animal and cell model for artemisinin to improve the inflammation of rosacea.
   ADVANTAGE - The artemisinin: improves the erythema, histopathological changes and the increase of interleukin (IL)-1 beta , IL-6, TNF alpha and TLR2 in mice with rosacea inflammation induced by LL37; reduces the infiltration of CD4+ T cells, macrophages and neutrophils in the skin lesions and inhibits the expression of immune cell-related chemokines (CXCL10, CCL20, CCL2 and CXCL2) in the skin lesions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Constructing animal and cell model for artemisinin to improve the inflammation of rosacea comprises (a) taking a 7-week-old BALB/C mouse as an animal model of artemisinin to improve the inflammatory phenotype of rosacea, giving artemisinin at 200mg/kg per day for seven consecutive days, shaving the back skin of mice on day 5, injecting LL37 polypeptide into back skin subcutaneously on day 6-7 to induce rosacea inflammation, (b) constructing a cell model for artemisinin to improve the inflammatory phenotype of rosacea by purifying the amino acid sequence by HPLC and identifying by mass spectrometry, placing HaCaT cells in a 30mm petri dish, culturing in a complete DMEM medium containing 10% serum at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator until the cells reach 90% confluence, dividing into blank group-no treatment, artemisinin group-artemisinin (50 mu M/l) stimulation for 12 hours, LL37 group-LL37(4 mu M/l) stimulation for 12 hours, artemisinin+LL37 group-Artemisinin (50 mu M/l), stimulating for 1 hour, adding LL37 (4 mu M/l), stimulating for 12 hours at the same time, collecting RNA afterwards, storing at -80 degrees C until analysis, (c) plating HaCaT cells in a 24-well plate containing cell slides with 10% serum-containing DMEM complete medium at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator, culturing the cells to reach 90% confluency, dividing into groups as above, adding LL37, stimulating for 5 hours, taking the cell slide, performing immunohistochemical staining to detect and analyze HaCaT cells P65 Nuclear translocation of protein and (d) placing 500 mu l 1640 complete medium containing 10% serum into a 24-well plate, setting the blank group, artemisinin group, LL37 artemisinin+LL37 group, adding 4 mu M/l LL37 to the 24-well plate, placing chemotactic chamber (8mm aperture) in the 24-well plate, planting HUVECs cells (2x 103) in the chamber, adding artemisinin to the chambers of the artemisinin group and the artemisinin+LL37 group, placing the 24-well plate in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide cell incubator, taking out after 24 hours, washing 24-well plate, fixing and staining with 0.5% crystal violet for 30 minutes, washing and drying the cell, observing the cells attached to the lower membrane of the cell under a microscope and photographing and counting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The construction of a cell model also includes a model of artemisinin affecting the migration of human umbilical vein endothelial cells (HUVECs): seeding HUVECs into a 6-well plate, setting the blank group, artemisinin group, LL37 group, artemisinin+LL37 group, in the LL37 group and artemisinin+LL37 group, scratching the bottom of the dish with a sterile 1ml Tip head, when the cells have grown to 100% confluence, rinsing each well with phosphate buffered saline (PBS), adding 1640 complete medium containing 10% serum, and in the artemisinin group and artemisinin, adding Artemisinin to LL37 group, adding LL37 to LL37 group and artemisinin+LL37 group, culturing HUVECs cells in an incubator at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours and evaluating the cell migration ability by the scratch spacing of a randomly selected area; also includes histology: fixing the back skin of mice with rosacea inflammatory lesions with paraffin, cutting into 4 mu m thick, staining the sections with hematoxylin and eosin, observing histomorphology under a standard light microscope; also includes skin tissue immunofluorescence: fixing 8mm frozen sections with paraformaldehyde for 15 minutes at room temperature, using 5% normal donkey serum and 0.2% TritonX Closed at room temperature for 1 hour, treating the skin sections with anti-CD4, F4/80, LY6G and CD31 antibodies at 4 degrees C overnight, staining with AlexaFluor488-labeled anti-goat IgG antibody at room temperature for 1 hour and capturing the images with ZeissAxioScopeA1; also includes real-time PCR: using TRIzol reagent to isolate total RNA from cells or skin lesions, using PrimeScriptRT kit, transcribing 2 mu g RNA into complementary DNA (cDNA), performing qPCR with iTaqUniversalSYBRGREEN Supermix (BioRad, California, USA) on an Applied Biosystems 7500 machine (Life Technologies) and also includes data statistics: expressing the data in the experiment in the form of mean plus minus standard error (SEM), using graphPadPrism5 software to map, using the t test to detect the difference between the two groups of data, adopting the two-sided test standard.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P14 (Animal management and care (A01K, L, M).)
MC B04-E01; B04-E99; B04-F02; B04-N02; B04-P01A; B11-C07A; B11-C07B3; B11-C08E; B11-C08E2; B11-C08F8; B11-C11; B12-K04E1; B12-K04F; B14-C03; B14-L01; B14-L03; B14-L06; C04-E01; C04-E99; C04-F02; C04-N02; C04-P01A; C11-C07A; C11-C07B3; C11-C08E; C11-C08F8; C11-C11; C12-K04E1; C12-K04F; D05-H09; D05-H18B; D05-H99; P14-A05

PT P
PN CN111847478-A; CN111847478-B
TI Comprehensive treatment for converting condensed liquid into aqueous ammonia, involves stripping ammonia, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen sulfide from condensate, pressurizing liquid phase, desulfurizing, and three-stage condensing.
AU LI H
   ZHANG X
   GUO R
   WANG Y
   ZHOU Q
   LI Q
   LI X
   XIA K
   SHEN K
   WANG L
   LIAO T
AE WUHAN KELIN CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2020B26889
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive treatment for converting a condensed liquid involves feeding (S1) a conversion condensate into an atmospheric stripping tower, heat-exchanging, feeding a bottom of the tower with a steam, stripping and separating ammonia, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen sulfide in the condensate, entering (S2) a treated acid gas at a top of the tower into a liquid separation tank, removing ammonia in the acid gas, pressurizing (S3) the liquid phase, washing ammonia, desulfurizing, extracting the gas phase from the side of the tower, passing (S4) the extracted gas phase into a three stage condensers, entering (S5) the gas phase at the top of the tertiary condensing cooler into a multi-effect acid tail gas purifier to remove hydrogen sulfide in crude ammonia, mixing (S6) the treated ammonia gas with water from an ammonia circulation pump, cooling to 40 degrees C, and entering into an ammonia absorption tank to recycle the ammonia gas and water to produce 20% aqueous ammonia.
   USE - Comprehensive treatment for converting condensed liquid into aqueous ammonia.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly and stable, and solves the problem of sulfur in ammonia and ammonia in acid gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Comprehensive treatment for converting a condensed liquid involves feeding (S1) a conversion condensate into an atmospheric stripping tower, heat-exchanging, feeding a bottom of the tower with a steam, stripping and separating ammonia, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen sulfide in the condensate, and returning purified water at the bottom of the tower to the conversion device, entering (S2) a treated acid gas at a top of the tower into a liquid separation tank of the stripping tower, heat-exchanging and cooling, passing the separated gas phase through a water scrubber to remove ammonia in the acid gas, passing through the liquid separation tank and desulfurization recovery device, and cooling the bottom liquid phase in a raw water buffer tank, pressurizing (S3) the liquid phase with a feed water pump, entering into a pressurizing stripper by cold feeding through a level (L1) condensing cooler and heat feeding through a raw water-purified water heat exchanger, passing the steam into the bottom of the stripping tower, reusing purified water at the bottom or discharging out of the device after heat-exchange and cooling, introducing into a water washing tower after the top gas phase is condensed and separated to wash ammonia, desulfurize and recover, extracting the gas phase from the side of the tower, passing (S4) the extracted gas phase into a primary condenser, passing the gas phase at the top of the primary condenser through a secondary condensing cooler and entering into a secondary condenser, passing the gas phase at the top of the secondary condenser to a tertiary condensing cooler and a tertiary condenser, cooling the liquid phase at the bottom of primary and secondary condensing coolers, combining the liquid phase of the tertiary condenser, sending to a raw material water tank, entering (S5) the gas phase at the top of the tertiary condensing cooler into a multi-effect acid tail gas purifier from bottom to top to remove hydrogen sulfide in crude ammonia, separating the liquid, removing hydrogen sulfide in the ammonia gas further in a fine desulfurization reactor, mixing (S6) the treated ammonia gas with water from an ammonia circulation pump, cooling to 40 degrees C with a cooler, and entering into an ammonia absorption tank to recycle the ammonia gas and water to produce 20% aqueous ammonia.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: In process (S5), the multi-effect acid tail gas purifier is divided into upper and lower alkali absorption reactors, and the gas passes through the purifier from bottom to top for alkali catalytic absorption by reacting hydrogen sulfide with 30-40% sodium hydroxide solution as an absorbent under the action of a metal-supported catalyst to produce sodium hydrosulfide. Preferred Conditions: In process (S1), the operating pressure of the atmospheric stripping tower is 0.3-0.4 MPa and bottom temperature of the tower is 135-145 degrees C. In process (S2), operating pressure of the liquid separation tank is 0.2-0.25 MPa and operating temperature is 80-95 degrees C, operating temperature of the washing tower is 40-60 degrees C, and water-washing adopts a forced circulation water-washing. In process (S3), operating pressure of the pressurized stripping tower is 0.5-0.6 MPa, bottom operating temperature is 155-165 degrees C, top operating temperature is 110-130 degrees C, and sideline production temperature is 150-155 degrees C, and stripping is performed by single-tower pressurized belt sideline extraction. In process (S4), operating pressure of the primary condensing cooler is 0.3-0.5 MPa and operating temperature is 130-140 degrees C, operating pressure of the secondary condensing cooler is 0.25-0.3 MPa and operating temperature is 100-110 degrees C, and operating pressure of the tertiary condensing cooler is 0.15-0.2 MPa and operating temperature is 40-50 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The desulfurization reactor is filled with a fine desulfurizing agent which is zinc oxide or a composite oxide fine desulfurizing agent capable of desulfurizing hydrogen sulfide in gas ammonia to less than 0.1 ppm. The alkali absorption catalyst is a composite metal mesh structure comprising alloy-loaded active components such as molybdenum, titanium, and manganese.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The multi-effect acid exhaust gas purifier is made of an austenitic stainless steel plate (S30408).

PT P
PN CN111239275-A
TI Determining the total phosphorus content of soil            or sludge comprises taking dry soil sample or dry            sludge sample and 5 to 15 mL concentrated nitric acid            into the digestion vessel, and heating.
AU ZHENG Y
   HU Z
   ZHANG X
   FU W
   ZHU R
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 2020517595
AB    NOVELTY - Determining the total phosphorus content of                soil or sludge comprises taking 0.1 to 0.4 g dry                soil sample or dry sludge sample and 5 to 15 mL                concentrated nitric acid into the digestion vessel,                and heating for 5-20min at 150-200 degrees C. The                solution with deionized water is diluted, then 1 to                10 mL of the solution is used in a centrifuge tube,                and 5 to 10 mL of sodium hydroxide solution and 0.5                to 1.0 g of calcium oxalate powder and deionized                water are added. The centrifuge tube is placed in a                constant temperature water bath shaker at 20-60                degrees C for vigorous stirring for 5-30 minutes,                and the supernatant of the above solution is                filtered, 0.1-2 mL of the above filtrate and                1.0-5.0 mL of sulfuric acid solution is put into                the headspace sample bottle, which is filled with                0.1-1mL potassium permanganate solution. After                capping, the two solutions in the headspace bottle                are mixed thoroughly to make it react                completely.
   USE - Method for determining the total phosphorus                content of soil or sludge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to determine the total                phosphorus content of soil or sludge, which                simplify the operation process and greatly reduce                the impact of interfering substances, does not use                organic extractants, and avoid secondary pollution.                The method is particularly suitable for the                analysis of large batch samples of soil and sludge                related research units.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Determining the total phosphorus content of                soil or sludge comprises taking 0.1 to 0.4 g dry                soil sample or dry sludge sample and 5 to 15 mL                concentrated nitric acid into the digestion vessel,                and heating for 5-20min at 150-200 degrees C. The                solution with deionized water is diluted, then 1 to                10 mL of the solution is used in a centrifuge tube,                and 5 to 10 mL of sodium hydroxide solution and 0.5                to 1.0 g of calcium oxalate powder and deionized                water are added. The centrifuge tube is placed in a                constant temperature water bath shaker at 20-60                degrees C for vigorous stirring for 5-30 minutes,                and the supernatant of the above solution is                filtered, 0.1-2 mL of the above filtrate and                1.0-5.0 mL of sulfuric acid solution is put into                the headspace sample bottle, which is filled with                0.1-1mL potassium permanganate solution. After                capping, the two solutions in the headspace bottle                are mixed thoroughly to make it react completely.                The filtrate is replaced with phosphate standard                solutions of known different concentrations and                placed in the headspace bottle. After capping and                mixing, the headspace bottle containing the                standard solution to be tested is placed in the                headspace sampler, the headspace sampler operating                conditions and gas chromatograph operating                conditions are set, then headspace gas                chromatography analysis and detection is performed,                based on the obtained carbon dioxide                chromatographic peak area signal value and a known                concentration of phosphate standard solution. The                corresponding relationship between the results of a                standard curve. The headspace bottle containing the                sample to be tested is placed in the headspace                sampler, using the same headspace sampler operating                conditions and gas phase and the carbon dioxide                chromatographic peak area signal value of the                sample is recorded. The carbon dioxide                chromatographic peak area signal value is compared                with the standard curve and the total phosphorus                content in the sample is calculated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of                  the concentrated nitric acid is 1.0 to 14.5                  mol/L. The sodium hydroxide concentration is 1.0                  to 5.0 mol/L. The sulfuric acid solution is 1.0                  to 5.0 mol/L, and the concentration of the                  potassium permanganate solution is 0.05 to 0.5                  mol/L.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC J04-B01C5; J04-C01; J04-C02; S03-E09C
IP G01N-030/02; G01N-030/06; G01N-030/86
