PN WO2020071631-A1; KR2020037943-A; KR2193010-B1
TI Bioelectrochemical reactor for biological gas conversion process, has electrical power supply device which is connected to anode and cathode through external circuits to supply power to anode and cathode chambers.
AU KIM J R
   SONG Y E
   IM C H
   KIM C M
   IM H S
   BEAK J Y
   RAE K
   LIM C
   LIMHYUNSUNG
   BAEK J Y
AE UNIV PUSAN NAT IND COOP FOUND (UYPU-C)
GA 202028469P
AB    NOVELTY - The reactor has an anode chamber (10) in which electrons and hydrogen ions are generated through electrolysis. A cathode electrode is provided in which a microorganism is inoculated, and an inert gas is continuously supplied to apply a reduction potential to the cathode electrode. An ion exchange membrane (30) is located between the anode chamber and the cathode chamber (20) to transfer hydrogen ions generated in the anode chamber to the cathode chamber. An electrical power supply device (40) is connected to the anode (11) and cathode (21) through external circuits to supply power to the anode and cathode chambers, and to apply a reduction potential or a reduction current to the cathode. The anode chamber and the cathode chamber are continuously supplied with the inert gas or C1 gas to maintain an absolute anaerobic condition. The inert gas is nitrogen or argon gas. The C1 gas is carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, or mixed gas.
   USE - Packed bed type or a trickle bed type bioelectrochemical reactor for biological gas such as carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, or mixed gas conversion process.
   ADVANTAGE - The bioelectrochemical reactor improves the conversion rate of C1 gas, the growth rate of microorganisms and the production rate of volatile acids by providing electrons necessary for bioconversion reaction. The recovery rate of carbon is improved through a conversion process, and the emission of greenhouse gas carbon dioxide is reduced. The bioelectrochemical reactor searches for a new strain capable of receiving electrons from an electrode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The anode chamber or cathode chamber includes a gas supply unit for supplying the inert gas or C1 gas into the anode chamber and the cathode chamber. A gas injection port injects the gas supplied by the gas studying into the anode and cathode chambers. A gas outlet discharges gas or gas generated through electrolysis or gas supplied through the gas inlet. An oxygen removal trap removes trace oxygen present in the inert gas supplied through the gas supply unit. The microorganism inoculated to the cathode is a pure microorganism or a complex microorganism. The electrons and hydrogen ions transferred from the anode chamber to the cathode chamber are transferred to the microorganisms in the cathode chamber through an electron transport medium. The anode or cathode is made of a carbon material, a metal material or a polymer material in the form of a sponge, carbon brush or particle. The ion exchange membrane is a hydrogen ion exchange membrane, a cation exchange membrane, an anion bridge membrane, a bipolar membrane, or a ceramic separation membrane. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a biological gas conversion process method using a bioelectrochemical reactor involves inoculating microorganisms in the cathode of the bioelectrochemical reactor. The inert gas or C1 gas is continuously supplied to the anode and cathode chambers of the bioelectrochemical reactor to maintain absolute anaerobic conditions. The C1 gas conversion process is performed by adding electricity to the bioelectrochemical reactor. The microorganism used in the process is a pure microorganism or a composite microorganism, and the microorganism is separated into a pure single microorganism in which foreign substances or impurities are removed by adding zero valent iron (ZVI) to the culture medium.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the bioelectrochemical reactor. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Anode chamber (10)
   Anode (11)
   Cathode chamber (20)
   Cathode (21)
   Ion exchange membrane (30)
   Electrical power supply device (40)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The microorganism reacts by receiving electrons through the cathode to generate volatile acids, and the volatile acids are acetate, butyrate, iso-butyrate, propionic acid, and caproic acid, valeric acid, iso-valerate, or salts. The electron transport medium is Neutral Red, Methyl Viologen, Methyl Benzyl, 2,6-Anthraquinone , 2-Hydroxy-1,4-naphthoquinone or Hydroquinone.

PN CN108948535-A
TI Preparation of antistatic sole material, involves drying coconut fiber, crushing, treating using supercritical carbon dioxide, carbonizing, and cooling to obtain carbonized coconut shell fiber, mixing with carbon fiber, and adding water.
AU RAO R
   RAO K
AE JIESHOU SHENGZE SHOE IND CO LTD (JIES-Non-standard)
GA 2018A0513R
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of antistatic sole material involves drying coconut fiber, crushing, treating using supercritical carbon dioxide, carbonizing, and cooling to obtain carbonized coconut shell fiber, mixing with carbon fiber, adding deionized water, ultrasonically treating, adding borate surfactant, heating, stirring and reacting, cooling, filtering, washing, and drying, adding to a ball-milling tank, adding hydrochloric acid solution, ball milling, adding amino acid, ball-milling, adding deionized water, diluting into suspension, adding to the stirred and reacted product, reacting, filtering, washing drying, adding absolute ethyl alcohol, ultrasonically treating, adding gum Arabic solution, heating, ultrasonically processing, filtering, drying, grinding to obtain product, adding polypropylene resin, lubricant, and antioxidant to mixer, adding plasticizer, mixing, adding plasticizer, ground product and foaming agent, granulating, drying, and injection molding.
   USE - Preparation method of antistatic sole material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of antistatic sole material with excellent performance, impact resistance, tensile strength and mechanical performance, low surface resistivity, and improved dispersibility and compatibility in the substrate, and which satisfies the requirements of antistatic shoes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of antistatic sole material involves (1) placing 5-10 pts. wt. coconut fiber in a freeze-drying box, drying for 3-5 hours, crushing to length of 50-500 nm, treating using supercritical carbon dioxide for 15-18 minutes, carbonizing for 3-4 hours at 350-370 degrees C and at a rate of 2-3 degrees C/minute, and cooling to room temperature to obtain carbonized coconut shell fiber, (2) mixing the carbonizing coconut shell fiber and 5-10 pts. wt. carbon fiber, adding deionized water, ultrasonically treating at 65-70 degrees C and 50-60 Hz for 40-50 minutes, adding 2-3 pts. wt. borate surfactant, heating at 95-105 degrees C, stirring and reacting at 400-500 rpm for 40-60 minutes, cooling, filtering and washing with deionized water 3-4 times, and drying in a vacuum drying box at 100-105 degrees C, (3) adding 10-15 pts. wt. rectorite to a ball-milling tank, adding 4-6% hydrochloric acid solution, ball milling into slurry with pH of 4-5, adding 3-5 pts. wt. amino acid, continuously ball-milling for 2-3 hours, placing in an environment with relative humidity of 70-75%, at 60-65 degrees C for 4-5 days, adding deionized water, diluting into suspension having rectorite content of 10-15%, adding to the step (2), reacting at 100-110 degrees C for 2-3 hours at 700-800 rpm, filtering, washing and drying, (4) adding product obtained in the step (3) to volume of 20-30 times of absolute ethyl alcohol, ultrasonically treating for 10-20 minutes, adding 10-20 pts. wt. gum Arabic solution, heating at 65-70 degrees C, continuously ultrasonically processing for 20-30 minutes, placing in a vacuum device, leaving still for 5-10 hours at -0.8 to -0.9 MPa at 40-50 degrees C, filtering and drying at 60-70 degrees C, and grinding by 250-400-mesh sieve, and (5) adding 80-100 pts. wt. polypropylene resin, 5-8 pts. wt. lubricant, and 3-5 pts. wt. antioxidant to a mixer, adding 3-5 pts. wt. plasticizer, mixing for 10-15 minutes at 100-105 degrees C, adding 3-5 pts. wt. plasticizer, the product obtained in the step (4) and foaming agent, transferring to an open mill machine for opening, transferring to a granulator, and granulating at 120-125 degrees C to obtain pellets, drying the pellets in a drying box at 90-95 degrees C for 3-3.5 hours, transferring to an injection molding machine, and injection molding at 135-140 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (1), the supercritical carbon dioxide processing pressure is 3-5 MPa. Preferred Property: The particle size of rectorite is 20-300 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lubricant is polyethylene wax. The antioxidant is tris(2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl)phosphite (antioxidant 168). The plasticizer is epoxidized soybean oil. The foaming agent is 4,4'-oxydibenzenesulfonyl hydrazide foaming agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The concentration of the gum Arabic solution in step (4) is 10-15%. Preferred Process: The gum Arabic solution is prepared by adding gum Arabic to deionized water, stirring at a speed of 300-400 rpm, heating at 90-95 degrees C, increasing the speed to 600-700 rpm, stirring for 10-15 minutes, cooling to 70-75 degrees C, and heat-preserving for 1-2 hours.

PN CN108794932-A
TI Preparing modified nanocalcium carbonate useful for PVC wood-plastic plate comprises e.g. conveying nano calcium carbonate slurry to ultrasonic agitator, heating, adding surface treatment agent, ultrasonic stirring, drying, and pulverizing.
AU SHI B
AE QINZHOU BAOYE NIXING POTTERY FACTORY (QINZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018927155
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified nanocalcium carbonate comprises (i) adding quicklime and hot water to the digestion reactor, aging, diluting to obtain lime emulsion, delivering the lime emulsion to the carbonation reaction tower, introducing carbon dioxide into the carbonization reaction, adding a crystal form controlling agent and dispersing, carbonizing,and controlling the specific surface area of calcium carbonate to be 20-30 m2 /g to obtain nanometer calcium carbonate slurry, (ii) the crystal control agent composed of phytic acid and ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid disodium saltdihydrate, (iii) conveying the nano calcium carbonate slurry to the ultrasonic agitator, heating to 60-80 degrees C, adding a surface treatment agent comprises oleic acid diethanolamide borate and dodecafluoroheptylpropyl cage silsesquioxane, and ultrasonic stirring for 20-40 minutes to obtain modified nano calcium carbonate slurry, and (iv) drying the modified nano calcium carbonate slurry, and pulverizing.
   USE - The modified nanocalcium carbonate is useful for PVC wood-plastic plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The PVC wood-plastic plate has high strength, toughness, strong shock resistance, good heat resistance, strong antibacterial property, heat resistance, good stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing modified nanocalcium carbonate comprises (i) adding quicklime and hot water to the digestion reactor for digestion reaction, removing impurities, allowing to stand, aging, and diluting to obtain lime emulsion, delivering the lime emulsion to the carbonation reaction tower, introducing carbon dioxide into the carbonization reaction, adding a crystal form controlling agent and a dispersing agent during the carbonization process, carbonizing to a pH of 6.5-7.5, stopping the introduction of carbon dioxide, controlling the specific surface area of calcium carbonate to be 20-30 m2 /g to obtain nanometer calcium carbonate slurry, (ii) the crystal control agent composed of phytic acid and ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid disodium saltdihydrate, adding in an amount of 0.5-1.5 wt.% of the dry basis of calcium carbonate, the dispersing agent is polyoxyethylene glyceryl ether monostearate and isocetyl stearate in an amount of 1.5-3% by weight of the dry basis of calcium carbonate, (iii) conveying the nano calcium carbonate slurry to the ultrasonic agitator, heating to 60-80 degrees C, adding a surface treatment agent comprises oleic acid diethanolamide borate and dodecafluoroheptylpropyl cage silsesquioxane, adding amount of surface treatment agent is 1.5-3.5 wt.% based on the dry basis of the calcium carbonate, and ultrasonic stirring for 20-40 minutes to obtain modified nano calcium carbonate slurry, and (iv) drying the modified nano calcium carbonate slurry, and pulverizing. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) polyvinyl chloride wood plastic board comprising 100 pts. wt. polyvinyl chloride resin, 10-20 pts. wt. ethylene-vinyl acetate resin, 40-60 pts. wt. bamboo powder, 30-50 pts. wt. modified nanometre calciumcarbonate, 3-5 pts. wt. asbestos fiber, 4-6 pts. wt. shell powder, 3-5 pts. wt. polyacrylamide, 1-3 pts. wt. polyether modified dimethyl silicone polymer, 3-5 pts. wt. composite foaming agent, 1-3 pts. wt. coupling agent, 2-4 pts. wt. stabilizer, 1-3 pts. wt. lubricant, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. antibacterial agent and 3-5 pts. wt. processing auxiliary agent; and
   (2) preparing PVC wood-plastic panel comprising (i) mixing the above material to obtain modified mixture, (ii) adding PVC resin and EVA resin into high speed mixer, heating up to 100-130 degrees C, adding modified mixed material, composite foaming agent, coupling agent, stabilizing agent, lubricant, antibacterial agent and processing auxiliary agent, uniformly stirring to obtain mixed material, (iii) conveying the above mixture material to a conical twin-screw extruder, extruding at 160-180 degrees C, cooling and setting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The dispersant is polyoxyethylene glyceryl monostearate and isocetyl stearate at mass ratio of 1-2:1. The mass ratio of phytic acid to ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid disodium saltdihydrate in the crystal form controlling agent is 1-2:1. The mass ratio of oleic acid diethanolamide borate and dodecafluoroheptylpropyl cage silsesquioxane in the surface treatment agent is 1:1-3. The composite foaming agent comprises sodium hydrogencarbonate and sucrose rosinate at mass ratio of 1-3:1. The coupling agent is polyetherimide andrare earth coupling agent. The lubricant is Polyethylene wax and castor oilpolyoxyethylene ether. The processing auxiliary agent is nonylphenol polyoxyethylene ether and polyaminopropylmethylsilsesquioxane at mass ratio of 1-3:1.

PN CN108620531-A
TI Wear-resistant sand casting mold comprises a bottom mold, wear-resistant sleeve mold, core mold and multiple sand boxes, where the sand boxes are horizontally set, and arranged in the vertical direction.
AU ZHAO F
   XU D
   HE T
   DAI H
   ZHU G
   DAI Y
   JI M
   SHI Y
AE JIANGSU XINGYUAN POWER STATION METALLURG (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201880275X
AB    NOVELTY - Wear-resistant sand casting mold comprises a bottom mold (1), wear-resistant sleeve mold (2), core mold (3) and multiple sand boxes (4). The sand boxes are horizontally set, and arranged in the vertical direction. The bottom mold is located in the bottommost sand box. The core mold is located in the wear-resistant sleeve mold, and the shape of the core mold is the same as the shape of the inner cavity of the wear-resistant sleeve mold. The wear-resistant sleeve is located directly above the bottom mold and the two are fitted together. The bottom of the mandrel is provided with a casting model. The core mold has a solid structure and the cross-sectional shape in the vertical direction is the same as the cross-sectional shape of the wear-resistant sleeve mold.
   USE - Used as wear-resistant sand casting mold.
   ADVANTAGE - The mold improves the good product rate of the wear sleeve and improves the service life of the wear sleeve.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for casting of wear-resistant sand casting mold, comprising (i) applying a release agent into the outer layer of the bottom mold, the wear-resistant sleeve mold and the core mold, placing the bottom on the ground and bring the sprue model up, placing the sand box with the grille mesh on the bottom mold and the sand box, filling with chrome ore, filling in the sea sand, compacting and making holes in the sea sand, passing carbon dioxide gas, turning the flask over the side with the grille mesh faces down, placing the wear sleeve on the bottom mold, and fixing the other flask above the bottommost sand box, filling the sand box with chrome ore and filling with sea sand, making vent holes in the sea sand and pass carbon dioxide gas, and placing the pouring riser positioning block in the uppermost sand box, and filling the chrome ore and sea sand in turn and blowing the carbon dioxide into the pores; (ii) removing the bolts between all the sand boxes, and separating the sand box from the sand box, removing the bottom mold, coating the wear-resistant sleeve mold with the release agent, and taking out the riser positioning block, brushing the paint three times on the inner wall of the formed cavity, and layering the Baume degree of the paint is 65-70 BE, igniting the baking parting surface, placing a filter above the cavity of the bottom mold in the flask, applying the sealant to the bottom of the core mold, and bolting all the sand boxes to the original position; (iii) smelting and casting, introducing the argon gas into the mold, discharging the air in the mold, allowing argon to fill the mold, allowing the molten steel to protect from secondary oxidation during casting, making the heat-insulating heating agent when filling the riser, adding 0.3 kg into each riser, and adding the plastic bag into the riser when casting to the top of the pouring riser; and (iv) casting for half an hour to one hour, loosening the bolt between the flasks, allowing the work-piece to freely expand, removing the formed work-piece from the flask, cleaning the surface of the work-piece, blasting the inner and outer surfaces of the work-piece, using the cutting at normal temperature to cut the riser; and (v) roughing the two ends of the work-piece, performing ultrasonic inspection on both end faces of the work-piece, testing hardness, and finishing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the wear-resistant sand casting mold.
   Bottom mold (1)
   Wear-resistant sleeve mold (2)
   Core mold (3)
   Multiple sand boxes (4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The casting model is a cross-shaped structure, and the flared ridges for buffering are provided on the four sides of the casting model. The sand boxes are square frame structures that are connected up and down. The bottom of the bottommost flask is provided with a grid. The flasks are fixedly connected by bolts, and the outer walls of all the flasks are provided with bolt support seats for the bolts to pass vertically. The outer walls of the flask are horizontally provided with a plurality of carrying handles.

PN CN108164418-A
TI One-step synthesizing ethyl methyl carbonate along with 1,2-propanediol from propylene oxide comprises reacting one step specific synthetic carbonate route 2-methyloxirane.
AU SHI L
   YU Y
AE UNIV SHENYANG CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (UYHG-C)
GA 201849205P
AB    NOVELTY - One-step synthesizing ethyl methyl carbonate along with 1,2-propanediol from propylene oxide comprises reacting one step specific synthetic carbonate route 2-methyloxirane of (I), where the raw material is propylene oxide, carbon dioxide, methanol and ethanol. The mol ratio product of 1,2-propanediol with carbonic ester is 1:1. The 1,2-propanediol is a bulk chemical that relies heavily on imports in China, and it can effectively produce a large amount of 1,2-propanediol by using this route. The one-step reaction catalyst is composite basic catalyst having mesoporous and micro-porous structure. The catalyst is of the general formula X/S. The X is a lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, caesium, beryllium, magnesium, calcium, strontium, barium, lanthanum, cerium, yttrium, zirconium, chromium, tungsten, manganese, iron, ruthenium, cobalt, rhodium, iridium, palladium, platinum, copper, silver, zinc, boron, aluminum and gallium oxide.
   USE - The method is useful for one-step synthesizing ethyl methyl carbonate along with 1,2-propanediol from propylene oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has good stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - One-step synthesizing ethyl methyl carbonate along with 1,2-propanediol from propylene oxide comprises reacting one step specific synthetic carbonate route 2-methyloxirane+ carbon dioxide + methanol + ethanol - 1,2-propanediol + acetacetato di etile of formula (I), where the raw material is propylene oxide, carbon dioxide, methanol and ethanol. The mol ratio product of 1,2-propanediol with carbonic ester is 1:1. The 1,2-propanediol is a bulk chemical that relies heavily on imports in China, and it can effectively produce a large amount of 1,2-propanediol by using this route. The one-step reaction catalyst is composite basic catalyst having mesoporous and micro-porous structure. The catalyst is of the general formula X/S. The X is a lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, caesium, beryllium, magnesium, calcium, strontium, barium, lanthanum, cerium, yttrium, zirconium, chromium, tungsten, manganese, iron, ruthenium, cobalt, rhodium, iridium, palladium, platinum, copper, silver, zinc, boron, aluminum and gallium oxide, S is the carrier of different topological structure comprises a topological structure of the electromagnetic topology, MOR, MWW, FAU, MFI, FER, BEA molecular sieve and oxides of conventional silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, zirconium dioxide, magnesium oxide and zinc oxide in a carrier S, molecular sieve carrier. The hydrogenous molecular sieve firstly is passes through ion exchange. The Bronsted acid is poisoned by metal ions, so that it does not have Bronsted acidity. The ion exchanger poison of molecular sieve is lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, caesium, calcium, hydrochloride, sulfate or nitrate of iron, magnesium, copper. The other molecular sieves and carriers are prepared in a similar manner, uniformly labeled as M-S, where the M comprises lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, caesium, calcium, iron, magnesium, copper. The supported alkali metal oxide catalyst having a mesoporous and microporous structure carrier, and a preparation method of the microporous molecular sieve carrier comprises (i) adding a certain mass of M-S carrier to a certain temperature, stirring volume and concentration of the acidic solution, subjecting to suction filtration, washing and drying to obtain a dealuminated M-S-DAl carrier, (ii) adding the M-S-DAl carrier prepared by a certain mass process 1 into a certain temperature, soaking the concentration and a certain volume of the alkaline solution and subjecting to suction filtration, washing and drying to obtain a multi-stage pore M-S-DAl-DSi carrier and (iii) placing finally obtained by the process 2 into a muffle furnace and baking to obtain the carrier mark is M-meso-S and continuous reacting the fixed bed preparing methylethyl carbonate .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of the mesoporous microporous molecular sieve carrier, molecular sieve with different topological structures of the carrier comprises subjecting EMT, MOR, MWW, FAU, BEA, MFI or FER, where the acidic solution comprises ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, etc., preferably ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid. The amount of the acidic solution to be added must at least submerge all the molecular sieves, which may be 10-20 times, preferably 15 times the volume of the molecular sieve. The alkaline solution comprises sodium hydroxide and sodium carbonate; preferably sodium carbonate. The alkaline solution concentration is 0.05-2.0 mol/l. The amount of the alkaline solution to be added must at least submerge all molecular sieves, which may be 5-15 times, preferably 10 times, the volume of the molecular sieve. The preparation method of the microporous molecular sieve carrier has a mesoporous pore size distribution of 4-34 nm obtained by the M-meso-S carrier, and the specific pore diameter is related to the concentration of the alkaline solution used. The supported alkali metal oxide catalyst having a mesoporous and microporous structure support by using a coprecipitation method or a dipping method: (i) coprecipitating with the alkaline solution includes, but are not limited to alkali metal and ammonium hydroxide, e.g. lithium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and ammonium hydroxide, carbonate, bicarbonate, formate and acetate of alkali metal, e.g. lithium, sodium carbonate, solution of potassium carbonate or mixed liquid can also be the alkaline solution. The liquid medium used for the alkaline substance solution is preferably water, but is not limited to water, (ii) adding amount of alkaline solution into submerge the carrier is 10-20 times of carrier volume, preferably 15 times, where the coprecipitation control pH value of the solution is 10-14, preferably pH is 11; co-precipitation process water bath temperature is room temperature to -99 degrees C, preferably 70 degrees C, the aging temperature is room temperature of -80 degrees C; the aging time is 1-20 days, preferably at 60 degrees C, aging time is 5 days, the catalyst washing medium is preferably water, but is not limited to water, liquid medium is 2-10 times of the volume of the catalyst, preferably 2-5 times, water washing time decided according to the pH of the wash effluent, catalyst drying temperature is room temperature to -150 degrees C and the time is 1 hours to -10 days, preferably 120 degrees C, drying for 3-5 hours, the roasting temperature is 150-800 degrees C, the time is 1-50 hours, preferably 500-700 degrees C, time is 30 hours, the content of metal X in the load type catalyst of the general formula of the composite hole structure is 0.1-50% of the weight of the carrier, (iii) subjecting active component of load catalyst pore structure of composite precursor is preferably aluminum nitrate, potassium nitrate, caesium nitrate, magnesium nitrate, calcium nitrate, barium nitrate, strontium nitrate, lanthanum (iii) salt, iron(iii) nitrate, manganese(ii) nitrate, aluminum chloride, potassium chloride, caesium chloride, magnesium chloride, calcium chloride, barium chloride, strontium chloride, lanthanum(iii) chloride, iron(iii) chloride, manganese(iii) chloride, aluminum sulfate, potassium sulfate, caesium sulfate, magnesium sulfate, calcium sulfate, lanthanum(iii) sulfate hydrate, iron(iii) sulfate and manganese(iii) sulfate, (iv) carrying out co-dipping method for dipping method and fractional dipping method, where the drying temperature is room temperature to 150 degrees C for 1 hour to 20 days, the roasting temperature is 550-650 degrees C for 1-50 hours and the composite hole structure of load type catalyst of the general content of the metal X is 0.1-50 % of the weight of the carrier and (v) carrying out the preferred embodiment, the active component of load catalyst pore structure of composite precursor is preferably aluminum nitrate, potassium nitrate, caesium nitrate, magnesium nitrate, calcium nitrate, barium nitrate, strontium nitrate, lanthanum (iii) salt, iron(iii) nitrate, manganese(ii) nitrate, aluminum chloride, potassium chloride, caesium chloride and magnesium chloride. The fixed bed continuous reaction specific scheme is as follows: feeding the propylene oxide and the carbon dioxide at a molar ratio of 0.1:1-10:1 at a reaction pressure from normal pressure is 10 MPa, and simultaneously pumping the liquid raw material methanol and ethanol into the feed, two molar ratio 1:5-5:1 in the presence of the above catalyst conditions, the next reaction to synthesize ethyl methyl carbonate, the space velocity is 0.1-30 h-1, the reaction temperature is 50-250 degrees C. The catalyst is various multifunctional composite base catalysts protected in the present invention. The amount of the catalyst is 0.1-3 wt.% of the mass of the raw material, the selectivity of the product ethyl methyl carbonate is less than or equal to 25%, and the yield is less than or equal to 20%. The fixed bed continuous reaction process of preparing ethyl methyl carbonate, the mol ratio preferred condition is propylene oxide:carbon dioxide: methanol: ethanol is 1:1:3:2, the airspeed is 0.5-5 h-1, the reaction temperature is 78-130 degrees C. The kettle-specific reaction scheme is as follows: the reaction pressure is 0.1-10MPa, the molar ratio propylene oxide and carbon dioxide is 0.1:1-10: 1, the mol ratio of methanol to ethanol is 1:5-5:1, in the presence of catalyst to prepare methyl carbonate, the reaction kettle in sealed 25-150 degrees C reacting for 0.5-10 hours; the catalyst is alkaline catalyst, the dosage of catalyst is 0.1-10 wt.% of mass of the raw materials.

PT P
PN WO2022050375-A1
TI Producing polypropylene resin extruded foam particles by melt-kneading composition containing resin mixture containing polypropylene-based resin having branched structure and foaming agent, extruding by die through region having lower pressure and liquid phase, and shredding composition in region.
AU NAKAYAMA K
   AMANO M
   SHIMODA M
AE KANEKA CORP (KANF-C)
GA 2022383399
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing polypropylene resin extruded foam particles, involves (a) a melt-kneading step of melt-kneading a composition containing a resin mixture containing a polypropylene-based resin having a branched structure and a foaming agent using an extruder equipped with a die, (b) an extrusion step of passing the die through a region having a lower pressure and a liquid phase than the extruder to extrude the composition, (c) a shredding step of shredding the composition in the region, and (d) obtaining polypropylene-based resin extruded foam particles. The foaming agent is carbon dioxide gas, and the amount of the foaming agent used is 0.5-7 pts. wt. with respect to 100 pts. wt. of the resin mixture. The temperature A of the composition immediately after entering the die is from the melting point of the resin mixture + 0 degrees C to the melting point of the resin mixture + 30 degrees C. The temperature B of the liquid phase is 20-90 degrees C.
   USE - The method is used for producing polypropylene resin extruded foam particles for producing a polypropylene resin foamed molded product (claimed). The extruded foam particles are used for obtaining a polypropylene-based resin in-mold foam molded article having excellent shape arbitraryness, cushioning property, light weight, and heat insulating property. The extruded foam particles are used in the applications of automobile interior members, cushioning materials, packaging materials, and heat insulating materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method inexpensively provides polypropylene resin extruded foam particles that exhibit an excellent moldability. The polypropylene-based resin extruded foam particles have excellent moldability can be provided at low cost. The extruded foam particles are used for obtaining a polypropylene-based resin in-mold foam molded article having excellent shape arbitraryness, cushioning property, light weight, and heat insulating property. The polypropylene-based resin extruded foam particles have excellent moldability even by the underwater concrete (UWC) method using carbon dioxide gas. The method has the advantages of low production cost and low environmental load as compared with the case of using an organic foaming agent. The foam particles provide excellent compressive strength, excellent fusion property, and/or a beautiful surface to the molded product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing polypropylene resin extruded foam particles, involves (a) a melt-kneading step of melt-kneading a composition containing a resin mixture containing a polypropylene-based resin having a branched structure and a foaming agent using an extruder equipped with a die, (b) an extrusion step of passing the die through a region having a lower pressure and a liquid phase than the extruder to extrude the composition, (c) a shredding step of shredding the composition in the region, and (d) obtaining polypropylene-based resin extruded foam particles. The foaming agent is carbon dioxide gas, and the amount of the foaming agent used is 0.5-7 pts. wt. with respect to 100 pts. wt. of the resin mixture. The temperature A of the composition immediately after entering the die is from the melting point of the resin mixture + 0 degrees C to the melting point of the resin mixture + 30 degrees C. The temperature B of the liquid phase is 20-90 degrees C, and the value T is 10-25 calculated by (Temperature A of the composition immediately after introduction into the die)-(Melting point)+(amount of foaming agent used)x 2.5-((Temperature A-Temperature B)x 0.08) (I). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing a polypropylene resin foamed molded product, involving molding polypropylene resin extruded foam particles produced by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The polypropylene-based resin having a branched structure is obtained by a method of melt-kneading a polypropylene-based resin having no branched structure, a conjugated diene compound, and a radical polymerization initiator. Preferred Properties: The pressure of the liquid phase with respect to the composition in the region is 0.05-0.60 MPa.G. The bulk density of the polypropylene-based resin extruded foam particles is 40-300 g/l. The open cell ratio of the polypropylene-based resin extruded foam particles is 10% or less. The melting point of the resin mixture is 130-165 degrees C. The melt tension of the polypropylene-based resin having the branched structure is 3-20 cN. The melt flow rate of the polypropylene-based resin having a branched structure at 230 degrees C is 0.5-20 g/10 minutes.
   EXAMPLE - Typical composition comprised of: 99.8 parts mass polypropylene resin (resin A) having a branched structure; 0.2 part mass talc; and 3 parts mass carbon dioxide gas.

PN US2020289973-A1; WO2020185507-A1; CN113490538-A; WO2020185507-A8; IN202147045244-A; US11471816-B2
TI Wet pollutant capturer and mobilizer for moving            housing of polluted gaseous substance, has container            whose exit port is mounted either on container cap or            on container, and configured to allow treated gaseous            substance to exit container.
AU SALEHPOOR K
AE SALEHPOOR K (SALE-Individual)
   SALEHPOOR K (SALE-Individual)
   SALIPOEL K (SALI-Individual)
GA 2020910005
AB    NOVELTY - The mobilizer has a secondary distributor (11)                that is in communication with an exit port of a                primary distributor (4). The secondary distributor                has an inlet port and multiple exit ports. Multiple                perforated disks (16) are mounted inside the                pollutant capturer and mobilizer. The perforated                disks are configured to divide a polluted gaseous                substance into smaller housings and flow the                polluted gaseous substance within a liquid                pollutant capturing component (PCC) (18) to capture                the polluting substances from the smaller housings.                An exit port (21) for each PCC container (17) is                mounted either on a PCC container cap (9) or on the                PCC container and configured to allow treated                gaseous substance to exit the PCC container. A                drain for each PCC container is configured to allow                drainage of the liquid PCC from the PCC container.                A drain isolating valve (60) is provided for each                drain.
   USE - Wet pollutant capturer and mobilizer for                moving housing of polluted gaseous substance from                one location to another location to remove carbon                dioxide and undesired gas in air in building,                atmospheric air, atmospheric air in building                blow-down demolishing site, atmospheric air nearby                active volcano and wild fire, exhaust from vehicle,                and facilities in combat negative health,                environmental, and economic impact of pollution on                communities.
   ADVANTAGE - The safe and reliable operation of the                pollutant capturer and mobilizer is achieved,                convenient replacement or replenishment of the                pollutant capturing components is obtained and                convenient removal of the captured polluting                substances is achieved. The pollutant capturer and                mobilizer accelerates or decelerates the movement                of an atmospheric housing of polluted gaseous                substance to control the amount of rainfall at                desired locations, or move an atmospheric housing                of polluted gaseous substance from multiple                locations towards multiple other locations to                disrupt a hurricane or formation of a hurricane, or                disrupt a tornado or formation of a tornado, or                disrupt heat and cold waves or formation of heat                and cold waves. The use of the produced crude oil                as the liquid pollutant capturing component in the                wet embodiment of the pollutant capturer and                mobilizer offers an opportunity to individual                members of the public community to contribute to                minimizing the amount of carbon dioxide that is                released into the atmosphere or the amount of                carbon dioxide that exists in the atmosphere. The                pollutant capturer and mobilizer is a feasible,                economical, affordable, efficient, and simple                device used to mobilize and move an atmospheric                housing of polluted gaseous substance from one                location towards another location and capture                particulate polluting substances or water vapor or                fog or desired gaseous polluting substances from                the atmospheric housing of polluted gaseous                substance or capture polluting substances from a                housing of polluted gaseous substance that flows in                a chimney or stack or in a vehicle exhaust pipe,                without significant undesired environmental                impacts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a dry pollutant capturer and                mobilizer;
   (2) a method for capturing polluting                substances from polluted gaseous substance                utilizing liquid pollutant capturing component in                wet pollutant capturer and mobilizer; and
   (3) a method for capturing particulate                polluting substances from polluted gaseous                substance utilizing dry pollutant capturing                component.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the wet                pollutant capturer and mobilizer.
   Primary Distributor (4)
   PCC container cap (9)
   Secondary distributor (11)
   Perforated disk (16)
   PCC container (17)
   Liquid PCC (18)
   PCC container exit port (21)
   Drain isolating valve (60)

PN CN111607149-A
TI High heat resistance polyolefin foam material            comprises high-density polyethylene, ethylene-vinyl            acetate copolymer, trimethylolpropane trimethacrylate,            auxiliary agent, flame retardants, foaming agent and            light calcium carbonate powder.
AU WANG Q
AE WANG Q (WANG-Individual)
GA 202087231Y
AB    NOVELTY - High heat resistance polyolefin foam material                comprises 40-50 pts. wt. high-density polyethylene,                10-14 pts. wt. ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer,                4-6 pts. wt. trimethylolpropane trimethacrylate,                0.5-0.8 pts. wt. auxiliary agent, 0.2-0.3 pts. wt.                flame retardant, 0.4-0.5 pts. wt. foaming agent,                and 5-7 pts. wt. light calcium carbonate powder.                The polyolefin foam material is prepared by (i)                pouring high-density polyethylene and                ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer between the heated                rolls of open rubber mixing machine to melt, mixing                glue machine, adding trimethylolpropane                trimethacrylate into mixing system, extruding and                granulating to obtain pellets, (ii) directly                irradiating the pellets obtained in step (i) at                dose 100 kGy and (iii) mixing the pellets with                light calcium carbonate powder, auxiliary agent,                flame retardants and foaming agents, making into                sheet, placing in autoclave, introducing                supercritical carbon dioxide and quickly relieving                the pressure to obtain final product.
   USE - Used as high heat resistance polyolefin foam                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces foam material with                uniform cell size distribution and excellent heat                resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High heat resistance polyolefin foam material                comprises 40-50 pts. wt. high-density polyethylene,                10-14 pts. wt. ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer,                4-6 pts. wt. trimethylolpropane trimethacrylate,                0.5-0.8 pts. wt. auxiliary agent, 0.2-0.3 pts. wt.                flame retardant, 0.4-0.5 pts. wt. foaming agent,                and 5-7 pts. wt. light calcium carbonate powder.                The polyolefin foam material is prepared by (i)                heating the two rolls of the open rubber mixing                machine at 130 degrees C, then pouring the                high-density polyethylene and ethylene-vinyl                acetate copolymer between the two rolls to melt and                soften, softening the pellets, starting the mixing                glue machine, adding trimethylolpropane                trimethacrylate into the mixing system, kneading                for 4-5 minutes, extruding and granulating to                obtain pellets, (ii) directly irradiating the                pellets obtained in the step (i) at a dose of 100                kGy at room temperature using high-frequency and                high-voltage electron accelerator and (iii) mixing                the irradiated pellets with light calcium carbonate                powder, auxiliary agent, flame retardants and                foaming agents and placing them in flat vulcanizing                agent at 180 degrees C and 10 MPa pressure for                compression molding to form sheet, cutting, placing                in an autoclave, then introducing supercritical                carbon dioxide under a pressure of 12-15 MPa at                90-100 degrees C, maintaining the pressure for 20                minutes and quickly relieving the pressure to                obtain polyolefin foam material. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is also included for preparing high heat                resistance polyolefin foam material, comprising (i)                heating the two rolls of the open rubber mixing                machine at 130 degrees C, then pouring the                high-density polyethylene and ethylene-vinyl                acetate copolymer between the two rolls to melt and                soften, softening the pellets, starting the mixing                glue machine, adding trimethylolpropane                trimethacrylate into the mixing system, kneading                for 4-5 minutes, extruding and granulating to                obtain pellets, (ii) directly irradiating the                pellets obtained in the step (i) at a dose of 100                kGy at room temperature using high-frequency and                high-voltage electron accelerator and (iii) mixing                the irradiated pellets with light calcium carbonate                powder, auxiliary agent, flame retardants and                foaming agents and placing them in flat vulcanizing                agent at 180 degrees C and 10 MPa pressure for                compression molding to form sheet, cutting, placing                in an autoclave, then introducing supercritical                carbon dioxide, the pressure is 12-15 MPa, the                temperature is 90-100 degrees C, maintaining the                pressure for 20 minutes and quickly relieving the                pressure to obtain polyolefin foam material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The auxiliary agent is                  combination of 2-hydroxy-4-(octyloxy)benzophenone                  (ultraviolet absorber UV-531), pentaerythritol                  tetrakis(3-(3',5'-di-tert-butyl-4'-hydroxyphenyl)propionate)                  (antioxidant 1010) and tris-(2,4-di-tert-butyl                  phenyl)phosphate (antioxidant 168) in a mass                  ratio of 4:2:3. Preferred Method: The flame                  retardant is prepared by adding 55.8 g dimethyl                  methylphosphonate, 28.1 g ethylene glycol and                  11.2 g non-butyl tin oxide in a three-necked                  flask equipped with magnetic stirring, nitrogen                  protection device, thermometer and water trap,                  carrying out trans-esterification reaction at                  165-175 degrees C, removing the by-product                  methanol, and subjecting the obtained product to                  reduced pressure esterification condensation at                  140-160 degrees C to obtain final product.                  Preferred Method: The step (ii) uses speed 10                  kGy/time for multi-step irradiation.

TI Establishing cell model of insulin resistant H9c2            cells induced by sodium oleate comprises adding e.g.            hepes, culturing, inoculating H9c2 cell, incubating,            measuring optical density, adding specific antibody,            washing and quantifying data.
AU ZHENG X
   ZHANG Q
   YUAN P
   FU Y
   HOU Y
   GAO L
   WEI Y
   FENG W
AE UNIV HENAN CHINESE MEDICINE (HECO-C)
GA 2020164240
AB    NOVELTY - Establishing cell model of insulin resistant                H9c2 cells induced by sodium oleate comprises (a)                using cell culture H9c2 by adding sodium                bicarbonate, hepes, penicillin and streptomycin in                dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM), adding                fetal bovine serum, culturing, (b) screening                working concentration of sodium oleate, inoculating                H9c2 cell, providing blank cells and NC group                complete medium, adding                3-(4,5-dimethylthiazol-2-yl)-2,5-diphenyl                tetrazolium bromide (MTT) solution, incubating,                measuring optical density (OD) value and (c)                inoculating H9c2, removing culture medium, fixing                cells, permeabilizing, sealing, adding specific                antibody diluted in blocking solution with glucose                transporter type 4 (GLUT4), insulin receptor                substrate 1 (p-IRS-1), insulin receptor substrate 1                (IRS-1) and beta -tubulin, incubating, removing                unbound antibody, incubating, washing, scanning,                imaging and quantifying data under the Odyssey                bicolour infrared fluorescence imaging                system.
   USE - The method is useful for establishing cell                model of insulin resistant H9c2 cells induced by                sodium oleate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Establishing cell model of insulin resistant                H9c2 cells induced by sodium oleate comprises (a)                using cell culture H9c2 by adding 3.70 g/l sodium                bicarbonate, 3.6 g/l hepes, 100 U/ml penicillin and                100 g/ml streptomycin in dulbecco's modified eagle                medium (DMEM), adding 10% fetal bovine serum,                culturing at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide in                a carbon dioxide culture box with saturated                humidity, when the cell grows to 70-80% in the                logarithmic phase for experiment, (b) screening the                working concentration of sodium oleate to set up                blank medium control wells, normal group and sodium                oleate concentration gradient group using glucose                consumption test, inoculating H9c2 cell having a                density of 5x 104 cells/ml in the test well of the                96-well plate except for blank medium well,                providing blank cells and NC group complete medium                after adhering the cells the next day, providing                complete medium containing different molar                concentrations of sodium oleate to the sodium                oleate concentration gradient groups, where each                group has 6 replicate wells in parallel, measuring                the glucose content in the culture supernatant of                each well after 24 hour incubation in a carbon                dioxide incubator, adding 20 mu l 5g/l                3-(4,5-dimethylthiazol-2-yl)-2,5-diphenyl                tetrazolium bromide (MTT) solution in each well,                incubating for 4 hours, measuring the optical                density (OD) value of each measuring well, where                the glucose consumption rate of each group is                glucose content in blank wells-glucose content in                measured wells/OD valuex 100% and (c) setting up NC                and SO group in In-cell Western and three duplicate                wells for each protein, inoculating H9c2 at a                density of 5x 104 cells/ml in a 96-well plate                protected from light, giving NC group complete                culture medium and SO group containing complete                medium with different molar concentration of sodium                oleate, removing the culture medium after 24 hours                of medical intervention, fixing cells using 3.7%                formaldehyde for 20 minutes, permeabilizing 0.1%                Triton 5times in 5 minutes, sealing 5% skim milk                powder for 1.5 hours, adding the specific antibody                of the test antigen diluted in blocking solution                with glucose transporter type 4 (GLUT4), insulin                receptor substrate 1 (p-IRS-1), insulin receptor                substrate 1 (IRS-1) and beta -tubulin, incubating                at 4 degrees C for 18 hours, removing the unbound                antibody using a phosphate buffer solution                containing 0.02% Tween-20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20),                incubating corresponding fluorescent secondary                antibody for 1 hour in the dark, washing again to                remove unbound secondary antibody, scanning,                imaging and quantifying data under the Odyssey                bicolour infrared fluorescence imaging                system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The optimal                  concentration of sodium oleate in the step (b) is                  100-250 mu mol/l. The optimal concentration of                  sodium oleate in the step (c) is 150 mu                  mol/l.

TI Slow-release phoxim pesticide produced by preparing hydrotalcite precursor and naphthalene acetate intercalated hydrotalcite precursor, forming hydrophobic environment containing naphthalene groups and mixing with phoxim acetone solution.
AU GENG D
   DONG M
AE ZIBO VOCATIONAL INST (ZIBO-C)
GA 202002676K
AB    NOVELTY - Slow-release phoxim pesticide is produced by preparing hydrotalcite precursor and naphthalene acetate intercalated hydrotalcite precursor, forming hydrophobic environment containing large number of naphthalene groups between layers, mixing with phoxim acetone solution, the phoxim diffuses into hydrotalcite layer, obtaining phoxim/naphthalene acetate intercalated hydrotalcite, and adding into mixed solution of hydrogen peroxide, ethanol and water. The slow-release phoxim pesticide is: ((M2+)1-x(M3+)x(OH)2)x+(naphthaleneacetic acid (NAA)-)x(Phoxim)y.mH2O (I).
   USE - As slow-release phoxim pesticide.
   ADVANTAGE - The slow-release phoxim pesticide has a stable intercalation structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Slow-release phoxim pesticide is produced by preparing hydrotalcite precursor and naphthalene acetate intercalated hydrotalcite precursor, forming hydrophobic environment containing large number of naphthalene groups between layers, mixing with phoxim acetone solution, the phoxim diffuses into hydrotalcite layer, obtaining phoxim/naphthalene acetate intercalated hydrotalcite, and adding into mixed solution of hydrogen peroxide, ethanol and water. The slow-release phoxim pesticide is of formula: ((M2+)1-x(M3+)x(OH)2)x+(naphthaleneacetic acid (NAA)-)x(Phoxim)y.mH2O (I).
   x = 0.25-0.33;
   y = 0.04-0.05;
   m = 3-6, the m is number of crystal water molecules between layers;
   M2+ = divalent metal ion;and
   M3+ = trivalent metal ion.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the pesticide, which involves (a) preparing hydrotalcite precursor NO3-LDHs or Cl-LDHs with an interlayer anion of NO3- or Cl- and bivalent and trivalent metal ions of laminate in molar ratio of M2+/M3+ = 2-3, formulating soluble salt of M2+ and soluble salt of M3+ into salt mixed solution, the molar concentration of M2+ is 0.1-1 M and the molar concentration of M3+ is 0.05-0.5 M, taking sodium hydroxide as alkali solution at a concentration of 1-5 M, under room temperature N2 protection, using double-drop method to add salt mixed solution and alkaline solution to a four-necked bottle, adjusting pH to 6-10 with 0.1-5 M sodium hydroxide solution, crystallizing the obtained slurry at 60-90 degrees C for 12-72 hours, adding into centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 2500-3500 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 3-5 minutes, washing with carbon dioxide containing water until neutral, removing sample, drying at 50-90 degrees C for 20-48 hours, and obtaining a hydrotalcite precursor, the soluble salt of M2+ and M3+ is hydrochloride or nitrate, the types of soluble salts of M2+ and M3+ are same, (b) dissolving sodium naphthalene acetate in deionized water to remove carbon dioxide, adding the hydrotalcite precursor, heating under N2 protection to 50-70 degrees C, reacting with stirring for 12-48 hours, centrifuging at 2500-3500 rpm for 3-5 minutes, washing with carbon dioxide containing water until neutral and drying at 50-90 degrees C for 20-48 hours to obtain NNA-LDHs, (c) dissolving phoxim in acetone, adding the naphthaleneacetic acid intercalated hydrotalcite NNALDHs obtained in step (b), stirring under N2 protection at 420-45 degrees C for 12-48 hours, thoroughly washing the product with acetone, adding into centrifuge, centrifuging at 2500-3500 rpm for 3-5 minutes and drying phoxim/NNALDHs at 50-90 degrees C for 20-48 hours, and (d) adding phoxim/NNALDHs intercalated with phoxim/naphthylacetate obtained in step (c) to mixed solution of hydrogen peroxide, ethanol and water, ultrasonically processing for 15-60 minutes and drying at 50-70 degrees C for 5-10 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed solution of hydrogen peroxide, ethanol and water comprises 5-10 %mass, preferably 5 %mass hydrogen peroxide, 25-35 %mass, preferably 30 %mass ethanol and 60-70 %mass, preferably 65 %mass water.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M2+ = Mg2+, Ni2+ or Zn2+;and
   M3+ = Al3+.
   ACTIVITY - Pesticide.

PN CN109796630-A; CN109796630-B
TI Preparing secondary modified light calcium            carbonate useful for plastic products comprises e.g.            hydrating calcium oxide and water, adjusting the            concentration of calcium hydroxide, purifying and            carbonizing the compressed lime kiln gas.
AU DU N
   SUN Y
AE LUSHI GUOSONGLAI NANO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LUSH-Non-standard)
GA 201947429L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing secondary modified light calcium                carbonate comprises e.g. hydrating calcium oxide                and water at a mass ratio of 1:5, and using                prepared calcium hydroxide emulsion for impurity                removal and aging, adjusting the concentration of                calcium hydroxide concentrate after aging to                1.3-1.5 mol/l, adding a crystal form control agent                having a dry weight of calcium carbonate of                0.1-0.3%, and adjusting the temperature to 70-80                degrees C, conveying calcium hydroxide concentrate                to the carbonization kettle, purifying the mixture,                cooling, stirring, carbonizing the compressed lime                kiln gas at kiln gas flow rate of 1.8-2.0                m3/minutes.m3, where the volumetric content of                carbon dioxide in the kiln gas is 33-35%, starting                gelation process of the reaction system, adding                dispersing agent having a dry weight of calcium                carbonate of 0.1-0.3%, stirring and adjusting the                kiln gas flow rate to 1.0-1.2 m3/minutes.m3, when                the pH of the reaction system dropping to 7, and                stopping carbonization treatment.
   USE - The calcium carbonate is useful for plastic                products (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate: has high filling                capacity, high impact strength, high product gloss                with process ability, whitening and toughening                property. The method: reduces PVC product cost, is                simple and economical, has feasible process and                good economic benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing secondary modified light calcium                carbonate comprises (1) hydrating calcium oxide and                water at a mass ratio of 1:5, and using prepared                calcium hydroxide emulsion for impurity removal and                aging, (2) adjusting the concentration of the                calcium hydroxide concentrate after aging to                1.3-1.5 mol/l, adding a crystal form control agent                having a dry weight of calcium carbonate of                0.1-0.3%, and adjusting the temperature to 70-80                degrees C, (3) conveying calcium hydroxide                concentrate to the carbonization kettle, purifying                the mixture, cooling, stirring, carbonizing the                compressed lime kiln gas at kiln gas flow rate of                1.8-2.0 m3/minutes.m3, where the volumetric content                of carbon dioxide in the kiln gas is 33-35%,                starting the gelation process of the reaction                system, adding dispersing agent having a dry weight                of calcium carbonate of 0.1-0.3%, stirring and                adjusting the kiln gas flow rate to 1.0-1.2                m3/minutes.m3, and the volumetric content of carbon                dioxide in the kiln gas to 33-35%, when the pH of                the reaction system dropping to 7, and stopping the                carbonization treatment, (4) adding a calcium                carbonate dry weight of 1.4-1.6% of the modifier in                the carbonized calcium carbonate slurry, performing                wet modification, stirring for at least 30 minutes,                and then transporting to the homogenization tank,                (5) centrifugally dehydrating and drying the slurry                in the homogenization tank, and (6) transferring                the dried powder to a ribbon mixing jar, adding                0.3-0.5% coupling agent of the calcium carbonate                dry weight, performing secondary dry modification,                stirring for at least 30 minutes, then pulverizing                and grading to obtain secondary modified light                calcium carbonate for plastic products, where the                plastic product uses a secondary modified light                calcium carbonate crystal shape as a rod shape, the                grain length is 1.5-2.0 mu m, the diameter is                150-200 nm, and the aspect ratio is about 8-10, the                BET specific surface area is 9-11 m2/g, and the oil                absorption value is 22-25 gDOP/g.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the water                  temperature is 60-65 degrees C. The aging time is                  48-56 hours. In the step (3), the gelation refers                  to a period in which the conductivity of the                  reaction system first decreases slightly and then                  rises again. Preferred Components: In the step                  (2), the crystal form controlling agent is                  perfluoroalkyl betaine and aluminum chloride, and                  the ratio is 1:1-3. The crystal form controlling                  agent is perfluoroalkyl betaine and magnesium                  sulfate, and the ratio is 1:1-3. The crystal form                  controlling agent is perfluoroalkyl betaine and                  magnesium chloride, and the ratio is 1:1-3. The                  dispersing agent comprises: sodium octyl sulfate,                  sodium decyl sulfate or sodium lauryl sulfate. In                  the step (4), the modifier is compounded fatty                  acid sodium. In the step (6), the coupling agent                  is titanate coupling agent and                  bis(diethylamino)silane coupling agent, and the                  ratio is 1:1-3.

TI Measuring a circulation amount of an exhaust gas outside a coke oven comprises e.g. obtaining a gas component of a gas for heating a coke oven, and obtaining a volume content of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas after combustion.
AU KANG T
   YANG J
   HU C
AE ACRE COKING & REFRACTORY ENG CONSULTING (CMEG-C)
GA 2018A28524
AB    NOVELTY - Measuring a circulation amount of an exhaust gas outside a coke oven comprises e.g. obtaining a gas component of a gas for heating a coke oven, and obtaining a volume content of (oxygen) O2 and carbon dioxide (CO2) in the exhaust gas after combustion, where the volume content of O2 and CO2 in ambient air and the volume content of O2 and CO2 in the combustion-supporting gas after mixing air and circulating exhaust gas, according to the obtained data, where the volume ratio of the circulating exhaust gas to the air in the combustion-supporting gas is calculated by the marking method, or where V is the volume of the circulating exhaust gas, m3, and V is the volume of the air, m 3, pO2, the volume of the air is O2 in the air, using the pO2 at the volume content of O2 in the combustion gas, pO2, the volume of O2 in the exhaust gas, pCO2, the volume of CO2 in the air, pCO2, the volume of CO2 in the combustion-supporting gas, pCO2, and waste volume content of CO2 in circulating waste gas.
   USE - The method is useful for measuring a circulation amount of an exhaust gas outside a coke oven.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is accurately measures the amount of circulating exhaust gas extracted by the fan from the flue gas, achieves precise adjustment, improves the accuracy of automatic control of exhaust gas circulation; and ensures the stability of combustion in the vertical heat channel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Measuring a circulation amount of an exhaust gas outside a coke oven comprises (i) obtaining a gas component of a gas for heating a coke oven, and obtaining a volume content of (oxygen) O2 and carbon dioxide (CO2) in the exhaust gas after combustion, where the volume content of O2 and CO2 in ambient air and the volume content of O2 and CO2 in the combustion-supporting gas after mixing air and circulating exhaust gas; (ii) according to the data obtained in step (i), where the volume ratio of the circulating exhaust gas to the air in the combustion-supporting gas is calculated by the marking method, or where V is the volume of the circulating exhaust gas, m3, and V is the volume of the air, m 3, pO2, the volume of the air is O2 in the air, using the pO2 at the volume content of O2 in the combustion gas, pO2, the volume of O2 in the exhaust gas, pCO2, the volume of CO2 in the air, pCO2, the volume of CO2 in the combustion-supporting gas, pCO2, waste volume content of CO2 in circulating waste gas; (iii) according to the data obtained in steps 1 and 2, the volume ratio of the combustion gas to the combustion exhaust gas in the combustion process is calculated by the combustion chemical reaction equation of the gas components, where, V is the volume of the combustion gas, m3, where the volume of exhaust gas produced, m3, alpha is the excess air coefficient in the combustion process calculated based on the O2 content of the combustion-supporting gas, pO2, the volume content of O2 in the combustion-supporting gas, VCO2, when the gas is completely burned by 1 m3 by calculating the volume of CO2 produced according to the theory of combustion chemical reaction equations of gas components at m3, V waste, when the 1 m3 gas is completely burned under the composition of the combustion gas, the volume of the generated exhaust gas is calculated according to the combustion chemical reaction equation theory of each component of the gas, m3
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the volume ratio of the circulating exhaust gas to the air in the combustion-supporting gas is calculated by using O2 as a marker. The volume ratio of the circulating exhaust gas to the air in the combustion-supporting gas is calculated by using CO2 as a marker.

PN CN108578301-A
TI Preparing anti-reflection facial mask used for avoiding skin aging, by crushing frozen rosemary leaves into powder, dispersing in absolute ethanol, collecting supernatant liquid and carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU GAO X
   DENG B
   CHEN S
AE GAO X (GAOX-Individual)
GA 201879823Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing anti-reflection facial mask involves: (1) placing fresh rosemary leaves in a freeze dryer, freezing, crushing the frozen rosemary leaves, sieving, collecting rosemary leaf dry powder, dispersing the rosemary leaf dry powder in absolute ethanol, stirring, heating, stirring and dispersing to get an extract, placing the extract in a high-speed centrifuge, carrying out centrifugal treatment, separating and removing the lower layer precipitate and collecting the supernatant liquid; (2) placing the supernatant liquid in a distillation flask, heating and refluxing to obtain rosemary extract, subjecting the rosemary extract to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, combining and collecting the rosemary extract in the first-stage separation kettle and the second-stage separation kettle; and (3) adding active dry yeast to water to prepare yeast milk, adjusting the pH-value of the yeast milk, heating, adding mixed protease and carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing anti-reflection facial mask, which is useful for avoiding skin aging.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares skin-friendly anti-reflection facial mask with excellent water-retaining properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing anti-reflection facial mask involves: (1) placing fresh rosemary leaves in a freeze dryer, freezing, crushing the frozen rosemary leaves, sieving over 200-mesh sieve, collecting rosemary leaf dry powder, dispersing the rosemary leaf dry powder in absolute ethanol, stirring at 200-250 rpm, heating, stirring and dispersing to get an extract, placing the extract in a high-speed centrifuge, carrying out centrifugal treatment at a speed of 3000-3500 rpm, separating and removing the lower layer precipitate, and collecting the supernatant liquid; (2) placing the supernatant liquid in a distillation flask, heating and refluxing to obtain rosemary extract, subjecting the rosemary extract to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, combining and collecting the rosemary extract in the first-stage separation kettle and the second-stage separation kettle; (3) adding active dry yeast to water to prepare yeast milk, adjusting the pH-value of the yeast milk with sodium citrate solution, heating, adding mixed protease and carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis to obtain enzymatic hydrolyzate; (4) introducing nitrogen into a three-necked flask equipped with a stirrer and a constant pressure dropping funnel to discharge air, adding 20-30 ml 1,4-butanediol, 40-50 ml boron trifluoride etherate and 30-35 ml tetrahydrofuran to the three-necked flask, placing the flask in an ice water bath, mixing and stirring to obtain an initial mixture; (5) dripping 30-40 ml epichlorohydrin using constant pressure dropping funnel at a dropping rate of 5-7 ml/minute into the above-mentioned three-necked flask, maintaining the reaction in an ice water bath condition, adding 4-5 ml distilled water to the three-necked flask, leaving still to obtain a reaction product, pouring the reaction product into a separatory funnel, successively washing with distilled water and sodium carbonate solution for 2-3 times, placing the washed reaction product in a distillation flask, adding 5-8 g sodium nitride and 2-3 g sodium sulfonate, heating and distilling to obtain an azide copolyether; and (6) impregnating pure cotton non-woven fabric with the azide copolyether, then coating the two sides of the pure cotton non-woven fabric with a glutinous rice film to obtain a mask fabric, mixing the rosemary extract, the enzymatic hydrolyzate, hyaluronic acid, tomato juice and pomegranate juice as an essence, cutting the mask fabric and bagging, pouring the essence according to a certain amount of filling and sealing under vacuum.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of rosemary extract, enzymatic hydrolyzate, hyaluronic acid, tomato juice and pomegranate juice is 2:1:4:3:3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Properties: The thickness of the pure cotton non-woven fabric in step (6) is 0.5-1 mm.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. TI Preparing crack-resistant transparent artificial marble comprises e.g. adding sodium aluminate solution into plastic tank, passing carbon dioxide gas into plastic tank, settling, filtering the precipitation mixture, heating, and drying.
AU LU N
   HE S
   ZHANG J
AE CHANGZHOU RICHEN ART DECORATIVE MATERIAL (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201847354J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing crack-resistant transparent artificial marble comprises e.g. (1) adding sodium aluminate solution into the plastic tank, and passing carbon dioxide gas into the plastic tank, after the completion of plastic groove through vent, and allowing to stand for settling to obtain the precipitation mixture, placing the precipitation mixture in the flat-plate filter, filtering to remove the filtrate, then heating, and drying the filter residue in the oven, , (2) mixing aluminum hydroxide powder in the high pressure air flow in the machine, airflow crushing to obtain aluminum hydroxide superfine powder, adding aluminum hydroxide superfine powder into a sodium silicate solution to obtain the colloid solution, adding the colloidal solution in high speed high speed dispersion machine, and dispersing; (3) adding polystyrene, and vinyl acetate resin, and mixing, pouring the mixed resin into a three-necked flask containing styrene, heating three-mouth flask, and stirring.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing crack-resistant transparent artificial marble.
   ADVANTAGE - The artificial marble has large affinity of aluminum hydroxide to resin, greater strength of artificial marble against heavy pressure, and is not easy to crack, has similar refractive index after curing, is not easy to produce silver streaks and voids, and increased density, and the light inside the marble is reduced due to refraction, thus improving the transparency of the artificial marble and making the artificial marble have natural marble appearance effect, and has wide use prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing crack-resistant transparent artificial marble comprises (1) adding 700-800 ml sodium aluminate solution into the plastic tank, and passing carbon dioxide gas into the plastic tank, after the completion of plastic groove through vent, and allowing to stand for settling to obtain the precipitation mixture, placing the precipitation mixture in the flat-plate filter, filtering to remove the filtrate to obtain the filter residue, the filter residue in the oven, heating, and drying to obtain the aluminum hydroxide powder, (2) mixing aluminum hydroxide powder in the high pressure air flow in the machine, airflow crushing to obtain aluminum hydroxide superfine powder, adding 200-220 g aluminum hydroxide superfine powder into a 500-550 ml sodium silicate solution to obtain the colloid solution, adding the colloidal solution in a high speed high speed dispersion machine, and dispersing to obtain dispersing solution; (3) adding 60-70 pts. wt. polystyrene, and 30-40 pts. wt. vinyl acetate resin, and mixing to obtain mixed resin, pouring the mixed resin into a three-necked flask containing 60-70 pts. wt. styrene, heating three-mouth flask; starting the stirrer, stirring and dispersing, keeping stirring reaction, discharging and naturally reducing the temperature to the room temperature to obtain the core-shell structure of low shrinkage additive; (4) adding 20-25 pts. wt. E-51 epoxy resin pellets, 50-60 pts. wt. standard bisphenol A epoxy vinyl resin pellets, mixing to obtain a marble base material, and placing 70-80 pts. wt. dispersing liquid in the metal groove, stirring and mixing, heating, and keeping the temperature, and then 10-12 pts. wt. vinyltrimethoxysilane, and 15-18 pts. wt. benzotriazole, adding to the metal tank, stirring and mixing to obtain marble blank; and (5) pouring the marble blank BMC in the metal mold, solidifying and forming after removing pressure, keeping the temperature, naturally cooling to room temperature, and demolding to obtain the anti-crack transparent artificial marble.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Method: In the step (2), the method comprises using the gas flow pulverization time is 10-15 minutes, the aluminum hydroxide ultrafine powder has a particle diameter of 50-80 mu m, the sodium silicate solution has a mass fraction of 30%, and the high-speed dispersing machine has a rotation speed of 3000-3300 revolutions per minute (rpm). In the step (3), the temperature of the three-necked flask heating to 80-90 degrees C, the stirring speed is 300-350 rpm, and the stirring and stirring reaction time is 4-5 hours. In the step (4),the temperature after heating and heating to 100-110 degrees C, heat preservation time is 4-5 hours, and the stirring and mixing time is 10-20 minutes. Preferred Components: In the step (1), the sodium aluminate solution is 20%, the rate of carbon dioxide gas is 20-30 ml/minute, the ventilating time is 40-45 minutes, then allowing to stand for precipitation for 3-4 hours, and then heating to temperature of 90-100 degrees C, and drying for 4-5 hours. In the step (5), the size of the BMC metal mold is 80x 60x 10 mm, controlling the temperature of the female mold at 115-120 degrees C during solidification molding, the temperature of the male mold is 130-135 degrees C, the molding pressure is 11-13 MPa, solidifying and forming time is 45-50 seconds, and heat preservation time is 4-5 hours.
