PN CN108129262-A
TI Synthesizing asymmetric ester-co-carbonate 1, 2-propylene glycol from propylene oxide involves using raw materials are respectively propylene oxide, carbon dioxide, methanol and ROH is linear alcohol, isomeric alcohol, aromatic alcohol.
AU SHI L
   YAO J
AE UNIV SHENYANG CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (UYHG-C)
GA 201846441L
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing asymmetric ester-co-carbonate 1, 2-propylene glycol from propylene oxide involves using raw materials are respectively propylene oxide, carbon dioxide, methanol and ROH, where ROH is linear alcohol, an isomeric alcohol, an aromatic alcohol, a phenol, a glycol such as ethylene glycol, diethylene glycol and a polyol alcohol. The product has a molar ratio of 1,2-propanediol to carbonate of 1:1, and the route is effectively co-produce 1,2-propanediol in a large amount, reaction catalyst is a composite neutral catalyst having a mesoporous and microporous structure. The composite neutral catalyst having a mesoporous and microporous structure is of the formula X/S. The S molecular sieve carrier hydrogen type molecular sieve is first subjected to ion exchange, and bronsted acid is poisoned by the metal ion so that it does not have bronsted acidity The ion exchanger of the poisoned molecular sieve is a hydrochloride, a sulfate or a nitrate of Fe, Zn.
   USE - Method for synthesizing asymmetric ester-co-carbonate 1, 2-propylene glycol from propylene oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to synthesize asymmetric ester-co-carbonate 1, 2-propylene glycol from propylene oxide is clean and eco-friendly, which has good activity and stability, and has high efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing asymmetric ester-co-carbonate 1, 2-propylene glycol from propylene oxide involves using raw materials are respectively propylene oxide, carbon dioxide, methanol and ROH, where ROH is linear alcohol, an isomeric alcohol, an aromatic alcohol, a phenol, a glycol such as ethylene glycol, diethylene glycol and a polyol alcohol. The product has a molar ratio of 1,2-propanediol to carbonate of 1:1, and the route is effectively co-produce 1,2-propanediol in a large amount, reaction catalyst is a composite neutral catalyst having a mesoporous and microporous structure. The composite neutral catalyst having a mesoporous and microporous structure is of the formula X/S. The S molecular sieve carrier hydrogen type molecular sieve is first subjected to ion exchange, and bronsted acid is poisoned by the metal ion so that it does not have bronsted acidity The ion exchanger of the poisoned molecular sieve is a hydrochloride, a sulfate or a nitrate of Fe, Zn, La, Zr, Pb, Ce, Pd, Sn, Sb, Ge, Ga, other molecular sieves and carriers are prepared in a similar manner, uniformly labeled as MS, where M comprises Fe, Zn, La, Zr, Pb, Ce, Pd, Sn, Sb, Ge, Ga, load of the mesoporous and microporous structure carrier type neutral metal oxide catalyst, microporous molecular sieve carrier is prepared by adding certain mass of MS carrier is separately to a certain temperature, acidic solution stirring, filtered, washed, and dried to obtain the dealuminated MS-DAl carrier The M-SDAl carrier prepared is added to a certain temperature, concentration, and a certain volume of an alkaline solution for stirring, and then subjected to suction filtration, washing, and drying to obtain a multi-stage pore M-SDAl- DSi carrier, and M-S-DAl-DSi carrier prepared is placed in a muffle furnace and fired to obtain final product.
   X=Fe, Zn, La, Zr, Pb, Ce, Pd, Sn, Sb, Ge, Ga. Oxide of the element; and
   S'=carrier of different topological structures, including one or more oxides of a carrier such as EMT, MOR, MWW, FAU, MFI, FER, BEA molecular sieve and conventional SiO2, Al2O3, ZrO2 and ZnO.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The molecular sieves of different topological structures of the microporous molecular carrier comprises EMT, MOR, MWW, FAU, MFI, FER, BEA. The acidic solution comprises one or more of H4EDTA, HCl, HNO3, preferably H4EDTA. The amount of the acidic solution to be added must at least submerge all molecular sieves, which is 10-20 times, preferably 15 times the volume of the molecular sieve. The alkaline solution comprises sodium hydroide, sodium carbonate, preferably sodium hydroxide, concentration of the alkaline solution is 0.05-2.0 mol/L, alkaline solution is at least submerged by all molecular sieves. The molecular sieve is 5-15 times, preferably 10 times the volume. The M-meso-S carrier obtained by the microporous molecular sieve carrier is introduced. The pore size distribution is 4-34 nm, and the specific pore size is related to the concentration of the alkaline solution used. The supported neutral metal having a mesoporous and microporous structure carrier oxide catalyst adopts the coprecipitation method or the impregnation metho, alkaline solution for coprecipitation includes alkali metal and ammonium hydroxides such as lithium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and ammonium hydroxide, solution of an alkali metal carbonate, a hydrogencarbonate, a formate and an acetate such as lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate or the like, or a mixture of two or more of the above alkaline solutions, alkaline liquid medium used for the substance solution is preferably water, amount of the alkaline solution to be submerged is at least 10-20 times the volume of the carrier, preferably 15 times, the pH of the control solution is 10-14, preferably pH 11. The temperature of the coprecipitation bath is from room temperature to 99 degrees C, preferably 70 degrees C, aging temperature is from room temperature to 80 degrees C, the aging time is from 1 hour to 20 days, preferably 6 degrees C, and the aging time is 5 days, catalyst washing medium is preferably water, liquid medium is 2-10 times and above the volume of the catalyst, preferably 2-5 times, and the number of washings depends on the pH of the washing effluent. The catalyst drying temperature is room temperature -150 degrees C for 1 hour-10 days, preferably 120 degrees C, drying for 3-5 days. The calcination temperature is 150-800 degrees C for 1-50 hours, preferably from 500-700 degrees C, for 30 hours, supported catalyst of the composite pore structure has a content of metal X of 0.1-50 wt.% carrier. The active component precursor of the supported catalyst of the composite pore structure is preferably Fe(NO3)3, Zn(NO3)2, La(NO3)3, Zr(NO3)2, Pb(NO3)4, Ce(NO3)4, Pd(NO3)2, Sn(NO3)4, Sb(NO3)4, Ge(NO3)4, Ga(NO3)3, FeCl3, ZnCl2, LaCl3, ZrCl2, PbCl4, CeCl4, PdCl2, SnCl4, SbCl4 , GeCl4, GaCl3, Fe2(SO4)3, ZnSO4, La2(SO4)3, ZrSO4, Pb(SO4)2, Ce(SO4)2, PdSO4, Sn(SO4)2, Sb(SO4)2, Ge(SO4)2, mixing of Ga2(SO4)3, impregnation method by the co-impregnation method, drying temperature is from room temperature to 150 degrees C for 1 hour to 20 days. The calcination temperature is 550-650 degrees C for 1 to 50 hours. The supported catalyst of the composite pore structure has a metal X content of 0.1-50 wt.% carrier. The active component precursor of the supported catalyst of the composite pore structure is preferably Fe(NO3)3, Zn(NO3)2, La(NO3)3, Zr(NO3)2, Pb(NO3)4, Ce(NO3)4, Pd(NO3)2, Sn(NO3)4, Sb(NO3)4, Ge(NO3)4, Ga(NO3)3, FeCl3, ZnCl2, LaCl3, ZrCl2, PbCl4, CeCl4, One or a mixture of PdCl2, SnCl4, SbCl4, GeCl4, GaCl3. The method for synthesizing asymmetric ester-co-carbonate 1, 2-propylene glycol from propylene oxide involves reacting at atmospheric pressure to 10 MPa Under pressure, propylene oxide and carbon dioxide are fed at a molar ratio of 0.1:1-10:1, molar ratio of liquid raw material methanol and other alcohols are pumped in the ratio of 1:5-5:1, asymmetric carbonate ester is synthesized with a space velocity of 0.1-30 per hour, and a reaction temperature is 50-250 degrees C. The catalyst is a composite neutral catalyst, and the amount of the catalyst is 0.1-3 wt.% of the mass of the raw material. The selectivity of the asymmetric carbonate is 25%, and the yield is 20%. The reacting the fixed bed to prepare an asymmetric carbonate ester, preferably ratio of propylene oxide: carbon dioxide: methanol: other alcohol is 1:1:3:2, space velocity 0.5-5 per hour, and reaction temperature is 78-130 degrees C. The reaction pressure of 0.1-10 MPa, molar ratio of oxypropane to carbon dioxide is 0.1:1-10:1, the molar ratio of methanol to other alcohols is 1:5-5:1, and the asymmetric ester of carbonic acid is prepared by reaction in the presence of catalyst, and the reaction vessel is sealed 25-150 degrees C for 0.5-10 hours, and the catalyst is a neutral catalyst, and the amount of the catalyst is 0.1-10 wt.% of the mass of the raw material.

TI Preparing composite material for use in            dehumidifying rotating wheel, involves adding thermal            conductive filler and carbonate to water glass            respectively, mixing, dispersing to obtain premix,            absorbing or infiltrating premix by carrier, drying to            obtain first intermediate, absorbing, and soaking.
AU DAI M
   ZHI W
AE JIANGSU SUJING PURIFICATION GROUP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B4789Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite material involves adding                thermal conductive filler and carbonate to water                glass respectively, mixing, dispersing to obtain                premix, absorbing or infiltrating premix by                carrier, drying to obtain first intermediate,                absorbing, soaking first intermediate into a metal                salt aqueous solution, performing double hydrolysis                reaction on water glass and metal salt in the metal                salt aqueous solution to obtain silica gel and                metal hydroxide, and forming a load layer on the                inner part and the surface of the carrier.
   USE - Method for preparing composite material for                use in dehumidifying rotating wheel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the lower desorption                temperature, the higher desorption efficiency and                the higher dehumidification capacity, and has                effectively improved heat conduction efficiency of                the material, and improved desorption                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing composite material involves adding                thermal conductive filler and carbonate to water                glass respectively, mixing, dispersing to obtain                premix, absorbing or infiltrating premix by                carrier, drying to obtain first intermediate,                absorbing, soaking first intermediate into a metal                salt aqueous solution, performing double hydrolysis                reaction on water glass and metal salt in the metal                salt aqueous solution to obtain silica gel and                metal hydroxide, forming a load layer on the inner                part and the surface of the carrier, where the load                layer comprises the silica gel, the heat-conducting                filler doped in the silica gel, the carbonate and                the metal hydroxide, drying to obtain a second                intermediate, treating the second intermediate with                acid liquor to obtain a third intermediate, during                the treatment process, reacting carbonate with acid                in the acid liquor to obtain a first moisture                absorbent and carbon dioxide, reacting the metal                hydroxide with acid in the acid liquor to obtain a                second moisture absorbent, where the carbon dioxide                escapes outwards to form holes on the silica gel,                cleaning, and drying the third intermediate to                obtain the composite material for dehumidifying                rotating wheel.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                composite material for dehumidifying rotating wheel                is prepared by preparation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The water glass is                  aqueous solution of silicate material including                  sodium silicate, potassium silicate and sodium                  potassium silicate. The thermally conductive                  filler is hexagonal boron nitride,                  &#945;-aluminum oxide (Al2O3),                  &#947;-Al2O3and silicon carbide. The                  carbonate is calcium carbonate and/or magnesium                  carbonate. The carrier is prepared from glass                  fiber. The metal salt is aluminum chloride,                  aluminum sulfate, magnesium chloride and ferric                  chloride.Preferred Conditions: The silicate material                  accounts for 20-40 wt.% based on total weight of                  the water glass, and the modulus of the silicate                  material is 2.2-3.5. The addition amount of the                  thermally conductive filler is 3-20 wt.% based on                  water glass. The total addition amount of the                  carbonate is 1-10 wt.% based on water glass. The                  carrier is in the form of a honeycomb. The                  carrier is soaked in the premix for a soaking                  time of 0.5-5 hours. The mass concentration of                  the metal salt aqueous solution is 10-25 wt.%.                  The double hydrolysis reaction is carried out at                  35-90&#8451;. The second intermediate is                  soaked in the acid solution, the soaking time is                  0.5-3 hours, and the pH value of the acid                  solution is 2-4 at the beginning of the soaking,                  after soaking the second intermediate in the acid                  solution, the pH value of the system is made                  constant by adding a new acid solution and/or the                  acid solution is an aqueous hydrogen chloride                  solution.

AB    NOVELTY - The method for recycling sulfur, nitrate,                fluorine, carbon dioxide and waste heat from                electrolytic aluminum flue gas comprises recycling                the waste heat of the electrolytic aluminum flue,                recovering the heat, dedusting the recycled heat to                collect the ash containing fluorine-containing                alumina, sending the purified flue to the                multi-stage fixed bed reactor (6) for multiple                stages of purification, drying and crushing mineral                powder with ore and grinding, where the ore                includes silicate mineral and red mud, discharging                the inactivated reaction material and uniformly                mixing with the reaction material to continue to                participate in the reaction, and draining the                purified gas.
   USE - Method for synergistic removal of sulfur,                nitrate, fluorine and carbon by dry process of                electrolytic aluminum flue gas and waste heat                recovery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes pollutant in electrolytic                aluminum flue gas, solid waste resource                utilization, combined waste heat recovery,                improving pollutant removing efficiency and solid                waste resource usage rate, realizing sulfur,                nitrate, fluorine, carbon depth cooperatively                removing and waste resource comprehensive                utilization, reducing energy consumption and                production cost, it has potential application                prospect in energy saving, emission reduction and                consumption reduction. The method uses red mud as                purifying raw material, realizing solid waste                recycling, which obviously reduces the production                cost and realizes economic, environment and social                benefit, realizes carbon dioxide in the silicate                mineral flue gases for carbonation reaction,                obtaining carbonate and silicon dioxide, realizing                flue-gas decarburization. The mineral powder has a                certain alkaline, and can purify sulfur oxide and                nitrogen oxide in the flue.

   a method for cooperatively removing sulfur;                and

TI Preparing low volatile organic compounds release            glass magnesium plate composite floor useful as house            floor comprises reacting sulfhydryl siloxane and            hydroxy siloxane, pre-polymerizing diisocyanate and            polymer polyol, adding dimethylolpropionic acid, and            coating adhesive on glass magnesium plate.

AE VTC IND WUXI CO LTD (VTCI-Non-standard)

AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (i) preparing polymer polyol                by taking sulfhydryl siloxane, hydroxy siloxane in                alcohol aqueous solution to react to obtain                hyperbranched polysiloxane, taking polycarbonate                dibasic alcohol with carbon dioxide, bromine                propylene to obtain double-bond-terminated modified                polycarbonate, taking modified polycarbonate,                hyperbranched polysiloxane to click reaction to                obtain polymer polyol, (2) preparing surface                coating by taking diisocyanate, epoxy resin,                polymer polyol, pre-polymerizing, adding                dimethylolpropionic acid, catalyst, reacting,                adding 1,4-butanediol chain extension, emulsion,                trifluoroethyl methacrylate, azodiisobutyronitrile,                ultrasonic auxiliary lower aggregate, calcium                carbonate, photoinitiator, obtaining surface                coating, and (iii) preparing compound floor by                coating adhesive on upper and lower two surfaces of                glass magnesium plate, polyvinyl chloride printing                layer, ethylene-vinyl acetate mute pad, UV curing,                forming surface layer.

The catalyst is a supported catalyst, where the active component is iron, cobalt, nickel, manganese or chromium, or iron oxide, cobalt oxide, nickel oxide, tungsten oxide, iron sulfide, molybdenum sulfide or tungsten sulfide, and the carrier is silica, alumina, titania or active carbon. The hydrogenation catalyst is a supported catalyst, where the active component is ruthenium, platinum or palladium, and the carrier is silica, alumina, titania or active carbon.
   USE - Preparation of hydrogen oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has no chimney, no discharge and no landfill, and greatly improves the utilization value of the wastes as resources. The comprehensive utilization technology of the garbage does not need to classify the waste, not only can fundamentally put an end to various pollution sources generated in the waste treatment process and meet the requirements of all environmental protection standards in the world at present, but also can convert the waste into hydrogen energy and various useful substances, and has good environmental protection and economic values. The method does not need high-purity hydrogen, and the hydrogenation reaction can be realized under the control of a catalyst and reaction, where the purity of the hydrogen is 75-85%
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for preparing hydrogen oil by pyrolysis of wastes.

PN CN113845115-A
TI Preparing heteroatom self-doped biomass porous carbon comprises introducing locust leaf clover into absolute ethyl alcohol for ultrasonic treatment, obtaining hydrothermal carbon, introducing sophora japonica leaf clover powder in hydrothermal reaction kettle, and carbonizing.
AU HUO M
   SUN Z
   ZHAO X
   REN P
AE UNIV XIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYXT-C)
GA 202209779V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing heteroatom self-doped biomass porous carbon, comprises introducing locust leaf clover into absolute ethyl alcohol for ultrasonic treatment, cleaning soluble impurity, (ii) drying locust leaf clover, ball milling, sieving to obtain powder, (iii) introducing powder in a hydrothermal reaction kettle for pre-carbonizing, washing, drying, obtaining hydrothermal carbon, (iv) mixing the hydrothermal carbon with the solid potassium hydroxide, introducing water to soak the mixture and drying to obtain the hydrothermal carbon/potassium hydroxide mixture, (v) carbonizing under the protection of nitrogen and keeping the temperature for a period of time, reducing the inlet rate of the nitrogen, introducing carbon dioxide to activate, naturally cooling under the protection of nitrogen to obtain the crude product, (vi) introducing crude product into the hydrochloric acid solution to soak, using de-ionized water to wash until the filtrate is neutral, drying.
   USE - The heteroatom self-doping biomass porous carbon can is useful to remove anionic and cationic dyes in wastewater (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The heteroatom self-doped biomass porous carbon has better removing ability to anionic dye and cationic dye, and has better application prospect in sewage purifying field.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing heteroatom self-doped biomass porous carbon, comprises introducing locust leaf clover into absolute ethyl alcohol for ultrasonic treatment, cleaning soluble impurity, (ii) drying locust leaf clover, ball milling, sieving to obtain the sophora japonica leaf clover powder, (iii) introducing sophora japonica leaf clover powder in a hydrothermal reaction kettle for pre-carbonizing, washing, drying, obtaining hydrothermal carbon, (iv) mixing the hydrothermal carbon with the solid potassium hydroxide, introducing water to soak the obtained mixture and drying to obtain the hydrothermal carbon/potassium hydroxide mixture, (v) carbonizing the hydrothermal carbon/potassium hydroxide mixture obtained in step (iv) under the protection of nitrogen and keeping the temperature for a period of time, reducing the inlet rate of the nitrogen, introducing carbon dioxide to activate, naturally cooling under the protection of nitrogen to obtain the crude product, (vi) introducing crude product into the hydrochloric acid solution to soak, using de-ionized water to wash until the filtrate is neutral, drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the mass ratio of the locust leaf clover and absolute ethyl alcohol is 1: 8; the ultrasonic processing time is 30-50 minutes. In the step (ii), the ball milling speed is 500-700 revolutions/minutes, the ball milling time is 6-12 hours, drying temperature is 60-80 degreesC, the drying time is 12-24 hours, and sieve is sieved by a 100-mesh sieve. In the step (iii), the pre-carbonization temperature is 180-230 degreesC, the pre-carbonization time is 10-14 hours, and drying temperature is 100-120 degreesC, and the drying time is 12-24 hours. In the step (IV) the mass ratio of the hydrothermal carbon to the solid potassium hydroxide to the water is 5: 2: 50. In the step (V), the carbonization temperature is 600-800 degreesC, the carbonization time is 2 hours, the heat preservation time is 2 hours; the activation time is 30 minutes; the nitrogen inlet rate is 60 ml/minutes, reduced nitrogen inlet rate is 20 ml/minutes, and inlet rate of the carbon dioxide is 60 ml/minutes. In the step (vi), the soaking time is 24 hours, the drying temperature is 60-80 degrees Celsius, and the drying time is 12-14 hours.

PN US2021395083-A1; WO2021257381-A1; US11492254-B2
TI Producing hydrogen using system, involves feeding            hydrocarbon and steam to pre-reformer having            pre-reforming catalyst, converting hydrocarbon to            methane via pre-reforming catalyst, and converting            hydrocarbon to methane via pre-reforming catalyst.
AU KATIKANENI S P
   SEBASTIANI F
   VAN VEEN H
   PAGLIERI S N
   JAMAL A
   HARALE A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2021E8145U
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen involves feeding                hydrocarbon and steam to a pre-reformer having a                pre-reforming catalyst, converting hydrocarbon to                methane via the pre-reforming catalyst in the                pre-reformer to give a pre-reformed intermediate                that is a methane-rich mixture, and feeding the                pre-reformed intermediate to a membrane reformer.                The membrane reformer comprises a vessel, reforming                catalyst disposed in the vessel, and tubular                membrane disposed in the vessel. The methane in the                pre-reformed intermediate is converted to hydrogen                and carbon dioxide by steam reforming via the                reforming catalyst in the membrane reformer in a                region in the vessel external to the tubular                membrane. The region is a retentate side of the                tubular membrane, and hydrogen is diffused from the                region through the tubular membrane into a bore of                the tubular membrane. The tubular membrane is                hydrogen selective, and the bore is a permeate side                of the tubular membrane.
   USE - Method for producing hydrogen using system,                where hydrogen is used as feedstock to chemical                processes, such as fuel cells, ammonia production,                aromatization, hydrodesulphurization, and                hydrogenation or hydrocracking of                hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient production of                hydrogen with high yield and purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for hydrogen production, which comprises a                pre-reformer to receive hydrocarbon and convert the                hydrocarbon to methane via a pre-reforming                catalyst, and membrane reformer to receive the                methane from the pre-reformer, where membrane                reformer comprises a reforming catalyst in a vessel                to convert the methane by steam reforming into                hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and a tubular membrane                in the vessel to diffuse the hydrogen through the                tubular membrane to a bore of the tubular membrane,                where the tubular membrane is hydrogen selective                and the bore is a permeate side of the tubular                membrane, and the reforming catalyst is disposed                external to the tubular membrane, and the vessel                comprising a region in the vessel and external to                the tubular membrane for the steam reforming, where                the region is a retentate side of the tubular                membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The hydrogen is                  produced in a range of 20 normal cubic meter per                  hour (Nm3/hr) to 10,000 Nm3/hr. The method                  particularly involves converting hydrocarbon, in                  addition to the methane, in the pre-reformed                  intermediate in the membrane reformer to hydrogen                  and carbon dioxide, wherein the steam reforming                  occurs at less than 600degreesC in the vessel,                  where the region comprises a reaction space for                  the steam reforming, and hydrogen is diffused                  through the tubular membrane is contemporaneous                  with converting the methane to the hydrogen. A                  sweep gas is provided to the bore, then hydrogen                  is displaced from the bore with the sweep gas,                  driving force is increased for hydrogen                  permeation through the tubular membrane to the                  bore from the region via displacing the hydrogen                  from the bore with the sweep gas, and a hydrogen                  stream is discharged from the bore. The steam in                  the hydrogen stream is condensed and steam as                  condensed is removed from the hydrogen stream,                  where the sweep gas comprises steam, and the                  hydrogen stream as discharged from the bore                  comprises 90 mol.% hydrogen on dry-basis. The                  hydrogen stream is purifried to give hydrogen                  product comprising 99.9 mol.% hydrogen, and                  hydrogen product is compressed to a pressure of                  350 bar. The spurifying comprises routing the                  hydrogen stream through a vessel having adsorbent                  and components are removed from the hydrogen                  stream via the absorbent. The step of purifying                  and compressing are performed via an                  electrochemical compressor. The carbon-dioxide                  stream is discharged from the region, the                  carbon-dioxide stream comprising 90 mol.% carbon                  dioxide. The water is condensied in the carbon                  dioxide stream, and water as condensed is removed                  from the carbon dioxide stream, and carbon                  dioxide stream is cryogenically purified to give                  liquid carbon-dioxide product comprising 99 mol.%                  carbon dioxide. Heat is provided to the membrane                  reformer via an electrical heater, where an                  operating temperature of the membrane reformer is                  less than 650degreesC, and the hydrocarbon is fed                  to the pre-reformer comprising naphtha, kerosene,                  gasoline, diesel, liquefied petroleum gas (LPG),                  natural gas or, mixture of hydrocarbons (1-5C),                  or any combinations. The sulfur compounds are                  removed from the hydrocarbon prior to feeding the                  hydrocarbon to the pre-reformer, where the                  hydrocarbon as fed to the pre-reformer comprises                  less than 0.1 parts per million (ppm) of sulfur                  compounds comprising sulfur (S). The pre-reformer                  comprises pre-reforming catalyst in an inlet                  portion of the vessel, and the pre-reformer and                  the membrane reformer are an integrated unit. The                  methane is converted to hydrogen via dry                  reforming catalyst disposed in an exit portion of                  the membrane reformer. The membrane reformer to                  receive the methane in a methane-rich mixture                  from the pre-reformer. Preferred Component: The                  tubular membrane comprises palladium.

TI Plastic waste recycling crushing device for recycling large amount of carbon dioxide discharge amount of waste plastic, has airbag mounting frame whose inner left and right ends are respectively installed with compression springs, where two compression springs are connected with airbag in middle.
AU XIE G
AE XIE G (XIEG-Individual)
GA 2021B7221E
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an inlet (1), an equipment outer box (2), a driving mechanism (3), an airbag mounting frame (4) and a storage box (5). The airbag mounting frame is fixedly connected to the outer left side of the equipment outer box. A discharge port is installed at the bottom of the inner side of the equipment outer box. A discharge plate is connected to the top of the discharge port. A crushing wheel is arranged above the discharging plate and the number of the crushing wheels is three. A liquid cooling device is arranged above the crushing wheel. A hydraulic cylinder is installed above the liquid cooling device. A liquid cooling water cylinder is connected to the upper left end of the liquid cooling device. A gas cylinder installation box is provided outside the liquid cooling water cylinder. The inner left and right ends of the airbag mounting frame are respectively installed with compression springs, and the two compression springs are connected with an airbag in the middle.
   USE - Plastic waste recycling crushing device for recycling large amount of carbon dioxide discharge amount of waste plastic such as PE, PET.
   ADVANTAGE - By setting the hot melting device, the plastic with different shapes and different hardness can be softened and molded to be easy to be crushed. The plastic plastic can be quickly cooled and formed into the crushing wheel through the liquid cooling device. The liquid cooling effect is better. The water in the hydraulic device is kept in one direction to flow. It ensures the effect of liquid cooling. The structure of the whole device floor area is not large and easy to control, reduces the waste of the resource. The air in the air bag can be connected to liquid cooling water cylinder for exhausting, it is good for saving energy and fully cooling liquid circulation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the structure of the plastic waste recycling crushing device.

TI Device for carbon monoxide production, has gas channels that house reverse shift catalyst to accelerate reverse shift reaction to generate carbon monoxide from raw material gas including carbon dioxide, where gas channels are between introduction openings for raw material gas and discharge openings.
AU KOHEI Y
   ATSUSHI U
   MASATOSHI S
AE HITACHI LTD (HITA-C)
GA 2021B6026N
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) comprises a reverse shift catalyst (20) and gas channels (15) that house the reverse shift catalyst. The reverse shift catalyst accelerates a reverse shift reaction that generates carbon monoxide and water from a raw material gas that includes carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The gas channels: are formed between introduction openings (11) for the raw material gas and discharge openings (12) that discharge a product gas that includes at least the carbon monoxide that has been generated; and are demarcated by first demarcation walls (40). At least a portion of the first demarcation walls are water-permeable walls (30) that are selectively permeable to water. The gas channel is arranged with the reverse shift catalyst. On the side of the water-permeable wall opposite to the gas channel, a discharge space (51) is provided which is demarcated by the second demarcation wall (50) and through which the water permeating the water-permeable wall is discharged.
   USE - The carbon monoxide production device is used for carbon monoxide production system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device can sustain carbon monoxide production. The device utilizes reverse shift catalyst which promotes the oxidation and reduction of the active ingredient and improves the catalytic performance. The device promotes the permeation of water through the water-permeable wall, and maintains the production of carbon monoxide. The device suppresses diffusion of water (water vapor) caused through the gas phase, suppresses an excessive increase in the amount of water in the gas channel, and sustains the production of carbon monoxide for a long period of time. The system or the device has improved thermal efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon monoxide production system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the carbon monoxide production device.
   Introduction openings (11)
   Discharge openings (12)
   Gas channels (15)
   Reverse shift catalyst (20)
   Water-permeable walls (30)
   First demarcation walls (40)
   Second demarcation wall (50)
   Discharge space (51)
   Carbon monoxide production device (100)

The carrier component contains an oxide containing cerium.

PN AU2021104438-A4
TI Preparing gas separation membranes involves preparing nanofiber mats by electrostatic spinning technology, and placing the nanofiber mat in the polymer membrane liquid, where nanofiber mat comprises polyacrylonitrile nanofibers.
AU ZHENG W
   SUN T
   HE G
   YAN X
   DAI Y
   RUAN X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2021A9956K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing gas separation membrane involves preparing nanofiber mats by electrostatic spinning technology. The nanofiber mat comprises polyacrylonitrile nanofibers, polyvinylpyrrolidone nanofibers, polyvinyl alcohol nanofibers, and polyvinylidene fluoride nanofibers with different molecular weights. Photo-initiator is added to the polymer polyethylene (glycol) diacrylate (PEGDA) or polymers PEGDA and poly(ethylene glycol) methyl ether acrylate (PEGMEA) with a mass ratio of not less than 3:7. The resultant is placed in a light resistant container, and stirred well to obtain the membrane liquid. The obtained membrane liquid is ultrasonically de-aerated and then cooled to the room temperature for standby application. The nanofiber mat is placed in the polymer membrane liquid. The air pressure is adjusted to 0.05-0.1 mega Pascals.
   USE - Method for preparing gas separation membranes by utilizing nanofibers subjected to electrostatic spinning.
   ADVANTAGE - The separation membrane has high carbon dioxide permeability, high selectivity of carbon dioxide/nitrogen and high mechanical strength, which can make the membrane to have more practical value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing gas separation membrane involves preparing nanofiber mats by electrostatic spinning technology. The nanofiber mat comprises polyacrylonitrile nanofibers, polyvinylpyrrolidone nanofibers, polyvinyl alcohol nanofibers, and polyvinylidene fluoride nanofibers with different molecular weights. Photoinitiator is added to the polymer polyethylene (glycol) diacrylate (PEGDA) or polymers PEGDA and poly(ethylene glycol) methyl ether acrylate (PEGMEA) with a mass ratio of not less than 3:7. The resultant is placed in a light resistant container, and stirred well to obtain the membrane liquid. The obtained membrane liquid is ultrasonically deaerated and then cooled to the room temperature for standby application. The nanofiber mat is placed in the polymer membrane liquid. The air pressure is adjusted to 0.05-0.1 mega Pascals. Nitrogen is injected to make the polymer membrane liquid pass through the nanofiber mat to obtain a wet nanofiber mat which is placed on the filter paper to soak up residual liquid. The oxygen in the device is removed by utilizing nitrogen. The nanofiber mat is irradiated with a 308-312 nm ultraviolet lamp under the protection of nitrogen for 2 minutes. The membrane is prepared by in situ polymerization of the polymer.
TF EXAMPLE - None given.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A12 (Polymers of di-and higher olefins; acetylenics; nitroso compounds.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN113318132-A
TI Extracting Ganoderma lucidum triterpenes substance comprises e.g. adding Ganoderma lucidum micro-powder into extraction kettle, adding extractant and surfactant, centrifuging, passing extract through analysis kettle and vaporizing into gas.
AU WANG Z
   XIE T
AE GUANGZHOU YOROYAL BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A4283U
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting Ganoderma lucidum triterpenes substance comprises e.g. (a) crushing the Ganoderma lucidum fruiting body slices and drying to obtain Ganoderma lucidum powder, introducing carbon dioxide for swelling treatment, and instantaneously releasing the pressure to obtain Ganoderma lucidum micro powder, (b) adding the Ganoderma lucidum micro powder into an extraction kettle, using the ethanol solution and 1-methyl-3-ethylimidazole bromide as the extractant, adding the surfactant, treating in a room temperature high-pressure pulse electromagnetic field, centrifuging and filtering to remove the powder slag to obtain the extract, and (c) passing the extract through an analysis kettle, vaporizing the Ganoderma lucidum triterpene substance into gas, flowing into the extraction kettle, so that the Ganoderma lucidum triterpene substance are separated from the extractant and depositing at the bottom of the analysis kettle.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting Ganoderma lucidum triterpenes substance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high extraction rate, strong operability; and is environment-friendly, energy-saving and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting Ganoderma lucidum triterpenes substance comprises (a) crushing the Ganoderma lucidum fruiting body slices and drying to obtain Ganoderma lucidum powder, introducing carbon dioxide for swelling treatment for 4-7 minutes, and instantaneously releasing the pressure to obtain Ganoderma lucidum micro powder, (b) adding the Ganoderma lucidum micro powder into an extraction kettle, using the ethanol solution and 1-methyl-3-ethylimidazole bromide as the extractant, adding the surfactant, at the same time, treating in a room temperature high-pressure pulse electromagnetic field for 5-20 minutes, centrifuging and filtering to remove the powder slag to obtain the extract, and (c) passing the extract through an analysis kettle, vaporizing the Ganoderma lucidum triterpene substance into gas by heating and vacuum extracting, flowing into the extraction kettle by condensing reflux and liquefaction, so that the Ganoderma lucidum triterpene substance are separated from the extractant and depositing at the bottom of the analysis kettle, and collecting the Ganoderma lucidum triterpenes substance. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for Ganoderma lucidum triterpenes substance extracted by the above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: In the step (b), the final concentration of the 1-methyl-3-ethylimidazole bromide is 0.1-0.5 mol/l and the final concentration of the surfactant is 0.02-0.1 mol/l. The surfactant is N-dodecyl-Nmethyl-2pyrrolidone bromide, N-hexadecyl-Nmethyl-2pyrrolidone bromide, N-octadecyl-Nmethyl-2-pyrrolidone bromide, Tween-20 (RTM: Polysorbate-20) and polyethylene glycol-4000. The surfactant is N-hexadecyl-N-methyl-pyrrolidone bromide. The volume concentration of the ethanol solution is 65-95%. The ratio of the Ganoderma lucidum micro powder and the extracting agent is 1:30-100 g/ml. Preferred Method: In the step (a), the density of carbon dioxide is controlled at 1.8-2.3 kg/m3. In the step (b), the intensity of pulsed electromagnetic field is controlled at 10-15 kv/cm, and the frequency is 10-15 Hz. In the step (c), the analyzing temperature of the analyzing kettle is 40-75 degrees C and the analyzing pressure is -0.10 to 0.10 MPa.

PN US2021215843-A1; US11448792-B2
TI Method for processing seismic data to locate            underground features, involves applying noise filter of            cluster of sensor to seismic traces of sensor and            analyzing filtered seismic traces to determine            underground property.
AU BOSSHART N W
   GLAZEWSKI K A
   BARAJAS-OLALDE C
AE EERC ENERGY & ENVIRONMENTAL RES CENT (EERC-Non-standard)
GA 202179802G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves capturing multiple seismic                traces which are associated with seismic signals                are generated by a seismic source (102) by multiple                sensors (116) which are distributed throughout a                region. The noise comprises active noise and                passive noise. The values are determined for                attributes that are associated with the identified                noise for multiple sensors. An unsupervised                machine-learning model is utilized to determine                noise characteristics for each sensor based on the                values of the attributes. The sensors are grouped                in multiple clusters based on the noise                characteristics for each sensor. A noise filter is                created based on the noise characteristics of the                sensors in the cluster for multiple clusters. The                noise filter of the cluster of the sensor is                applied to the seismic traces of the sensor for                sensors. The filtered seismic traces are analyzed                to determine an underground property. The                underground property is a location of carbon                dioxide (CO2) underground.
   USE - Method for processing seismic data to locate                underground features.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods reduces the interference of noise                in the measurements of seismic data can enable the                improved monitoring of CO2 under the surface. The                active noise records are used as input which allows                the application of the workflow to seismic surveys                that do not record passive noise data, which is the                case in most seismic surveys based on cable data                acquisition systems with very tight deadlines to                complete data acquisition. The improvement with                regard to data processing workflows facilitates the                adequate parameterization of algorithms to                attenuate ambient noise. The seismic surveys using                nodal systems for the acquisition can easily                implement the acquisition of passive noise records                since the nodes are activated during long periods                before or after data acquisition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (a) a system for processing seismic data to                locate underground features; and

PN CN112811432-A; CN112811432-B
TI Preparation of ytterbium ion-doped silica laser microsphere involves taking tetraethyl orthosilicate, hexamethyldisiloxane and ethanol, placing in glass reactor at room temperature, sealing, stirring, and adding dilute nitric acid.
AU CHEN J
AE UNIV YANCHENG TEACHERS (UAET-C)
GA 202158668C
AB    NOVELTY - An ytterbium ion-doped silica laser microsphere is prepared by taking tetraethyl orthosilicate, hexamethyldisiloxane and ethanol, placing in glass reactor at room temperature, sealing, stirring, adding dilute nitric acid, taking ytterbium nitrate pentahydrate, adding dilute nitric acid, ultrasonicating, dissolving, adding to glass reactor, stirring, concentrating, forming gel, gently inserting with thin glass rod, placing in long crucible, flat alumina plate or crucible, placing in programmable furnace, gradually heating, cooling, obtaining transparent glass filament doped with ytterbium ions, taking out, placing in glass capillary, fixing capillary with glass filament on the three-dimensional adjustable processing platform, adjusting and fixing the beam focus position of the carbon dioxide laser, fine-tuning platform position, focusing glass filament exposed end at laser beam, turning on the laser and adjusting the output power for laser processing, and heating and melting.
   USE - The method for preparing ytterbium ion-doped silica laser microsphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has ultra-low laser threshold, is chemically stable, nontoxic, with a handle, and the diameter of the microspheres can be controlled within the range of 50-160 mu m.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An ytterbium ion-doped silica laser microsphere is prepared by taking tetraethyl orthosilicate, hexamethyldisiloxane and ethanol, placing in glass reactor at room temperature, sealing, stirring, adding dilute nitric acid, taking 1-2 wt.% main material ytterbium nitrate pentahydrate, adding dilute nitric acid, ultrasonicating, dissolving, adding to glass reactor, stirring, concentrating, forming gel, gently inserting with thin glass rod, lifting glass rod to pull out the gel wire about 15-20 cm in length, placing in long crucible, flat alumina plate or crucible, placing in programmable furnace, gradually heating at 800-1000 degrees C for 10 minutes, cooling, obtaining transparent glass filament doped with ytterbium ions, taking out, placing in glass capillary with diameter of 1 mm, exposing about 5-10 mm, fixing capillary with glass filament on the three-dimensional adjustable processing platform, adjusting and fixing the beam focus position of the carbon dioxide laser, fine-tuning platform position, focusing glass filament exposed end at laser beam, turning on the laser and adjusting the output power for laser processing, heating and melting to form a transparent microsphere, and obtaining handle laser microsphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The main materials comprises 1:6:43 molar ratio of tetraethyl orthosilicate, hexamethyldisiloxane and ethanol. The dilute nitric acid comprises 1:150 molar ratio of nitric acid and deionized water. The additive comprises nitric acid, deionized water and ytterbium nitrate pentahydrate. The volume of main material and additive is (28-57):1. Preferred Methods: The sol-gel method is used to prepare uniform ytterbium ion-doped fiber comprising adjusting fiber diameter between 20-120 mu m, using carbon dioxide laser processing to obtain uniform ytterbium ion doped silica laser microspheres to diameter of 50-160 mu m. During the initial stirring, the agitator stirs at a speed of 200-350 revolutions/minute for 3-4 hours, and the continuous stirring time is 2-3 hours. The gradual heating is carried out at 60-80 degrees C for 20-30 hours at rate of 0.1-0.5 degrees C/minute. The laser processing time varies according to the diameter of the glass filament, and the value range is 5-9 minutes.
DC L01 (Glass - includes chemical compositions, batch treatment, furnaces, flat glass forming, hollow-ware forming, postforming and glass/ceramics, but not lens designs, bottling, bottle-washing, closures for containers, glazing designs, glass cutting, chamfering edges, printing on glass, disposing of used glass or the production of pure sodium silicate. Chemical aspects of optical fibres (C03).)

PT P
PN CN112500603-A; CN112500603-B
TI Preparing light flexible isolation elastomer complex microporous strain sensing material comprises (i), placing the thermoplastic elastomer beads in a high pressure kettle filled with supercritical carbon-dioxide for saturation absorption.
AU MA Z
   WEI A
   MA J
   SHAO L
   XIANG X
   KANG S
   ZHANG M
   JING J
AE UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
GA 202128605J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing light flexible isolation elastomer complex microporous strain sensing material comprises (i), placing thermoplastic elastomer beads in high pressure kettle filled with supercritical carbon-dioxide for saturation absorption for 3-6 hours at 35-60 degrees C and pressure is 8-16 MPa, then placing fully saturated thermoplastic elastomer beads in the foaming device for micro-porous foaming, obtaining thermoplastic elastomer micro-porous foaming beads with different foaming ratio, then using dip coating method to coat the conductive silver paste on thermoplastic elastomer micro-porous foaming bead surface and drying at room temperature; (ii) dissolving polydimethylsiloxane prepolymer in tetrahydrofuran, dispersing dried multi-walled carbon nanotubes in polydimethylsiloxane prepolymer solution, ultrasonically stirring for 30-50 minutes to make multi-walled carbon nanotubes uniformly dispersed in polydimethylsiloxane prepolymer solution, placing in oven at 60-100 degrees C to remove solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing light flexible isolation elastomer complex microporous strain sensing material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has the characteristics of light weight, flexibility, low filling, high sensitivity, good application value in wear-resistant devices, human health and sports monitoring, artificial intelligence and functional soles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method further comprises (iii) mixing the polydimethylsiloxane/multi-walled carbon nanotube precursor conductive liquid obtained in step (ii) with curing agent according to mass ratio of 5:1-10:1, stirring for 40-50 minutes, uniformly mixing to obtain polydimethylsiloxane/multi-walled carbon nanotube conductive liquid, then placing the polydimethylsiloxane/multi-walled carbon nanotube conductive liquid in a vacuum drying box at room temperature, de-foaming for 20-40 minutes, until the polydimethylsiloxane/multi-walled carbon nanotube conductive liquid has no bubble; (iv) uniformly mixing the silver-coated thermoplastic elastomer micro-porous beads obtained in step (i) with the polydimethylsiloxane/multi-walled carbon nanotube conductive liquid obtained in (iii), pouring into the prefabricated mold, placing in the oven to solidify to obtain the isolation elastomer complex micro-porous strain sensing material, then pre-compressing the isolation elastomer complex microporous strain sensing material, using the conductive silver paste to bond the copper foil to the two ends of the complex microporous material as electrodes, and drying at room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The density of the sensing material is 0.62-0.9 g/cm3. The electrical conductivity of the sensing material is 8.64x 10-5-0.25 seconds/minute. The thermoplastic elastomer is thermoplastic nylon elastomer, a thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer, a thermoplastic polyester elastomer, and/or a thermoplastic polyolefin elastomer. The length of the multi-walled carbon nanotubes is 10-30 mu m, the diameter is 10-20 nm, and the purity is less than 97%. The curing agent is vinyl-terminated poly-dimethyl-methylvinylsiloxane. Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the curing temperature of the isolated elastomer complex microporous strain sensing material is 60-90 degrees C for 1 hour 10 minutes to 1 hour 50 minutes.

PN US2020348608-A1; US11029613-B2
TI Method for cleaning extreme ultra violet (EUV)            radiation source apparatus, involves forming and            ejecting pressurized dry ice particles through cleaner            blasting nozzle toward residual material at nozzle of            target droplet generator.
AU CHEN Y
   TSAI M
   WANG S
   CHANG H
   CHEN L
   CHEN C
AE TAIWAN SEMICONDUCTOR MFG CO LTD (TSMC-C)
GA 2020A72316
AB    NOVELTY - The method (S100) involves forming (S110)                pressurized dry ice particles. The pressurized dry                ice particles are ejected (S120) through the                cleaner blasting nozzle toward a residual material                at a nozzle of the target droplet generator, thus                removing (S130) the residual material from the                target droplet generator. The residual material and                sublimated gaseous carbon dioxide are collected                (S140) from the pressurized dry ice particles. The                EUV radiation source apparatus comprises a target                droplet generator for generating a metal droplet                within a chamber. A cleaner blasting assembly has a                cleaner blasting nozzle and a cleaner supporting                unit.
   USE - Method for cleaning EUV radiation source                apparatus (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The stable generation of target droplets that                travel from the target droplet generator to the                zone of excitation at a uniform speed contributes                to efficiency and stability of the LPP EUV                radiation source. The EUV tool is operated under                low pressure or vacuum to prevent loss of exposure                light energy during imaging operations. The gaseous                carbon dioxide enlarges the crack and reduces                adhesion of the residual material. The droplet                generator is cleaned and maintenance and servicing                time and/or cost are reduced without contamination                or damaging the nozzle of the droplet generator.                The adaptation of the cleaning system allows an                improved process resulting in reduced manpower                required to perform the maintenance, and an                increased output of conforming servicing items of                the EUVL tools both of which ultimately result in a                cost savings. The downtime during maintenance and                servicing of EUVL tools is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an EUV                radiation source apparatus including the a target                droplet generator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a                method for cleaning a droplet generator of the EUV                radiation source apparatus.
   Method for cleaning a droplet generator of                the EUV radiation source apparatus (S100)
   Step for forming pressurized dry ice                particles (S110)
   Step for ejecting the pressurized dry ice                particles (S120)
   Step for removing the residual material                (S130)
   Step for collecting the residual material                (S140)

PT P
PN CN111748109-A; CN111748109-B
TI Preparing microsphere materials used for carbon            aerogel microsphere, by preparing dispersion liquid,            adding chitosan, soaking, forming chitosan aqueous            solution and oil phase into chitosan emulsion droplets,            and curing by coagulant.
AU CAI J
   ZHONG Y
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 2020A1107W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing microsphere materials                using chitosan solution having a pH value of 6-8,                involves (i) preparing dispersion liquid, (ii)                adding chitosan to the dispersion, controlling the                temperature at the freezing point to 35 degrees C,                passing carbon dioxide into the solution while                stirring to dissolve the chitosan, when the                chitosan is completely dissolved, stopping passing                the carbon dioxide into the solution, and soaking                to obtain a transparent chitosan solution with a pH                value of 6-8, and (iii) forming the chitosan                aqueous solution and the oil phase obtained in step                (ii) into chitosan emulsion droplets, and curing                the emulsion droplets by a coagulant or chemical                crosslinking agent to form chitosan microsphere                material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing microsphere                material using chitosan solution having pH value of                6-8 used for functional chitosan microsphere                material for preparing nitrogen-doped carbon                aerogel microsphere (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the degradation of chitosan                molecular chains and production cost, improves                production efficiency, and is carried out in                environmentally-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a chitosan microsphere material prepared                by the above method;
   (2) a method for preparing functional chitosan                microspheres, which involves introducing functional                organic or inorganic additives, or introducing                natural polymer or synthetic polymer for blending                in the preparation process of the chitosan                microsphere material, where the additives are added                by chitosan stock solution or coagulant, the                functional organic or inorganic additive comprises                plasticizers, reinforcing agents, refractory                additives, dyes, optical stabilizers, antibacterial                agents, conductive materials and/or surfactants,                and the natural polymer or synthetic polymer                comprises polymer nanofibers, cellulose and its                derivatives, animal protein, plant protein,                collagen, alginate, conductive polymer,                polyethylene glycol, polyvinyl alcohol or                conductive polymer;
   (3) a functional chitosan microsphere material                prepared by the above method; and
   (4) a method for preparing nitrogen-doped                carbon aerogel microspheres, which involves                exposing the chitosan microspheres or the                functional chitosan microspheres under the                protection of inert gas atmosphere, and performing                heat treatment at a temperature of more than 150                degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersion                  comprises urea aqueous solution and/or thiourea                  aqueous solution. The crosslinking agent used in                  step (iii) comprises aldehyde crosslinking agent,                  glycidyl ether crosslinking agent, epoxy                  compound, iridoid and polyphenol. The coagulant                  comprises mixed aqueous solutions of alcohols,                  salts, amides, dimethyl sulfoxide, ethyl acetate                  and/or acetone. Preferred Composition: The                  concentration of the urea aqueous solution is                  6-60 wt.%, preferably 18-60 wt.%. The                  concentration of the thiourea aqueous solution is                  3-14 wt.%, preferably 7-14 wt.%. The mass ratio                  of chitosan and dispersion in step (ii) is                  1:7-1:1000.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method of forming the                  chitosan emulsion droplets involves performing                  emulsification using a stirring device, a                  microfluidic device or a membrane emulsifier. The                  method for forming emulsion droplets using the                  membrane emulsifier involves pressing the                  chitosan solution into the oil phase through a                  microporous membrane with pressure to obtain                  emulsion droplets of uniform size. The method for                  forming emulsion droplets using the microfluidic                  equipment involves using a micro pump to                  transport the oil phase and the aqueous phase to                  two intersecting channels in the microfluidic                  chip, and using the oil phase as a continuous                  phase to separate the aqueous phase to form                  monodisperse emulsion droplets. The method for                  forming emulsion droplets using the stirring                  device involves mixing the oil phase and the                  aqueous phase, and performing mechanical stirring                  to disperse the aqueous phase into the oil phase                  to form emulsion droplets. The coagulant is used                  to form microspheres through physical                  crosslinking. Preferred Components: The oil phase                  in step (iii) comprises an oily substance that is                  liquid at room temperature and an oil-soluble                  emulsifier. The oily substance comprises liquid                  paraffin, petroleum ether, olive oil, cottonseed                  oil, soybean oil or sunflower oil. The oily                  emulsifier comprises sorbitan sesquioleate                  (Arlace 183), glycerol ether polymers (such as                  PO-500, PO-310), polyoxyethylene hydrogenated                  castor oil, Span 85 (RTM: Sorbitan trioleate),                  Span 80 (RTM: Sorbitan monooleate), Span 65 (RTM:                  Sorbitan tristearate), Tween 80 (RTM:                  Polyethylene sorbitol ester) or                  lipophilic-hydrophilic block copolymer. Preferred                  Composition: The concentration of the emulsifier                  in the oil phase is 0.5-10 wt.%. The volume ratio                  of the chitosan solution and the oil phase in                  step (iii) is 1:1-1:1000, preferably 1:2-1:100.                  The molar ratio of crosslinking agent and                  chitosan is 1:(0.1-10).

PN WO2020158777-A1; JP2020120737-A; JP2020120736-A; JP2020121118-A; JP2020185175-A; CN113316461-A; BR112021013710-A2; EP3919095-A1; IN202117036350-A; US2022096721-A1
TI Artificial-lung apparatus for removing carbon dioxide in blood, has flow straightening frame which is provided for directing gas which passes through gas exchanger in accordance with flow of blood back to gas exchanger in housing.
AU KIKUMOTO S
   YASUMURA N
   MINOHARA R
AE NIPRO CORP (NIPR-C)
   NIPRO CORP (NIPR-C)
   NIPRO CORP (NIPR-C)
   NIPRO CORP (NIPR-C)
GA 2020739766
AB    NOVELTY - The artificial-lung apparatus (1) has a housing (2) which is formed in a cylindrical shape blocked at both ends, and which has a blood inflow port (16) and a blood outflow port (17) and is arranged with the axial center in the transverse direction. A hollow fiber structure e.g. gas exchanger (43) is provided for performing gas exchange with blood as the blood flows from the blood inflow port to the blood outflow port. A flow straightening frame e.g. gas guidance portion (56) is provided for directing gas which passes through the gas exchanger in accordance with the flow of blood back to the gas exchanger in the housing.
   USE - Artificial-lung apparatus for removing carbon dioxide contained in blood and adds oxygen.
   ADVANTAGE - The artificial-lung apparatus which can suppress that the foam carried with the blood accumulates excessively is provided. The management of the tube which connects a blood inflow port and a blood outflow port, and an apparatus is made easy. The artificial-lung apparatus which can fully heat the blood is provided. The foam entering in a patient's structure is prevented. The blood outflow port prevents deviating from the port attaching portion. Since a blood leak is prevented in seal structure with low pressure resistance by comprising the blood outflow port so that rotation is possible, thus, the cost of the artificial-lung apparatus is reduced. The sanitary risk is reduced by mutually arranging the blood inflow port and the medium outflow port on the opposite side of the artificial-lung apparatus, implementing heat exchange sufficient by pouring the blood on the right side from the left side of the artificial-lung apparatus, and ensuring the flow path length.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the artificial-lung apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Artificial-lung apparatus (1)
   Housing (2)
   Blood inflow port (16)
   Blood outflow port (17)
   Hollow fiber structure (43)
   Flow straightening frame (56)

PN CN111426816-A
TI Particle swarm optimization-long short-term memory method for predicting concentration of dissolved gas in transformer oil, involves selecting concentration of components in oil, constructing prediction model and re-establishing.
AU LIU K
   GOU J
   LI H
   XU Y
   HE J
   WANG Q
   LIU T
   WU S
   CHEN L
   CHEN X
   RUAN J
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202069340J
AB    NOVELTY - Particle swarm optimization-long short-term memory (PSO-LSTM) method involves selecting concentration of dissolved hydrogen, methane, ethane, ethylene, acetylene, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide in the transformer oil as characteristic parameter, obtaining chromatographic sample data, normalizing the sample data to randomly divide the training set data and the test set data, constructing a long and short-term memory network prediction model, optimizing the long-term short-term memory network prediction model by particle swarm algorithm using training set data, determining the long-term short-term memory network prediction model, re-establishing the optimized long-short-term memory network prediction model, and using the test set data as the input variable of the optimized long and short-term memory network prediction model, and selecting parameters as output variables, and predicting the concentration of dissolved gas in transformer oil.
   USE - Particle swarm optimization-long short-term memory method for predicting concentration of dissolved gas in transformer oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The change of dissolved gas concentration in transformer oil can be predicted accurately.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Particle swarm optimization-long short-term memory (PSO-LSTM) method for predicting the concentration of dissolved gas in transformer oil involves selecting concentration of dissolved hydrogen, methane, ethane, ethylene, acetylene, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide in the transformer oil as characteristic parameter, obtaining chromatographic sample data of the characteristic parameters, normalizing the sample data to randomly divide the training set data and the test set data, constructing a long and short-term memory network prediction model including input layer, hidden layer, and output layer, optimizing the long-term short-term memory network prediction model by particle swarm algorithm using training set data, determining the long-term short-term memory network prediction model on the basis of satisfying the optimal evaluation index of the prediction model, re-establishing the optimized long-short-term memory network prediction model, and using the test set data as the input variable of the optimized long and short-term memory network prediction model, and selecting parameters as output variables to get the prediction results.

PN CN110894467-A
TI Culturing chlorella using starch fermentation wastewater anaerobic fermentation broth involves preparing culture liquid, and performing pretreatment of starch processing wastewater after anaerobic fermentation.
AU TAN X
   WAN X
   WEI L
   ZHAO X
   MENG J
   HUANG L
AE UNIV HUNAN TECHNOLOGY (UYHL-C)
GA 202023889D
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing chlorella using starch fermentation wastewater anaerobic fermentation broth involves preparing culture liquid, performing pretreatment of starch processing wastewater after anaerobic fermentation, so that TSS concentration in the wastewater is lower than 70mg/L to obtain starch anaerobic fermentation wastewater. The pretreatment of acidified organic wastewater is performed to make the TSS concentration in the wastewater less than 70mg/L to obtain wastewater acidification solution. The wastewater acidified solution obtained after the pretreatment is added to the starch anaerobic fermentation wastewater, and mixed to obtain culture solution. The proportion of fermentation wastewater is not more than 10 wt.% of starch anaerobic fermentation wastewater.
   USE - Method for culturing chlorella using starch fermentation wastewater anaerobic fermentation broth.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to cultur chlorella using starch fermentation wastewater anaerobic fermentation broth that realizes the transformation of high-concentration organic wastewater treatment from simple pollutant removal to resource recycling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Culturing chlorella using starch fermentation wastewater anaerobic fermentation broth involves preparing culture liquid, performing pretreatment of starch processing wastewater after anaerobic fermentation, so that TSS concentration in the wastewater is lower than 70mg/L to obtain starch anaerobic fermentation wastewater. The pretreatment of acidified organic wastewater is performed to make the TSS concentration in the wastewater less than 70mg/L to obtain wastewater acidification solution. The wastewater acidified solution obtained after the pretreatment is added to the starch anaerobic fermentation wastewater, and mixed to obtain culture solution. The proportion of fermentation wastewater is not more than 10 wt.% of starch anaerobic fermentation wastewater. The algae species grown in artificial medium are first acclimatized and cultured in the culture solution obtained, under conditions of light intensity of 10,000-14000 lux and appropriate temperature of 27-30 degrees C, cultivated and acclimated for 13-17 days to obtain acclimated algae species. The culture medium and domesticated algae species enters photobioreactor for cultivation, and temperature is controlled at 7-39 degrees C. The aeration is a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and air, volume ratio of carbon dioxide is 5-10%, and hourly aeration volume is 0.95-2 times the volume of the photoreactor and best hydraulic retention time is 3-5 days in spring, summer and autumn. The best hydraulic retention time is 7-13 days in winter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The wastewater acidification liquid is added in proportion of 4-6 wt.% of the starch anaerobic fermentation wastewater. The wastewater acidification liquid is acidified by the alcohol processing wastewater generated by fermentation and production of alcohol by granules precipitated and separated from the starch processing wastewater. The initial inoculation concentration of the artificial medium is 0.15 plus minus 0.02g/L.The volume ratio of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas of carbon dioxide and air is 5-8% which is mixed gas of carbon dioxide and air in tubular photobioreactor and medium carbon dioxide volume ratio is 8-10%. The aeration volume per hour is 0.95-1.65 times the volume of the photoreactor.

PN CN110885756-A
TI Culturing Chlorella by digesting sludge to obtain pretreated wastewater, acclimatizing in artificial medium, the culturing in pretreated wastewater for acclimatizing and culturing using pretreated wastewater in photobioreactor.
AU TAN X
   WAN X
   ZHAO X
   LU J
   WEI L
   MENG J
AE UNIV HUNAN TECHNOLOGY (UYHL-C)
GA 202022420W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for culturing Chlorella using sludge anaerobic digestion liquid involves (a) digesting remaining sludge discharged from the second settling tank of the municipal sewage treatment plant, performing anaerobic digestion and removing the suspended matter through pretreatment to obtain the sludge anaerobic fermentation broth and mixing with secondary effluent for concentration adjustment to obtain pretreated wastewater, (b) acclimatizing the algae species grown in artificial medium, continuously culturing in the pre-treatment wastewater under light intensity and acclimatizing to obtain acclimatized algae species and (c) adding pretreated wastewater and algae into photobioreactor and culturing under the optimal hydraulic retention time and aeration using mixed gas of carbon dioxide and air.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing Chlorella used as biomass diesel raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes recycling of waste water, which is treated in a low energy consumption manner, hence is environmentally-friendly. The method realizes wastewater purification and nutrient recycling, greenhouse gas capture and oil-rich microalgae biomass harvesting.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for culturing Chlorella using sludge anaerobic digestion liquid involves (a) digesting remaining sludge discharged from the second settling tank of the municipal sewage treatment plant, performing anaerobic digestion and removing the suspended matter through pretreatment to obtain the sludge anaerobic fermentation broth and mixing with secondary effluent for concentration adjustment to obtain pretreated wastewater, (b) acclimatizing the algae species grown in artificial medium, continuously culturing in the pre-treatment wastewater obtained in step (a) under light intensity of 10000-14000 lux at 27-30 degrees C and acclimatizing for 13-17 days to obtain acclimatized algae species and (c) adding pretreated wastewater and algae into photobioreactor and culturing at 7-39 degrees C under the optimal hydraulic retention time of 3-5 days in summer, 4-6 days in spring and autumn and 9-13 days in winter, pH is 5.7-8.1 in spring, 5.7- 8.1 in summer, 5.7- 7.3 in autumn and 6.8-8.1 in winter and aeration of 0.95-2 times per hour using mixed gas of 5-10% carbon dioxide and air.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the pretreatment includes precipitation and filtration. The step (a) involves feeding carbon dioxide or methane combustion gas produced by the anaerobic digestion into photobioreactor to supplement the carbon source, where the sludge anaerobic fermentation broth has total suspended solids (TSS) of less than 60 mg/l and the volume ratio of the sludge anaerobic fermentation broth to the secondary effluent is 1:1-2. In step (b), the initial inoculation concentration of the algal species is 0.15 plus minus 0.02 g/l. In step (c), when the photobioreactor is a tubular photoreactor, the mixed gas contains 5-8 vol.% carbon dioxide and the aeration amount is 1.4-2 times per hour and when the photobioreactor is a plate-type photoreactor, the mixed gas contains 8-10 vol.% carbon dioxide and the aeration amount is 0.95-1.65 times per hour. In step (c), the pH in spring is 5.7-6.5, the pH in summer is 7.6-8.1, the pH in autumn is 6.8-7.3 and the pH in winter is 6.8-7.3.
