PN CN110835571-A
TI Desulfurization method of raw coal based on ionic liquid-nonpolar supercritical fluid system comprises crushing raw coal, heating extraction kettle using microwave heating device,.
AU GONG L
   PENG Y
   LIU J
   ZHAO G
   TIAN G
   GUI X
AE SHANXI COKING COAL GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
   JIANGSU TANQINGYUAN CLEAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY RES INSTITU (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202020208M
AB    NOVELTY - Desulfurization method of raw coal based on ionic liquid-nonpolar supercritical fluid system comprises crushing raw coal, preparing coal samples for industrial analysis, elemental analysis, analysis the total sulfur content, regulation of organic sulfur content and adhesion index , (ii) preparation of ionic liquid-nonpolar supercritical fluid system comprising selecting an ionic liquid, ionic liquid is boron tetraflouride or hexafluorophosphate , selecting non-polar supercritical fluid as solvent, where, the non-polar supercritical fluid is carbon dioxide, n-hexane or toluene, the ionic liquid and solvent introducing coal sample in step into extraction kettle, sealing extraction kettle, pressurizing ionic liquid-non-polar supercritical fluid system into extraction kettle, heating extraction kettle using microwave heating device, desulfurization of coal samples, mechanically stirring , analysis, collecting coal samples for analysis and testing.
   USE - Used as desulfurization method of raw coal based on ionic liquid-nonpolar supercritical fluid system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly and saves energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Desulfurization method of raw coal based on ionic liquid-nonpolar supercritical fluid system, comprises crushing raw coal, preparing coal samples for industrial analysis, elemental analysis, analysis the total sulfur content, regulation of organic sulfur content and adhesion index , (ii) preparation of ionic liquid-nonpolar supercritical fluid system comprising selecting an ionic liquid, ionic liquid is boron tetraflouride or hexafluorophosphate , selecting non-polar supercritical fluid as solvent, where, the non-polar supercritical fluid is carbon dioxide, n-hexane or toluene, the ionic liquid and solvent in accordance with 0.05-0.25: 1 mol/l, introducing coal sample in step into extraction kettle, sealing extraction kettle, pressurizing ionic liquid-non-polar supercritical fluid system into extraction kettle, the fluid flow is 18-30 l/ hours and ratio of coal sample to ionic liquid-nonpolar supercritical fluid system is 0.5-2: 1, heating extraction kettle using microwave heating device, maintaining water bath at 100 degrees C, desulfurization of coal samples in an ionic liquid-nonpolar supercritical fluid system, mechanical stirring during reaction, analysis of coal samples, extract is obtained after desulfurization reaction, extracting extract from a non-polar supercritical fluid, collecting coal samples for analysis and testing and size of raw coal is crushed to 0.5 ~ 1mm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The extraction kettle is cleaned with a non-polar supercritical fluid and before adding material the testing is performed for leak tightnesS. The microwave frequency of microwave heating device is 2.45 GHz, the output power is 500-800W, and microwave irradiation time is 60-300 seconds. The mechanical stirring is performed by a stirrer, the stirrer is stirred for every 10-30 minutes, the stirring rate is 40-80 revolutions/minute and stirring time is 3-5 minutes. The catalytic reaction time is 1-24 hours. The non-polar supercritical fluid in extraction kettle is flowed to cooler for recycling.

PT P
PN CN110819019-A
TI Preparation of composite extruded polystyrene thermal insulation board involves mixing e.g. nanocellulose-modified silica aerogel and talc, melt-plasticizing, extruding, foaming, adding sodium bicarbonate foaming agent, and extruding.
AU GUO X
   XU P
AE NANJING JUNENG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202019127F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of composite extruded polystyrene thermal insulation board involves mixing crosslinked polystyrene, nanocellulose-modified silica aerogel, graphene and talc, adding obtained mixture to a twin-screw extruder, heating, melt-plasticizing, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide combined foaming agent into the melt-plasticized material, extruding and foaming in a twin-screw extruder, placing obtained product in a reactor, adding sodium bicarbonate foaming agent, extruding, molding, and cooling. The nanocellulose-modified silica aerogel is prepared by crushing Moso bamboo, sieving, adding lye, leaching, filtering, washing, adding an acid solution, adjusting pH to 4-6, extracting, filtering, washing, dispersing washed extract in an aqueous sulfuric acid solution, reacting, adding deionized water to stop the reaction, centrifuging, obtaining nanocellulose, adding the nanocellulose to a mixed solution of ethyl orthosilicate/anhydrous ethanol, heating, freezing, and drying.
   USE - Preparation of composite extruded polystyrene thermal insulation board.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of composite extruded polystyrene thermal insulation board with improved mechanical property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of composite extruded polystyrene thermal insulation board involves mixing crosslinked polystyrene, nanocellulose-modified silica aerogel, graphene and talc, adding obtained mixture to a twin-screw extruder, heating, pressurizing, melt-plasticizing, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide combined foaming agent into the melt-plasticized material, extruding and foaming in a twin-screw extruder under the rotation of the twin-screw, placing obtained product in a reactor, adding sodium bicarbonate foaming agent, extruding through the head of another twin-screw extruder, molding, and cooling. The nanocellulose-modified silica aerogel is prepared by crushing Moso bamboo, sieving, adding lye, leaching in a water bath at 60-90 degrees C, filtering, washing, adding an acid solution, adjusting pH to 4-6, extracting at 60-90 degrees C in a water bath, filtering, washing, dispersing washed extract in an aqueous sulfuric acid solution, reacting, adding deionized water to stop the reaction, centrifuging, obtaining nanocellulose, adding the nanocellulose to a mixed solution of ethyl orthosilicate/anhydrous ethanol, heating at 40-70 degrees C for 8-16 hours in a water bath, freezing at -20 degrees C to -10 degrees C, and drying for 12-24 hours in a vacuum freeze dryer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The lye is 3-8 %mass sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide solution. The acid solution is 8-15 %mass hydrochloric acid solution. The amount of graphene and talc is 2-4 pts. wt. and 1-3 pts. wt., respectively.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The sodium bicarbonate foaming agent is prepared by dissolving sodium bicarbonate in water, adding a dispersant, stirring, and adding obtained mixture to absolute ethanol. Preferred Composition: The supercritical carbon dioxide combined foaming agent contains carbon dioxide, ethanol and n-butane. The dispersant is sodium lauryl sulfate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The amount of crosslinked polystyrene and nanocellulose-modified silica aerogel is 60-80 pts. wt. and 5-10 pts. wt., respectively.

PN CN110624535-A; WO2021052257-A1; GB2592516-A
TI Preparing black tungsten acid bismuth            photocatalyst comprises ultrasonically dispersing e.g.            white bismuth tungstate, applying on quartz sheet,            drying, performing plasma discharge processing and            secondary treatment and passing reaction gas.
AU XU H
   ZHU X
   LI Q
   CHU J
   DU Y
   YANG J
   MO Z
   LI H
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
   UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 202002644F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing black tungsten acid bismuth                photocatalyst comprises using dielectric barrier                discharge to generate plasma, processing white                bismuth tungstate, reducing the bismuth elementary                to the tungsten acid bismuth surface using plasma                processing, promoting the separation of                photo-generated holes and electrons, expanding the                photo absorption range and improving the                photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction capability                comprising (a) ultrasonically dispersing white                bismuth tungstate and anhydrous ethanol, applying                the mixture on a quartz sheet and drying, (b)                placing quartz sheet with white bismuth tungstate                in a dielectric barrier discharge reactor,                performing plasma discharge processing, introducing                a reaction gas, (c) collecting tungsten acid                bismuth, ultrasonically dispersing in anhydrous                ethanol, applying the mixture on the quartz sheet                and drying and (d) placing the dried quartz sheet                with bismuth tungstate for secondary treatment and                passing the reaction gas.
   USE - The method is iseful for preparing black                tungsten acid bismuth photocatalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing black tungsten acid bismuth                photocatalyst comprises using dielectric barrier                discharge to generate plasma under different                atmosphere, processing white bismuth tungstate to                obtain black tungsten acid bismuth photocatalyst,                reducing the bismuth elementary to the tungsten                acid bismuth surface using plasma processing,                promoting the separation of photo-generated holes                and electrons, expanding the photo absorption range                and improving the photocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction capability comprising (a) weighing white                bismuth tungstate and anhydrous ethanol for                ultrasonically dispersing to obtain a uniform                mixture, uniformly applying the mixture on a quartz                sheet and drying, (b) placing the quartz sheet with                white bismuth tungstate in a dielectric barrier                discharge reactor, performing plasma discharge                processing with a certain power and time,                introducing a reaction gas at a constant speed                during the process, (c) collecting the tungsten                acid bismuth after first plasma treatment,                ultrasonically dispersing in anhydrous ethanol to                obtain a uniform mixture, uniformly applying the                mixture on the quartz sheet and drying and (d)                placing the completely dried quartz sheet with                bismuth tungstate in a dielectric barrier discharge                reactor for secondary treatment, passing the                reaction gas at a constant speed during the process                and finishing the processing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises                  ultrasonically dispersing at a power of 100-150W                  for 5-10 minutes. The step (b) comprises                  performing plasma discharge processing for 1-5                  minutes and introducing reaction gas at a flow                  rate of 100-200 ml/minute. The step (c) comprises                  ultrasonically dispersing at a power of 50-100 W                  for 3-5 minutes. The step (d) comprises changing                  the dielectric barrier discharge power,                  processing time and gas flow rate, performing                  secondary treatment for 5-15 minutes and passing                  the reaction gas at a flow rate of 200-300                  ml/minute. Preferred Components: The adding                  amount of white tungsten acid bismuth is 5-20 mg.                  The adding amount of anhydrous ethanol is 2-4 ml.                  The thickness of the quartz sheet is 0.5 mm. The                  power of dielectric barrier discharge in the step                  (a) is 50-100 W. The reaction gas is argon,                  ammonia or hydrogen gas. The adding amount of                  anhydrous ethanol is 1-2 ml. The power of                  dielectric barrier discharge in the step (d) is                  100-150 W.
DC E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); V05 (Valves, Discharge Tubes and CRTs)

PN CN110355970-A; CN110355970-B
TI Method for preparing colored thermoplastic            polyurethane foaming particles, involves taking            thermoplastic polyurethane resin,maleic anhydride            grafted ethylene-octene copolymer and organic pigment,            followed by shaping extruded material.
AU CHEN Y
   CHEN K
   DENG H
   ZHAO N
AE DONGGUAN YONPROUD PLASTIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
   DONGGUAN YONPROUD PLASTIC TECHNOLOGY CO (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 201989289G
AB    NOVELTY - A colored thermoplastic polyurethane foaming                particles preparing method involves taking                thermoplastic polyurethane resin, maleic anhydride                grafted ethylene-octene copolymer and organic                pigment, putting the raw materials into a stirrer,                stirring and mixing. The mixed material is put into                the twin-screw hopper and the temperature of the                first to fifth zones of the twin screw is adjusted                to a melting section, the material is completed the                melting process through first to fifth zones, a                reverse thread is arranged in sixth zone of the                twin screw, and nitrogen or carbon dioxide gas is                injected, the engaging blocks is arranged in the                seventh to ninth sections of the twin screw, and                the injected gas, the organic pigment, the                thermoplastic polyurethane resin and the maleic                anhydride grafted ethylene-octene copolymer are                mixed, cool down in the tenth to eleventh zones of                the twin screw, and extruded from the twin screw                die, the extruded material is cooled and shaped by                underwater ring cutting.
   USE - Method for preparing colored thermoplastic                polyurethane foaming particles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing colored                thermoplastic polyurethane foaming particles with                better popularization, in simple and cost effective                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A colored thermoplastic polyurethane foaming                particles preparing method involves taking 100 pts.                wt. thermoplastic polyurethane resin, 2-5 pts. wt.                maleic anhydride grafted ethylene-octene copolymer                and 1-3 pts. wt. organic pigment, putting the raw                materials into a stirrer and stirring and mixing.                The mixed material is put into the twin-screw                hopper and the temperature of the first to fifth                zones of the twin screw is adjusted to a melting                section, the material is completed the melting                process through first to fifth zones, a reverse                thread is arranged in sixth zone of the twin screw,                and nitrogen or carbon dioxide gas is injected, the                engaging blocks is arranged in the seventh to ninth                sections of the twin screw, and the injected gas,                the organic pigment, the thermoplastic polyurethane                resin and the maleic anhydride grafted                ethylene-octene copolymer are thoroughly mixed,                cool down in the tenth to eleventh zones of the                twin screw, and extruded from the twin screw die,                the extruded material is cooled and shaped by                underwater ring cutting.

PT P
PN CN110257153-A
TI Regenerating environment-friendly waste            lubricating oil by adding polyaluminum chloride into            waste lubricating oil, stirring, adding demulsifier,            stirring, sedimenting, vacuum distilling, separating,            reacting, and gas stripping and drying.
AU YANG W
AE CHONGQING LAIEN LUBRICATING OIL CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201991231J
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating method of environment-friendly                waste lubricating oil involves analyzing physical                and chemical properties and each component of waste                lubricating oil, qualitative and quantitative                determining non-ideal component and ideal                component, adding flocculating agent, first                stirring, adding demulsifier, second stirring,                sedimenting, taking waste lubricating oil, vacuum                distilling upper layer, separating, passing through                first stage membrane module under supercritical                carbon dioxide conditions, entering second stage                membrane module, reacting waste lubricating oil                under catalyst condition, removing non-ideal                components including various oxidation products and                additives in waste lubricating oil, and gas                stripping and drying. The first stage membrane                module membrane element is ceramic filter membrane.                The second stage membrane module membrane element                is composite nanofiltration membrane. The catalyst                is stable H-Y-type molecular sieve-alumina or                molybdenum nickel catalyst.
   USE - The method is used for regenerating                environment-friendly waste lubricating oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is green and environment-friendly,                has no environment pollution and comprehensive                utilization rate of waste lubricating oil reaching                to more than 90%, reduces waste lubricating oil to                more than 80%, increases membrane filtration flux,                and does not decompose and not produce waste water,                waste liquid and waste gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Regenerating method of environment-friendly                waste lubricating oil involves analyzing each                component and physical and chemical properties of                waste lubricating oil, qualitative and quantitative                determining ideal component and non-ideal component                in waste lubricating oil, adding flocculating agent                into waste lubricating oil, first stirring, adding                demulsifier, second stirring to obtain mixture,                sedimenting by sedimentation tank for 10 hours,                taking waste lubricating oil, vacuum distilling                upper layer waste lubricating oil, separating                pre-treated waste lubricating oil, passing                pre-treated waste lubricating oil through first                stage membrane module under supercritical carbon                dioxide conditions, entering second stage membrane                module, reacting waste lubricating oil under                catalyst condition, removing non-ideal components                including various oxidation products and additives                in waste lubricating oil, and gas stripping and                drying to obtain base oil and kerosene. The                membrane element of first stage membrane module is                ceramic filter membrane. The membrane element of                second stage membrane module is composite                nanofiltration membrane. The catalyst is molybdenum                nickel catalyst or stable H-Y-type molecular                sieve-alumina.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The composite                  nanofiltration membrane is prepared by dissolving                  polyethersulfone in polyvinylpyrrolidone,                  arranging 30 wt.% casting solution, stirring at                  45-50 degrees C for 5-7 hours, standing and                  defoaming at 45-50 degrees C for 2-3 hours,                  cooling to room temperature, pouring casting                  solution on glass film, putting into 10-25                  degrees C water-bath to solidify to film,                  dispersing graphene oxide in deionized water,                  preparing 1-15 mg/L water phase monomer solution,                  immersing polyethersulfone-based membrane for                  40-60 minutes, taking out, drying using drying                  machine, dissolving N,N-dimethylformamide in                  acetylene, configuring to 1-5 g/L organic phase                  monomer solution, soaking polyethersulfone-based                  membrane soaked in water phase monomer solution                  at 20 degrees C for 5 minutes, taking out, and                  oven drying at 50 degrees C for 10 minutes. First                  stirring is done at 70 degrees C for 2-4 hours.                  Second stirring is done at 50 degrees C for 1-3                  hours. Vacuum distilling is done at 100 degrees C                  at pressure of - 0.5 MPa. The first stage                  membrane module has pressure of 150 MPa,                  temperature of 40-60 degrees C and supercritical                  carbon dioxide flow rate of 10-20 m/second. The                  second stage membrane module has pressure of 0.6                  MPa and temperature of 80-90 degrees C.                  Hydrogenation refining is done at 250-330 degrees                  C at 0.5-1.0 MPa and space velocity of                  3-3.5/hour. Preferred Components: The                  flocculating agent is polyaluminum chloride. The                  demulsifier is AR type demulsifier.

The section includes lubricants of non-petroleum origin eg silicone oils (C10M).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN109971036-A
TI Preparing monodisperse polyethylene terephthalate transparent film for ultra-narrow distribution process for preparing calcium carbonate, involves making lime milk, adding prepared lime milk into slurry, adding crystal control agent.
AU MO S
   HE L
   LONG F
AE UNIV GUILIN TECHNOLOGY (UYGI-C)
   GUILIN WEISHI NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (GUIL-Non-standard)
GA 201960505S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing monodisperse polyethylene terephthalate transparent film involves making lime milk, adding prepared lime milk into 14-22% slurry of solid content, adding crystal control agent in slurry, then introducing carbon dioxide for carbonization reaction to prime pH is 6.0-8.0, to obtain calcium carbonate pulp, crystal form control agent is composed of polyethylene glycol and polyacrylamide or sodium polyacrylate and hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide compound or ethyl cellulose and carboxymethylcellulose complex or sodium hexametaphosphate and polyvinyl pyrrolidone composite. The crystal control agent is calcium carbonate dry base mass, prepared calcium carbonate pulp has solid content of 20-40% of slurry, use stripping machine or ball mill dispersing agglomerated particles. The dispersed calcium carbonate slurry pump added into sealed container, crystallizing agentis added, crystallize to obtain complete crystal suspension.
   USE - Method for preparing monodisperse polyethylene terephthalate transparent film for ultra-narrow distribution process for preparing calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare monodisperse polyethylene terephthalate transparent film that has high film forming property, safety, good heat resistance and improves dispersing effect of the product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing monodisperse polyethylene terephthalate transparent film involves making lime milk, adding prepared lime milk into 14-22% slurry of solid content, adding crystal control agent in slurry, then introducing carbon dioxide for carbonization reaction to prime pH is 6.0-8.0, to obtain calcium carbonate pulp, crystal form control agent is composed of polyethylene glycol and polyacrylamide or sodium polyacrylate and hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide compound or ethyl cellulose and carboxymethylcellulose complex or sodium hexametaphosphate and polyvinyl pyrrolidone composite. The crystal control agent is calcium carbonate dry base mass, prepared calcium carbonate pulp has solid content of 20-40% of slurry, use stripping machine or ball mill dispersing agglomerated particles. The dispersed calcium carbonate slurry pump added into sealed container, crystallizing agentis added, crystallize to obtain complete crystal suspension, crystallization agent is calcium carbonate dry basis mass of prepare in complete crystal of calcium carbonate grain suspension obtained by adding activating agent for surface treatment, then filtering, drying, crushing to obtain powder product. The amount of activator is calcium carbonate dry basis mass of 1-5%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The crystallizing agent is ethanol, propanol, butanol, isopropanol, ethylene glycol, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, nitric acid, ammonia water and urea. The activating agent is amphiphilic polyacrylic acid block copolymer and polystyrene compound is pentaerythritol stearate. Preferred Conditions: The carbide processed starting temperature is less than 55 degrees C, kiln carbon dioxide content is more than 28%.

PN CN109681170-A; CN109681170-B
TI Liquid discharge and gas collection by foam drainage agent by mixing foam drainage agent with water to obtain foam drainage agent solution, fully contacting with oil and gas to form foam liquid, and expelling water or oil-water mixture.
AU SHEN Z
   CHEN A
   HE X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 201941068Y
AB    NOVELTY - Liquid discharge and gas collection by foam drainage agent comprise mixing foam drainage agent (I) with water to obtain foam drainage agent solution, fully contacting with oil and gas to form foam liquid, and expelling the water or oil-water mixture in the foam drainage agent solution, where gas is air, nitrogen, methane and/or natural gas optionally containing hydrogen sulfide (H2S) or carbon dioxide (CO2) acidic gases, and the oil is coal oil, crude oil and/or condensate.
   USE - The method is for liquid discharge and gas collection by foam drainage agent. It is useful for acidic high temperature and high salt ultra-deep gas well water discharge and gas collection.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of prior art foam drainage agent, which has poor high temperature and high salt performance in an acidic environment containing H2S and CO2.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Liquid discharge and gas collection by foam drainage agent comprise mixing foam drainage agent with water to obtain foam drainage agent solution, fully contacting with oil and gas to form foam liquid, and expelling the water or oil-water mixture in the foam drainage agent solution, where the foam drainage agent is of formula (I). The gas is air, nitrogen, methane and/or natural gas optionally containing hydrogen sulfide (H2S) or carbon dioxide (CO2) acidic gases. The oil is coal oil, crude oil and/or condensate.
   R1=4-32C hydrocarbyl or substituted hydrocarbyl;
   R2-R4=H, 1-5C alkyl carboxylate or substituted alkyl carboxylate, 1-5C alkyl sulfonate or substituted alkyl sulfonate, 1-5C alkylphosphate or substituted alkylphosphate, and/or 1-5C alkylsulfate or substituted alkylsulfate, and not simultaneously H;
   m=number of fragments of -N(A)CH2CH2-, preferably 1-10;
   A=substituent of formula (A2);
   1-s3=adduct number of propoxy, preferably 0-30; and
   r1-r3=adduct number of ethoxy, preferably 0-30, where s1+s2+mx s3 and r1+r2+mx r3 are not zero at the same time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The gas is nitrogen, methane and/or natural gas having H2S and CO2 content of 15-35%. The oil is kerosene and/or condensate.

PT P
PN CN109368903-A
TI Treating metallurgical wastewater by membrane            integration technology comprises sending untreated            metallurgical wastewater into reaction tank through a            pipeline, and adding sodium hydroxide into reaction            tank.
AU ZHANG D
   CHEN Y
   SU N
   ZHAO J
   LI G
   YANG P
   SU X
AE UNIV LANZHOU TECHNOLOGY (UYLZ-C)
GA 201920423V
AB    NOVELTY - Treating metallurgical wastewater by membrane                integration technology comprises (i) sending the                untreated metallurgical wastewater into reaction                tank through a pipeline, and adding sodium                hydroxide into reaction tank to convert HCO3- in                the waste liquid into CO32-; (ii) processing the                metallurgical wastewater after the reaction by the                ultrafiltration system and the nanofiltration                system, draining the filtrate concentrate and                filtering the permeate; (iii) sending the obtained                nanofiltration concentrated liquid into a reverse                osmosis system through a pipeline for concentration                treatment, and obtaining a reverse osmosis                concentrate and a reverse osmosis permeate; and                (iv) feeding the obtained reverse osmosis                concentrate into the reaction tank through a                pipeline, and introducing carbon dioxide or waste                flue gas into the reaction tank, sending the                concentrated liquid into the heating and                evaporation section for heating and evaporation                treatment, and evaporating the water.
   USE - The method is useful for treating                metallurgical wastewater by membrane integration                technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and ensures low energy                consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating metallurgical wastewater by membrane                integration technology comprises (i) sending the                untreated metallurgical wastewater into reaction                tank through a pipeline, and adding sodium                hydroxide into reaction tank to convert HCO3- in                the waste liquid into CO32-; (ii) processing the                metallurgical wastewater after the reaction by the                ultrafiltration system and the nanofiltration                system, draining the filtrate concentrate and                filtering the permeate; (iii) sending the obtained                nanofiltration concentrated liquid into a reverse                osmosis system through a pipeline for concentration                treatment, and obtaining a reverse osmosis                concentrate and a reverse osmosis permeate; (iv)                feeding the obtained reverse osmosis concentrate                into the reaction tank through a pipeline, and                introducing carbon dioxide or waste flue gas into                the reaction tank, sending the concentrated liquid                into the heating and evaporation section for                heating and evaporation treatment, evaporating the                water in the concentrated liquid to obtain                concentrated liquid containing crystals; (v)                centrifugally separating the concentrate containing                crystals, obtaining solid sodium bicarbonate, and                heating and crystallizing the separated mother                liquor, and obtaining sodium sulfate and sodium                carbonate; (vi) introducing the obtained                nanofiltration permeate into the salt pool through                a pipeline, and adding sodium chloride and                obtaining the caustic soda; and (v) sending the                obtained reverse osmosis permeate into a production                tank through a pipeline, and collecting and                reusing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  total salt content in the metallurgical                  wastewater is 60-80 g/l, the content of sodium                  chloride is 25-30 g/l, the content of sodium                  sulfate is 35-40 g/l, the content of sodium                  carbonate is 5-7 g/l, and the sodium bicarbonate                  content is 2-4 g/l. In the step (i), the sodium                  hydroxide having mass fraction of 20-30% is used,                  reacting with sodium carbonate in the wastewater                  to convert all of the carbonate ions into                  bicarbonate ions and the amount of sodium                  hydroxide added is 1-1.1 times that of                  bicarbonate ions in the wastewater. In the step                  (ii), the wastewater from the ultrafiltration                  system enters the nanofiltration membrane module                  through a booster pump at 2-3.5 Mpa, and the                  treatment temperature is 25 degrees C, the                  nanofiltration concentrate is waste water                  containing divalent ions and high-valent ions,                  the total content of sulfate ions and carbonate                  ions in the nanofiltration permeate is less than                  5 g/l accordance with industrial alkali                  conditions. In the step (vi), sodium chloride is                  additionally added into the salt pool and the                  sodium chloride content in the solution is                  170-240 g/l. Preferred Method: The reverse                  osmosis concentrate is a salt solution containing                  sodium sulfate, sodium carbonate and trace amount                  of sodium chloride, the amount of carbon dioxide                  introduced is 1-1.1 times the amount of sodium                  hydroxide added, so that CO32- is converted to                  HCO3- and treating metallurgical wastewater by a                  membrane integration process. In the step (v),                  the solid sodium bicarbonate is decomposed into                  carbon dioxide and sodium carbonate after heating                  after crystallization.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)

AE ANHUI WANCHUN DAILY CHEM IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019095429
AB    NOVELTY - Toothpaste comprises 10-20 pts. wt. Cinnamomum camphora powder, 10-12 pts. wt. Phyllostachys heterocycla powder, 3-5 pts. wt. Asarum, 1-2 pts. wt. rose petals, 1-3 pts. wt. ginger, 4-6 pts. wt. fruit of Zanthoxylum armatum, 5-7 pts. wt. Momordica grosvenori, 2-3 pts. wt. yam, 3-5 pts. wt. attapulgite, 10-20 pts. wt. sodium alginate, 10-20 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol, 20-30 pts. wt. xanthan gum, 1-2 pts. wt. cocamidopropyl betaine, and 2-4 pts. wt. sodium chloride.
   USE - Toothpaste.
   ADVANTAGE - The toothpaste has good stain removing effect, prolonged effect and good whitening effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the toothpaste comprising adding Cinnamomum camphora powder and Phyllostachys heterocycla powder into ball milling pot, ball milling for 10-20 minutes at 2-4 degrees C, reverse ball milling for 10-20 minutes, repeating ball milling step for 3-4 times, heating to 70-78 degrees C, and ball milling for 3-4 times to obtain mixed powder; adding mixed powder into drying cellar, steam drying at 120-130 degrees C for 10-15 hours, transferring substances into sealed environment, introducing nitrogen at 0.5-1 MPa, drying at 70-74 degrees C until water content is 7-9%, putting into carbonization kiln, and performing carbonization treatment to obtain carbonized powder; washing Asarum, rose petals, raw ginger, fruit of Zanthoxylum planispinum, fructus momordicae and yam with water, naturally drying, putting into vacuum drying box, drying at 50-55 degrees C for 12-15 hours, taking out and grinding to 10-1000 mu m granules, microwave treating for 10-14 seconds, adding into supercritical carbon dioxide extracting tank, introducing carbon dioxide, and extracting for 80-100 minutes at 30-32 degrees C; and adding carbonized powder into extracting solution, adding attapulgite, stirring at 70-73 degrees C and 900-1000 revolutions/minute for 20-30 minutes, adding sodium alginate, polyvinyl alcohol, xanthan gum, cocamidepropyl betaine and sodium chloride, and grinding until fineness is 10-100 mu m.

   USE - Used as auxiliary agent modified copper-based catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: alleviates the negative effect of by-product water on the performance of the catalyst; improves the stability of copper-based catalysts; improves 8-20% stability; and has excellent catalyst activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing (i) dissolving copper(II) nitrate, zinc nitrate and auxiliary metal nitrate in deionized water, then adding into a nitrate mixed solution having a total metal ion concentration of 1-2 mol/l, dissolving sodium carbonate in deionized water, and then adding into an alkali solution having a concentration of 1-2 mol/l, and (ii) adding sodium carbonate solution into the nitrate mixed solution, adjusting the sodium carbonate droplet speed to control the pH of the suspension to 7-7.5, adding the nitrate mixed solution dropwise, continuing stirring, aging for 1-2 hours, then hot filtering, washing and drying for overnight to obtain precursor, calcining the precursor at a temperature of 350-450 degrees C, grinding, tableting and granulating to obtain catalyst has 20-40 mesh; and
   (2) use of catalyst for reaction of syngas hydrogenation to methanol on a fixed bed reactor comprising first reducing catalyst under normal pressure in a hydrogen/nitrogen mixture atmosphere, the reduction temperature is 240-260 degrees C, the reduction time is 4-6 hours and the air-fuel ratio is 4500-9000 ml/g/h, the reaction pressure is 3-5 MPa, and the reaction temperature is 230-250 degrees C, carrying out synthesis gas hydrogenation reaction of methanol under the reaction conditions of a space velocity of 3000-6000 ml/g/h, the volume fraction of syngas components is 54-64 wt.% hydrogen gas, 24-32 wt.% carbon dioxide, 24-8 wt.% carbon dioxide, 4-10 wt.% argon.

PN CN108946680-A
TI Method for preparing aluminum titanate powder and co-producing acid, involves mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier, grinding, calcining, separating to obtain alumina powder, and preparing aluminum titanate powder.
AU CHEN X
   LI J
   WANG S
   LI M
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2018A0556Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing aluminum titanate powder and co-producing acid, involves mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier and grinding, sending to the kiln for calcining (I) to obtain clinker, dissolving clinker and subjecting to solid-liquid separation to obtain separated solution and residue, adding carbon dioxide to separated solution, filtering, washing, drying, burning and pulverizing to obtain alumina powder, mixing alumina powder with titanium oxide, magnesium oxide, cerium oxide, iron oxide, silicon oxide, zirconium oxide and cobalt oxide, adding deionized water, mixing and grinding, drying to obtain mixture, sintering, cooling, pulverizing, adding deionized water, spray drying to obtain aluminum titanate powder, subjecting residue to flotation, separating sulfide, placing sulfide in 30-50% oxygen-rich environment, calcining (II), catalyzing flue gas by vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing by concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric acid.
   USE - Method for preparing aluminum titanate powder and co-producing acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of high quality aluminum titanate powder and co-production of acid by simple and economical process, and achieves high utilization rate of waste residue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation of aluminum titanate powder to produce acid involves mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier and grinding to make raw materials, sending to the kiln for calcining (I) to obtain clinker, dissolving clinker and subjecting to solid-liquid separation to obtain separated solution and residue, adding carbon dioxide to separated solution until white precipitate is not generated, filtering off the white precipitate, washing, drying, burning and pulverizing to obtain alumina powder, mixing alumina powder with titanium oxide, magnesium oxide, cerium oxide, iron oxide, silicon oxide, zirconium oxide and cobalt oxide, adding deionized water, mixing and grinding, drying to obtain mixture, sintering the mixture, cooling, pulverizing, adding deionized water, spray drying to obtain aluminum titanate powder, subjecting residue to flotation, separating sulfide, placing sulfide in 30-50% oxygen-rich environment, calcining (II) at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours, catalyzing flue gas by vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing by concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The weight ratio of phosphogypsum and fly ash is 1:(1-1.8). The additive ratio is 1 according to sum of sodium oxide and aluminum oxide and iron oxide in the raw material is 1:1. The mixing ratio of modifier is 10-25 of total weight of the raw material. The kiln is industrial rotary kiln, industrial tunnel kiln or industrial vertical kiln. The aluminum titanate powder comprises 30-35 pts. wt. alumina powder, 28-33 pts. wt. titanium oxide, 3-5 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 1-2 pts. wt. cerium oxide, 2-3 pts. wt. iron oxide, 1-2 pts. wt. silicon oxide, 1-2 pts. wt. zirconia and 0.5-1 pt. wt. cobalt oxide. The liquid to solid ratio at the time of elution is (4-6):1. Preferred Properties: The particle diameter of mixed material is 300-400 mu m. Preferred Conditions: The calcining (I) temperature is 1000-1350 degrees C and calcining (I) time is 1-2 hours. The sintering temperature of mixture is 1300-1400 degrees C and sintering time is 2-3 hours. The burning time is 3-5 hours and burning temperature is 800-1200 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN108855194-A; CN108855194-B
TI Highly catalytically active nitrogen-doped nano-BiOX-type photocatalyst has molecular formula, and is prepared by heating fine bismuth to obtain melt and introducing nitrogen into melt with continuously stirring and heating.
AU ZHANG L
AE ZHANG L (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201895690U
AB    NOVELTY - Highly catalytically active nitrogen-doped nano-BiOX-type photocatalyst has molecular formula which is represented as (BiOX)1-yNy, where X is selected from chloride, bromide and iodide, and y equals to 0.25-0.35%.
   USE - Highly catalytically active nitrogen-doped nano-BiOX-type photocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The highly catalytically active nitrogen-doped nano-BiOX-type photocatalyst improves electronmobility and catalytic activity of photocatalyst, and increases separation rate of electron.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing nitrogen-doped nano-BiOX-type photocatalyst, which involves:
   (A) heating fine bismuth at 280-300 degrees C to obtain melt, introducing nitrogen into melt with continuously stirring and keeping temperature for 20-50 minutes;
   (B) heating continuously at 860-950 degrees C to obtain hot melt, passing superheated melt through draft tube into atomizing device, where atomizing gas is continuously crushed by hotmelt runnel to obtain superfine bead-bead in oxygen combustion to obtain liquid bismuth oxide;
   (C) adding bismuth oxide under high-speed stirring at 600-800 revolutions/minute at 60-80 degrees C in excess of hydrogen halide HX to obtain clear solution, adding ammonium aqueous solutions with constant stirring for 20-40 minutes;
   (D) reducing speed to 400 revolutions/minute with continuously stirring for 20-40 minutes, and reducing speed to 100 revolutions/minute then cooling naturally to obtain BiOX colloid;
   (E) separating colloid at centrifugal speed of 4000-6000 revolutions/minute for 10-20 minutes, centrifuging obtained solid material;
   (F) washing with distilled water and absolute ethyl alcohol for 3 times at pressure of 8-10 megaPascal at 35 degrees C under flow rate of carbon dioxide at rate of 5-10 m/s;
   (G) replacing anhydrous ethanol as liquid carbon dioxide, recovering normal pressure, cooling to room temperature, preferably at 450 degrees C for 150 minutes to obtain BiOX gel;
   (H) placing BiOX gel placed in reactor, under normal pressure uniformly and non-balance nitrogen direct current plasma followed by nitrogen doping reaction; and
   (I) reacting for 10-15 minutes and finishing reaction to obtain nitrogen-doped nano BiOX photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The flow rate of nitrogen is 2-5m/s. The diameter of draft tube is 1-4mm. The flow speed of atomized gas is 60-100m/s. The temperature of atomized gas is 80-100 degrees C. Preferred Components: The atomizing gas is selected from air, nitrogen gas and argon gas. The HX is selected from hydrogen chloride, hydrogen bromide and hydrogen iodide. Preferred Composition: The mass concentration of ammonia aqueous solution is 15-25 wt.%.

PN CN108706550-A
TI Producing acid by combining phosphogypsum and red mud comprises e.g. mixing phosphogypsum, red mud, additives and modifying agent, grinding, drying, sintering, calcining, generating flue gas by the calcination and absorbing.
AU CHEN X
   MAO X
   ZHAO P
   WANG L
   ZHENG K
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2018861383
AB    NOVELTY - Producing acid by combining phosphogypsum and red mud comprises e.g. (i) mixing phosphogypsum, red mud, additives and modifying agent and grinding to obtain raw materials and sending into the kiln for roasting; (ii) clinking the clinker and performing solid-liquid separation; (iii) adding carbon dioxide into the above separated solution until white precipitate no longer occurs, filtering white precipitate, washing, drying, burning and pulverizing; (iv) mixing alumina powder with ferric oxide, zirconium oxide, zinc oxide, manganese oxide, titanium oxide and magnesium oxide, drying to obtain a mixed powder, carrying out compression-molding of mixed powder and sintering to obtain a heat-resistant plate; (v) subjecting obtained residue to flotation, and separating sulfide; and (vi) placing separated sulfide in oxygen-rich environment, calcining, generating flue gas by the calcination and absorbing with concentrated sulfuric acid to produce sulfuric acid.
   USE - The method is useful for producing acid by combining phosphogypsum and red mud.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical and simple; has high utilization rate; and produces heat-resistant plate with high compressive strength, good heat resistance and strong thermal shock resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing acid by combining phosphogypsum and red mud comprises (i) mixing phosphogypsum, red mud, additives and modifying agent and grinding to obtain raw materials and sending into the kiln for roasting to obtain clinker; (ii) clinking the clinker and performing solid-liquid separation; (iii) adding carbon dioxide into the above separated solution until white precipitate no longer occurs, filtering off the white precipitate, washing, drying, burning and pulverizing to obtain alumina powder; (iv) mixing alumina powder with ferric oxide, zirconium oxide, zinc oxide, manganese oxide, titanium oxide and magnesium oxide, drying to obtain a mixed powder, carrying out compression-molding of mixed powder and sintering to obtain a heat-resistant plate; (v) subjecting obtained residue to flotation, and separating sulfide; and (vi) placing separated sulfide in 30-50% oxygen-rich environment, calcining at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours, generating flue gas by the calcination which is catalyzed by vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing with concentrated sulfuric acid to produce sulfuric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The kiln in step (i) is industrial rotary kiln, an industrial tunnel kiln or an industrial vertical kiln.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The red mud in step (i) is a red mud produced by the Bayer process for producing alumina, additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda and modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The heat-resistant plate in step (iv) comprises 30-35 pts. wt. alumina powder, 15-20 pts. wt. iron oxide, 7-10 pts. wt. zirconia, 10-15 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 5-8 pts. wt. manganese oxide, 3-4 pts. wt. titanium oxide and 2-3 pts. wt. magnesium oxide Preferred Composition: The mixed ratio of phosphogypsum and the red mud in step (i) is 1:0.7-1.8, additive addition ratio is 1:1 according to the molecular ratio of the sum of sodium oxide and aluminum oxide and ferric oxide contained in the raw meal, and mixing ratio of the modifier is 10-25 wt.% of total raw meal. Preferred Method: The roasting in step (i) is carried out for 1-2 hours at 1000-1350 degrees C. The step (ii) comprises grinding clinker firstly ground and dissolving, where liquid and solid ratio at the time of dissolution is 4-6:1. The burning in step (ii) is carried out at 900-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours. The drying in step (iv) is carried out at 100-120 degrees C and the pressing pressure is 100-120 MPa. The sintering in step (iv) is carried out at 1350-1450 degrees C for 2-3 hours.

PN CN108693131-A
TI Method for judging tobacco chlorosis, involves            selecting tobacco Trichoplusia collection of sample,            utilizing sterile tissue culture seedling, followed by            selecting high growth tobacco seedling and            transplanting in water culture chamber.
AU CHEN J
   CHEN Z
   LI X
   ZZHANG C
   YANG G
   ZHANG T
   XU L
   DENG L
   WANG J
AE CHINA TOBACCO YUNNAN IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 201885013Y
AB    NOVELTY - A tobacco chlorosis judging method involves                selecting the tobacco Trichoplusia collection of                sample, utilizing a sterile tissue culture                seedling, after the tobacco seedling is reached                about 10 cm, selecting high growth and diameter                tobacco seedling, transplanting in water culture                chamber, entering into the group period, putting                the tobacco leaf into the agate mortar, freezing                and grinding into powder, and performing freeze                drying to obtain the ground powder. The ground                powder sample is subjected to infrared scanning,                performed infrared testing, scanned automatically                for deducting the interference of water and carbon                dioxide to obtain the original spectrum to                absorbance. The automatic baseline correction and                normalization process are performed to obtain                corresponding standard spectrum. The result is                analyzed and judged according to the standardized                spectrum for judging when satisfying the conditions                as given in the specification.
   USE - Method for judging tobacco chlorosis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables judging the tobacco                chlorosis with effectively improving the quality of                the tobacco in rapid, accurate and cost-effective                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A tobacco chlorosis judging method involves                selecting the tobacco Trichoplusia collection of                sample, utilizing a sterile tissue culture                seedling, after the tobacco seedling is reached                about 10 cm, selecting high growth and diameter                tobacco seedling, transplanting in water culture                chamber, entering into the group period or long or                bud period sampling, taking one or more pieces of                tobacco leaf in middle sheet tobacco leaf of 8-12,                preparing the sample by removing the tobacco leaf                vein by blade, putting the tobacco leaf into the                agate mortar under liquid nitrogen environment,                freezing and grinding into powder, and performing                freeze drying to obtain the ground powder. The                ground powder sample is subjected to infrared                scanning, performed infrared testing, scanned                automatically for deducting the interference of                water and carbon dioxide to obtain the original                spectrum to absorbance. The automatic baseline                correction and normalization process are performed                to obtain corresponding standard spectrum. The                result is analyzed and judged according to the                standardized spectrum for judging when satisfying                the conditions as given in the specification.

PN CN108686129-A
TI Traditional Chinese medicinal ginseng essential oil eye patch comprises hydrophilic cataplasm and ginseng essential oil.
AU MIAO M
   BAI M
   LIU B
AE UNIV HENAN CHINESE MEDICINE (HECO-C)
GA 2018862685
AB    NOVELTY - Traditional Chinese medicinal ginseng essential oil eye patch comprises hydrophilic cataplasm and ginseng essential oil. The hydrophilic cataplasm comprises kappa M, polyvinyl alcohol, gelatin and polyacrylic acid in a ratio of 1:1:4:0.1 to obtain eye patch. The ginseng essential oil comprises 4-6 ml semen celosiae essential oil, 4-6 ml pale butterflybush flower essential oil, 8-12 ml Chrysanthemum essential oil, 8-12 ml Salvia miltiorrhiza essential oil, 8-12 ml licorice essential oil, 4-6 ml Lophatherum gracile essential oil, 4-6 ml Inula flower essential oil, 4-6 ml medlar essential oil and 4-6 ml rose essential oil. The eye patch comprises licorice essential oil, Salvia miltiorrhiza and Chrysanthemum. The semen celosiae essential oil is prepared by crushing semen celosiae into coarse powder, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction of the mixture, extracting the mixture and separating the mixture.
   USE - The traditional Chinese medicinal eye patch is useful for treating dry eye and xerophthalmia.
   ADVANTAGE - The eye patch is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Traditional Chinese medicinal ginseng essential oil eye patch comprises hydrophilic cataplasm and ginseng essential oil. The hydrophilic cataplasm comprises kappa M, polyvinyl alcohol, gelatin and polyacrylic acid in a ratio of 1:1:4:0.1 to obtain eye patch. The ginseng essential oil comprises 4-6 ml semen celosiae essential oil, 4-6 ml pale butterflybush flower essential oil, 8-12 ml Chrysanthemum essential oil, 8-12 ml Salvia miltiorrhiza essential oil, 8-12 ml licorice essential oil, 4-6 ml Lophatherum gracile essential oil, 4-6 ml Inula flower essential oil, 4-6 ml medlar essential oil and 4-6 ml rose essential oil. The eye patch comprises 0.1 ml licorice essential oil, Salvia miltiorrhiza and Chrysanthemum. The eye patch is adhered on the eye. The semen celosiae essential oil is prepared by crushing semen celosiae into coarse powder of 10-20 meshes sieve, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction of the mixture, extracting the mixture at 42-50 degrees C and extracting pressure of 20-25 MPa, and separating the mixture at 50-60 degrees C and separating pressure of 4.5-5.5 MPa for 1.5-2.5 hours.

AU INVENTOR U
AE GUANGDONG MINGCHENTANG HEALTH IND SHARES (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201881427V
AB    NOVELTY - An external spraying agent comprises 20-50 wt.%, preferably 38.8 wt.% purified water, 20-60 wt.%, preferably 40 wt.% ethanol, 0.1-0.6 wt.%, preferably 0.2 wt.% menthol, 0.01-0.6 wt.%, preferably 0.1 wt.% borneol, 1-8 wt.%, preferably 4 wt.% thyme extract, 1-8 wt.%, preferably 4 wt.% golden chamomile extract, 1-8 wt.%, preferably 4 wt.% honeysuckle flower extract, 1-8 wt.%, preferably 4 wt.% citronella oil, 1-8 wt.%, preferably 4 wt.% permanent flower extract, and 5-12 wt.% or 0.9 wt.% flavor.
   USE - External spraying agent for removing miliaria.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the spraying agent, which involves drying citronella at low temperature, pulverizing, sieving through 20 mesh sieve, adding 200 g citronella powder in an extractor, cooling carbon dioxide in a condenser, heating the extractor and separator, pressurizing the system after it reaches to 45 degrees C, adjusting the carbon dioxide flow rate to 30 kg/hour, performing extraction in cycles, maintaining the constant temperature and constant pressure of 15 MPa and collecting citronella oil after 70 minutes, removing impurities and non-medicinal portions from permanent flowers, honeysuckle, thyme and golden chamomile, adding purified water, heating to 80-100 degrees C, boiling for 1-2 hours, filtering, cooling to room temperature, adding ethanol to make the alcohol content 50-70%, stirring, refrigerating for 24-72 hours, filtering, recovering ethanol from the filtrate, concentrating at 45-60 degrees C to obtain a concentrate containing permanent flower extract, honeysuckle extract, thyme extract and golden chamomile extract, adding and heating the purified water in a stirring pot, heating to 80 degrees C, heat-preserving for 20 minutes, cooling until the temperature drops to 30 degrees C, adding ethanol, cooling until the temperature drops to 25 degrees C or room temperature, adding menthol, borneol, permanent flower extract, honeysuckle extract, thyme extract and golden chamomile extract, stirring at 30 rpm for 20 minutes, dissolving, cooling to 22 degrees C or room temperature, adding flavor and citronella oil, stirring at 25 rpm for 30 minutes, inspecting and discharging.

AU YANG F
   TANG G
   DENG H
   BIAN Z
   LI Z
   FAN Z
   WANG Y
   LIU S
AE NAT TOBACCO QUALITY SUPERVISION & INSPEC (CNTA-C)
GA 2018652098
AB    NOVELTY - A enantiomers of chiral pesticide quizalofop and pirfenoxaline separating and determining method involves preparing sample, subjecting sample to convergent chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry, separating the elution peaks, according to the elution peak retention time, the target compound quantitative ion pair and the qualitative ion pair to obtain the chiral pesticide enantiomers and supercritical carbon dioxide/ethanol, according to the chromatographic and mass spectrometric condition and record the peak corresponding to each enantiomer, determining the area by the concentration value of each enantiomer as the independent variable and the corresponding peak area is used as the dependent variable to obtain the linear regression equation of R-quinfloxacin and R-piroxifene and the peak area corresponding to each enantiomer is substituted into the linear regression equation to obtain the concentration of each enantiomer in the sample.
   USE - Method for separating and determining enantiomers of chiral pesticide quizalofop and pirfenoxaline by ultra-high performance convergent chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables separating and determining enantiomers with high sensitivity and better separation degree between chiral isomer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A enantiomers of chiral pesticide quizalofop and pirfenoxaline separating and determining method involves preparing sample, subjecting sample to convergent chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry, separating the elution peaks, according to the elution peak retention time, the target compound quantitative ion pair and the qualitative ion pair to obtain the chiral pesticide enantiomers and supercritical carbon dioxide/ethanol, separating the elution peaks according to the retention time of the elution peak and the mass-to-charge ratio of the parent ion/child ion and peak is R-piroxifene, preparing matrix mixed standard working solution of R-quineline and R pirfenoxacil, separate according to the chromatographic and mass spectrometric condition and record the peak corresponding to each enantiomer, determining the area by the concentration value of each enantiomer as the independent variable and the corresponding peak area is used as the dependent variable to obtain the linear regression equation of R-quinfloxacin and R-piroxifene, sample is separated and the peak area corresponding to each enantiomer is recorded, the peak area corresponding to each enantiomer is substituted into the linear regression equation to obtain the concentration of each enantiomer in the sample.

PN CN107988282-A
TI Extracting glutamic acid from corn gluten meal, involves pretreating corn gluten meal, fermenting corn gluten meal, and extracting and purifying glutamic acid.
AU ZHAO S
AE ANHUI AVISION ENTERPRISE INVESTMENT GROUP CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201838659Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting glutamic acid from corn gluten meal, involves pretreating corn gluten meal, fermenting corn gluten meal, and extracting and purifying glutamic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting glutamic acid from corn gluten meal (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective and simple to perform, and provides high product purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves removing impurities and screening corn gluten meal, crushing the corn protein powder into ultra-fine corn protein powder by impact of supersonic airflow colliding, mixing crushed ultrafine corn gluten powder, diluted sulfuric acid and diluted hydrochloric acid solution, incubating the solution at 60-70 degrees C under conditions of 5-8 minutes, placing the solution in oven, drying the solution until the moisture content reaches 13%, subjecting superfine corn protein powder after drying to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and impurity removal, where the extraction conditions are pressure of 10 MPa, extraction temperature of 37 degrees C, carbon dioxide flow rate of 30 l/hour, and dynamic extraction for 30 minutes, and drying the mixture at 2-8 degrees C under vacuum until the moisture content reaches 8% or less. The number of viable cells is 1x 105-1x 106 colony forming unit (CFU). The fermented under anaerobic conditions are temperature of 37 degrees C for 36-72 hours. The fermentation broth comprises strains of Brevibacterium ternata, Corynebacterium crenatum and Corynebacterium pekinensis. The method involves subjecting fermentation glutamic acid to the first filtration of the nano membrane and the second filtration of the super membrane, separating solid-liquid, evaporating separated liquid, concentrating the liquid at 150-170 degrees C to obtain glutamic acid particles, adding hydrochloric acid for acid hydrolysis at 60-70 degrees C under the conditions of acid hydrolysis for 5-7 hours, neutralizing the mixture with sodium hydroxide to pH to 7, adding 1% sodium chloride and 150% isopropanol to the separated solids, and reacting the mixture at 500 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 15-30 minutes, and maintaining the precipitates at -8 degrees C. The temperature of the low temperature condition is 2-8 degrees C.

PN CN107934959-A
TI Preparing lignin-based hierarchical porous carbon            material comprises e.g. carrying out crude purification            of papermaking black liquor alkali lignin and preparing            magnesium acetate tetrahydrate aqueous solution.
AU LIU J
   SONG Y
   SUN K
   XU W
   HOU X
AE INST CHEM IND FOREST PROD CHINESE ACAD (CLYK-C)
GA 201832790Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lignin-based hierarchical porous                carbon material comprises e.g. carrying out crude                purification of papermaking black liquor alkali                lignin, then dissolving triblock polymer Pluronic                F127 (RTM:                Poly(oxyethylene/oxypropylene/oxyethylene) in                tetrahydrofuran, preparing magnesium acetate                tetrahydrate aqueous solution, adding magnesium                acetate tetrahydrate, then mixing two solutions,                adding 6 mol/l hydrochloric acid solution, stirring                and drying to obtain dark brown solid; placing dark                brown solid in a tube furnace for carbonization                reaction and carrying out carbonization reaction                under the protection of nitrogen or an inert gas,                cooling under nitrogen gas or inert gas, then                pickling, washing and drying to obtain black                powdered alkali lignin-based mesoporous carbon                material, then protecting by nitrogen or inert gas                through carbon dioxide activation or potassium                hydroxide activation, heating, pickling, washing                and drying.
   USE - The lignin-based hierarchical porous carbon                material is useful for preparing electric double                layer supercapacitor electrode (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing lignin-based hierarchical porous                carbon material comprises carrying out crude                purification of papermaking black liquor alkali                lignin, then dissolving triblock polymer Pluronic                F127 (RTM:                Poly(oxyethylene/oxypropylene/oxyethylene) in                tetrahydrofuran at a mass ratio of 1:1-3, preparing                magnesium acetate tetrahydrate aqueous solution,                adding magnesium acetate tetrahydrate is 3-11 times                mass of alkali lignin, then mixing the two                solutions, adding 6 mol/l hydrochloric acid                solution and stirring 4-12 hours, placing in an                oven at 35-80 degrees C for drying to obtain dark                brown solid; placing dark brown solid in a tube                furnace and carrying out carbonization at 850-1000                degrees C, carrying out carbonization reaction                under the protection of nitrogen or an inert gas,                continuously cooling to room temperature under                nitrogen gas or inert gas, then pickling, washing,                and drying to obtain black powdered alkali                lignin-based mesoporous carbon material, then                protecting by nitrogen or inert gas through carbon                dioxide activation or potassium hydroxide                activation, heating to 750-900 degrees C at 10                degrees C/minute for 0.5-4 hours, pickling,                washing, and drying to obtain black powdered                lignin-based fractional pore carbon material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The crude purification                  method of alkali lignin comprises alkali lignin                  obtained by spray drying directly from                  papermaking black liquor is dissolved in water,                  filtering and taking the filtrate, adjusting the                  pH to 6-9 with inorganic acid, filtering and                  taking the filtrate, then continuing using the                  inorganic acid to adjusting the pH value to 2-4,                  filtering, washing with water for 2-3 times and                  drying to obtain crude purified alkali lignin.                  The heating process in the carbonization process:                  heating from room temperature to 300-450 degrees                  C at a rate of 1 degrees C/minute, and then                  continuously heating to 850-1000 degrees C at a                  rate of 2-5 degrees C/minute for carbonization                  and insulating for 15-60 minutes. The two                  pickling is inorganic acid washing to neutrality                  with hydrochloric acid and sulfuric acid. The                  electrode material is prepared from a graded pore                  carbon material, acetylene black and                  polytetrafluoroethylene in a ratio of 80:10:10                  wt.%, and the electrolyte is a 1 mol/l sulfuric                  acid solution. The carbon dioxide activation                  method comprises directly heating lignin-based                  mesoporous carbon material under protection of                  nitrogen or other inert gas to 750-900 degrees C                  at a temperature of 10 degrees C/minute, and fast                  switching to a high-temperature activation of                  carbon dioxide gas for 0.5-4 hours, cooling to                  room temperature under the protection of nitrogen                  or other inert gas, where the carbon dioxide gas                  flow rate of 0.5-3 l/minute; the potassium                  hydroxide activation method comprises adding                  lignin-based mesoporous carbon material to                  potassium hydroxide solution having a mass                  fraction of 50 wt.%, then placing in an oven at                  150 degrees C to fully drying, and finally                  heating at to 750-900 degrees C at a rate of 10                  degrees C/minute for 0.5-4 hours under protection                  of nitrogen or other inert gas, where the mass                  ratio between potassium hydroxide solid and                  mesoporous carbon is 1:1, 1:2, 1:3, 1:4, or 1:5.                  Preferred Components: The inert gas is argon or                  nitrogen-argon mixed gas. The graded pore carbon                  material is a black powdery solid particle with a                  specific surface area of 1000-1800 m2/g, total                  pore volume of 1-2.5 cm3g with clearly graded                  tunnel structure and the pore sizes of 0.5-1 nm,                  1-1.5 nm and 9-11 nm.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. TI Welding comprises grooving first pipe end of first pipeline and second pipe end of second pipeline, cold metal transfer extra-radial welding on groove, filler welding pair of grooves and cap welding based on preset welding parameters.
AU LUO G
   LIU H
AE HUBEI SANJIANG HONGYANG MECHANICAL & ELE (CAES-C)
   HUBEI SANJIANG HONGYANG MECHANICAL & ELE (CAES-C)
GA 2018301485
AB    NOVELTY - Welding comprises grooving a first pipe end of a first pipeline and a second pipe end of a second pipeline, where the groove angle between the first pipe end and second pipe end is 5-7 degrees , matching first pipe end and the second pipe end, forming a set on the groove, using an external root-welding machine to perform cold metal transfer (CMT) extra-radial welding on the groove of the set based on a preset root-welding parameter, filler welding the pair of grooves using based on a preset filler welding parameter, cap welding the pair of grooves based on preset cap welding parameters.
   USE - The method is useful for welding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Welding comprises grooving a first pipe end of a first pipeline and a second pipe end of a second pipeline, where the groove angle between the first pipe end and second pipe end is 5-7 degrees , matching first pipe end and the second pipe end, forming a set on the groove, using an external root-welding machine to perform cold metal transfer (CMT) extra-radial welding on the groove of the set based on a preset root-welding parameter, filler welding the pair of grooves using all-position automatic welding machine based on a preset filler welding parameter, and cap welding the pair of grooves based on the preset cap welding parameters using all-position automatic welding machine, where the root welding parameters include: a welding voltage of 12.5-13.5 V, a welding current of 165-175 A, a wire diameter of 1 mm and a welding speed of 50 plus minus cm/minute, where the mixed gas comprises argon/carbon dioxide in a ratio of (75/25)-(85/15)%, the filler welding parameters is same as the cap welding parameters including a welding voltage of 22.5-26 V, a welding current of 155-185 A, a wire diameter of 1 mm, a welding rate of 45-55 cm/minute and a mixed gas percentage including argon/carbon dioxide in a ratio of (75/25)-(85/15)%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The arc radius of the first pipe end and second pipe end opening is 2.4 mm. A first horizontal transition section having a length of 0.5-0.6 mm is set between the first pipe end of the groove and the first blunt edge. A second horizontal transition section having a length of 0.5-0.6 mm is set between the second pipe end of the groove and the second blunt edge. The thickness of the first blunt end and second blunt end are 1.4-1.8 mm. The error variable between the first pipe end and the second pipe end is less than or equal to 1 mm. The welding gap of the first pipe end and the second pipe end is less than 1 mm. The shape of the groove opening is U-shaped groove. Preferred Method: The processing of setting a preset root-welding parameter prior to CMT external root welding also includes: preheating the first pipe end and the second pipe end to 50-75 degrees C.The filler welding process comprises welding in a layer by layer manner on the groove, where the change amount of the welding height of each layer is 3.7-4.3 mm. The method further comprises setting the included angle between the welding gun and the pipe shaft while performing filler welding and cap welding of the pair of grooves is 90 degrees C.

TI Biological agent useful for e.g. treating drug-resistant tuberculosis, comprises tuberculosis-specific lymphocyte prepared using e.g. interleukin (IL)-1alpha, IL-2 and cluster of differentiation 3 (CD3) monoclonal antibody.
AU XU P
   XU Z
   WU M
   XU J
   SONG H
   ZHANG J
   YE Z
   CHEN H
   TANG P
   ZHAO J
   CHEN X
   SHI C
   ZHU X
   WANG F
AE XU P (XUPP-Individual)
   XU P (XUPP-Individual)
GA 201823955P
AB    NOVELTY - Biological agent comprises a tuberculosis-specific lymphocyte, which is prepared using interleukin (IL)-1 alpha , IL-2, cluster of differentiation 3 (CD3) monoclonal antibody and Mycobacterium tuberculosis-specific antigen CFP10 and early secreted antigenic target of 6 kDa (ESAT-6).
   USE - The biological agent is useful for treating drug-resistant tuberculosis, preferably pulmonary tuberculosis, improving the peripheral blood, mental state and appetite and reducing weight.
   ADVANTAGE - The biological agent is rapidly absorbed in the lung, treats the disease in short period and improves the quality of life of patient compared with the existing treatment method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of preparing the biological agent, which involves (a) collecting normal human peripheral blood, diluting with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) in a ratio of 1:2 and performing ficoll density gradient centrifugation by placing ficoll at room temperature, adding into a tube, where the ratio of the ficoll and diluted blood is 1:2, then slowly adding the diluted blood along the wall of the tube and centrifuging at 20-24 degrees C and 1800 revolutions/minute for 30 minutes in a temperature-controlled centrifuge, carefully pipetting the mist layer cells and washing with 10 volumes of PBS to obtain a peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC), (b) formulating PBMC cells into cell suspension with RMPI1640 complete culture medium and then adding the corresponding cytokines to each group of cell culture solution, adding 100 ng/ml anti-CD3 monoclonal antibody (mAb), 1000 U/ml interferon (IFN)- gamma , 10 ng/ml IL-1 alpha , 10 ng/ml CFP10 and 10 ng/ml ESAT6 on the 0 day and culturing the cells at 37 degrees C with 5%carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide incubator saturated with 95% humidity, (c) adding 1000 U/ml IL-2 on the first day and then adding the cytokine solution for every 3 days depending on the state of the cells, where the rehydration cytokine is 500 U/ml IL-2, and (d) detecting endotoxin on the tenth day of culture, detecting the changes in subpopulation using flow cytometry and preparing tuberculosis-specific T cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The tuberculosis-specific lymphocyte is a group of specific lymphocyte obtained by co-culturing human peripheral blood mononuclear cell with several cytokine, CD3 monoclonal antibody, IL-2, IFN- gamma and M.tuberculosis-specific antigen CFP10 and ESAT6 in vitro. Preferred Medium: The culture medium is lymphocyte culture medium, which comprises medicinal grade human albumin, CD3 monoclonal antibody, IL-2, IFN- gamma and M.tuberculosis-specific antigen CFP10 and ESAT6. Preferred Product: The pH value of the biological agent is 8.
   ACTIVITY - Antitubercular; Tuberculostatic; Nootropic; Anabolic; Anorectic.
