PN CN107777707-A; CN107777707-B
TI Production of synthetic ammonia by recycling silicon carbide exhaust gas, involves controlling temperature of silicon carbide exhaust gas, pressurizing, removing large particles of dust and circulating water.
AU ZHUO Y
AE ZHUO Y (ZHUO-Individual)
GA 201823433X
AB    NOVELTY - The production method of synthetic ammonia, involves controlling temperature of silicon carbide exhaust gas at 80-120 degrees C and pressurizing, passing silicon carbide exhaust gas to cyclone dust collector and removing large particles of dust, passing silicon carbide exhaust gas to scrubber tower, removing dust in silicon carbide exhaust gas by circulating water and reducing temperature of silicon carbide exhaust, cleaning gas cabinet, drying, finishing and storing processed silicon carbide exhaust gas.
   USE - Production of synthetic ammonia by recycling silicon carbide exhaust gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of synthetic ammonia by completely recycling silicon carbide exhaust gas, by simple and environmentally-friendly process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The production method of synthetic ammonia, involves (1) controlling temperature of silicon carbide exhaust gas at 80-120 degrees C and pressurizing, (2) passing silicon carbide exhaust gas to cyclone dust collector for dust removal, removing large particles of dust from silicon carbide exhaust gas, (3) passing silicon carbide exhaust gas to scrubber tower, removing the dust in silicon carbide exhaust gas by circulating water in scrubber tower and reducing the temperature of silicon carbide exhaust at 30-70 degrees C, cleaning the gas cabinet 3-5 times, drying and finishing, (4) storing the processed silicon carbide exhaust gas at 30-40 degrees C, passing pure compressed air directly passed into nitrogen oxide separation device for collecting nitrogen, (5) filtering the nitrogen, heating nitrogen at 30-50 degrees C, separating oxygen and harmful substances in nitrogen, mixing nitrogen with silicon carbide exhaust to form mixed gas, passing mixed silicon carbide exhaust gas to adsorption plate, removing tar and impurities from silicon carbide exhaust gas, treating silicon carbide exhaust gas into pressurizing device, pressurizing at 0.8 MPa, carrying out low-to-medium temperature conversion process to convert carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide and hydrogen, removing carbon dioxide, transferring silicon carbide exhaust gas to ammonia synthesis tower, passing silicon carbide exhaust gas to ammonia synthesis tower at high speed, heating, carrying out catalyst reaction, pressurizing at 16-22 MPa and heating at 400-500 degrees C and cooling.

PN CN107712684-A
TI Method for preserving sea cucumbers, involves heating ginger, scallion section, small scallion, onion, pepper, and fennel into water, adding sea cucumber, boiling, cooling and drying, followed by placing sea cucumber on crushed ice.
AU FEI Q
AE JURONG KONGFENGLAIYI COMMERCE & TRADE CO (JURO-Non-standard)
GA 201816842H
AB    NOVELTY - A sea cucumbers preserving method involves heating ginger, scallion section, small scallion, onion, pepper, and fennel into water, adding sea cucumber, boiling, cooling and drying. The dried sea cucumbers are subjected to sterilization, sprayed with disinfectant provided with composite packaging film and dried, followed by filling nitrogen discharging carbon dioxide into the film, placing into the nitrogen-filled compound packaging film, filling nitrogen gas, placing the sealed and packed sea cucumber on the crushed ice, and laying a layer of crushed ice on the sea cucumber.
   USE - Method for preserving sea cucumbers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preserving sea cucumbers with better food quality, rich nutrition, various taste and better market prospect in convenient and fast manner, and the sea cucumber is convenient to store and carry, and better for the health of human body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A sea cucumbers preserving method involves heating ginger, scallion section, small scallion, onion, pepper, and fennel into water for half an hour, adding sea cucumber, cooking wine, aromatic vinegar, boiling for 10 minutes, removing the sea cucumber, cooling to room temperature and drying to obtain dried sea cucumbers. The dried sea cucumbers are subjected to high-temperature instantaneous sterilization to kill microorganisms at 110-140 degrees C for 5-10 seconds, sprayed with disinfectant provided with composite packaging film and dried, followed by filling nitrogen discharging carbon dioxide into the composite packaging film and stopping filling, when it is detected that the concentration of nitrogen is more than 80% to obtain sterilized sea cucumbers, placing the sterilized sea cucumbers into the nitrogen-filled compound packaging film, filling nitrogen gas, stopping filling, when it is detected that the concentration of nitrogen is more than 98, sealing, laying a layer of crushed ice on the bottom of the incubator, placing the sealed and packed sea cucumber on the crushed ice, laying a layer of crushed ice on the sea cucumber, and lay the crushed ice on the staggered interval with the sea cucumber layer.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); Q31 (Packaging processes and equipment (B65B, C)); Q34 (Types of goods packaged/bottled/bound/labelled/unpacked (B65B, C, D))

A transfer conveying belt                is arranged on the rotating disk for connecting                with the first main conveying belt or the                sub-conveying belt. An end of the converging                conveying belt is connected with the sub-conveying                belt. A coma                detecting chamber and a hemp compensating chamber                are sequentially arranged on the second main                conveying belt. The system conveys                corona livestock to the second main conveying belt                through the converging conveying belt and detects                whether the livestock completely enters into coma                by the coma detecting chamber so as to perform                secondary hemp electric shock operation by the hemp                compensating chamber if the livestock is not                completely unconscious, thus ensuring that the                livestock is completely unconscious.

PN CN114914570-A
TI Method for recycling waste lithium ion battery            electrolyte in electronic device, involves adding            soluble carbonate and/or carbon dioxide to react in            second solution to obtain second mixture, and filtering            second mixture to obtain fourth solid and third            solution.
AU HUANG B
   SU J
   HAN H
   LIN H
   CHEN C
   LIU Y
   WEI W
   WU Y
AE GUANGZHOU TINCI MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022A82112
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling method of waste lithium ion battery                electrolyte comprises (i) crushing the waste                lithium battery to obtain the battery crushed                material, where the battery breaker includes                electrolyte of the waste battery, (ii) cleaning the                battery with an organic solvent to obtain a first                mixture, (iii) filtering the first mixture with a                first filter to obtain first solid matter and a                first solution, (iv) heating and decomposing the                second solid matter to obtain second tail gas and                third solid matter, (v) dissolving the third solid                substance and acid solution to get a second                solution (vi), where the second solution includes                lithium ion, (vi) adding soluble carbonate and/or                carbon dioxide to react in the second solution to                obtain a second mixture, and (vii) filtrating the                second mixture to obtain fourth solid and a third                solution (viii). The fourth solid comprises lithium                carbonate.
   USE - The method and device are useful for recycling                waste lithium ion battery electrolyte in electronic                device (claimed). Uses include but are not limited                to mobile phone, personal digital assistant (PDA),                portable multimedia player (PMP), digital camera,                digital video camera (DVC), digital photo frame,                portable game machine, portable music player,                portable video camera, portable telephone, portable                computer, portable electronic book (e-book) reader,                portable DVD player, MPEG audio layer-3 (MP3)                player, global positioning system (GPS) navigation                device, portable medical device, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem that the waste                lithium ion battery electrolyte cannot be                completely recycled, improves the recycling                benefit, avoids the tail gas processing cost is too                high and pollus the environment. The electrolyte in                the battery crushing substance is dissolved in the                organic solvent filtering the mixture after                dissolving, and the electrolyte is recycled in the                form of phosphorus pentafluoride and lithium                carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   electrolyte recycling device of waste lithium                ion battery; and
   computer-readable storage medium comprising a                set of instructions for recycling waste lithium ion                battery electrolyte.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the method for recycling waste lithium ion battery                electrolyte in electronic device (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

PN CN114452935-A
TI Preparing carbon-based denitration efficient            adsorbent with short chain, comprises carrying out            pretreatment on Dongyuan coal, performing acidification            and demineralization, calcining treated raw coal            powder, performing calcination and activating.
AU ZHAO G
   YUN D
   GENG W
   SUN R
   XUAN C
   HAN S
   WANG L
AE ENERGY RES INST SHANDONG ACAD SCI (UYQQ-C)
GA 202267819G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon-based denitration efficient                adsorbent with short chain, comprises (i) carrying                out pretreatment on the Dongyuan coal, which is                cleaned and dried, and crushing and screening to                obtain the target powder; (ii) performing                acidification and demineralization of pretreated                powder into a mixed solution of nitric acid and                hydrochloric acid for heating in a water bath,                cooling, filtering and drying to obtain the                processed raw coal powder; (iii) calcining the                treated raw coal powder in a tube furnace under a                nitrogen atmosphere of preliminary carbonization to                obtain a preliminary carbonized carbon material;                (iv) mixing hydrothermal reaction pottassium                carbonate solution with the carbon material of                preliminary carbonization; (v) performing                calcination and activating using a tube furnace,                calcining carbon material under nitrogen and carbon                dioxide atmosphere, and its further                activation.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                carbon-based denitration efficient adsorbent with                short chain.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves good adsorption and                denitrification effect; has the characteristics of                simple preparation method, short cycle, economical                and environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon-based denitration efficient                adsorbent with short chain, comprises (i) carrying                out pretreatment on the Dongyuan coal, which is                cleaned and dried, and crushing and screening to                obtain the target powder; (ii) performing                acidification and demineralization of pretreated                powder into a mixed solution of nitric acid and                hydrochloric acid for heating in a water bath,                cooling, filtering and drying to obtain the                processed raw coal powder; (iii) calcining the                treated raw coal powder in a tube furnace under a                nitrogen atmosphere of preliminary carbonization to                obtain a preliminary carbonized carbon material;                (iv) mixing hydrothermal reaction pottassium                carbonate solution with the carbon material of                preliminary carbonization, adding into the                hydrothermal reaction kettle, adding reaction                kettle into the drying oven to carry out the                hydrothermal reaction, and drying carbon material                after cooling; (v) performing calcination and                activating using a tube furnace, calcining carbon                material under nitrogen and carbon dioxide                atmosphere, and its further activation, where                carbon-based adsorbent was subsequently obtained                after cooling to room temperature in a protective                nitrogen atmosphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  cleaning is performed to remove miscellaneous                  ores and humus contained in the coal itself,                  where the crushed particle size of Zhundong raw                  coal is 30-50 mesh. In the step (ii), the ratio                  of nitric acid and hydrochloric acid in the acid                  solution configured for pickling is 2:1. In the                  step (ii), in the pickling process, the beaker                  needs to be sealed, the heating temperature is                  60-90degrees C, and the temperature is kept for                  2-12 hours, cooling, deionized water is used for                  suction filtration to ensure that the residual                  acidic substances on the surface of the carbon                  material are washed away. In the step (ii), the                  carbon material after suction filtration is dried                  in a blast drying oven at 100-120degrees Celsius                  for 2-24 hours, preferably at 105degrees                  Celsiusfor 4 hours. In the step (iii), the                  heating temperature of the tube furnace is                  400-600degrees Celsius, calcination is performed                  for 2-10 hours, preferably 2-5 hours, and the                  heating rate is 10degrees Celsius/minutes, and                  preliminary carbonization is performed to remove                  nitrate ions. In the step (iv), the concentration                  of potassium carbonate solution is 0.1 mol/l, the                  temperature of the reaction kettle is                  120-200degrees Celsius, and the holding time is                  12-48 hours. In the step (iv), the hydrothermal                  reaction is completed, and the method of rapid                  cooling is adopted to cool it. In the step (v),                  the temperature during calcination is                  700-1000degrees C, and the temperature is kept                  for 1-5 hours. In the step (v), when calcining,                  when the temperature rises to the designated                  calcining, feeding carbon monoxide, stop feeding                  carbon monoxide after the insulation finishes and                  feeding gas flow rate is 1000 ml/minutes, and                  where carbon monoxide and nitrogen ratio is 1:                  4.

PN CN114438272-A
TI Oxygen isolation method for cooling high temperature direct reducing material of iron ore rotary kiln, involves blowing pressure steam into high temperature reducing material discharging pipe by steam inlet device.
AU WANG G
   ZHENG X
   HU J
   YU H
   ZHANG X
   KOU M
   WANG J
   ZHANG H
   LEI P
   WANG M
   QUAN F
AE JIUQUAN IRON & STEEL GROUP CO LTD (JGHX-C)
GA 202266409U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves discharging high temperature reducing material (13) from a high temperature roasting material outlet and then entering into a high-temperature material discharging pipe (3). Pressure steam is blown into the high temperature removing material discharge pipe through a steam inlet device (5). The high temperature removal material in the high heat removal material discharge pipeline is sprayed with pressure steam in the flowing process of the high-heat removing material in contact with the pressure steam to make the temperature of high heat removing material reach 950-1150?oC. The water vapor is reacted with carbon in the residual carbon of the heat-reducing material to generate hydrogen and carbon dioxide (CO) used as a reducing atmosphere to isolate the heat dissipation material from the air to prevent secondary oxidation during the conveying and cooling process, so as to prevent the secondary oxidation of high temperature reduction material.
   USE - Oxygen isolating method for cooling high temperature direct reducing material of iron ore rotary kiln used in non-blast furnace iron making of iron.
   ADVANTAGE - By spraying the pressurized steam in the high temperature material discharging pipe, the steam is sprayed out from the water steam outlet and reacting with the carbon in the material residual carbon to generate hydrogen and carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide as reducing atmosphere. It can prevent the secondary oxidation problem of the high-temperature reducing material in the process of conveying and cooling. The water vapor reacts with the water vapor in the residual carbon and generates water gas, and the water gas enters into the rotary kiln. The method makes the waste heat temperature reducing material to be recovered and recycled. At the same time, the water reducing material after water gas reaction-primary cooling, reduces the cooling load of the roller type cooler and improves the cooling efficiency of the material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a partial enlarged view of the steam inlet device.
   High temperature material discharging pipe (3)
   Steam inlet device (5)
   Steam pipeline (6)
   Steam ring pipe (7)
   Steam outlet (8)
   Spiral feeder (9)
   High temperature reducing material (13)

TI Production of nitrogen-based fertilizer used for            growing agricultural crops, involves acidifying rock            phosphate or fluoride-containing rock phosphate with            nitric acid to obtain acidified solution containing            phosphoric acid, calcium nitrate and water, cooling            acidifying solution, and neutralizing.
AU VERHELST P
   VANCAUWENBERGHE A
AE EUROCHEM ANTWERPEN NV (EURO-Non-standard)
   EUROCHEM ANTWERPEN (EURO-Non-standard)
GA 2022793600
AB    NOVELTY - Production of nitrogen-based fertilizer                involves acidifying a rock phosphate or                fluoride-containing rock phosphate with a nitric                acid solution to obtain an acidified solution                containing phosphoric acid, calcium nitrate, and                water or hydrofluoric acid, cooling the acidifying                solution to a temperature at which the calcium                nitrate can crystallize, optionally neutralizing                the reaction mixture of with ammonia to obtain a                mixture of ammonium phosphate, calcium phosphate                and ammonium nitrate, converting the calcium                nitrate into ammonium nitrate and calcium carbonate                using carbon dioxide and ammonia, separating the                calcium carbonate and ammonium nitrate in an                acidification tank, in which water is added to the                exhaust gas section of the acidification tank to                prevent the formation of silica in the exhaust gas                line.
   USE - Production of nitrogen-based fertilizer used                for growing agricultural crops.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of                nitrogen-based fertilizer with reduced sanding in                off-gas lines of acidification tank.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of nitrogen-based fertilizer                involves acidifying a rock phosphate (Ca5(PO)3OH)                or fluoride-containing rock phosphate (Ca5(PO)3F)                with a nitric acid solution to obtain an acidified                solution containing phosphoric acid, calcium                nitrate, and water or hydrofluoric acid, reacting                silica in the rock phosphate with the resulting                hydrofluoric acid to obtain silicon tetrafluoride                and water, again reacting the silicon tetrafluoride                with the hydrofluoric acid to obtain                hexafluorosilicic acid, cooling the acidifying                solution to a temperature at which the calcium                nitrate can crystallize, optionally neutralizing                the reaction mixture of with ammonia to obtain a                mixture of ammonium phosphate, calcium phosphate                and ammonium nitrate, converting the calcium                nitrate into ammonium nitrate and calcium carbonate                using carbon dioxide and ammonia, separating the                calcium carbonate and ammonium nitrate in an                acidification tank, in which water is added to the                exhaust gas section of the acidification tank to                prevent the formation of silica in the exhaust gas                line. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                water to limit, control or eliminate sand build-up                in the off-gas lines of the acidification                tank.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves adding water to reduce or eliminate the                  formation of sand in the exhaust gas line. Water                  is added in the form of a mist or an aerosol                  generated by one or more nozzles. The obtained                  ammonium nitrate is further processed into simple                  nitrogen-based fertilizer,                  nitrogen-phosphorus-based fertilizer or                  nitrogen-phosphorus-potassium-based                  fertilizer.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PN CN113980928-A
TI Producing trehalose by inoculating bacterial strain in fermentation substrate for fermentative, controlling flow acceleration rate of sugar supplementation, performing cell wall breaking treatment on fermented thalli to obtain trehalose synthase and reacting trehalose synthase with maltodextrin.
AU SHANG C
   REN F
   ZHANG L
AE DEZHOU HUIYANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DEZH-Non-standard)
GA 2022246990
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing trehalose involves (a) inoculating bacterial strain in fermentation substrate to perform fermentative propagation, and controlling flow acceleration rate of sugar supplementation by feedback of carbon dioxide evolution rate (CER) data obtained by fermentor tail gas analysis, (b) separating the fermentation liquid at the end of fermentation to obtain fermentation thalli and fermentation waste liquid, (c) subjecting the fermented thalli to cell wall breaking treatment to obtain trehalose synthase, (d) reacting the trehalose synthase with maltodextrin to obtain trehalose A liquid and (e) combining the fermentation waste liquid with the trehalose A liquid, purifying and separating to obtain the trehalose product and crystallization mother liquor.
   USE - The method is useful for producing trehalose.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves yield of trehalose, since it regulates fermentation stage in real time and prolongs logarithmic growth phase, hence expresses more protein products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing trehalose involves (a) inoculating bacterial strain in fermentation substrate to perform fermentative propagation, and controlling flow acceleration rate of sugar supplementation by feedback of carbon dioxide evolution rate (CER) data obtained by fermenter tail gas analysis, (b) separating the fermentation liquid at the end of fermentation to obtain fermentation thalli and fermentation waste liquid, (c) subjecting the fermented thalli to cell wall breaking treatment to obtain trehalose synthase, (d) reacting the trehalose synthase with maltodextrin to obtain trehalose A liquid and (e) combining the fermentation waste liquid with the trehalose A liquid, purifying and separating to obtain the trehalose product and crystallization mother liquor and using the crystallization mother liquor, as source of sugar supplementation in step (a), where the flow acceleration rate of CER data feedback control of sugar supplementation is adjusted by controlling the slope of the total CER value and fermentation time, when the total CER value is less than 40, the control slope is 3-4 and when the total CER value is greater than or equal to 40, the control slope is 8-9.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the bacterial strain is Escherichia coli BW25113-TRS, the fermentation substrate comprises 28 g/l monopotassium phosphate, 12 g/l diammonium hydrogenphosphate, 8 g/l citric acid, 7.6 g/l magnesium sulfate heptahydrate and 10 g/l trace element and the fermentation substrate further comprises 1 g/l inducer L-arabinose, where the L-arabinose is added when the optical denity value at 600 nm (OD600 value) of the fermentation liquid is more than 30, and the fermentation temperature is lowered to 28-30degrees Celsius after the addition. The step (a) involves performing fermentation at 37-37.5degrees Celsius under dissolved oxygen content of 20-30%. The step (b) involves terminating fermentation when the OD600 value of the fermentation broth is more than 120 and separating the fermentation liquid using membrane having pore size of 30-50 nm. The step (c) involves homogeneously subjecting the fermented thalli to cell wall breaking treatment at 80-100 MPa, where the flow rate is 1 m3/hour. The step (d) involves reacting the trehalose synthase with maltodextrin in a weight-volume ratio of 0.3% to obtain trehalose liquid at 37-40degrees Celsius for 12-14 hours. The step (e) involves combining the fermentation waste liquid with the trehalose A liquid in a volume ratio of less than 10%. The step (e) involves successively purifying and decolorizing, ionizing and desalting, evaporating and concentrating, performing chromatographic separation, performing cooling crystallization, and performing centrifugal separation to obtain primary trehalose product and primary crystallization mother liquor, purifying and decolorizing, ionizing and desalting, evaporating and concentrating, performing chromatographic separation, performing cooling crystallization, and performing centrifugal separation on the primary crystallization mother liquor to obtain secondary trehalose product and secondary crystallization mother liquor. In step (e), the soluble sugar content at the end of the evaporation concentration is 65-68% and the effect of the chromatographic separation is that the trehalose purity is more than 95%. In step (e), the secondary crystallization mother liquor is used in step (a) as sugar supplement.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E13 (Heterocyclics.)

Preferred Method: In step (3), the adherent cells are washed 1-3 times with normal saline before adding the subsequent first supplemental DC cell culture solution to the culture flask with adherent cells. PN CN112741111-A
TI Molecular sieve used for providing antibacterial effect, comprises molecular sieve carrier containing powder antibacterial ceramics, mica powder and water, and antibacterial additives containing sodium chloride and zinc ion solutions.
AU ZHANG J
   QIAN B
AE JIANGSU WANYUAN NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202152834K
AB    NOVELTY - Antibacterial molecular sieve comprises molecular sieve carrier and antibacterial additives. The antibacterial molecular sieve carrier comprises 40-60 pts. wt. powder antibacterial ceramics, 5-8 pts. wt. inorganic silicon powder, 5-8 pts. wt. mica powder, 1-1.5 pts. wt. organic silicon quaternary ammonium salt antibacterial agent, and 50-65 pts. wt. deionized water. The antibacterial additives comprise 20-35 pts. wt. sodium chloride solution, 9-15 pts. wt. zinc ion salt solution, 1-2.5 pts. wt. nano silver powder, 5-12 pts. wt. silver ion salt solution, and 12-16 pts. wt. basic aluminum salt solution.
   USE - The molecular sieve is useful for providing antibacterial effect.
   ADVANTAGE - The molecular sieve has improved quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the antibacterial molecular sieve, which involves (a) adding the antibacterial molecular sieve carrier raw material such as powder antibacterial ceramics, inorganic silicon powder, mica powder, organosilicon quaternary ammonium salt antibacterial agent, and deionized water into a closed reactor, and mixing under stirring condition to form a viscous shape, (b) filling the anti-closed reactor with high-pressure carbon dioxide gas, and stirring again in a low-temperature environment, (c) opening the valve of the closed reactor and vent slowly, and when the air pressure in the closed reactor is equal to the outside air pressure, filling the closed reactor with hot air to make the viscous raw material form a solid, (d) taking out the solid mixed raw materials, adding into the vibrating screen, screening out qualified molecular sieves, baking at high temperature, and hardening the structure of the molecular sieve while the residual carbon dioxide gas overflows to form molecular sieve holes, (e) adding the sodium chloride solution, zinc ion salt solution, nano silver powder, silver ion salt solution, and basic aluminum salt solution into the open reactor and stirring thoroughly, and (f) adding the molecular sieve into an open reactor to soak for 1-1.5 hours, removing and drying to obtain antibacterial molecular sieve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The antibacterial molecular sieve carrier comprises 50 pts. wt. powder antibacterial ceramics, 6.5 pts. wt. inorganic silicon powder, 6.5 pts. wt. mica powder, 1.25 pts. wt. organic silicon quaternary ammonium salt antibacterial agent, and 52.5 pts. wt. deionized water. The antibacterial additives comprise 27.5 pts. wt. sodium chloride solution, 12 pts. wt. zinc ion salt solution, 1.75 pts. wt. nano-silver powder, 8.5 pts. wt. silver ion salt solution, and 14 pts. wt. basic aluminum salt solution. The weight ratio of the molecular sieve carrier to the antibacterial additive is 1:1.1-1.3, and the mass of the inorganic silicon powder and mica powder nano-silver powder is not less than 38% of total weight. Preferred Method: The pressure of the high-pressure carbon dioxide gas is 2-3 atm, and the low-temperature environment is an environment with a temperature of 12-16 degrees C. In step (c), the hot air temperature is 35-44 degrees C, and in step (d), the high-temperature baking temperature is 800-1200 degrees C. The vibrating sieve is a three-layer vibrating sieve, and the product of the middle layer is a qualified molecular sieve.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The tablet further comprises 2 wt.% copovidone, 2 wt.% magnesium stearate, 2 wt.% polyethylene glycol and 2 wt.% talc.
   ACTIVITY - Hypertensive; Osteopathic.

TI Egg yolk flavoring element used in snack foods and baked products, comprises salted egg yolk, oxidized egg butter, polydextrose, propylene glycol fatty acid ester, tea polyphenol palmitate, salt, monosodium glutamate and sugar.
AU CHEN L
   CHEN Y
   TANG H
   LI H
   ZHANG J
AE ZHEJIANG AGRIC SCI ACAD (ZJAG-C)
GA 2021124229
AB    NOVELTY - An egg yolk flavoring element comprises 80-92 wt.% salted egg yolk, 2-4 wt.% oxidized egg butter, 1-2 wt.% polydextrose, 1-2 wt.% propylene glycol fatty acid ester, 0.01-0.03 wt.% tea polyphenol palmitate, 2-5 wt.% salt, 1-4 wt.% monosodium glutamate and 1-3 wt.% sugar.
   USE - Egg yolk flavoring element used in snack foods and baked products.
   ADVANTAGE - The egg yolk flavor element has a golden color, obvious egg yolk characteristic flavor and unique taste, and has great market development potential.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing yolk flavoring, which involves mixing 50-90 wt.% potassium sorbate and 10-50 wt.% sodium diacetate, dissolving in 5-20% brine, mixing well, pouring into the container of fresh egg, marinating at room temperature for 15-20 days, separating the salted egg yolk when it is ripe and taking the salted egg yolk out of the jar, drying the fresh egg yolk at 50-65 degrees C to a moisture content less than 5%, crushing and sieving to obtain egg yolk powder, extracting the egg butter by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, where carbon dioxide flow rate is 10-40 L/hour, extraction pressure 30-40 MPa, extraction temperature 35-50 degrees C, continuous extraction time 1-4 hours, blowing the extracted egg butter into air at a stirring speed of 350-800 revolutions/minutes, an oxidation temperature of 90-120 degrees C, and an air flow of 0.5-2.0 m3/(kg-h), after stirring and oxidizing for 1-3 hours, adding 0.05-0.2 wt.% antioxidant, mixing well and cooling to obtain oxidized egg butter, crushing the salted egg yolk, passing through a 40-100 mesh sieve, stirring, emulsifying the oxidized egg butter, polydextrose, propylene glycol fatty acid ester, tea polyphenol palmitate and other auxiliary materials, homogenizing for 1-3 times at a mass pressure of 20-30 MPa to obtain egg yolk flavoring, measuring and packing into the seasoning bag and sealing to obtain the yolk flavoring.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Compositions: The antioxidant used in the oxidation process of the egg butter is 20-60 wt.% tea polyphenol palmitate and 40-80 wt.% vitamin E compounded with 0.05-0.2 wt.% egg butter.

TI Extracting lithium from lithium sulfate solution containing alkali metal sulfate comprises e. g. adding barium carbonate into a mixed salt solution, reacting with sulfate at room temperature to produce barium sulfate and carbonate.
AU HE C
AE HE C (HECC-Individual)
GA 2020C21898
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting lithium from lithium sulfate solution containing alkali metal sulfate comprises e. g. adding barium carbonate into a mixed salt solution, reacting with sulfate at room temperature and pressure to produce barium sulfate and carbonate, where the precipitation of lithium carbonate is slightly soluble and mixed with the precipitation of barium sulfate, where the chemical reaction equation is as specified, completing the reaction, separating the solution and the precipitate by filtration, obtaining the precipitate contains lithium carbonate, barium sulfate and barium carbonate, adding the precipitate to pure water to adjust the slurry, adding carbon dioxide gas to hydrogenate the slurry, obtaining lithium bicarbonate solution and barium sulfate and barium carbonate precipitates, where the chemical reaction equation is as specified, and subjecting the lithium bicarbonate solution into ion exchange and pyrolysis to obtain high-purity lithium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting lithium from lithium sulfate solution containing alkali metal sulfate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low production cost and high product purity, and is worthy of promotion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting lithium from lithium sulfate solution containing alkali metal sulfate comprises adding barium carbonate into a mixed salt solution, reacting with sulfate at room temperature and pressure to produce barium sulfate and carbonate, where the precipitation of lithium carbonate is slightly soluble and mixed with the precipitation of barium sulfate, where the chemical reaction equation is as specified, completing the reaction, separating the solution and the precipitate by filtration, obtaining the precipitate contains lithium carbonate, barium sulfate and barium carbonate, adding the precipitate to pure water to adjust the slurry, adding carbon dioxide gas to hydrogenate the slurry, obtaining lithium bicarbonate solution and barium sulfate and barium carbonate precipitates, where the chemical reaction equation is as specified, subjecting the lithium bicarbonate solution into ion exchange and then pyrolysis to obtain high-purity lithium carbonate, adding dilute sulfuric acid into the precipitate of barium sulfate and barium carbonate, reacting barium carbonate with dilute sulfuric acid to produce barium sulfate precipitate, washing, filtering, and drying to obtain barium sulfate, where the chemical reaction equation is as specified.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the barium carbonate is added at 14 times the lithium content, and the solution is stirred or ball milled in a closed reaction kettle at room temperature during the reaction. In the step (i), step (ii), and step (iii), the filtrate is recovered and added dilute sulfuric acid to remove the carbonate in the filtrate, the filtrate after the reaction is concentrated and crystallized by MVR, the carbon dioxide in the filtrate is removed, and a mixture of sulfate precipitation is obtained by filtration, the mother liquor is returned to the process of lithium extraction or recovery of lithium, rubidium, and cesium. The sulfate precipitate includes barium sulfate, barium carbonate, lithium carbonate mixture, washing and filtering, sending to hydrogen carbonate, after the hydrogen carbonate is completed, filtering to obtain lithium bicarbonate solution and a mixture of barium sulfate and small amount of barium carbonate.

PN CN111825628-A
TI Synthesizing antioxidant 565 involves firstly using 2,6-di-tert-butylphenol as raw material, and then dissolving 2,6-di-tert-butylphenol in an organic solvent to form a 2,6-di-tert-butylphenol solution.
AU ZHANG W
   LUO S
AE HUBEI HUAHENGDA CHEM CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2020B0891K
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing antioxidant 565 involves firstly using 2,6-di-tert-butylphenol as raw material, dissolving 2,6-di-tert-butylphenol in an organic solvent to form a 2,6-di-tert-butylphenol solution. 6-di-tert-butylphenol solution is added dropwise with sodium nitrite solution to generate 2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-nitrosophenol solution. 2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-nitrosophenol solution formed is placed in a closed container, adding Raney nickel to the 2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-nitrosophenol solution, adding carbon monoxide to reduce to obtain aminophenol solution. After the 2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-nitrosophenol solution is completely reduced, iron crystals are added to the aminophenol solution, and the iron crystals react with the remaining carbon monoxide to produce carbon dioxide and carbon element.
   USE - Method for synthesizing antioxidant 565.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to synthesize antioxidant 565 with high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing antioxidant 565 involves firstly using 2,6-di-tert-butylphenol as raw material, dissolving 2,6-di-tert-butylphenol in an organic solvent to form a 2,6-di-tert-butylphenol solution. 6-di-tert-butylphenol solution is added dropwise with sodium nitrite solution to generate 2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-nitrosophenol solution. 2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-nitrosophenol solution formed is placed in a closed container, adding Raney nickel to the 2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-nitrosophenol solution, adding carbon monoxide to reduce to obtain aminophenol solution. After the 2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-nitrosophenol solution is completely reduced, iron crystals are added to the aminophenol solution, and the iron crystals react with the remaining carbon monoxide to produce carbon dioxide and carbon element. At this time, the aminophenol solution is taken out, and the glass stirring rod is used for continuous stirring, and then the aminophenol solution is filtered through a screen, and the carbon in the aminophenol is filtered, leaving the filtrate. The filtrate is condensed with melamine to obtain 6-(4-hydroxy-3,5-di-tert-butylaniline)-2,4-dichloro-1, 3,5-triazine, and finally 6-(4-hydroxy-3,5-di-tert-butylphenylamino) and n-octyl mercaptan are condensed to obtain antioxidant 565.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent used is methanol or ethanol. The aminophenol solution is condensed with melamine to obtain 6-(4-hydroxy-3,5-di When tert-butyl phenylamino)-2,4-dichloro-1,3,5-triazine. Preferred Conditions: When the 2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-nitrosophenol solution is reduced, the temperature of the reduction reaction is 40-60 degrees C. The reaction time is 2-3h. When the aminophenol solution is continuously stirred with a glass stirring rod, the stirring time is 5-10min. The molar ratio of 6-(4-hydroxy-3,5-di-tert-butylphenylamino) and n-octyl mercaptan is 2:1.

Can                also be used for insulation and/or other home and                industrial applications.

   (1) two-component polyurethane or                polyisocyanurate spray foam composition comprising                A-side component comprising greater than or equal                to 1 polyisocyanate, greater than or equal to 1                blowing agent, and optionally, greater than or                equal to 1 surfactant, and B-side component                comprising greater than or equal to 1 polyol,                greater than or equal to 1 blowing catalyst and/or                gelation catalyst, greater than or equal to 1                blowing agent comprising pressurized gaseous carbon                dioxide and greater than or equal to 1 liquid                blowing agent, optionally greater than or equal to                1 flame retardant, and optionally greater than or                equal to 1 surfactant; and

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: B-side component                  contains 10-40 wt.% liquid blowing agent, based                  on the total weight of the B-side component.                  Liquid blowing agent in the B-side component is                  or comprises a hydrohaloolefin. Blowing agent in                  the A-side component is or comprises a                  hydrohaloolefin gaseous blowing agent. Liquid                  blowing agent in the B-side component is or                  comprises (E)-1-chloro-3,3,3-trifluoropropene                  (HFO-1233zd).

TI Producing olefins comprises forming reaction            insert which contains oxygen and paraffins, and            converting oxygen in reaction insert with part of            paraffins to obtain process gas by oxidative process,            preferably by oxidative dehydrogenation.
AU WOEHL A
   SCHUBERT M
   ZELLHUBER M
   SCHULTE S
   MEISWINKEL A
   SCHOEDEL N
   FRITZ H
   SCHODEL N
   WOHL A
   SCH D N
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 202088389M
AB    NOVELTY - Producing olefins, comprises forming a                reaction insert which contains oxygen and                paraffins, and converting oxygen in the reaction                insert with part of the paraffins to obtain a                process gas by an oxidative process, preferably by                an oxidative dehydrogenation or an oxidative                methane coupling, to the olefin, the process gas at                least the unconverted part of the paraffin and the                oxygen, the olefin, acetylenes, carbon dioxide and                water. The process gas or a gas mixture that is                formed using at least part of the process gas is                partially or completely subjected to partial                removal of the oxygen and the acetylene, at least                partial removal of the oxygen and of the acetylenes                or acetylenes takes place by catalytic conversion                using a catalyst containing copper oxide or                ruthenium, and the catalytic conversion takes place                at least in part in the form of a                hydrogenation.
   USE - The method is useful for producing                olefins.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows acetylene and oxygen to be                removed in simple manner from a gas mixture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                plant for producing olefins, which is set up to                form a reaction feed that contains oxygen and one                or more paraffins, and which is set up to mix some                of the oxygen in the reaction feed with some of the                paraffins obtaining a process gas by an oxidative                process, by an oxidative dehydrogenation or an                oxidative methane coupling, to convert to the                olefin, the process gas at least the unconverted                part of the paraffin and the oxygen, the olefin or                contains several acetylenes, carbon dioxide and                water. The plant is set up to partially or                completely subject the process gas or a gas mixture                that is formed using at least part of the process                gas to an at least partial removal of the oxygen                and the acetylene, with the partial removal of the                oxygen and the acetylene is provided by the                catalytic conversion of a copper oxide or                ruthenium-containing catalyst which is set up to                catalyze the catalytic conversion at least                partially in the form of a hydrogenation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The partial removal of the                  oxygen and the acetylene is carried out after the                  process gas or a part has undergone partial or                  complete condensate separation and compression                  was subjected and before a gas mixture remaining                  after the at least partial removal of the oxygen                  and the acetylene or acetylenes is at least                  partially subjected to stages of carbon dioxide                  removal. The partial removal of the oxygen and                  the acetylene is carried out downstream of stages                  of carbon dioxide removal and upstream of further                  stages of carbon dioxide removal. In which drying                  and separation steps are carried out downstream                  of the partial removal of the oxygen and the                  acetylene or acetylenes. The copper                  oxide-containing catalyst is used, and in which                  the partial removal of the oxygen and the                  acetylene is a catalytic hydrogenation or partial                  reaction of the oxygen with carbon monoxide. The                  partial removal of the oxygen and the acetylene                  is carried out under reaction conditions which                  are a temperature of 180-360 degrees C, a                  pressure of 1-30 bar abs, a gas hourly space                  velocity of 1000-15000 h-1 and a hydrogen to                  oxygen ratio of 0-5. The ruthenium-containing                  catalyst is used and in which the at least                  partial removal of the oxygen and the acetylene                  comprises a catalytic hydrogenation. The partial                  removal of the oxygen and the acetylene or                  acetylenes is carried out under reaction                  conditions. The partial removal of the oxygen and                  the acetylene or acetylenes is carried out with                  the addition of hydrogen. The partial removal of                  the oxygen and the acetylene is carried out                  isothermally or at least in one stage                  adiabatically. The oxidative dehydrogenation is                  carried out as the oxidative dehydrogenation of                  ethane.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111440461-A
TI Preparation of calcium carbonate used for e.g. plastic, involves forming lime slurry formed from crushed limestone, feeding slurry into carbonation reactor, adding surface modifier containing polyetherimide to slurry, filtering, and sieving.
AU WEI M
AE GUANGXI XIAYANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202071718S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of calcium carbonate involves crushing limestone, sieving, calcining at 1000-1200 degrees C to obtain quicklime, passing quicklime to the digestion tank for the digestion reaction to obtain the lime slurry, sieving, removing impurity, aging, and adding water to obtain lime slurry with specific gravity of 1.050-1.070, transporting lime slurry to the carbonation reaction tower, adding 0.8-2.5% crystal form control agent containing urea, entering kiln gas containing carbon dioxide for carbonated mixture, when the pH of carbonated mixture reaches 8, adding sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate, cocamide diethanolamine, 0.5-2.5% surface modifier comprising polyetherimide and titanate coupling agent, turning off the kiln gas, stopping the carbonation reaction to obtain calcium carbonate slurry, (S3) filtering carbonate slurry through a filter press, drying slurry, crushing, and sieving slurry.
   USE - Preparation of calcium carbonate used for plastics and coatings.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of calcium carbonate having excellent stability, dispersion property, mechanical properties, and reduced oil absorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of calcium carbonate involves crushing limestone, sieving, calcining at 1000-1200 degrees C to obtain quicklime, passing quicklime to the digestion tank for the digestion reaction to obtain the lime slurry, sieving, removing impurity, aging, and adding water to obtain lime slurry with specific gravity of 1.050-1.070, transporting lime slurry to the carbonation reaction tower, heating mixture to 40-60 degrees C, adding 0.8-2.5% crystal form control agent comprising urea, melamine, sodium citrate, and Acacia galactomannan, stirring mixture, entering kiln gas containing carbon dioxide for carbonated mixture, when the pH of the carbonated mixture reaches 8, adding sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate, cocamide diethanolamine, 0.5-2.5% surface modifier comprising polyetherimide and titanate coupling agent, based on dry weight of calcium hydroxide in the slurry, continuously performing carbonation reaction until the pH reaches 6.5-7, turning off the kiln gas, stopping the carbonation reaction to obtain calcium carbonate slurry, (S3) filtering carbonate slurry through a filter press, drying slurry, crushing, and sieving slurry.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of urea, melamine, sodium citrate, and Acacia galactomannan in the crystal form control agent is 3-5:1-3:2-4:1-3. The mass ratio of sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate, cocamide diethanolamine, polyetherimide and titanate coupling agent in the surface modifier is 3-7:1-3:3-5:1-3. The digestion reaction is at a ratio of gray to water of 1:5-7. Preferred Process: The drying process is performed using a pulse air dryer. Preferred Conditions: The control temperature during the carbonation reaction is 30-35 degrees C at a speed of 150-300 rpm. Water temperature is 70-80 degrees C for digestion reaction. The feed rate of the pulse air dryer is 200-300 kg/hour, the drying temperature is 80-110 degrees C, and the wind speed is 10-15 m/second.

PT P
PN CN110931796-A; CN110931796-B
TI Lithium ion battery positive electrode material coated with special composition comprises core comprising positive electrode materials, and coating layer comprising hydrophobic reactant which is wrapped around core.
AU ZHOU J
AE YANGZHOU HAIJI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YANG-Non-standard)
GA 202025684D
AB    NOVELTY - A lithium ion battery positive electrode material coated with a special composition comprises a core and a coating layer wrapped around the core. The core is one or more of the positive electrode materials of the lithium ion battery. The coating layer is a hydrophobic reactant obtained by mixing and reacting gas-phase organic component generated after thermal decomposition of a solid organic molecular material with the positive electrode material of the lithium ion battery.
   USE - Lithium ion battery positive electrode material coated with a special composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The positive electrode material coated with a special composition has hygroscopic property which can absorb moisture and carbon dioxide in the air when stored, is easy to transport and use under normal temperature and pressure atmospheric environment or high humidity conditions. The positive electrode material effectively reduces the reaction of environmental moisture and carbon dioxide with surface free lithium and the side reaction of trace water with electrolyte, and improves the safety, cycle and storage performance of batteries.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the lithium ion battery positive electrode material coated with a special composition, which involves (1) processing solid organic molecular materials into blocks, sheets or strips or a mixture of granules or the above shapes, (2) heating the processed solid organic molecular material to decompose to produce a gas-phase organic component, coating a positive electrode material of a lithium ion battery with a hydrophobic special component, (4) cooling, and discharging; and
   (2) coating device, which comprises heating cavity which is used to decompose solid organic molecular material to produce a specific gas-phase organic component, and mixing cavity which has two inlets at the feed end and a discharge end. The specific gas-phase organic component enters the mixing chamber from one feed end, and the positive electrode powder material of the lithium battery enters the other feed end in the mixing chamber, and the specific gas-phase organic component forms a coating layer of a hydrophobic reactant on the surface of the positive electrode powder material of the lithium battery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The positive electrode material of the lithium ion battery comprises lithium-metal oxide chosen from compounds of formulae: xLi2MO3.(1-x)LiMO2, LiM2O4 and LiMPO4.x = 0-1 (excluding 1);andM = metal elements with an atomic number of 6 or more, preferably Co, Ni, Mn, V, Fe, Cr or Al.Preferred Process: The preparation of the lithium ion battery positive electrode material coated with a special composition involves (1) processing solid organic molecular materials into blocks, sheets or strips or a mixture of granules or the above shapes, (2) mixing the positive electrode material of the lithium ion battery and the processed solid organic molecular material, heating to 100-380 degrees C to generate a gas phase organic component, (3) mixing and reacting coating gas-phase organic component with the lithium ion battery positive electrode material for 5-1800 seconds to form a lithium ion battery positive electrode material coated with a hydrophobic special component, and (4) cooling and discharging.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The reactant is a polymerization reactant or a repolymerization reactant. The solid organic molecular material is chosen from one or more of rubber, olefin, silicone, polyacrylate, organic aluminate, organic titanate, and organic aluminum-titanium composite esters, preferably is chosen from one or more synthetic rubber, synthetic polyolefin, synthetic polysiloxane, synthetic polyacrylate, synthetic organic aluminate, synthetic organic titanate, and synthetic organic aluminum-titanium composite ester. The solid organic molecular material is a polymer material comprising homopolymer or a multi-monomer copolymer polymer. Preferred Property: The thickness of the coating layer is 0.5-10 nm. The core has particle size of 10 nm-50 mu m.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. MC A10-E05; A12-E06A; E05-B03; E05-L01; E10-J02C4; E12-A05; E31-K05B; E31-K05C; E34-C03; E35; L03-E01B5B; L03-E08B; X16-B01F1; X16-E01A; X16-E01J

PT P
PN CN110104665-A; CN110104665-B
TI Preparing lithium carbonate using lithium fluoride waste liquid comprises e.g. taking lithium fluoride waste liquid, adding calcium and magnesium slag, stirring, filtering to obtain defluorinated lithium-containing first solution.
AU LI L
   LIAO C
   XIE J
   HU Z
   YE M
   HU Y
   HU B
   LI F
   XIE X
   LIU M
   PENG A
AE JIANGXI GANFENG LITHIUM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   XINYU GANFENG LITHIUM CO LTD (XINY-Non-standard)
GA 201970695K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lithium carbonate using lithium fluoride waste liquid comprises (i) taking lithium fluoride waste liquid, adding calcium and magnesium slag, stirring, filtering, repeating to obtain defluorinated lithium-containing first solution, (ii) washing the calcium and magnesium slag to obtain a second solution, adding calcium magnesium slag filtered to pure water to washing, (iii) mixing the first solution and second solution obtained in the steps (i) and (ii), evaporating and crystallizing to obtain third solution, (iv) adding lithium hydroxide and sulfuric acid and third solution of the step (iii) to adjusting the pH of the solution to 11-13, and filtering to obtain gettering solution, (v) freezing the impurity solution, and filtrating to obtain lithium-containing fine solution, and (vi) passing the resultant lithium-containing fine solution through carbon dioxide to carrying out carbonization, and centrifuging and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing lithium carbonate using lithium fluoride waste liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, economical and environmentally friendly, reduces waste resources; has high recovery rate, less impurity, and stable product quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing lithium carbonate using lithium fluoride waste liquid comprises (i) taking lithium fluoride waste liquid, adding calcium and magnesium slag, stirring, filtering, repeating the above operation for 4-6 times to obtain defluorinated lithium-containing first solution, (ii) washing the calcium and magnesium slag to obtain a second solution, adding calcium magnesium slag filtered to pure water to washing, stirring and filtering to obtain lithium-containing second solution, (iii) mixing the first solution and second solution obtained in the steps (i) and (ii), evaporating and crystallizing to obtain third solution, (iv) adding lithium hydroxide and sulfuric acid and third solution of the step (iii) to adjusting the pH of the solution to 11-13, and filtering to obtain gettering solution, (v) freezing the impurity solution obtained in the step (iv) to -10 to10 degrees C to precipitating the sodium salt, potassium salt, and filtrating to obtain lithium-containing fine solution, and (vi) passing the resultant ithium-containing fine solution through carbon dioxide to carrying out carbonization, and centrifuging and drying An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for battery comprising the lithium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the calcium magnesium slag added is calcium magnesium slag produced by the preparation of lithium carbonate from spodumene, where the main components of the calcium magnesium slag are magnesium hydroxide, calcium sulfate and a lithium-containing compound. The solid-liquid ratio of the calcium magnesium slag and lithium fluoride waste liquid added is 1:3-1:8. In the step (i), the stirring time is 1-2.5 hours. In the step (ii), the pure water mass is added in accordance with the mass ratio of the calcium-magnesium slag filtered in the step (1) to 1:1-2:1.In the third solution step (iii), the volume of is 1/4 to 1/3 of the volume after mixing the first solution and the second solution. In the step step (iv), lithium hydroxide and sulfuric acid are added dropwise while stirring. In the step (vi) the reaction is carried out while introducing carbon dixode. In the step (vi), the drying condition is 130-160 degrees C, and the drying time is 15-25 minutes.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC E05-A01; E10-A11B1; E11-W; X16-E01C1; X16-E01G

PN CN109320252-A
TI Wear resistant porous ceramic material comprises            e.g. aluminum oxide, iron oxide, magnesium oxide,            nickel oxide, silica, nano titanium dioxide, calcium            silicate, silicon carbide, boron nitride, mica powder,            clay, kieselguhr and binder.
AU XU C
AE ANHUI QINGHUAFANG FINE BONE PORCELAIN CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201917286T
AB    NOVELTY - Wear resistant porous ceramic material                comprises 10-20 pts. wt. aluminum oxide, 5-15 pts.                wt. iron oxide, 5-10 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 2-8                pts. wt. nickel oxide, 5-15 pts. wt. silica, 5-10                pts. wt. nano titanium dioxide, 5-15 pts. wt.                calcium silicate, 10-20 pts. wt. silicon carbide,                2-8 pts. wt. boron nitride, 2-7 pts. wt. mica                powder, 5-15 pts. wt. clay, 5-10 pts. wt.                kieselguhr, 1-5 pts. wt. binder, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt.                foaming agent, and 1-3 pts. wt. stabilizer.
   USE - Used as wear resistant porous ceramic                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The ceramic material: has high porosity,                uniform pores and excellent mechanical properties,                no harmful substances are emitted during the                preparation process, and meets environmentally                friendly consciousness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the wear resistant porous ceramic                material, comprising (i) placing sericite powder,                clay and kieselguhr into ball mill, ball milling                for 2-3 hours at a speed of 130-170                revolutions/minute, adding aluminum oxide, iron                oxide, magnesium oxide, nickel oxide, silicon                oxide, nano titanium dioxide, calcium silicate,                silicon carbide and boron nitride, and continuously                ball milling for 1.5-2.5 hours to obtain mixed                powder, (ii) adding mixed powder in the step (i),                binder, and stabilizer to the barrel of the                injection molding machine, preheating to 150-200                degrees C, and melting and plasticizing, (iii)                quickly injecting supercritical carbon dioxide into                the melt in the barrel of the injection molding                machine under the condition of 60-150 Mpa,                dispersing supercritical carbon dioxide and                homogenizing by screw cutting, fully dissolving the                melt, forming single-phase melt, maintaining the                pressure at this time, after being saturating and                balancing, injecting it into mold having                temperature of 30-80 degrees C for filling, and                cooling and shaping, (iv) taking out the mold,                pre-sintering at 700-900 degrees C for 4-6 hours to                obtain semi-finished product, and (v) sintering the                semi-finished product in the step (iv) at high                temperature of 1100-1300 degrees C for 8-12                hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The wear resistant                  porous ceramic material comprises 15 pts. wt.                  aluminum oxide, 10 pts. wt. iron oxide, 8 pts.                  wt. magnesium oxide, 5 pts. wt. nickel oxide, 10                  pts. wt. silica, 8 pts. wt. nano titanium                  dioxide, 10 pts. wt. calcium silicate, 15 pts.                  wt. silicon carbide, 5 pts. wt. boron nitride, 4                  pts. wt. mica powder, 10 pts. wt. clay, 8 pts.                  wt. kieselguhr, 3 pts. wt. binder, 1 pts. wt.                  foaming agent, and 2 pts. wt. stabilizer.                  Preferred Components: The binder comprises 10-20                  pts. wt. bentonite, 5-10 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 2-8                  pts. wt. zirconium silicate, 5-10 pts. wt.                  sepiolite powder, 5-10 pts. wt. zeolite powder,                  5-15 pts. wt. asbestos powder, 5-10 pts. wt.                  hydroxypropyl methyl cellulose, 10-20 pts. wt.                  phenolic resin, 5-15 pts. wt. nitrile rubber, 2-8                  pts. wt. sodium benzoate, 2-6 pts. wt. glycerin,                  1-5 pts. wt. stearamide, 2-8 pts. wt.                  polyethylene glycol and 100-150 pts. wt. water.                  The working condition of the microwave processor                  is that the microwave frequency is 2450 MHz, and                  the output power is 800 W. The foaming agent is                  supercritical carbon dioxide. The stabilizer is                  rare earth stabilizer. Preferred Method: The                  binder is prepaerd by, (a) adding sepiolite                  powder, zeolite powder and asbestos powder to                  water, ultrasonicating for 0.5-1 hours, adding                  bentonite, hydroxypropyl methyl cellulose, zinc                  oxide, zirconium silicate, shaking for 0.5 hours,                  microwave refluxing for 20-30 minutes, and                  maintaining the temperature for 10-30 minutes,                  (b) thoroughly mixing phenolic resin, nitrile                  rubber, glycerin, stearamide, and polyethylene                  glycol, uniformly mixing, heating to 120-150                  degrees C, stirring at constant temperature for                  10-20 minutes, continuously heating to 150-180                  degrees C, stirring at constant temperature for                  5-10 minutes, and stirring at a speed of 800-1000                  revolutions/minute, and (c) uniformly mixing the                  mixture in the step (a), the mixture in the step                  (b), and sodium benzoate, transferring them into                  ball mill, ball milling for 4-5 hours, taking                  out, and sealing and storing.

PN CN109305694-A
TI Desalinating seawater concentrated brine combined with alkali comprises e.g. filtering seawater desalinated brine to remove insoluble impurities, adding sodium chloride into the filtrate, performing carbonization and recycling.
AU PANG S
   LI Z
   XIE Z
   WANG S
   ZHANG F
   PEI S
   LIU Y
   LI M
   NIE Z
AE TIANJIN BOHUA YONGLI CHEM CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201916081U
AB    NOVELTY - Desalinating seawater concentrated brine combined with alkali comprises e.g. (i) filtering seawater desalinated brine to remove insoluble impurities, and adding sodium chloride into the filtrate, (ii) introducing ammonia gas into saturated brine to obtain ammonia brine I, (iii) performing carbonization by introducing excess carbon dioxide into the ammonia brine I, (iv) filtering carbonized mixed solution to separate white crystals and carbonized mother liquor, (v) washing white crystal separated in step (iv) with distilled water, calcining washed product, and (vi) introducing ammonia gas into carbonized mother liquor separated in step (iv), absorbing second ammonia gas to obtain ammonia brine II, and cooling, adding 160-200 g sodium chloride per liter of ammonia brine II, and crystallizing ammonium chloride product by salting out, continuously mixing filtered salting mother liquor with filtrate by seawater desalinated brine, and recycling.
   USE - The method is useful for desalinating seawater concentrated brine combined with alkali.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: meets the requirements of national standard, has simple operation, and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Desalinating seawater concentrated brine combined with alkali comprises (i) filtering seawater desalinated brine to remove insoluble impurities, and adding sodium chloride into the filtrate to obtain saturated brine, (ii) introducing ammonia gas into the saturated brine at 20-25 degrees C ammonia for 12-20 minutes to obtain ammonia brine I, (iii) performing carbonization by introducing excess carbon dioxide into the ammonia brine I to form white crystals, (iv) filtering the carbonized mixed solution to separate the white crystals and the carbonized mother liquor, (v) washing white crystal separated in step (iv) with distilled water at a mass ratio of 0.8:1-1.2:1, calcining washed product at 200-240 degrees C for 30-35 minutes to obtain soda ash product, and (vi) introducing ammonia gas into the carbonized mother liquor separated in step (iv), absorbing second ammonia gas to obtain ammonia brine II, and cooling to 10-15 degrees C, adding 160-200 g sodium chloride per liter of ammonia brine II, and crystallizing ammonium chloride product by salting out, continuously mixing filtered salting mother liquor with the filtrate by seawater desalinated brine, and recycling.

PN CN109266179-A
TI Preparing anti-corona coating material comprises e.g. mixing nano zeolite, wheat bran in deionized water, molding, adding bacterial suspension and reacting, ultrasonic oscillating, carbonizing, adding ethylenediamine stirring and canning.
AU CHEN Q
   DING C
AE CHEN Q (CHEN-Individual)
GA 2019118900
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing anti-corona coating material comprises (i) mixing nano zeolite, wheat bran and deionized water, molding substrate in ceramic pot, adding dark green suspension of Trichoderma viridae bacteria, stirring uniformly to obtain premix, (ii) placing ceramic pot in molding chamber, moisturizing xand mildew reaction and resulting product is modified nano zeolite, (iii) mixing modified nano zeolite and sodium silicate solution in a beaker, dripping hydrochloric acid, ultrasonic oscillating in ultrasonic oscillator and reacting, filtering and separating to obtain residue, (iv) carrying out carbonization under carbon dioxide atmosphere in carbonization furnace to obtain carbonized product, placing in electric resistance furnace and reacting, discharging to obtain the anti-corona filler, and (v) heating epoxy resin, corona resistant filler, xylene, heating, stirring to obtain primer, adding ethylenediamine continuously in mixer, stirring, discharging and canning.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing anti-corona coating material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing anti-corona coating material comprises (i) mixing nano zeolite, wheat bran and deionized water, molding substrate in ceramic pot, adding dark green suspension of Trichoderma viridae bacteria (concentration of 108cfu/ml) into the ceramic pot, stirring uniformly to obtain premix, (ii) placing ceramic pot in molding chamber, moisturizing and mildew reaction is performed for 1-2 weeks and resulting product is modified nano zeolite, (iii) mixing modified nano zeolite and sodium silicate solution (mass fraction of 40%) in beaker, dripping hydrochloric acid having concentration of 0.5 mol/l, adjusting the pH to 4-5, ultrasonic oscillating in the ultrasonic oscillator and reacting, filtering and separating to obtain residue, (iv) placing residue in carbonization furnace, carrying out carbonization under carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain carbonized product, placing in electric resistance furnace, maintaining temperature and reacting, discharging to obtain the anti-corona filler, and (v) heating 50-60 pts. wt. epoxy resin, 12-15 pts. wt. corona resistant filler, 20-25 pts. wt. xylene, heating at 100-110 degrees c, stirring to obtain primer, adding ethylenediamine continuously into the mixer at room temperature, stirring for 10-15 minutes, discharging and canning.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components:In the step (i), the mass ratio of nano zeolite, wheat bran and de-ionized water is 2:1: 10 and addition amount of Trichoderma viridae suspension is 10% of molding subsatrate. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the heating at 25-30 degrees C in molding chamber and relative air humidity is 60-70%.In the step (iii), the mass ratio of modified nano zeolite and sodium silicate solution is 1:8 and the frequency of the ultrasonic oscillation reaction is 25-35 kHz and ultrasonically oscillating for 1-2 hours.In the step (iv),carbonizing at 400-500 degrees C for 1-2 hours, maintaining temperature and reacting at 2000-2100 degrees C and reacting time is 4-5 hours. In the step (v), the addition amount of ethylenediamine is 3% (5% mass of primer).

TI Method for preparing mixed matrix membrane for carbon dioxide separation, involves preparing dispersion, and modified nanoparticles followed by dispersing nanoparticles in aqueous solution, applying yellow dispersion to membrane and drying.
AU WANG Z
   XU R
   DONG S
   SHENG M
   WANG J
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 201910496Y
AB    NOVELTY - A mixed matrix membrane preparing method involves adding UIO-66-NH2 powder to n-nonanol under nitrogen protection, dispersing and stirring ultrasonic uniformity, adding an excess of polyethylene glycol diglycidyl ether to the UIO-66-NH2 dispersion, and heating dispersion. The excess unreacted polyethylene glycol diglycidyl ether and solvent n-nonanol are washed with methanol, centrifugal washing, the solid obtained by vacuum drying is vacuumed to obtain chemically modified nanoparticles PEG-UIO-66-NH2 followed by dispersing the PEG-UIO-66-NH2 nanoparticles in an aqueous solution of 1 wt.% polyvinylamine, adding 10-40 wt.% PEG-UIO-66-NH2 nanoparticles, heating oil bath to obtain a light yellow dispersion, applying pale yellow dispersion to a polysulfone ultrafiltration membrane, drying obtained membrane under constant temperature and humidity to obtain a chemically bridged gas separation mixed matrix membrane.
   USE - Method for preparing mixed matrix membrane for carbon dioxide separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing mixed matrix membrane with short operation time, excellent permeation selection performance and improved interface compatibility of the phases after chemical bridging, in simple, easy and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A mixed matrix membrane preparing method involves adding UIO-66-NH2 powder to n-nonanol under nitrogen protection, dispersing and stirring ultrasonic uniformity, adding an excess of polyethylene glycol diglycidyl ether dropwise to the UIO-66-NH2 dispersion, and heating dispersion at 115-125 degrees C by an oil bath under nitrogen protection conditions. The excess unreacted polyethylene glycol diglycidyl ether and solvent n-nonanol are washed with methanol, centrifugal washing, the solid obtained by vacuum drying is vacuumed to obtain chemically modified nanoparticles polyethylene glycol-UIO-66-NH2 followed by dispersing the polyethylene glycol-UIO-66-NH2 nanoparticles in an aqueous solution of 1 wt.% polyvinylamine, adding 10-40 wt.% polyethylene glycol-UIO-66-NH2 nanoparticles, heating oil bath at 65-75 degrees C to obtain a light yellow dispersion, applying pale yellow dispersion to a polysulfone ultrafiltration membrane, drying obtained membrane under constant temperature and humidity to obtain a chemically bridged gas separation mixed matrix membrane.

PT P
PN CN109126461-A; CN109126461-B
TI Method for regenerating flue gas desulfurization absorbent, involves mixing calcium oxalate and sulfuric acid solution, mixing sodium base absorption desulfurization product with pre-reaction solution followed by drying and recycling system.
AU YANG L
   ZHU M
   ZHANG J
   MA Y
   HUANG Y
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 201904960G
AB    NOVELTY - A flue gas desulfurization absorbent regenerating method involves mixing calcium oxalate and sulfuric acid solution, heating and filtering the reaction slurry to obtain a pre-reaction solution and the solid phase is calcium sulfate. The sodium base absorption desulfurization product is mixed with the pre-reaction solution, the reaction is stirred and the filter cake and dilute sulfuric acid filtrate is filtered. The filter cake is mixed with water, the causticizing reaction is carried out by adding calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide, the reaction product is solidified and the liquid is separated to obtain a calcium oxalate precipitate and a causticizing reaction mother liquor containing sodium hydroxide, where the calcium oxalate precipitate is returned. The carbon dioxide-containing gas is introduced into the reaction mother liquid to obtain sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate or a mixed solution with sodium hydroxide, dried and recycled the flue gas desulfurization system.
   USE - Method for regenerating flue gas desulfurization absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables regenerating flue gas desulfurization absorbent with high conversion rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A flue gas desulfurization absorbent regenerating method involves mixing calcium oxalate and sulfuric acid solution in a molar ratio of 1:1-3, heating for 30-90 minutes and filtering the reaction slurry to obtain a pre-reaction solution and the solid phase is calcium sulfate. The sodium base absorption desulfurization product is mixed with the pre-reaction solution, the reaction is stirred for 10-60 minutes and the filter cake and dilute sulfuric acid filtrate is filtered. The filter cake is mixed with water, the causticizing reaction is carried out by adding 40-80 mass% calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide, after fully reacting for 30-90 minutes, the reaction product is solidified and the liquid is separated to obtain a calcium oxalate precipitate and a causticizing reaction mother liquor containing sodium hydroxide, where the calcium oxalate precipitate is returned. The carbon dioxide-containing gas is introduced into the reaction mother liquid to obtain sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate or a mixed solution with sodium hydroxide, which is directly or after is concentrated, dried and recycled the flue gas desulfurization system.
