AB    NOVELTY - Producing foam flameproof interface agent for fly ash and phosphogypsum acid comprises (i) mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier and milling, adding into the kiln, roasting, subjecting clinker to water-milling and dissolution, and carrying out solid-liquid separating, (ii) floating precipitate, separating out sulfide, roasting separated sulfide, generating flue gas by the baking vanadium pentoxide, catalyzing reaction and absorbing concentrated sulfuric acid, (iii) adding calcium oxide into liquid obtained by solid-liquid separating, heating and stirring, and filtering, (iv) introducing carbon dioxide gas into the sodium metaaluminate solution until no precipitation occurs, washing precipitate with distilled water, and drying washed precipitate and grounding and (v) processing aluminum hydroxide, and polyethylene-vinyl acetate emulsion, wetting agent, thickening agent, water, bamboo fiber, magnesium oxide, expanded graphite, dispersant and paraffin oil.
   USE - The method is useful for producing foam flameproof interface agent for fly ash and phosphogypsum acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, high utilization rate, high additional value, and good flame retardant material of foam flame-proof interface agent and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing foam flameproof interface agent for fly ash and phosphogypsum acid comprises (i) mixing the phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier and milling to obtain raw material, adding into the kiln, roasting to obtain clinker, subjecting clinker to water-milling and dissolution, and carrying out solid-liquid separating, (ii) floating the precipitate obtained by the solid-liquid separation, separating out sulfide, roasting separated sulfide, generating flue gas by the baking vanadium pentoxide, catalyzing reaction and absorbing concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric acid, (iii) adding calcium oxide into the liquid obtained by solid-liquid separating, heating and stirring, and filtering to obtain a sodium metaaluminate solution, (iv) introducing carbon dioxide gas into the sodium metaaluminate solution until no precipitation occurs, washing the precipitate with distilled water, and drying washed precipitate and grounding to obtain aluminum hydroxide and (v) processing the aluminum hydroxide, and polyethylene-vinyl acetate emulsion, wetting agent, thickening agent, water, bamboo fiber, magnesium oxide, expanded graphite, dispersant and paraffin oil processing to obtain foam flame-proof interface agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or sodium hydroxide, the modified agent is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue, industrial rotary kiln. The kiln is industrial tunnel kiln or industrial vertical kiln. In the step (i), the weight ratio of phosphogypsum and fly ash is 1:1.7, the additive ratio contained in the raw material molar ratio of sodium oxide and aluminum oxide + iron(III) oxide sum of 1:1 is added, the mixing ratio of the modifier material is 8-24 % of the total weight, the roasting temperature is 1020-1400 degrees C, the liquid-solid ratio at the time of elution of the water mill is 2-7:1. In the step (ii), the calcination temperature is 1150-1250 degrees C, the time is 3-5 hours; the condition is that the sulfide is placed in an oxygen-rich environment of 40-50%. In the step (iii), the calcium oxide is analytically pure, the dosage is 7-10 g/l, the heating temperature is 75-95 degrees C and the stirring time is 1-2 hours. In the step (iv), the concentration of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide gas is greater than 38%, the temperature of the drying is 95-105 degrees C. In the step (iv), the wetting agent is nekal, the thickening agent is polyurethane thickening agent, the dispersant is dispersant 5040. In the step (v), the foam flame-proof interface agent comprises 30-40 pts. wt. aluminum hydroxide, 30-40 pts. wt. polyethylenevinyl acetate emulsion, 1-2 pts. wt. wetting agent, 3-5 pts. wt. thickener, 35-45 pts. wt. water, 20-30 pts. wt. bamboo fiber, 10-15 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 5-10 pts. wt. expanded graphite, 1-3 pts. wt. dispersant and 3-5 pts. wt. paraffin oil. In the step (v), the method of the processing is as follows: adding polyethylene-vinyl acetate emulsion, wetting agent and thickening agent into the stirrer for stirring, adding water, bamboo fiber and magnesium oxide for the secondary mixing, finally adding aluminum hydroxide, expandable graphite, the dispersant and paraffin oil to stirring for three times. The time of stirring of the stirring speed is 50-100 revolutions/minutes, and time is 10-15 minutes, the secondary stirring of the stirring speed is 300-400 revolutions/minutes, the time 20-25 minutes for three times, the stirring is stirring speed is 500-600 revolutions/minutes and the time is 15-20 minutes.

AB    NOVELTY - Treating heparin production wastewater by collecting waste water, adjusting pH value, removing suspended impurities, squeezing and dehydrating cleaned sediment, entering waste water into desalination section, desalting, sending waste water to flotation section, orderly passing through primary air floatation and secondary air floatation, removing suspended colloid in waste water, adding polyaluminum chloride (PAC) and polyacrylamide (PAM), flocculation reacting, discharging air floating scum into dross tank for residue water separating, returning sewage into adjusting tank, flowing air floating water into hydrolyzed acid chemical section, decomposing macromolecule organic matter into easily degradable small molecule, flowing acidification tank water into aerobic moving bed biofilm reactor (MBBR) section, adopting aerobic MBBR reactor, adding betaine, and converting large amount of organic substances in water to carbon dioxide and water by aerobic microorganism.
   USE - Method for treating heparin production wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good purifying effect and high efficiency, avoids environment pollution, and improves recovery rate of protein.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating heparin production wastewater involves collecting waste water, adjusting pH value to 2-3, removing suspended impurities using automatic mechanical barrier, squeezing and dehydrating cleaned sediment, entering waste water into desalination section, desalting waste water by mechanical vapor recompression (MVR) evaporator, sending waste water to flotation section by lifting pump, orderly passing through primary air floatation and secondary air floatation, removing suspended colloid in waste water, adding PAC and PAM, flocculation reacting, discharging air floating scum into dross tank for residue water separating, returning sewage into adjusting tank, flowing air floating water into hydrolyzed acid chemical section, using pulse acidification reactor, decomposing macromolecule organic matter into easily degradable small molecule by facultative microorganisms, flowing acidification tank water into aerobic moving bed biofilm reactor (MBBR) section, adopting aerobic MBBR reactor, adding betaine, converting large amount of organic substances in water to carbon dioxide and water by aerobic microorganism, flowing aerobic tank effluent into secondary sedimentation tank, separating mud, flowing supernatant into advanced oxidation section, adopting Fenton high-grade oxidation tower, adding hydrogen peroxide and iron sulfate, removing difficultly degraded organic matter, flowing water from oxidation tower into sedimentation separation section, and separating sludge and water by final sedimentation tank.

PN CN108793954-A
TI Preparing daily-use ceramic mud and acid comprises e.g. beating pyrophyllite and kaolin, washing, demineralizing, sieving, and mixing with alumina powder, potassium feldspar, illite and montmorillonite, grinding, screening and pressing.
AU CHEN X
   LI J
   LI M
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2018927378
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing daily-use ceramic mud and acid comprises (i) mixing phosphogypsum, low-grade bauxite, additive and modifier and ground to form raw meal, subjection to kiln for roasting to obtain clinker, (ii) dissolving clinker and carrying solid and liquid separation, (iii) mixing carbon dioxide to solution until white precipitate is no longer produced, filtering, drying, burning and crushing to obtain alumina powder, (iv) mechanically beating pyrophyllite and kaolin, washing, demineralizing, sieving, and mixing with alumina powder, potassium feldspar, illite and montmorillonite obtained in step (iii), and grinding in magnetic separator to remove iron, screening and pressing to obtain daily-use ceramic mud, (v) floating residue obtained in step (ii) and separating sulfide, and (vi) placing in oxygen-rich environment, baking, producing flue gas generated by calcination is catalytically reacted with vanadium pentoxide and absorbing with concentrated sulfuric acid to produce sulfuric acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing daily-use ceramic mud and acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The mud and acid has good high utilization rate, high strength, high white, high thermal shock resistance and is economical. The method is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing daily-use ceramic mud and acid comprises (i) mixing phosphogypsum, low-grade bauxite, additive and modifier and ground to form raw meal, subjection to kiln for roasting to obtain clinker, (ii) dissolving clinker and carrying solid and liquid separation, (iii) mixing carbon dioxide to solution until white precipitate is no longer produced, filtering white precipitate, washing, drying, burning and crushing to obtain alumina powder, (iv) mechanically beating pyrophyllite and kaolin, washing, demineralizing by magnetic separator, sieving, and mixing with alumina powder, potassium feldspar, illite and montmorillonite obtained in step (iii), and grinding in magnetic separator to remove iron, screening and pressing to obtain daily-use ceramic mud, (v) floating residue obtained in step (ii) and separating sulfide, and (vi) placing in 30-50% oxygen-rich environment, baking at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours, producing flue gas generated by calcination is catalytically reacted with vanadium pentoxide and absorbing with concentrated sulfuric acid to produce sulfuric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: In the step (i) the additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda and the modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The mixed ratio of phosphogypsum and low-grade bauxite is 1:0.9-1.8. The additive addition ratio is added according to the molecular ratio of the sum of sodium oxide, aluminum oxide and iron oxide contained in the raw material is 1:1. The mixing ratio of modifier is 10-25%. The kiln is industrial rotary kiln, industrial tunnel kiln or industrial vertical kiln. In step (ii), the clinker is firstly grounded and then dissolved. The liquid to solid ratio at time of dissolution is 4-6:1. In the step (iv), the daily-use ceramic mud comprises 15-20 pts. wt. alumina powder, 10-12 pts. wt. pyrophyllite, 20-25 pts. wt. kaolin, 25-30 pts. wt. potassium feldspar, 8-10 pts. wt. illite and 8-10 pts. wt. Monto Stone. In the step (iv), the mesh number of mixed raw material is 300-400 meshes. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the baking is carried out at 1000-1350 degrees C for 1-2 hours. In the step (iii), the burning is carried out at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours.

TI Co-producing acid by phosphogypsum and high-sulfur bauxite flame retardant starch adhesive comprises e. g. mixing and grinding phosphogypsum, high-sulfur bauxite, additives and modifiers into raw meal and roasting to obtain clinker.
AU CHEN X
   GU W
   WANG L
   ZHENG K
   LUO X
   ZHOU D
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201892706P
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing acid by phosphogypsum and high-sulfur bauxite flame retardant starch adhesive comprises e. g. (i) mixing and grinding phosphogypsum, high-sulfur bauxite, additives and modifiers into raw meal, sending into the kiln, roasting to obtain clinker, and subjecting the clinker to water-grinding and dissolution, and carrying out solid-liquid separation; (ii) adding calcium oxide into the liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation, heating and stirring, and filtering to obtain sodium meta-aluminate solution; (iii) passing carbon dioxide gas into the sodium meta-aluminate solution, precipitating the aluminum hydroxide, washing the aluminum hydroxide precipitate with distilled water, drying and washing the washed aluminum hydroxide precipitate to obtain aluminum hydroxide; and (iv) taking aluminum hydroxide, and processing with corn starch, deionized water and thickener to obtain flame retardant starch adhesive.
   USE - The method is useful for co-producing acid by phosphogypsum and high-sulfur bauxite flame retardant starch adhesive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces environmental pollution and ensures high additional value and high utilization rate, and is simple, and has low production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Co-producing acid by phosphogypsum and high-sulfur bauxite flame retardant starch adhesive comprises (i) mixing and grinding phosphogypsum, high-sulfur bauxite, additives and modifiers into raw meal, sending into the kiln, roasting to obtain clinker, and subjecting the clinker to water-grinding and dissolution, and carrying out solid-liquid separation; (ii) adding calcium oxide into the liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation, heating and stirring, and filtering to obtain sodium meta-aluminate solution; (iii) passing carbon dioxide gas into the sodium meta-aluminate solution, precipitating the aluminum hydroxide, washing the aluminum hydroxide precipitate with distilled water, drying and washing the washed aluminum hydroxide precipitate to obtain aluminum hydroxide; (iv) taking aluminum hydroxide, and processing with corn starch, deionized water and thickener to obtain flame retardant starch adhesive; and (v) performing solid-liquid separation to obtain flotation of the precipitate, separating the sulfide, calcining the separated sulfide, generating the flue gas by the calcination, catalyzing by vanadium pentoxide, obtaining the sulfuric acid by absorption with concentrated sulfuric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the sulfur content in the high-sulfur bauxite is higher than 0.7%, the additive includes sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda, the modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue and the kiln is an industrial rotary kiln, an industrial tunnel kiln or an industrial vertical kiln. In the step (i), the phosphogypsum and the high-sulfur bauxite are mixed in a ratio of 1-1.4:1-1.7 by weight, the additive addition ratio is 1:1 according to the molecular ratio of the sum of sodium oxide and aluminum oxide + iron oxide contained in the raw meal, the mixing ratio of the modifier is 11-28% of the total weight of the raw meal. In the step (iv), the flame retardant starch adhesive comprises 18-25 pts. wt. aluminum hydroxide, 80-100 pts. wt. corn starch, 200-250 pts. wt. deionized water, and 1-3 pts. wt. thickening agent. The thickening agent is polyvinyl alcohol. Preferred Method: The calcination temperature is 1150-1350 degrees C, the time is 2-4 hours, the liquid-solid ratio at the time of elution of the water mill is 3-6:1. In the step (ii), the calcium oxide is analytically pure, and the amount is 7-12 g/l, the heating temperature is 80-90 degrees C and the stirring time is 1.5-2 hours. In the step (iii), the carbon dioxide concentration in the carbon dioxide gas is greater than 38%, and the dust content is less than 20 mg/hm3, and the drying temperature is 95-115 degrees C. In the step (iv), the processing method is to first heat the deionized water, stirring in cornstarch to make gelatinized cornstarch, adding thickener and mixing, adding aluminum hydroxide and mixing at high temperature. The heating temperature is 80-85 degrees C, the time of stirring the corn starch is 60-90 minutes and the temperature at the high temperature is 85-90 degrees C for 2-3 hours. In the step (v), the calcination condition is the sulfide is calcined in oxygen-rich environment of 40-45%, the temperature is 1100-1200 degrees C and the time is 2-5 hours.

PN CN108546084-A
TI Co-production of polished tile, involves mixing phosphogypsum, red mud, additive and modifier, performing solid-liquid separation, drying, adding decolorizing agent, grinding, mixing quartz, ball milling, drying, calcining, polishing.
AU CHEN X
   LU F
   WANG H
   YANG H
   WANG Q
   HUANG B
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201875861U
AB    NOVELTY - The method for co-production of polished tile, involves mixing phosphogypsum, red mud, additive and modifier, grinding, calcining, clinking, performing solid-liquid separation, separating the sulfide from precipitate, calcining, absorbing concentrated sulfuric acid, adding carbon dioxide to liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation until the precipitate is no longer produced, drying the precipitate at 100-120 degrees C, after the moisture is below 0.5%, obtaining crude aluminum hydroxide, adding 0.1-0.6 wt.% decolorizing agent and 0.05-0.2 wt.% dispersing agent to crude aluminum hydroxide, grinding to obtain aluminum hydroxide, mixing quartz, talc powder, feldspar, diatomaceous earth and mica powder, smashing, sifting after ball milling, mixing kaolin, aluminum hydroxide and water to form slurry, drying the slurry to obtain raw material powder, pressing the raw material powder into a brick blank, calcining the brick, cooling and polishing to obtain final product.
   USE - Method for co-production of polished tile.
   ADVANTAGE - The environmentally-friendly method enables co-production of polished tile with comprehensive utilization rate and high utilization rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for co-production of polished tile, involves (1) mixing phosphogypsum, red mud, additive and modifier, grinding to form a raw meal, delivering into the kiln, calcining to form clinker, clinking the clinker by grinding with water, performing solid-liquid separation, (2) separating the sulfide from precipitate, calcining the separated sulfide, absorbing concentrated sulfuric acid, after the flue gas generated by the calcination is catalyzed by vanadium pentoxide, (3) adding carbon dioxide to liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation until the precipitate is no longer produced, drying the precipitate at 100-120 degrees C, after the moisture is below 0.5%, obtaining crude aluminum hydroxide, adding 0.1-0.6 wt.% decolorizing agent and 0.05-0.2 wt.% dispersing agent to crude aluminum hydroxide, grinding to obtain aluminum hydroxide, (4) mixing quartz, talc powder, feldspar, diatomaceous earth and mica powder, smashing, sifting after ball milling, mixing kaolin, aluminum hydroxide and water to form slurry, drying the slurry to obtain raw material powder, pressing the raw material powder into a brick blank, calcining the brick, cooling and polishing to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Process: The red mud is subjected to Bayer process for producing alumina. In step (1), the kiln is selected from industrial rotary kiln, industrial tunnel kiln or industrial vertical kiln. The grinding process is carried out using alumina lining ball mill. The grinding medium is alumina ball. The ball to material ratio is 6-9:1. In step (4), the sieving is carried out using 80-100 mesh screen. In step (4), the dusting is dried until the water content of the slurry is 0.1-0.5%, reacting at temperature of 1200-1250 degrees C for 3-5 hours and calcining for 1-3 hours. Preferred Composition: The additive is selected from sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The mass ratio of raw meal, phosphogypsum and red mud is 1-1.6:1. The mass ratio of sodium oxide and total amount of aluminum oxide and ferric oxide is 1:1. The modifier is 13-28% of the total weight of the raw meal. The liquid to solid ratio at the time of elution is 4-7:1. The decolorizing agent is selected from sodium percarbonate, sodium perborate and/or sodium persulfate. The polished brick comprises 10-15 pts. wt. quartz, 5-10 pts. wt. talc powder, 15-20 pts. wt. feldspar, 15-20 pts. wt. diatomaceous earth, 5-10 pts. wt. mica powder, 20-25 pts. wt. kaolin, 20-25 pts. wt. aluminum hydroxide and 70-100 pts. wt. water. Preferred Conditions: In step (1), the calcining process is performed at a temperature of 900-1300 degrees C for 1-5 hours. The step (2) comprises adding 40-45% sulfide in oxygen-rich environment of 40-45% and calcining at 1000-1200 degrees C for 2-5 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersing agent is selected from ethanol or polyethylene glycol.

PN CN108479744-A
TI Preparation of desulfurization catalyst comprises            using coal gangue, calcining, steam activating, pore            expanding, and loading carbon skeleton structure and            active component.
AU SUN Y
   LIU C
   HOU W
   WANG Z
AE SUN Y (SUNY-Individual)
GA 2018707546
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of desulfurization catalyst                comprises using coal gangue as raw material;                crushing, ball milling, adding sodium carbonate,                mixing, placing in tube furnace, and calcining with                nitrogen, carbon dioxide and oxygen gas; adding                into high temperature reaction kettle, heating,                controlling pressure, introducing water at                supercritical state, and reacting; adding sample                into aqueous solution of pore-expanding agent,                stirring, heating, filtering, and drying; adding                carbon skeleton structure to ultrasonic reactor,                adding organic titanium solution to control                ultrasonic frequency, temperature and ultrasonic                time, drying sample, calcining at 800-900 degrees C                in muffle furnace, and roasting for 1-2 hours; and                soaking into mixed solution of active component as                rare earth metal salt and alkali metal salt,                ultrasonically immersing for 20-80 minutes, suction                filtering, placing in oven at 110 degrees C in                nitrogen atmosphere, and calcining.
   USE - Method for preparing desulfurization                catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of desulfurization catalyst                comprises using coal gangue as raw material;                crushing, ball milling to less than or equal to 10                mesh size, adding sodium carbonate, mixing, placing                in tube furnace, and calcining with nitrogen,                carbon dioxide and oxygen gas at 700-1200 degrees C                for 20-30 minutes, where mass ratio of coal gangue                and sodium carbonate is 3-4:1; adding into high                temperature reaction kettle, heating to 300-400                degrees C, controlling pressure at 22.4 MPa,                introducing water at supercritical state, and                reacting at 10-15 minutes; adding sample into                aqueous solution of pore-expanding agent, stirring,                heating for 1-3 hours, filtering, and drying at                110-130 degrees C, where pore expanding agent is                lithium metal salt; adding carbon skeleton                structure to ultrasonic reactor, adding organic                titanium solution to control ultrasonic frequency,                temperature and ultrasonic time, drying sample at                110-120 degrees C for 2-3 hours, calcining at                800-900 degrees C in muffle furnace, and roasting                for 1-2 hours; and soaking into mixed solution of                active component as rare earth metal salt and                alkali metal salt, ultrasonically immersing for                20-80 minutes, suction filtering, placing in oven                at 110 degrees C to constant weight in nitrogen                atmosphere, and calcining at 600-1000 degrees C for                2-3 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of                  nitrogen, carbon dioxide and oxygen gas is                  1:2-3:4-8. The lithium salt is lithium nitrate,                  lithium sulfate, lithium carbonate, or lithium                  chloride. The rare earth metal salt is lanthanum                  nitrate, cerium nitrate or yttrium nitrate. The                  alkali metal salt is potassium nitrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The organic titanium is                  titanium methoxide, or titanium butoxide.

PN CN108450858-A
TI Direct and immediate curing of dehydrated kimchi by cleaning vegetable, drying, sterilizing, spreading, drying, chopping, optionally adding little salt, placing to sterilized glass bottle, placing to high-quality vacuum bag, and covering.
AU WU J
AE WU J (WUJJ-Individual)
GA 201869269E
AB    NOVELTY - A direct and immediate curing of dehydrated kimchi includes selecting vegetable, cleaning, drying, high temperature sterilizing with boiling water, spreading, drying, and chopping; optionally adding little salt, placing to sterilized glass bottle while squeezing out air, piling, covering with hot old leaf, wrapping entire bottle with hazelnut leaves, and tying with a straw vine; washing exterior of glass bottle with sterile water, placing to high-quality vacuum bag, covering top of bottle with small porcelain dish, performing quick freeze sterilization, placing to sterilized water to carry out low temperature fermentation, and naturally decomposing sugar contained in vegetables to produce lactic acid bacteria while timely discharging carbon dioxide gas and liquid; and vacuum packaging fermented mature vegetable into common vacuum bag, adding small amount of sterilized water to provide a more sealing effect, cold storing, and inverting bottle.
   USE - Method for direct and immediate curing of dehydrated kimchi.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is pollution-free. The kimchi can be directly eaten.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A direct and immediate curing of dehydrated kimchi comprises selecting vegetable grown in Winter, cleaning, drying, high temperature sterilizing with boiling water, spreading, drying, and chopping; optionally adding little salt, placing to sterilized glass bottle while squeezing out air using a stick, piling vegetables until it reaches the top of bottle, covering with hot old leaf, wrapping entire bottle with hazelnut leaves, and tying with a straw vine; washing exterior of glass bottle with sterile water, placing to high-quality vacuum bag, covering top of bottle with small porcelain dish to form disposable vacuum suction package and prevent contact with air pollution, performing quick freeze sterilization at - 2 degrees C for half a day, placing to sterilized water to carry out low temperature fermentation at 13-20 degrees C, and naturally decomposing sugar contained in vegetables for 20-30 days to produce lactic acid bacteria while timely discharging carbon dioxide gas and liquid; and vacuum packaging fermented mature vegetable into common vacuum bag, adding small amount of sterilized water to provide a more sealing effect, cold storing to 2-5 degrees C for 1 year, and inverting bottle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The vegetable is mustard, cabbage, carrot or cucumber leaf.

TI Ammonia extraction system of coal chemical conversion acid condensate comprises e.g. carbon dioxide separating tower, ammonia desorbing tower, ammonia rectifying tower and amine-containing condensate set with carbon dioxide separating tower.
AU DENG M
   LI H
   WANG S
   ZHOU F
   LIU H
AE CHINA CHENGDA ENG CO LTD (CNCE-C)
   CHINA CHENGDA ENG CO LTD (CNCE-C)
GA 2018642729
AB    NOVELTY - Ammonia extraction system of coal chemical conversion acid condensate comprises CO2 separating tower (1), ammonia desorbing tower (2) and ammonia rectifying tower (3). The amine-containing condensate is connected to CO2 separation tower through ammonia-containing condensate pump (4). The bottom of CO2 separation column is connected to ammonia desorption tower. The top of the ammonia desorption column is connected to the ammonia distillation column through a condensation module. The top of the ammonia rectification column is sequentially connected to a sulfur adsorber (7), ammonia compressor, ammonia condenser and liquid ammonia buffer tank.
   USE - Used as ammonia extraction system of coal chemical conversion acid condensate.
   ADVANTAGE - The ammonia extraction system solves a series of problems including unstable production composition of existing single tower side line production method, low liquid ammonia yield, poor quality of liquid ammonia product and corrosion of equipment sulfur that affects operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for extracting ammonia, comprising pressurizing ammonia-containing condensate by ammonia-containing condensate pump, sending hot and cold materials to upper part and middle part of CO2 separation tower, separating CO2, H2S and NH3 mixed gas at the top of CO2 separation tower and entering water washing section of CO2 separation tower, washing with low temperature condensate and water, washing off ammonia to obtain high concentration CO2 and hydrogen sulfide (H2S) acid gas in CO2 separation tower top, sending dilute ammonia water at the bottom of CO2 separation tower to ammonia desorption tower to desorb ammonia and CO2, sending washing liquid at the bottom of the ammonia desorption tower after heat recovery, most of the desorbed gas at the top of ammonia desorption column is condensed and refluxing to ammonia desorption column through desorber condenser, desorbing remaining ammonia gas and condensing by secondary condenser connected in series, returning condensate from secondary condenser to CO2 separation column for separation, sending ammonia gas at the top to ammonia rectification column, subjecting ammonia gas to ammonia rectification, desulfurization, compression and condensation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the ammonia extraction system.
   CO2 separating tower (1)
   Ammonia desorbing tower (2)
   Ammonia rectifying tower (3)
   Ammonia-containing condensate pump (4)
   Sulfur adsorber (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises pressurizing amine-containing condensate by ammonia-containing condensate pump with 8-20 BAR and operating pressure of CO2, H2S and NH3 mixture separated by top of CO2 separation tower is 8-20 BAR, and washing water temperature in the top of the CO2 separation tower is 40-50 degrees C. The operating pressure of ammonia desorption column is 2-6 BAR.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Composition: The top of the CO2 separation tower is connected to the sulfur recovery system through CO2 separation tower condenser. The bottom of the ammonia desorption column is connected to the CO2 separation tower. The bottom of ammonia rectification tower is connected to CO2 separation column through recycle reflux tank and circulation pump. The amine-containing condensate is divided into two parts by ammonia-containing condensate pump and a part is connected to the upper portion of the CO2 separation tower. The other part is connected to the middle of the CO2 separation column via ammonia-containing condensate preheater. The condensing assembly includes desorption column condenser and secondary condenser. The top of ammonia desorption column is passed through desorption column condenser and secondary condenser and is connected to the ammonia rectification column. The condensate of the desorber condenser is returned to the ammonia desorption column. The condensate of secondary condenser is returned to the CO2 separation column via a circulation pump.

   HEE H J

AE UNIV IND COLLABORATION & CONSULTING FOUN (UYIN-Non-standard)
GA 201846019A

TI Preparing surface modified composite active aluminum oxide-zinc oxide carrier spherical particle comprises e.g. grinding active alumina and zinc oxide ball, mixing sodium bicarbonate with carboxymethyl cellulose, activating and modifying.
AU CHEN F
   DUAN X
AE DALIAN QUANJING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 2018401320
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing surface modified composite active aluminum oxide-zinc oxide carrier spherical particle comprises e.g. (i) grinding active alumina ball to obtain alumina powder, then grinding zinc oxide ball to obtain zinc oxide powder, then mixing sodium bicarbonate and silver nitrate with carboxymethyl cellulose, ball milling to obtain binder micro powder, (ii) mixing three fine powders prepared in step (i), granulating, molding, placing in mixed gas of ethanol, carbon dioxide and water vapor, fumigating for passivation, and (iii) activating and modifying obtained particles and heating.
   USE - The method is useful for absorbing indoor air-oxidation process.
   ADVANTAGE - The particle has high adsorption capacity and excellent structural strength characteristics; and is excellent oxidant carrier.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing surface modified composite active aluminum oxide-zinc oxide (Al2O3-ZnO) carrier spherical particle comprises (i) grinding active alumina ball to particle size of 0.8-1.0 mu m to obtain alumina powder, then grinding zinc oxide ball to particle size of 1-1.5 mu m to obtain zinc oxide powder, then mixing sodium bicarbonate and silver nitrate with carboxymethyl cellulose and ball milling to particle size of 0.8-1 mu m to obtain binder micro powder, (ii) mixing three fine powders prepared in step (i), granulating, sieving to particle size of 5-7 mm, then molding under pressure of 130-150 MPa, placing in mixed gas of ethanol, carbon dioxide and water vapor and fumigating for 3-4 hours for passivation, where mass ratio of each component is: 800-900 pts. wt. alumina, 50-100 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 20-40 pts. wt. silver nitrate, 20-30 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate and 10-15 pts. wt. carboxymethyl cellulose, and (iii) activating and modifying particles obtained in step (ii) at 350-450 degrees C, heating at a rate of 35-40 degrees C/hour, insulating for 2.5-3.5 hours and cooling to obtain composite active particles. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for surface modified composite active aluminum oxide-zinc oxide (Al2O3-ZnO) carrier spherical particle obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The activated alumina is active gamma -aluminum oxide or rho -aluminum oxide. The zinc oxide is active zinc oxide. In step (ii), volume ratio between ethanol, carbon dioxide and water vapor is 1:1-2:3-4. In step (iii), water content of composite active carrier particle is not greater than 3.5%.

PN CN107919235-A
TI Preparation of polymer capacitor involves carbonizing common coal tar pitch, grinding, heating, cooling, washing, stirring e.g. activated carbon, coating active slurry on aluminum foil, laminating in shell, sealing and injecting liquid.
AU WANG Y
AE SHAANXI SHENGMAI OIL CO LTD (SHSH-C)
GA 201831290X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polymer capacitor involves carbonizing common coal tar pitch, removing small molecule volatile components, grinding, mixing intermediate phase asphalt with potassium hydroxide in crucible, introducing argon, heating, introducing carbon dioxide for combined activation, cooling, neutralizing, washing, adding activated carbon, acetylene black and binder into stirring kettle for stirring, coating electrode active slurry on aluminum foil, drying coated pole piece, rolling dried pole piece, cutting, laminating cut pole pieces in shell, sealing and injecting liquid.
   USE - Preparation of polymer capacitor.
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer capacitor is prepared by adopting self-made activated carbon material. The polymer capacitor has improved capacitance of l670F, internal resistance of 6 m Omega , activated carbon electrode material specific capacity of 110.6 F/g, no significant attenuation of capacity after 5000 cycles and the leakage current of the supercapacitor is almost constant at about 50 mA after 1 hour constant voltage. The prepared polymer capacitor has excellent effects in four safety tests such as overcharge, short circuit, extrusion and needling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of polymer capacitor involves carbonizing common coal tar pitch, removing small molecule volatile components in coal tar pitch to obtain intermediate phase asphalt, grinding intermediate phase asphalt, mixing intermediate phase asphalt with potassium hydroxide according to an alkali carbon mass ratio of 3:1, uniformly mixing in crucible, introducing argon, heating, activating, introducing carbon dioxide to carry out combined activation for 2 hours, cooling, neutralizing excess potassium hydroxide with dilute hydrochloric acid after the mixture is discharged out of the furnace, washing with deionized water until the pH value is close to 6.5 to obtain activated carbon, adding activated carbon, acetylene black and binder in the mass ratio of 80:10:10 into stirring kettle for stirring, coating electrode active slurry on an aluminum foil with thickness of 0.3mm after stirring is completed, drying the coated pole piece under vacuum at 110 degrees C, rolling dried pole piece, cutting, laminating cut pole pieces in shell, sealing and injecting liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The carbon dioxide is used in combined activation at a flow rate of 60 mL/minute.

PN CN107624294-A
TI Breeding of peony involves immersing peony seeds in nutrient solution, soaking seeds in germination liquid, immersing seeds in organic selenium solution, sowing germinated seeds to nursery box and transplanting seedling in soil.
AU CHEN H
AE DANGTU HAINA AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DANG-Non-standard)
GA 201810269W
AB    NOVELTY - Peony seeds are sterilized, immersed in nutrient solution, reduced and stored. The seeds are soaked in germination liquid, immersed in colchicine solution, gamma -ray irradiated and ultraviolet irradiated, to obtain mutagenized seeds. The mutagenized seeds are immersed in organic selenium solution, immersed in nutrient solution and germinated, to obtain germinate seeds. The germinated seeds are sowed to greenhouse nursery box, sprayed to nutrient solution, and seedling is grown in seedling box and transplanted in greenhouse soil.
   USE - Breeding of peony.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables breeding of peony with mortality rate reduced by 20.46%, mutation rate increased by 11.49%, number of varieties increased by 5 and flowering time of peony new varieties extended for 11 days.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Peony seeds are sterilized, immersed in nutrient solution for low-temperature storage, stored at 8-10 degrees C for 3-4 hours, reduced to 2-4 degrees C for 12-14 days and stored. The seeds are soaked in germination liquid for 2-3 hours, immersed in a colchicine solution in vacuum tank for mutation breeding with vacuum degree of 100%, 80-100 gamma -ray irradiated for 8-12 minutes, 370-410 lux ultraviolet irradiated for 20-25 minutes, and further gamma -ray irradiated and ultraviolet irradiated for 3-4 times, to obtain mutagenized seeds. The mutagenized seeds are immersed in organic selenium solution, soaked at 12-15 degrees C for 2-3 hours, immersed in nutrient solution, sprouted at 24-26 degrees C, processed with light intensity of 240-270 lux and ultraviolet light of 310-340 lux for 1/20 minute and germinated for 12-14 hours, to obtain germinate seeds. The germinated seeds are sowed to greenhouse nursery box, sprayed to nutrient solution for every 2 days, transplanted within 10 days and processed with carbon dioxide having volume concentration of 0.08-0.1%. After 10 days of transplanting, the volume concentration of carbon dioxide is 0.04-0.06%, and seedling is grown in seedling box and transplanted in greenhouse soil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The bactericidal solution comprises sodium hypochlorite solution with concentration of 50 ppm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The nutrient solution comprises 14-17 pts. wt. ivy, 11-15 pts. wt. iron compound, 9-12 pts. wt. Juniperus chinensis leaves, 7-10 pts. wt. Coptis chinensis, 6-9 pts. wt. pumpkin, 5-7 pts. wt. sweet potato leaves and 4-7 pts. wt. corn flour. The nutrient solution is prepared using selenium-enriched yeast, Lactobacillus and Aspergillus oryzae in weight ratio of 1:2-3:0.5-1. The germination liquid comprises naphthaleneacetic acid and gibberellins in mass ratio of 1:2-3, and has concentration of 1-2 %mass. The colchicine solution has concentration of 0.6-0.8 %mass. The organic selenium solution has concentration of 0.7-0.9 %mass.

IP A01C-001/00; A01C-001/08

AU LU Y
   CHAI R
   ZHAO G
   LIU Y
   HE M
AE UNIV EAST CHINA NORMAL (UYEN-C)
GA 201803587W
AB    NOVELTY - Self-supporting nanosheet nickel-based monolithic catalyst is new.
   USE - Self-supporting nanosheet nickel-based monolithic catalyst used in conversion of methane to syngas, syngas to methanogenic natural gas, ethane oxidative dehydrogenation to ethylene, methane carbon dioxide reforming, methane steam reforming or methane autothermal reforming (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The self-supporting nanosheet nickel-based monolithic catalyst has high activity, selectivity, good stability, good thermal conductivity, and high-throughput at low pressure with significant industrial application value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Self-supporting nanosheet nickel-based monolithic catalyst of general formula aNiO-bMO/Al2O3-ZT, is new.
   NiO=represents metal nickel oxide;
   MO=stands for other metal oxides selected from alkali earth metal, lanthanide rare earth metals, manganese, iron, cobalt, copper, zinc, aluminum, gallium, indium, tin, yttrium, titanium, zirconium or niobium oxides;
   Al2O3-ZT=anchored aluminum oxide nano-sheet equipped structure carrier;
   ZT=made of nickel, copper, aluminum, iron, copper, brass, titanium, stainless steel, iron-chromium-aluminum or silicon carbide;
   a=0.1-20%; and
   b=1-25%.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing self-supporting nanosheet nickel-based monolithic catalyst, which involves:
   (A) growing aluminum hydroxide oxide or aluminum hydroxide or nanosheets on monolith structure support with water vapor or under hydrothermal conditions by reaction of monolithic support vector to obtain integral structure carrier;
   (B) dissolving divalent metal nickel, divalent metal, other divalent metal and trivalent metal or tetravalent metal in water with stirring under sonication to prepare mixed aqueous solution;
   (C) placing the resulting mixed solution into autoclave at 200-250 degrees C for 3-24 hours;
   (D) cooling and drying the resulting mixture to obtain the self-supporting nickel-containing hydrotalcite nanosheet monolith structure catalyst precursor; and
   (E) calcining the obtained self-supporting nickel-containing hydrotalcite nanosheets at 300-900 degrees C for 0.5-12 hours to obtain the self-supporting nickel nanoparticle titanate-structured catalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic X-ray diffraction pattern of the prepared aluminum hydroxide oxide-aluminum-fiber and aluminum oxide-aluminum-fiber. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The morphological structure of the ZT support is made of fiber, filament, screen, tube, sheet, foil, foam and other. The fiber has a diameter of 1-150 mu m or a monolithic metal fiber having a three-dimensional porous structure obtained by sintering the fiber. The wire has a diameter of 0.15-5.00 millimeters. The screen is made of wire having a diameter of 0.15-5.00 mm. The tube has an outer diameter of 2-50 mm and a wall thickness of 0.5-5 mm. The sheet has a thickness of 0.1-2 mm. The foil has a thickness of 0.5-100 microns. The metal compound is nitrate, sulfate, chloride, oxalate, acetate or acetylacetone salt.

   CHEN C
   BI W
   LI N
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 2022B02912
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing glutaric acid based on microbial                physiological parameters for feedback regulation of                nitrogen source stream addition comprises                inoculating strain into fermenter containing                fermentation medium, feeding nitrogen source and                controlling based on real-time microbial                physiological parameters, and glutaric acid is                obtained by fermentation, where the microbial                physiological parameters include the oxygen                consumption rate OUR and the carbon dioxide                production rate CER.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing glutaric                acid based on microbial physiological parameters                for feedback regulation of nitrogen source stream                addition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high acid production, high                conversion rate, easy scale amplification, and lays                a foundation for the industrialization of                biological glutaric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nitrogen source is                  aqueous ammonia, liquid ammonia, ammonium sulfate                  solution, ammonium chloride solution, urea                  solution, ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium                  bicarbonate solution. The fermentation process                  also includes feeding glucose solution so that                  the concentration of glucose in the fermentation                  broth is 0.5-1 wt.%/vol.%. The fermentation                  medium includes 3-8 g/l potassium dihydrogen                  phosphate, 1-3 g/l magnesium sulfate, 0.01-0.03                  g/l ferrous sulfate, 0.01-0.04 g/l manganese                  sulfate, 5-15 g/l ammonium sulfate, 20-40 g/l                  glucose, 5-30 g/l corn steep liquor, 0.5-3 g/l                  betaine, 0.002-0.006 g/l vitamin B1, and                  antibiotics, where the antibiotic is 30-200                  &#956;g/ml ampicillin, 30-100 &#956;g/ml                  chloramphenicol, and/or 30-200 &#956;g/ml                  kanamycin. The nitrogen source flow rate                  adjustment is to control the nitrogen source flow                  rate, and the nitrogen source flow acceleration                  is adjusted based on real-time OUR, CER automatic                  feedback adjustment, or according to real-time                  OUR, CER manual feedback adjustment. When the OUR                  and CER in the fermentation process were lower                  than 140-180 mmol/l/hour, the nitrogen source                  flow acceleration was increased to keep OUR and                  CER at 140-180 mmol/l/hour. The glucose solution                  is fed in, where the concentration of the glucose                  solution is 50-70% (wt.%/vol.%). The fermentation                  process parameters include: the inoculum amount                  is 10-20%, the fermentation temperature is                  35-37&#176;C, the pH value of the                  fermentation broth is controlled to be 6.7-7.2,                  the stirring speed was related to dissolved                  oxygen DO, so that the DO was maintained at                  20-40%, the tank pressure was maintained at                  0.05-0.1 MPa, and the fermentation period was                  50-70 hours.

PN KR2022065221-A; KR2429275-B1
TI Producing acid gas adsorbent using marine waste,            used for removing air pollutants comprises e.g.            pulverizing shellfish, removing organic matter            contained in pulverized shell, removing salt of            pulverized shell, and separating waste liquid.
AU YOUNG P
   YUN Y I
   HEE Y S
   NAMSUNGCHAN
   KIM S H
   KIM J
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2022727033
AB    NOVELTY - Producing acid gas adsorbent using marine                waste, comprises (a) pulverizing shellfish; (b)                removing the organic matter contained in the                pulverized shell; (c) removing the salt of the                pulverized shell from which organic matter is                removed; (d) separating the waste liquid containing                the pulverized shell and salt; and (e) activating                the separated crushed shell as an adsorbent.
   USE - The method is useful to produce acid gas                adsorbent for removing air pollutants including                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly and                economical, reduces energy consumption                significantly compared to removal method by firing,                and does not cause environmental problems by using                agglomerates as emulsifiers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises                  pulverizing shellfish into a size of less than or                  equal to 1 mm. The step (b) comprises removing                  the organic matter contained in the pulverized                  shell through heating at 250-350 degrees C for                  2-5 hours. The step (c) putting the pulverized                  shells into a reactor containing water and                  performing aeration, to blow compressed air into                  the reactor for 10-30 minutes by performing                  mechanical stirring at greater than or equal to                  300 revolutions/minute. The step (e) comprises                  activating the separated crushed shell as an                  adsorbent at greater than or equal to 200 degrees                  C for 2-5 hours. The method further comprises                  agglomerating the suspended matter of the                  separated waste liquid using 1-3 ppm anionic                  flocculant. The supernatant of the aggregates is                  separated from the aggregates by the decantation                  method. Preferred Components: The pulverized                  shellfish are oyster shells, conch shells, crab                  shells, and/or scallop shells. The anion                  coagulant is a polyacrylamide-based polymer                  coagulant.
   EXAMPLE - The example provides details of production of                marine waste that do not support claims.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                manufacturing complex resin comprising preparing                complex resin composition, separating the metallic                impurity particles in the composition from the                impurity remover by extruding the complex resin                composition for the power cable, and removing the                metallic impurity particles with cooling                water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The base resin                  comprises 25-35 wt.% polypropylene, 45-55 wt.%                  ethylene-propylene copolymer, and 15-25 wt.%                  alpha olefin block copolymer. The metallic impurity particles are potassium,                  calcium, sodium, magnesium, aluminum, zinc, iron,                  nickel, copper and silicon. Preferred Method: In                  the extrusion, the complex resin composition for                  power cables is extruded at a temperature of                  185-230 degrees C, and the chelating agent is                  homogeneously melted in the complex resin                  composition for power cables to combine with                  metallic impurity particles to form a complex                  compound. By maintaining the cooling water at a                  temperature of 15-55 degrees C to dissolve the                  complex compound, the metallic impurity particles                  are removed.

The reaction vessel was taken out from the apparatus, and 1.19 parts mass guanidine carbonate was promptly added to the reaction vessel to obtain mixture, and at the same time, the fatty acid salt synthesis step was started. PN KR2021144437-A; KR2348848-B1
TI Rubber composition e.g. for tire comprises raw rubber containing at least one of styrene-butadiene rubber, natural rubber and butadiene rubber, and reinforcing filler containing silica extracted from rice bran.
AU LYOUNG L G
AE HANKOOK TIRE & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HKOK-C)
GA 2021E1508U
AB    NOVELTY - Rubber composition comprises 100 pts. wt. raw rubber containing at least one of styrene-butadiene rubber, natural rubber and butadiene rubber, and 60-150 pts. wt. reinforcing filler containing silica extracted from rice bran.
   USE - The rubber composition is useful for tire (claimed), and as a rubber reinforcing agent in the tire industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The rubber composition has improved physical performance e.g. tensile strength, elongation and modulus of elasticity; reduces carbon dioxide generated in the silica extraction process and the amount of energy used; and utilized in tire has improved environmental sustainability and reduced costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for tire comprising a tread or a sidewall manufactured using rubber composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silica extracted from the rice bran has a nitrogen surface area per gram (N2SA) of 160-250 m2/g, and cetyl trimethyl ammonium bromide (CTAB) adsorption specific surface area of 150-250 m2/g. The reinforcing filler is 55-130 pts. wt. silica extracted from the rice bran and 5-20 pts. wt. carbon black.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The raw rubber comprises 0-100 pts. wt. styrene-butadiene rubber, 0-100 pts. wt. natural rubber and 0-50 pts. wt. butadiene rubber. The styrene-butadiene rubber has a styrene content of 15-50 wt.%, a vinyl content of 10-40 wt.%, and a glass transition temperature (Tg) of -50 degrees C to -20 degrees C and treated distillate aromatic extract (TDAE) oil or solvent residual aromatic extract (SRAE) oil is contained in an amount of 20-40 parts per hundred rubber (PHR). The styrene-butadiene rubber is graft copolymerized with (meth)acrylate containing an alkoxysilyl group and a glycidyl group. The butadiene rubber has a cis-1,4-butadiene content of 95-100 wt.%, and a glass transition temperature (Tg) of -100 degrees C to -120 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - Rubber composition was prepared with 90 pts. wt. of Solution-polymerized styrene-butadiene rubber (S-SBR) F3440 (styrene content is 35.5wt.%, vinyl content is 27.1 wt.%, glass transition temperature (Tg) is -32 degrees C, and contains SRAE oil at 27.5 PHR, and alkoxysilyl (meth)acrylate containing a group and a glycidyl group is graft polymerized), SBR-2: S-SBR SOL5220 (styrene content is 26 wt.%, vinyl content is 19 wt.%, glass transition temperature (Tg) is -53 degrees C, does not contain SRAE oil), 10 pts. wt. RM-Nd- butadiene rubber (BR) (cis-1,4-butadiene content is 96 wt.% or more, glass transition temperature (Tg) is -108 degrees C), silica (nitrogen adsorption specific surface area 150-200 m2/g), 95 pts. wt. rice husk silica-1 (nitrogen adsorption specific surface area 150-200 m2/g) and rice husk silica-2 (nitrogen adsorption specific surface area 200-230 m2/g). 95 pts. wt. silica was added based on 100 pts. wt. raw rubber to 40 pts. wt. process oil as compounding agent, 2 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 1 pts. wt. stearic acid, 0.75 pts. wt. antioxidant, 1 pts. wt. wax, 0.7 pts. wt. vulcanization accelerator, 1 pts. wt. sulfur and 1 pts. wt. accelerator was mixed.

PN CN113663728-A
TI Preparing covalent triazine-based nanosheet with            broad visible light response used in photocatalysis,            involves preparing layered bulk covalent triazine-based            material and preparing oxidized covalent triazine-based            nanosheet.
AU WANG C
   XU Y
AE UNIV WESTLAKE (UYWE-Non-standard)
GA 2021D72531
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing covalent triazine-based nanosheet                with broad visible light response involves                preparing layered bulk covalent triazine-based                material, preparing oxidized covalent                triazine-based nanosheet, preparing reduced                covalent triazine-based nanosheet, and subjecting                the obtained reduced covalent triazine-based                nanosheet to freezing drying or supercritical                carbon dioxide drying to obtain a photocatalyst                with a wide visible light response range.
   USE - Method for preparing covalent triazine-based                nanosheet with broad visible light response used in                photocatalysis (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared covalent triazine-based nanosheet                has eco-friendly, environmental protection,                structural designability, and the photocatalysis                has a relatively high visible light response range,                which greatly improves the efficiency of                photocatalysis.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a X-ray diffraction                analysis result of the prepared covalent                triazine-based nanosheets with broad visible light                response.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidizing acid is                  selected from concentrated or dilute nitric acid,                  concentrated sulfuric acid, permanganic acid,                  hypochlorous acid, chloric acid, chlorous acid,                  perchloric acid, and nitrous acid. Preferred                  Process: The preparation of layered bulk covalent                  triazine-based material involves adding 0.1-0.8 g                  terephthalonitrile and 100-800 micromL                  trifluoromethanesulfonic acid into the glass tube                  for reaction, sealing the glass tube with a                  hydrogen-oxygen flame; putting the sealed glass                  tube in a 120-200degrees Celsius blast drying box                  for 24-40 hours, dropping naturally the                  temperature to room temperature, reacting he                  glass tube, putting into liquid nitrogen to                  cooling for 3-5 minutes after cooling, using a                  glass knife to opening the glass tube, taking out                  the reactant, washing the reactant multiple times                  with deionized water and ethanol and drying in                  vacuum to obtain a bulk covalent triazine-based                  material CTF. The preparation of oxidized                  covalent triazine-based nanosheet involves taking                  300-800 mg the prepared massive covalent                  triazine-based material, adding to 10-20 ml                  oxidizing acid, stirring, stirring for 30-100                  minutes, adding 20-50 ml deionized water,                  stirring continuously for 3-8 hours, filtering by                  suction, washing with deionized water to                  neutrality, freezing, and drying to obtain                  oxidized triazine nanosheet o-CTF NSs. The                  preparation of reduced covalent triazine-based                  nanosheet involves taking 50-100 mg the prepared                  oxidized triazine nanosheet, adding 10-20 ml                  deionized water, dispersing ultrasonically,                  adding slowly a small amount of reducing agent,                  stirring continuously for 3-8 hours, filtering by                  suction, washing with deionized water to                  neutrality, freezing and drying to obtain reduced                  triazine nanosheet r-CTFNSs.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing agent is                  selected from ascorbic acid, sodium ascorbate,                  sodium sulfite, and hydrazine hydrate.

PN IN202121008652-A
TI Method of producing carbonated cold press fruit juice, involves sanitizing the fruits, and pasteurizing cold press juice or normal fresh juice carbonated at high pressure, and doing carbonation of juice in carbonator machine.
AU SAXENA V S
AE SAXENA V S (SAXE-Individual)
GA 202139780L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves sanitizing the fruits. The juice is obtained from the sanitized fruit. The cold press juice or normal fresh juice carbonated is pasteurized at high pressure. The carbon dioxide is added to the juice in a soda carbonation machine. The carbonated juice is transferred to a packaging container and sealed. The fruits are sanitized by washing with sanitizing solutions containing chlorine, ozone, hydrogen peroxide, bromine, acids, or ultra-violet (UV) treatment. The carbonation of juice is done in carbonator machine.
   USE - Method of producing carbonated cold press fruit juice.

PT P
PN WO2020084592-A1; CN112771019-A
TI Aromatic acid preparation involves contacting alkyl aromatic compound, oxidant, and catalyst composition in solvent under conditions effective to oxidize alkyl aromatic compound and provide reaction product comprising aromatic acid.
AU HASHMI S A
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 202035932U
AB    NOVELTY - Aromatic acid preparation involves contacting an alkyl aromatic compound, an oxidant, and a catalyst composition in a solvent under conditions effective to oxidize alkyl aromatic compound and provide a reaction product comprising aromatic acid. The catalyst composition comprises 400-1000 parts per million cobalt, 200-500 parts per million manganese, 300-800 parts per million bromine, and 20-50 parts per million copper. The solvent comprises a carboxylic acid and water.
   USE - Method for preparing aromatic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the aromatic acid in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with improved selectivity and conversion, and reduced byproducts, carbon dioxide production and reduced 4-carboxybenzaldehyde production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cobalt compound comprises cobalt bromide, cobalt acetate, cobalt carbonate, cobalt oxide or combination. The manganese compound comprises manganese bromide, manganese acetate, manganese carbonate, and manganese oxide. The bromine compound comprises bromine, hydrobromic acid, a metal bromide, an organic bromide, and ammonium bromide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bromine compound comprises hydrobromic acid, benzyl bromide, bromobenzene, bromoacetic acid, dibromoacetic acid, tetrabromomethane, bromoacetyl bromide, or a combination. The bromine compound is an ionic liquid, where the ionic liquid comprises 1-benzyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide, benzyltributylphosphonium bromide, benzyltributylammonium bromide, 1 -phenyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide, phenyltributylphosphonium bromide, phenyltributylammonium bromide, 1,3-dibenzylimidazolium bromide, 1-butyl-3- methylimidazolium bromide, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide, tetrabutylphosphonium bromide, trihexyltetradecylphosphonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium bromide, (2-hydroxyethyl)trimethylammonium bromide, and 1-butyl-3 - methylimidazolium bromotrichloroaluminate. The bromine compound is metal bromide, where the metal bromide comprises cobalt bromide, manganese bromide, and copper bromide. The alkyl aromatic compound is di(1-6C alkyl) aromatic compound. preferably xylene, 2,6- dimethylnaphthalene, 2,7-dimethylnaphthalene, and 2,6-diisopropylnaphthalene. The aromatic acid is an aromatic diacid, preferably a phenyl dicarboxylic acid, isophthalic acid or terephthalic acid. The oxidant comprises hydrogen peroxide, air, dioxygen, ozone, an anthraquinone, 2-32C alkyl peroxide, 2-32C alkyl hydroperoxide, 2-32C ketone peroxide, 2-32C diacyl peroxide, 3-22C diperoxy ketal, 2-32C peroxyester, 2-32C peroxydicarbonate, 2-32C peroxy acid, 6-32C perbenzoic acid, a periodinane, a periodate or combination. The carboxylic acid is acetic acid, propionic acid, n-butyric acid, isobutyric acid, n-valeric acid, trimethylacetic acid, caproic acid, benzoic acid or combination.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Porous lithium metal anode for lithium metal            battery, comprises porous metal collector, composite            metal lithium layer provided on collector and lithium            layer covering metal lithium layer.
AU LAI Y
   HONG B
   FAN H
   DUAN B
   ZHANG Z
   ZHANG K
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2020208014
AB    NOVELTY - A porous lithium metal anode comprises a                porous metal collector, a composite metal lithium                layer provided on the collector, and a lithium                layer covering the metal lithium layer.
   USE - Porous lithium metal anode used for lithium                metal battery e.g. lithium sulfur battery, lithium                iodine battery, lithium selenium battery, lithium                tellurium battery, lithium oxygen battery or                lithium carbon dioxide battery (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The porous lithium metal anode has suppressed                in situ formation of lithium metal during contact                with electrolyte, effectively suppresses the                generation of interface reaction, maintains high                specific surface area, has excellent stability of                lithium ion transmission, and provides batteries                with improved cycle life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the lithium metal anode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium collector                  comprises at least one of titanium, chromium,                  manganese, iron, cobalt, nickel and copper and                  has porosity of 20-99%, preferably 30-90%.

AU LIANG J

   USE - Ecological slope protection brick.

PT P
PN CN110695057-A; CN110695057-B
TI Cracking sludge comprises heating the sludge in a feed pre-treatment device, evaporating, cracking, melting the heavy oil residue in a high-temperature plasma melting device, and transforming into vitreous waste residue and synthesis gas.
AU SHU X
AE ANHUI SPACE ENVIRONMENTAL ENG CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
   AEROSPACE SHENHE BEIJING ENVIRONMENTAL (AERO-Non-standard)
GA 2020084917
AB    NOVELTY - Cracking sludge comprises (i) adding the sludge into a feed pre-treatment device for heating the sludge while conveying the materials, so that the moisture and light components are evaporated and cracked by heating to obtain heavy oil residue, (ii) discharging the heavy oil residue from the feed pretreatment device discharge port into a high-temperature plasma melting device, and (iii) melting the input heavy oil residue with a high-temperature plasma torch in the high-temperature plasma melting device, and transforming into vitreous waste residue and synthesis gas containing carbon dioxide, cabon monoxide and hydrogen.
   USE - The method is useful for cracking sludge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high degree of harmless treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the feed pretreatment device heats the oil sludge while conveying the material, so that the moisture and light components therein are evaporated and cracked by heat. The method for obtaining heavy oil residue includes: (ia) starting the electric heating device of the second heating device installed outside the screw propeller tube wall of the feed pretreatment device to heat the screw propeller, (ib) obtaining the ambient temperature value measured by a sixth temperature sensor installed on the inner wall of the screw propeller installed between the first heating device and the second heating device, (ic) determining whether the ambient temperature value is more than or equal to a preset preheating temperature value, (id) starting the screw propeller to send material to the high-temperature plasma melting device, when the ambient temperature value is greater than or equal to the preset preheating temperature value, (ie) passing the synthesized gas into the heating gas pipe wound around the wall of the screw propeller, and turning off the second heating device, (if) obtaining the material temperature value measured by the sixth temperature sensor, (ig) determining whether the material temperature value is more than or equal to the preset heating temperature value, and (ih) starting the electric heating device again to heat the material again, when the temperature of the material is lower than the preset heating temperature value, and adjusting the output power of the electric heating device according to the temperature of the material.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

TI Red mud cracking processing method by feeding red mud into feeding device, dividing into more than two parts, respectively inputting input two columns, heating to evaporate and crack, and sending into high temperature plasma fusion device.

AE JEONNAM BIOINDUSTRY FOUND (JEON-Non-standard)
GA 2020048074
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ginseng leaf extract comprises (a) washing ginseng leaves in running water, removing water, and slicing ginseng leaves, (b) freeze drying pretreated ginseng leaves, (c) preparing ginseng leaf extract by supercritical extraction of freeze-dried ginseng leaf with supercritical carbon dioxide as solvent, (d) adding 50 vol./vol.% ethanol aqueous solution to the ginseng leaf supercritical extract to obtain ginseng leaf solvent extract.
   USE - The ginseng leaf extract is useful in health functional food and cosmetic composition for preventing inflammation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces residual pesticides of ginseng leaves, and provides ginseng leaf extract having immune improving effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for ginseng leaf extract prepared by the above method
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The freeze drying is carried out by sequentially drying the pretreated ginseng leaves at -20 degrees C for 240 minutes, at -10 degrees C for 240 minutes, at 0 degrees C for 240 minutes, at 10 degrees C for 360 minutes, at 20 degrees C for 360 minutes, and at 30 degrees C for 1500 minutes. The method comprises (i) performing ultra-high pressure treatment of the freeze-dried ginseng leaves at temperature of 50 degrees C and pressure of 400 bar, (ii) injecting supercritical carbon dioxide at rate of 60 ml/minute under temperature condition of 40 degrees C and pressure of 40 bar, and performing supercritical extraction for 180 minutes, and (iii) separating carbon dioxide into gas phase from ginseng leaf supercritical extract and supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method comprises (i) dissolving different metal salts in a solvent in a certain molar ratio, (ii) adding certain amount of bacterial cellulose pore-forming agent, heating, stirring at a certain temperature, and obtaining a viscous colloid, and (iii) aging at room temperature for a certain time, forming a gel, drying the colloid in an oven, calcining in a muffle furnace at a certain temperature for a certain period of time, and obtaining the catalyst. Preferred Components: The metal solution is an acetate of a transition metal or main group metal, dissolved in a solvent.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The biodegradable catalyst comprises 0.1-2 pts. wt. acetyl carbamate metal salt, 1-5 pts. wt. unsaturated fatty acid, 1-5 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate and 1-5 pts. wt. stearic acid. Preferred Method: The step (c) is carried out at 500 degrees C under screw rotation speed of 5. Preferred Components: The binder resin includes linear low density polyethylene, low density polyethylene and/or polypropylene. The plastic polymer includes polypropylene or polyethylene terephthalate glycol. The styrene-butadiene rubber: contains 60-80 wt.% styrene; and is impregnated with 1-5 wt.% natural oil.
   EXAMPLE - Typical environmentally friendly biofilament comprised of 99 wt.% styrene-butadiene rubber and 1 wt.% biodegradable catalyst obtained by mixing using 30 pts. wt. polylactic acid starch, 0.1 pts. wt. ferric acetyl carbamate, 0.3 pts. wt. aluminum acetyl carbamate, 40 pts. wt. low-density polyethylene, 5 pts. wt. linoleic acid, 20 pts. wt. calcium carbonate, 2 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate and 2.6 pts. wt. stearic acid.

PN CN109718643-A
TI Method for treating refinery dry gas, involves subjecting refinery dry gas to amine and alkali washing, drying, purifying and separating amine and alkali-washed gas followed by passing via absorption tower, desorption tower and crude column.
AU LIU Y
AE LIU Y (LIUY-Individual)
GA 201942926T
AB    NOVELTY - A refinery dry gas treating method involves subjecting the refinery dry gas to amine washing and alkali washing for dividing into hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide. The amine and alkali-washed gas is dried and purified to separate water and arsenic. The obtained purified gas is passed to an absorption tower for treatment and passed to the desorption tower to separate carbon ions and the gas separated by the desorption tower is sent to the crude column to separate ethylene and ethane.
   USE - Method for treating refinery dry gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables avoiding direct combustion of refinery dry gas, thus reducing waste of resources.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The liquid separated by the absorption tower is sent to an expander for treatment and to a compressor for treatment to obtain methane and hydrogen.

PN CN109306073-A
TI Preparing hydrophobic three-dimensional porous material useful for improving red blood cell deformability comprises e.g. preparing porous structure sugar template using water-soluble sugar and injecting polydimethylsiloxane.
AU HAN Y
   LIU J
   HUA W
AE HAN Y (HANY-Individual)
   LIU J (LIUJ-Individual)
   HUA W (HUAW-Individual)
GA 201916074T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrophobic three-dimensional porous material comprises (i) preparing porous structure sugar template using water-soluble sugar, (ii) injecting polydimethylsiloxane into porous structure sugar template and curing and (iii) removing porous structural sugar template in solid material.
   USE - The hydrophobic three-dimensional porous material is useful for improving red blood cell deformability.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, is economical, does not require organic solvent residue. The material has irregular pore structure used in blood storage bags to significantly increase the oxygen partial pressure of the blood sample, and reduces carbon dioxide partial pressure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for hydrophobic three-dimensional porous material prepared according to the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The solubility of water-soluble sugar in water at 25 degrees C is 1.9-2.1 g sugar/g water, the water-soluble sugar is sucrose, coarse granulated sugar and cotton white sugar, where the particle diameter of coarse granulated sugar is 300-1250 mu m, the particle diameter of cotton white sugar is 100-500 mu m; the content of coarse granulated sugar is 20-80 wt.% based on total amount of water-soluble sugar and 20-80 wt.% cotton white sugar. In the step (i) mixing water-soluble sugar with water, heating and shaping to obtain porous structure sugar template, where the amount of water is 0.5-5 ml relative to 100 g of water-soluble sugar, heating at 70-120 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours. In the step (ii) mixing polydimethylsiloxane with a curing agent, first vacuuming first vacuuming to obtain mixture, contacting mixture with porous structural sugar template, and curing at 30-45 degrees C for 2-5 hours; the mass ratio of polydimethylsiloxane to curing agent is 8-12:1; the contacting is carried out under second vacuum condition at 10-20 degrees C for 7-17 hours. In the step (iii) dissolving porous structural sugar template in solid material and removing using deionized water at 35-50 degrees C. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of polydimethylsiloxane to water-soluble sugar is 1-3:1, preferably 1.5-2.5:1.
