PT P
PN WO2018189598-A1; CN110494429-A; US2020024227-A1; EP3609879-A1
TI Producing glycerol carbonate and/or urea by reacting portion of urea with glycerol, decomposing glycerol carbamate, transferring unreacted urea to urea product stream, and flowing glycerol carbonate in glycerol carbonate product stream.
AU ANSARI M B
   BODAS V D
   LEAL G
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201881183J
AB    NOVELTY - Glycerol carbonate and/or urea is produced by (i) employing carbon dioxide and ammonia in synthesizing urea; (ii) reacting a portion of urea with glycerol to form glycerol carbamate and ammonia; (iii) decomposing glycerol carbamate to form glycerol carbonate and ammonia; (iv) feeding a portion of ammonia from step (ii) and/or step (iii) to step (i) for synthesizing; (v) transferring any unreacted urea from step (ii) to a urea product stream; and (vi) flowing glycerol carbonate from step (iii) in a glycerol carbonate product stream.
   USE - Production of glycerol carbonate and/or urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can reduce the cost for urea production and increase product value of excessive glycerol produced from other chemical plants.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Catalyst comprises a metal as a catalytically active species. Metal is selected from zinc(II) (Zn++), magnesium(II) (Mg++), manganese(II) (Mn++), iron(II) (Fe++), nickel(II) (Ni++), cadmium(II) (Cd++), calcium(II) (Ca++), and/or lithium(I) (Li+) cations. Catalyst comprises metal oxide as a catalytically active species. Metal oxide is selected from zinc oxide (ZnO), cobalt(II,III) oxide (Co3O4), calcium oxide (CaO), lanthanum(III) oxide (La2O3), magnesium oxide (MgO), and/or zirconium(IV) oxide (ZrO2). Metal oxide is supported by a matrix selected from silica, alumina, hydrotalcite, and/or polymers. Preferred Process: Catalyst is calcined before step (ii).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Step (i) comprises (a) reacting ammonia with carbon dioxide to form ammonium carbamate, and (b) decomposing ammonium carbamate to form water and urea. Step (i) further comprises (c) heating urea, unreacted ammonia, and ammonium carbamate from steps (a) and (b) to further decompose ammonium carbamate and form urea and water; (d) concentrating urea from water and urea from steps (b) and (c); and (e) granulating concentrated urea from step (d) to form granulated urea. Steps (ii) and (iii) comprise contacting urea with glycerol over a catalyst under reaction conditions sufficient to form the glycerol carbonate. Urea and glycerol in step (ii) reacts in a solvent. The method further includes (vii) collecting any unreacted urea from step (ii); (viii) concentrating collected urea from step (vii); and (ix) granulating concentrated urea from step (viii). Preferred Compositions: Urea and glycerol in step (ii) is provided at a molar ratio of 0.33:1-1.2:1. Catalytically active species is present in the form of sulfate, phosphate, stearates, carboxylates, and/or derivative of natural fatty acids. Catalyst includes metal-alkoxide-based catalytic system comprising titanium alkoxides, aluminum alkoxides, and/or zirconium alkoxides. Catalyst includes alkyl-tin-based catalytic system comprising dibutyltin oxide, dibutyltin dimethoxide, and/or triphenyltin chloride. Solvent is selected from dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, dichloromethane, nitro-benzene, dimethylacetamide, and/or methanol. Preferred Conditions: Reaction conditions comprise reaction temperature of 90-220 degrees C; reaction pressure of 2x 10-5-2x 10-1 MPa; and batch time of 1-48 hours.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN108585057-A; CN108585057-B
TI Separation of arsenic and alkali in arsenic-alkali residue involves oxidizing acidic ferric ion solution and solution-containing ferrous salt and arsenite, washing arsenic-alkali residue, adding sodium arsenite and decomposing.
AU HU Y
   SUN W
   HAN H
   XU Z
   LIU S
   ZHANG Y
   WANG L
   YANG Y
   ZHANG C
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2018795804
AB    NOVELTY - The separation method of arsenic and alkali in arsenic-alkali residue involves weighing acidic ferric ion solution and solution-containing ferrous salt and arsenite and then carrying out oxidation reaction, performing neutralization reaction, washing arsenic-alkali residue using water, adding sodium carbonate, sodium arsenite and sodium citrate and then oxidizing, carrying out solid-liquid separation, adding carbon dioxide and then reacting, separating sodium bicarbonate crystals and thermally decomposing.
   USE - The method is useful for separation of arsenic and alkali in arsenic-alkali residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple separation of arsenic and alkali in arsenic-alkali residue with high yield and efficiency and excellent stability, solubility and crystallinity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves using alkaline neutralization agent including sodium carbonate and sodium hydroxide and oxidizing agent including hydrogen peroxide, oxygen, ozone, sodium hypochlorite and sodium perchlorate.
DC E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN US2018220679-A1
TI Manufacturing aerated pet chew composition involves preparing composition containing protein having amount of sodium caseinate and/or whey, and water, and injection molding and/or extruding composition to produce aerated pet chew.
AU REISER R B
   CEPEDA C A
   ELLIOTT M
AE MARS INC (MRSC-C)
GA 2018659334
AB    NOVELTY - An aerated pet chew composition is manufactured by preparing composition containing protein having amount of sodium caseinate and/or whey, and 5 wt.% water; and injection molding and/or extruding composition to produce aerated pet chew having greater than or equal to 2x 104 cells per cubic centimeter (cc) where cells are created by release of amount of supercritical fluid sufficient to occupy 5-55% of overall volume of aerated pet chew when supercritical fluid is transformed to gas, and supercritical fluid is nitrogen or carbon dioxide.
   USE - Manufacture of aerated pet chew composition.
   ADVANTAGE - Chew or treat facilitates weight loss or weight control properties.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the pet chew.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Supercritical fluid contacts the composition during injection molding process. Composition is extruded prior to injection molding process. Supercritical fluid is added to composition during extrusion process. Composition is extruded to produce final pet chew product. Supercritical fluid contacts the composition during extrusion process. Bubbles produced by supercritical fluid provide the pet chew with rougher texture than pet chew with same composition that is not processed using supercritical fluid. Bubbles formed by supercritical fluid provide an increased surface area when fractured during chewing process of animal. Preferred Conditions: Friction between portions of mouth of animal and pet chew are increased on downward bite and upward pull in comparison to pet chew with the same composition that is not processed using supercritical fluid. Aerated pet chew has monotexture.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PT P
PN CN108249466-A; CN108249466-B
TI Preparing calcium oxide-calcium carbonate core-shell nano-particle comprises e.g. dispersing calcium oxide particles in deionized water, adding composite surfactant, stirring and then treating solution under microwave.
AU TANG J
AE NINGBO PLASTIC METAL PROD CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201854547L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium oxide-calcium carbonate core-shell nano-particle comprises (i) dispersing calcium oxide particles having a particle size of 2-3 mu m in deionized water, adding composite surfactant and stirring, (ii) treating solution obtained in step (i) under microwave for 25-35 minutes, (iii) adding liquid obtained in step (ii) into the liquid inlet end of the atomizing nozzle, passing carbon dioxide gas into the intake end of the atomizing nozzle under ultrasound conditions and spraying liquid, and (iv) collecting liquid after spraying, washing with deionized water for 2-3 times and then drying to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium oxide-calcium carbonate core-shell nano-particle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composite surfactant is dodecyldimethylbenzylammonium chloride or lauric acid imidazoline surfactant. The power of microwave is 500-600 W. The molar ratio between calcium oxide, dodecyl dimethyl benzyl ammonium chloride and lauric acid imidazoline surfactant is 1:0.5-0.8:0.2-0.4. In step (i), mass ratio between calcium oxide and deionized water is 1:100-200. In step (iii), ratio between material liquid and carbon dioxide is 1 g:200-400 ml. In step (iv), controlling the number of revolutions is 4000-6000 revolutions per minute. In step (iii), ultrasonic power is 150-200 W.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E07-D09B; E10-A22A; E10-C04L2; E31-U01; E34-D01; E34-D03A

TI Steelwork product used as partial or total replacement of clinker during manufacture of cement used as material in road paving and rail ballast, is obtained by mixing steelwork slag with siliceous additives.
AU BARBOSA M R

AE GERDAU SA (GERD-Non-standard)
   GERDAU SA (GERD-Non-standard)

AB    NOVELTY - A steelwork product is obtained by mixing a steelwork slag with siliceous additives.
   USE - Steelwork product is used as partial or total replacement of clinker during manufacture of cement (all claimed) used as filling material in road paving and rail ballast, and corrective of soil acidity or fertilizer in agriculture applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The steelwork product has high mobility and retains high content of alite formed at high temperature, thereby avoiding its decomposition in slow cooling process. The use of additivated slag as cementitious additives promotes the reduction amount of carbon dioxide released into the environment by partial replacement of the clinker.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining the manufacture of the steelwork product.

Preferred Process: The product is subjected to concentration processes, preferably magnetic concentration to remove excessive fractions of metallic iron and other iron-containing magnetic phases.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing corrosion inhibitor, comprising mixing                the above raw materials with water, and stirring                uniformly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The corrosion                  inhibitor comprises 8-10 wt.% quaternary ammonium                  salt, 8-10 wt.% benzalkonium chloride, 7.5-10                  wt.% styrene ketone, 3-5 wt.% peregal O-25, 2.5-4                  wt.% thiourea, 8-10 wt.% polyepoxysuccinic acid,                  12-15 wt.% polyether methylene phosphonate, 6-8                  wt.% dipropylene glycol diacrylate, 9-10 wt.%                  2-phosphonobutane-1,2,4-tricarboxylic acid, 1-2                  wt.% monoethanolamine and 20-25 wt.% water.                  Preferred Components: The sodium hydroxide is                  5-6% of the total mass of the acetophenone and                  37% formaldehyde aqueous solution. The high                  boiling organic solvent is dimethylformamide and                  the low boiling organic solvent is methanol. The                  alkylpyridine comprises 16-18%                  3,5-dimethylpyridine, 5-6% 3-propylpyridine,                  30-35% 2,3,5-trimethylpyridine, 3.5-5%                  3-ethylpyridine, 7-8% 3-propyl-4-methylpyridine,                  3-3.5% 2,3-cyclopentenopyridine, 2-2.5%                  porphyrin, 1-1.5% 2,3,4,5-tetramethylpyrrole,                  0.6-1% 2,3-dimethyl-1H-pyrrolo(3,2-b)pyridine,                  and 0.5-1% 2,6-dimethyl pyridazine. Preferred                  Method: The preparation of styrene ketone                  comprises mixing acetophenone with 37% aqueous                  formaldehyde solution at a mass ratio of 1:1,                  adding sodium hydroxide solution at 0-5 degrees                  C, incubating at 0-5 degrees C for 1-3 hours,                  heating to 85-90 degrees C for 3-4 hours, cooling                  to room temperature, allowing to stand, layering,                  and taking the upper layer. The preparation of                  quaternary ammonium salt comprises dissolving                  complex alkylpyridine in a high boiling organic                  solvent, adding benzyl chloride dropwisely at                  70-80 degrees C, controlling the temperature of                  the system is less than 140 degrees C, after the                  completion of the dropwise addition, heating to                  140 degrees C for 6-8 hours, reducing the                  temperature below 100 degrees C, and adding low                  boiling point organic solvent dropwisely, where                  the mass ratio of the complex alkylpyridine,                  benzyl chloride, high boiling organic solvent,                  and low boiling organic solvent is                  9:6:0.3:1.2.

TI Preserving freshness of broccoli for selling involves spraying broccoli with 6-benzyladenine solution before harvesting, packaging broccoli with fresh-keeping bags individually and putting ethylene absorbent to fresh-keeping bag.
AU HU H
   LI P
   LUO S
   ZHANG L
   ZHOU H
AE JIANGSU AGRIC SCI INST (JSAS-C)
   JIANGSU AGRIC SCI INST (JSAS-C)
GA 201842420A
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving freshness of broccoli for selling involves spraying broccoli with 20-40 mg/l 6-benzyladenine solution before 6-10 hours of harvesting, packaging broccoli with fresh-keeping bags individually, putting ethylene absorbent to fresh-keeping bag, introducing oxygen and carbon-dioxide gas in fresh-keeping bag, sealing fresh-keeping bag and putting sealed fresh-keeping bag in foam box.
   USE - Method for preserving freshness of broccoli for selling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to provide technical solution of operation, prolong sale radius to reach purpose of market supply, improve economic benefit in effective manner, preserve freshness of broccoli in simple and cost effective manner and is good for popularization and application.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Compositions: The weight of ethylene absorbent in fresh-keeping bag is 5-15 g. Preferred Components: The ethylene absorbent is made of potassium permanganate solution saturated with vermiculite. Preferred Conditions: Spraying with 6-benzyladenine solution is performed before harvesting when buds shows no signs of water on surface. The fresh-keeping bag is bacteriostatic preservative, composed of inner and outer layers, where outer layer is polyethylene layer, thickness is 0.025 mm, layer is non-woven fabric which is coated with 1-3 wt.% chitosan solution. The foam box is set in air bag.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The oxygen introduced to fresh-keeping bag accounts for 5-8% of total gas volume in fresh-keeping bag and incoming carbon dioxide accounts for 8-15% of total gas volume in fresh-keeping bag.

PT P
PN CN107812450-A
TI Alkaline non-aqueous wet catalytic oxidation of hydrogen sulfide involves mixing ferric sodium edetate with disubstituted alkylimidazolium hydroxide to form basic ionic liquid, evaporating moisture and dissolving in organic solvent.
AU WNAG J
   ZHAO X
AE BEIJING INST PETROCHEMICAL TECHNOLOGY (SNPC-C)
GA 2018239468
AB    NOVELTY - Performing alkaline non-aqueous wet catalytic oxidation of hydrogen sulfide involves mixing ferric sodium edetate with disubstituted alkylimidazolium hydroxide to form basic ionic liquid, evaporating the moisture in the basic ionic liquid, then dissolving the dried material in organic solvent with boiling point higher than 150 degrees C to obtain iron-based ionic liquid desulfurization solution and oxidizing hydrogen sulfide-containing gas catalytically using the iron-based ionic liquid desulfurization solution.
   USE - Method for performing alkaline non-aqueous wet catalytic oxidation of hydrogen sulfide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to have high yield of sulfur and can remove gas impurities such as carbon dioxide and water without corroding the equipment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is selected from N,N-dimethyl formamide, N-methyl pyrrolidone, dimethyl sulfoxide and 1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolone. The disubstituted alkylimidazolium is selected from 1-alkenyl-3-alkylimidazole, 1-alkenyl-3-alkylimidazole and 1-benzyl-3-alkylimidazole and 1-benzyl-3-vinylimidazole, where the alkyl group contains 1-16 carbon atoms and the olefin group has 2-8 carbon atoms. The hydrogen sulfide-containing gas comprises natural gas, biogas, refinery exhaust gas and shale gas.Preferred Conditions: The pH value of alkaline ionic liquid is less than 10.Preferred Process: The catalytic oxidation of the hydrogen sulfide-containing gas is performed using the iron-based ionic liquid desulfurizing solution which involves firstly passing a gas containing hydrogen sulfide into a gas purification equipment containing the iron-based ionic liquid desulfurization solution to perform a catalytic oxidation treatment so that the hydrogen sulfide is converted into sulfur, and then the desulfurization solution is passed into a regeneration tower. An oxygen-containing gas is introduced into the regeneration tower for oxidation to oxidize the desulfurization solution to an iron-based ionic liquid. The regenerated iron-based ionic liquid is returned to the gas purification equipment for catalytic oxidation treatment of the hydrogen sulfide-containing gas. The oxygen-containing gas is oxygen or air.

TI Processing ginkgo seed involves crushing dried ginkgo seed, soaking in first mixed solution to obtain primary processed ginkgo seed, performing secondary treatment by soaking in second mixed solution and soaking in third mixed solution.
AU XUE H
   XU H
   ZHANG L
AE CHANGZHOU HAOTAN COMMERCE & TRADE CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018168794
AB    NOVELTY - Processing ginkgo seed involves crushing dried ginkgo seed, soaking in first mixed solution for 45-60 minutes to obtain primary processed ginkgo seed, performing secondary treatment by soaking in second mixed solution for 45-60 minutes, soaking secondary processed ginkgo seed in third mixed solution for 3-5 hours, introducing carbon dioxide gas to mixed solution at rate of 40-60 ml/min, drying to obtain three times processed ginkgo seed, soaking obtained ginkgo seed in fourth mixed solution for three times for 2-4 hours, washing with solvent for 3-5 times and drying.
   USE - Method for processing ginkgo seed (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve gingko seed germination rate in an effective manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The first mixed solution comprises phospholipids and water in weight ratio of 1:50-1:60. The second mixed solution comprises 3-5 pts. wt. inositol, 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. vitamin, 0.01-0.03 pts. wt. lysine, 0.03-0.05 pts. wt. caffeine and 80-100 pts. wt. water. The third mixed solution comprises 2-4 pts. wt. allyl amine, 3-5 pts. wt. polyethylene imine, 100-120 pts. wt. water and fourth mixed solution comprises 80-100 pts. wt. liquid resin and 60-80 pts. wt. The vitamin comprises vitamin B6 and vitamin B12 in weight ratio of 20:1-30:1. The liquid resin comprises phenolic resin and isocyanate in weight ratio of 10:1-10:3. essential oil. The solvent comprises anhydrous ethanol and acetone in weight ratio of 1:1-1:3. Preferred Components: The phospholipid is soybean lecithin or yolk lecithin. The essential oil is rose essential oil, lavender essential oil or jasmine essential oil.

   USE - Catalyst is used for dry reforming catalyst                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The dry reforming catalyst exhibits excellent                catalytic activity for prolonged periods at high                temperature without using a noble metal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of the catalyst, which                involves dissolving an active material precursor, a                metal oxide support and a surfactant in a polyol                solvent to obtain a mixture, and adding a reducing                agent to the resulting mixture; and
   (2) method for dry reforming methane, which                involves reacting carbon dioxide and methane at                600-800 degrees C in presence of the catalyst to                convert it into a synthesis gas of hydrogen and                carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The metal oxide is                  chosen from zirconium oxide, magnesium oxide,                  titanium dioxide, aluminum oxide, silicon                  dioxide, chromium dioxide, ferric oxide, ferrous                  oxide, cupric oxide, zinc oxide, calcium oxide,                  antimony tetroxide, cobalt oxide, lead tetroxide,                  manganese tetroxide, silver peroxide, triuranium                  octoxide, lithium oxide, rubidium oxide, silver                  oxide, tellurium oxide, cadmium oxide, beryllium                  oxide, germanium dioxide, hafnium dioxide, lead                  oxide, manganese oxide, tin oxide, lanthanum                  oxide, tungsten dioxide, uranium dioxide, thorium                  dioxide, tellurium dioxide and molybdenum                  trioxide. The                  surface of the magnesium oxide support is                  impregnated with nickel and molybdenum. The                  nickel is surrounded by the surfactant,. The                  nickel content is 0.0-100 wt.% and the molybdenum                  content is 0-99.9 wt.% in the catalyst. The                  catalyst comprises a nickel-molybdenum complex of                  formula: Ni10Mo2-MgO. The weight ratio of nickel                  to molybdenum is 0.01-100: 0-99.9-0. The                  precursor of nickel is of formula: NiCl2,                  NiCl2.6H2O, Ni (NO3) 2, NiSO4, (NH4)2Ni (SO4)                  2.6H2O, Ni (OCOCH3) 2.4H2 O, NiBr2, NiCO3, NiF2,                  NiC2O4.2H2O or/and Ni (ClO4) 2.6H2O, and                  precursor of molybdenum is of formula: Mo (CO)6,                  MoO3, Na2MoO4, CaMoO4, ZnMoO4 or/and (NH4)                  6Mo7O2.4H2O. The reducing agent is chosen from                  hydrazine, sodium borohydride, lithium aluminum                  hydride, diisobutylaluminum hydride, and diborane                  . The weight ratio of active material, magnesium                  oxide support and surfactant is 0.01-40:                  59.6-99.5: 0.04. Preferred Property: The catalyst                  has a pore width of 0-5000 m2/g and a conversion                  of 95-98%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The surfactant is                  chosen from polyvinylpyrrolidone, oleylamine,                  oleic acid, benzyl ether, trioctylphosphine,                  octadecylamine, hexadecylamine, cetyl                  trimethylammonium bromide, polyethylene glycol,                  1,2-hexadecanediol, and 1-octadecene.

The oxygen source is pure oxygen, mixed gas containing oxygen and air. The active material of zinc anode is mainly zinc. The first spunbond nonwoven fabric layer and the second spunbond nonwoven fabric layer respectively comprise neither polyethylene nor biopolyethylene alone. The first spunbond nonwoven fabric layer or the second spunbond nonwoven fabric layer comprises a core type composite fiber, where the core type composite fiber comprises core polypropylene and the core portion comprises biopolyethylene. The electro-treated melt-blown nonwoven fabric layer comprises polypropylene, polyethylene, or biopolyethylene configured to be closely adhered to biopolyethylene. The electro-treated melt-blown nonwoven fabric layer further comprises polypropylene. The first spunbond nonwoven fabric layer and the second spunbond nonwoven fabric layer are respectively and independently provided from each other in such a manner that a carbon dioxide emission amount is 1-6 kg per 1 ton of each spunbond nonwoven fabric layer. Preferred Composition: The nonwoven fabric comprises 10-70 pts. wt., preferably 20-50 pts. wt. biopolyethylene based on 100 pts. wt. of the total weight of the nonwoven fabric. The content of the electro-treated melt-blown nonwoven fabric layer is 3-50 pts. wt. with respect to 100 pts. wt. of the composite nonwoven fabric. Preferred Properties: The composite nonwoven fabric emits carbon dioxide at a temperature of 1-20 kg per 1 ton of the composite nonwoven fabric. The charged melt-blown nonwoven fabric layer has a carbon dioxide emission of 1-6 kg per 1 ton of the charged melt-blown nonwoven fabric layer. The composite nonwoven fabric has 18-99.9% at a fine dust removal efficiency and 2-10 mm water in pressure loss.
   EXAMPLE - Example 1-5 and comparative example 1-2 describes about a preparation of a nonwoven fabric, so does not support the claim.

PN CN113333011-A
TI Preparing composite catalyst useful for catalytic            ozone oxidation treatment of wastewater, comprises            loading metal oxide and doped nitrogen element on            carbon base material to obtain composite catalyst.
AU CAO H
   XIE Y
   DUAN F
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 2021A4636B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite catalyst comprises loading                metal oxide and doped nitrogen element on the                carbon base material to obtain the composite                catalyst, where the nitrogen-containing precursor                doped with nitrogen element is ammonia gas and/or                inorganic ammonium salt.
   USE - The composite catalyst is useful for catalytic                ozone oxidation treatment of wastewater                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical, is suitable                for large-scale application and has high efficiency                in catalytic ozone oxidation treatment of waste                water. The catalyst has long stable service life                and wide application prospect, and can not                selectively mineralize the organic matter in the                waste water into carbon dioxide and water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                composite catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon-based                  material comprises activated carbon, active coke,                  biological carbon, graphene material or carbon                  nano-tube, preferably activated carbon and/or                  biological carbon. The metal in the metal oxide                  comprises iron, cobalt, manganese, nickel,                  copper, lanthanum, cerium, molybdenum or                  titanium. The nitrogen-containing precursor is                  ammonia gas, and based on the mass of the                  carbon-based material being 1 g, the amount of                  the ammonia gas is 900-60000 ml. The                  nitrogen-containing precursor is an inorganic                  ammonium salt, and based on the mass of the                  carbon-based material as 100%, the mass of                  nitrogen in the inorganic ammonium salt is                  0.1-15%, preferably is 1-8%. The inorganic                  ammonium salt is ammonium chloride, ammonium                  bicarbonate, ammonium carbonate, ammonium nitrate                  or ammonium sulfate. Preferred Method: The                  mehthod comprises placing the carbon-based                  material or carbon-based material doped with                  nitrogen element in the metal precursor solution                  for dipping, where dipping method is equal volume                  impregnation method, dipping at 15-45 degrees C                  for 8-24 hours, drying at 100-120 degrees C for                  5-24 hours, roasting at 300-900 degrees C for                  0.5-5 hours in vacuum or protective atmosphere,                  where the protective atmosphere is inert                  atmosphere and/or reducing atmosphere, the inert                  atmosphere comprises nitrogen atmosphere and/or                  argon atmosphere and the reducing atmosphere                  comprises hydrogen atmosphere and/or ammonia                  atmosphere to obtain the carbon-based material                  loaded with metal oxide, where the metal                  precursor solution is aqueous solution of metal                  salt, the metal salt is metal inorganic salt,                  preferably metal nitrate, metal sulfate or metal                  halide, the mass of the carbon-based material is                  100% and the mass of the metal in the metal                  precursor solution is 0.5-20%, preferably 2-15%,                  doping nitrogen element comprises method A or                  method B, the method A comprises taking any one                  of carbon-based material, carbon-based material                  loaded with metal oxide or carbon-based material                  impregnated with metal precursor as carrier,                  uniformly mixing the carrier with the inorganic                  ammonium salt, roasting in the oxygen-free                  environment to obtain the carbon-based material                  doped with nitrogen element and the method B                  comprises baking the carbon-based material,                  carbon-based material loaded with metal oxide or                  carbon-based material impregnated with metal                  precursor in ammonia atmosphere to obtain                  carbon-basedmaterial doped with nitrogen element,                  preferably, the roasting temperature in method A                  and method B is independently 300-900 degrees C,                  preferably, the roasting time in method A and                  method B are each independently 2-6 hours.

PT P
PN US2020347467-A1; BR102020008626-A2
TI Iron making feedstock for producing iron,            comprises solid calcium-iron oxide phase and is in form            of agglomerates or pellets, produced by reacting            calcium source and magnetite at elevated temperature            under reducing conditions.
AU PUROHIT S
   BROOKS G
   RHAMDHANI M A
   POWNCEBY M
AE UNIV SWINBURNE TECHNOLOGY (UYSW-Non-standard)
   COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
   UNIV SWINBURNE TECHNOLOGY (UYSW-Non-standard)
   COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
GA 2020A7314L
AB    NOVELTY - Iron making feedstock comprises a solid                calcium-iron oxide (CaFe3O5) phase.
   USE - Iron making feedstock for producing iron                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The iron making feedstock has better emissions                profile in terms of carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions                for iron making processes, from the stage of                mineral processing to final iron production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a process for producing an ironmaking                feedstock, which involves reacting a combination of                a calcium source and magnetite at elevated                temperature under reducing conditions sufficient to                produce a solid CaFe3O5 phase; and
   (2) a method of producing iron, which involves                reducing the ironmaking feedstock to form                iron.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium source is                  selected from the group consisting of calcium                  carbonate, calcium oxide, dolomite, gypsum and                  combinations. Preferred Compositions: The iron                  making feedstock comprises at least 10 wt.% solid                  calcium-iron oxide (CaFe3O5) phase as measured by                  quantitative phase analysis using the Rietveld                  method. Preferred Conditions: The ironmaking                  feedstock is in the form of agglomerates or                  pellets having a compressive strength of at least                  14 mega Pascals (MPa). The CaFe3O5 phase is the                  primary bonding phase. The ironmaking feedstock                  has a reducibility index, scaled down to a 6-7                  gram sample size, of at least 10.0. The                  ironmaking feedstock comprises the product of a                  combination of a calcium source and magnetite                  that is subjected to reductive conditions at                  elevated temperature sufficient to produce the                  solid CaFe3O5 phase. The elevated temperature is                  a temperature between 500-1150 degrees C. The                  reducing conditions comprise a CO2/CO ratio of                  1.0 or greater and a reduction potential of 9.8x                  10-16 or less. The molar ratio of calcium source                  to magnetite is between 0.1:1.0 and 1.2:1.0. The                  reaction is conducted over a time period of                  between 20 minutes and 8 hours. Preferred                  Process: The process for the production of an                  ironmaking feedstock further involves pelletising                  that includes pelletising the combination of the                  calcium source and the magnetite before                  conducting the reaction at elevated temperature                  under reducing conditions; and processing an iron                  ore to yield a magnetite concentrate, and using                  the magnetite concentrate as the magnetite source                  for the reaction between the calcium source and                  the magnetite.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)
MC M24-A02; M24-A03; M24-B01A

TI Preparation of organic amine from syngas includes            adjusting solid content of iron-based catalyst filter            cake, adding melamine, crushing, calcining and reducing            precursor of iron-based catalyst containing graphitic            carbon nitride.
AU QING M
   LI L

   LIU S
   WANG J
   BAI Y

AE ZHONGKE SYNTHETIC OIL TECH CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
   SYNFUELS CHINA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SYNF-Non-standard)
GA 2020A9403T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of organic amine from syngas                comprises preparing iron-based catalyst filter                cake, adjusting solid content of iron-based                catalyst filter cake, adding melamine, crushing to                obtain slurry, spray drying slurry, calcining to                obtain iron based catalyst precursors containing                graphitic carbon nitride, reducing precursor of                iron-based catalyst containing graphitic carbon                nitride, performing nitridation to obtain catalyst,                using catalyst to catalyze carbon monoxide                hydrogenation reaction and adding ammonia to feed                gas.
   USE - The method is for preparation of organic amine                e.g. fatty amine from syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively modify product                selectivity of catalyst, has low selectivity of low                carbon hydrocarbons e.g. methane, low selectivity                of carbon dioxide and increases selectivity of                aliphatic amine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The crushing is at 20-70                  degrees c for 0.1-24 hours. The spray drying air                  inlet temperature is 300-450 degrees C and outlet                  temperature is 99-130 degrees C. The calcination                  is carried out in air atmosphere and/or inert                  atmosphere. The roasting in air atmosphere is                  200-550 degrees C for 2-50 hours. The roasting in                  inert atmosphere is at 300-750 degrees c for 5-60                  hours. The carbon monoxide hydrogenation reaction                  is at 200-350 degrees c under 0.1-5 MPa.                  Preferred Components: The iron-based catalyst                  filter cake is iron, structural additive, carrier                  and electronic additive. The structural additive                  is chromium, zinc, boron, tungsten and/or                  molybdenum. The carrier is silicon dioxide and/or                  aluminum oxide. The electronic additive is                  copper, lithium, sodium, potassium, ruthenium                  and/or cesium. The mass ratio of iron, structural                  additive, carrier and electronic additive is                  100:0.1-20:0.1-40:0.1-15. The mass ratio of iron                  to ammonium nitrate is 100:0.1-30. The mass ratio                  of iron to melamine is 100:0.1-400. The inert                  atmosphere is nitrogen, argon and/or helium                  gases. The mole ratio of hydrogen to carbon                  monoxide in carbon monoxide hydrogenation                  reaction is 0.5-5. The volume percentage of                  ammonia added in fresh gas is 0.5-25.5%.

PT P
PN CN111647554-A
TI Preparing exosomes from cells used for activating            proliferation and regeneration, promoting wound            healing, involves using cell culture, washing in            phosphate-buffered saline when cell culture reaches            confluence.
AU XIAO Y
   HUO L
   LEI Q
   ZHONG M
AE GUANGZHOU DAKANG GENE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020914864
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing exosomes from cells involves using                cell culture contains 104 cells, washing in                phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) when the cell                culture reaches confluence, adding KnockOut (RTM:                Culture medium) (KO)-Dulbecco's Modified Eagle                medium (DMEM) and placing to an incubator                containing carbon dioxide for culture, centrifuging                medium for 15 minutes to remove cell debris in the                medium. The cells are cultured for 2-20 days, and                exosomes are prepared by ultracentrifugation                technology, size exclusion filtration, chemical                precipitation, discontinuous density gradient,                immunoaffinity, ultrafiltration and/or high                performance liquid chromatography.
   USE - Method for preparing exosomes from cells used                for activating proliferation and regeneration,                promoting wound healing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare exosomes from                cells, which is stable, high in purity, and                comprehensive in positive markers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The cells are primary                  cells, immortalized cells, human cells and/or                  stem cells. The stem cells are mesenchymal stem                  cells, stem cells derived from heart tissue, stem                  cells derived from adipose tissue, stem cells                  derived from neural tissue, and/or cells that                  produce insulin. The cell culture conditions are                  36-38 degrees C and 1-19% carbon dioxide, in a                  volume containing 5-20% fetal bovine serum or                  purified human platelet lysate and 1-5%                  penicillin/streptomycin Culture in KO-Dulbecco's                  Modified Eagle medium (DMEM). The                  ultracentrifugation uses a centrifugal force of                  1000-10000g, the size exclusion filtration uses a                  filter with a pore size of 0.1-5 micrometer, the                  chemical precipitation is the use of a                  precipitation reagent added to the cell culture                  medium to precipitate exosomes, and high                  performance liquid chromatography is flow field                  flow classification. The separated exosomes is                  filter-sterilized with a 0.10-1.00 micrometer                  microbial exclusion filter, and 0.45 micrometer                  filter exosomes is used to remove cell debris.                  The precipitation reagent is polyethylene glycol.                  The final concentration of the polyethylene                  glycol is 5-12.0% by volume, and the polyethylene                  glycol has a molecular weight of 4000-23000                  Daltons. The exosome preparation containing                  105-1012 exosomes and exosomes contained in the                  exosomes preparation have a diameter of 50-250                  nanometer. The exosome preparation is a liquid,                  the cell culture is mesenchymal stem cells, the                  exosome preparation is in a state below minus 20                  degrees C, and the exosome preparation contains a                  mass-to-volume ratio of less than 0.01%                  polyethylene glycol. The culture medium of the                  cells is centrifuged at 500-2500g to remove dead                  cells and apoptotic bodies, and the supernatant                  is collected. The culture medium of the cells is                  continuously precipitated with polyethylene                  glycol for 2 hours, and the precipitated solution                  is centrifuged at a centrifugal force of 2000 g                  to obtain exosomes. The exosomes are subjected to                  a second, third or more polyethylene glycol (PEG)                  precipitation for further purification. The                  exosomal preparation is freeze-dried in advance,                  and then resuspended in an aqueous solution. The                  purified exosomes are mixed with 1% to 6% of a                  lyoprotectant of mannitol, lactose, sucrose or                  trehalose. PN CN111254457-A; CN111254457-B
TI Electrochemical synthesis method of aromatic            carboxylic acid and alkyl carboxylic acid, comprises            adding organic halide or pseudo-halide, nickel            catalyst, stirring, quenching with hydrochloric acid,            extracting, concentrating and purifying.
AU YU D
   SUN G
   ZHANG W
   LIAO L
   NIE Z
   WU J
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 202053643N
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical synthesis method of aromatic                carboxylic acid and alkyl carboxylic acid,                comprises (i) adding organic halide or                pseudo-halide, nickel catalyst, ligand, base,                supporting electrolyte into a container with                electrodes fixed on both sides, adding solvent                under carbon dioxide atmosphere, and stirring to                dissolve; (ii) passing a constant current between                the two electrodes, and carrying out the reaction                at 0-100 degrees C, quenching with hydrochloric                acid, extracting and concentrating to obtain a                crude product; and (iii) purifying the crude                product.
   USE - Used as electrochemical synthesis method of                aromatic carboxylic acid and alkyl carboxylic                acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has mild reaction conditions, a                wide range of substrates, cheap and readily                available raw materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  amount of the nickel catalyst is 1-20% molar                  equivalent of the organic halide or pseudohalide,                  the amount of ligand is 1-40% molar equivalent of                  organic halide or pseudohalide, the amount of                  base is 0.1-3 times the molar equivalent of                  organic halogen or pseudo-halogen, and the                  supporting electrolyte concentration is 0.05-0.4                  M. The organic halide or pseudohalide is aryl                  chloride, aryl bromide, aryl iodide, aryl                  sulfonate or alkyl bromide. The nickel catalyst                  is nickel bromide, nickel iodide, nickel chloride                  ethylene glycol dimethyl ether adduct, nickel                  bromide ethylene glycol dimethyl ether adduct, or                  nickel acetylacetonate. The ligand is                  2,2'-bipyridine, 6,6'-dimethoxy-2,2'-bipyridine,                  4,4'-dimethoxy-2,2'-bipyridine At least one of                  4,4'-di-tert-butyl-2,2'-bipyridine, 6,6'-dimethyl                  2,2'-bipyridine and 4-dimethylaminopyridine. The                  base is potassium tert-butoxide, cesium fluoride,                  sodium ethoxide, sodium tert-butoxide or cesium                  carbonate. The supporting electrolyte is sodium                  iodide, lithium perchlorate, tetrabutylammonium                  tetrafluoroborate, lithium bromide, lithium                  chloride, or lithium iodide. The solvent is                  N-methylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethylacetamide,                  N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide,                  1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolidinone or acetonitrile.                  In the step (ii), the constant current is 1-30                  mA. The (iii) is purified by column                  chromatography.

AU KIM S W
AE GRENERGY CO LTD (GREN-Non-standard)
GA 2020438326
AB    NOVELTY - Surface treating of lithium metal cathode (12), comprises cleaning the surface of the lithium metal cathode using dry ice blasting.
   USE - The method is useful for surface treating of lithium metal cathode in lithium metal battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves performance of lithium metal batteries and stable charging and discharging efficiency during its lifetime.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) surface-treated lithium metal cathode, comprising lithium metal cathode, a first protective layer is lithium carbon dioxide, lithium oxalate, lithium carbonate and/or lithium carbonite; and
   (2) lithium metal battery (11) comprising the surface-treated lithium metal cathode.

   Lithium metal battery (11)
   Cathode (12)
   Anode (13)
   Separator (14)
   Battery case (15)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises performing solid carbon dioxide injection using liquid carbon dioxide for producing dry ice or dry ice pellets prepared using the liquid carbonic acid. The method comprises performing solid carbon dioxide injection by making the dry ice pellet through adiabatic expansion of a liquefied carbonic acid in the vicinity of the cleaning nozzle or the cleaning nozzle, and spraying the dry ice pellets on the surface of the lithium metal cathode through the cleaning nozzle. The method comprises performing solid carbon dioxide injection by spraying liquefied carbonic acid or dry ice pellets on the surface of the lithium metal cathode with compressed air. The method comprises injecting solid carbonic acid, the injection pressure is within 0.5-10 bar, injection angle is 15-90 degrees , and injection distance is 5-150 mm. The method comprises forming a first protective layer on the surface of the lithium metal cathode in the solid carbon injection process. The method comprises performing solid carbon injection by mixing liquefied carbonic acid or dry ice pellets with fluorine gas. The method further comprises spraying a fluorine gas on the surface of the lithium metal cathode. Preferred Components: The first protective layer further comprises lithium fluoride. The surface-treated lithium metal cathode further comprises a second protective layer containing lithium fluoride.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN RU2718022-C1
TI METHOD OF ELECTROCHEMICAL PRODUCTION OF NANO-SIZED POWDER OF METAL SILICIDE.
AU LEONTEV L I
   LISIN V L
   PETROVA S A
   KOSTYLEV V A
   VARAKSIN A V
AE AS RUSSIA URALS METALLURGY INST (ASUR-Non-standard)
   KOSTYLEV V A (KOST-Individual)
GA 2020343202
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to production of nano-sized powder of metal silicide. Method comprises loading into sealed crucible electrolyte consisting of alkali metal halide and metal salt, and consumable components of micron size in form of powders of metal and silicon, heating to operating temperatures of synthesis of metal silicide is performed above melting point of electrolyte to obtain ionic melt in atmosphere of argon or carbon dioxide. For transfer in ion melt of metal to silicon with formation of nano-sized powder of metal silicide provides anionic-cationic composition of ion melt with electrochemical potential of metal, more negative than potential of silicon by value greater than 0.5V. Solidified electrolyte with formed powder is extracted from crucible, is ground and subjected to hydrometallurgical treatment to produce nano-sized metal silicide powder.
   USE - Technological processes.
   ADVANTAGE - Providing electrochemical production of nano-sized powder of metal silicide.
1 cl, 1 ex

AE SHANDONG JISHUN ENERGY-SAVING BUILDING (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020014579
AB    NOVELTY - Heat proof insulating material comprises graphite extruded polystyrene board is used as a base plate in which one side of the graphite extruded polystyrene board is compounded with a layer of vitrified microbeads. The raw material composition of graphite extruded polystyrene board comprises ethylene, expanded graphite, talc, flame retardant, carbon black, and carbon dioxide foaming agent.
   USE - The heat proof insulating material is useful as paste-type insulation board and cast-in-place insulation board (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material: applied as thermal insulation board in the construction process of cast-in-situ walls; constructs insulation board conveniently; and has 30% thermal insulation performance and A-grade combustion performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Components: The thickness of the base plate is 50-100 mm, the thickness of the vitrified microbead layer is 8-12 mm, and the density of the graphite extruded polystyrene board is 30-35Kg/m3. The other side of the graphite extruded polystyrene board has composite rubber powder polystyrene particle layer. The thickness of the rubber powder polystyrene particle layer is 18-22 mm. Preferred Composition: The raw material composition of graphite extruded polystyrene board comprises 75-85 pts. wt. ethylene, 6-14 pts. wt. expanded graphite, 1-7 pts. wt. talc, 6-11.5 pts. wt. flame retardant, 0.22-2.1 pts. wt. carbon black, and 6-8 pts. wt. carbon dioxide foaming agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition of the vitrified microbead layer comprises 124-126 pts. wt. Portland cement, 37-38 pts. wt. heavy calcium powder, 12-13 pts. wt. quartz powder, 12-13 pts. wt. fly ash, 62-63 pts. wt. vitrified microbead particles, 2-4 pts. wt. silicone water repellent, 0.7-0.8 pts. wt. anti-cracking fiber, 1 pts. wt. cellulose ether and 2-3 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol, preferably comprises 125 pts. wt. Portland cement, 37.5 pts. wt. heavy calcium powder, 12.5 pts. wt. quartz powder, 12.5 pts. wt. fly ash, 62.5 pts. wt. vitrified beads, 3 pts. wt. silicone water repellent, 0.75 pts. wt. Anti-cracking fiber, 1 pts. wt. cellulose ether and 2.5 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The rubber powder polystyrene particle layer comprises Portland cement, fly ash, quartz sand, heavy calcium powder, latex powder WACKER, silicone water repellent, anti-cracking fiber, cellulose ether, starch ether, aqueous reducer and polystyrene particles, preferably mass ratio of Portland cement, fly ash, quartz sand, heavy calcium powder, latex powder WACKER, silicone water repellent, anti-cracking fiber, cellulose ether, starch ether and aqueous reducer is 180-190:24-26:24-26:12-13:9-11:2-3:0.7-0.8:1-2:1:1-2, preferably the mass ratio is 185-188:24-26:24-26:12.4-12.6:9-11:2-3: 0.73-0.76:1-2:1:1-2, preferably diameter of the polystyrene particles is 4-6 mm, and 6-8 liters of polystyrene particles are mixed into the polystyrene particle layer of the rubber powder. Preferred Method: The preparation method of graphite extruded polystyrene board comprises mixing raw material ethylene, expanded graphite, talc, flame retardant, and carbon black for the first plasticization, injecting carbon dioxide foaming agent under high pressure, and performing second plasticization process and extrusion molding, preferably the first plasticizing temperature is greater than 190 degrees C, and the plasticizing by screw extrusion, and the extrusion length is 9-11 m, preferably the second plasticizing temperature is 90-60 degrees C, and plasticizing by screw extrusion, and the extrusion length is 9-11 m.

PT P
PN CN110538653-A
TI New catalyst i.e. rhodium-lanthanum titanium oxide-lanthanum zirconate complex useful for producing syngas by methane dry reforming.
AU YAO Y
   WU J
   ZHOU Z
   QIAO L
   QIN Y
   ZONG S
   CUI G
   XU D
AE FUJIAN MATERIAL STRUCTURE INST (CAFU-C)
GA 2019A44224
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst i.e. rhodium-lanthanum titanium oxide-lanthanum zirconate complex (I) is new. The addition amount of rhodium is 1-2 wt.% based on the total mass of the catalyst.
   USE - (I) is useful for producing syngas by methane dry reforming (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): solves the problems of catalyst sintering and carbon deposition due to high temperature in producing syngas by methane dry reforming, and conversion rate of carbon dioxide more than 85%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst i.e. rhodium-lanthanum titanium oxide-lanthanum zirconate complex of formula (Rh/xLa2Ti2O7-yLa2Zr2O7) (I) is new. The addition amount of rhodium is 1-2 wt.% based on the total mass of the catalyst.
   x = less than 0-1;
   y = less than 0-1;
   x + y = 1; and
   Rh = active component.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) dissolving the soluble lanthanum salt, titanium salt and zirconium salt in absolute ethanol, adding the three salt solutions into the mixing container and ultrasonically mixing at 25-30 KHz for 10-15 minutes to obtain mixed salt solution A, where the molar ratio between lanthanum/(titanium and zirconium) is 1-2, the addition amount of titanium salt and zirconium salt satisfy x+y is 1, and the concentration of lanthanum salt is 0.1-0.8 M, preferably 0.2-0.5 M, the soluble lanthanum salt is lanthanum nitrate or lanthanum(III) chloride, the soluble titanium salt is tetrabutyl titanate or titanium tetrachloride, and the soluble zirconium salt is zirconium nitrate or zirconium tetrachloride, (ii) adding soluble rhodium salt solution and complexing agent solution to the mixed salt solution A, then mixing uniformly and then performing continuously ultrasonication for 1-2 hours to obtain mixed solution B, where the amount of rhodium salt is calculated based on the percentage of rhodium salt in the final catalyst of 1-2 wt.%, the molar ratio between complexing agent and lanthanum is 1-8, the soluble rhodium salt solution is an ethanol solution of rhodium nitrate or rhodium chloride with a concentration of 0.5-2 M, the complexing agent is citric acid, glacial acetic acid, nitric acid or ascorbic acid, and the complexing agent is dissolved in anhydrous ethanol to form a complexing agent solution and the concentration is 0-1 M, (iii) sealing the mixed solution B, then continuously stirring at room temperature for 8-12 hours and performing rotary evaporation in a vacuum environment at 50-90 degrees C to evaporate the solvent to obtain catalyst precursor, and (iv) drying the catalyst precursor obtained in step (iii) at 80-120 degrees C for 8-15 hours and baking in a muffle furnace at 800-1000 degrees C for 4-10 hours to obtain final product. Preferred Components: In step (i): the concentration of lanthanum salt is 0.2-0.5 M; and in step (ii): the molar ratio of complexing agent and lanthanum is 2-4 and the complexing agent is citric acid.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN110316717-A; CN110316717-B
TI Preparing nitrogen-containing porous material useful as absorbing material, comprises ball milling carbon source, nitrogen source, and mixed eutectic salt and mixing, high temperature carbonizing, cooling and ultrasonically cleaning.
AU WANG S
   XU Y
   DI Z
   ZHANG L
   LIU Z
AE ENERGY RES INST HEBEI ACAD SCI (ENER-Non-standard)
GA 201987343M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrogen-containing porous material comprises (i) ball milling carbon source, nitrogen source, and mixed eutectic salt and mixing, (ii) high temperature carbonizing the mixture under vacuum and protective gas at a rate of 10-30 ml/minute, heating to 900 plus minus 10 degrees C, heat preserving for 2-4 hours from room temperature at a rate of 2-3 degrees C/minute, cooling to room temperature to obtain black nitrogen-containing carbon material, and (iii) washing by hydrochloric acid solution, ultrasonically cleaning for half an hour and cleaning for 3 times.
   USE - The material is useful as absorbing material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the adsorption capacity of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of the carbon source, nitrogen source and eutectic salt mixture is 1:1:1. The carbonate is lignin, chitosan, sodium alginate, chitin and/or cellulose. The nitrogen source is melamine, urea, dicyandiamide and/or hexamethylenetetramine. The mixed eutectic salt comprises 36-42% lithium nitrate, 46-52% potassium nitrate and 12-18% potassium sulfate. The mass ratio of the mass of the grinding ball to the carbon source, the nitrogen source and the mixed eutectic salt is 2.8-3.2:1. The molar concentration of the hydrochloric acid is 0.1 mol/l. Preferred Method: The ball milling is carried out at 500-650 revolutions/minute for 10-30 minutes using grinding ball with 5 mm diameter, which contains the grinding tank has a volume of 45 ml. The method specifically comprises (i) ball milling carbon source, and nitrogen source and mixed eutectic salt 550 revolutions/minute for 15 minutes using grinding ball with 5 mm diameter, which contains the grinding tank has a volume of 45 ml and mixing, where the total mass ratio of the salt is 3:1, the rotation speed is r /min, the grinding time is 15min, and the ball mill is mixed; (ii) high temperature carbonizing the mixture under vacuum and protective gas at a rate of 20 ml/minute, heating to 900 plus minus 10 degrees C, heat preserving for 3 hours from room temperature at a rate of 2-3 degrees C/minute, cooling to room temperature to obtain black nitrogen-containing carbon material, and (iii) washing by 0.1 mol/l hydrochloric acid solution, ultrasonically cleaning for half an hour and cleaning for 3 times.

The stripping solution is aqueous                solution, alkali metal salt or aqueous ammonium                salt solution. The aqueous solution is prepared by                introducing carbon dioxide into purified                water.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of                lithium carbonate with high efficiency in                environmentally-friendly manner.

PN CN109876831-A
TI Multiphase catalytic system used for synthesis of cyclic carbonate comprises hydrotalcite-like compound containing iodide, chloride, bromide and/or halogen as anion, and hydrotalcite-derived oxide obtained after high-temperature roasting.
AU LIU H
   WANG Y
   HAN B
   YANG G
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
GA 201955023V
AB    NOVELTY - A multiphase catalytic system comprises hydrotalcite-like compound or hydrotalcite-derived oxide. The hydrotalcite-like compound is hydrotalcite compound containing iodide, chloride, bromide and/or halogen as anion. The hydrotalcite-derived oxide is a product obtained after high-temperature roasting of hydrotalcite compound.
   USE - The multiphase catalytic system is used for synthesis of cyclic carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalytic system exhibits excellent activity and stability at less than 100 degrees C, and is suitable for larger scale industrial application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for synthesis of cyclic carbonate comprising reacting carbon dioxide and epoxy compound using multiphase catalytic system of hydrotalcite-like compound or hydrotalcite-derived oxide as catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrotalcite compound further comprises divalent cation and trivalent cation. The divalent cation is magnesium, iron, cobalt, copper, nickel, zinc and/or calcium. The trivalent cation is aluminum, chromium, gallium, indium, manganese and/or iron. Preferred Method: Roasting is done at 200-600 degrees C for 1-6 hours. Reacting is done at 40-140 degrees C, preferably 80 degrees C for 1-24 hours, preferably 12 hours in solvent-free condition or in solvent condition.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide, dichloromethane, toluene, N-methylpyrrolidone, cyclohexane, dioxane, ethanol and/or tetrahydrofuran.

TI Method for purifying industrial boiler flue gas involves discharging flue gas generated in industrial boiler through oxidation reaction by catalytic oxidation using methane oxidant carrier containing ceria.
AU LIANG Q
   LI J
   YUE T
   LIANG W
   HE H
   ZUO P
AE BEIJING MUNICIPAL INST LABOUR PROTECTION (BJKJ-C)
   UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 2019545684
AB    NOVELTY - A method for purifying industrial boiler flue gas involves discharging flue gas generated in industrial boiler through oxidation reaction by catalytic oxidation reaction using methane oxidant carrier comprising 0.5-1 wt.% platinum, 0.5-1 wt.% palladium, 5-20 wt.% ceria, 5-15 wt.% zirconium dioxide, 0.5-1.5 wt.% manganese and 62-80 wt.% titanium dioxide at 450-600 degrees C.
   USE - Method for purifying industrial boiler flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method contributes to energy saving, can improve the conversion rate of methane in the flue gas, reduce the emission of methane in the flue gas, and converts into harmless carbon dioxide gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for industrial boiler flue gas purification device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carbon monoxide oxidant carrier comprises 0.5-1 wt.% platinum, 0.5-1 wt.% ruthenium, 0.5-1 wt.% iridium, 15-20 wt.% ceria, 0.5-1.5 wt.% copper, 0.5-1.5 wt.% manganese and 74-80 wt.% titanium dioxide. The mass ratio of platinum, palladium, cerium oxide, zirconium dioxide and manganese in the methane oxidant carrier is (1-2):(1-2):(10-40):(10-30):(1-3), preferably 1:1:15:12:2. The mass ratio of platinum, ruthenium, iridium, cerium oxide, copper and manganese in the carbon monoxide oxidant carrier is (1-2):(1-2):(1-2):(30-40):(1-3):(1-3), preferably 1:2:1:36:3:2. Preferred Property: The carbon monoxide oxidant carrier is a spherical, sheet, honeycomb extruded or honeycomb coated carrier structure.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The methane oxidant carrier is a honeycomb extruded or honeycomb coated carrier structure.

PT P
PN CN109762589-A; CN109762589-B
TI Method for preparing hydrocarbon product, involves contacting reaction gas containing carbon monoxide with water in presence of carbon monoxide conversion catalyst followed by generating hydrocarbon product.
AU MA D
   XU Y
   LI J
AE UNIV PEKING (UYPK-C)
GA 2019464223
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrocarbon product preparation method involves contacting reaction gas containing carbon monoxide with water, in presence of carbon monoxide conversion catalyst, such that causing carbon monoxide in the reaction gas to undergo conversion reaction and generating hydrocarbon product, where the carbon monoxide conversion catalyst comprises mixture of water vapor migration reaction catalyst and Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction catalyst and the carbon monoxide conversion catalyst is composite catalyst supported with active metal capable of catalyzing water vapor migration reaction and active metal capable of catalyzing Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction.
   USE - Method for preparing hydrocarbon product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing hydrocarbon product by converting carbon monoxide to hydrocarbon product, even in absence of hydrogen and presence of other impurity gases and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon monoxide conversion catalyst comprises mixture of a water vapor migration reaction catalyst and Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction catalyst. The water vapor migration reaction catalyst is selected from carbide and/or nitride-supported active metal (platinum, palladium, gold, copper, nickel, cobalt, iridium and rhodium). The catalyst is carbon material loaded with one-component active metal (ruthenium, cobalt or iron) or Fischer-Tropsch catalyst supporting two-component active metal (M-iron, M-cobalt or M- ruthenium), where M is selected from platinum, palladium, rhodium, ruthenium and iridium. The carbon monoxide conversion catalyst is composite catalyst supported with active metal capable of catalyzing water vapor migration reaction and active metal capable of catalyzing Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction. The composite catalyst is selected from carbides, nitride and carbon material capable of catalyzing active metals (platinum, palladium, gold, copper, nickel, cobalt, iridium and rhodium) of water vapor migration reaction and material capable of catalyzing active metals ruthenium, cobalt and iron of the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction. The carbide is selected from silicon carbide, molybdenum carbide, iron carbide, nickel carbide, cobalt carbide and titanium carbide. The nitride is selected from silicon nitride, molybdenum nitride, iron nitride, cobalt nitride, nickel nitride and titanium nitride. The carbon material is selected from activated carbon, carbon black, coconut shell charcoal, acetylene black, carbon nanotubes, graphene and nanodiamond. The reaction gas does not contain hydrogen. Preferred Composition: The reaction gas further comprises 0.5-70 vol.% carbon dioxide.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

PN KR2019032099-A
TI Catalyst prepared by mixing catalyst comprising copper-zinc-aluminum oxide; methanol synthesis catalyst containing accelerator of catalyst comprising oxide of metals, and dehydration catalyst useful for direct synthesis of dimethyl ether.
AU CHO W
   LEE J
   YU H
   LEE M
AE BIO FRIENDS INC (BIOF-Non-standard)
   DAEHEUNG IND (DAEH-Non-standard)
GA 201930317H
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst prepared by mixing catalyst comprising copper-zinc-aluminum oxide; dimethylether synthesis catalyst containing accelerator of catalyst comprising oxide of at least two metals including gallium, magnesium, zirconium and calcium, and dehydration catalyst comprising aluminum phosphate and gamma -aluminum oxide, is claimed.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for direct synthesis of dimethyl ether (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst improves selectivity and yield of dimethyl ether.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for direct synthesis of dimethyl ether using the catalyst, synthesis gas in which hydrogen: carbon monoxide are mixed at ratio of 1:1.1-1.25, and gas containing carbon dioxide at flow ratio of 90:10-99:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper-zinc-aluminum oxide based catalyst and the accelerator are mixed at weight ratio of 6:4-8:2. The dimethylether synthesis catalyst and dehydration catalyst are mixed at weight ratio of 6:4-7:3. The accelerator includes mixture of oxides of at least two metals. The aluminum phosphate and gamma -aluminum oxide are mixed at molar ratio of 20:80-40:60. The specific surface area of aluminum phosphate is 320-350 m2/g. The specific surface area of gamma -aluminum oxide is 220-250 m2/g. The catalyst for direct synthesis of dimethyl ether has specific surface area of 100-120 m2/g before the synthesis of dimethyl ether; and greater than or equal to 130 m2/g after the dimethyl ether synthesis reaction. The selectivity of the dimethyl ether is greater than or equal to 55%. The yield of the dimethyl ether is greater than or equal to 50%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN WO2019036820-A1
TI Performing in vitro cell preconditioning process for obtaining mesenchymal stem cells, involves obtaining mesenchymal stem cells from tissues of donor animals, transporting and processing of biopsies, developing cultures by incubation.
AU CASTRO REBOREDO F O
   RODRIGUEZ ALVAREZ L
   SARAVIA RAMOS F E
   CABEZAS SALAZAR J G
   MANRIQUEZ OLAVE J R
AE UNIV CONCEPCION (UNVC-C)
GA 201919706A
AB    NOVELTY - Performing in vitro cell preconditioning process involves obtaining the MSCs from tissues of donor animals (biopsies), transporting and processing of biopsies, developing cultures by incubation at 39 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide, determining cell viability, analyzing MSC surface markers, preconditioning the previously described cells with substance P, PGE2, oxytocin and/or combinations and under conditions of atmospheric oxygen tension (normoxia) and/or hypoxia, confirming the acquisition of an immunomodulatory phenotype, and using the preconditioned cells directly in patients or frozen for future use.
   USE - Method for performing in vitro cell preconditioning process for obtaining mesenchymal stem cells with immumodulatory characteristics, and used in allogeneic therapies without being immune rejection, and is transversal to any species of human and non-human mammals (all claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The method for obtaining MSCs involves obtaining a biopsy from tissues of donor animals, such as fatty tissue, endometrial, bone marrow, gingiva, teeth, placenta, umbilical cord or adjacent tissues or components of it, but does not exclude others not mentioned, where the amount of tissue is 15-20 grams of original tissue, especially fatty and 1 gram for other sources, which are placed in a tube with earle's balanced salt solution medium (EBSS) at room temperature. The processing of the samples involves heating the biopsy for 10-15 minutes at 37 degrees C in a water bath in tubes containing EBSS, manually cutting tissues into small pieces of less than 1 mm and washing them five times in EBSS supplemented with 10 units/mL penicillin, 10 pg/mL streptomycin and 0.25 pg/mL fungizone antifungal. The processed samples are transferred to tubes with 1 ml DMEM/F12 culture medium supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum and 1-2.5 mg/ml collagenase type I and incubated for 8-18 hours at 39 degrees C. The in vitro cell preconditioning process for obtaining mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) involves determining the presence of surface markers for CD44, CD90 and MHC1 and obligatorily negative for CD34, CD45 and MHC2, and there may be more markers depending on the species through flow cytometry. The preconditioning of the cells involves culturing for 24 hours with prostaglandin E2 (PGE 2) at a final concentration of 3 micrometer, substance P (SP) at a final concentration of 100 nM, and/or oxytocin (OXT) at a final concentration of 10nM. Preferred Conditions: The cultivating under conditions of atmospheric oxygen tension (normoxia) and/or hypoxia contemplates the reduction of atmospheric oxygen tension from 20% to 1-5% of oxygen.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The system enables                  dental pulp mesenchymal stem cell to grow without                  adherence and capable of forming                  three-dimensional spherical three-dimensional                  structure. The system is compatible with the                  cell incubator. The density of the dental pulp                  mesenchymal stem cells is 4x 104-1x 106                  inoculated in a cell culture flask or a cell                  culture dish. Preferred Method: The system is                  capable of maintaining a constant rotating speed                  and oscillating in a certain space with a speed                  of 50-300 rotations/minute. The method further                  comprises (iii) amplifying the cell microsphere                  to the diameter of 40-200 mu m, moving out the                  culture system containing cell spheroids,                  centrifuging and collecting cell spheroid at                  300-800 rotations/minute; (iv) washing the                  collected cell spheroid by 0.9% physiological                  saline solution for 3-6 times, collecting the                  three-dimensional stem cell spheroid,                  re-suspending the precipitate in 0.1-30 ml                  volume; and (v) aspiring microspheres using 0.5-3                  mm pinhole syringe and injecting into the                  corresponding transplant target tissue.

PN CN108844084-A
TI Method for treating powder oven exhaust gas, involves maintaining temperature of powder oven exhaust gas, leaving space region of RTO ceramic as inlet of powder oven exhaust gas and directly extracting powder oven exhaust gas by fan.
AU LU Y
AE YANGZHOU HENGTONG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTI (YANG-Non-standard)
GA 201894271M
AB    NOVELTY - A powder oven exhaust gas treating method involves maintaining temperature of powder oven exhaust gas above 135 degrees C, leaving a space in 200-300 degrees C region of RTO ceramic as inlet of powder oven exhaust gas, directly extracting powder oven exhaust gas by a fan, passing through a flame arrester, placing to RTO ceramic 200-300 degrees C area, processing together with other exhaust gases entering RTO at 700-850 degrees C and breaking down powder oven exhaust gas into carbon dioxide and water.
   USE - Method for treating powder oven exhaust gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables treating powder oven exhaust gas with low energy consumption and easy maintenance.

AU WANG T

   FANG M
   CEN J
   WANG Q

   GAO X
   LUO Z
   NI M
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2018919182
AB    NOVELTY - Producing aluminum-calcium carbonate composite cementitious material, comprises (1) mixing predetermined contents of red mud with calcium and silicon, performing thermal activation, cooling, washing with water, and drying to obtain heat activated red mud, and (2) mixing the heat-activated red mud, cement and auxiliary cementing materials, solidifying with water, and strengthening carbon dioxide mineralization to obtain aluminum-calcium carbonate composite cementitious material.

PN CN108782917-A
TI Seafood candy comprises sea cucumber, prepared by cleaning sea cucumber, ripening, adding to sugar water, and drying.
AU XU J
AE XU J (XUJJ-Individual)
GA 201892968B
AB    NOVELTY - A seafood candy comprises 5-85 wt.% sea cucumber.
   USE - Seafood candy.
   ADVANTAGE - The candy is convenient for patients, mothers and children that does not want to eat sea cucumber, and can be added with various seasonings to make different flavors. The method is simple and short, removes astringency of sea cucumber, keep the nutrients of sea cucumbers, enable consumers to visually see the quality of sea cucumbers and eat them with confidence, uses pure water or warm water with low oxygen content and carbon dioxide as pressure medium, and reduces the degree of oxidation of sea cucumbers and amount of oxygen free radicals which is more beneficial to human health.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the seafood candy comprising cleaning sea cucumber, ripening, adding to sugar water, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method further comprises heating and aging ripened sea cucumber at 1-10 kg/cm2, adding to pure water or warm water, and pressurizing. The soaking in sugar water is done for 2-3 times every 5-300 minutes.
