TI Gas-evolution inhibitor used for positive electrode for electrical storage device, comprises carbonaceous material, and titanate of alkali metal and/or titanate of alkali earth metal.
AU RYOSUKE S
   KEIICHI W
AE TAYCA CORP (TKAK-C)
   TAYCA CORP (TKAK-C)
GA 2018878261
AB    NOVELTY - A gas-evolution inhibitor comprises a carbonaceous material, and titanate of an alkali metal and/or titanate of an alkali earth metal.
   USE - Gas-evolution inhibitor is used for positive electrode for electrical storage device (all claimed) e.g. lithium-ion battery, lithium-ion capacitor and electrical double layer capacitor.
   ADVANTAGE - The inhibitor has excellent water absorption resistance, and can inhibit generation of gas such as carbon dioxide gas, hydrogen gas and fluoride gas, and provides positive electrode having excellent electroconductivity. The electrical storage device having the positive electrode has excellent rate characteristics and cycle characteristics, and suppressed electrical resistance. The change of structure of titanate of alkali metal or titanate of alkali earth metal can be prevented, even under high-humidity conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alkali metal is lithium, sodium and/or potassium. The alkaline-earth metal is magnesium, calcium, strontium and/or barium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The gas-evolution inhibitor contains 0.5-10 wt.% carbonaceous material based on amount of titanate of alkali metal and/or titanate of alkali earth metal.

PN WO2018195582-A1; AR111415-A1; AU2018259149-A1
TI Herbicidal formulation used for e.g. controlling unwanted vegetation in sugarcane crop, comprises imazapic in form of urea or ammonium salt, hexazinone and solvent, and is free of alkylene carbonate and methanol.
AU HORSFIELD A
   VAUGHAN P H
AE ADAMA AUSTRALIA PTY LTD (CNCC-C)
   ADAMA AUSTRALIA PTY LTD (CNCC-C)
GA 2018862307
AB    NOVELTY - Herbicidal formulation in the form of a soluble concentrate comprises (a) an imazapic in the form of urea and/or ammonium salt, (b) an hexazinone and (c) a solvent, where the formulation is substantially free of alkylene carbonate and methanol.
   USE - The herbicidal formulation is useful for controlling unwanted vegetation in one or more crops, preferably in sugarcane crop and controlling weeds in wastelands, around buildings, along fence lines or along railway lines (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The formulation is convenient to handle and safe to use, avoids issues associated with dry herbicide products, is stored in standard high density polyethylene capped bottle without any ventilation, remains stable for extended periods of storage without carbon dioxide emission and has improved shelf life.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The formulation comprises imazapic and hexazinone in a ratio of 1:4-1:5, preferably 1:2. The formulation comprises 25 g/l imazapic in the form of urea and/or ammonium salt and 125 g/l hexazinone. Preferred Components: The solvent is chosen from 1-methylpyrrolidin-2-one, 1-butylpyrrolidin-2-one and their mixtures. The formulation comprises one or more buffering agents. The buffering agent is chosen from potassium dihydrogen phosphate, acetic acid, citric acid and their mixture.
   ACTIVITY - Herbicide. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Application of the formulation is in the form of a soluble concentrate (claimed). No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN108706807-A; CN108706807-B
TI Processing of mercaptoacetic acid production wastewater involves mixing catalytic oxidation aid containing soluble manganese salt with mercaptoacetic acid production wastewater, preheating, oxidizing, reacting, and desalting.
AU WANG J
   ZHANG G
   CUI H
   YANG M
   LIU Q
   ZHANG Y
   WANG Y
AE SHANDONG ZHANHUA JINJIALI CHEM TECHNOLOG (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG JIANLONG CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201886132D
AB    NOVELTY - Processing of mercaptoacetic acid production wastewater involves mixing catalytic oxidation aid containing soluble manganese salt with mercaptoacetic acid production wastewater, preheating in a bubbling reactor filled with silica-supported neodymium oxide porous solid catalyst, oxidizing using an oxidant to oxidize organic matter in the wastewater to hydrogen oxide, carbon dioxide and divalent sulfate ion, reacting, and performing flash evaporation and concentration for desalting.
   USE - Processing of mercaptoacetic acid production wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively removes organic matter in the wastewater due to the usage of soluble manganese salt and silica-supported neodymium oxide porous solid catalyst having excellent synergistic effect, and reduces COD content and solid salt in the wastewater. The separated water can be reused and the solid salt can also be used as a by-product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar concentration of divalent manganese ion in the mercaptoacetic acid production wastewater is 0.2-0.5 mmol/L. Preferred Properties: The silica-supported neodymium oxide porous solid catalyst is in the form of pellets having a porosity of 30%. The mercaptoacetic acid production wastewater has a salt content of 17.0-22.0 wt.%, chemical oxygen demand (COD) content of 15000-20000 mg/L, and pH of 0.5-5.0. Preferred Components: The oxidant comprises oxygen or air. The salt in the mercaptoacetic acid production wastewater is sodium chloride.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)

TI Superalloy target used for forming components e.g. blade and vane of industrial gas turbine and aircraft engine, has polycrystalline structure of random grain orientation having average grain size and porosity less than preset value.
AU POLCIK P
   RAMM J
AE OERLIKON SURFACE SOLUTIONS PFAEFFIKON AG (OERL-C)
   PLANSEE COMPOSITE MATERIALS GMBH (METX-C)
   OERLIKON SURFACE SOLUTIONS PFAFFIKON AG (OERL-C)

GA 201884013E
AB    NOVELTY - A superalloy target has a polycrystalline structure of random grain orientation. The polycrystalline structure has an average grain size of less than 20 mu m and a porosity of less than 10%.
   USE - Superalloy target is used for forming components e.g. blade and vane of industrial gas turbine and aircraft engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The superalloy target has excellent mechanical strength, resistance to thermal creep, and durability, and provides engine with excellent robustness and stability, even when operated in a harsh environment, high efficiency, and reduced fuel-consuming property and carbon dioxide emission, by simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of superalloy target.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The superalloy is a cobalt-based superalloy containing cobalt as a main metallic component and an element(s) chosen from carbon, chromium, tungsten, nickel, titanium, aluminum, iridium, and tantalum as alloying element, a nickel-based superalloy containing nickel as main metallic component and an element(s) chosen from chromium, iron, cobalt, molybdenum, tungsten, tantalum, aluminum, titanium, zirconium, niobium, rhenium, yttrium, vanadium, carbon, boron, and hafnium as alloying element, or an aluminide-based alloy chosen from titanium-aluminum-based superalloy, nickel aluminide, iron aluminide, hafnium aluminide, chromium aluminide, niobium aluminide, tantalum aluminide, platinum aluminide, and zirconium aluminide. The content of fee crystal structure in the superalloy is 80-99%. The superalloy target has different intermetallic phases with similar lattice constants, and comprises precipitates.

AU ZHANG L
AE NANTONG TAIHE FOODS CO LTD (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 2018805028
AB    NOVELTY - Natural preservative comprises 20-30 pts. wt. onion extract, 5-10 pts. wt. tea polyphenol and 70-80 pts. wt. sodium propionate.
   USE - The preservative is useful for preserving food.
   ADVANTAGE - The preservative adjusting water activity of food, inhibiting growth of microorganism and releases antibacterial gas which maintains anaerobic and bacteriostatic environment in packaged food, and is harmless to human body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of preparing onion extract involving cleaning onion, freezing, crushing, adding water, mixing uniformly, adding citric acid to adjust pH value to 5, heating at 80-90 degrees C, passing carbon dioxide continuously, stirring continuously for 1-2 hours , filtering to obtain filtrate and distilling filtrate to obtain onion extract.

PN WO2018129621-A1; CA3049603-A1; US2019390355-A1
TI Manufacture of carbonaceous anode for electrolysis            cell for production of aluminum, involves contacting            core particles with boron-containing solution, mixing            boron-impregnated coke particles with coal tar pitch to            form paste and forming.
AU ALAMDARI H
   ISHAK R
   LAROCHE G
   FAFARD M
   PICARD D
   ZIEGLER D
AE UNIV LAVAL (ULVL-C)
   ALCOA USA CORP (ALUM-C)
   UNIV LAVAL (ULVL-C)
   ALCOA USA CORP (ALUM-C)
GA 201856546V
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of carbonaceous anode involves                contacting coke particles with a boron-containing                solution to obtain boron-impregnated coke                particles, mixing the impregnated coke particles                with coal tar pitch to form an anode paste, and                forming a green anode with the anode paste.
   USE - Manufacturing of carbonaceous anode used for                electrolysis cell for production of aluminum                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides anode with good                resistivity to air and carbon dioxide oxidation.                The process provides less dusting of the anode and                improves integrity of anode throughout its                life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) carbonaceous anode; and
   (2) process for pre-treating core                particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The boron-impregnated                  coke particles comprise fraction of coke                  particles (a) comprising 20 wt.% or less fine                  coke particles based on the total weight of the                  anode paste and fraction of coke particles (b)                  comprising coarser coke particles. The                  boron-containing solution is water-based boron                  containing solution comprising at least one of                  boron oxide and boric acid dissolved in water.                  The carbonaceous anode comprises at least coke                  particles (a), coke particles (b) and coal tar                  pitch. Preferred Property: The fine coke                  particles have D99 smaller than 200 US mesh                  particle size. Preferred Composition: The boron                  concentration in the anode is 300 ppm, preferably                  150 ppm.

   LI H

   USE - Method for in vitro culturing retinal pigment epithelial cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The cell can be passaged to P8 generation and be passaged to P4 generation without added additives, has proportion of cells with positive CD133 (i.e. CD133+ retinal pigment epithelial cells) is 99%, solves lack of surgical transplant donor and reduces ethical dispute. PN TW201804909-A; TW604797-B1
TI Carbonated drink and preparing method thereof includes dissolving the main ingredient, pectin and hydrophobic constituent.
AU TSAI A
   LO Y
AE UNI-PRESIDENT ENTERPRISES CORP (SNPH-C)
GA 201833472P
AB    NOVELTY - The invention relates to a carbonated drink and a preparing method thereof. The preparing method includes dissolving the main ingredient, pectin and hydrophobic constituent in water respectively to prepare a main ingredient solution, a pectin solution and a hydrophobic constituent solution; mixing the main ingredient solution, the pectin solution and the hydrophobic constituent solution to prepare a mixture solution; and infusing the carbon dioxide gas to obtain the carbonated drink. The pectin is ranged of 0.02%-0.06% of the carbonated drink, and is dissolved at 80 DEG C ~88 DEG C for 5-8 minutes in water. The hydrophobic constituent is dissolved at 60 DEG C ~70 DEG C for 3 seconds-1minute in water. The carbonated drink preparing by the method has good emulsion stability, and an oil ring may not generate and float up in long time storage.

PT P
PN CN114530212-A
TI Predicting and evaluating nephrotoxicity of chemical components in traditional Chinese medicine involves collecting adverse drug reaction information from western medicine adverse reaction database, and using Pubchem ito download sdf files of compound with nephrotoxic adverse reaction records.
AU ZHANG Z
   HOU H
   PENG B
   CHEN T
   GAO Y
   SONG L
   ZHANG G
   LI H
AE INST CHINESE MATERIA MEDICA CHINA ACAD T (CACM-C)
GA 202274549D
AB    NOVELTY - Predicting and evaluating nephrotoxicity of chemical components in traditional Chinese medicine involves collecting adverse drug reaction information from the western medicine adverse reaction database, and adverse drug reaction information includes compound with nephrotoxic adverse reaction record and compound without nephrotoxic adverse reaction records, and finding out known nephrotoxic Chinese medicine component by consulting the literature. The Pubchem is used to download sdf files of compound with nephrotoxic adverse reaction records, compounds without nephrotoxic adverse reaction records collected from the western medicine adverse reaction database, and sdf files of traditional Chinese medicine compounds with known nephrotoxicity collected by literature, and then mathematical model training set of these compounds are established, and Mold2 software is used to calculate the molecular structure descriptors of each compound in the training set to obtain descriptor matrix data.
   USE - Method for predicting and evaluating nephrotoxicity of chemical components in traditional Chinese medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method predicts and evaluates nephrotoxicity of chemical components in traditional Chinese medicine, which utilizes toxicity substitution method to combine accurate prediction with effective evaluation, performs evaluation in more accurate and more comprehensive manner, reduces and prevents adverse reaction of traditional Chinese medicine, and improves safety of clinical medication.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Predicting and evaluating nephrotoxicity of chemical components in traditional Chinese medicine involves collecting adverse drug reaction information from the western medicine adverse reaction database, and adverse drug reaction information includes compound with nephrotoxic adverse reaction record and compound without nephrotoxic adverse reaction records, and finding out known nephrotoxic Chinese medicine component by consulting the literature. The Pubchem is used to download sdf files of compound with nephrotoxic adverse reaction records, compounds without nephrotoxic adverse reaction records collected from the western medicine adverse reaction database, and sdf files of traditional Chinese medicine compounds with known nephrotoxicity collected by literature, and then mathematical model training set of these compounds are established. The descriptors and optimal early prediction model of traditional Chinese medicine nephrotoxicity are established by using R software for descriptors, removing the descriptors whose calculated values are constant in more than 90% of the total compounds and then eliminating the multivariate correlations between the remaining descriptors. The optimal prediction model is used to predict nephrotoxicity of the chemical components contained in toxic traditional Chinese medicine recorded in Pharmacopoeia of People's Republic of China, and chemical components with nephrotoxicity are preliminarily predicted. The method involves evaluation HRGEC cells seeded in a 96-well black translucent bottom culture plate at a density of 8000 cells/well, and cultured at 37degreesC and 5% carbon dioxide to logarithmic phase, where predicted results obtained by the optimal prediction model are added during the evaluation, and experimental group is formed by treating HRGEC cells with nephrotoxic chemical components for 24 hours. The blank group is added, where in blank group the cells are inoculated without adding drugs for 48 hours, and original medium is discarded, and the cells are washed with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), and the washing is repeated twice. The Hoechst 33342, Green Reagents and Mito Tracker (RTM: Red-fluorescent dye) Red CMXRos dyes are diluted to appropriate concentration of dye mixture with Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum according to the instructions. The cells are added to cell well plates of experimental group and blank group, respectively in a 37degreesC and 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 45 minutes, and then the dye mixture is discarded. The cells are washed twice with PBS buffer, and 100muL of PBS buffer is added to each well. The Gen5 software is used to collect and analyze images, where 20x objective lens is selected, and first channel wavelength is 350/461nm to detect Hoechst 33342-labeled nuclei and number of cells and DNA content are recorded in the nucleus, and second wavelength is 485/520nm to detect ROS labeled by Green Reagents, and third channel wavelength is 579/599nm to detect MitoTracker Red CMXRos-labeled mitochondrial membrane potential. The Gen5 software is used to analyze the fluorescence intensity of the images, where number of cells are number of fluorescence in first channel, and DNA content in the nucleus is fluorescence intensity of the first channel, ROS is fluorescence intensity of the second channel in the cytoplasm and nucleus, and mitochondrial membrane potential is the fluorescence intensity in the cytoplasm. The Hoechst 33342 pass through the cell membrane and bind to living cells, so it is used for living cell labeling. The CellROX (RTM: Fluorogenic probe) oxidative stress reagents are used to detect reactive oxygen species ROS in living cells, and when it has no fluorescence or very weak fluorescence, and is once oxidized then it emits strong fluorescence in the reduced state. The excessive accumulation of oxidative free radicals leads to lipid peroxidation in the cell membrane, which changes integrity and permeability of the cell membrane that leads to the breakage of the protein polypeptide chain in cell, and induces gene mutation leading to apoptosis. The Mito Tracker Red CMXRos dye specifically label biologically active mitochondria in cells and detect mitochondrial membrane potential as a cell-permeable X-rosamine derivative. The ROS promote the opening of mitochondrial membrane permeability transfer pores, and it leads to an increase in concentration of calcium ions in mitochondria, breaking calcium ion homeostasis, blocking the transmission of electrons in the respiratory chain, terminating ATP production, and changing energy metabolism which leads to mitochondrial dysfunction and induces apoptosis. The release of cytochrome C in the mitochondrial intermembrane space activates the related caspase protein to activate the downstream apoptosis pathway, which leads to apoptosis, and then cell membrane integrity and permeability, mitochondrial membrane potential and oxidative stress damage are used as indicators for high-content detection of nephrotoxicity of test substances. The SPSS Statistics 21 statistical software is used for statistical analysis of data in each group, and one-way analysis of variance is used for statistical analysis between multiple groups, where P less than 0.05 indicates that the difference is statistically significant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Condition: The number of iterations is set to 10 in the construction of the prediction model.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P31 (Diagnosis, surgery (A61B).); S05 (Electrical Medical Equipment); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC B11-C07B3; B14-S18; B04-A09; B04-A08; B04-A10; B04-A98; B11-C11; B12-K04E; D05-H09; P31-A05; S05-G02G3; T01-N01A2; T01-J13A; T01-J06A; T01-J05B4P; T01-J05A2; T01-J04E; T01-J04B2
IP A61B-005/00; G06Q-010/04; G16B-020/50; G16B-005/00; G16C-020/30; G16C-020/70

PN CN113945715-A
TI Detecting donor-specific IL-21 and IFN-gamma comprises e.g. extracting donor peripheral blood mononuclear cells and recipient peripheral blood mononuclear cells, freezing, coating reaction plate and sealing, and thawing donor and recipient peripheral blood mononuclear cells.
AU BAI Y
   LI Y
   YAN L
AE UNIV SICHUAN WEST CHINA HOSPITAL (USCU-C)
GA 2022204032
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting donor-specific IL-21 and IFN-gamma comprises (S1) extracting donor peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMC) and recipient PBMC, and freezing, (S2) coating the reaction plate and sealing, thawing the donor PBMC and recipient PBMC in step (S1), and (S3) coating and sealing the reaction plate, refusing the donor PBMC and recipient PBMC in step (S1), counting the cells as follows: adding 3 ml of cell culture medium, staining with trypan blue, counting under a microscope, irradiating 40Gy to inactivate donor cells for 20 minutes, centrifuging at 2000 revolutions per minute for 5 minutes after irradiation, adding 100 mul diluted horseradish peroxidase-labeled streptavidin, incubating at 37degrees Celsius for 2 hours, rinsing using deionized water for 5 times, naturally drying the reaction plate, and counting the number of spots with a bioreader.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting donor-specific IL-21 and IFN-gamma, which is used in a product for evaluating the sensitization degree of the immune system of organ transplant recipients before and after surgery or predicting the probability of acute and chronic rejection after kidney transplantation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can realize the detection of recipient cells secreting donor-specific cytokines; can detect recipient IL21 or IFN-gamma secreting cells activated by donor antigen stimulation after organ transplantation; predicts the occurrence of acute and chronic rejection after kidney transplantation; and dynamic monitoring can help to adjust the individualized medication of immunosuppressants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting donor-specific IL-21 and IFN-gamma comprises (S1) extracting donor peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMC) and recipient PBMC, and freezing, (S2) (1) coating the reaction plate and sealing, thawing the donor PBMC and recipient PBMC in step (S1), the cells are counted as follows: adding 3 ml cell culture medium, staining with trypan blue, counting under a microscope, irradiating 40Gy to inactivate donor cells for 20 minutes, centrifuging at 2000 revolutions per minute for 5 minutes after irradiation, and then using cell culture medium to correct the final concentration of donor or recipient cells to 3x106/ml, (2) adding stimulator, donor or recipient cell suspension to the reaction plate of step (1), as follows: (A) incubating 100 mul 3x105 recipient cells and 100 mul cell culture medium, (B) 100 mul 3x105 recipient cells and 100 mul 3x105 inactivated donor cells, (C) 100 mul 3x105 recipient cells and 100 mul 3x105 3rdparty inactivated donor cells, (D) 100 mul 1x105 recipient cells and 100 mul ICE 10x, (E) 100 mul 5x104 recipient cells and 100 mul SEB 100 ng/ml, (F) 100 mul 5x104 recipient cells and 100 mul PHA 5 mug/ml (G) 100 mul cell culture medium and 100 mul inactivated donor cells, and (H) 100 mul of cell culture medium and 100 mul inactivated donor cells at 37degreesC and 5% carbon dioxide for 44 hours, (3) 9 ml phosphate-buffered saline-I, 1 ml 10x dilution buffer, 100 mul biotin-labeled detection antibody, washing for 5 times using 250 mul phosphate-buffered saline-I, adding 100 mul diluted biotin-labeled detection antibody, incubating at room temperature for 2 hours, washing the plate for 5 times on both sides with phosphate-buffered saline-I, diluting horseradish peroxidase-labeled streptavidin, adding 100 mul diluted horseradish peroxidase-labeled streptavidin, incubating for 1 hour at room temperature, blocking, washing the plate 5 times using phosphate-buffered saline-I on both sides, adding 100 mul of AEC substrate solution, incubating at room temperature for 30 minutes in the dark, washing the plate, naturally drying the reaction plate, and counting the number of spots with a bioreader, and (S3) coating and sealing the reaction plate, refusing the donor PBMC and recipient PBMC in step (S1), counting the cells as follows: adding 3 ml of cell culture medium, staining with trypan blue, counting under a microscope, irradiating 40Gy to inactivate donor cells for 20 minutes, centrifuging at 2000 revolutions per minute for 5 minutes after irradiation, then correcting the final concentration of donor or recipient cells to 3x106/ml with cell culture medium, (2) adding stimulator, donor or recipient cell suspension to the reaction plate of step (1), as follows: (A) 100 mul 3x105 recipient cells and 100 mul cell culture medium, (B) 100 mul 3x105 recipient cells and 100 mul 3x105 inactivated donor cells, (C) 100 mul 3x105 recipient cells and 100 mul 3x105 3rdparty inactivated donor cells, (D) 100 mul 5x104 recipient cells and 100 mul ICE 10x, (E) 100 mul 2.5x104 recipient cells and 100 mul SEB 100 ng/ml, (F) 100 mul 2.5x104 recipient cells and 100 mul PHA 5 mug/ml, (G) 100 mul cell culture medium and 100 mul inactivated donor cells, and (H) 100 mul of cell culture medium and 100 mul inactivated donor cells at 37degreesC and 5% carbon dioxide for 20 hours, and (3) diluting biotin-labeled detection antibody: 9 ml phosphate-buffered saline-I, 1 ml dilution buffer (10x), and 100 mul biotin-labeled detection antibody, washing for 2 times with 250 mul phosphate-buffered saline-I and 5 times with 250 mul phosphate-buffered saline-Tween, adding 100 mul diluted biotin-labeled detection antibody and incubating at room temperature for 2 hours, washing the plate 5 times with phosphate-buffered saline-I on both sides, diluting horseradish peroxidase-labeled streptavidin, adding 100 mul diluted horseradish peroxidase-labeled streptavidin, incubating at 37degreesC for 2 hours, blocking, washing the plate for 5 times using phosphate-buffered saline-Tween on both sides, adding 100 mul AEC substrate solution and incubating at room temperature for 25 minutes in the dark, rinsing using deionized water for 5 times, naturally drying the reaction plate, and counting the number of spots with a bioreader.

PN EP3611249-A1
TI Hoping wort or beer, comprises determining hop dose, mixing hops with wort or beer to be hopped, adding hop in single dose or in partial doses, preferably two, three or four partial doses, and homogenizing the mixture.
AU BASTGEN N
AE ZIEMANN HOLVRIEKA GMBH (CIMC-C)
GA 2020144961
AB    NOVELTY - Hoping a wort or a beer, comprises: (a) determining a hop dose, where the hops are given in amount, type and time of the hops; (b) mixing the hops determined in step (a) with the wort or beer to be hopped, preferably adding the hops determined in step (a) to the wort to be hopped or the beer to be hopped, and the hops can be given in a single dose or in partial doses, preferably two, three or four partial doses; and (c) homogenizing the mixture obtained in step (b). The hops are added at a single addition point or at a single point in the wort or beer preparation process if the hop is added in a single dose, or added at several addition points or at several points in the wort or beer preparation process or at one addition point. At least one of the parameters are provided to determining the administration of hops, which include: e.g. parameters relating to the brewing water used to produce the wort or beer.
   USE - The method is useful for hoping wort or beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves or optimizes the administration of hops in wort or beer preparation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Hoping a wort or a beer, comprises: (a) determining a hop dose, where the hops are given in amount, type and time of the hops, in which the amount is expressed in terms of the amount of hops per volume of the wort or beer to be hopped or the mass of alpha -acids contained in the hops per volume of the wort or beer to be hopped, the type of hop administration includes natural hops, umbel hops, hop pellets, preferably hop pellets T45, or hop pellets T90, hop extract, preferably carbon dioxide extract or ethanol extract, hop products, preferably reduced or pre-isomerized hop products, and the time of the hops refers to the course of the wort or beer preparation process; (b) mixing the hops determined in step (a) with the wort or beer to be hopped, preferably adding the hops determined in step (a) to the wort to be hopped or the beer to be hopped, and the hops can be given in a single dose or in partial doses, preferably two, three or four partial doses; and (c) homogenizing the mixture obtained in step (b). The hops are added at a single addition point or at a single point in the wort or beer preparation process if the hop is added in a single dose, or added at several addition points or at several points in the wort or beer preparation process or at one addition point or at one point in the wort or beer preparation process if the hops are given in partial doses. At least one of the parameters are provided to determining the administration of hops, which include; parameters relating to the brewing water used to produce the wort or beer; pH value, conductivity, calcium, calcium hardness, magnesium, magnesium hardness, carbonate hardness, non-carbonate hardness, total hardness, residual alkalinity; parameters relating to the malt used to produce the wort or beer; viscosity, water content, fine meal extract, dry air, fine meal extract, dry matter, floury flour, fruit glass, vitreous pH, saccharification, Hartong VZT 45 degrees C, protein content; parameters relating to the wort intended for isomerization or hopping; pH value, original wort content, volume of the wort, mass of the hop product, free amino-nitrogen, total soluble nitrogen, coagulable nitrogen, magnesium, calcium, polyphenols, evaporation rate, particle size distribution (median value), solids content, particle load, turbidity, total polyphenols, magnesium sulfate precipitable nitrogen; soluble nitrogen, protein content, Kolbach number, grist sorting, thiobarbituric acid number, dimethyl sulfide, dimethyl sulfide precursor, beta -glucan, anthocyanogens, zinc, oxalate, manganese, pre-cooking or preheating holding time, cooking time, holding time; parameters relating to the lauter system; mash filter or lauter tun or turntable filtration; a refining system in combination with the mill system; parameters relating to the wort heating system; inner cooker, outer cooker, holding device; pumping when heating up on the way to the cooking vessel, time, temperature, wort parameters; pH value, original wort content, pH value, magnesium content, protein content; parameters relating to the hops intended for hops; type of hops, preferably natural hops, cone hops, hop pellets, preferably hop pellets T45 or hop pellets T90, hop extract, preferably carbon dioxide extract or ethanol extract, hop products, preferably reduced or pre-isomerized hop products, alpha -acid content, content of aroma components, results of previous individual analyzes, storage conditions; parameters relating to the additives, preferably the amount added, concentration in the wort or in the beer and/or type; defoamer, mineral acid, mineral salts, carboxylic acids, raw material additives, sugar, colored beer, hop resins; parameters relating to the separation device; and temperature of the wort during clarification, dwell time, clarification efficiency or solids content of the wort after clarification, change in particle load or measurement, hop addition during clarification. The hops can be given at a single addition point or at a single point in the wort or beer preparation process if the hops are given in a single dose. The hops are added at several addition points or at several points in the wort or beer preparation process or at one addition point or at one point in the wort or beer preparation process if the hops are given in partial doses. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) use of at least one of the parameters for determining the administration of hops, preferably for the hop of wort or beer, preferably for hopping wort or beer;
   (2) wort or beer produced by the above method; and
   (3) a control device.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110771425-A
TI Three-dimensional cultivation method for Morchella by mixing chestnut calcareous and gray calcareous soils, transferring into cultivation tank, culturing Morchella in culture medium, then culturing strain on tank and harvesting Morchella.
AU SA W
   WANG L
   SHANG Q
   LIANG J
AE UNIV QINGHAI (UYQH-C)
GA 202015814D
AB    NOVELTY - Three-dimensional cultivation method for Morchella involves mixing chestnut calcareous soil and gray calcareous soil, adding quicklime, transferring into a cultivation tank, culturing Morchella spp. in mother seed culture medium, filling polypropylene strain bags with sawdust, bran, humus soil, sucrose, superphosphate and gypsum, uniformly mixing, sterilizing, inoculating the prepared mother seed, culturing, crushing the cultivated stock, evenly spreading strain on the soil surface of the cultivation tank, covering the cultivation surface with a layer of cultivation soil, adjusting the air temperature, air humidity and carbon dioxide concentration, using the cross sprayer installed above the culture tank to control the relative soil moisture, culturing, piercing holes on one side of the nutrition bag, adjusting the LED light intensity, performing illumination, ventilating and harvesting Morchella.
   USE - The method is useful for three-dimensional cultivation of Morchella.
   ADVANTAGE - The cultivation method avoids limitation of natural factors, realizes the large-scale annual production of morel, make Morchella annual production stable and efficient and increases the yield per unit area of Morchella up to 262 g/m, which is 12.5-40% (125-150 kg/acre) more than the traditional field cultivation method. The cultivation method is simple, saves cost and labor, and uses the three-dimensional cultivation rack which has a simple structure and convenient operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Three-dimensional cultivation method for Morchella involves (a) sieving chestnut calcareous soil and gray calcareous soil with a pH greater than 7 through 5 mm sieve to remove weeds and stones, mixing the soil with quicklime in a ratio of 100:1, adding water to adjust the water content at 50% and then transferring into a cultivation tank on prefabricated three-dimensional cultivation rack, (b) (i) purchasing and storing mother seed of Morchella spp., adding 200 g potato slices into water, boiling for 20 minutes while stirring to prevent sticky pots, filtering with four layers of gauze, removing potato pieces, adding 20 g agar and 20 g glucose to the filtrate, heating until the agar is dissolved, adding water to make the volume to 1000 ml, keeping the pH natural, aliquoting in clean test tubes, sterilizing at 121 degrees C for 30 minutes, leaving the seed culture medium in a clean bench to obtain a slanted surface, inoculating stored Mel-6 primary mother seed and incubating at 22 degrees C for 7 days in constant temperature and light, (ii) filling 12 cmx 24 cm polypropylene strain bags with 75% sawdust, 20% bran, 2% humus soil, 1 3 g sucrose, 3 g superphosphate and 3 g gypsum, uniformly mixing, adjusting the water content to 60-65% to complete the configuration of the original seed medium, sterilizing original seed medium at 121 degrees C for 1.5 hours, drying, transferring into ultra clean bench, inoculating the prepared mother seed in original seed medium in a clean bench and incubating at 22 degrees C in a constant temperature and dark place for 2 weeks until the mycelium is full of the culture bag, (iii) filling 12 cmx 24 cm polypropylene strain bags with 75% sawdust, 20% bran, 2% humus soil, 1 3 g sucrose, 3 g superphosphate and 3 g gypsum, uniformly mixing, adjusting the water content to 60-65% to complete the configuration of the culture medium, sterilizing culture medium at 121 degrees C for 1.5 hours, drying, transferring into ultra clean bench, crushing the cultivated stock to small pieces, inoculating the pieces in culture medium in a clean bench and incubating at 22 degrees C in a constant temperature and dark place for 2 weeks until the mycelium is full of the culture bag, (c) crushing the cultivated strains, evenly spreading 250-400 g strain on the soil surface of the cultivation tank and covering the cultivation surface with a layer of cultivation soil having a thickness of 2-4 cm, (d) filling 50% wood chips, 40% husk and 10% humus soil in a 12 cmx 24 cm polypropylene strain bag, then adding 3 g quick lime, 6 g gypsum, 0.3 g potassium dihydrogen phosphoate and 90-120 g wheat, adjusting the water content to 60-65%, sterilizing at 121 degrees C for 1.5 hours, and cooling the nutritional bag, (e) (i) adjusting the air temperature of the mushroom house at 18-20 degrees C, air humidity at 50-70% and carbon dioxide concentration at 350-450 ppm, using the cross sprayer installed above the culture tank to control the relative soil moisture at 50-55% and culturing for 7-15 days in the dark, (ii) piercing 9-18 holes on one side of the nutrition bag using a nail plate 7-20 days after sowing, staggering 15-16 bags in each culture tank with the hole faces facing down at intervals of 20-30 cm, (iii) adjusting the relative water content of the soil to saturation, controlling temperature of the mushroom room at 18-22 degrees C during the day and 8-10 degrees C at night, maintaining relative humidity of the air at 60-70%, adjusting the LED light intensity at 600-1000 Lx, performing illumination for 12-14 hours and ventilating once in every 8 hours for 20 minutes and (iv) when the mycelium gradually subsides, observing the gray-brown translucent primordium that appears on the soil surface and then enters the mushrooming stage, adjusting the relative water content of the soil to 40, controlling temperature of the mushroom room at 15-20 degrees C, maintaining relative humidity of the air at 70-85%, adjusting the LED light intensity at 600-1000 Lx, performing illumination for 12-14 hours and ventilating once in every 8 hours for 20 minutes and (f) when a portion of the Morchella cover length reaches 4-6 cm, and color changes from dark gray to brown, harvesting Morchella 10-15 days after the formation of the primordium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Device: The culture tank has height of 15 cm, width of 60 cm and length of 200 cm. The spacing between each layer of the tank is 35 cm. Preferred Organism: The mother species of Morchella is a Mel-6 primary mother species by the State Key Laboratory of Sanjiangyuan Ecology and Plateau Agriculture and Animal Husbandry of Qinghai University.

PN CN110567924-A; CN110567924-B
TI Preparing graphene-rare earth composite material comprises e.g. weighing citric acid, histidine, ethylenediamine, and folic acid, mixing, adding deionized water, ultrasonic dissolving to obtain reaction solution.
AU LI Z
   JIANG Y
   LI R
   SUN X
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 2019A6566L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene-rare earth composite material comprises e.g. (1) weighing citric acid, histidine, ethylenediamine, and folic acid, mixing, adding deionized water, ultrasonic dissolving to obtain reaction solution, placing the reaction solution in an oven, reacting in oven, directly obtaining histidine quantum dot solid product, preparing histidine quantum dot solid product into aqueous solution; (2) mixing yttrium(III) chloride solution, ytterbium(III) chloride solution, and erbium(III) chloride solution, adding histidine quantum dot aqueous solution obtained in step (1), and stirring at room temperature for half hour; (3) dropwise adding sodium fluoride solution to the solution obtained in step (2) under stirring, and stirring for one hour at room temperature; and (4) transferring the solution obtained in step (3) to polytetrafluoroethylene high-pressure reactor, heating in oven for reaction, reaction temperature is 150-300 degrees C, and the reaction time is 1-10 hours.
   USE - The composite material is useful for detecting the residual concentration of carbendazim, and combined toxicity effect of benzimidazole pesticide residues (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material: has good biocompatibility, high safety, high sensitivity of detection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing graphene-rare earth composite material comprises (1) weighing citric acid, histidine, ethylenediamine, and folic acid, mixing, adding deionized water, ultrasonic dissolving to obtain reaction solution, placing the reaction solution in an oven, reacting for 0.5-10 hours in oven at 150-250 degrees C, directly obtaining histidine quantum dot solid product, preparing histidine quantum dot solid product into aqueous solution of 25-200 mg/ml; (2) mixing 5-50 mu l yttrium(III) chloride solution (0.01-1 mol/l), 5-50 mu l ytterbium(III) chloride solution (0.01-1 mol/l), and 5-50 mu l erbium(III) chloride solution (0.01-1 mol/l), adding 0.1-10 ml histidine quantum dot aqueous solution obtained in step (1), and stirring at room temperature for half hour; (3) dropwise adding 1-10 ml sodium fluoride solution (0.01-1 mol/l) to the solution obtained in step (2) under stirring, and stirring for one hour at room temperature; (4) transferring the solution obtained in step (3) to polytetrafluoroethylene high-pressure reactor, heating in oven for reaction, reaction temperature is 150-300 degrees C, and the reaction time is 1-10 hours, and (5) naturally cooling the solution in the reaction kettle to room temperature, taking out, adding into beaker, allowing to stand for 12 hours, taking the supernatant for centrifuging and washing for many times, and freeze-drying to obtain solid graphene quantum dot-rare earth composite nano-materials. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) use of graphene-rare earth composite material for detecting the residual concentration of carbendazim (a) connecting graphene quantum dot-rare earth composite upconversion nanomaterials to hairpin DNA with gold nanometers via EDC/NHS, where hairpin DNA-linked gold nanoparticles quench the fluorescence emission of graphene quantum dot-rare earth composite upconversion nanomaterials, (b) adding carbendazim aptamer, complementary pairing carbendazim aptamer with hairpin DNA bases to emit graphene quantum dot-rare earth composite upconversion nanomaterial fluorescence, and (c) introducing carbendazim with the increase of carbendazim concentration, fluorescence quenching of graphene quantum dot-rare earth composite up-conversion nanomaterials, according to the linear relationship between carbendazim concentration and fluorescence quenching, and detecting residual carbendazim concentration, where the graphene-rare earth composite material is graphene quantum dot-rare earth composite up-conversion nano material prepared by above mentioned method; and
   (2) use of graphene-rare earth composite material to combined toxicity effect of benzimidazole pesticide residues comprising (i) culturing Hep G2 liver cancer cell strain by: culturing MEM medium containing 10(vol.%/vol.%) fetal bovine serum in 37 degrees C, 5% CO2 saturated humidity constant temperature cell incubator, (ii) cancelling the Hep G2 cell suspension diluted with complete culture medium, adjusting the density to 3.5x 105 cells/ml, inoculating 100 mu l/well in 96-well transparent cell culture plate in 5% CO2, and incubating for 24 hours at 37 degrees C, (iii) adding 90 mu l fresh serum-free culture medium to the blank cell culture plate as the zeroing group, absorbing the culture medium in step (ii), adding 90 mu l fresh serum-free medium as control group, absorbing the culture medium in step (2), adding 90 mu l fresh serum-free medium and 10 mu l two benzimidazole pesticides at different concentrations in ratio of 1:1 as treatment groups, incubating the zeroing group, the control group and the treatment group in 5% carbon dioxide, incubating for 24 hours at 37 degrees C in constant temperature cell incubator, (iv) removing the medium of zeroing group, the control group and the treatment group, adding 100 mu l graphene quantum dot-rare earth composite upconversion nanomaterial solution to each well, and incubating for 3.5 hours at 5% carbon dioxide and 37 degrees C, (v) continuously adding the phosphate buffered saline solution to the zeroing group, the control group and the treatment group, placing them on a shaker to shake, so that the cells on the cell culture plate are dispersed in the solution as much as possible, (vi) centrifugal washing the solution obtained by dispersing the zeroing group, the control group and the treatment group, removing the graphene quantum dot-rare earth composite up-conversion nanomaterials that have not been treated in the treatment group, and using fluorescence spectrometer to detect the fluorescence emission intensity at 541 nm under 980 nm excitation, (vii) removing the non-functioning graphene quantum dot-rare earth composite upconversion nanomaterials from the zeroing group and the control group, and measuring the fluorescence emission intensity at 541 nm of the zero adjustment group and the control group, and (viii) according to: cell survival rate = (fluorescence intensity of the treatment group-fluorescence intensity of the zeroing group)/(fluorescence intensity of the control group-fluorescence intensity of the zeroing group)x 100%, establishing linear regression equation based on the pesticide concentration and cell survival rate, and calculating cell LC50 based on the established linear regression equation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the molar ratio of citric acid, histidine, ethylenediamine and folic acid is 1:0.1-1:0.01-0.2:0.01-0.2. In the step (2), the molar ratio of yttrium(III) chloride, ytterbium(III) chloride and erbium(III) chloride in the mixed solution of yttrium(III) chloride, ytterbium(III) chloride and erbium(III) chloride is 0.78:0.2:0.02.

PN CN113896265-A; CN216426792-U
TI Treating residual ammonia by steaming ammonia, by providing remaining ammonia water from outside boundary area, dividing into feeds, introducing remaining ammonia water feed into feed cooler, cooling, and introducing into tower by feed port at top of ammonia distillation tower.
AU CHAI Y
   SHANG E
   WANG Z
AE TIANJIN CHUANGJU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202214284T
AB    NOVELTY - Process for treating residual ammonia by steaming ammonia, involves providing the remaining ammonia water from outside the boundary area; after entering the boundary area, dividing into two feeds, and introducing the first remaining ammonia water feed into the feed cooler (15) and cooling by circulating water, and then introducing into the tower by the feed port at the top of the ammonia distillation tower; passing the second residual ammonia water feed through the feed preheater, and exchanging heat of the waste water pump with the waste water of the tower reactor of the ammonia distillation tower to heat up, and then introducing into the tower from the feed port two above the gas phase side extraction port of the ammonia distillation tower; and evaporating the carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and ammonia in the second remaining ammonia water feed by the gas phase from bottom to top, and flowing to the top of the ammonia distillation tower in the form of gas phase.
   USE - Process for treating residual ammonia by steaming ammonia.
   ADVANTAGE - The process reduces the sulfide content in wastewater from tower, reduces the cost of biochemical treatment, increases the fluidity, prevents low temperature solidification, which completely solves the problem of blockage of the tar discharge pipe, operates at low load, which reduces the excessive area allowance, is not easy to scale, and increases the operating time and service life of sub-contractor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for treating residual ammonia by steaming ammonia, involves providing the remaining ammonia water from outside the boundary area; after entering the boundary area, dividing into two feeds, and introducing the first remaining ammonia water feed into the feed cooler (15) and cooling by circulating water, and then introducing into the tower by the feed port at the top of the ammonia distillation tower; passing the second residual ammonia water feed through the feed preheater, and exchanging heat of the waste water pump with the waste water of the tower reactor of the ammonia distillation tower to heat up, and then introducing into the tower from the feed port two above the gas phase side extraction port of the ammonia distillation tower; evaporating the carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and ammonia in the second remaining ammonia water feed by the gas phase from bottom to top, and flowing to the top of the ammonia distillation tower in the form of gas phase; after introducing the first remaining ammonia feed into the tower, it meets the rising gas phase of carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and ammonia, reabsorbing ammonia, heating the first remaining ammonia feed, releasing the carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide in it, and finally producing gas mainly composed of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide and producing a small amount of ammonia and water gas from the top of the tower; introducing ammonia into the stripping section of ammonia distillation tower after being absorbed; eventually extracting the ammonia entering the stripping section from the gas phase side extraction port of the ammonia distillation tower, where the extracted gas phase is mainly composed of ammonia and water and contains a small amount of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, after extraction, introducing the lower tube box of the sub-contractor into the tube side, and condensing part of the gas phase, flowing back the condensed liquid phase into the tower by its own gravity along the gas phase pipeline from the gas phase side extraction port of the ammonia distillation tower, and participating in the subsequent gas separation; using the destination of the ammonia gas from the gas outlet of the sub-contractor as the desulfurization section outside the boundary or the condensing cooler; condensing by the condensing cooler and cooling the gas phase out of the subcontractor to be more than 20% concentrated ammonia water; dividing the concentrated ammonia water from the condensing cooler into two strands, where the first strand flows to the desulfurization section outside the boundary area, as the supplement of the desulfurization liquid ammonia source, and the second part enters the concentrated ammonia water tank for storage, and is sent out of the boundary area by the concentrated ammonia water pump; arranging the lye inlet below the gas phase side mining opening of the ammonia distillation tower (1), introducing the lye into the tower and mixing with the liquid on the tray, and converting the fixed ammonia into free ammonia for separation, and when the remaining ammonia water flows to the tray at the inlet of the lye solution, extracting the carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide in the tower from the extraction outlet at the top of the tower and the gas phase side extraction outlet; using the heat source of the ammonia distillation tower as low-pressure saturated steam, which is indirectly heated by the reboiler, passing the low-pressure saturated steam through the shell side of the reboiler, and discharging the steam condensate to the outside or fed to the steam condensate storage tank rehydration; taking the circulating liquid phase of the tower kettle by the tube side of the reboiled; flowing the tower kettle circulating liquid phase first into the oil removal tank from the circulating liquid phase outlet of the ammonia distillation tower kettle, and finally settling the heavy oil with higher density than water at the bottom of the tank; after degreasing, flowing out the circulating liquid phase from the middle and upper part of the degreasing tank (3), and then introducing into the reboiler for heating, and after being heated into a gas-liquid mixture, returning to the ammonia distillation tower from the gas phase inlet of the ammonia distillation tower to provide a heat source; after partial oil removal, flowing back the circulating liquid phase into the oil removal tank from the tube-side outlet at the bottom of the reboiler; and intermittently discharging waste water containing tar from the bottom of the degreasing tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a process flow of a process for treating residual ammonia by steaming ammonia.
   Degreasing tank (3)
   Steam condensate storage tank (12)
   Steam condensate circulating pump (13)
   Feed cooler (15)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further involves: flowing out the steam condensate from the steam condensate storage tank (12) and introducing into the steam condensate circulation pump, and pumping into the steam condensate cooler by the steam condensate circulation pump to cool down, then introducing into the sub-contractor to cool the gas phase, and then introducing into the steam condensate storage tank after the water is discharged, and reciprocating the cycle; using the cooling source of the steam condensate cooler as circulating water; discharging out the waste water from the tower reactor from the waste water outlet of the ammonia distillation tower, and introducing into the waste water pump, and after being pressurized, pumping into the feed preheater, after exchanging heat and cooling with the second remaining ammonia water feed, introducing into a section of waste water cooler for cooling; separating the cooled waste water into one and directly introducing into the top of the ammonia distillation tower, and mixing with the first remaining ammonia water feed; cooling the other part of the waste water cooled by the first-stage waste water cooler by the second-stage waste water cooler, after heat exchange, discharging to the outside world, and using the cold source of the second-stage wastewater cooler as refrigerating water.

PN WO2020207926-A1; AU2020273228-A1; CA3126620-A1; CN113498403-A; AR118599-A1; IN202117034139-A; KR2021151776-A; EP3953296-A1; US2022081292-A1
TI Producing product stream by providing plant, supplying feeds to syngas stage, providing syngas stream in syngas stage, and converting syngas stream into product stream and hydrocarbon-containing off-gas stream in synthesis stage.
AU DE SARKAR S
   OSTBERG M
   CHRISTENSEN T S
   AASBERG-PETERSEN K
   DESARCARSUDIP
   WSTBERG M
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 2020A0286D
AB    NOVELTY - Producing product stream involves providing a plant comprising: (a) a syngas stage (A), the syngas stage comprising an autothermal reforming (ATR) section, and (b) a synthesis stage (B); the plant comprising: a 1st feed (1) comprising hydrogen to the syngas stage; a 2nd feed (2) comprising carbon dioxide (CO2) to the syngas stage; a 3rd feed (3) comprising hydrocarbons to the syngas stage; and 4th feed (4) comprising oxygen to the ATR section; where syngas stage is arranged to provide a syngas stream (100) and feed the syngas stream to the synthesis stage; supplying a 1st feed comprising hydrogen to the syngas stage; supplying a 2nd feed comprising carbon dioxide to the syngas stage; supplying a 3rd feed comprising hydrocarbons to the syngas stage; supplying a 4th feed comprising oxygen to the ATR section; providing a syngas stream in the syngas stage; and converting the syngas stream into product stream (500) and a hydrocarbon-containing off-gas stream in the synthesis stage.
   USE - The method or plant is useful for producing product stream (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The layout of the method and plant has at least the following advantages: (1) CO2, H2, and oxygen (O2) can be converted to syngas with a desired H2:carbon monoxide (CO) ratio, suitably without using any hydrocarbon feed to the plant; if needed, one or more hydrocarbon co-feed to the plant can be used as well; (2) utilization of any hydrocarbons generated in the synthesis stage for synthesis gas production; (3) a higher utilization of the carbon dioxide feed is possible compared to a stand-alone rWGS section; one particular aim is to utilize more CO2 feed instead of hydrocarbon feed as a source of carbon; (4) conversion of any hydrocarbon co-feed streams fed to the syngas stage is possible; and (5) if an electrolyzer is used as portion or the entire source of the hydrogen feed to the process, portion or all of the oxygen, generated in the electrolyzer along with H2, can be used as the oxygen source that is required in the proposed process layout.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing a product stream, involves providing a plant comprising: (a) a syngas stage (A), the syngas stage comprising an autothermal reforming (ATR) section, and (b) a synthesis stage (B); the plant comprising: a 1st feed (1) comprising hydrogen to the syngas stage; a 2nd feed (2) comprising carbon dioxide (CO2) to the syngas stage; a 3rd feed (3) comprising hydrocarbons to the syngas stage, upstream of the ATR section; and 4th feed (4) comprising oxygen to the ATR section; where the syngas stage is arranged to provide a syngas stream (100) and feed the syngas stream to the synthesis stage; supplying a 1st feed comprising hydrogen to the syngas stage; supplying a 2nd feed comprising carbon dioxide to the syngas stage; supplying a 3rd feed comprising hydrocarbons to the syngas stage, upstream of the ATR section; supplying a 4th feed comprising oxygen to the ATR section; providing a syngas stream in the syngas stage from at least the 1st, 2nd, 3rd and fourth feeds, and feeding the syngas stream to the synthesis stage; converting the syngas stream into product stream (500) and a hydrocarbon-containing off-gas stream in the synthesis stage. The ratio of moles of carbon in the 3rd feed comprising hydrocarbons, when external to the plant, to the moles of carbon in CO2 in the 2nd feed is less than or equal to 0.5. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for plant comprising the syngas stage and synthesis stage above, the syngas stage additionally comprises a methanation section arranged upstream the ATR section; where a portion or all of the 1st feed is fed to the methanation section; a portion or all of the 2nd feed is fed to the methanation section; and where a portion or all of the 3rd feed is fed to the syngas stage; upstream the methanation section or between the methanation section and the ATR section.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic layout of the plant.
   1st feed (1)
   2nd feed (2)
   3rd feed (3)
   4th feed (4)
   Syngas stream (100)
   Product stream (500)
   Syngas stage (A)
   Synthesis stage (B)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The method further involves feeding portion of the hydrocarbon-containing off-gas stream to the syngas stage in addition to the 3rd feed comprising hydrocarbons; upstream of the ATR section. The 3rd feed comprising hydrocarbons is fed to the syngas stage, directly upstream of the ATR section. Preferred Composition: The ratio of moles of carbon in the 3rd feed comprising hydrocarbons, when external to the plant, to the moles of carbon in CO2 in the 2nd feed is less than or equal to 0.3, preferably less than or equal to 0.25 and more preferably less than or equal to 0.20 or even less than or equal to 0.10. The syngas stream at the inlet of the synthesis stage has a hydrogen/carbon monoxide ratio of 1.00-4.00; preferably 1.50-3.00, more preferably 1.50-2.10. The ratio of hydrogen (H2):CO2 provided at the plant inlet is 1.0-9.0, preferably 2.5-8.0, more preferably 3.0-7.0. The synthesis stage is FT synthesis stage and the H2:CO2-ratio at the plant inlet is 3.0-7.0 or more preferably 3.0-6.0 and most preferably 3.0-5.0. Preferred Components: The 3rd feed comprising hydrocarbons is a natural gas feed.

PN GB2605212-A
TI Dielectric barrier electrical discharge apparatus            comprises sub-macroscopic structure arranged to            field-emit electrons, electrical discharge            establishable between dielectric portion and            electrodes, and drive circuit arranged to provide real            power to fluid in use.
AU NEUMAYR D
   RAMSAY W J
   MICHAN J M
AE DAPHNE TECHNOLOGY SA (DAPH-Non-standard)
GA 2022C4572D
AB    NOVELTY - Dielectric barrier electrical discharge                apparatus comprises two electrodes arranged in use                to provide at least one anode and a cathode. The                electrodes are separated to allow a fluid to be                present between the electrodes in use. The                electrodes have a dielectric portion connected to                the electrode. A sub-macroscopic structure is                connected to the two electrodes and/or to the                dielectric portion. A drive circuit is connected to                each of the electrodes and arranged in use to                establish an electric field between the electrodes.                The sub-macroscopic structure is arranged to                field-emit electrons. The electrical discharge is                establishable between the dielectric portion and                the electrodes. The drive circuit is also arranged                to provide real power to the fluid in use.
   USE - Dielectric barrier electrical discharge                apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The dielectric barrier electrical discharge                apparatus allows the discharge to be more                controllable by reducing the amount of sparking,                reduces ground currents, which are currents flowing                in the parasitic capacitance, and provides a simple                mechanism for providing the inverter functionality                while also allowing direct and easy control over                the output from the inverter to achieve passive                and/or active recuperation of the energy stored in                the tank at the end of every pulse-train.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following: (1) an apparatus for removing carbon                dioxide from a gas, which comprises a first                electrode and a second electrode, a dielectric                portion connected to the first electrode and a                sub-macroscopic structure connected to the first or                second electrode or to the dielectric portion, and                a housing coupled to the electrodes;
   (2) a system for removing carbon dioxide from                a gas, which comprises an apparatus comprising                electrodes separated to allow gas to be present                between the electrodes in use, and a conduit                connected to the apparatus and arranged in use to                provide gas to the apparatu; and
   (3) a method for removing carbon dioxide from                a gas, which comprises establishing an electric                field between a first electrode to which a                dielectric portion is connected and a second                electrode, exposing gas to be scrubbed to the                electrical discharge and electrons, and providing                real power to the gas on exposure to the electrical                discharge and electrons.

PN WO2022189689-A1; ES2926393-A1
TI Obtaining hydrogen, carbon monoxide or synthesis            gas by heating perovskite-type metal oxide for thermal            reducing metal oxide in gaseous stream having air or            inert gas and reacting reduced the oxide with gaseous            stream having carbon dioxide.
AU ORFILA DEL HOYO M
   LINARES SERRANO M
   MOLINA GIL R
   MARUGAN AGUADO A J
   BOTAS ECHEVARRIA J A
   SANZ MARTIN R
   AZCONDO SANCHEZ M T
   AMADOR ELIZONDO U J
AE UNIV REY JUAN CARLOS (UYRE-Non-standard)
   UNIV FUNDACION SAN PABLO CEU (UYSA-Non-standard)
GA 2022B6715P
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining hydrogen, carbon monoxide or                synthesis gas, comprises: (i) heating a                perovskite-type metal oxide of (I) for thermal                reduction of the metal oxide in a gaseous stream                that is air or an inert gas; and (ii) reacting the                reduced metal oxide obtained from step (i) with a                gaseous stream comprising steam and/or carbon                dioxide to obtain hydrogen, carbon monoxide or                synthesis gas and re-oxidation of the metal oxide                to recover the oxide metallic of (I), in which                steps (i) and (ii) are carried out at a temperature                of 300-800&#176; C and a pressure of 0.1-20                MPa.
   USE - The method is useful for obtaining hydrogen,                carbon monoxide or synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides materials in the field of                hydrogen, carbon monoxide and synthesis gas to be                able to produce these fuels efficiently, compatible                with current solar technology and prolonged over                time; utilizes perovskite-type metal oxides that to                shows superior activity to that of other                perovskite-type oxides found in the state of the                art and operate at lower temperatures and have very                good cyclability, good production synthesis gas                throughout the cycles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Obtaining hydrogen , carbon monoxide or                synthesis gas, comprises: (i) heating a                perovskite-type metal oxide of formula                (A11-xB11xM1l-yN1yO3-Z) (I) for thermal reduction                of the metal oxide in a gaseous stream that is air                or an inert gas; and (ii) reacting the reduced                metal oxide obtained from step (i) with a gaseous                stream comprising steam and/or carbon dioxide to                obtain hydrogen, carbon monoxide or synthesis gas                and re-oxidation of the metal oxide to recover the                oxide metallic of (I), in which steps (i) and (ii)                are carried out at a temperature of                300-800&#176; C and a pressure of 0.1-20                MPa.
   A1=element of the lanthanide, alkaline or                alkaline earth series;
   B1=an element of the alkali or alkaline earth                series;
   M1=Fe or Co;
   N1=a transition metal or an element selected                from the groups of the p block of the periodic                table;
   x, y=0-1; and
   y, z=0-0.75.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                of the perovskite-type metal oxide of (I) for the                production of hydrogen, carbon monoxide or                synthesis gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gaseous stream of                  step (ii) additionally comprises air or an inert                  gas. The gaseous current of stage (ii) comprises                  10-100 vol.%, preferably 30-100 vol.% carbon                  dioxide. The gaseous current of step (i) is air.                  The gaseous stream of stage (ii) comprises 10-90                  vol.% a mixture of a stream of steam and carbon                  dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - 0.7235 g of Lanthanum(III) nitrate                hexahydrate, 1.4552 g of cobalt(II) nitrate                hexahydrate and 0.4921 g of strontium nitrate to                obtain 1 g of the perovskite oxide of composition                of lanthanum(III) nitrate hexahydrate-cobalt(II)                nitrate hexahydrate- strontium nitrate. The mixture                of reagents was ground and homogenized. This                mixture was subjected to a first heat treatment at                800&#176; C for 12 hours in an air oven. After                grinding and homogenizing the mixture of                precursors, a pellet was formed and heated at                1100&#176; C for 72 hours with intermediate                grinding every 24 hours. The resulting sample was                characterized by X-ray diffraction, obtaining a                highly crystalline pure material corresponding to a                highly symmetrical perovskite to produce carbon                monoxide.

PN CN114763765-A
TI Gas processing system for use in chemical            industry, has gas purifying unit for removing solid            impurity in target gas and absorbing carbon dioxide in            target gas, and gas generating unit for generating            electricity based on combustion process of input            gas.
AU WANG Y
   XIE K
   XIAO L
   ZHU D
   LI Z
   WANG Z
   YUAN M
AE CNPC EASTCHINA DESIGN INST CO LTD (CNPC-C)
   CHINA NAT PETROLEUM CORP (CNPC-C)
GA 202297262D
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a gas processing system                comprising gas purifying unit, gas adjusting unit                and fuel gas generating unit. The gas purifying                unit comprises gas inlet and gas outlet. The gas                adjusting unit comprises first gas inlet and first                gas outlet. The gas generating unit comprises gas                inlet and power conveying outlet. The gas inlet of                gas purifying unit is used for inputting target gas                to be processed. The gas outlet of gas purifying                unit is connected with first gas inlet of gas                adjusting unit. The first gas outlet of gas                adjusting unit and gas inlet connected with gas                generating unit. The power transmission outlet of                gas generating unit is used for outputting power.                The gas purifying unit is used for removing solid                impurity in target gas, and absorbing carbon                dioxide in target gas. The gas adjusting unit is                used for adjusting concentration of each component                in input gas. The gas generating unit is used for                generating electricity based on combustion process                of input gas.
   USE - Fuel gas processing system for use in a                chemical industry, and can also be used for a gas                purification system, a gas separation system and a                gas oxidation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas processing system can improve the                utilization rate of middle and low heat value gas.                The gas purifying unit can remove solid impurities                in the target gas, and absorb carbon dioxide in                target gas and the concentration of each component                in the input gas can be adjusted by the fuel gas                adjusting unit. The fuel gas generating unit is                used for generating electricity based on the                combustion process of input gas, so that when                target gas (raw material gas) is middle-low                calorific value gas sequentially through the gas                purification unit, medium-low gas gas is                sequentially converted into the normal combustion                of gas, then through the generator assembly can                convert the heat value in the low gas heat value of                the target into electric power, so as to improve                the usage rate of the middle gas and low gas.

PN CN113078382-A; CN113078382-B
TI Recycling and processing lithium batteries            recycling and processing lithium batteries by            discharging lithium battery, immersing in deep eutectic            solvent to mechanically separate battery case, adding            acetic acid solution to adjust pH value, introducing            into electrocatalytic device, and evaporating.
AU FU C
   WANG F
   SUN Z
   ZHANG X
AE KUNSHAN HUIFENG ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY (KUNS-Non-standard)
GA 202185139H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recycling and processing lithium                batteries involves (a) discharging the lithium                battery, (b) immersing the discharged lithium                battery in a deep eutectic solvent to mechanically                separate the battery case, and using the deep                eutectic solvent to absorb the organic electrolyte                of the lithium battery, (c) drying and pulverizing                the positive and negative electrodes, copper foil,                aluminum foil and separator in the lithium battery,                (d) adding alkaline solution, (e) adding acetic                acid solution to the first solid to adjust to the                first target pH value, and filtering, (f)                pyrolyzing the second solid at a first preset                temperature, (g) introducing the carbon dioxide                into an electrocatalytic device and catalytically                converting it into methanol fuel, (h) adding a                lithium hydroxide solution to the first solution to                adjust to a second target pH value, and filtering,                and (i) evaporating the second solution in air and                decomposing at a second preset temperature to                obtain lithium oxide.
   USE - The method is used for recycling and                processing lithium batteries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the improvement of the                recycling rate of lithium batteries.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for recycling and processing lithium                batteries involves (a) discharging the lithium                battery to be recycled, (b) immersing the                discharged lithium battery in a deep eutectic                solvent to mechanically separate the battery case,                and using the deep eutectic solvent to absorb the                organic electrolyte of the lithium battery, (c)                drying and pulverizing the positive and negative                electrodes, copper foil, aluminum foil and                separator in the lithium battery, (d) adding                alkaline solution until the aluminum foil is                dissolved, and filtering to obtain the first solid,                (e) adding acetic acid solution to the first solid                to adjust to the first target pH value, and                filtering to obtain the second solid and the first                solution, (f) pyrolyzing the second solid at a                first preset temperature to obtain copper oxide and                carbon dioxide, (g) introducing the carbon dioxide                into an electrocatalytic device and catalytically                converting it into methanol fuel, (h) adding a                lithium hydroxide solution to the first solution to                adjust to a second target pH value, and filtering                to obtain a second solution, and (i) evaporating                the second solution in air and decomposing at a                second preset temperature to obtain lithium                oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The step (f) is                  carried put at a preset temperature of 500-550                  degrees C. The step (i) is carried out at a                  second preset temperature of 900-1000 degrees C.                  Preferred Components: The hydrogen bond acceptor                  is a quaternary ammonium salt, and the preset                  ratio is 2:3:3 or 3:5:2. The alkaline solution is                  one of sodium hydroxide solution or potassium                  hydroxide solution, where the concentration of                  the alkaline solution is 20-36%. The catalyst of                  the electrocatalytic device is an iron-nickel                  nano-alloy. Preferred Properties: The first                  target pH value is 6.8. The second target pH                  value is greater than 8.5, where the mass                  fraction of the lithium hydroxide solution is                  5-8%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The deep eutectic                  solvent is a polyol, a carboxylic acid and a                  hydrogen bond acceptor mixed in a certain ratio.                  The polyol is isopropanol, glycerol and/or                  butanol. The carboxylic acid is oxalic acid,                  oxalic acid, malonic acid, and/or benzoic acid.                  The concentration of the acetic acid solution is                  0.1-1 M.
