PN CN112430830-A; CN112430830-B
TI Preparation of electrode used in electrocatalytic reduction reaction of carbon dioxide, involves adding e.g. copper-based macrocyclic compound/carbon electrocatalyst, water and alcohol, stirring, coating on substrate, and heating.
AU SONG Y
   LI M
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202123468L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of electrode involves dispersing (i) a carbon material and a copper-based macrocyclic compound in a solvent, ultrasonically-stirring, removing the solvent by rotary evaporation, drying, heat-treating in a mixed atmosphere of hydrogen, helium, nitrogen, argon and/or ammonia to obtain a copper-based macrocyclic compound/carbon electrocatalyst, immersing (ii) a copper base material in a mixed solvent of ethanol, isopropanol, acetone and/or dilute hydrochloric acid, rinsing with deionized water, vacuum-drying and cutting to obtain a copper substrate, adding (iii) the copper-based macrocyclic compound/carbon electrocatalyst, water, alcohol and Nafion (RTM: sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene-based fluoropolymer-copolymer) in sequence, ultrasonically-dispersing to obtain a catalyst slurry, and coating the catalyst slurry on the copper substrate to obtain a copper electrode loaded with electrocatalyst, and heating (iv) the copper electrode loaded with electrocatalyst.
   USE - Preparation of electrode used in electrocatalytic reduction reaction of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, easy, environmentally-friendly and large-scale preparation of electrode under mild reaction conditions. The stability and conductivity of the catalyst are improved by controlling the conditions of heat treatment temperature, atmosphere and duration. The electrode enables electrocatalytic reduction reaction of carbon dioxide with high product selectivity at low potential.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of electrode involves dispersing (i) a carbon material and a copper-based macrocyclic compound in a solvent, ultrasonically-stirring at 20-60 degrees C for 3-10 hours, removing the solvent by rotary evaporation, drying, heat-treating at 200-900 degrees C for 1-5 hours in a mixed atmosphere of hydrogen, helium, nitrogen, argon and/or ammonia to obtain a copper-based macrocyclic compound/carbon electrocatalyst, immersing (ii) a copper base material in a mixed solvent of ethanol, isopropanol, acetone and/or dilute hydrochloric acid at 20-60 degrees C for 10-40 minutes, rinsing with deionized water, vacuum-drying and cutting to obtain a copper substrate, adding (iii) the copper-based macrocyclic compound/carbon electrocatalyst, water, alcohol and Nafion (RTM: sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene-based fluoropolymer-copolymer) in sequence, ultrasonically-dispersing for 0.1-1 hour to obtain a catalyst slurry, and coating the catalyst slurry on the copper substrate to obtain a copper electrode loaded with electrocatalyst, and heating (iv) the copper electrode loaded with electrocatalyst in a mixed atmosphere of hydrogen, helium, nitrogen, argon and/or ammonia at 500-1000 degrees C for 1-5 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of the carbon material in the solution is 0.1-10 mg/mL. The mass ratio of the copper-based macrocyclic compound with respect to carbon material is more than 0.1. The carbon material comprises carbon black, carbon nanotubes, activated carbon, carbon fibers and/or graphene. The copper substrate comprises foamed copper and/or polished copper sheet.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The copper-based macrocyclic compound comprises 5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-carboxyphenyl)copper porphyrin, protoporphyrin copper chloride, meso-tetrakis(4-benzenesulfonyl) copper porphyrin, copper tetramethoxyphenylporphyrin, copper porphyrin, copper peptide cyanine, tetrasodium copper phthalocyanine tetrasulfonate, meso-tetrakis(4-benzenesulfonic acid)copper phthalocyanine and/or copper o-phenanthroline. The solvent is water, methanol, ethanol, ethylene glycol, propanol, isopropanol, dichloromethane, N,N-dimethylformamide and/or N,N-dimethylacetamide. The alcohol is methanol, ethanol, propanol and/or isopropanol. The concentration of the electrocatalyst in the catalyst slurry is 0.1-5 mg/mL. The loading amount of the electrocatalyst on the copper electrode is 0.1-5 mg/cm2.

TI Oral capnography accessory device for use with bite block, has latch arms and main housing that form cavity into which bite block is inserted, and tube attachment that extends from lingual facing surface of main housing.
AU SCHIAPPA M
   WITT K
   STAHL H
   BLAHO R
AE OCA HOLDINGS LLC (OCAH-Non-standard)
GA 2021065209
AB    NOVELTY - The device (20) has a main housing that extends linearly along a longitudinal axis. The main housing comprise first and second opposite ends. A buccal facing surface extends between the ends and an opposite lingual facing surface extending between the ends. A first latch arm extends from the buccal facing surface at the first end of the main housing. A second latch arm extends from the buccal facing surface at the second end of the main housing. An opening is provided between the latch arms. The latch arms and main housing form a cavity into which the bite block is inserted. The cavity is in communication with the opening. A tube attachment extends from the lingual facing surface of the main housing and comprises a passageway. The passageway comprises a first portion which extends parallel to the longitudinal axis of the main housing and a second portion which is curved relative to the axis of the main housing. The oral capnography accessory device is formed of polyurethane.
   USE - Oral capnography accessory device for use with bite block used in assembly (claimed) for sampling oral exhalations of carbon dioxide from mouth of patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The oral capnography accessory device increases the accuracy of carbon dioxide sampling from the mouth of a patient and provides a more reliable capnography reading The surgeon is provided with the ability to access the mouth of the patient, since the oral capnography accessory device does not cover the mouth of the patient. The oral capnography accessory device is robust and withstands 10 lbs of force before fracturing and 2 lbs of force before dislodging from the bite block and enables the tubing to be positioned within 10 mm of the top of the patient airway and at a minimum of 5 cm away from the back of the patient throat. The fluid aspiration prevention unit minimizes the amount of water, blood and debris that enters into the tubing from the mouth of the patient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an assembly for sampling oral exhalations of carbon dioxide from mouth of patient.

PN CN111748106-A; CN111748106-B
TI Preparing chitosan gel material by using chitosan solution with pH of 6-8 comprises e.g. preparing dispersion liquid, and adding chitosan to dispersion, controlling temperature at freezing point, and introducing carbon dioxide into solution.
AU CAI J
   ZHONG Y
   ZHANG L
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 2020A11281
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing chitosan gel material by using chitosan solution with pH of 6-8 comprises (1) preparing dispersion liquid, (2) adding chitosan to the dispersion, controlling the temperature at the freezing point to 35 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide into the solution while stirring to dissolve the chitosan, stopping the introduction of carbon dioxide when the chitosan is completely dissolved, and obtaining a transparent chitosan solution with a pH value of 6-8 after defoaming, and (3) heating the chitosan aqueous solution obtained in the step (2), and chemically crosslinking or dipping in a coagulation bath.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing chitosan gel material by using chitosan solution with pH of 6-8.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is carried out in an environment with a pH value of 6-8; reduces degradation of chitosan molecular chain; has high stability; is highly efficient, energy-saving, and environmentally friendly; is easy to realize large-scale production; and has no strong acid and strong alkaline substances remain.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) chitosan gel material prepared by the above preparation method;
   (2) preparing the chitosan organic gel, comprising placing the chitosan gel in an organic solvent for replacement, where the organic solvents used to replace chitosan gel is low boiling point, volatile alkanes, halogenated hydrocarbons, alcohols, phenols, ethers and acetals, ketones, acids and anhydrides, and esters, nitrogen-containing organic compounds, sulfur-containing organic compounds, or a mixture of the former;
   (3) chitosan organic gel prepared by the preparation method of the chitosan organic gel;
   (4) preparing chitosan aerogel, comprising drying the chitosan gel or the organic gel to remove liquid to obtain chitosan aerogel, where the drying method comprises supercritical drying, normal pressure drying or freeze drying;
   (5) preparing hydrophobic chitosan aerogel, comprising hydrophobically modifying the chitosan organic gel;
   (6) hydrophobic chitosan aerogel prepared by the preparation method of the hydrophobic chitosan aerogel;
   (7) preparing carbon aerogel, comprising protecting the chitosan aerogel at a temperature above 150 degrees C to obtain a carbon aerogel under the protection of an inert atmosphere; and
   (8) preparing functional chitosan gel and aerogel materials, comprising introducing functional organic or inorganic additives and low-dimensional nano-materials into the chitosan gel or the aerogel, or introducing natural or synthetic polymers, and blending.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The dispersion is 6-60 wt.%, preferably 18-60 wt.% urea aqueous solution and/or 3-14 wt.%, preferably 7-14 wt.% thiourea aqueous solution. In the step (2), the mass ratio of chitosan to dispersion is 1:7-1:1000. The coagulator used in the coagulation bath is a mixed aqueous solution of alcohols, salts, amides, dimethyl sulfoxide, ethyl acetate, and/or acetone. The chemical crosslinking agent is aldehyde crosslinking agents, glycidyl ether crosslinking agents, epoxy compounds, iridoids, and/or polyphenols. The alcohols are methanol and/or ethanol. The concentration of the alcohols is 10-100 wt.%. The salt is ammonium salt, sodium salt, potassium salt, magnesium salt, calcium salt, and/or aluminum salt. The concentration of the salt solution is 5-40 wt.%. The hydrophobic modification method is by chemical vapor deposition or chemical grafting or physical coating. The surface of chitosan rich in hydroxy and amino groups is deposited or grafted or coated with hydrophobic compounds with lower surface chemical energy. The compound with lower surface chemical energy comprises inorganic oxides, silanes, long-chain alkane chlorides, and fluorides. The functional organic or inorganic additives are plasticizers, reinforcing agents, flame-resistant material additives, dyes, optical stabilizers, antibacterial and antibacterial agents, conductive materials, and/or surfactants. The low-dimensional nano-materials are graphene and derivatives, carbon nanotubes and derivatives, metal or metal oxide nanoparticles, organic framework compounds, and/or layered nano-materials. The natural polymer and synthetic polymer are polymer nano-fibers, animal protein, vegetable protein, collagen, alginate, cellulose and derivatives, conductive polymer, polyvinyl alcohol, and/or polyethylene glycol. Preferred Method: In the step (3), the heating temperature of heating to prepare the gel is 40-130 degrees C.

PN WO2020191197-A1; IN202127036060-A; CN113573795-A; KR2021135257-A; EP3941610-A1; BR112021018433-A2; US2022219110-A1; JP2022526771-W
TI Filter unit used for electric automobile            ventilation system used, comprises adsorption lines            comprising water adsorbent material and gas adsorbent            material, and heater coupled to water adsorbent            material and gas adsorbent material in lines.
AU BUELOW M T
   XIN Y
   TRAN P
   ABRAHAM A
   AVRAHAM A
   SIN Y
   THOMAS V M
   SHINWISHIAN
   AKASHI A
AE BASF CORP (BADI-C)
   BASF CORP (BADI-C)
   BASF CORP (BADI-C)
GA 2020937838
AB    NOVELTY - A filter unit (200) comprises two adsorption                lines, each adsorption line comprising a water                adsorbent material (201) and a gas adsorbent                material (202), and a heater (203) coupled to the                water adsorbent material and the gas adsorbent                material in each adsorption line. The gas adsorbent                material is downstream from the water adsorbent                material in a direction of operation.
   USE - Filter unit used for system e.g. electric                automobile ventilation system used in vehicle,                airplane, helicopter, spacecraft, automobile, van,                bus, train, truck and submarine (all claimed) for                removing water and gas from air.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) system, which comprises a passenger cabin,                a heating, ventilation and air conditioning (HVAC)                system (205) for maintaining air quality in the                passenger cabin, and the filter unit for                maintaining humidity and carbon dioxide levels                within the passenger cabin;
   (2) electric automobile ventilation system,                which comprises a passenger cabin, HVAC system for                maintaining air quality in the passenger cabin, a                battery, and the filter unit for maintaining                humidity and carbon dioxide levels within the                passenger cabin;
   (3) automobile ventilation system, which                comprises a passenger cabin, a heating, ventilation                and air conditioning (HVAC) system for maintaining                air quality in the passenger cabin, and the filter                unit for maintaining humidity and carbon dioxide                levels within the passenger cabin; and
   (4) method of using filter unit, which                involves operating the adsorption line (L1) of the                filter unit comprising a water adsorbent material                (W1), and a gas adsorbent material (G1), in which                gas adsorbent material (G1) is downstream from the                water adsorbent material (W1) in a direction of                operation, the adsorption line (L1) adsorbs water                and gas from surrounding air, and regenerating a                adsorption line (L2) of the filter unit comprising                a water adsorbent material (W2) in the form of                water adsorbent particles in a packed bed and a gas                adsorbent material (G2) in the form of gas                adsorbent particles in a packed bed, in which the                gas adsorbent material (G2) is upstream from the                water adsorbent material (W2) in a direction of                regeneration and desorbs water and gas from the                water adsorbent material (W2) and the gas adsorbent                material (G2).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                filter unit and the system.
   Filter unit (200)
   Water adsorbent material (201)
   Gas adsorbent material (202)
   Electric heater (203)
   HVAC system (205)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: At least one of the                  water adsorbent material and the gas adsorbent                  material is in the form of a units comprising at                  least one of powder, beads, extrudates, tablets,                  pellets, agglomerates and granules. The units                  have a shape of at least one chosen from round,                  spherical, spheres, ellipsoidal, regular granules                  and irregular granules. At least one of the water                  adsorbent material and the gas adsorbent material                  is in the form of units in a packed bed. At least                  one of the water adsorbent material and the gas                  adsorbent material is at least one of coated onto                  a nonwoven media material and contained within                  layers of the nonwoven media material. The layers                  of nonwoven material are at least one of                  corrugated and pleated. At least one of the water                  adsorbent material and the gas adsorbent material                  is at least one of coated onto a honeycomb                  substrate and contained within channels of the                  honeycomb substrate. The filter unit further                  comprises a screen or nonwoven material to hold                  at least one of the water adsorbent material and                  the gas adsorbent material within the honeycomb                  structure. At least one of the water adsorbent                  material and the gas adsorbent material is coated                  onto a substrate. The substrate comprises at                  least one of a honeycomb structure, a foam and a                  nonwoven media. At least one of the water                  adsorbent material and the gas adsorbent material                  is an extruded honeycomb structure or is coated                  onto an extruded honeycomb structure. The high                  surface area supports comprise at least one of                  beads, granules and extrudates. The alkali metal                  oxide comprises at least one of lithium,                  potassium, sodium, rubidium and cesium. The                  alkaline earth metal oxide comprises at least one                  of barium, strontium, calcium, beryllium,                  magnesium and radium. The filter unit further                  comprises a rotary adsorption apparatus                  comprising a housing, sectors within the housing                  forming adsorption beds, in which the water                  adsorbent material and the gas adsorbent material                  are arranged in the rotary adsorption apparatus                  so that a flow path of gas can pass over each                  adsorption bed during operation and a                  counter-current flow path of regeneration air can                  pass over each adsorption bed during                  regeneration. Each of the water adsorbent                  material (W1), the gas adsorbent material (G1),                  the water adsorbent material (W2) and the gas                  adsorbent material (G2) is coupled to at least                  one heater. The adsorption line (L1) completes                  operation and begins regenerating and the                  adsorption line (L2) completes regeneration and                  begins operating. Preferred Composition: The gas                  adsorbent material comprises 35-55 wt.% amine,                  preferably 45 wt.% amine, and 45-65 wt.% silica,                  preferably 55 wt.% silica. The weight ratio of                  water adsorbent material to gas adsorbing                  material is (1:10)-(1:1). The gas adsorbent                  material further comprises one or more binders,                  and fibrillated polymer fibers. The binders                  comprise at least one of an organic binder, a                  styrene acrylic polymer, an inorganic binder or                  sodium silicate. The water adsorbent material                  comprises a high porosity and high surface area                  material. The water adsorbent material comprises                  at least one of silica, alumina and a metal                  organic framework. The adsorption line comprises                  0.1-15.0 L/passenger water adsorbent material and                  0.5-20.0/passenger gas adsorbent material.                  Preferred Conditions: The regeneration of the                  adsorption line (L2) is carried out at 50-200                  degrees C. Preferred Process: The regeneration                  process involves exhausting desorbed water and                  gas into an external atmosphere. The operation of                  the adsorption line (L1) and regeneration of the                  adsorption line (L2) occurs simultaneously. When                  the adsorption line (L2) is finished                  regenerating, the adsorption line (L2) remains                  idle until the adsorption line (L1) requires                  regenerating. The regeneration process involves                  passing external air over the gas adsorbent                  material (G2) and the water adsorbent material                  (W2) and exhausting the external air comprising                  desorbed gas and desorbed water into an external                  atmosphere. Preferred Properties: The units have                  a size of 0.05-10 mm, preferably 0.05-6.0 mm. The                  nonwoven media material has a cross-sectional                  area of 100-10000 cm2. The high surface area                  supports have a pore volume of more than 0.8                  cc/g, and mean pore diameter of more than 100 A.                  The filter unit increases life of the battery by                  1-20% as compared to no filter unit, and                  decreases power consumption of the HVAC system by                  1-20% as compared to no filter unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas adsorbent                  material comprises at least one of amines and                  carbamates impregnated onto one or more high                  surface area supports. The carbamates are the                  reaction product of an ethylamine and dimethyl                  carbonate. The gas adsorbent material comprises                  at least one of an amine, a carbamate,                  attapulgite, a metal organic framework (MOF), a                  zeolite, activated carbon, an alkali metal oxide,                  an alkaline earth metal oxide, and their surface                  modified analogs.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The amines comprise an                  amine functionalized polymer comprising                  polystyrene polymer comprising benzylamine                  groups.

PN CN111248380-A
TI Processing cucumber fermented carbonated beverage comprises cleaning cucumbers, adding Lactobacillus lyophilized powder, performing ultrasonic treatment, incubating, transplanting and performing carbon dioxide charging.
AU ZHANG J
   LENG C
   LI H
   XIN G
   SONG X
   JIANG N
   JIN J
   ZHENG X
   YANG Y
AE SDIC ZHONGLU FRUIT JUICE CO LTD (SDIC-C)
GA 202053122P
AB    NOVELTY - Processing cucumber fermented carbonated beverage comprises (a) selecting fresh, non-damaged eight ripe cucumbers as raw materials, (b) cleaning the fresh cucumbers, crushing and beating after peeling, (c) adding Lactobacillus lyophilized powder to cucumber juice, uniformly mixing, performing ultrasonic treatment, performing anaerobic fermentation, feeding the cucumber fermentation broth with compressed ozone after fermentation treatment and performing microwave resonance treatment on the solution, (d) shaking the activated strains thoroughly, inoculating on separation medium plate, incubating, transplanting, (e) preparing Lactobacillus reinforced microspheres with photosensitive decomposition characteristics, (f) preparing micrometer-level electrostatic functional pellets and (g) performing carbon dioxide charging.
   USE - The method is useful for processing cucumber fermented carbonated beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the color of juice.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing cucumber fermented carbonated beverage comprises (a) selecting fresh, non-damaged eight ripe cucumbers as raw materials, (b) cleaning the fresh cucumbers, crushing and beating after peeling, where the speed of the mixer is 100 revolutions/minute for 15-25 minutes, adding 5 g milk powder per 100 g cucumber juice, mixing well and performing 31.2-32.2 kGy radiation sterilization for 20-30 minutes after coarse filtration to obtain cucumber juice, (c) adding 1wt.% Lactobacillus lyophilized powder to cucumber juice, uniformly mixing, performing ultrasonic treatment, where ultrasonic enhanced stability process parameters are duty cycle of 0.5-1s/s, ultrasonic temperature of 50-65 degrees C and ultrasonic time of 8-12 minutes, performing anaerobic fermentation of the solution after ultrasound at 35-45 degrees C for 5-6 hours to obtain cucumber fermentation broth, feeding the cucumber fermentation broth with compressed ozone after fermentation treatment with commonly using compressor of 3-5 p, exhaust volume of 0.1-0.12 m3/minute, exhaust pressure of 0.8-0.9mpa and compressed air temperature of 45-50 degrees C and performing microwave resonance treatment on the solution for 10-15 seconds, (d) shaking the activated strains thoroughly in 90 ml sterile water, inoculating on the separation medium plate in turn, incubating at 30 degrees C for 3-4 days, transplanting onto slant medium and incubating at 3 degrees C for 24-48 hours to obtain a culture medium of Lactobacillus (CCTCCNOM2016512), (e) preparing Lactobacillus reinforced microspheres with photosensitive decomposition characteristics, (f) preparing micrometer-level electrostatic functional pellets and (g) performing carbon dioxide charging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The process of preparing micrometer level electrostatic function pellets comprises (i) adding chitosan to 1.5% acetic acid to make the concentration 1%, mechanically stirring until it is completely dissolved, filtering with gauze to obtain clear liquid and allowing to stand, (ii) soaking the agar in water, dissolving in hot water and controlling the concentration at 3 wt.%, (iii) heating to 65 degrees C in a constant temperature water bath at 85 degrees C, adding 65 degrees C hot water (water and alcohol in a ratio of 2:1 and MC concentration of 3%), performing mechanical stirring for 8 minutes, adding hot agar solution (agar and MC in a ratio of 2:12), adding chitosan clear solution, adding functional substances e.g. active enzymes, stirring for 2 minutes, where the average film thickness is 4.9-5.2 (x 0.01 mm), the tensile strength is 29.5-30.8 Ts and FM is 2.11-2.53, when the addition amount of chitosan/MC is from 1/36-6/36 to obtain different chitosans, (iv) defoaming the membrane liquids with different amounts of chitosan with a high-speed centrifuge, pouring into a glass film-forming template, scraping off the excess membrane liquid, air-drying, peeling the film carefully and adding in a dryer and (v) performing ball making, which breaks the prepared film into micrometer-sized balls with different particle sizes. The process of preparing Lactobacillus-reinforced microspheres with light-sensitive decomposition comprises using 60% pectin, 20% sodium alginate, 15% whey protein isolate and 5% 6-bromo-7-hydroxy-4-methylcoumarin as the composite wall material, dissolving in sterile phosphate buffer, adding olive oil and emulsifying using a high-speed shear pre-emulsifier under a water bath at 50 degrees C for 3-5 minutes, stirring at 50 degrees C for 5 minutes until a uniform emulsion is formed, using acetic acid to adjust the pH to 4-4.9 to promote colloidal aggregation, where the core material is made up of a composite solution, which is prepared by placing 30% citric acid, 30% malic acid, 30% lactic acid bacteria strain with a concentration of 1010 colony forming unit (CFU)/ml and 10% lactic acid in a portion of water, adjusting the pH to 2-3 and continuously stirring for 20 minutes to obtain the core material solution, taking a core material solution, adding an equal volume of wall material solution, uniformly mixing, spraying the mixed solution into 5% calcium chloride solution, stirring with a magnetic stirrer for a certain period of time to form a capsule, filtering, rinsing the capsule 3 times with distilled water to get a wet capsule, freeze-drying the wet capsules at -60 degrees C for 36 hours to prepare freeze-drying microcapsules.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN110922628-A
TI Preparation of nano composite foam material involves adding polystyrene and foaming agent prepared using e.g. sodium chloride solid and lithium chloride powder to reactor, heating, supplying carbon dioxide, saturating, and cooling.
AU ZHANG J
   CHEN Y
AE XUZHOU SHUNPING BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202025897T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano composite foam material involves adding polystyrene and foaming agent to reactor, heating to 150 degrees C to melt polystyrene, leaving still for 10 minutes, supplying carbon dioxide, saturating for 30 minutes, quickly releasing pressure in the reactor to atmospheric pressure, and tanking out blank, and cooling. The nano composite foaming agent is prepared by adding sodium chloride solid, lithium chloride powder and potassium nitrate to deionized water, adding mesoporous silica microspheres, drying at 110 degrees C for 4 hours, placing in a muffle furnace for calcination, continuously introducing high-purity nitrogen, naturally cooling, grinding, sieving through 200 mesh sieve, obtaining product (A), adding product (A) and 4,4'-oxybis(benzenesulfonyl hydrazide)-montmorillonite composite material to deionized water and anhydrous ethanol, stirring at 150 rpm, ultrasonically dispersing for 30 minutes, adding modifier, stirring, suction-filtering, and drying.
   USE - Preparation of nano composite foam material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of nano composite foam material having excellent thermal-insulating property and tensile strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of nano composite foam material involves adding 300 pts. wt. polystyrene and foaming agent to reactor, heating to 150 degrees Cto melt polystyrene, leaving still for 10 minutes, supplying carbon dioxide, saturating for 30 minutes, quickly releasing pressure in the reactor to atmospheric pressure, and tanking out blank, and cooling. The nano composite foaming agent is prepared by adding 28 pts. wt. sodium chloride solid, 21 pts. wt. lithium chloride powder and 50 pts. wt. potassium nitrate to 200 pts. wt. deionized water, stirring at 150 rpm, adding 60-100 pts. wt. mesoporous silica microspheres, stirring at 150 rpm for 20 minutes, drying at 110 degrees C for 4 hours, placing in a muffle furnace for calcination, heating from room temperature to 600 degrees C at 5 degrees C/minute, naturally cooling, continuously introducing high-purity nitrogen, naturally cooling, grinding, sieving through 200 mesh sieve, obtaining product (A), adding 15 pts. wt. product (A) and 10-42 pts. wt. 4,4'-oxybis(benzenesulfonyl hydrazide)-montmorillonite composite material to 20 pts. wt. deionized water and 180 pts. wt. anhydrous ethanol, stirring at 150 rpm, ultrasonically dispersing for 30 minutes, adding 8 pts. wt. modifier, stirring at 150 rpm for 24 hours, suction-filtering through microporous filter, and drying in oven at 60 degrees C for 24 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The mesoporous silica microspheres are prepared by stirring 500 pts. wt. isopropanol and 450 pts. wt. deionized water in reactor at 150 rpm for 30 minutes, heating to 30 degrees C, adding 5 pts. wt. hexadecylamine, stirring for 30 minutes, adding 8.5 pts. wt. ammonia solution with concentration of 28%, and 65 pts. wt. ethyl orthosilicate, reacting at 30 degrees C for 24 hours, washing with 100 pts. wt. anhydrous ethanol, and drying at 60 degrees C for 3 hours. The 4,4'-oxybis(benzenesulfonyl hydrazide)-montmorillonite composite material is prepared by dispersing 2.5 pts. wt. sodium-based montmorillonite in 50 pts. wt. deionized water, adding 5 pts. wt. 1 mol/L hydrochloric acid to acidify for 4 hours, stirring at 50 degrees C and 300 rpm for 30 minutes, adding transparent solution obtained by dissolving 4 pts. wt. 4,4'-oxobisbenzenesulfonyl hydrazide in 15 pts. wt. dimethyl sulfoxide, mixing, adding 20 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide with concentration of 35%, and 4 pts. wt. 1-butyl 3-methylimidazole tetrafluoroborate ionic liquid, stirring, adding 10 pts. wt. methyl methacrylate monomer, adding resultant to microwave catalytic/synthetic extractor, introducing nitrogen, adding 30 pts. wt. potassium persulfate solution with concentration of 65%, reacting, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging, washing with 50 pts. wt. dimethyl sulfone and 50 pts. wt. carbon tetrachloride, and drying at 60 degrees C for 24 hours. Preferred Composition: The modifier is KH-550 (RTM: 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane), KH-560 (RTM: glycidoxypropyltrimethoxysilane) and/or KH-570 (RTM: gamma -methacryloxypropytrimethoxysilane).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Property: The polystyrene has a weight average molecular weight of 2x 105 to 5x 105.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A13 (Polymers of aromatic mono-olefins; including polystyrene.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.)
MC A08-B03; A08-R06A; A11-A03; A11-B06A; A12-P01; A12-S01A; A12-W14

TI Method for determining deacetylation degree and substitution degree of carboxymethyl chitosan, involves adding carboxymethyl chitosan and bicarbonate solution to headspace bottle, followed by recording gas chromatographic signal.
AU YAN N
   CHAI X
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201997924R
AB    NOVELTY - A carboxymethyl chitosan deacetylation degree and substitution degree determining method involves adding carboxymethyl chitosan and bicarbonate solution to the headspace bottle, sealing and mixing uniformly to obtain sample-A, and adding carboxymethyl chitosan to the strong acid solution, stirring and dissolving to obtain acidified carboxymethyl chitosan, diluting with water, transferring the diluted mixture into a dialysis bag, and adding water to dialyze, taking the water mixture outside the dialysis bag and adding to the headspace bottle, adding the bicarbonate solution, sealing and mixing uniformly to obtain sample-B, adding water to the headspace bottle, sealing uniformly to obtain a blank sample, placing sample-A, sample-B and blank sample in the headspace sampler, and calculating the degree of deacetylation and substitution of carboxymethyl chitosan based on the measured value of the gas chromatography signal of carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for determining deacetylation degree and substitution degree of carboxymethyl chitosan.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables determining deacetylation degree and substitution degree of carboxymethyl chitosan with more effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carboxymethyl chitosan deacetylation degree and substitution degree determining method involves adding carboxymethyl chitosan and bicarbonate solution to the headspace bottle, sealing and mixing uniformly to obtain sample-A, where the mass ratio of solute in carboxymethyl chitosan and bicarbonate solution is 1-5: 1, and adding carboxymethyl chitosan to the strong acid solution, stirring and dissolving to obtain acidified carboxymethyl chitosan, diluting with water, transferring the diluted mixture into a dialysis bag, and adding water to dialyze, after reaching the dialysis equilibrium, taking the water mixture outside the dialysis bag and adding to the headspace bottle, adding the bicarbonate solution, sealing and mixing uniformly to obtain sample-B, when the mass ratio of solute in carboxymethyl chitosan to the strong acid solution is 1-3: 0.75, adding water to the headspace bottle, sealing uniformly to obtain a blank sample, placing sample-A, sample-B and blank sample in the headspace sampler, setting the headspace sampler and gas chromatograph operating conditions, carrying out headspace gas chromatography detection and recording the gas chromatographic signal values ? degrees f carbon dioxide corresponding to sample-A, sample-B and blank sample, and calculating the degree of deacetylation and substitution of carboxymethyl chitosan based on the measured value of the gas chromatography signal of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The strong acid solution is hydrochloric acid solution. The bicarbonate solution is sodium bicarbonate solution.

PN CN109394646-A
TI Sunscreen repair cream comprises jojoba oil, vitamin E, squalane, olive oil, glycerol monostearate, glycerol, amino acid moisturizer, Dendrobium nobile, radix curcumae, Pinellia and deionized water.
AU LI Y
   DAI H
   CAI C
AE HAINAN SHIBOLI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
   INST TROPICAL BIOSCIENCE & BIOTECHNLOGY (CTAG-C)
GA 201922819F
AB    NOVELTY - Sunscreen repair cream comprises 1-1.5 %mass jojoba oil, 2-3 %mass vitamin E, 1-2 %mass squalane, 4-6 %mass olive oil, 1-2 %mass glycerol monostearate, 5-6 %mass glycerol, 3-4 %mass amino acid moisturizer, 5-10 %mass Dendrobium nobile, 1-3 %mass radix curcumae, 2-3 %mass Pinellia and the balance amount is deionized water.
   USE - As sunscreen repair cream.
   ADVANTAGE - The cream has excellent anti-repair effect and an ideal UV absorption property, absorbs and shields UVA and UVB, reduces the contact and damage to the skin, increases superoxide dismutase and glutathione peroxidase activity, reduces malondialdehyde content, blocks various aspects of damage caused by photoaging, repairs the skin after sunburn and improves the skin's self-protection ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the cream involving (i) weighing the D.nobile, radix curcumae, Pinellia and crushing the medicinal materials to 80-120 mesh, (ii) soaking the crushed D.nobile raw material in ethyl acetate at 80-90 degrees C for 20-40 minutes and filtering, (iii) adding the soaked D.nobile and crushed radix curcumae to an ethanol solution, microwave extracting at 50-70 degrees C for 80-100 minutes, extracting for 2-4 times, combining the extract, filtering and concentrating the filtrate to a thick paste to obtain D.nobile and radix curcumae mixture, (iv) placing the crushed Pinellia into a steam tank forsteam explosion, extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method, adding the Pinellia into the carbon dioxide extraction tank, circularly adding carbon dioxide liquid extraction, extracting at 20-30 degrees C with a pressure of 20-30 MPa for 80-100 minutes to obtain Pinellia extract, (v) taking jojoba oil, vitamin E, squalane, olive oil and Pinellia extract and mixing, heating the mixture and stirring at 80-85 degrees C to form a oil-phase, (vi) taking glycerol monostearate, glycerol and amino acid moisturizer, placing the mixture in deionized water, heating and stirring at 85-90 degrees C to form a aqueous phase and (vii) slowly adding the oil-phase to the aqueous phase, while stirring at 1500-2000 rpm until completely mixed and starting the homogenizer, reducing the temperature to 60-70 degrees C, adding D.nobile and radix curcumae mixture, continuously stirring, cooling the emulsion to room temperature, stopping stirring and leaving still for 16-20 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The cream comprises 1.2 %mass jojoba oil, 2.5 %mass vitamin E, 1.5 %mass squalane, 5 %mass olive oil, 1.5 %mass glycerol monostearate, 5.5 %mass glycerol, 3.5 %mass amino acid moisturizer, 8 %mass D.nobile, 2 %mass radix curcumae, 2.5 %mass Pinellia and the balance amount is deionized water. In step (ii), the mass of the ethyl acetate is 2-4 times the mass of the D.nobile. In step (iii), the volume concentration of the ethanol solution is 70-90%. In step (iii), the mass of the ethanol solution is 3-8 times the total mass of the D.nobile and radix curcumae raw materials. Preferred Method: The step (iii) involves adding ethanol in a volume concentration of 85% and microwave extracting at 60 degrees C for 90 minutes. The step (iv) involves carrying out steam explosion at a pressure of 15-25 MPa for 20-40 minutes. The step (iv) involves carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at a pressure of 25 MPa at 25 degrees C for 90 minutes. The stirring speed of the oil-phase is 800-1200 rpm and the stirring speed of the aqueous phase is 1000-1500 rpm.

PN CN108706553-A
TI Production of glass hot bending mold brick and acid, involves mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier, milling, feeding to kiln, dissolving, carrying out solid-liquid separation, adding carbon dioxide, filtering and cleaning.
AU CHEN X
   LI M
   LI J
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2018861380
AB    NOVELTY - The production method of glass hot bending mold brick and acid involves (A) mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier, and milling to obtain raw material, and feeding to a kiln to-be-roasted to obtain clinker, (B) dissolving, and carrying out solid-liquid separation, (C) adding carbon dioxide to obtained solution until white precipitate is not generated, filtering white precipitate, cleaning, drying, burning, and crushing, (D) mixing with perlite, silica powder, magnesia powder, clay powder, calcium lignosulfonate, glass fiber, zirconia, titanium oxide and water, and grinding to obtain mixed material (a), placing in a mold, press-forming, pressure-maintaining, demolding, curing, drying and sintering to obtain glass hot bending mold brick, (E) separating the residue obtained in step (B) by flotation to obtain sulfide, and (F) placing the sulfide in oxygen-enriched environment, calcining, catalyzing flue gas, and absorbing using concentrated sulfuric acid.
   USE - Production method of glass hot bending mold brick and acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical production of glass hot bending mold brick and acid with high utilization rate of waste, excellent high temperature resistance and thermal shock stability, uniform structure, and high intensity, by simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The production method of glass hot bending mold brick and acid involves (A) mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier, and milling to obtain raw material, and feeding to a kiln to-be-roasted to obtain clinker, (B) dissolving the prepared clinker, and carrying out solid-liquid separation, (C) adding carbon dioxide to the obtained solution until white precipitate is not generated, filtering the white precipitate, cleaning, drying, burning, and crushing to obtain alumina powder, (D) mixing the alumina powder with perlite, silica powder, magnesia powder, clay powder, calcium lignosulfonate, glass fiber, zirconia, titanium oxide and water, and grinding to obtain mixed material (a), placing the mixed material (a) in a mold, press-forming, pressure-maintaining, demolding, curing, drying and sintering to obtain glass hot bending mold brick, (E) separating the residue obtained in step (B) by flotation to obtain sulfide, and (F) placing the sulfide in oxygen-enriched environment of 30-50%, calcining for 3-5 hours at 800-1200 degrees C, catalyzing the flue gas produced by calcination by vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing using concentrated sulfuric acid to prepare sulfuric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or sodium hydroxide. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. Preferred Composition: In step (A), the raw materials phosphogypsum and fly ash are mixed in a weight ratio of 1:(0.7-2). The additive addition ratio is 1:1 according to the molecular ratio of sodium oxide and sum of aluminum oxide and ferric oxide contained in the raw material. The mixing ratio of the modifier material is 10-25 wt.% of the total weight of the raw material. In the step (B), the liquid-solid volume ratio during dissolution is (4-6):1. In the step (D), the mixed material (a) comprises 25-30 pts. wt. alumina powder, 10-15 pts. wt. perlite, 12-16 pts. wt. silica powder, 6-8 pts. wt. magnesia powder, 10-13 pts. wt. clay powder, 13-16 pts. wt. calcium lignosulfonate, 4-7 pts. wt. glass fiber, 3-4 pts. wt. zirconia, 2-3 pts. wt. titanium oxide, and 25-30 pts. wt. water. Preferred Process: In the step (B), the clinker is ground before dissolution. Preferred Conditions: In the step (A), the roasting time is 1-2 hours and the roasting temperature is 1000-1350 degrees C. In the step (C), the burning process is performed at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours. In the step (D), the curing time is 12-13 days, the drying is performed at 100-120 degrees C and the drying time is 1-2 hours, and the sintering process is carried out at 1450-1550 degrees C for 4-6 hours.
DC L01 (Glass - includes chemical compositions, batch treatment, furnaces, flat glass forming, hollow-ware forming, postforming and glass/ceramics, but not lens designs, bottling, bottle-washing, closures for containers, glazing designs, glass cutting, chamfering edges, printing on glass, disposing of used glass or the production of pure sodium silicate. PN CN108558374-A
TI Preparing high wear-resistant high-temperature ceramic and acid comprises e.g. mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier, grinding, roasting, performing solid-liquid separation, adding carbon dioxide, filtering and separating.
AU CHEN X
   LI J
   LI M
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2018779928
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high wear-resistant high-temperature ceramic and acid comprises e.g. mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier and grinding to form raw meal, roasting, performing solid-liquid separation, adding carbon dioxide, filtering, washing, drying, and burning to obtain crushed alumina powder, mixing alumina powder, silicon carbide, zirconia and silicon micropowder, ball milling, powdering and drying to obtain mixture a, heating paraffin and oleic acid to 110-130 degrees C until molten to obtain mixture b, mixing mixture a and mixture b, and hot die casting to form high wear resistant and high temperature resistant ceramics, subjecting the residue obtained after solid-liquid separation to flotation, and separating, placing in an oxygen-rich environment, baking, catalyzing by vanadium pentoxide, absorbing with concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high wear-resistant high-temperature ceramic and acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: high waste utilization rate; and high wear-resistant and high-temperature ceramic has high strength, and simple acid production process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high wear-resistant high-temperature ceramic and acid comprises (i) mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier and grinding to form raw meal, and delivering into the kiln for roasting to obtain clinker, (ii) dissolving the clinker prepared in step (i), and performing solid-liquid separation, (iii) adding carbon dioxide into the solution separated in step (ii) until the white precipitate is no longer produced, then filtering the white precipitate off, washing and drying, and burning to obtain crushed alumina powder, (iv) mixing alumina powder obtained in step (iii), silicon carbide, zirconia and silicon micropowder, ball milling, powdering and drying to obtain mixture a, heating paraffin and oleic acid to 110-130 degrees C until molten to obtain mixture b, mixing mixture a and mixture b, and hot die casting to form high wear resistant and high temperature resistant ceramics, (v) subjecting the residue obtained by separating step (ii) to flotation, and separating of sulfides, (vi) placing the sulfide separated in step (v) in an oxygen-rich environment of 30-50%, baking at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours, catalyzing the flue gas generated by the calcination by vanadium pentoxide, absorbing with concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda; the modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. In step (i), in the raw meal phosphogypsum and fly ash are mixed in a ratio of 1:0.8-1.8 by weight, the additive addition ratio is 1:1 according to the molecular ratio of the sum of sodium oxide and aluminum oxide+ iron(III) oxide contained in the raw meal, and the mixing ratio of the modifier is 10-25% of the total weight of the raw meal. The kiln is an industrial rotary kiln, an industrial tunnel kiln or an industrial vertical kiln. In step (ii), the clinker is firstly grinding and then dissolving; the liquid to solid ratio at the time of dissolution is 4-6:1. The high wear resistant high temperature ceramic comprises 70-90 pts. wt. alumina powder, 30-40 pts. wt. silicon carbide, 20-30 pts. wt. zirconia, 1-3 pts. wt. silicon micropowder, 10-15 pts. wt. paraffin wax and 0.4-0.6 pts. wt. oleic acid. In step (iv), the mixture a has a particle size of 200-300 mesh. Preferred Method: In step (i), the calcination is carried out at a temperature of 1000-1350 degrees C for 1-2 hours. In step (iii), the burning is performing at a temperature of 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours. In step (iv), the hot die casting pressure is 0.5-1 MP, the temperature is 70-80 degrees C, the sintering temperature is 1500-1600 degrees C, and sintering time is 3-4 hours.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PN CN108358220-A; CN208413873-U
TI Preparing sodium bicarbonate comprises e.g. conveying the raw material solution containing sodium carbonate into the carbonization tower, carrying out carbonization reaction with carbon dioxide contained in industrial gases, and mixing.
AU ZHANG Y
   CHEN Z
   LI J
AE WUHAN DEZE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECH (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 201862906X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sodium bicarbonate comprises e.g. conveying the raw material solution containing sodium carbonate into the carbonization tower, carrying out carbonization reaction with carbon dioxide contained in industrial gases, mixing high temperature solution A1 of the carbonization tower containing high concentration sodium bicarbonate and solid sodium hydrogencarbonate crystal with the low temperature solution A2 containing low concentration sodium hydrogencarbonate and solid sodium hydrogencarbonate crystal in the mixer, conveying the clear liquid at the top of the mixer into the cooler or the flasher, and cooling obtain low temperature solution A2, conveying the low temperature solution A2 into the mixer, completing the cycle of cooling, and lowering the temperature of the solution, crystallizing the sodium hydrogencarbonate in the mixer solution, passing the bottom slurry of the mixer through solid-liquid separator to obtain solid sodium hydrogencarbonate crystals and filtrate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sodium bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has operating period more than 60 days, produces product has large crystal size, and excellent high product rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing sodium bicarbonate comprises conveying the raw material solution containing sodium carbonate into the carbonization tower, carrying out carbonization reaction with carbon dioxide contained in industrial gases, mixing high temperature solution A1 of the carbonization tower containing high concentration sodium bicarbonate and solid sodium hydrogencarbonate crystal with the low temperature solution A2 containing low concentration sodium hydrogencarbonate and solid sodium hydrogencarbonate crystal in the mixer, conveying the clear liquid at the top of the mixer into the cooler or the flasher, and cooling obtain low temperature solution A2, conveying the low temperature solution A2 into the mixer, completing the cycle of cooling, and lowering the temperature of the solution, crystallizing the sodium hydrogencarbonate in the mixer solution, passing the bottom slurry of the mixer through solid-liquid separator to obtain solid sodium hydrogencarbonate crystals and filtrate, sending the filtrate into the filtrate barrel for recycling for the dissolution of the raw materials, sending the solid sodium hydrogencarbonate crystals into a drying process to obtain dried sodium hydrogencarbonate. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for preparing sodium bicarbonate, comprising carbonization tower, mixer, cooler or flasher, solid-liquid separator, and filtrate tank are sequentially connected.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises taking raw material solution containing sodium carbonate, dissolving solid soda ash in a filtrate to obtain sodium carbonate, or dissolving solid solution containing sodium carbonate in the filtrate to obtain raw material solution containing sodium carbonate, or dissolving solid solution containing sodium bicarbonate in the filtrate, pyrolyzing or partially decomposing to obtain raw material solution containing sodium carbonate, and recovering the from the filtrate barrel. Preferred Components: The carbonization tower is not provided with a built-in or external cooler called as uncooled carbonization tower. The heat of the carbonization reaction is not removed from the carbonization tower. The heat of the carbonization reaction is all removed in the cooler or flasher connected to the mixer. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide in the carbonization tower and sodium carbonate in the raw material solution is 1:1-1.2.The temperature inside the carbonization tower at 20-120 degrees C, and the pressure ranges from 0.2-2.2 MPa. The high temperature solution A1 containing high concentration of sodium hydrogencarbonate and solid sodium hydrogencarbonate crystals conveys the mixer. The sodium bicarbonate crystal grows in the mixer. The residence time of A1 in the mixer is 5-300 minutes. The residence time of A2 in the mixer is 5-300 minutes. The temperature range of the high temperature solution A1 containing a high concentration of sodium hydrogencarbonate and solid sodium hydrogencarbonate crystal is 28-120 degrees C. The low temperature solution A2 containing low concentration of sodium hydrogencarbonate and solid sodium hydrogencarbonate crystals has temperature of -20 degrees C to 80 degrees C.The volume ratio of A1 and A2 is 1:0.005-29. The device comprises carbonization tower, mixer, cooler, solid-liquid separator, and filtrate barrel. The upper part of the carbonization tower is provided with a feeding port. The two sides of the lower part are respectively provided with an air inlet and a discharge port. The discharge port is connected to the mixer through a pipe. A clear liquid nozzle is provided on each of the top sides of the mixer. The clear liquid nozzle is connecting with the bottom of the cooler through the clear liquid pipe. The top end of the cooler is connected with the inlet of the mixer through a return line. The bottom discharge port of the mixer is connected to the solid-liquid separator through a pipe. The filtrate separated by the solid-liquid separator is connected to the filtrate tank through the liquid outlet. The discharge port of the solid-liquid separator is connected to the drying device. The cooler is replaced by a flasher. The clearing nozzle of the mixer is connected with the inlet of the flasher through the clearing conduit. The top of the flasher is provided with a flash steam outlet. The bottom outlet of the flasher is connected to the inlet of the mixer. The cooler is connected to the cold energy refrigerant generating device. The cold energy refrigerant generating device is a circulating water pipe, an air compressor or a refrigerator. The cold energy refrigerant conveying and leaving the cooler is cold air, circulating water, low temperature water or refrigerant. A clearing pump is arranged between the mixer and the cooler or the flasher for transport the liquid of the mixer clear nozzle to the cooler or the flasher. The carbonization tower is an uncooled carbonization tower, and no built-in or external cooler is provided. The top of the carbonization tower is provided with an exhaust gas outlet. The mixer is a thickener, a clarification tank, a crystallizer, and a stirring device. The solid-liquid separator is a belt type vacuum filter, a drum vacuum filter, a disc filter, a plate, frame filter press, a centrifuge or a settling tank. The cooler is a coil heat exchanger, a tubular heat exchanger, a plate heat exchanger, and a kettle heat exchanger. The flasher is a liquid top down spray type, and a liquid bottom down spray type.

TI Processing water comprehensive treatment involves            passing oil and water into a sedimentation tank,            introducing alkali and carbon dioxide from outside,            adjusting pH value of oil and water, and allowing to            stand for period of time.
AU HAN X
   YANG S
AE GUANGZHOU OBSIDIAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018419220
AB    NOVELTY - Processing water comprehensive treatment                involves passing oil and water into a sedimentation                tank (1), introducing alkali and carbon dioxide                from the outside, adjusting pH value of the oil and                water to 12, and allowing to stand for a period of                time. The precipitate in the sedimentation tank is                discharged to a drying and dehydration device (5),                calcium carbonate is dehydrated by centrifugal                drying, and the supernatant in the sedimentation                tank is discharged to an evaporation tank for                vacuum evaporation. Crystallization of sodium                chloride crystal is performed, followed by                centrifugation, drying, to obtain solid sodium                chloride. The mother liquor of sodium chloride is                further heated and concentrated through the                concentration tank to a thick brine, halogenation                process is performed to obtain thick brine crude                potassium chloride. The industrial sodium chloride                is obtained through centrifugation. The mother                liquor from sodium chloride production step is                discharged into bromine tower (11).
   USE - Method for processing water comprehensive                treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to process water                comprehensive treatment whch effectively recycle                the resources, avoids the pollution of the                discharge of oil field water to the                environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing water comprehensive treatment                involves passing oil and water into a sedimentation                tank (1), introducing alkali and carbon dioxide                from the outside, adjusting pH value of the oil and                water to 12, and allowing to stand for a period of                time. The precipitate in the sedimentation tank is                discharged to a drying and dehydration device (5),                calcium carbonate is dehydrated by centrifugal                drying, and the supernatant in the sedimentation                tank is discharged to an evaporation tank for                vacuum evaporation. Crystallization of sodium                chloride crystal is performed, followed by                centrifugation, drying, to obtain solid sodium                chloride. The mother liquor of sodium chloride is                further heated and concentrated through the                concentration tank to a thick brine, halogenation                process is performed to obtain thick brine crude                potassium chloride. The industrial sodium chloride                is obtained through centrifugation. The mother                liquor from the sodium chloride production step is                discharged into the bromine tower (11), and                chlorine is oxidized into the bromine tower with                water vapor, elemental bromine is                precipitated.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                method for processing water comprehensive                treatment.
   Sedimentation tank (1)
   Sediment outlet (4)
   Drying and dehydration device (5)
   Waterproof electric push rod (8)
   Bromine tower (11)

PN CN107746064-A; CN107746064-B
TI Preparing calcium-doped MCM-48 molecular sieve used for carbon dioxide adsorption, by dissolving templating agents in water, adding alkali source, silicon source and calcium source, carrying out hydrothermal crystallization and calcining.
AU JI X
   JIA H
   WANG Y
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV SHANXI (UYSX-C)
   UNIV SHANXI (UYSX-C)
GA 201818931C
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing a calcium-doped MCM-48 molecular sieve involves (a) dissolving a templating agent (I) and templating agent (II) in water, (b) adding alkali source, (c) slowly adding silicon source, (d) slowly adding calcium source while stirring, (e) carrying out hydrothermal crystallization and adjusting the pH value to 10.5-12 using acetic acid, (f) again carrying out hydrothermal crystallization, (g) washing the hydrothermal crystallization product, drying and calcining to obtain the calcium-doped MCM-48 mesoporous molecular sieve.
   USE - Method is useful for preparing calcium-doped MCM-48 molecular sieve, which is useful for carbon dioxide adsorption (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and energy-efficient preparation of the calcium-doped MCM-48 molecular sieve with large specific surface area and excellent carbon dioxide adsorption performance, in an environmentally-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for preparing a calcium-doped MCM-48 molecular sieve involves (a) dissolving a templating agent (I) and templating agent (II) in water, (b) adding alkali source under stirring condition, (c) slowly adding silicon source while stirring, (d) slowly adding calcium source while stirring, where the steps (a) to (d) are carried out in a water bath at 45-50 degrees C, (e) carrying out hydrothermal crystallization at a low temperature of 60-90 degrees C for 5-50 hours and adjusting the pH value to 10.5-12 using acetic acid, (f) again carrying out hydrothermal crystallization at a low temperature of 60-90 degrees C for 10-60 hours, (g) washing the hydrothermal crystallization product, drying and calcining at 500-600 degrees C for 3-12 hours to obtain the calcium-doped MCM-48 mesoporous molecular sieve. The molar ratio of silicon source, alkali source, calcium source, water, templating agent (I) and templating agent (II) is 1:0.1-0.23:0.005-0.1:100-200:0.18-0.38:0.008-0.12. The silicon source, alkali source and calcium source are based on silicon dioxide:aluminum oxide:calcium oxide. The templating agent (I) is cetyltrimethylammonium bromide. The templating agent (II) is sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the calcium-doped MCM-48 molecular sieve prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali source is sodium hydroxide and/or potassium hydroxide. The silicon source is tetraethyl orthosilicate, silica sol or silica gel. The calcium source is calcium acetate, calcium nitrate or calcium oxalate. Preferred Conditions: In the step (e), the hydrothermal crystallization step is carried out at 60-78 degrees C for 12-36 hours. In the step (f), the hydrothermal crystallization step is carried out for 18-40 hours. In the step (g), the calcining step is carried out at 500-600 degrees C for 5-10 hours.

PT P
PN IN201611023590-A; IN361456-B
TI Synthesis of membrane for separating carbon dioxide from gaseous mixture, involves adding ethylenediamine and methyl acrylate to ethanol for obtaining dendrimer, adding solution containing polysulfone, and casting mixture on glass plate.
AU HAZARIKA S
   BHARALI P
   BORAH H J
   DAS I
AE COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
   COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
GA 201814701D
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of membrane involves adding 15 mmol ethylenediamine, 60 mmol methyl acrylate and laccase enzyme to ethanol, filtering resulting mixture, distilling the filtrate to obtain half-generation poly(amidoamine) dendrimer, dissolving the dendrimer and ethylenediamine in ethanol, purifying the resulting product to obtain generation-zero dendrimer, mixing 1-5% generation-zero dendrimer solution with solution containing 5-20% polysulfone, casting resulting mixture comprising the membranes, on a glass plate, and evaporating the solvent from the membranes followed by gelation of membranes.
   USE - Synthesis of membrane used for separation of carbon dioxide from gaseous mixture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, continuous and economical as no ionic liquid is required and environmentally-friendly as laccase enzyme is used as catalyst instead of toxic chemicals, compared to existing methods, and efficiently produces membranes having excellent carbon dioxide permeability compared to existing membranes, within 4 hours. The membrane is stable and can be regenerated and reused consecutively for more than 10 times without any loss in the activity of the membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of membrane involves (a) dissolving 15 mmol ethylenediamine in 30 ml ethanol, cooling the mixture in an ice bath and adding 60 mmol methyl acrylate dropwise, (b) stirring the mixture continuously at 300 rpm and room temperature for 4-6 hours in the presence of laccase enzyme as catalyst and filtering the mixture, (c) distilling the filtrate for removing ethanol and unreacted methyl acrylate by purifying the filtrate using column chromatography, to obtain half generation poly(amidoamine) dendrimer as yellowish oil, (d) dissolving the poly(amidoamine) dendrimer and ethylenediamine in ethanol by stirring at room temperature for 4-6 hours in presence of the catalyst, (e) filtering the catalyst from the mixture after 12-14 hours, distilling the solvent to remove ethanol and purifying the product by column chromatography to obtain generation-zero dendrimer as yellowish oil, (f) mixing 1-5% generation-zero dendrimer solution with solution comprising 5-20% polysulfone and additives dissolved in N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, under constant stirring at 45 degrees C until a homogeneous solution is achieved, (g) casting the resulting mixture comprising membranes, on a glass plate using a doctor's knife by phase inversion process, and (h) evaporating the solvent from the membranes for 2-3 hours followed by gelation in ice cold water for 20-28 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of the laccase enzyme used in the synthesis is 2 mg/mL.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The additive(s) is chosen from 6 wt.% polyethylene glycol, 6 vol.% ethanol, a mixture of 6 vol.% ethanol, 6 vol.% isopropanol and 6 vol.% butanol, a mixture of 6 vol.% ethanol and 6 vol.% isopropanol, and a mixture of 6 vol.% ethanol and 6 vol.% butanol, preferably 6 wt.% polyethylene glycol. The method uses 10% polysulfone. Preferred Property: The ratio of carbon dioxide selectivity of membrane to nitrogen selectivity of the membrane is 63309. The carbon dioxide permeance of the membrane is 337.68 GPU when maximum carbon dioxide permeation of the membrane is observed for 8 hours of reaction time.
