PT P
PN CN109486906-A
TI Intracellular microRNA non-enzymatic amplification            detection based on an electrostatic affinity nano            transporter-cell imaging, comprises e.g. synthesizing            nano gold particles and designing hairpin probe of            non-enzymatic amplification system.
AU HUANG X
   LIAO Y
   ZHAO Z
AE UNIV SUN YATSEN FIFTH AFFILIATED HOSPITA (UYSY-C)
GA 201927131F
AB    NOVELTY - Intracellular microRNA non-enzymatic                amplification detection based on an electrostatic                affinity nano transporter and cell imaging,                comprises synthesizing nano gold particles (AuNPs);                (b) designing hairpin probe for non-enzymatic                amplification system according to the principle of                non-enzymatic amplification and the microRNA                sequence to be tested, and designing probe H1 and                the hairpin probe H2 sequence required for the                non-enzymatic amplification system by using hairpin                probe H1 as a double-head labeling strategy, and                respectively marking 5' end and the 3' end with                fluorescent group FAM and quenching group BHQ-1,                marking the 3' end of H2 with fluorescent group FAM                abnd 8th base adenine-labeled quenching group BHQ-1                from the 5' end and dissolving hairpin probe H1, H2                dissolved in tris TE buffer, final concentration is                100 mu M, and storing in a refrigerator at -20                degrees C; and carrying out hairpin probe                pretreatment to make the hairpin probes H1, H2                sufficiently form the hairpin structure.
   USE - The method is useful for intracellular                microRNA non-enzymatic amplification detection                based on an electrostatic affinity nano                transporter-cell imaging.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple principle and low                detection cost, and high sensitivity, simple and                easy operation procedure; and achieves constant                temperature and no enzyme amplification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Intracellular microRNA non-enzymatic                amplification detection based on an electrostatic                affinity nano transporter and cell imaging,                comprises (a) synthesizing nano gold particles                (AuNPs) by (a1) using nano gold particle synthesis                system includes phosphate-buffered saline (PBS)                buffer solution, poly-L-lysine (PLL) solution,                chloroauric acid solution, adding nuclease-free                water to make up the volume to the required volume                of the system, fully oscillate and mixing, where                the whole process is protected from light, (a2)                instantaneously centrifuging solution obtained in                the step (a1), so that the solution with the cap of                the tube is dropped in the tube, and the whole                process is protected from light, (a3) placing                solution obtained in step (a2) into a constant                temperature mixing device, incubating at a constant                temperature and avoiding light, and (a4) taking                solution of step (a3) changes from yellow to red,                indicating the formation of nano gold particles,                and storing in a refrigerator at 4 degrees C; (b)                designing hairpin probe for non-enzymatic                amplification system according to the principle of                non-enzymatic amplification and the microRNA                sequence to be tested, and designing probe H1 and                the hairpin probe H2 sequence required for the                non-enzymatic amplification system by using hairpin                probe H1 as a double-head labeling strategy, and                respectively marking 5' end and the 3' end with                fluorescent group FAM and quenching group BHQ-1,                marking the 3' end of H2 with fluorescent group FAM                abnd 8th base adenine-labeled quenching group BHQ-1                from the 5' end and dissolving hairpin probe H1, H2                dissolved in tris TE buffer, final concentration is                100 mu M, and storing in a refrigerator at -20                degrees C; (c) carrying out hairpin probe                pretreatment to make the hairpin probes H1, H2                sufficiently form the hairpin structure, adding                phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) to the hairpin                probes H1 and H2, respectively, where the final                concentration of the PBS is set to 1X, subsequently                heating to 95 degrees C, fully denaturing, cooling                down gradient to normal temperature and                respectively obtaining pretreatment products of                hairpin probes H1 and H2, storing product in an                environment of 4 degrees C or -20 degrees C, after                H1 and H2 form a sufficient hairpin structure,                fluorescent group FAM interacts with the quenching                group BHQ-1 and is fluorescence quenched; (d)                constructing extracellular non-enzymatic                amplification detection system by taking a certain                amount of the pretreatment product of the hairpin                probes H1 and H2 obtained in the step (c), adding                PBS buffer solution, adding RNase inhibitor, and                finally adding diethylpyrocarbonate (DEPC) water to                the required volume of the detection system, and                mixing; (e) verifying extracellular non-enzymatic                amplification detection system and its sensitivity                and specificity detection by adding different                concentrations of the microRNA to be tested to the                non-enzymatic amplification detection system in                step (d), measuring the temperature, detecting                fluorescence intensity of the solution by a                fluorescence spectrophotometer, and determining the                sensitivity of non-enzymatic amplification system,                setting control group and same concentration of the                microRNA to be tested and the microRNA 210 and                microRNA214 similar to the base sequence of the                microRNA to be tested are added to the step (d)                non-enzymatic amplification detection system,                respectively, warming, measuring the fluorescence                intensity of the obtained solution with a                fluorescence spectrophotometer, and determining the                specificity of the non-enzymatic amplification                system; (f) constructing non-enzymatically                amplified nanogold particle transport system by                taking a certain amount of pretreatment product of                hairpin probes H1 and H2 obtained in the step (c),                adding to AuNPs solution obtained in the step (a),                fully mixing and then warming the bath; (g)                carrying out non-enzymatic amplification of tumor                cells by nanogold complex transport by taking a                certain amount of the solution obtained in step                (f), adding to tumor cells, placing in a 37 degrees                C carbon dioxide incubator for at least 1 hour; (h)                staining of the nucleus and skeleton by taking out                product after the incubation in the step (f) from                the carbon dioxide incubator, and then the nuclei                and the skeleton are stained, observing under a                microscope, or placing in a refrigerator at 4                degrees C to protect from light; and (g) detecting                cellular fluorescence intensity and intracellular                imaging by taking final product obtained in the                step (f) observed for intracellular imaging by a                laser scanning confocal microscope, and recording                the data.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The steps of the synthesis                  of the nano gold particles comprises dissolving                  30 mg PLL in 3 ml water, fully oscillating and                  mixing for 5 minutes to dissolve, preparing PLL                  solution with a concentration of 10 mg/ml, and                  storing in a low temperature refrigerator at -20                  degrees C, taking 10 mu l of the original                  concentration of 1.52 mol/l chloroauric acid                  solution, adding 142 mu l of water, mixing                  thoroughly, avoiding the light to prepare a                  concentration of 0.1 mol/l chloroauric acid                  solution, placing in a refrigerator at 4 degrees                  C protected from light, taking 20X PBS 50 mu l,                  while taking the solution obtained is 60 mu l,                  the solution obtained is 10 mu l, adding 880 mu l                  nuclease-free water to 1 ml, fully oscillating                  and mixing, the whole process is protected from                  light; solution obtained in step c is                  instantaneously centrifuged, so that the solution                  covered by the cap is dropped in the tube, and                  the whole process is protected from light;                  solution obtained in step d is placed in a                  constant temperature mixing instrument,                  incubating at 300 revolutions/minute, 66 degrees                  Cconstant temperature for 3 hours, the whole                  process is protected from light; and solution                  changes from yellow to red, indicating the                  formation of nano gold particles, and stored in a                  refrigerator at 4 degrees C. In the step (c), the                  hairpin probes H1 and H2 were respectively                  dissolved in 1Xphosphate buffer PBS to a final                  concentration of 500 nM. The gradient cooling in                  the step (c) starts from 95 degrees C, is kept                  for 5 minutes, and then is cooled to the next                  temperature point at a rate of 5 degrees C/minute                  for 1 minute, and the cycle is continued until                  the normal temperature is 25 degrees C. The                  detection system in the step (d) is 100 mu l, the                  final concentration of the H1 and H2 pretreatment                  products is 50 nM, the final concentration of the                  PBS buffer is 1X, and the final concentration of                  the RNase inhibitor is 1 U/ mu l. The method                  comprises after adding different concentrations                  of the microRNA to be tested in the step (e), the                  bath is heated at 37 degrees C for 1 hour, and                  then the fluorescence intensity of the obtained                  solution is detected by a fluorescence                  spectrophotometer to determine the sensitivity of                  the non-enzymatic amplification system and after                  adding the same concentration of the microRNA,                  microRNA210, and microRNA214 to be tested, the                  bath was heated at 37 degrees C for 1 hour, and                  then the fluorescence intensity of the obtained                  solution was measured by a fluorescence                  spectrophotometer to determine the specificity of                  the non-enzymatic amplification system, where                  fluorescent spectrophotometer is a Hitachi F-7000                  fluorescence spectrophotometer. In the step (f),                  the pretreatments of the hairpin probes H1 and H2                  are taken, and added to the AuNPs solution                  obtained in the step (a), and the final                  concentration of the pretreatments of the hairpin                  probes H1 and H2 is 500 nM, and the mixture is                  fully mixing and warm bath at 37 degrees C for 2                  hours. The steps of the nuclear and skeleton                  staining in the step (g) comprises discarding the                  culture medium of the cells; adding 1X PBS,                  gently shaking, then discarding PBS, repeating                  this step again; adding 4% p-formaldehyde,                  covering the bottom cells, and fixing at room                  temperature for 10 minutes; discarding 4%                  p-formaldehyde, and washing the cells twice with                  1XPBS for 5 minutes each time; taking 5 mu l of                  20 mu M TRITC rhodamine labeled phalloidin dye                  solution dissolved in 1 ml 1X PBS, fully mixed;                  dye solution obtained is added to the cells                  obtained, staining at room temperature for 30                  minutes in the dark; discarding the dye solution                  and washing the cells twice with 1XPBS for 5                  minutes each time; taking 1 mu l of 5 mg/ml DAPI                  dye solution dissolved in 1 ml 1XPBS, and mix                  well, dye solution obtained is added to the cells                  obtained, and stained at room temperature for 5                  minutes in the dark and discarding the dye                  solution and washing the cells twice with 1XPBS                  for 5 minutes each time; and taking Fluoromount-G                  (RTM: Transparent liquid medium containing                  fluorescent nuclear stain) a water-soluble sealer                  is added to the cells obtained, allowing to stand                  in the dark for 5 minutes at room temperature,                  dried tablets are allowed to dry and then                  observed under a microscope, or placed in a                  refrigerator at 4 degrees C to protect them from                  light. Preferred Components: The microRNA to be                  tested is microRNA21, and the sequence is                  5'-uagcuuaucagacugauguuga-3' (SEQ ID NO: 1);                  sequence of the microRNA210 is:                  5'-cugugcgugugacagcggcuga-3' (SEQ ID NO: 2); and                  sequence of microRNA214 is:                  5'-acagcaggcacagacaggcagu-3' (SEQ ID NO: 3). The                  sequence of the hairpin probe H1 is: fully                  defined sequence of 61 base pairs (SEQ ID NO: 4)                  as given in the specification, and 5' end and the                  3' end are respectively labeled with a                  fluorescent group FAM and a quenching group                  BHQ-1, and sequence of the hairpin probe H2 is:                  fully defined sequence of 44 base pairs (SEQ ID                  NO: 5) as given in the specification, where 3'                  end is labeled with a fluorescent group FAM, and                  the 8th base T is labeled with a quenching group                  BHQ-1 from the 5' end.

PN CN109134657-A
TI Preparing antibody of 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 useful for e.g. immunofluorescence, and preventing and treating rickets, osteomalacia and infantile hand comprises e.g. mixing 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 protein with N,N dimethylamide, and mixing.
AU TANG J
   TIAN Q
   CHEN X
   ZHOU Y
AE NINGBO AOCHEER BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201905496G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing antibody of 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 comprises e.g. (i) mixing 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 protein with N,N dimethylamide, adding triethylamine, uniformly mixing, reacting at 4 degrees C, adding isobutyl chloroformate, mixing, reacting at 4 degrees C to obtain an activated 25-hydroxy vitamin D3 protein, dissolving OVA in ultrapure water, adjusting pH to 8.5 with sodium hydroxide, adding N,N dimethylamide, uniformly mixing, reacting at 4 degrees C to obtain an activated ovalbumin, mixing activated 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 protein was with activated ovalbumin, reacting at 4 degrees C, dialyzing against phosphate buffered saline buffer, repeating twice, and dialyzing against ultrapure water and repeating for 2 times to obtain 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 artificial antigen, and (ii) using BALB/c mice, mixing 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 artificial antigen with Freund's complete adjuvant in equal volume, subcutaneously injecting mice, and mixing 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 artificial antigen with Freund's incomplete adjuvant.
   USE - The antibody is useful for immunofluorescence, enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay, magnetic microparticle immunoassay to qualitatively or quantitatively detect the expression level of 25-hydroxy vitamin D3 in human body, and clinically assist in the prevention and treatment of rickets, osteomalacia, and infantile hand and foot.
   ADVANTAGE - The antibody has high titer, excellent affinity and binding with 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 antigen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing antibody of 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 comprises (i) mixing 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 protein with N,N dimethylamide, adding triethylamine, uniformly mixing, reacting at 4 degrees C, adding isobutyl chloroformate, mixing, reacting at 4 degrees C to obtain an activated 25-hydroxy vitamin D3 protein, dissolving ovalbumin (OVA) in ultrapure water, adjusting the pH to 8.5 with sodium hydroxide, adding N,N dimethylamide, uniformly mixing, reacting at 4 degrees C to obtain an activated ovalbumin, mixing the activated 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 protein was with activated ovalbumin, reacting at 4 degrees C, dialyzing against phosphate buffered saline buffer, repeating twice, and then dialyzing against ultrapure water and repeating for 2 times to obtain 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 artificial antigen, (ii) using BALB/c mice, mixing 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 artificial antigen with Freund's complete adjuvant in equal volume, subcutaneously injecting mice at intervals of 3 weeks, mixing 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 artificial antigen with Freund's incomplete adjuvant in equal volume, subcutaneously injecting mice at multiple points at intervals of 3 weeks, intravenously injecting 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 artificial antigen into the tail vein, after 3 days, taking the immune spleen cells of high titer mouse, and resuspending the immune spleen cells in a culture solution, (iii) culturing myeloma cells in a complete Roswell Park Memorial Institute (RPMI) 1640 medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum, and exchanging cells one day before fusion, where the cells were in a logarithmic growth phase on the day of fusion, (iv) 4 days before cell fusion, sacrificing unimmunized 8-week-old KM female mice, sterilizing, taking mouse peritoneal cells e.g. feeder cells under sterilized conditions, using hypoxanthine-aminopterin-thymidine (HAT) medium, suspending pelleted feeder cells, adding the above-mentioned feeder cell suspension to a 96-well plate, and incubating at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator for cultivation, (v) mixing the immune spleen cells with the myeloma cells, placing in a fusion tube, centrifuging, discarding the supernatant, dripping 1 ml 50% PEG 4000 along a rotating tube wall, completing the addition within 60 seconds, adding 25 ml Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) medium for 5 minutes, terminating the reaction, centrifuging in a centrifuge, discarding the supernatant, adding HAT medium, gently blowing to suspend the precipitated cells, adding the cell suspension to the 96-well plate with the feeder cells, incubating at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide in the incubator, semi-exchanging HAT complete medium on the 7th day after the fusion, semi-exchanging sodium hypoxanthine and thymidine (HT) complete medium on the 14th day after the fusion, and using RPMI-1640 on the 21st day after the fusion and completely exchanging culture solution, (vi) when the fused cells grow to 1 divide by 10 of the bottom of the well, diluting 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 artificial antigen to 5 mu g/ml with carbonate buffer, adding to the wells of the enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) plate, leaving at 4 degrees C overnight, removing the liquid in the well, washing twice with phosphate buffer, adding blocking solution at 4 degrees C overnight, washing with the washing solution, adding a culture supernatant of hybridoma cells to be detected, incubating, washing with washing liquid, adding horseradish oxidase-labeled goat anti-mouse IgG, incubating, washing with washing solution, adding 3% hydrogen peroxide in o-phenylenediamine substrate solution, terminating with sulfuric acid, reading OD value, selecting OD ratio hybridoma cells 5-10 times higher than the negative control, re-cloning, and expanding and freezing, (vii) sub-cloning hybridoma cells by using soft agar method, and cloning multiple times until it is 100% cell positive rate, resulting in a highly specific 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 hybridoma cell line, and (viii) expanding and culturing the above-mentioned highly specific 25-hydroxyvitamin D3 hybridoma cell strain, stopping the dosing when the cell concentration reaches 105/ml or more, continuously culturing until the cell culture medium turns yellow, collecting the culture solution, centrifuging in a centrifuge, filtering the supernatant is through a filter, placing the filtered supernatant in a protein-A affinity chromatography column, washing the column with phosphate buffer to remove the impurity protein, absorbing 5 times the column volume of glycine solution eluting the antibody protein, adding tris solution to neutralize glycine to pH 7.2, dialyzing against 10 column volumes of phosphate buffer for 16 hours, and filtering the dialyzed sample through a filter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the immunization amount of the mouse is 100 mu l/unit/time. In the step (vi), the washing liquid is phosphate buffer solution of 0.1% Tween 20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20), and the blocking solution is phosphate buffer containing 2% BSA. Preferred Method: In the step (v), the addition speed of the DMEM culture comprises 1 ml is added in 1 minute, 4 ml is added in 2 minutes, and the remaining 20 ml is added in 5 minutes.

TI Oxyfuel combustor exhaust gas cooling, heat            recovery and carbon dioxide collecting device for use            in gas turbine power plant, has carbonated water            supplied to hydrogen production system to produce            hydrogen and carbonated carbon dioxide.
AU YANG G W
   YANG S
AE YANG G W (YANG-Individual)
GA 2022B2246U
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a combustion chamber for                performing injecting oxygen and preheating                circulating water as working fluid with superheated                steam. The circulating water is preheated to 185                degree Celsius or higher through a circulating                water preheater and a condensate heater, and is                cooled to 105 degree Celsius. Liquid refrigerant                nitrogen is supplied from a nitrogen liquefaction                cooling system, and the amount produced by                combustion in the liquid refrigerant is transferred                to a condensed cooler. An exhaust heat absorber is                installed at an inlet through which a turbine                exhaust is sucked, and an exhaust mini-cooler is                installed in a rear end of the turbine                exhaust.
   USE - Oxygen fuel combustion turbine exhaust gas                cooling, heat recovery and CO2 capture device for a                gas turbine power plant.

AB    NOVELTY - Tri-metallic oxide foam catalyst composition,                comprises: 20-30 wt.% cupric nitrate trihydrate;                15-25 wt.% cobalt nitrate hexahydrate; 5-10 wt.%                nickel nitrate hexahydrate; and 45-55 wt.% ethylene                glycol.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                synthesizing tri-metallic oxide catalyst                composition, comprising (a) dissolving the cupric                nitrate trihydrate, cobalt nitrate hexahydrate and                nickel nitrate hexahydrate in ethylene glycol to                obtain a solution, (b) agitating the solution for                8-15 minutes at a room temperature followed by                subjecting the agitated solution to ultrasonication                for 30-70 minutes to obtain a blended form of                homogeneous precursor solution, (c) dispersing the                homogeneous precursor solution in a pre-heated                petri-dish, where dispersing of the precursor                solution into the petri-dish leads to boiling and                frothing of the precursor solution along with                release of certain gases to obtain a foam, and (d)                crushing the foam into fine powder to obtain the                trimetallic foam catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method: the                  homogeneous precursor solution is dispersed in                  the pre-heated petri-dish slowly in a circular                  motion to obtain appropriate boiling and frothing                  of the precursor solution and the ultrasonication                  was performed at a room temperature. Preferred                  Components: The cupric nitrate trihydrate, cobalt                  nitrate hexahydrate and nickel nitrate                  hexahydrate are used as nitrate precursors. The                  gases include carbon dioxide, ammonia, carbon                  mono-oxide or nitric oxide.
   EXAMPLE - 26.31% Cupric nitrate trihydrate, 19.73%                cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, and 6.57% nickel                nitrate hexahydrate were dissolved in 0.9 ml                ethylene glycol to obtain a solution. The solution                was agitated for 10 minutes at a room temperature                followed by subjecting the agitated solution to                ultrasonication for 60 minutes to obtain a blended                form of homogeneous precursor solution. The                homogeneous precursor solution was dispersed in a                pre-heated petri-dish, where dispersing of the                precursor solution into the petri-dish leads to                boiling and frothing of the precursor solution                along with release of certain gases to obtain foam.                The foam was crushed into fine powder to obtain the                tri-metallic foam catalyst composition.

Only pure oxygen is injected into the combustion chamber of the boiler, and the exhaust from this combustion chamber flows into this exhaust cooler and cooled with refrigerant R-134a and oxygen to collect steam. carbon dioxide is captured at sub-zero low temperature. Water as a (purified) working fluid is preheated with superheated steam in circulating water preheater by heat pump technology. Oxygen preheated in the steam cooler is further preheated in the oxygen preheater and coal dust, fine dust and sulfuric acid in the inlet exhaust cargo is collected along with condensate. Carbon dioxide captured at sub-zero temperatures is dissolved in cooling water and seawater with carbonated water in a mixer.

TI MWW/DDR-type gas-separation membrane for separating carbon dioxide gas from mixture comprises MWW- and DDR-type zeolites which are alternately provided with each other, and at least one of which contains material which is epitaxially grown.
AU CHOI J K
   JEONG Y H
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 2021E1844X
AB    NOVELTY - An MWW/DDR-type gas-separation membrane comprises greater than or equal to 1 MWW-type zeolite, and greater than or equal to 1 DDR-type zeolite. MWW-type zeolite and DDR-type zeolite are alternately provided with each other; and at least one of the MWW-type zeolite and the DDR-type zeolite contains a material which is epitaxially grown.
   USE - An MWW/DDR-type gas-separation membrane for separating carbon dioxide gas from a gas mixture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The gas separation membrane can have excellent separation effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of gas separation membrane which involves first zeolite formation step by producing first zeolite particles via hydrothermal synthesis method using a first zeolite precursor solution containing a first structure inducer and a first raw material on a support; and second zeolite formation step of growing second zeolite on first zeolite via hydrothermal synthesis method using a second zeolite precursor solution containing a second structure inducer and a second raw material. The first or second raw material contains silicon and/or aluminum. First zeolite is provided in a form of a set of particles on the support; and second zeolite includes the a material which is epitaxially grown on the first zeolite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: MWW-type zeolite and DDR-type zeolite are alternately and repeatedly provided to form an independent layer. MWW type zeolite has silicon:aluminum (Si:Al) molar ratio of 100:0-10. On the MWW-type zeolite, if the DDR type zeolite is provided in the first direction, the MWW-type zeolite comprises a set of MWW-type zeolite particles; and MWW-type zeolite particle has average length of 10 nm to 1 mu m in parallel with first direction; and on the DDR-type zeolite, if MWW-type zeolite is provided in the first direction, the DDR type zeolite consists of a set of DDR-type zeolite particles, and DDR-type zeolite particles have average length parallel to the first direction of 0.1-10 mu m. First zeolite has average length of the particles of 10 nm to 1 mu m, and molar concentration ratio of first zeolite precursor solution is Si:Al:structural inducer:solvent=100:0-10:10-500:500-20000, preferably 100:0-10:1-20000:500-20000; and molar concentration ratio of precursor solution of second zeolite is Si:Al:structural inducer:solvent=100:0-10:1-20000:500-20000, preferably 100:0-10:10-500:500-20000. Support body is alpha -alumina; gamma -alumina; polypropylene; polyethylene; PTFE; polysulfone; polyimide; silica; glass; mullite, zirconia; titania; titania; yttria; ceria; vanadia; silicon; stainless steel; carbon; calcium oxide; and/or phosphorus oxide. Preferred Processes: On the MWW-type zeolite, DDR-type zeolite is provided in a first condition, in a hydrothermal synthesis method, and is epitaxially grown; or on the DDR-type zeolite, MWW type zeolite is provided in a second condition, in a hydrothermal synthesis method, and is epitaxially grown. Preferred Conditions: First condition and second condition are independent and include hydrothermal synthesis at 100-200 degrees C for 0.5-15 days.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The first and second structure-inducing agents are selected from hexamethyleneimine (HMI), piperidine, adamantylamine, ethylenediamine, Methyltropinium salt, N,N,N-trimethyl-1-adamantylammonium (1TMAdaOH), N,N,N-trimethyl-1-adamantylammonium bromide (TMAdaBr), N,N,N-trimethyl-1-adamantylammonium (TMAdaF), N,N,N-trimethyl-1-adamantylammonium (TMAdaCl), N,N,N-trimethyl-1-adamantylammonium (TMAdaI), tetraethylammonium hydroxide (TEAOH), tetraethylammonium bromide (TEABr), tetraethylammonium fluoride (TEAF), tetraethylammonium chloride (TEACl), tetraethylammonium iodide (TEAI), dipropylamine, and/or cyclohexylamine. Preferred Processes: The method includes buffer portion formation step for forming a buffer portion covering at least a portion of a set of first zeolite particles of first zeolite forming step between first zeolite forming step and second zeolite forming step, where buffer portion precursor solution comprises adamantylamine:ethylenediamine:fumed silica:solvent ratio of 1-20:100-200:100:100-20000 in a molar concentration, and a hydrothermal synthesis method is used. In the buffer formation step, the first and second zeolite consists of mutually different kinds of zeolite, and the zeolite structure formed by the buffer precursor solution is provided and which is formed in the same manner as the second zeolite structure. First step of forming the zeolite and step for forming the second zeolite are independent; and after the hydrothermal synthesis, at a rising speed of 0.2-0.5 degrees C/minute, for 12-40 hours, the method further includes heating at 450-550 degrees C and calcining. The method further includes performing rapid heat treatment at 700-1200 degrees C for 10 s to 5 minutes after forming a second zeolite.

AU YE N
   CHEN Y
   JI X
   MU L
   ZHU J
   LU X
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2021D5540H
AB    NOVELTY - Nanomaterial with high-density ionic liquids surface, formed by loading ionic liquids on the surface of porous materials, is claimed. The density of the ionic liquid loaded on the surface is greater than 1.5 g/cm3, preferably 1.60-1.84 g/cm3. The cation in the ionic liquid is imidazolium cation, pyridinium cation, quaternary phosphine cation or quaternary ammonium cation. The anion in the ionic liquid is bis-trifluoromethanesulfonimide anion, acetate anion, amino acid anion or tris(pentafluoroethyl) trifluorophosphate anion. The ionic liquid is 1-hexyl-3-methylimidazolium bis-trifluoromethanesulfonimide salt or 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium acetate ionic liquid. The porous material is silicon dioxide, titanium dioxide, molecular sieve or activated carbon.
   USE - The nanomaterial with high-density ionic liquid surface is useful as a gas trapping material, preferably carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, sulfur dioxide and/or ammonia gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanomaterial with high density ion liquid surface can obviously enhance the trapping ability of gas by loading the ion liquid on the surface of the porous material, and has high recycling performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing nanomaterial with high-density ionic liquids surface; and
   (2) testing the density of ionic liquids surface of nanomaterials with high-density ionic liquids.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The preparation method utilizes organic solvent which is methanol, ethanol, acetone, toluene or ether. The testing method utilizes solvent which is squalane, n-heptane or isopentane.

PN CN113480697-A
TI Monodisperse carbondioxide and ammonia type anisotropic nanoparticle comprises middle particle comprising polyacrylic acid derivative and periphery particle of polystyrene.
AU LI X
   ZHANG X
   GONG Y
   ZHANG W
   TIAN X
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI HEFEI INST PHYS SCI (CAWZ-C)
GA 2021B9019L
AB    NOVELTY - Monodisperse carbondioxide and ammonia type anisotropic nanoparticle comprises middle particle comprising polyacrylic acid derivative and the periphery particle of polystyrene. The peripheral particle have a uniform particle size distribution and a particle size range of 280-400 nm.
   USE - Used as monodisperse carbondioxide and ammonia type anisotropic nanoparticle.
   ADVANTAGE - The monodisperse carbon dioxide and ammonia type anisotropic nanoparticle: preparation method avoids the use of emulsifiers and stabilizers which simplifies the process and prepares anisotropic nanoparticles with an ultra-narrow particle size distribution colloidal molecule carbon dioxide type or ammonia structure has been realized in a relatively short time thus provides the possibility for the application of anisotropic nanoparticles in many fields.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing monodisperse carbondioxide and ammonia type anisotropic nanoparticle, comprising (i) taking the acrylic monomer, crosslinking agent and initiator, dissolving in the de-ionized water, heating to 30-100degrees Celsius under the nitrogen atmosphere and reacting for 0.5-3 hours to obtain seed nano-particle dispersion; (ii) adding styrene into the reaction system in the step (i), reacting continuously for 1-8 hours and cooling to stop reaction; and (iii) centrifuging the reaction product at 3000-14000 revolutions/minute, taking the centrifuged product at the bottom of the centrifuge tube, redispersing it into the ethanol solution, repeating it for many times and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The middle particles and periphery particle form carbon dioxide type or ammonia type structure. The acrylic acid monomer comprises methacrylic acid, acrylamide, acrylic acid and/or N-isopropyl acrylamide. The cross-linking agent is an aqueous-soluble molecule with double-vinyl groups.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The initiator is persulfate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method of heating is carried out at 70-100degrees Celsius and reacting for 1-2 hours at 100-500 revolutions/minute. The reacting is carried out continuously for 3-5 hours. The preparation of monodisperse type anisotropic nanoparticle preferably comprises (a) dissolving 0.1-0.2 g ammonium persulfate in 100 ml deionized water to obtain a uniform solution, which is recorded as solution 1, dissolving 6-7 g N-isopropylacrylamide and 0.6-0.7g N,N-methylenebisacrylamide in 100 ml of deionized water to obtain a uniform solution which is recorded as solution 2, dissolving 1-2 ml acrylic acid in 100 ml deionized water to obtain a uniform solution, which is recorded as solution 3, measuring 3 ml solution 1, 10 ml solution 2 and 5 ml solution 3 in a reactor, heating to 30-100degrees Celsius in a nitrogen atmosphere and maintaining to react for 0.5-3 hours to obtain seed nanoparticle dispersion; (b) adding 2-8 ml styrene into the reaction system described in step (a), reacting continuously for 1-8 hours, and stopping to react after cooling down; and (c) centrifuging the reaction product at 3000-14000 revolutions/minute, collecting the centrifuged product at the bottom of the centrifuge tube, redispersing it in the ethanol solution, repeating it for many times and drying it.
DC A13 (Polymers of aromatic mono-olefins; including polystyrene.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.)
MC A02-A01; A04-C04A; A04-F01; A04-B01A; A08-S02; A10-B04; A12-S09

TI Use of volcanic basalt tuff of each fraction expressed in millimeters from Poland, as natural additive for production of cement, concrete and concrete prefabrication to reduce carbon dioxide emissions, and as substitute for fly ash.

AE STARZYK KS DORADZTWO BIZNESOWE KRZYSZTOF (STAR-Non-standard)
GA 2021E3109M
AB    NOVELTY - Use of volcanic basalt tuff of each fraction expressed in millimeters from Poland, is claimed as a natural additive for production of cement, concrete and concrete prefabrication to reduce carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions, and as a substitute for fly ash from coal combustion and blast furnace slag from steel production limiting the clinker content in cement. The volcanic basalt tuff is mined in opencast mines and pre-crushed to fractions of 0-800 mm containing natural ingredients such as silicon dioxide (SiO2), aluminum oxide (Al2O3), iron oxide (Fe2O3), calcium oxide (CaO), magnesium oxide (MgO), titanium oxide (TiO2), sodium oxide (Na2O) and potassium oxide (K2O) then transported and added via conveyors directly to the cement mill in a fraction of 0-90 mm in the cement plant to reduce the clinker content in the cement by 30% and to reduce CO2 emissions of Portland pozzolan cement by 30% and to enable the production of new cement.

PN CN113025778-A; CN113025778-B
TI Reducing consumption of carbon powder in electric            furnace oxidation process comprises leaving furnace in            electric furnace according to charging requirement,            smelting steel and adding auxiliary material and waste            steel material into furnace.
AU CHEN L
   ZHENG H
   WANG J
   ZHOU W
AE PANGANG GROUP PANZHIHUA IRON & STEEL RES (ANSH-C)
GA 202178968H
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing consumption of carbon powder                consumption in electric furnace oxidation,                comprises (a) charging process: leaving a furnace                in the electric furnace according to the charging                requirement for smelting steel, and adding                auxiliary material and waste steel material into                the furnace, (b) melting process: after                electrifying, melting the material, and adding                carbon powder in the material melting process, and                simultaneously blowing oxygen to the furnace, and                (c) blowing process: using the bottom blowing gas                mode; when the blowing begins to 1/3 stage,                introducing nitrogen, controlling the flow rate as                0.06-0.25 Nm3 /t blowing 1/3 to 2/3 stage using                carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide mixed gas,                controlling the flow rate is 0.10-0.35 Nm3 /t                blowing 2/3 to adopt carbon dioxide and argon mixed                gas before tapping, and controlling the flow rate                is 0.2-0.45 Nm3 /t minute.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing consumption                of carbon powder in electric furnace oxidation                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: promotes the absorption of the                carbon powder in the smelting process; improves the                utilization rate of the carbon powder; and solves                the problem that the existing electric furnace                oxidation steel-making consumes too much carbon                powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The content of calcium                  oxide in lime is 85-90 wt.%; the fluorite                  component has more than 48 wt.% calcium fluoride,                  more than 50 wt.% calcium oxide and unavoidable                  impurities (remaining amount). The chemical                  components of the slagging agent are 20-30 wt.%                  calcium oxide, 40-50 wt.% aluminum oxide, 10-20                  wt.% iron oxide, greater than or equal to 15 wt.%                  singly charged cation aluminum, greater than or                  equal to 0.05 wt.% phosphorus, greater than or                  equal to 0.15 wt.% sulfur, 5-15 wt.% silico                  dioxide, and unavoidable impurities (remaining                  amount). In the step (c), nitrogen is used from                  the beginning of blowing to the 1/3 stage. In the                  step (c), a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and                  carbon monoxide is used in the 1/3 to 2/3 stage                  of blowing. In the step (c), a mixed gas of                  carbon dioxide and argon is used before blowing                  2/3 to tapping.

PN JP2021090996-A
TI Metal flux-cored wire used for gas-shielded arc welding of steel structure, is obtained by filling flux containing metal fluoride, and silicon oxide, to steel outer layer containing carbon, silicon, manganese, nickel, and magnesium.
AU IWAGAMI T
   CHIBA R
   IKEDA M
   SASAKI M
AE NIPPON STEEL WELDING & ENG CO LTD (YAWA-C)
GA 202165408F
AB    NOVELTY - A metal flux-cored wire is obtained by filling a flux to a steel outer layer. The steel outer layer comprises 0.04-0.18 %mass carbon, 0.4-1.2 %mass silicon, 1.5-3.5 %mass manganese, 0.2-1.5 %mass nickel, 0.03-0.20 %mass magnesium, and remainder of iron. The flux comprises 0.01-0.15 metal fluoride, 0.01-0.15 %mass sodium oxide, sodium fluoride, potassium fluoride, and/or potassium oxide, less than 0.20 %mass silicon oxide, and remainder of iron of iron alloy powder, and unavoidable impurities.
   USE - Metal flux-cored wire used for gas-shielded arc welding of steel structure. Uses include but are not limited to shipbuilding, bridge, marine structure, and steel frame.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal flux-cored wire has excellent welding workability, strength, hot-tear sensitivity, toughness in low temperature, and quality of welded portion, and provides stable arc by reducing sputter generation amount even when using carbon dioxide gas or argon-carbon dioxide mixed gas as shield gas. In the gas arc welding, in downward multipass welding, the blowhole or slag-inclusion defects at the root run of the single-sided welding using a solid backing material, are reduced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The metal flux-cored wire comprises 0.01-0.50 %mass titanium, and 0.002-0.015 %mass boron.

AU BRAUER A
AE UNIV FREIBERG TECH BERGAKADEMIE (UYFB-C)
GA 202158244F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves extracting the substance from the substance mixture using a first solvent stream which contains a first solvent to obtain a loaded solvent stream. The extracted material is converted from the loaded solvent stream to a second solvent provided with a second solvent stream to obtain a third solvent stream containing first solvent, second solvent and extracted material. The first solvent is separated from the third solvent stream to obtain a fourth solvent stream which contains second solvent and extracted substance. The first solvent has a pressure of at least 6 MPa and the second solvent is in the liquid state. The first solvent is carbon dioxide and that the second solvent is a polymer, a resin, a fat or a mixture of two or more of these substances.
   USE - Method for high-pressure extraction of substance from substance mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The pressurization of the carbon dioxide from the low pressure level in the low pressure separator to the high pressure level in the extractor can be avoided.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for high-pressure extraction of a substance from a mixture of substances.

AB    NOVELTY - A cement paste is formed by providing growth                medium; providing greater than or equal to 1                biomineralizing microorganisms and biomineralizing                macroorganisms; producing mineral particles using                greater than or equal to 1 biomineralizing                microorganisms and biomineralizing macroorganisms;                and forming greater than or equal to 1 Portland                cement and Portland cement paste using the mineral                particles.

Mineral particles are an additive to                  Portland cement paste. Mineral particles comprise                  amorphous silicon dioxide. Mineral particles                  comprise crystalline calcium carbonate. Preferred                  Properties: Morphology of mineral particles is                  rounded or angular. Average cross-sectional                  dimension of mineral particles is 10 nm to 1 um,                  or 1 um to 5 mm. Preferred Processes: Mineral                  particles are pressed together to form larger                  particles having an average cross-sectional                  dimension of up to about 5 cm. Cement paste                  formation further includes grinding the mineral                  particles to an average particle size of 1-100 um                  to form a powder; heating the ground particles                  with other additives to greater than or equal to                  1500 degrees C to form clinker; and grinding the                  clinker to form Portland cement. Cement paste                  formation further includes adding water to the                  Portland cement to form the cement paste.

GA 2021337192
AB    NOVELTY - Hybrid oxygen supply device (10) for pure oxygen combustion power generation comprises a first reactor for separating pure oxygen of required concentration from the incoming air, a combustion chamber connected to the first reactor through a circulation line and mixing and burning pure oxygen and fuel separated through the first reactor, and second reactor (30) connected to the circulation line between the first reactor and the combustion chamber and separating pure oxygen of predetermined concentration from the incoming air. Part of the carbon dioxide contained in the exhaust gas discharged after combustion in the combustion chamber is introduced into the first reactor through the circulation line and circulated as a transport gas for moving the pure oxygen separated in the first and second reactors to the combustion chamber.
   USE - Used as a hybrid oxygen supply device for pure oxygen combustion power generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: has good stability; and high oxygen permeability; is economical; maximizes combustion efficiency; reduces emission of toxic gases; and easily captures the high concentration carbon dioxide contained in the exhaust gas.

AB    NOVELTY - Method of performing reaction in reverse flow                reactor involves reacting a fuel mixture comprising                fuel, 0.1 vol.% or more oxygen and diluent under                combustion conditions in a combustion zone in a                reverse flow reactor to form a flue gas and to heat                a conversion zone to an average conversion zone                temperature of 400degrees Celsius or more, and                exposing a reactant stream comprising 5.0-50 vol.%                carbon dioxide, 20 vol.% or more hydrogen to the                catalyst composition in the conversion zone under                reverse water gas shift reaction conditions to form                a product stream comprising water and carbon                monoxide, in which direction of flow for reactant                stream in the reaction zone is reversed relative to                a direction of flow for the fuel mixture. The                conversion zone comprises catalyst composition. The                ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide is 2.0 or                more.
   USE - Method of performing reaction in reverse flow                reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows efficient heating of                surfaces in the reactor to provide heat for the                endothermic reverse water gas shift reaction when                reducing or minimizing incorporation of combustion                products into the desired reaction products.

PT P
PN US2021048408-A1
TI Calibrating pH sensor fixed within enclosed            vessel, by introducing buffer and carbon dioxide into            enclosed vessel, measuring pH signal of solution with            pH sensor, calculating pH value, and calibrating pH            sensor with calibration parameter.
AU LEE H
   LEE K S
AE ERBI BIOSYSTEMS INC (ERBI-Non-standard)
GA 202118316B
AB    NOVELTY - Method (M1) for calibrating a pH sensor fixed                within an enclosed vessel involves (a) introducing                a buffer into the enclosed vessel at a controlled                temperature, (b) introducing a gas mixture                comprising carbon dioxide (CO2) into the enclosed                vessel to form gas mixture and buffer solutions                having variable concentrations of CO2, (c)                measuring a pH signal of each solution with the pH                sensor and a CO2 concentration of a headspace gas                of each solution, (d) calculating a pH value for                each solution from the CO2 concentration with a                calibration curve for the buffer, (e) calculating a                calibration parameter for the pH sensor from the pH                signal and the respective pH value for each                solution with a calibration curve for the pH                sensor, and (f) calibrating the pH sensor with the                calibration parameter.
   USE - The method (M1) is useful for calibrating pH                sensor fixed within enclosed vessel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method automatically calibrates pH sensors                without sampling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (a) a reactor system comprising an enclosed                reactor, a temperature control subsystem, and a                controller; and
   (b) a method (M2) for facilitating pH sensor                calibration without sampling in a bioreactor system                comprising an enclosed bioreactor, a pH sensor and                a CO2 sensor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves (i)                  measuring a pH signal of at least four solutions                  and a CO2 concentration of the headspace gas of                  the at least four solutions, calculating at least                  four respective pH values, and calculating the                  calibration parameter for the pH sensor from the                  at least four pH signals and the at least four                  respective pH values, and (ii) measuring the pH                  signal with the pH sensor and the CO2                  concentration after the solution reaches                  equilibrium. The method further involves                  generating the calibration curve for the buffer.                  The calibration curve for the buffer is in the                  form of: pHfit = A+Basterisklog10(C+CO2perc),                  where A, B and C are fitting parameters and                  CO2perc is the measured CO2 concentration as a                  percentage of the headspace gas total                  composition. The method involves (i) calculating                  the fitting parameters A, B and C by introducing                  a stable mixture of CO2 and air into the buffer                  at a controlled temperature to form stable                  mixture and buffer combinations, measuring a pH                  signal of the combination and a CO2 concentration                  of a headspace gas of the combination, fitting                  the pH signal and the CO2 concentration in the                  calibration curve formula, and determining the                  fitting parameters A, B, and C which produce                  lowest least squares error between the fitted pH                  value, pHfit, and the measured pH signal, and                  (ii) measuring the pH signal of the combination                  and the CO2 concentration of the headspace gas of                  the combination after the combination reaches                  equilibrium. The calibration curve for the pH                  sensor is in the form of: pHsensor =                  (log2(S-W)-log2(X-S))asteriskY+Z, where W, X, Y                  and Z are fitting parameters, and S is the pH                  signal. The method further involves (i)                  calculating the fitting parameters W, X, Y and Z                  by fitting the pH signal and the pH value in the                  calibration curve formula, and determining the                  fitting parameters W, X, Y and Z which produce                  lowest least squares error between the fitted pH                  value, pHsensor, and the measured pH signal, (ii)                  introducing a humidification solution into the                  vessel in an amount effective to compensate for                  evaporation of the buffer, and (iii) sterilizing                  the enclosed vessel by at least one of steam                  sterilization, autoclave sterilization, gamma                  irradiation, e-beam irradiation, and ethylene                  oxide sterilization. The buffer is controlled to                  a predetermined temperature of                  30-39degreesC.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); ; S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); T01 (Digital Computers); T06 (Process and Machine Control)
MC D05-H09; D05-A03B; J04-C03; J04-C04A; J04-C02A; J04-X03; J04-C02; S03-H03C; S03-F10; S03-E14P; S03-E03C; T01-J04A; T06-B06
IP G01N-027/416; G01N-033/00; G05D-021/02

AU KONG L
AE KONG L (KONG-Individual)
GA 202103851B
AB    NOVELTY - Structure has a damping plate (300) formed with a damping cavity. A bottom part of the damping cavity is placed with calcium carbonate. A top part of a raft plate (100) is provided with a slurry inlet hole (301). A top side wall of the damping plate is provided with a liquid storage well (400), which is connected with the slurry inlet hole. A liquid storage well is stored with diluted hydrochloric acid. The diluted hydrochloric acid enters into the damping cavity and carbon dioxide gas generated by reaction of the calcium carbonate to form a damping barrier in horizontal direction. The dilute hydrochloric acid is entering into the damping cavity through the slurry inlet hole and the carbon dioxide gas generated by the calcium carbonate reaction to form a damping barrier in the horizontal direction under the action of earthquake horizontal force.
   USE - Used as shock-proof foundation structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure: reduces and consumes seismic vibration energy in a horizontal direction and a vertical direction; has better damping effect, compact structure and low investment cost.

TI Preparing a solid mixed type brown sugar ginger tea for e.g. improving and relieving dysmenorrhea and other symptoms comprises e.g. taking brown sugar, white sugar, red date and turmeric powder for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU KONG Y
   WANG M
   GUO X
   HAN L
AE SHAANXI GONGFUSHOU FOOD CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2020B5863B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a solid mixed type brown sugar ginger tea comprises (i) taking 65-70 pts. wt. brown sugar, 25-30 pts. wt. white sugar, 5-10 pts. wt. red date powder and 3-5 pts. wt. turmeric powder for performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, (ii) placing the brown sugar and white granulated sugar and pulverizing and passing through a 60-70 meshes sieve, (iii) pouring all ingredients into tank mixer and stirring,(iv) uniformly stirring the mixture continuously and placing into rotary granulator, (v) placing the prepared brown sugar ginger tea particles into tray and using industrial fan to cooling, (vi) placing the cooled semi-finished products into granulator for granulation and placing into container for grade selection, (vii) placing the semi-finished products in container into vibrating screen for 3-level screening, circulating and granulating and placing into the container, and (viii) continuously placing the product into the automatic granule packaging machine for packing.

   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and convenient, consumes low energy; and reduces the microorganism pollution risk and the obtained product is smooth, convenient to carry and drink; has excellent flavor.

TI Dendritic copper electrode with hydrophobic            surface used in electrocatalytic carbon dioxide            reduction, comprises a gas diffusion layer and a copper            layer which is deposited on the surface of the gas            diffusion layer.
AU GAO M
   NIU Z
   GAO F
   YANG P
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 202094604P
AB    NOVELTY - Dendritic copper electrode with hydrophobic                surface comprises a gas diffusion layer and a                copper layer which is deposited on the surface of                the gas diffusion layer, where the microscopic                morphology of the copper layer is dendritic.
   USE - Dendritic copper electrode with hydrophobic                surface used in electrocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The dendritic copper electrode with                hydrophobic surface efficiently enriches the                cations in the electrolyte to form a local high                electric field, increases the carbon-carbon                coupling rate, and exhibits excellent                electrocatalytic CO2 reduction activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing a dendritic copper                electrode with hydrophobic surface, which involves                using a copper salt aqueous solution as an                electroplating solution, using a gas diffusion                layer material as a working electrode, and using an                Ag/AgCl electrode as a reference electrode, and                performing potentiostatic deposition to obtain a                dendritic copper electrode with hydrophobic                surface, where the potential of the constant                potential deposition relative to the Ag/AgCl                reference electrode is -0.1 to -0.7 volt and the                time of the constant potential deposition is 100 to                700 s; and
   (2) a flow electrolytic cell, which comprises                cathode of the flow electrolytic cell is the                hydrophobic dendritic copper electrode and a                dendritic copper electrode with hydrophobic                surface.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas diffusion                  layer is a gas diffusion carbon paper. The carbon                  rod is used as the counter electrode of the                  three-electrode system. Preferred Conditions: The                  water contact angle of the copper layer is 150                  degrees . The copper ion concentration in the                  copper salt aqueous solution is 0.05-0.5 mole/L.                  The pH value of the copper salt aqueous solution                  is 2-4. The constant potential deposition is                  performed in a three-electrode system.

PN US10704143-B1; US2020240016-A1; KR2020093447-A; TW202028515-A
TI Forming oxide film by providing precursor to            reaction space including substrate and susceptor, and            forming oxide film on substrate by introducing carbon            dioxide as reactant gas into reaction space while            applying pulse radio frequency.
AU HISAMITSU T
   OKURA S
AE ASM IP HOLDING BV (ASMI-C)
   ASM IP HOLDING BV (ASMI-C)
GA 202060901D
AB    NOVELTY - An oxide film is formed by providing precursor                to reaction space including substrate and                susceptor; and forming oxide film on substrate by                introducing carbon dioxide (CO2) as a reactant gas                into the reaction space while applying a pulse                radio frequency (RF) power having duty cycle less                than or equal to 50% to an RF plate to generate                plasma of the reactant gas, where RF plate is                provided in the reaction space to face the                susceptor, and where providing and forming steps                are repeated for a predetermined number of times,                and frequency of pulse RF power is less than or                equal to 250 Hz.
   USE - Formation of oxide film.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of CO2 as the reactant gas and the use                of the pulse RF power reduce the amount of film                loss. The duty cycle of the pulse RF power and the                amount of film loss have a substantially                proportional relationship. In the domain where the                duty cycle is less than or equal to 50%, the                configuration having the pulse RF power with a                frequency of less than or equal to 250 Hz can                facilitate reduction in the amount of film loss.                The reduction in the duty cycle can increase the                radical reaction, and reduce the damage to the                underlying film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameter: Duty cycle of the pulse                  RF power is 0-15%. Preferred Components:                  Precursor includes silicon (Si), titanium (Ti),                  or germanium (Ge), and oxide film includes                  silicon oxide (SiO), titanium (TiO), or germanium                  oxide (GeO). Preferred Processes: The method                  includes purging the precursor without adsorbing                  to the substrate remaining in the reaction space,                  after providing the precursor and before                  generating the plasma; and purging the reaction                  space after forming the oxide film, where the                  reaction space is supplied with the reactant gas                  through the precursor providing, the precursor                  purging, the oxide film forming, and the reaction                  space purging. When the oxide film is formed,                  oxygen plasma is generated in addition to the                  plasma of the reactant gas. When the oxide film                  is formed, plasma of the reactant gas is                  generated without providing oxygen to the                  reaction space.

PN CN111227076-A
TI Preparing cold extraction black tea powder            comprises e.g. cooling reverse osmosis water, carrying            out preliminary crushing treatment of black tea leaves            and adding frozen reverse osmosis water and preliminary            crushed material for leaching.
AU LI Y
AE SICHUAN STRONG FOOD CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2020530234
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cold extraction black tea powder                comprises (i) cooling reverse osmosis water to 2                plus minus 1 degrees C to obtain frozen reverse                osmosis water; (ii) carrying out preliminary                crushing treatment of black tea leaves to prepare                preliminary crushed tea material; (iii) adding the                frozen reverse osmosis water and the preliminary                crushed tea material into the extraction tank at a                weight ratio of 12:1 for leaching for 16 hours,                blowing carbon dioxide gas into the extraction                tank, where blowing volume is 20 liters/hours,                controlling the extraction temperature at 1-3                degrees C, and performing the extraction for every                2 hours to obtain the extraction tea liquid; (iv)                filtering the extracted tea liquid to obtain tea                soup, freeze concentrating the tea soup to obtain a                cold extract tea concentrate, freeze-drying the                cold extract tea concentrated solution, crushing                again to obtain a second crushed tea material and                sieving the second crushed tea material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cold                extraction black tea powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can apply ultra-low temperature                extraction of the initially crushed black tea                leaves with carbon dioxide to prevent oxidation;                and retains the ingredients.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                cold extraction black tea powder, prepared as                mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method of preliminary                  crushing treatment of black tea leaves to obtain                  preliminary crushed tea materials comprises                  squeezing, tearing and curling the black tea                  leaves to produce uniformly granular tea                  particles with a diameter of 2.5-3 mm as                  preliminary crushed tea materials. The step of                  filtering the extracted tea liquid to obtain a                  tea soup uses an inorganic ceramic membrane to                  filter the extracted tea liquid. The method                  comprises freeze-dried tea concentrate is                  freeze-dried and crushed again and in the step of                  obtaining the second crushed tea material, a                  vacuum freeze-drying device is used to freeze-dry                  the concentrated cold tea extract, freezing                  temperature is -20 degrees C, the vacuum degree                  is 0.3 mbar, and the freeze drying for 2 hours.                  In the step of sieving the second crushed tea                  material and obtaining the cold-extracted black                  tea powder and using a vibrating sieve for                  sieving the second crushed tea material. In the                  step of freezing and concentrating the tea soup                  to obtain a cold extract tea concentrate                  comprises using progressive freezing and                  concentrating device to freeze and concentrate                  the tea soup. In the step of preliminary crushing                  black tea leaves in the step of preparing                  preliminary crushed tea material, the black tea                  leaves are Cinnamomum zeylanicum black tea                  leaves. Preferred Components: The mesh number of                  the vibrating screen is 40 meshes. In the step of                  freezing and concentrating the tea soup to obtain                  a cold extract tea concentrate, the soluble solid                  content of the cold extract tea concentrate is                  36%.

PN CN110484346-A; CN110484346-B
TI Regenerating waste lubricating oil based on supercritical carbon dioxide method comprises e.g. pretreating waste lubricating oil, removing air from extraction device, injecting pre-treated waste lubricating oil into autoclave and heating.
AU YANG X
   XIANG S
   SU P
   WANG J
   ZHU L
   HE Y
   LIU P
AE PLA GROUND FORCE SERVICE COLLEGE (PLAG-Non-standard)
   PLA GROUND FORCE SERVICE COLLEGE (PLAG-Non-standard)
GA 2019A0386X
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating waste lubricating oil based on supercritical CO2 comprises using supercritical CO2 extraction device comprising autoclave and gas phase outlet end of autoclave connected to analysis device. The analysis device comprises steel cylinder connected to needle valve at the inlet and outlet and steel cylinder is placed in an ice-water mixture. The method comprises (i) pretreating waste lubricating oil; (ii) removing air from extraction device; (iii) injecting pre-treated waste lubricating oil into the autoclave; (iv) heating autoclave, injecting liquid CO2 into the autoclave after the temperature is constant and keeping liquid CO2 in full contact with pre-treated waste lubricating oil under agitation; and (v) opening needle valve at the inlet end of the cylinder to allow the base oil components obtained by supercritical CO2 extraction and separating in the autoclave to flow into the cylinder and opening needle valve at the outlet end of the cylinder to discharge the CO2 gas.
   USE - The method is useful for regenerating waste lubricating oil based on supercritical carbon dioxide method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves performance indicators including flash point, pour point, viscosity index, acid value, sulfate ash significantly, yield of recycled oil reaches 19.8% and reduces metal content.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device utilized in the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The co-solvent is also added to the liquid CO2, where co-solvent is a non-polar co-solvent. The non-polar co-solvent includes n-hexane. The content of metal elements in the base oil components separated by supercritical CO2 extraction decreased to 1/6-1/2 of the original waste lubricating oil. The mass percentage of co-solvent in the liquid CO2 is 5-15%. The mass percentage of co-solvent in the liquid CO2 is 10%. Preferred Method: The extraction pressure in autoclave is 6-18 Mpa, temperature is 305-350 k and time is 10-200 minutes. The extraction pressure in autoclave is 16 Mpa, temperature is 308.2k and time is 180 minutes. The pretreatment method of waste lubricating oil comprises natural precipitation of the used lubricating oil, filtering and de-watering under reduced pressure.

The section includes lubricants of non-petroleum origin eg silicone oils (C10M).)

PT P
PN CN110301671-A
TI Method for processing hemp fiber for cigarette, involves selecting fresh industrial hemp stalks, chopping and placing in air, followed by placing in extraction vessel, subjecting to steam explosion, water washing and drying, and mixing.
AU ZHANG W
   XIE J
   ZHAN Z
   LI J
AE YUNNAN FENGSHENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2019961194
AB    NOVELTY - A hemp fiber processing method involves selecting fresh industrial hemp stalks, chopping and placing in the air for 12-24 hours to obtain material-A, adjusting the water content of the material-A to 8-10% to obtain material-B. The material-B is placed in an extraction vessel of a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, and the liquid is extracted at an extraction temperature of 50-55 degrees C, an extraction pressure of 30-35 MPa, and a carbon dioxide flow rate of 30-50 kg/h to obtain liquid-C and residue-D. The residue-D is immersed in water for 40-80 minutes to a water content of 80-90% to obtain material-E. The material-E is subjected to steam explosion, water washing and drying to obtain material-F. The material-F with an amount of 5-10 mass% is added with, stabilizer and liquid-C, uniformly mixed and subjected to extrusion granulation to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for processing hemp fiber for cigarette.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables processing hemp fiber with better effect of improving activity of the tobacco fiber to reduce the activity of harmful substances, and improved economic value of industrial hemp stalk.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The stabilizer is calcium oxide, calcium hydroxide, aluminum hydroxide, titanium oxide, diethyl phosphate or ammonium phosphite.

   R2 = 1-20C alkylamine.

PN CN110143604-A; CN110143604-B
TI Recovering alkali, selenium and arsenic in arsenic alkali slag by smelting smelting comprises e.g. subjecting arsenic alkali slag to water immersion, filtration and washing, concentrating the arsenic alkali solution and adjusting the pH.
AU ZHENG Y
   LONG H
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 201974019M
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering alkali, selenium and arsenic in arsenic alkali slag by smelting comprises (1) subjecting arsenic alkali slag to water immersion, filtration and washing to obtain slag and arsenic alkali solution, (2) concentrating the arsenic alkali solution to an arsenic concentration of 12-17 g/l, passing through carbon dioxide, filtering to obtain sodium hydride and primary crystallization mother liquor, and concentrating the primary crystallization mother liquor to an arsenic concentration of 48-60 g/l, passing carbon dioxide and filtering to obtain sodium hydrogencarbonate and secondary crystallization mother liquor, (3) adjusting the pH of the secondary crystallization mother liquor with acid to 1-4, heating the reaction, and filtering to obtain black selenium and selenium-removing liquid, and (4) completing selenization, reducing the solution by sulfur dioxide, concentrating by evaporation, cooling and crystallizing, and filtering to obtain arsenic trioxide and arsenic removal liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering alkali, selenium and arsenic in arsenic alkali slag by smelting smelting.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, realizes efficient recovery of alkali, selenium and arsenic in arsenic alkali slag, and realizes enrichment of bismuth is achieved without the need for oxidative deep dislocation, has low acid consumption and reducing agent usage, and has good economic and environmental benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), crushing the arsenic slag to a particle size of less than 5 mm and water immersed, where the water immersion conditions are: liquid to solid ratio of 2-5 ml/g, and leaching temperature of 80-110 degrees C for 1-3 hours, returning the slag to the smelting system. In the step (2), the reaction temperature of the alkali-recovered carbon dioxide is 15-40 degrees C, and the pH value of the end point is 7.5-8.5. In the step (2), washing the mixture with water at 10-35 degrees C when the arsenic content in the sodium hydrogencarbonate is less than 0.5 wt.%, where the liquid-solid ratio of 1 4 ml/g, stirring for 20-40 minutes, filtering, washing sodium hydrogencarbonate and washing liquid, heating sodium hydrogencarbonate at 100-300 degrees C for 1-4 hours to obtain sodium carbonate and carbon dioxide, returning crude antimony sodium antimony smelting refining process, carbon dioxide returning to the alkali recovery process recycling, washing liquid returning to the arsenic slag leaching process, and when the content of arsenic in the sodium hydrogencarbonate is less than or equal to 0.5wt.%, the sodium carbonate and carbon dioxide are obtained by directly heating at 100-300 degrees C for 1 to 4 hours, returning the sodium carbonate to the smelting crude smelting process, carbon dioxide to and the alkali recovery process for recycling. In the step (3), the acid is sulfuric acid, the sulfuric acid is adjusted by the sulfuric acid to adjust the pH, and the alkali recovery process is recycling. In the step (3), the reaction temperature is 60-100 degrees C, and the time is 20-90 minutes. In the step (4), passing the selenium-removing solution through the sulfur dioxide at 20-50 degrees C under stirring to reduce arsenic (V) to arsenic (III), then concentrating the concentrated liquid for 3-5 times, and cooling the concentrated liquid and crystallizing at 0-30 degrees C, and filtering to obtain the arsenic trioxide and arsenic removal liquid, and incorporating the arsenic removal liquid into the liquid after removing the selenium. In the step (4), when the purity of arsenic trioxide is less than 95%, washing the water is 30-60 degrees C and the liquid to solid ratio of 1-4 ml/g for 20-60 minutes, filtering, washing, arsenic trioxide and washing solution, cooling the solution and crystallizing at 0-10 degrees C, filtering to obtain a solution of Glauber's salt and sodium sulfate, removing the sodium sulfate, and incorporating the solution is into the step (3) after the selenium removal solution.
