PN JP2019122908-A; JP7042479-B2
TI Processing of unburned methane gas in exhaust gas of gas engine, involves providing photocatalyst capable of decomposing unburned methane in exhaust flow path, transforming water and unburned gas to hydrogen and carbon dioxide and oxidizing.
AU KISHI T
   NISHIO S
   KURIHARA T
AE NAT MARITIME PORT & AVIATION TECHNOLOGY (NAMA-Non-standard)
   NAT MARITIME PORT & AVIATION TECHNOLOGY (NAMA-Non-standard)
GA 201964572A
AB    NOVELTY - Processing of unburned methane gas involves providing a photocatalyst capable of decomposing the unburned methane gas from the combustion unit (200) in the exhaust flow path of combustion unit to burn methane gas, transforming water and unburned methane gas to hydrogen and carbon dioxide using the photocatalyst under irradiation of light, introducing unconverted secondary unburned methane gas and hydrogen into the oxidation catalyst provided in the downstream side of the photocatalyst, and oxidizing secondary unburned methane gas using the heat generated during oxidation of hydrogen.
   USE - Processing of unburned methane gas in exhaust gas of gas engines.
   ADVANTAGE - The unburned methane gas contained in exhaust gas can be removed efficiently and economically. The discharge amount of the unburned methane gas can be reduced. The processing capacity of the unburned methane gas can be increased. The deteriorated photocatalyst can be regenerated without stopping the process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for unburned methane gas processing apparatus (100).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure of unburned methane gas processing apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Unburned methane gas processing apparatus (100)
   Toxic substance processing unit (102)
   Photocatalyst tank (104)
   Light irradiation unit (106)
   Oxidation-catalyst tank (108)
   Control unit (110)
   Combustion unit (200)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The methane gas is a portion of combustion gas in the combustion unit. The catalyst amount of the photocatalyst is larger than the catalyst amount of the oxidation catalyst. The unburned gas is processed using the photocatalyst such that quantity of secondary unburned methane gas is 1/2 or less of the quantity of unburned methane gas. The harmful substance contained in the exhaust gas is pretreated and introduced into the photocatalyst. The photocatalyst is provided in the upstream side and downstream side of the oxidation catalyst, the photocatalyst of the upstream side deteriorated during processing of unburned methane gas is replaced with the downstream photocatalyst, and regenerated using the heat generated during oxidation. The photocatalyst is bypassed or temporarily heated during start and/or stop of combustion unit, such that exhaust gas is not passed along the photocatalyst. When the temperature of the oxidation catalyst is less than predetermined temperature, the oxidation catalyst is heated. The concentration of secondary unburned methane gas, performance of the photocatalyst, or performance of the oxidation catalyst are detected, and the process according to the detection result is performed.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photocatalyst contains titanium oxide. The oxidation catalyst contains a noble metal.

The high-concentration solution is carbon dioxide absorption liquid.
   USE - Salt difference power generation based energy self-intelligent farm system.
   ADVANTAGE - The smart farm system is capable of producing electricity using the difference in concentration between fresh water, waste or fertilizer solution, while being capable of supplying a growth material suitable for the growth of crops. Therefore, the smart farm system can achieve self-sufficiency in energy and contribute to the effective growth of crops, thus solving the water-energy-food bond problem. Therefore, the intelligent farm system can be applied to smart farm chain enterprises, environmentally friendly cities, cities with anti-aging functions, and large-scale environmentally-friendly water-energy-disposable self-sufficient smart cities.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of an energy self-intelligent farm system. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PN CN108339289-A; CN208212571-U
TI Centrifugal rotating crystallizer useful in tower for efficiently recovering carbon dioxide, comprises a crystallizer body, a motor that drives the crystallizer body to rotate, and a heat sink mounted on both sides of the crystallizer body.
AU CHEN G
AE SHANGHAI TAOCHUAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201861758J
AB    NOVELTY - Centrifugal rotating crystallizer comprises a crystallizer body, a motor that drives the crystallizer body to rotate, and a heat sink mounted on both sides of the crystallizer body. The crystallizer body includes a central shaft, multiple crystal tubes, a fixed structure and a reinforcing structure, where one end of the central shaft is connected to the motor, and the crystallization tubes are arranged around the central axis and parallel to the central axis, and the two of the crystallization tubes fixed to a fixed structure. The fixing structure is sleeved on the central shaft and fixed in position. The reinforcing structure strengthens the relative positional relationship between the crystallization tube and the central axis. The motor rotates to drive the central axis to rotate. The crystallizer body is rotated as a whole in a turn.
   USE - The crystallizer is useful in tower for efficiently recovering carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The crystallizer does not influence production, has high device utilization rate, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for tower comprising a tower body is arranged with multiple centrifugal sodium hydrogencarbonate crystallizers from top to bottom, where the top of the tower body is provided with a liquid inlet, and a discharge port provided at the bottom.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The heat dissipation structure includes multiple heat sinks. The heat sink is fixed on the fixed structure in a central axis. The crystallization tubes are spaced apart and arranged in a diamond shape centering on the central axis. The crystallization tube is a hollow tube processed into a steel sheet. The motor adopts a direct current motor to drive the main body of the crystallizer to rotate in a positive and negative direction. The fixed structure adopts a hollow disc structure. Each of the centrifugal sodium bicarbonate crystallizers in the tower body is provided with end face circular sealing plates at both ends. The tower includes a liquid collecting plate for collecting the flow provided on both sides above each of the centrifugal sodium hydrogencarbonate crystallizers. The liquid collecting plates on both sides above the centrifugal sodium hydrogencarbonate crystallizer are inclined downward to block the ascending air current. The collected liquid in the column is led to the centrifugal sodium hydrogencarbonate crystallizer. The liquid inlet of the column body is fed to a mixed aqueous solution containing Na2CO3 at concentration of 20-40%.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN US2018038277-A1
TI Closed-loop gas turbine generator for usable power            production, has oxygen transport reactor which includes            ion transport membrane between feed and permeate sides            and bypass conduit connecting feed and permeate            sides.
AU HABIB M A
   AHMED P
   NEMITALLAH M A
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
   UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 201813605L
AB    NOVELTY - The generator (10) has an oxygen transport                reactor (18) having a selectively oxygen-permeable                ion transport membrane (24) between a feed side                (20) and a permeate side (22) and a bypass conduit                connecting the feed and permeate sides. The reactor                transports oxygen in exhaust from a first gas                turbine (14) via the membrane and combusted with                syngas of the exhaust, producing carbon dioxide and                water for driving a second gas turbine (26). A heat                exchanger (28) exchanges heat between compressed                air (CA) from a compressor (34) and exhaust output                from the second gas turbine, producing pre-heated                air.
   USE - Closed-loop gas turbine generator, which is a                combustion-based gas reactor for producing usable                power for driving external loads.
   ADVANTAGE - The pre-heating of the compressed air improves                energy conservation by reducing the fuel flow rate                into the combustion chamber, thus improving overall                system efficiency. The heat exchange results in                condensation of the water, which can then be easily                separated out, leaving behind only carbon dioxide                gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of a                closed-loop gas turbine generator.
   Generator (10)
   First gas turbine (14)
   Oxygen transport reactor (18)
   Feed side (20)
   Permeate side (22)
   Ion transport membrane (24)
   Second gas turbine (26)
   Heat exchanger (28)
   Compressor (34)
   Compressed air (CA)

PT P
PN CN107601755-A; CN107601755-B
TI Treating ecological farm sewage involves separating solid and liquid from farm waste and sewage, then putting sewage into digester for anaerobic treatment, putting biogas slurry effluent into aerobic and oxygen unit with microorganisms.
AU JIANG J
   LUO L
AE FUJIAN FANGMING ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 201808082C
AB    NOVELTY - Treating ecological farm sewage involves separating solid and liquid from farm waste and sewage, then putting sewage into digester for anaerobic treatment, putting biogas slurry effluent into aerobic and oxygen unit with aerobic microorganisms in presence of oxygen under biological metabolism to degrade organic matter in sewage, where aerobic microorganisms include facultative microorganisms. The obtained wastewater is put into electrocatalytic oxidation unit for degradation of organic molecules and ammonia nitrogen complex macromolecule into carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas.
   USE - Method for treating ecological farm sewage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating ecological farm sewage involves separating solid and liquid from farm waste and sewage, then putting sewage into digester for anaerobic treatment, putting biogas slurry effluent into aerobic and oxygen unit with aerobic microorganisms in presence of oxygen under biological metabolism to degrade organic matter in sewage, where aerobic microorganisms include facultative microorganisms. The obtained wastewater is put into electrocatalytic oxidation unit for degradation of organic molecules and ammonia nitrogen complex macromolecule into carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas. The treated sewage put into microalgae unit to absorb and digest ammonia nitrogen and total phosphorus, and release of oxygen occurs under oxidation reaction, and then degradation of chemical oxygen demand (COD), and finally put into separation tank to separate sewage to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Condition: Method for treating ecological farm sewage involves cleaning farm manure, then washing, separating solid and liquid, collecting the manure and waste into organic fertilizer. The sewage put into digester, under condition of no energy consumption, followed by addition of hydrolytic acidifying bacteria. The macromolecular organic substances are degraded into small organic molecules to improve biodegradability of sewage and produce biogas. The sewage put into aerobic and oxygen unit and then biochemical reaction is carried out to release energy, generate low-level inorganic or organic matter, and removed sludge. The electrocatalytic oxidation unit is electropositive to generate hydroxyl radical ion with strong oxidizing property, and produce gas bubbles, and directly reacted with organic pollutants in sewage to degrade into carbon dioxide, water and simple organic matter and effectively removes suspended solids in water. The electrocatalytic oxidation unit works under electric catalysis, oxidation, adsorption, energy redox reaction, and electric flocculation process to break macromolecules into micromolecules. The microalgae are continuously cultured and treated, and sewage is introduced into cultivation tank at ratio of 10-15 wt.%, and cultured in incubation tank for 3-5 days to achieve best growth state, and then continue to supplement biogas slurry and discharge algae liquid. The sewage is pumped into algae liquid separation tank through microalgae unit by water pump to separate algae liquid, and water. The discharged water is tested and algae mud is treated by filter press. The solid residue is used to make organic fertilizer. The obtained filtrate is returned to microalgae unit.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
MC C04-F01; C05-C01; C05-C02; C05-C04; C11-B04; C14-X; D04-A01F1; D04-A01F3; D04-A01J; D04-A01K2; D04-B07C; D05-A04A; H09-F03

PT P
PN CN109127656-A; CN109127656-B
TI Performing mechanochemical conversion and recovery of sodium and fluorine-containing compounds in aluminum electrolysis hazardous wastes involves crushing, grinding and homogenizing aluminum electrolytic hazardous waste slag.
AU LIU E
   SHAO X
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 2019049301
AB    NOVELTY - Performing mechanochemical conversion and recovery of sodium and fluorine-containing compounds in aluminum electrolysis hazardous wastes involves crushing, grinding and homogenizing aluminum electrolytic hazardous waste slag to obtain hazardous waste powder with particle of less than 200 micrometer, analyzing and determining the molar amount of sodium and fluorine in unit mass aluminum electrolytic hazardous waste powder or quality, and analyzing and determining the mole number or mass of cyanide ions in unit mass aluminum electrolytic hazardous waste powder. The grinding body is added to the conversion mill, and the conversion mill is subjected to air evacuation or nitrogen replacement, and the metered hazardous waste powder is added to the conversion mill. The mass ratio of hazardous waste powder to the grinding body is controlled at 1: (0.2-10).
   USE - Method for performing mechanochemical conversion and recovery of sodium and fluorine-containing compounds in aluminum electrolysis hazardous wastes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform mechanochemical conversion and recovery of sodium and fluorine-containing compounds in aluminum electrolysis hazardous wastes, which has simple process, easy mass production, low production cost, no waste pollution, and is environmental friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The metered water is added to the conversion mill, and the solid-liquid mass ratio of the hazardous waste powder to the water is 1: (1 to 10). The conversion agent A is added to the conversion mill according to the amount of sodium or fluorine-containing compound in the hazardous waste powder converted to the chemical reaction ratio of the corresponding product by 1-3 times, or 5-80 wt.% of the hazardous waste powder. The grinding aid is added to the conversion mill in an amount of 0-1 wt.% of the waste waste powder. The conversion mill is turned on, and the rotation speed of the conversion mill is controlled to be 10-1000 revolutions per minute, transformation temperature is 10-110 degrees C, and the conversion time is 0.5-5 hours. The slurry is transferred to a stirred reactor for gel breaking and aging treatment, such that the temperature is controlled at 60-150 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours. The slurry obtained is subjected to solid-liquid separation by filtration or centrifugal separation, and the aqueous solid phase is in a reforming mill or a stirred reactor. The water is used as a detergent to repeat a number of dispersion washings at a solid-liquid ratio of 1: (1-10), and the solid-liquid separation and washing until the content of soluble ions in the solid phase reaches the required level to obtain a soluble mixture separation liquid C and a wet solid phase of fluorine-containing minerals and carbon. The wet fluorine-containing mineral obtained and the solid phase of the carbon are placed in a reforming mill. The grinding body is added in a mass ratio of the hazardous waste powder to the grinding body 1: (0.2-10). The solid-liquid mass ratio of hazardous waste powder and water of 1: (1-5) is added. The conversion agent B is added to the conversion mill according to the amount of sodium or fluorine-containing compound in the hazardous waste powder converted to the chemical reaction ratio of the corresponding product by 1-3 times, or 5-80 wt.% of the hazardous waste powder. The slurry is sampled and analyzed in batches until the sodium ion content in the slurry solid phase reaches the enterprise standard during the conversion process, and the soluble flourine ion content stops the conversion mill when the national emission standard is reached. The slurry obtained is subjected to solid-liquid separation by filtration or centrifugal separation. The aqueous solid phase is subjected to multiple dispersion washings and solid-liquid separation in a conversion mill or a stirred reactor by using water as a detergent at a solid-liquid ratio of 1: (1-10). The content of soluble ions in the solid phase is washed until it reaches the enterprise standard to obtain a solid mixture of the soluble mixture separation liquid D and the wet fluorine-containing mineral and carbon. The wet solid phase obtained is dried or heat-treated at a temperature of 80-300 degrees C for 1-10 hours, and then pulverized to obtain a mixed powder material of a fluorine-containing mineral and carbon. The mixed powder material of fluorine-containing mineral and carbon is calcined in a high-temperature furnace at an air atmosphere and a temperature of 700-1200 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours, where the carbon component is completely oxidized and burned, and the residue is a mixture of fluorine-containing minerals. The soluble mixture separation liquid C obtained and the soluble mixture separation liquid D obtained are separately concentrated or crystallized to obtain a soluble mixture concentrated solution C or a soluble solid phase mixture C, a soluble mixture concentrated solution D or a soluble solid phase mixture D, respectively. The 1-5 times the stoichiometric ratio of cyandide ions are converted to nitrogen or ammonia and carbon dioxide in hazardous waste powder, or 0.1-10 wt.% hazardous waste powder. The cyanide conversion agent is added to oxidatively convert the cyanide-containing compound into harmless nitrogen or ammonia and carbon dioxide. The conversion system is analyzed and inspected in batches until the cyanide ion content in the system reaches the national emission standard and the conversion reaction is stopped. The ammonia, carbon dioxide, and water gasses generated are converted and dried by an absorbent, and the generated reducing gas is collected or directly oxidized and burned.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The aluminum electrolysis hazardous waste is all solid waste containing flourine ion compounds and cyanide containing compounds produced in electrolytic aluminum production exceeding national emission standards. The pH is controlled to not lower than 6, and the proton acid substance is not used, and the sodium compound and the fluorine compound conversion agent, cyanide conversion agent, and grinding aid substance do not contain chlorine element. The elements in the hazardous waste mainly include two or more of carbon, sodium, flourine, aliminium, calcium, oxygen, nitrogen, lithium, magnesium, silicon, potassium, and iron. The sodium-containing compound includes two or more of sodium fluoride, sodium aluminum hexafluoride, sodium cyanide, sodium ferrocyanide. The fluorine-containing compound includes two or more of sodium fluoride, sodium aluminum hexafluoride, aluminum fluoride, lithium fluoride, calcium fluoride, magnesium fluoride, and potassium fluoride. The conversion agent A includes one or more of oxides and hydroxides corresponding to each element of calcium, aluminium, magnesium, strontium, barium, lanthanum, and cerium. The conversion agent B includes one or more of a sulfate, a nitrate, a carbonate, an acetate, and a citrate corresponding to each element of calcium, aluminium, magnesium, strontium, barium, lanthanum, and cerium. The cyanide conversion agent comprises one or two of sodium percarbonate, sodium peroxide, potassium peroxide, calcium peroxide, strontium peroxide, barium peroxide, hydrogen peroxide, ammonium persulfate, sodium persulfate, potassium persulfate, potassium permanganate, oxygen, ozone. The grinding aid comprises one or more of a polymeric polyol, a polymeric alcohol amine, a triisopropanolamine, ethylene glycol, propylene glycol, glycerol, diethylene glycol, triethanolamine, amide, stearic acid, oleic acid, hexa-bias, sodium phosphate and sodium stearate. The conversion agent and the grinding aid are added in a solid state, or an aqueous solution or a gas state, and are added in one time, in a batchwise manner, or continuously. The conversion mill is a modified airtight ball mill, and the machine body is provided with an inlet and outlet of the grinding body, powder inlet, liquid material inlet, gas outlet, slurry outlet, cleaning vent, gas sampling port, and slurry sampling. The abrasive body is one or more of corundum balls, zirconia balls, porcelain balls, steel balls, stainless steel balls, and alloy balls.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)
MC E05-B03; E05-P; E10-B03B2; E10-C02A; E10-C04H; E10-C04J2U; E10-C04L2; E10-E04H1; E10-E04H2; E10-E04J4; E11-Q02B; E11-W; E31-B03C; E31-D02; E31-D03; E31-E01; E31-E02; E31-E03; E31-E05; E31-K05D; E32-B; E33-G; E33-S; E34-B; E34-C02; E34-C03; E34-D; E34-E01; E34-E02A; E35-S; A11-C03A; A11-C07; A12-W11F; A12-W12F; P43-E05
IP B09B-003/00; B09B-005/00; C01B-009/08; C01F-011/22

PT P
PN CN109127655-A; CN109127655-B
TI Converting sodium and fluorine-containing compound in aluminum electrolytic waste carbon residue comprises e.g. crushing, grinding and homogenizing aluminum electrolytic waste carbon residue to obtain waste carbon residue powder.
AU LIU E
   LV S
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 2019049302
AB    NOVELTY - Converting sodium and fluorine-containing compound in aluminum electrolytic waste carbon residue comprises e.g. (1) crushing, grinding and homogenizing aluminum electrolytic waste carbon residue to obtain waste carbon residue powder with particles less than or equal to 200 mu m; analyzing and determining the mole number or mass of sodium and fluorine in unit mass waste carbon residue powder; analyzing and determining the mole number or mass of CN- ions in the unit mass of waste carbon residue powder; adding the grinding body to conversion mill, evacuating air or replacing nitrogen gas (N2) by conversion mill, adding the metered waste carbon residue powder to the conversion mill, controlling the mass ratio of waste carbon residue powder to grinding body is 1: (0.2-10), adding metered water to the conversion mill, controlling the solid-liquid mass ratio of waste carbon residue powder to water: 1: (1-10); according to amount of sodium and fluorine-containing compounds in waste carbon residue powder.
   USE - The method is useful for converting sodium and fluorine-containing compound in aluminum electrolytic waste carbon residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, economical, and environmentally-friendly; and can achieves the goal of harmless and resourceful disposal of aluminum electrolytic waste carbon residue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Converting sodium and fluorine-containing compound in aluminum electrolytic waste carbon residue comprises (1) crushing, grinding and homogenizing aluminum electrolytic waste carbon residue to obtain waste carbon residue powder with particles less than or equal to 200 mu m; analyzing and determining the mole number or mass of sodium and fluorine in unit mass waste carbon residue powder; analyzing and determining the mole number or mass of CN- ions in the unit mass of waste carbon residue powder; adding the grinding body to conversion mill, evacuating air or replacing nitrogen gas (N2) by conversion mill, adding the metered waste carbon residue powder to the conversion mill, controlling the mass ratio of waste carbon residue powder to grinding body is 1: (0.2-10), adding metered water to the conversion mill, controlling the solid-liquid mass ratio of waste carbon residue powder to water: 1: (1-10); according to amount of sodium and fluorine-containing compounds in waste carbon residue powder, converting to amount of 1-3 times stoichiometric ratio of corresponding product, or adding the conversion agent to conversion mill in amount of 5-80 wt.% of waste carbon residue powder; adding grinding aid to the conversion mill according to the amount of waste carbon slag powder 0-1%; (2) turning on the conversion mill, controlling the rotational speed of the conversion mill at 10-1000 revolutions/minute, and the transformation temperature at 10-110 degrees C; sampling and analyzing the slurry in batches during the conversion process, until the Na+ ion content in the solid phase of the slurry meets the enterprise standard, and the soluble F-ion content meets the national emission standard; and stopping conversion mill; (3) completing step (2), transferring the slurry to stirred reactor for gel breaking and aging treatment, controlling temperature at 60-150?C, processing time is 0.5-5 hours; (4) subjecting the slurry obtained in the step (3) to solid-liquid separation by filtration or centrifugal separation, adding aqueous solid phase into reforming mill or stirring reactor, many times repeating of dispersing washing and solid-liquid separation with water as detergent at solid-liquid mass ratio of 1: (1-10), washing until the content of soluble ions in the solid phase meets the enterprise standard, combining the separation liquid to obtain mixture of wet fluorine-containing minerals and carbon, that is, aqueous solid phase and dilute solution of mixture; (5) drying the aqueous solid phase obtained in the step (4) at 80-300 degrees C or heat treatment for 1-10 hours, further pulverizing to obtain mixed powder material of fluorine-containing minerals and carbon; calcining the mixed powder material of fluorine-containing mineral and carbon in air atmosphere and high-temperature furnace at temperature of 700-1200 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours, completely oxidizing and burning carbon component, the residue is fluorine-containing minerals mixture; (6) concentrating or crystallizing dilute solution of the mixture obtained in the step (4) to obtain concentrated solution or solid phase mixture; (7) converting 1-5 times the stoichiometric ratio of CN- ions to N2 or ammonia and carbon dioxide in waste carbon residue powder, or 0.1-10 wt.% of waste carbon residue powder, adding cyanide conversion agent to either step (2), or step (3), or step (6), oxidatively converting the cyanide-containing compound to harmless N2 or ammonia (NH3) and carbon dioxide, analyzing and checking the conversion system in batches, until the CN-ion content in the system meets the national emission standards, and stopping the conversion reaction; and (8) subjecting ammonia, carbon dioxide, water gas generated in the step (2) or the step (3) or the step (6) to absorption conversion and drying, collecting generated reducing gas or directly oxidizing and burning. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for conversion system comprising waste carbon residue crushing device, milling device, conversion mill, stirring reactor and solid-liquid separation device which are sequentially connected, the solid-liquid separation device is directly connected to the concentration or crystallization equipment, drying or heat treatment equipment; the drying or heat treatment equipment is connected to the crushing device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In all the steps, controlling pH to be at not less than 6, do not use protic acid substances; the sodium-containing and fluorine-containing compound-containing conversion agent, cyanide conversion agent, and grinding aid substance do not contain chlorine. Preferred Components: The waste carbon slag powder comprises carbon, sodium, fluorine, aluminum, calcium, oxygen, nitrogen, lithium, magnesium, silicon, potassium, and/or iron; the sodium-containing compound includes sodium fluoride, sodium hexafluoroaluminate, sodium cyanide, and/or sodium ferrocyanide; the fluorine-containing compound includes sodium fluoride, trisodium hexafluoroaluminate, aluminum fluoride, lithium fluoride, calcium fluoride, magnesium fluoride, and/or potassium fluoride. The conversion agent containing sodium compound and fluorine-containing compound includes oxide, hydroxide, sulfate, nitrate, carbonate, acetate, and/or citrate corresponding to each element of calcium, aluminum, magnesium, strontium, barium, lanthanum, and cerium. The cyanide conversion agent includes sodium Percarbonate(Na2CO3.1.5H2O2), K2CO3.1.5H2O2, sodium peroxide, potassium peroxide, calcium peroxide, strontium peroxide, barium peroxide, hydrogen peroxide, ammonium persulfate, sodium persulfate, potassium persulfate, potassium permanganate, dioxygen (O2), and/or trioxygen (O3). The grinding aid comprises polymeric polyol, polymeric alcohol amine, triisopropanolamine, ethylene glycol, propylene glycol, glycerol, diethylene glycol, triethanolamine, amide, stearic acid, oleic acid, sodium hexametaphosphate, and/or sodium stearate. In the method, the conversion agent and grinding aid are added in solid state, aqueous solution or gas state, and are added in one time, in batchwise manner, or continuously.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The conversion system further comprises condensing equipment, exhaust gas collection and absorption equipment, storage equipment and analytical equipment; the condensing equipment is connected to the concentration or crystallization equipment, the exhaust gas collection and absorption equipment is connected with the milling equipment, the conversion mill, the stirred reactor, and the condensing equipment, the storage equipment comprises powder material storage device connected to the pulverizing device and a storage device for the concentrated liquid or crystalline product, analytical equipment analyzes and samples samples taken from milling equipment, conversion mills, stirred reactors, concentration or crystallization equipment, drying or heat treatment equipment, and storage equipment. The waste carbon residue crushing device comprises jaw crusher, cone crusher, column crusher, double shaft crusher or impact crusher; the milling device comprises column mill, raymond mill, vertical mill, wind sweep or roller press, and homogenizing device for selecting a powder screening powder; the concentration or crystallization device is a multi-effect evaporation concentration or crystallization device; the conversion mill is a modified airtight ball mill, and the machine body is provided with an inlet and outlet of the grinding body, a powder inlet, a liquid material inlet, gas outlet, slurry outlet, cleaning vent, gas sampling port, slurry sampling port; the grinding body is corundum balls, zirconia balls, porcelain balls, steel balls, stainless steel balls, and/or alloy balls.

PT P
PN CN114538470-A; CN114538470-B
TI Preparation of ammonia used in e.g. textile            industry, involves adding diol, urea and catalyst to            kettle-type reactor, heating, raising temperature,            reacting to obtain mixture of ammonia gas, carbon            dioxide and diol, passing to condensers and processing            to obtain aqueous ammonia.
AU ZHANG S
   TENG W
   CAO D
AE SHANDONG HAIKE XINYUAN MATERIAL TECHNOLO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202274939E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ammonia involves adding diol,                urea and catalyst in mass ratio of                1.1-2.0:1:0.01-0.08 to kettle-type reactor (A),                stirring and steam tracing, heating, continuously                stirring for 2 hours, raising the temperature of                reactor (A) to 110-130degrees Celsius, reacting to                obtain mixture of ammonia gas, carbon dioxide and                diol as ammonia gas crude product, passing the                mixture through condenser (A) and condenser (B),                returning the liquid phase to kettle-type reactor                (A) to continue the reaction, subjecting crude                ammonia product to subsequent processing process to                obtain liquid ammonia or aqueous ammonia, raising                the temperature of the material in reactor (A) to                140-170degrees Celsius, reacting to obtain ammonia                gas crude product, passing to condensers (A) and                (B), returning liquid phase to reactor (A),                processing to obtain liquid ammonia or ammonia                water, and passing liquid-phase product obtained                after reaction in reactor (A) to material buffer                tank for subsequent processing.
   USE - Preparation of ammonia used in textile                industry and chemical industry to manufacture                various ammonium salts, aminating agent for organic                synthesis, and reducing agent in denitration                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is continuous process on the whole,                and uses same reactor for mixing the material and                carrying out the reaction, thus reducing the                reaction complexity, and adopts two condensers to                realize efficient separation of gas and liquid                phases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of ammonia involves adding diol,                urea and catalyst in mass ratio of                1.1-2.0:1:0.01-0.08 to kettle-type reactor (A),                stirring and steam tracing under absolute pressure                of 5-30 kPa, heating to 60-80degrees Celsius,                continuously stirring for 2 hours, so that urea is                completely dissolved in diol, raising the                temperature of reactor (A) to 110-130degrees                Celsius, reacting under stirring condition for 2                hours to obtain mixture of ammonia gas, carbon                dioxide and diol as ammonia gas crude product,                passing the mixture through condenser (A) and                condenser (B), returning the liquid phase to                kettle-type reactor (A) to continue the reaction,                subjecting crude ammonia product to subsequent                processing process to obtain liquid ammonia or                aqueous ammonia, raising the temperature of the                material in kettle-type reactor (A) to                140-170degrees Celsius, reacting under stirring                condition for 4 hours to obtain ammonia gas crude                product, passing to condensers (A) and (B),                returning the liquid phase to kettle-type reactor                (A) to continue the reaction, processing to obtain                liquid ammonia or ammonia water, passing                liquid-phase product i.e. crude cyclic carbonate                product obtained after 4 hours of reaction in                kettle-type reactor (A) to material buffer tank                through reactor pump for subsequent processing,                adding reaction mixture to kettle-type reactor (B)                when material of the reactor (A) begins to heat and                dissolve according to mass ratio of diol, urea and                catalyst under absolute pressure of 5-30 kPa,                stirring and steam tracing, heating the mixture to                60-80degrees Celsius, reacting under stirring                condition for 2 hours, raising the temperature of                the material in reactor (B) to 110-130degreesC for                pre-reaction, stirring for 2 hours to obtain crude                ammonia product, passing to condensers (A) and (B),                returning the liquid phase to the kettle-type                reactor (B) to continue the reaction, subjecting                the ammonia gas crude product to the subsequent                process for processing to obtain liquid ammonia or                aqueous ammonia, raising temperature of the                material in kettle type reactor (B) to                140-170degrees Celsius, reacting under stirring                condition for 4 hours to obtain ammonia gas crude                product, passing to condensers (A) and (B),                returning liquid phase to the kettle-type reactor                (B) to continue the reaction, processing the crude                ammonia product to obtain liquid ammonia or aqueous                ammonia, passing liquid-phase product i.e. crude                cyclic carbonate product obtained after 4 hours of                reaction in kettle-type reactor (B) to material                buffer tank through reactor pump (B) for subsequent                processing, adding reaction mixture in reactor (B)                to kettle-type reactor (C) when the pre-reaction in                the reactor (A) ends, and the material in the                reactor (B) begins to heat up and dissolve,                stirring and steam tracing under absolute pressure                of 5-30 kPa, heating the mixture in the reactor (C)                to 60-80degrees Celsius, continuously stirring for                2 hours, raising the temperature of the material in                reactor (B) to 110-130degreesC for pre-reaction,                stirring for 2 hours to obtain crude ammonia                product, passing to condensers (A) and (B),                returning the liquid phase to the kettle-type                reactor (B) to continue the reaction, subjecting                the ammonia gas crude product to the subsequent                process for processing to obtain liquid ammonia or                aqueous ammonia, raising temperature of the                material in kettle type reactor (B) to                140-170degrees Celsius, reacting under stirring                condition for 4 hours to obtain ammonia gas crude                product, passing to condensers (A) and (B),                returning liquid phase to the kettle-type reactor                (B) to continue the reaction, processing the crude                ammonia product to obtain liquid ammonia or aqueous                ammonia, passing liquid-phase product i.e. crude                cyclic carbonate product obtained after 4 hours of                reaction in kettle-type reactor (C) to material                buffer tank through reactor pump (C) for subsequent                processing, then adding materials in proportion to                the reactor (A) when the pre-reaction in the                reactor (B) ends, and the material in reactor (C)                begins to heat and dissolve, and repeating the                reaction, which ensures continuous production of                ammonia.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The diol is ethylene                  glycol or propylene glycol.

PN CN109593818-A
TI Detecting influence of total particle phase of heated non-burning cigarette on cell lipid peroxidation, comprises e.g. utilizing a linear smoking machine, placing flask on the ultrasound system and collecting the dimethyl sulfoxide extract.
AU GUAN Y
   LI X
   HE D
   ZHANG W
   GAO Q
   XU Y
   TANG J
   ZHU D
   ZHANG X
   HAN J
AE CHINA TOBACCO YUNNAN IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 201934986R
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting influence of total particle phase of heated non-burning cigarette on cell lipid peroxidation, comprises e.g. utilizing a linear smoking machine with a suction capacity of 55 ml, a duration of 3 seconds, and a suction frequency of 30 seconds, continuous suction of 10 ports, with a diameter of 44 mm of Cambridge filter for the collection, the mass of the flue gas trap is weighed before and after the suction, and calculating the mass mTPM of the total non-combusted cigarette, placing the captured Cambridge filter in a 50 ml flask and adding dimethyl sulfoxide to make the concentration of the total non-combusted cigarette phase in dimethyl sulfoxide 40 mg/ml, placing the flask on the ultrasound system for 30 minutes, collecting the dimethyl sulfoxide extract by filtration through a sterile filter paper, adding 10 ml RPMI1640 cell culture medium, and calculating the cell concentration of the human nasal epithelial cell single cell suspension by a hemocytometer counting method.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting influence of total particle phase of heated non-burning cigarette on cell lipid peroxidation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting influence of total particle phase of heated non-burning cigarette on cell lipid peroxidation, comprises (i) utilizing a linear smoking machine with a suction capacity of 55 ml, a duration of 3 seconds, and a suction frequency of 30 seconds, continuous suction of 10 ports, with a diameter of 44 mm of Cambridge filter for the collection, the mass of the flue gas trap is weighed before and after the suction, and calculating the mass mTPM of the total non-combusted cigarette mass according mTPM = m1-m0, where: m0- the mass of the flue gas trap before pumping, in milligrams (mg), M1 - the mass of the flue gas trap after pumping in milligrams (mg), placing the captured Cambridge filter in a 50 ml flask and adding dimethyl sulfoxide to make the concentration of the total non-combusted cigarette phase in dimethyl sulfoxide 40 mg/ml, placing the flask on the ultrasound system for 30 minutes, collecting the dimethyl sulfoxide extract by filtration through a sterile filter paper, dispensing into a 1 ml cryotube, and storing at -80 degrees C until use, after resuscitation of human nasal epithelial cells RPMI2650, inoculating into a 25 cm2 cell culture flask, adding 10 ml RPMI1640 cell culture medium, inoculating in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide incubator, observing invert confluence and morphology of the cultured cells under a microscope, when the cells were grown to a confluence of 70-90%, removing the RPMI1640 cell culture medium in the culture flask, washing twice with a phosphate buffer solution, discarding the washing solution, adding 1 ml of 0.25% (w/v) trypsin solution to a monolayer for 1-2 minutes, and suspending with RPMI1640 cell culture medium to form a single cell suspension, (iii) cell concentration calculation of single cell suspension of human nasal epithelial cells: calculating the cell concentration of the human nasal epithelial cell single cell suspension by a hemocytometer counting method to obtain a single cell suspension per ml number of living cells, (iv) inoculation of human nasal epithelial cell single cell suspension: diluting the single cell suspension of human nasal epithelial cells counted to 2x 105 cells/ml with RPMI1640 cell culture medium, and inoculated into a 100 mm cell culture dish medium, 6 ml per dish, and placing the cell culture dish in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, (v) diluting the sample prepared with phosphate buffered saline into a working solution of 400 mu g/ml, taking out the cell culture dish to remove the cell culture liquid, adding different doses of the test liquid according to Table 1 in the cell culture dish, each well is set with 3 duplicate wells, and placing in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, (vi) removing the medium in the cell culture dish, washing the cells with phosphate buffered saline phosphate buffer, and adding a trypsin solution with a mass concentration of 0.25%, suspending the cells in each well with RPMI1640 cell culture medium, colleting in a 15 ml centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 1000 revolutions/minute at 4 degrees C for 10 minutes, and removing the supernatant, (vii) adding 100 mu l cell lysate to each centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 0 degrees C for 1 minutes, centrifuging at 1600 revolutions/minute for 10 minutes at 4 degrees C, and testing the supernatant taken, (viii) diluting the malondialdehyde standard to 1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50 mu M with distilled water to obtain standard dilutions of different concentration gradients, (ix) adding 0.1 ml of the supernatant to be tested and the standard dilutions of different concentration gradients to different centrifuge tubes, adding 0.2 ml sulfur at a concentration of 0.185 mg/ml, mixing the barbituric acid MDA detection working solution, heating the boiling water bath at 100 degrees C for 15 minutes, centrifuging at 1000 revolutions/minute for 10 minutes at room temperature, adding 200 mu l supernatant to a 96-well plate, measuring the absorbance at 532 nm by a microplate reader, and (x) preparing a standard curve based on the absorbance values of the standard solutions, and calculating the amount of malondialdehyde in the sample.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN108839744-A
TI Bicycle structure comprises frame and front wheel, frame includes hollow shaft, input axle frame fixed to input end of hollow shaft, and center axle support, hollow shaft disposed along length direction of bicycle.
AU ZHANG D
AE ZHANG D (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201894368A
AB    NOVELTY - Bicycle structure comprises frame and front wheel. The frame includes a hollow shaft, an input axle frame fixed to an input end of the hollow shaft, and a center axle support fixed to a front end of the hollow shaft, the hollow shaft being disposed along a length direction of the bicycle. An input axle is fixed on the input axle frame, input hub is mounted on the input axle, and a front sleeve is arranged on the rotating sleeve of the hollow shaft. The input end of the front sleeve provided with a sliding strip, and input end of the front sleeve slidably mounted with input sleeve by a sliding strip. The hollow shaft has a hollow inner cavity, and the hollow inner cavity is mounted with a first motor and a battery. The battery is powered by the first motor, and the output end of the first motor is connected to a screw. A screw cap is mounted on the screw, a guide post is disposed on a radial direction of the nut.
   USE - Used as bicycle structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure is simplified; speed adjusting structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Bicycle structure comprises frame and front wheel. The frame includes a hollow shaft, input axle frame fixed to input end of the hollow shaft, and a center axle support fixed to a front end of the hollow shaft, the hollow shaft being disposed along a length direction of the bicycle. An input axle is fixed on the input axle frame, an input hub is mounted on the input axle, and a front sleeve is arranged on the rotating sleeve of the hollow shaft. An input end of the front sleeve is provided with a sliding strip, and an input end of the front sleeve is slidably mounted with a input sleeve by a sliding strip. The hollow shaft has a hollow inner cavity, and the hollow inner cavity is mounted with a first motor and a battery. The battery is powered by the first motor, and the output end of the first motor is connected to a screw. A screw cap is mounted on the screw, a guide post is disposed on a radial direction of the nut, a strip groove is disposed on the hollow shaft, and an annular groove is disposed on the input sleeve. The position of the strip groove corresponds to the input sleeve, and the guide post is slidably disposed in the annular groove through the strip groove. A front bearing surface of the front bushing and an input end surface of the input bushing are uniformly distributed with multiple of bearing mounting brackets in a circumferential direction. A ceramic bearing is mounted on each bearing mount. The rotation axis of the ceramic bearing is parallel to the axial direction of the hollow shaft, and the center shaft is mounted with the central shaft. A front sprocket wheel is mounted on the central shaft, and a circumscribing portion is vertically disposed on an outer edge of the front sprocket input toothed disc is mounted on the input hub, and multriple of teeth are vertically disposed on the input toothed disc. The teeth on the front toothed disc face the outer ring gap of two adjacent ceramic bearings on the front bushing. The tooth portion on the input toothed disc faces an outer ring of two adjacent ceramic bearings on the input sleeve, and the tooth portion of the front toothed disc cooperates with a ceramic bearing on a bearing mounting support of the front sleeve. The tooth portion of the input toothed disc cooperates with a ceramic bearing on a bearing mounting frame of the input sleeve; the middle shaft frame is coated with an anti-rust paint. The raw material of the anti-rust coating comprises 10 pts. wt. chlorosulfonated polyethylene resin, 20 pts. wt. aqueous-soluble epoxy resin, 20 pts. wt. styrene-acrylic emulsion, 20 pts. wt. fluororesin-reinforced hydrophobic material, 3 pts. wt. curing agent, 10 pts. wt. filler, 2 pts. wt. flatting agent. The filler is obtained by mixing aluminum tripolyphosphate, zinc phosphate and barium sulfate in a weight ratio of 5:5:5. The preparation of the fluorinated resin hydrophobic material is as follows (i) mixing uniformly 2 pts. wt. n-butyl titanate, 10 pts. wt. absolute ethanol, and 1 pts. wt. 10 mol/l aqueous nitric acid to obtain a solution A; (ii) mixing uniformly 5 pts. wt. montmorillonite, 10 pts. wt. anhydrous ethanol y and introducing into 30 pts. wt. solution A, stirring, allowing to stand, drying and calcining at 500 degrees C for 4 hours; (iii) mixing uniformly 10 pts. wt. hexafluorobutyl methacrylate, 10 pts. wt. butyl acrylate, 10 pts. wt. methyl methacrylate, 2 pts. wt. modified montmorillonite, 2 pts. wt. silane coupling agent KH792, 2 pts. wt. potassium persulfate, placing into the first processing equipment, and filling the first processing equipment with carbon dioxide for gas replacement, and placing first processing equipment in a water bath, and charging continuously the carbon dioxide into the first processing equipment to the first internal pressure of the processing equipment is 10 MPa, and maintaining for 10 hours, discharging the carbon dioxide to obtain the material B; and (iv) placing the material B into the second processing equipment containing xylene, increasing the temperature of the second processing equipment to 60 degrees C, keeping for 5 minutes, cooling, and taking out the material C, and washing with anhydrous ethanol and deionized water successively and drying to obtain fluororesin hydrophobic material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the bicycle structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The front wheel includes a support frame and a cutter ring that are rotatably connected to the front axle. The support frame is equilateral triangle, the front axle is located at the center of the triangle, and three spring pressure bars are arranged between the three corner points of the support frame and the cutter ring. A vertical spring bar that connects the corners of the triangle to the center of the triangle when unstressed and the spring strut includes a barrel hinged to the support frame and a rod hinged to the cutter ring, a spring is mounted on the bottom of the barrel, and the other end of the spring is disposed on the rod body.

PN CN108753702-A
TI New subpopulation of human periodontal ligament stem cells enriched by cell surface markers useful e.g. for preparing regenerative medicine and tissue engineering materials for bone diseases, and transferring CD18 gene.
AU XU S
AE NANJING TAISHENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018910911
AB    NOVELTY - Subpopulation of human periodontal ligament stem cells enriched by cell surface markers, where the stem cells are human periodontal ligament stem cells, and the cell surface marker is CD18, STRO-1, CD106/VCAM-1, CD146/MUC-18, HOP-26, CD49A/integrin beta 1 and/or SB-10/CD166, is new.
   USE - The stem cells is useful for preparing regenerative medicine and tissue engineering materials for bone diseases, and transferring CD18 gene into stromal stem cells by transgenic technology (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The stem cells provides a method for detecting the biological characteristics of an enriched subset of periodontal ligament stem cells, detecting colony formation ability related to dryness, detecting proliferative ability, detecting in vitro osteogenic differentiation ability, and detecting in vivo osteogenic differentiation ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) enriching the stem cell, comprising (i) collecting normal adult removed wisdom teeth, immediately placing in 4 degrees C pre-cooled alpha -minimum essential media culture medium comprising 50-300 U/ml penicillin, 50-30 mu g/ml streptomycin, and 1-5 mu g/ml amphotericin B, transporting to a clean laboratory at 2-8 degrees C, where the time interval for the collection to the start of extraction of the periodontal ligament stem cells is not more than 72 hours and the adult age is 19-29 years old, (ii) transferring to a biological safety cabinet, taking out the wisdom tooth from the culture dish, and washing with 4 degrees C pre-cooled sterile phosphate buffer for cleaning the outer surface, (iii) gently peeling off residual periodontal ligament tissue from the surface of the tooth root, (iv) placing the periodontal ligament tissue in phosphate buffer solution comprising 1-10 mg/ml type I collagenase and 1-10 mg/ml dispase, and digesting at 37 degrees C for 1 hour, (v) filtering the digestive juice through a 70 micron cell sieve to obtain a single cell suspension containing human periodontal ligament stem cells, centrifuging at 900 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes, and washing the cells once with phosphate buffer solution, (vi) re-suspending the cells in phosphate buffer solution, adding CD18 monoclonal antibody, mixing, incubating at room temperature for 30 minutes in the dark, (vii) washing, centrifuging at 900 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes, re-suspending the cells in phosphate buffer solution, and (viii) sorting by flow cytometry or immunomagnetic bead sorting to obtain CD18 positive stem cell subpopulation;
   (2) detecting a dryness of a human periodontal ligament stem cell comprises either (a) taking an enriched CD18 positive stem cell subpopulation and counting, (b) centrifuging at 900 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes, (c) re-suspending the cells in alpha -minimum essential media culture medium comprising 10-20 wt.% fetal bovine serum, 50-200 mu M ascorbic acid 2-phosphate, 2 mM glutamine, 100 U/ml penicillin and 100 mu g/ml streptomycin, (d) inoculating 104 CD18-positive stem cells into T25 cell culture flask, incubating at 37 degrees C in an 5% carbon dioxide incubator, and (e) changing the solution once in every 3-4 days, pouring culture solution after 14-16 days, washing the adherent cells with phosphate buffer solution, performing fixation, staining with phosphate buffer solution comprising 0.1% toluidine blue and 2% paraformaldehyde, or (f) inoculating the cells obtained in step (c) into a 12-well plate at 104/ well, and culturing in an 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C, (g) culturing for 1-2 days, adding bromodeoxyuridine reagent, and incubating for 24 hours, and (h) performing bromodeoxyuridine dyeing for cell staining, haematoxylin compound staining, counting number of positive stem cells was counted under the microscope, and measuring the proliferation rate of the stem cells, or (i) inoculating the cells obtained in step (c) into a 6-well plate at 4x 105/ well, and culturing in an 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C, (j) replacing with osteogenic induction medium when the stem cells are expanded to 80% confluency, where the medium is an alpha -minimum essential media culture medium comprising 5-30 nM dexamethasone, 50-300 mu g/mL vitamin C and 1-10 mM beta -glycerophosphate, (k) changing the solution once in every 3-4 days, observing the stem cells under the microscope after 3-4 weeks of induction forming dark yellow calcium nodules, (l) discarding the culture solution, washing with phosphate buffer solution for 2 times, and discarding the phosphate buffer solution, and (m) replacing with 60% isopropyl alcohol for 1 minute, discarding isopropanol, washing twice with double distilled water, staining with 1% alizarin red at room temperature for 3 minutes, washing with phosphate buffer solution for 2 times, and taking a picture after draining the liquid, or (n) mixing 2x 106 CD18-positive stem cells with 15 mg hydroxyapatite/tricalcium phosphate particles, and transplanting into the back portion of the 6-8 week old immunodeficient animals, (o) euthanizing the immunodeficient animal after 8 weeks of transplantation, carefully separating the subcutaneous cell mass and skin, and fixing with 4% paraformaldehyde for overnight at 4 degrees C, (p) discarding the fixative solution, washing with phosphate buffer solution, replacing with 10% EDTA for decalcification, changing the decalcification solution once in every 3 days, decalcifying for 21 days, dehydrating and embedding, (q) sectioning, and performing hematoxylin/eosin staining and Masson's staining, and (r) performing microscopic examination;
   (3) use of human periodontal ligament stem cell for preparing regenerative medicine and tissue engineering materials for bone diseases; and
   (4) use of human periodontal ligament stem cell for transferring CD18 gene into stromal stem cells by transgenic technology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The labeling of CD18 is carried out by a fluorescent label or a magnetic bead. The transgenic technology is mediated by retroviruses. Preferred Components: The immunodeficient animal is a nude rat. The stromal stem cells source is teeth, fat, bone marrow, umbilical cord, placenta or umbilical cord blood.

TI Constructing vascular endothelial growth factor and mothers against decapentaplegic homolog-7 double gene overexpression lentiviral vector comprises synthesizing two primer sequences and carrying out PCR amplification.
AU MA L
   LI Z
   CAI B
   MA Q
   XING Q
   GUAN Y
AE MA L (MALL-Individual)
GA 201808575L
AB    NOVELTY - Constructing vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) and mothers against decapentaplegic homolog-7 (Smad7) double gene overexpression lentiviral vector comprises: synthesizing two primer sequences according to GV492 lentiviral vector map design restriction sites of BamHI/AgeI, where the primers includes a fusion gene (22541-1) -P1 of fully defined 52 nucleotides (SEQ ID NO: not defined) and a fusion gene (22541-1) -P2 of fully defined 43 nucleotides (SEQ ID NO: not defined), as given in the specification; carrying out PCR amplification.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing VEGF and Smad7 double gene overexpression lentiviral vector.
   ADVANTAGE - The vector has high titer and transfection efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Constructing VEGF and Smad7 double gene overexpression lentiviral vector comprises: (i) synthesizing two primer sequences according to GV492 lentiviral vector map design restriction sites of BamHI/AgeI, where the primers includes a fusion gene (22541-1) -P1 of fully defined 52 nucleotides (SEQ ID NO: not defined) and a fusion gene (22541-1) -P2 of fully defined 43 nucleotides (SEQ ID NO: not defined), as given in the specification; (ii) carrying out PCR amplification using fusion gene (22541-1)-P1 and fusion gene (22541-1) -P2 as primers to obtain a gene product, recovering target fragment using agarose gel DNA recovery kit, digesting the GV492 empty vector at BamHI/AgeI, purifying, inserting the recovered target fragment into digested GV492 vector under the action of recombinant reaction using Exnase-II, transferring the recombinant product into Escherichia coli DH5 alpha competent cells, picking a single colony for PCR identification using aseptic tips, identifying the positive clones and inoculating into antibiotics lysogeny broth liquid medium, culturing at 37 degrees C for 12-16 hours, sequencing the strain, analyzing the sequencing results with gene sequence, transfecting 293T cells with correct recombinant plasmids; (iii) culturing 293T cells in 10% fetal calf serum-containing Dulbecco's modified eagle's medium for 24 hours, digesting the logarithmic growth phase 293T cells with trypsin, adding 10% serum-containing cell culture medium, adjusting cell density to 5x 106 cells/15 ml, re-seeding 10 cm cells into culture dish, culturing at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours to obtain cell confluency of 70-80%, replacing with serum-free medium after 2 hours, adding DNA solution into a sterile centrifuge tube, adding 20 mu g GV vector plasmid, 15 mu g pHelper 1.0 vector plasmid, 10 mu g pHelper 2.0 vector plasmid and proper volume of transfection reagent, mixing, adjusting total volume of 1 ml, incubating at room temperature for 15 minutes, adding mixed droplets of 293T cell culture, mixing, incubating at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide cell incubator for 6 hours, discarding the medium containing transfection mixture, washing once with 10 ml phosphate buffer saline, gently shaking the Petri dish to wash remaining transfection mixture and discarding, adding 10% serum-containing 20% cell culture medium, culturing at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 48-72 hours, collecting the transfected virus, collecting the supernatant in 40 ml centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 4000 G for 10 minutes at 4 degrees C to remove cell debris, filtering the supernatant via 0.45 mu m filter into 40 ml ultracentrifuge tube, centrifuging at 4 degrees C at 25000 rotations/minute for 2 hours, discarding the supernatant, removing the residual liquid in the tube wall, adding virus preservation solution or phosphate buffer saline or cell culture medium, repeatedly blowing to re-suspend, centrifuging at 10000 rotations/minute for 5 minutes to obtain the supernatant; carrying out fluorescence titration by (a) inoculating 293T cells (2x 105/500 mu l) into 96-well plates 24 hours before infecting with virus, (b) preparing 7-10 sterile eppendorf tubes according to the expected virus titer, where each tube is added with 90 mu l serum-free medium, (c) adding 10 mu l virus stock solution into the first tube, mixing, taking 10 mu l and adding into a second tube, continuing the same operation until the last tube, (d) selecting the desired cell wells, discard 90 mu l medium, adding 90 mu l diluted virus solution, placing in the incubator for 24 hours, adding 100 mu l complete medium, and (e) observing the fluorescence expression after 4 days and calculating the lentivirus titer; screening the medicine by adding 5 mu g/ml drug resistance puromycin into fluorescence titer after 72 hours of infection, culturing for 1 day and observing the cell growth status; and (iv) performing Western blot by collecting 48 hours infected cells, washing twice with pre-chilled phosphate buffer saline, adding phenylmethylsulfonyl fluoride-containing cell lysate, carrying out ice reaction at 4 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, centrifuging at 12000 G for 15 minutes, collecting the total protein supernatant, extracting the supernatant total protein using sodium dodecyl sulfate-polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis, transferring the protein on a polyvinylidene difluoride membrane at 300 mA, blocking the membrane using 5% skimmed milk containing tris-buffered saline-0.1% Tween 20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20) (TBST) blocking solution, placing at room temperature for 1 hour, diluting with FLAG (RTM: Peptide tag) antibody in a ratio of 1:3000, placing overnight at 4 degrees C, washing 4 times with TBST, diluting with MOUSE antibody in a ratio of 1:4000, incubating at room temperature for 1.5 hours, washing 4 times with TBST, detecting the target protein by radiography.

PN CN112089742-A
TI Use of Blumea balsamifera ethanol extract for inhibiting influenza virus, particularly influenza A and B viruses.
AU CHAI L
   JIA J
   LI X
   GAO T
   ZHU Y
AE GUIZHOU HOLY PHARM CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2020C8921D
AB    NOVELTY - An ethanol extract of Blumea balsamifera is used for inhibiting influenza virus.
   USE - The ethanol extract of Blumea balsamifera is used for inhibiting influenza virus (claimed), particularly influenza A and B viruses.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of B. balsamifera ethanol extract by subjecting B. balsamifera to supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction, cold soaking, ultrasonic extraction, cold soaking and ultrasonic extraction, percolation, or reflux extraction using 50-95% ethanol as solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Supercritical CO2 extraction includes extracting B. balsamifera with 3-10 times 60-95% ethanol at 30-65 degrees C and 20-38 MPa for 30-240 minutes, preferably with 5-6 times 95% ethanol at 45 degrees C and 22-26 MPa for 120 minutes, and evaporating ethanol, in which temperature of first separation tank is 35-65 degrees C, preferably 45 degrees C and pressure is 5-10 MPa, preferably 6 MPa, and temperature of second separationg tank is 35-65 degrees C, preferably 45 degrees C and pressure is 3-8 MPa, preferably 5 MPa. Cold soaking includes adding 60-95%, preferably 95% ethanol to B. balsamifera at material-liquid ratio of 1:5-40, preferably 1:20; cold soaking for 2-72 hours, preferably 48 hours; filtering; collecting filtrate; and evaporating ethanol. Ultrasonic extraction comprises adding 60-95%, preferably 95% ethanol to B. balsamifera at material-liquid ratio of 1:5-40, preferably 1:20; ultrasonically extracting 1-3 times, preferably 2 times, each for 10-120 minutes, preferably 60 minutes at room temperature; collecting filtrate; and evaporating ethanol. Cold soaking and ultrasonic extraction comprises adding 60-95%, preferably 95% ethanol to B. balsamifera at material-liquid ratio of 1:5-40, preferably 1:20; cold soaking for 2-72 hours, preferably 48 hours; ultrasonically extracting at room temperature for 0.5-4 hours, preferably 2 hours; filtering; collecting filtrate; and evaporating ethanol. Percolation method includes adding 1-3 times 60-95% ethanol, preferably 1 times 95% ethanol to B. balsamifera; uniformly mixing; sealing for 1-5 hours, preferably 2 hours; wetting and expanding; filling cylinder; adding 10-15 times 60-95% ethanol, preferably 15 times 95% ethanol and soaking for 24-48 hours, preferably 24 hours; percolating at 1-5 mL/minute, preferably at 2-3 mL/minute; collecting percolation liquid; and evaporating ethanol. Reflux extraction comprises adding 50-70%, preferably 60% ethanol to B. balsamifera; heating to reflux; continuously boiling; extracting 1-3 times, preferably once or twice, each for 0.5-4 hours, preferably 2 hours; filtering; collecting filtrate; and evaporating ethanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The ethanol extract contains 18.56-38.48 wt.%, preferably 24-29 wt.% total flavonoids.
   ACTIVITY - Virucide.

TI Method for operating blast furnace involves blowing solid reducing material adsorbed with preset amount of oxygen from blast furnace tuyere via lance.
AU MURAO A
   YAMAMOTO N
   MORIYA K
   FUKADA K
AE JFE STEEL CORP (JFES-C)
GA 2018133564
AB    NOVELTY - A method for operating a blast furnace involves blowing a solid reducing material adsorbed with 0.055 kg/kg coal or more of oxygen from a blast furnace tuyere via a lance.
   USE - Method for operating blast furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures suppressed carbon dioxide emission, and improved coal combustion, coal surface area, and its contact property with oxygen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of oxygen on the dry basis is less than 10 mass%. The adsorption amount of oxygen is 2.0 m2/g or more. The solid reducing material is powdered coal, and a substance chosen from a waste plastic, a refuse derived fuel, and/or an organic resources is mixed with the powdered coal.

PT P
PN CN112500872-A
TI Magnetizing garbage by pyrolyzing waste raw materials, volatilizing thermal decomposition of organic hydrocarbons, decomposing heavy hydrocarbon materials, performing endothermic catalytic cracking reaction, condensing, and purifying.
AU ZHOU D
AE ZHOU D (ZHOU-Individual)
GA 202128593W
AB    NOVELTY - Magnetization of garbage, involves (a) feeding waste raw materials into pyrolysis furnace, (b) passing the garbage raw materials in the pyrolysis furnace to transport the magnetized air to the combustion zone, volatilizing the thermal decomposition reaction of organic hydrocarbons, and pyrolyzing the raw materials into a product of gas and ash, (c) entering ashes and gas products to ash buffer chamber, flowing the ash into the ash discharge machine, discharging it, and flowing it upwards through the perforated catalyst sieve into the jacketed catalytic fixed bed, (d) decomposing heavy hydrocarbon materials, undergoing the heavy hydrocarbons in the gas product to an endothermic catalytic cracking reaction, and undergoing the nitrogen oxides, carbon monoxide and small molecular hydrocarbon gases in the gas products to an exothermic catalytic reaction, and (e) discharging the gas after the catalytic fixed bed treatment, condensing, purifying the exhaust gas, and discharging.
   USE - The method is used for low-temperature magnetization treatment of garbage, where the garbage includes household garbage, kitchen garbage, agricultural and forestry organic garbage, industrial organic waste, and medical garbage (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of low-temperature magnetization treatment of garbage, involves (a) feeding waste raw materials into pyrolysis furnace through the top feeding port of the closed vertical pyrolysis furnace, (b) passing the garbage raw materials in the pyrolysis furnace through the drying zone, the cracking zone, and the combustion zone in order to transport the magnetized air to the combustion zone, controlling the temperature in the cracking zone at 250-350 degrees C, volatilizing the thermal decomposition reaction of organic hydrocarbons in the cracking zone, controlling the temperature in the combustion zone at 450-600 degrees C, mainly occurring the burning reaction of hot carbon residue in the combustion zone, and pyrolyzing the raw materials into a product of gas and ash, (c) passing ashes and gas products through the furnace grate, entering the ash buffer chamber, flowing the ash into the ash discharge machine at the lower portion of the ash buffer chamber, discharging it from the furnace body, after flowing the gas product downwards to bypass the ash baffle to remove dust, and flowing it upwards through the perforated catalyst sieve into the jacketed catalytic fixed bed, (d) configuring the jacketed catalytic fixed bed with a particulate hydrocarbon cracking catalyst for thermally decomposing heavy hydrocarbon materials, equipping with a particulate oxidation-reduction catalyst that converts carbon monoxide, nitrogen oxides and hydrocarbon gases into carbon dioxide, nitrogen and water, undergoing the heavy hydrocarbons in the gas product to an endothermic catalytic cracking reaction under the action of the cracking catalyst in the catalytic fixed bed, and obtaining small molecular hydrocarbon gases, undergoing the nitrogen oxides, carbon monoxide and small molecular hydrocarbon gases in the gas products to an exothermic catalytic reaction under the action of the oxidation-reduction catalyst and to become harmless carbon dioxide, nitrogen and water, supplying the heat released by the oxidation-reduction reaction to the heat required for the pyrolysis of heavy hydrocarbons, transferring the high temperature generated in the combustion zone to the catalyst in the catalytic fixed bed at the periphery of the combustion zone through thermal radiation, which heats the catalyst in the fixed bed to provide the heat required for the catalytic reaction, and (e) continuously discharging the gas after the catalytic fixed bed treatment from the pyrolysis furnace, transporting it to the gas collection and treatment equipment for condensation, collecting and purifying the exhaust gas to become qualified gas, and discharging the qualified gas into the atmosphere through the induced draft fan. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for low-temperature magnetization treatment of garbage that implements the method, comprising furnace body of the pyrolysis furnace, where the top of the furnace body is equipped with a feeding port and a feeding mechanism, and the furnace body has a drying zone, a cracking zone and a combustion zone, the combustion zone connected with the magnetized air delivery pipe discharged from the air magnetizer, and the bottom of the combustion zone equipped with a furnace grate for discharging ash and residue, an ash baffle arranged under the grate, and a jacketed catalytic fixed bed arranged on the periphery of the combustion zone of the furnace body, where the jacketed catalytic fixed bed is equipped with granular or honeycomb catalysts, and the upper portion of the jacketed catalytic fixed bed is equipped with a catalyst feeding port plate and a gas product outlet, where the bottom of the jacketed catalytic fixed bed is equipped with a gas product inlet, and the discharge port plate of the granular catalyst is arranged on the lower portion of the side wall of the jacketed catalytic fixed bed, a catalyst mesh plate with holes, arranged between the gas product inlet at the bottom of the jacketed catalytic fixed bed and the ash buffer chamber to prevent the catalyst from falling into the ash buffer chamber, to can communicate with the gas, where below the grate is the ash buffer room, and the lower portion of the ash buffer room is equipped with an ash discharge machine, and there is a certain distance between the grate and the ash discharging machine for ash storage and buffer space.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Sample: The garbage includes household garbage, kitchen garbage, agricultural and forestry organic garbage, industrial organic waste, and medical garbage. Preferred Device: The air magnetizer is a magnetic cylinder composed of ring-shaped permanent magnets arranged in series, where the inside of the magnetic cylinder forms an air channel, and the diameter of the magnetic cylinder is 5-30 cm, and where the length of the magnetic cylinder is 10-100 cm, and the periphery of the magnetic cylinder is wrapped and protected by a non-magnetic cylindrical pipe. The two ends of the magnetic cylinder are provided with circular tubular connectors. The ring-shaped permanent magnets are ferrite magnets, neodymium iron boron magnets, alnico magnets, samarium cobalt magnets or iron chromium cobalt magnets, preferably neodymium iron boron magnets with a magnetic energy product of 300-390 MGOe.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalyst is formed by uniformly mixing a particulate hydrocarbon cracking catalyst and a particulate oxidation-reduction catalyst. Preferred Components: The oxidation-reduction catalyst is a catalyst formed by carrying metal platinum, rhodium, and palladium, and spraying one of them on the carrier aluminum oxide and/or silica, or a three-way catalyst for purifying automobile exhaust. The hydrocarbon cracking catalyst is ZSM-5 catalyst, Y-type catalyst or mordenite molecular sieve.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))
MC E10-J02D; E11-Q02B; E11-W; E31-H01; E31-N05B1; E31-P02B; E31-P03; E34-C02; H09-F03; J04-E04A; J04-E09; N02-E02; N02-F02; N06-A; N06-F; N07-L01C; N07-L02C; N07-L02D; Q73-A01; Q73-A05; Q73-U01; Q73-U20
