PT P
PN CN109082882-A
TI Ultraviolet protection garment comprises garment body and clothes cap, where clothes body comprises sleeve and zipper, where clothes cap comprises hat and brim, where sleeve is located on two sides of upper garment.
AU LUO D
AE FOSHAN ACCJ UNDERWEAR CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201901564B
AB    NOVELTY - Ultraviolet protection garment comprises a garment body (4) and a clothes cap, where clothes body comprises a sleeve (5) and a zipper (1). The clothes cap comprises a hat (2) and a brim (3), where the sleeve is located on two sides of the upper garment, and the zipper is located at an intermediate position of the front side of the garment body. The clothes cap is coupled to the garment body collar, and the brim is located at an upper end of the opening of the cap. The cap is in the shape of a crescent and comprises a thin plastic plate. The material of the garment and the cap is a modified fabric. The modified fabric is a fabric as a base layer, and the surface of the base layer is provided with anti-ultraviolet. The treatment layer has a hydrophilic and hydrophobic layer on the surface of the anti-ultraviolet treatment layer.
   USE - Ultraviolet protection garment.
   ADVANTAGE - The ultraviolet protection garment has imparts good ultraviolet resistance and temperature-responsive hydrophobic-hydrophobic conversion performance to the fabric, and has anti-ultraviolet, comfortable wearing, anti-fouling and easy washing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing ultraviolet protection garment, which involves:
   (A) adding nano titanium dioxide 10 g of tetrabutylorthotitanate (TBOT) dropwise to 35 ml absolute ethanol and sonicated for 1 minute to obtain a TBOT aqueous solution;
   (B) mixing amount of 10 g deionized water uniformly with 35 ml absolute ethanol and 40 ml glacial acetic acid to obtain an aqueous solution of acetic acid in ethanol;
   (C) adding aqueous solution of TBOT dropwise to the aqueous solution of acetic acid in ethanol, and the mixture is kept uniformly stirred at high speed during the dropwise addition;
   (D) stirring the mixture for 40 minutes, after the completion of the dropwise addition, transfer to a petri dish to form a gel;
   (E) drying the formed gel at 90 degrees C for 24 hours, the temperature is lower to room temperature, and then ground, and heat treating at 600 degrees C for 2 hours to obtain nano-titanium dioxide;
   (F) dispersing nano-titanium dioxide and the nano-titanium dioxide is uniformly to a calcium chloride solution with a mass fraction of 11% at a ratio of 1:6, and stirring at a speed of 800 revolution per minute (rpm) for 30 minutes to obtain a calcium chloride dispersion of nano-titanium dioxide, and increasing the speed up to 1000 rpm to introduce a mixture of ammonia and carbon dioxide at a ratio of 1:1;
   (G) taking ratio of the amount of carbon dioxide to the amount of calcium chloride in the dispersion is 1.5:1, after the reaction is complet, it is filter and dry at constant weight to get a solid precipitate;
   (H) adding twice the mass of anhydrous ethanol to the precipitate, and the mixture is ground for 2 hours, then filtering and drying to constant weight to obtain nano-titanium dioxide-coated calcium carbonate nanoparticles;
   (I) immersing the fabric in a 1g/LPDDA solution prepared with 0.5mol/L sodium chloride solution, washing with deionized water after 10 miutes, and drying by rolling and rolling ratio is 70%, and then add deionized water to get composite;
   (J) adding 1 wt.% polyethylene glycol and 0.5 wt.% nano-titanium dioxide coated calcium carbonate nanoparticles are ultrasonically dispersing for 2 minutes to prepare a finishing liquid;
   (K) immersing the fabric in the finishing liquid, and after 10 minutes, it is rolling and the rolling ratio is 70%, after drying at 160 degrees C for 5 minutes;
   (L) synthesizing of hydrogel dissolves N-isopropylacrylamide monomer, N-methylol acrylamide monomer and crosslinker N, m-methylenebisacrylamide which is 2% by mass of monomer in distilled water to form a 1 mol/L monomer solution, the ratio of monomer N-isopropyl acrylamide to N-methylol acrylamide is 10:1;
   (M) adding acetic acid having a solution mass of 2.5% of 1 g/L chitosan and solution is stirring for 30 minutes, and then add 2% by mass of potassium persulfate and sodium bisulfite are add as redox initiator;
   (N) adding ratio of oxidizing agent to reducing agent is 1:1, and mixing polymerization solution and stirring for 30 minutes, and then sealing and polymerizing at room temperature for 24 hours;
   (O) immersing synthesized product in distilled water for 48 hours at room temperature to prepare a synthetic hydrogel;
   (P) adding hydrophobic treatment hydrogel with 5% glutaraldehyde as cross-linking agent to prepare a water-repellent finishing liquid, and the fabric loaded with calcium carbonate nanoparticles is immersing for 15 minutes in the finishing liquid; and
   (Q) rolling mill is used to roll, and residual rate is 70%, pre-baking and drying at 60 degrees C for 5 minutes, repeating impregnation and pre-baking, and finally bake and dr aty 150 degrees C for 3 minutes, wash with distilled water, and then subjecting under vacuum drying at 60 degrees C to obtain a desired product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of ultraviolet protection garment.
   Zipper (1)
   Hat (2)
   Brim (3)
   Garment body (4)
   Sleeve (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The substrate layer is cotton, polyester, spandex and blended fabric. The anti-ultraviolet treatment layer is a layer of calcium carbonate nanoparticles coated with nano titanium dioxide. The hydrophilic-hydrophobic variable layer is a copolymer-supported hydrogel material obtained by polymerization of N-isopropylacrylamide, N-methylolacrylamide and chitosan.
DC A83 (Clothing, footwear.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); ; P21 (Wearing apparel (A41, A42).)
MC A02-A03; A04-B; A04-D04A1; A05-H03; A08-C; A08-D; A08-S02; A08-S08; A10-B04; A10-E09; A11-B05A; A12-C02; A12-G03; A12-W14; F03-C02A; F03-C02B; F03-C05; F03-C07; F03-E01; F04-C03; P21-F

TI Device for purifying metal oxide by ammonia complexing method comprises leaching kettle, ammonia steaming reaction kettle, pyrolysis reactor, condensing tank, water injection pump, and four centrifugal pumps.
AU ZHANG X
   LIU Q
   ZHAO Y
   YANG P
   LIANG S
   LIANG T
   ZHANG Y
AE ZHANG X (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201870983H
AB    (CN207811282-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a device for purifying metal oxide by ammonia complexation method, comprising the steps of leaching kettle, the ammonia evaporation kettle, pyrolyzing reaction kettle, condensing, liquid, a water jet pump, the first centrifugal pump and the second centrifugal pump, the centrifugal pump, a fourth centrifugal pump, the leaching reaction kettle, a third centrifugal pump, the ammonia evaporation kettle, pyrolyzing reaction kettle, condensing tank are connected by pipe, the ammonia evaporation kettle, a second centrifugal pump, reservoir, leaching reaction kettle is orderly connected with it through pipe; the condensing tank to form a gas path, and condensate through the pipe and a water jet pump, the fourth centrifugal pump, leaching reaction kettle is orderly connected to form a liquid passage through the conduit. the device is structured simply, using circulating production technique of the device will not generate ammonia leakage, little wastewater discharge, it is suitable for multi-oxidized mineral leaching, purifying, economic and environment-friendly, low production cost, less energy consumption and less pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for recycling production process of purifying metal oxide by using ammonia complex method comprising (a) adding mineral material to be leached in the leaching reactor, then adding ammonium bicarbonate, and aqueous ammonia, stirring, carrying out low temperature leaching, turning on the third centrifugal pump after completing the reaction, pumping the leaching liquid into ammonia reaction kettle, and discharging the residue in the leaching reactor, (b) adding mineral material to be leached, ammonium bicarbonate, and pure water to the leaching reactor, stirring, carrying out leaching at low temperature, opening the first centrifugal pump, heating the ammonia reaction kettle, steaming the ammonia reaction kettle, decomposing the leachate for releasing ammonia gas, then forming a basic carbonate precipitate, then passing the ammonia gas to the leaching reactor through the water jet pump for carrying out reaction, turning on the second centrifugal pump after completing the pumping of ammonia, pumping the solution in the ammonia reaction kettle into the liquid storage tank, opening the valve below the ammonia reaction kettle, precipitating the alkali carbonate into the pyrolysis reactor for opening the third centrifugal pump for pumping the leaching solution in the leaching reactor into the ammonia reaction kettle, discharging the residue in the leaching reactor, and allowing the solution in the storage tank for entering the leaching reactor, (c) adding the leaching reactor with mineral material to be leached, stirring, carrying out leaching at low temperature, turning on the first centrifugal pump, heating the steamed ammonia reaction kettle and the pyrolysis reactor, heating ammonia reaction kettle, passing the ammonia gas released by the decomposition to the leaching reactor through the water jet pump, pyrolyzing the basic carbonate in pyrolysis reactor to obtain oxides, carbon dioxide and water vapor, condensing the water vapor, passing the carbon dioxide to the leaching reactor through the water jet pump for carrying out the reaction, then opening the valve below pyrolysis reactor to obtain purified oxide, turning on the second centrifugal pump for pumping the solution in the ammoniated reaction reactor into the liquid storage tank, then opening the valve between ammonia reaction kettle and pyrolysis reaction reactor, passing the basic carbonate to the pyrolysis reactor, closing the valve, opening the third centrifugal pump, pumping the leachate in the leaching reactor into the ammonia reaction kettle, starting the fourth centrifugation, collecting the distilled water in the condensing tank into the leaching reactor for washing, pumping the washing liquid into the steamed ammonia reaction kettle, discharging the residue in the leaching reactor, then introducing the solution in the liquid storage tank into the leaching reactor, and (d) repeating the step (c), then passing to the next reaction cycle and circulating.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device.
   Leaching reactor (1)
   Ammonia reaction kettle (2)

The condensing tank communicates with water jet pump through a conduit to form a gas passage, and condensing tank, fourth centrifugal pump and leaching reactor connected in series through a conduit to form a liquid passage.
   USE - The device is useful for purifying metal oxide by using an ammonia complex method.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: has simple structure, low production cost, and low energy consumption; does not cause ammonia leakage by circulating production process; discharges less waste water; and is suitable for leaching and purifying different oxidation type minerals, economical and environmentally friendly.

PN CN113750747-A; CN216024025-U
TI Performing alkaline azodicarbonamide foaming agent condensation by-product ammonia separation and concentration process involves cooling mixed vapour phase discharged from biuret reactor, and separating mixed gas of hydrazine hydrate.
AU YANG P
   LUO Z
   YANG X
   ZHANG Q
   WEI Z
   YAN Y
AE CHONGQING TENGZE CHEM CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 2022058211
AB    NOVELTY - Performing alkaline azodicarbonamide (ADC) foaming agent condensation by-product ammonia separation and concentration process involves using separation and concentration device, where separation and concentration device comprises condenser (1), heat exchanger (2), storage tank (3), ammonia absorption tower (4) and ammonia regeneration tower (5). The lower portion of shell side of heat exchanger is connected with top steam outlet of biuret reactor through condenser, and top portion of shell side of heat exchanger is used for connecting with sodium hydroxide solution connected to source, and bottom of shell side of heat exchanger feeds storage tank through first pipeline (11).
   USE - Method for performing alkaline ADC foaming agent condensation by-product ammonia separation and concentration process used in foaming plastic and rubber in chemical field.
   ADVANTAGE - The alkaline ADC foaming agent condensation by-product ammonia separation, concentrating process has simple structure, separating and recycling efficiency is high, and effectively avoid the device caused by crystal blocking phenomenon using the separation. The concentrating process is recycled to obtain pure ammonia, which is used as production raw material directly. The temperature control greatly reduces or even avoids the accumulation of ammonium carbonate crystal generated by ammonia and carbon dioxide in the pipeline and the condenser. The separation and concentration device for a long time normal operation is ensured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing alkaline azodicarbonamide (ADC) foaming agent condensation by-product ammonia separation and concentration process involves using separation and concentration device, where separation and concentration device comprises condenser (1), heat exchanger (2), storage tank (3), ammonia absorption tower (4) and ammonia regeneration tower (5). The lower portion of shell side of heat exchanger is connected with top steam outlet of biuret reactor through condenser, and top portion of shell side of heat exchanger is used for connecting with sodium hydroxide solution connected to source, and bottom of shell side of heat exchanger feeds storage tank through first pipeline (11). The bottom of storage tank is connected to top of shell side of heat exchanger through second pipeline (12), and second pipeline is provided with first pump. The air inlet in middle of ammonia absorption tower is connected with upper portion of shell side of heat exchanger through third pipeline, and liquid inlet at top of ammonia absorption tower is connected with second pipeline through fourth pipeline, and is positioned at downstream of first pump. The liquid discharge port of ammonia absorption tower is used to supply ammonia regeneration tower through fifth pipeline, fifth pipeline is provided with second pump, and ammonia regeneration tower is provided with re-boiler (6). The connecting pipeline between heat exchanger and condenser, connecting pipeline between condenser and biuret reactor, first pipeline, second pipeline, third pipeline and fourth pipeline are all provided with steam tracers. The biuret reactor is used to synthesize ammonia by-product of biuret, ammonia decomposed by urea, carbon dioxide and water vapor, and mixed vapor phase formed by partially volatilized hydrazine hydrate is cooled to 60-80degrees Celsius through condenser, hydrazine hydrate is condensed into liquid state, and biuret reactor is returned. The mixed vapor phase of separated hydrazine hydrate is passed into shell side of heat exchanger from lower portion of shell side of heat exchanger, top of shell side of heat exchanger is sprayed with sodium hydroxide solution through distributor, and is in counter-current contact with mixed vapor phase of separated hydrazine hydrate. The first absorption rich liquid is obtained at bottom of shell side of heat exchanger, and scrubbing gas is obtained at upper portion of shell side of heat exchanger. The first absorption rich liquid at the bottom of shell side of heat exchanger is passed into storage tank through first pipeline, and first absorption rich liquid in storage tank is returned to top of shell side of heat exchanger through second pipeline as a washing liquid. The first absorption rich liquid portion in storage tank is passed into top of ammonia absorption tower through fourth pipeline as a washing liquid. The scrubbing gas in upper portion of shell side of heat exchanger is passed into middle of ammonia absorption tower through third pipeline, and is in counter-current contact with the scrubbing liquid. The second absorption rich liquid is obtained at the bottom of ammonia absorption tower after secondary absorption. The second absorption rich liquid at the bottom of the ammonia absorption tower is passed into ammonia regeneration tower through fifth pipeline, heated by the re-boiler, ammonia in second absorption rich liquid is discharged in the form of vapor phase, and is recovered and concentrated to obtain ammonia water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of a connection of a method for performing alkaline ADC foaming agent condensation by-product ammonia separation and concentration process.
   Condenser (1)
   Heat exchanger (2)
   Storage tank (3)
   Ammonia absorption tower (4)
   Ammonia regeneration tower (5)
   Reboiler (6)
   First pipeline (11)
   Second pipeline (12)
   Third pipeline (13)
   Fourth pipeline (14)
   Sixth pipeline (16)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of sodium hydroxide solution is 10 wt.%, and mass ratio of ammonia and carbon dioxide in mixed vapor phase is 18-19:30-40. Preferred Conditions: The cooler is arranged on the top of ammonia regeneration tower. The tower bottom liquid of ammonia regeneration tower is sent to waste water treatment unit for processing.

PT P
PN CN111349071-A
TI 9-(2-carboxyphenyl)-6(diethylamino)-4(4-benzylidene)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroxanthene for detecting peroxynitrite (I) used as molecular probe to detect e.g. peroxynitrite, contains activated carbon-carbon double bond as reactive group.
AU YOU J
   LU J
   LI Z
   TAN J
   SUN Z
   CHANG R
AE UNIV QUFU NORMAL (UNQN-C)
GA 202063028W
AB    NOVELTY - A 9-(2-carboxyphenyl)-6(diethylamino)-4(4-benzylidene)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroxanthene (I) comprises 9-(2-carboxyphenyl)-6(diethylamino)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroxanthene as fluorescent group, and activated carbon-carbon double bond as reactive group.
   USE - 9-(2-carboxyphenyl)-6(diethylamino)-4(4-benzylidene)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroxanthene used as molecular probe to detect total content of peroxynitrite, detect exogenous peroxynitrite concentration of cells or endogenous peroxynitrite concentration of cells and fluorescence imaging of cells peroxynitrite (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound (I) has response time to peroxynitrite of within a few seconds, and low detection limit about 3.2 nm/L, which is much lower than the normal content of these two substances in the cell, and can detect excessive peroxynitrite in time, and detect the concentration of endogenous and exogenous peroxynitrite in living cells in situ.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A 9-(2-carboxyphenyl)-6(diethylamino)-4(4-benzylidene)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroxanthene of formula (I) comprises 9-(2-carboxyphenyl)-6(diethylamino)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroxanthene as fluorescent group, and activated carbon-carbon double bond as reactive group. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the compound (I), which involves (1) adding concentrated sulfuric acid into container, adding cyclohexanone of formula (a) and 2-((4-diethylamino)-2-hydroxybenzoyl)benzoic acid of formula (b), reacting at 70-90 degrees C, cooling to room temperature, pouring into ice water, stirring, adding perchloric acid in drops to the mixed liquid, precipitating solid, obtaining 9-(2-carboxyphenyl)-6-(diethylamino)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroxanthene of formula (A), (2) dissolving the compound (A) obtained in step (1) in absolute ethanol, adding 4-methoxybenzaldehyde of formula (c), stirring and refluxing at 60-90 degrees C, cooling to room temperature, spinning the filtrate to dryness, and separating product by chromatography column method;
   (2) use of the compound (I) as molecular probe to detect total content of peroxynitrite, detect exogenous peroxynitrite concentration of cells or endogenous peroxynitrite concentration of cells and fluorescence imaging of cells peroxynitrite;
   (3) method of using the compound (I) to detect the concentration of endogenous peroxynitrite in living cells, which involves (1) selecting RAW cells(monocyte/macrophage-like cells), culturing the recovered cells, culturing medium containing 10% bovine embryo serum, 1% double antibody and 89% Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium, culturing at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours to obtain well-growing cells for use, (2) placing the RAW cells in the culture medium and cultivating 4 groups respectively, adjusting inoculation amount in each group of medium to 2x 107 to 9x 107 mL, culturing for 24 hours, (3) taking 2 sets of medium, adding 10 mu L of 0.5 mM compound (I) dimethyl sulfoxide solution as a control to one group, (4) adding 100 mu L of lipopolysaccharide solution with a concentration of 1 mu g/mL and 100 mu L of phorbol myristate acetate solution with a concentration of 1 mu g/mL to the other group to stimulate the cells to produce endogenous peroxynitrite , adding 10 mu L of 0.5 mM compound (I) dimethyl sulfoxide solution, heat-preserving, placing the observation and imaging under confocal, collecting the fluorescence intensity of different color light channels, performing fluorescence intensity ratio, comparing the linear equation of the ratio of the concentration of endogenous peroxynitrite to the fluorescence intensity according to the linear equation of the ratio of the concentration of peroxynitrite in the cell to the fluorescence intensity, and obtaining the concentration of endogenous peroxynitrite in the cell; and
   (4) method of using the compound (I) to detect the concentration of exogenous peroxynitrite in living cells involves (1) selecting RAW cells, culturing the recovered cells, culturing medium containing 10% bovine embryo serum, 1% double antibody, 89% Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium , culturing at 37 degrees C for 24 hours in a 5% carbon dioxide environment to obtain well-growing cells for use, (2) placing the RAW cells in the culture medium to cultivate, cultivating total of 5 groups, adjusting inoculation amount in each group of medium to 2x 107 to 9x 107 mL, culturing for 24 hours, (3) taking 0 mu L, 2.5 mu L, 5 mu L, 7.5 mu L and 10 mu L of 1 mM peroxynitrite standard solution, adding 10 mu L of 1 mM compound (I) in dimethyl sulfoxide solution, heat-preserving at 37 degrees C for a total of 2 hours, placing the cells in a buffer solution of phosphate-buffered saline, placing the observation and imaging under confocal, collecting the fluorescence intensity of different color light channels, performing the fluorescence intensity ratio, comparing the linear equation of the ratio of the concentration of exogenous peroxynitrite to the fluorescence intensity according to the linear equation of the ratio of the concentration of exogenous peroxynitrite to the fluorescence intensity, and obtaining the concentration of peroxynitrite exogenous to the cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The cell endogenous peroxynitrite concentration detection is induced by adding lipopolysaccharide and phorbol myristate acetate drugs to make the cell self-produce peroxynitrite. The cell exogenous peroxynitrite concentration is detected by externally adding peroxynitrite , which is finally absorbed by the cell. The fluorescence imaging is peroxynitrite imaging in cells. Preferred Process: The method of establishing linear working curve of the compound (I) and peroxynitrite involves (1) preparing phosphate-buffered saline buffered salt solution with pH of 7.40 and concentration of 10 mM, preparing a standard solution of peroxynitrite-water with a concentration of 1 mM and a dimethyl sulfoxide solution of compound (I) with a concentration of 1 mM, (2) taking 0 mu L, 10 mu L, 20 mu L, 30 mu L, 40 mu L, 50 mu L, 60 mu L, 70 mu L, 80 mu L, 90 mu L and 100 mu L respectively, obtaining total of 11 standard solutions of peroxynitrite with a concentration of 1 mM, adding 10 mu L of compound (I) solution with a concentration of 1 mM, adding 1 mL of 10 mM PBS buffered salt solution, adding 1 mL ethanol and mixing well, (3) testing the fluorescence intensity with a fluorescence spectrophotometer, obtaining fluorescence intensity ratio, reacting for 30 minutes in excitation wavelength of 425 nm for detecting peroxynitrite fluorescence intensity, and (4) taking the concentration of peroxynitrite as the abscissa, and taking the fluorescence intensity ratio as the ordinate. The method of establishing linear working curve between the compound (I) and peroxynitrite in the external environment of the cell involves (1) preparing phosphate-buffered saline buffered salt solution with a concentration of 10 mM and pH of 7.40, preparing a standard solution of hydrogen sulfite and hypochlorite with a concentration of 1 mM and a standard solution of dimethyl sulfoxide with a concentration of 1 mM compound (I), (2) taking 0 mu L, 2.5 mu L, 5 mu L, 7.5 mu L and 10 mu L of 1 mM peroxynitrite standard solution and heat-preserving at 37 degrees C for 2 hours, placing cells in a buffer solution of phosphate-buffered saline, placing the observation and imaging under confocal, collecting the fluorescence intensity of different color light channels, performing the fluorescence intensity ratio, obtaining linear equation of the ratio of the concentration of exogenous peroxynitrite to the fluorescence intensity, and obtaining the cell map of exogenously added peroxynitrite and the linear equation of the ratio of intracellular fluorescence intensity to peroxynitrite concentration.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the compound (b) and the compound (a) is 1:2. The molar ratio of the compound (A) to 4-methoxybenzaldehyde is 1:2. Preferred Conditions: In step (1), the concentrated sulfuric acid into container and stirred for 5 minutes in an ice water bath. In step (1), the cyclohexanone is added to the reaction mixture and stirred at 0 degrees C for 5 minutes. In step (2), the refluxing process is performed at 60-90 degrees C for 2 hours.

PN CN110885741-A
TI Device for increasing the amount of biogas in livestock manure by microalgae fermentation technology comprises solid-liquid separation device connected to biogas liquid processing device provided with power and reflection heating device.
AU NIU X
   LV M
   ZHANG L
   LI X
   WANG Y
   YU C
AE UNIV GUANGDONG PETROCHEMICAL TECHNOLOGY (UNGP-C)
GA 202023134D
AB    NOVELTY - Device for increasing the amount of biogas in livestock manure by microalgae fermentation technology comprises solid-liquid separation device (1), biogas liquid processing device (2), power device (3) and reflection heating device (4). The solid-liquid separation device includes a solid-liquid separation chamber and upper end of solid-liquid separation chamber provided with openable and closable separation chamber cover. A feed inlet provided at center of separation bin cover and air outlet provided on each side of feed inlet. The shape of biogas slurry processing device hollow semi-ellipsoid hollowed out at center and sleeved on lower end of solid-liquid separation device through a circular notch at center. The reflection heating device comprises reflection mirrors arranged circumferentially with the power device as center. The reflecting mirror can be placed so that bottom end of biogas slurry processing device can be irradiated with sunlight during all daylight hours.
   USE - Used as device increasing the amount of biogas in livestock manure by microalgae fermentation technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: cooperates with reflection heating device and biogas liquid processing device to increase the photosynthesis capacity of the microalgae; enables the solid-liquid separation device to uniformly heat up; accelerates the anaerobic fermentation rate and water evaporation rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device for increasing the amount of biogas in livestock manure by microalgae fermentation technology comprises solid-liquid separation device (1), biogas liquid processing device (2), power device (3) and reflection heating device (4). The solid-liquid separation device includes a solid-liquid separation chamber and an upper end of the solid-liquid separation chamber provided with an openable and closable separation chamber cover. A feed inlet provided at the center of the separation bin cover and an air outlet provided on each side of the feed inlet. A rotary separation cylinder is provided in an inner cavity of the solid-liquid separation bin and the rotary separation cylinder connected to the power unit through a rotating shaft. The lower end of the solid-liquid separation bin naturally closed and a biogas slurry water inlet arranged on the inner side of the solid-liquid separator. The shape of the biogas slurry processing device a hollow semi-ellipsoid hollowed out at the center and is sleeved on the lower end of the solid-liquid separation device through a circular notch at the center. An upper end of the socket is provided with a carbon dioxide inlet and a lower end is provided with a biogas slurry outlet connected with the biogas slurry inlet. The inner cavity of the biogas slurry processing device is provided with a blocking plate. The bottom end of the blocking plate is lower than the biogas slurry water outlet. The inner cavity of the biogas slurry processing device separated into upper and lower chambers and microalgae substrates arranged on the upper surface of the blocking plate the circumferential direction. The bottom of the lower cavity of the biogas slurry processing device is provided with a filtered liquid inlet. The upper end of the power device connected to the biogas slurry processing device and the lower end is installed on the ground. The outer wall is provided with a control panel and the inner cavity is provided with a motor. The motor connected to the rotating shaft. A filter liquid outlet also provided on both sides of the power device and the filter liquid outlet runs through the outer wall of the power device and connected to the filter liquid inlet. The reflection heating device comprises reflection mirrors arranged circumferentially with the power device as the center. The reflecting mirror can be placed so that the bottom end of the biogas slurry processing device can be irradiated with sunlight during all daylight hours. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for using the device comprising (1) collecting livestock and poultry manure and after pretreatment, pumping through the feed port into the rotary separation cylinder the solid-liquid separation device to separate the biogas slurry from the biogas residue, leaving biogas residue in the rotating separation cylinder and throwing the biogas liquid on the inner wall of the solid-liquid separation bin due to inertia and naturally flows down into the biogas liquid inlet, (2) receiving the biogas liquid processing device and the light source from the reflector and uniformly irradiating the solid-liquid separation device with the principle of the lens and the internal temperature of the solid-liquid separation device rises and intensifies and evaporating the water in the original livestock and poultry manure, (3) separating the biogas slurry and the by-product carbon dioxide thin-film, introducing into biogas slurry processing device to provide nutrients and carbon sources for the growth of microalgae, (4) adjusting the angle of the mirror to ensure that the temperature range in the solid-liquid separation device at 42-75 degrees C and (5) using biogas slurry for irrigation or direct discharge after purification and using microalgae as algae biofuel after harvesting.

PN CN109760258-A
TI Polyphenylene sulfone physical foaming sheet, obtained by mixing polyphenylene sulfone resin, nucleating agent, foaming agent in plastic extruder heating, melt mixing and physical foaming, die extruding and leveling with leveling machine.
AU GUO X
   XU P
AE NANJING FEININGER ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLO (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 201946883X
AB    NOVELTY - Polyphenylene sulfone physical foaming sheet, is claimed. The sheet is obtained by mixing polyphenylene sulfone resin, nucleating agent, foaming agent in plastic extruder heating, melt mixing and physical foaming, die extruding, leveling with leveling machine, conveying to the pressure roller, cooling and cutting.
   USE - Used as polyphenylene sulfone physical foaming sheet.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) production system of a polyphenylene sulfone physical foaming sheet, comprising a feeder, a twin-screw plastic extruder, a foaming agent injection device, a screen changer, and a heat exchange device , extrusion die, leveler, press roll, cooling device and cutting machine. The feeder is installed on the side of the twin-screw plastic extruder, and the feed barrel outlet of the feeder is connected to the twin-screw plastic extruder feed hopper through the blanking tube, and the twin-screw plastic extruder is quantitatively fed through the feeder. The foaming agent injection device is installed on the barrel side of the twin-screw plastic extruder, and is connected to the foaming agent inlet on the barrel of the twin-screw plastic extruder through the foaming agent conveying pipe to quantify the twin-screw plastic extruder and adding a blowing agent. The front end of the twin-screw plastic extruder is equipped with a screen changer, and the twin-screw plastic extruder extrudes the mixed melt, and the impurities in the mixed melt are filtered by the screen changer and extruded into the heat exchanger. The heat exchanger is installed at the front end of the screen changer, and the temperature of the mixed melt is lowered by the heat exchanger. The extrusion die is installed at the outlet end of the flow tube at the front end of the heat exchanger, and is extruded through an extrusion die of the extrusion die. The polyphenylene sulfone mixed melt containing the blowing agent is quickly released after exiting the extrusion die a foam forming foam material, that is, a foam material of a polyphenylene sulfone sheet. The leveling machine is installed at the front part of the extrusion die, and the melt is freely foamed after leaving the exit die of the extrusion die. The thickness of the melt is controlled by a leveler and the upper and lower surfaces of the polyphenylene sulfone physical foam sheet material are leveled. The front of the leveling machine is equipped with a pressure roller machine, which further controls the thickness of the initially shaped sheet in the height direction and functions as a traction. The cooling device is installed at the front part of the pressure roller machine, and the flattening polytetraphenyl sulfone physical foaming sheet is conveyed to the cooling device through the leveling machine for natural cooling of the air. The front part of the cooling device is equipped with a cutting machine, cooling by the cooling device, the polyphenylene sulfone physical foaming sheet is transferred to the cutting machine, and the final width and length are cut by the cutting machine, and the polyphenylene is obtained by packaging; and
   (2) preparing polyphenylene sulfone physical foaming sheet, comprising (1) adding polyphenylene sulfone resin and nucleating agent, to a twin-screw extruder through a feeder for shear mixing, and twin-screw extruding, where the metering pump of the foaming agent injection device is used to inject liquid carbon dioxide foaming agent, carbon dioxide and PPSU resin nucleating agent, and thoroughly mixed by twin-screw extruder to form mixed melt and mixed melt at 250-260 degrees C, and the mixed melt is used to filter the impurities in the mixed melt by the screen changer, and the heat exchanger is used to reduce the temperature of the mixed melt, so that the temperature of the mixed melt is uniformly reduced to about 220 degrees C, and the mixed melt containing the blowing agent is rapidly foamed after leaving the extrusion die to form a foamed material, (2) since the polyphenylene sulfone mixed melt leaves the extrusion die and is in a free foaming state, the thickness of the foamed material is controlled by a leveling machine, and the polyphenylene sulfone softening temperature is about 180 degrees C, encountering cold The air will solidify rapidly, and the released foaming agent will quickly escape from the melt to form cells, and the flattened polyphenylene sulfone physical foaming sheet will be flattened on the upper and lower surfaces, and irregular portions will still exist in the width direction edge, (3) the press roll machine further controls the thickness and the traction effect in the height direction by the initially-formed polyphenylene sulfone physical foamed sheet, and (4) after the plate is sufficiently cooled by the cooling device, the final width and length direction of the plate are cut and packaged to obtain a polyphenylene sulfone physical foamed sheet.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the production system of a polyphenylene sulfone physical foaming sheet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The addition amount of polyphenylene sulfone resin is 88-100 pts. wt., nucleating agent is 0.1-2 pts. wt. and foaming agent is 0.1-10 pts. wt. The nucleating agent is made of silica or talc. The blowing agent is nitrogen or carbon dioxide.

PN CN107648253-A
TI Use of cytokinin to induce apoptosis of human leukemia cells by nucleoside transporter, inducing apoptosis of human leukemia cells by e.g. removing human leukemia THP-1 cells vial from liquid nitrogen, aspirating the cells and centrifuging.
AU CUI C
   ZHANG H
   WANG L
   CHENG J
   LIN F
   DENG Y
   PAN H
   LIU S
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2018115897
AB    NOVELTY - Use of cytokinin to induce apoptosis of human leukemia cells by nucleoside transporter, is claimed. The method comprises e.g. removing human leukemia THP-1 cells vial from liquid nitrogen, placing into water bath and rapidly melting, aspirating the cells into a centrifuge tube, centrifuging, discarding the supernatant, suspending human leukemia cell line THP-1 in sterile DMEM medium containing inactivated fetal bovine serum, placing in carbon dioxide incubating, where cells grow to bottom area of the culture bottle passage and dissolving cytarabine in sterile DMEM medium.
   USE - The cytokinin is useful for inducing apoptosis of human leukemia cells by nucleoside transporter.
   ADVANTAGE - The cytokinin ensures based on the basic chemical structure of the cytokinin oTR, which is a nucleoside analogue, provides a molecular channels on the surface of the cell membrane to study which molecular channels oTR enter into cells and research on the anti-tumor effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use of cytokinin to induce apoptosis of human leukemia cells by nucleoside transporter, is claimed. The method comprises (i) removing the human leukemia THP-1 cells vial from liquid nitrogen, placing into 37 degrees C water bath and rapidly melting, aspirating the cells into a centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 1000 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 5 minutes, discarding the supernatant, suspending human leukemia cell line THP-1 in sterile DMEM medium containing inactivated fetal bovine serum, placing in a concentration of 5% carbon dioxide (CO2) to a relative humidity of 90%, incubating at 37 degrees C in an incubator, where cells grow to 70-80% of the bottom area of the culture bottle passage for 1 time; (ii) dissolving cytarabine (ara-C) in sterile DMEM medium, dissolving oxygen transmission rate (oTR) in 0.1% dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO), continuously dissolving the solubilized Ara-C and oTR in sterile DMEM medium to a concentration of 1-300 mu M, dissolving antagonists in DMSO to a concentration of 5 mM, filtering, sterilizing, storing at room temperature, where the antagonists includes two classes, respectively adenosine receptor antagonist and nucleoside transporter antagonist, adenosine receptor antagonists includes DPCPX, SCH58261, MRS1754 with MRS1191, nucleoside transporter antagonists including dipyridamole; (iii) providing the logarithmic growth phase of 5x 105-1x 106 cells and seeded in each well containing 3 ml DMEM culture medium 6-well plates, setting 3 groups of experiments by two holes in each group respectively includes: group 1: blank control group; group 2:0.3 mu M oTR group; group 3:1.5 mu M oTR group, placing in a concentration of 5% CO2 to a relative humidity of 90%, incubating at 37 degrees C in an incubator for 3-5 days, and observing optical cell morphology under an inverted microscope; (iv) collecting logarithmic growth phase of THP-1 cells, counting the blood count plate for cell, diluting the cells at a density of 5x 104/ml, adding dulbecco's modified eagle's medium (DMEM) medium containing 10% FBS in each well 100 mu l cell suspension, seeding in 96-well plates, setting up nine experimental groups and one control group in each group of 5 complex hole, respectively is: group 1:blank control group; group 2:0.1% DMSO; group 3:0.3 mu M oTR group; group 4:1.5 mu M oTR group; group 5:15 mu M oTR group; group 6:30 mu M, oTR group; group 7:0.3 mu M Ara-C group; Group 8:1.5 mu M Ara-C group; group 9:15 mu M Ara-C group; group 10:30 mu M Ara-C group, placing in a concentration of 5% CO2 to a relative humidity of 90%, incubating for 4 days in an incubator at 37 degrees C, staining trypan blue for viable cells/dead cells, adding the cell suspension into 0.4% trypan blue solution, trypan blue at a final concentration of 0.04% live blood cells and dead cells and counting with a hemocytometer within three minutes, and respectively recording the data; and (v) providing 96 well plates in logarithmic growth phase of 5x 103-5x 104 cells, seeding in each well containing 100 mu l DMEM culture liquid, setting 5 experiments, respectively: group 1:DPCPX group; group 2:SCH58261 group; group 3:MRS1754 group; group 4:MRS1191 group; group 5:dipyridamole group in each group set up a blank control group, antagonist alone group, 1.5 mu M oTR treatment group and antagonist combined with oTR group, each 5 complex hole, where antagonists work at a concentration of 5 mu M, working at a concentration of oTR in the combination of antagonist and oTR is 1.5 mu M, placing in a concentration of 5% CO2 to a relative humidity of 90%, incubating in incubator at 37 degrees C for 4 days by using CCK-8 kit for testing, adding 10 mu l CCK-8 solution to each well of cell suspension, placing at 37 degrees C, incubating in CO2 incubator for 2 hours, measuring the absorbance of each group of cells at 450 and recording data.

AU UNANNOUNCED I
AE JIANGSU SHUOMAO SUCAI NEW MATERIAL CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D30215
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-ultraviolet release paper comprises a base paper provided by coating, the surface of the coating is provided with silicon oil layer, and the coating comprises acrylic acid emulsion, white oil, ultraviolet absorbent, de-ionized water, the coating is: adding acrylic emulsion, white oil, ultraviolet absorbent, and de-ionized water, the ultraviolet absorbent is prepared by (a1) adding pyrocatechol, sodium bicarbonate, de-ionized water into the reaction kettle dichloromethane and aluminum chloride into reaction kettle, adding intermediate 5, resorcinol, chlorobenzene into the reaction kettle, stirring and adding aluminum chloride to react, adding tetrahydrofuran, three times freezing-vacuum-thawing-nitrogen removing oxygen, adding pentamethyl divinyl triamine, after reacting, adding the reaction liquid into ethanol to precipitate, dissolving precipitate in tetrahydrofuran, removing copper bromide, and concentrating and precipitating to obtain ultraviolet absorbent.
   USE - Used as anti-ultraviolet release paper.
   ADVANTAGE - The anti-ultraviolet release paper: can reduce and eliminate the presence of peroxide, thus inhibit macromolecule material aging, adds ultraviolet absorbent, which is capable of absorbing ultraviolet wavelength range is wider, and increases the use range of the release paper.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Anti-ultraviolet release paper comprises a base paper, where the surface of the base paper is provided with a coating, the surface of the coating is provided with a silicon oil layer, the coating comprises 100-120 pts. wt. acrylic acid emulsion, 10-15 pts. wt. white oil, 5-10 pts. wt. ultraviolet absorbent, 20-30 pts. wt. de-ionized water, the coating is: acrylic emulsion, white oil, ultraviolet absorbent, and de-ionized water, the ultraviolet absorbent is prepared by (a1) adding the pyrocatechol, sodium bicarbonate, de-ionized water into the reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide, performing reflux reaction, cooling to room temperature and filtering, adjusting the pH of the filtrate, crystallizing and washing to obtain intermediate 1, adding the resorcinol, intermediate 1, zinc chloride, phosphorus oxychloride, sulfolane into the reaction kettle, heating and reacting to obtain intermediate 2, dissolving the cyanuric chloride in acetone, adding intermediate 2 and sodium hydroxide, reacting to obtain intermediate 3; (a2) adding dichloromethane and aluminum chloride into the reaction kettle, stirring and adding intermediate 3 and benzoic acid, reacting to obtain intermediate 4, adding intermediate 4 and 1, 2, 2, 6, 6-pentamethyl piperidine alcohol into the reaction kettle, stirring and dropping concentrated sulfuric acid, reacting to obtain intermediate 5; (a3) adding intermediate 5, resorcinol, chlorobenzene into the reaction kettle, stirring and adding aluminum chloride, to react to obtain the intermediate 6, the intermediate 6, potassium carbonate, bromine propyne, acetone into the reaction kettle, performing reflux reaction to obtain intermediate 7, the polyvinyl chloride, sodium azide, tetra-n-octyl ammonium bromide, adding tetrahydrofuran into the reaction kettle, reacting to obtain intermediate 8; (iv) adding o-nitroaniline, de-ionized water, hydrogen chloride into the reaction kettle, stirring and adding sodium nitrite, reacting, adding urea continuously stirring to obtain the diazo liquid, adding resorcinol, ethanol, de-ionized water into the diazonium liquid, reacting to obtain the azo, the azo, ethanol, sodium hydroxide solution after mixing uniformly, heating and adding thiourea dioxide, to react, adjusting the pH value of the reaction solution to obtain intermediate 9, intermediate 8, intermediate 9, tetrahydrofuran into the reaction kettle, stirring to obtain intermediate 10; (v) intermediate 7, intermediate 10; adding copper bromide into the reaction kettle, vacuumizing, charging nitrogen, repeating the operation for 3 times, under the protection of nitrogen, adding tetrahydrofuran, three times freezing-vacuum-thawing-nitrogen removing oxygen, adding pentamethyl divinyl triamine, after reacting, adding the reaction liquid into the ethanol to precipitate, dissolving the precipitate in tetrahydrofuran, removing copper bromide, and then concentrating and precipitating to obtain the ultraviolet absorbent.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the anti-ultraviolet release paper comprising coating a coating on the surface of the raw paper, coating a silicon oil layer on the surface of the coating to obtain the anti-ultraviolet release paper.

AE HU J (HUJJ-Individual)
GA 2021C42589
AB    NOVELTY - High flame-retardant elastic non-woven fabric                comprises polyurethane chip G83, 6 pts. wt.                modified metal organic framework material and                magnesium hydroxide. The non-woven fabric is                prepared by (a) mixing activated carbon nanotubes                and methanol, adding polyvinylpyrrolidone,                ultrasonically dispersing, adding cobalt nitrate                hexahydrate and stirring to obtain a mixed                dispersion, (b) mixing 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic                acid and 70% methanol aqueous solution, adding nano                cobalt oxide and stirring to obtain a cobalt oxide                mixed dispersion, mixing the cobalt oxide mixed                dispersion and the mixed dispersion obtained in                step (a), adding 2-nitroimidazole, stirring and                filtering, vacuum drying filter cake to obtain a                modified metal organic framework material, (d)                heating and melting the polyurethane chip G83, and                adding the modified metal organic framework                material and magnesium hydroxide, mixing and                extruding the spinning material and spinning to                obtain the fabric.
   USE - As high flame-retardant elastic non-woven                fabric.
   ADVANTAGE - The elastic non-woven fabric has excellent                flame-retardant effect, and improved mechanical                property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High flame-retardant elastic non-woven fabric                comprises 60 pts. wt. polyurethane chip G83, 6 pts.                wt. modified metal organic framework material and 1                pts. wt. magnesium hydroxide. The method for                preparing the non-woven fabric involves (a) mixing                activated carbon nanotubes and methanol in a beaker                in a mass ratio of 1:100, and adding                polyvinylpyrrolidone in 6 times the mass of                activated carbon nanotubes to the beaker, and                ultrasonically dispersing for 40 minutes under                frequency of 45 kHz, adding cobalt nitrate                hexahydrate in 4 times the mass of activated carbon                nanotubes into the beaker, stirring and mixing to                obtain a mixed dispersion, (b) mixing                2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid and 70% methanol                aqueous solution in a mass ratio of 1:80 in a                flask, adding 1.5 times the mass of nano cobalt                oxide to the flask with 2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic                acid, and stirring and mixing for 40 minutes at a                rotation speed of 1000 rpm to obtain a cobalt oxide                mixed dispersion, mixing the cobalt oxide mixed                dispersion obtained in step (b) and the mixed                dispersion obtained in step (a) in a stirrer in a                mass ratio of 1:1.5, and stirring and mixing for 12                hours, adding 2-nitroimidazole in 0.2 times the                mass of the mixed dispersion to the stirrer,                stirring and mixing for 20 hours, and filtering to                obtain a filter cake, vacuum drying the filter cake                at 105degrees Celsius for 5 hours, placing in a                carbon dioxide atmosphere for 20 hours to obtain a                modified metal organic framework material, (d)                heating and melting the polyurethane chip G83, and                adding the modified metal organic framework                material in 0.1 times the mass of the polyurethane                chip G83 and magnesium hydroxide in 0.15 times the                mass of the polyurethane slice G83, stirring and                mixing to obtain a spinning material, and extruding                the spinning material and spinning to obtain a high                flame-retardant elastic non-woven fabric, and (e)                carrying out quality analysis on the fabric                obtained in step (d). The method for preparing                activated carbon nanotubes in step (a) involves                mixing carbon nanotubes and mixed concentrated acid                in a mass ratio of 1:40, and stirring and reacting                for 4 hours, performing filtration to obtain                pretreated carbon nanotubes, and washing the                pretreated carbon nanotubes with deionized water 8                times, and obtaining the activated carbon                nanotubes, where the mixed concentrated acid is a                mixture of 68% nitric acid and 75% sulfuric acid in                a volume ratio of 3:1. In extrusion spinning in                step (d), the diameter of the spinning hole is 0.3                mm, the hot air pressure is 0.48 MPa, and the                receiving distance is 20 cm.

into 50 ml centrifuge tubes according to not greater than 35 ml per tube in the biological safety cabinet, setting the centrifugation parameter to 9-7, centrifuging at 2000 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 8 minutes, drawing the upper plasma into a 50 ml centrifuge tube after the peripheral blood is centrifuged, obtaining four tubes, adding 1 ml plasma to each tube, inactivating in a water bath at 56 degrees C for 30 minutes, centrifuging at 3000 rpm for 10 minutes, obtaining upper plasma to clean 50 ml centrifuge tube, collecting all cells by centrifugation, resuspending in 30 ml CIK culture medium (with 1.5 ml autologous serum added, 5%), adding 2 ml CIK amplification reagents, setting the centrifugation parameters, centrifuging at 2000 rpm for 8 minutes, discarding the remaining upper clear liquid, sucking 15 ml CIK cell culture solution into a centrifuge tube using an electric suction device, suspending the precipitate, pipetting and mixing, adding to the culture flask, adding 15 ml CIK cell culture solution to the centrifuge tube and transferring to the culture flask, horizontally placing at 37 degrees C, cultivating 7.5% culture in carbon dioxide incubator, culturing cells to the third day by centrifugation, medium change, and bottle change, collecting the cells, centrifuging at 1500 rpm for 5 minutes, resuspending centrifuged cell pellet in 30 ml CIK cell culture medium containing 5% autologous plasma on day 4-6, adding fluid according to the actual growth situation, transferring the culture bag, counting on day 7 and the number of cells of more than 1x 108, adding 4 ml amplification reagent, if the number of cells is 3-8x 107, growing to the 8th day, adding amplification reagent and CIK cell culture medium containing 5% autologous plasma to make the total cell concentration of 1x 106 cell, if the volume of the culture medium is more than 200 ml or the number of cells is more than 1x 108, observing on the 8th to 11th day, adding CIK cell culture medium containing 1% autologous plasma to make the cell concentration around 1x 106 cell/ml, observing every day from 12th to the 14th day, adding fluid to make cell concentration around 2x 106 cell/ml, when the cells grow to the required number, collecting cells in 13 or 14 days, analyzing the growth curve of cell CIK, analyzing the expression of CIK surface antigen of cells, analyzing the cell CIK cycle, freezing and resuscitating immune cells CIK, where the experimental group has four sets of immune cell CIK cryopreservation experiments and a set of control groups, cryopreserving five groups of experiments for 10 days and 30 days respectively, and detecting various indicators of immune cells.

   BUCHER E

AE HOLCIM TECHNOLOGY LTD (HOLF-C)
   HOLCIM TECHNOLOGY LTD (HOLF-C)
GA 202169724A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a 1st calcined material, which is                cement clinker, and a 2nd calcined material, such                as a material having pozzolanic and/or latent                hydraulic properties, where the cement clinker is                produced in a 1st production line, involves: (a)                preheating raw meal in a preheater in counter                current flow to hot exhaust gasses coming from a                clinker furnace, (b) optionally pre-calcining the                preheated raw meal in a pre-calciner, (c) calcining                the preheated and optionally pre-calcined raw meal                in the clinker furnace in counter current flow to                combustion gasses of a clinker furnace burner, (d)                cooling cement clinker in a clinker cooler, where                the 2nd calcined material is produced from a raw                material in a 2nd production line by: (e)                optionally drying a raw material in a dryer, (f)                optionally crushing the optionally dried raw                material in a crusher, (g) calcining optionally                dried and optionally crushed raw material in a                rotary kiln, and (h) optionally cooling 2nd                calcined material in a cooler.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a 1st                calcined material, which is cement clinker, and a                2nd calcined material, such as a material having                pozzolanic and/or latent hydraulic properties                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can recycle waste products in an                ecologically friendly way and can reduce carbon                dioxide (CO2) footprint of composite cements. The                method has the following advantages: (a) increasing                the energy efficiency in the thermal treatment of                raw materials to obtain a material having                pozzolanic and/or latent hydraulic properties; (b)                replacement of scarce materials required for the                production of composite cement (i.e. limestone, fly                ash, granulated blast furnace slags); (c) the                thermal treatment of raw materials for the                production of a material having pozzolanic and/or                latent hydraulic properties can be performed with                100% alternative or renewable fuels; (d)                ecologically friendly recycling of contaminated                wastes; (e) production of a material having                pozzolanic and/or latent hydraulic properties                suitable for the utilization in composite cements                with a maximum feed of contaminated material                streams; (f) replacement of energy intense clinker                resulting in ecological and economic benefits; (g)                lower CO2 footprint compared to conventional                methods (e.g. flash calcination); and (h) reduced                investment and operating costs compared to a flash                calciner. By separating excess water and volatile                elements from the raw material, the thermal energy                consumption for the thermal treatment process can                be significantly reduced and the impact on the                clinker production is minimized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing a 1st calcined material, which is                cement clinker, and a 2nd calcined material, such                as a material having pozzolanic and/or latent                hydraulic properties, where the cement clinker is                produced in a 1st production line, involves: (a)                preheating raw meal in a preheater in counter                current flow to hot exhaust gasses coming from a                clinker furnace, (b) optionally pre-calcining the                preheated raw meal in a pre-calciner, (c) calcining                the preheated and optionally pre-calcined raw meal                in the clinker furnace in counter current flow to                combustion gasses of a clinker furnace burner to                obtain cement clinker, (d) cooling the cement                clinker in a clinker cooler, where the 2nd calcined                material is produced from a raw material in a 2nd                production line by carrying out the following                steps: (e) optionally drying a raw material in a                dryer, (f) optionally crushing the optionally dried                raw material in a crusher, (g) calcining the                optionally dried and optionally crushed raw                material in a rotary kiln to obtain the 2nd                calcined material, and (h) optionally cooling the                2nd calcined material in a cooler. The sensible                heat of a hot gas in the 1st production line is                used as a heat source in the calcining step (g) for                calcining the raw material, and the rotary kiln                exhaust gas coming from the calcining step (g) is                introduced into the 1st production line for the                secondary combustion of the rotary kiln exhaust                gas. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                comprising a 1st production line for producing a                1st calcined material, which is clinker, and a 2nd                production line for producing a 2nd calcined                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Method: The rotary kiln exhaust                  gas is introduced into the pre-calciner or the                  preheater of the 1st production line. The raw                  material is dried in the drying step (e) for                  reducing the amount of free water to less than or                  equal to 20 wt.%, preferably less than or equal                  to 5 wt.%. The sensible heat of a hot gas in the                  1st production line is used as a heat source in                  the drying step (e) for drying the raw material.                  The dryer and the cooler of the 2nd production                  line are operated in a heat exchanging                  relationship so that the sensible heat drawn from                  the 2nd calcined material during the cooling step                  (h) is used as a heat source for drying the raw                  material in the drying step (e). The dryer and                  the cooler of the 2nd production line are                  operated in a heat exchanging relationship so                  that the loss of sensible heat to the raw                  material during the drying step (e) is used as a                  cooling source for cooling the 2nd calcined                  material in the cooling step (h). A drying screw                  is used for drying the raw material in the drying                  step (e). Fuel is burnt in a burner of the rotary                  kiln for calcining the raw material in step (g),                  where the fuel preferably is a renewable fuel.                  Preferred Components: A clinker cooler off-gas                  from the clinker cooler is used as the heat                  source in the calcining step (g). The raw                  material is selected from sludges resulting from                  the washing of sand or aggregates, asphalt,                  (waste) gypsum, contaminated earth, wet fly ash,                  other waste or demolition materials, such as                  plasterboards for the production of gypsum, road                  cleaning sludges, filter cakes from the treatment                  processes of soil and waste washing, and                  combinations.

TI Chicken box with heat preservation function has processing box installed with plant basin provided with green plant, electric plate provided with lamp holder, odor removing box, liquid storage tank, heating box, blower, and radiating cover.
AU FENG J
   TU J
   SONG X
   SHI S
AE ANHUI ZHONGSHENG AGRIC DEV CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202131585S
AB    NOVELTY - The box has chicken manure collecting frame (2), box door, separating net (4), and processing box (6) whose inner bottom wall is fixedly installed with a plant basin (7) that is provided with green plant (8). The processing box is fixedly installed with electric plate (9) whose bottom is provided with a lamp holder. Odor removing box (12) is provided with filter block. Upper and lower ends of the odor removing box are respectively connected with first conduit and second conduit. One end of third conduit passes through and extends to the inside of the liquid storage tank. Right end of the chicken box is orderly fixed with a heating box (19) and a blower (20). Inner top wall of the chicken box is fixedly installed with two opposite electric push rods. Bottom of the two electric push rods is fixedly installed with a radiating cover (23).
   USE - Used as chicken box with heat preservation function.
   ADVANTAGE - The box utilizes heat blowing, physical adsorption and photosynthesis to reduce carbon dioxide and generate oxygen, thus ensuring temperature and oxygen concentration in a chicken.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The box has chicken manure collecting frame (2), box door, and separating net (4) whose bottom is fixedly installed with lower manure hopper. Bottom of the lower manure bucket is connected with top of the chicken manure collecting frame. Top of the chicken box is fixedly installed with processing box (6) whose inner bottom wall is fixedly installed with a plant basin (7) that is provided with green plant (8). Inner top wall of the processing box is fixedly installed with electric plate (9) whose bottom is provided with a lamp holder that is in threaded connection with a bulb. Odor removing box (12) is provided with filter block. Upper and lower ends of the odor removing box are respectively connected with first conduit and second conduit. One end of the first conduit is connected with bottom of the left side of the chicken box; and one end of the second conduit is connected with the left end of the processing box. Middle part of the right end of the processing box is fixedly connected with liquid storage tank, and the right top of the processing box is connected with a third conduit. One end of the third conduit passes through and extends to the inside of the liquid storage tank. Right end top of the liquid storage tank is connected with fourth conduit. Right end of the chicken box is orderly fixed with a heating box (19) and a blower (20) from top to bottom. One end of the fourth conduit is connected with the right end of the heating box. Output end of the blower is connected with the bottom of the heating box. Inside of the heating box is provided with a heating wire. Inner top wall of the chicken box is fixedly installed with two opposite electric push rods. Bottom of the two electric push rods is fixedly installed with a radiating cover (23). Right end and the bottom of the radiating cover are respectively provided with a telescopic air pipe and a radiating hole. Other end of the telescopic air pipe passes through the chicken box and is connected with the left end of the heating box. Left side and the bottom of the liquid storage tank are respectively provided with a liquid inlet pipe and liquid outlet pipe. The liquid outlet pipe is provided with a valve. The bottom of the chicken box is fixedly installed with two symmetrical supporting legs.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic view illustrating the chicken box.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: Material of the filter block is active carbon.

TI New siRNA useful for inhibiting duck RIG-I gene expression, prepared by obtaining cDNA sequence of duck RIG-I protein, designing nucleotide strip, subculturing, transferring synthesized siRNA, and diluting reverse transcribed cDNA.
AU CHEN S
   FANG T
   ZHU X
   WANG S
   LIN F
   CHENG X
   XIAO S
   DONG H
AE INST ANIMAL & VETERINARY FUJIAN ACAD AGR (IAMV-C)
GA 202130656E
AB    NOVELTY - A siRNA capable of inhibiting duck RIG-I gene expression, is new. The siRNA is prepared by obtaining cDNA sequence of duck RIG-I protein (NM-001310380.1) through GenBank (RTM: National institute of health (NIH) genetic sequence database) based on RNA interference technology, according to the basic principles of siRNA design, designing 21-nucleotide strip based on duck RIG-I gene siRNA, adding two dTdTs to synthesized siRNA, enhancing the stability, interfering the siRNA with the transcription and expression of duck RIG-I gene, using siDirect siRNA design software, adding two overhangs dTdT, subculturing, changing the medium, transferring synthesized siRNA into the growing cells with the lipofectamine 2000, changing the medium to MEM, culturing, extracting the total RNA and thoroughly digest the remaining genomic DNA with DNAase I, performing reverse transcription of purified RNA by Hifair III into cDNA, and diluting the reverse transcribed cDNA twice for qPCR reaction.
   USE - The siRNA is useful for inhibiting duck RIG-I gene expression (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A siRNA capable of inhibiting duck RIG-I gene expression, is new. The siRNA is prepared by obtaining cDNA sequence of duck RIG-I protein (NM-001310380.1) through GenBank (RTM: National institute of health (NIH) genetic sequence database) based on RNA interference technology, according to the basic principles of siRNA design, designing 21-nucleotide strip based on duck RIG-I gene siRNA, where siRNA comprises siRNA sense strand comprising a base pair sequence of: ggauagaggcaacaauguuag (SEQ ID NO: Not defined) and antisense strand comprising a base pair sequence of: aacauuguugccucuauccgc (SEQ ID NO: Not defined), adding two dTdTs to the 3' end of the synthesized siRNA, enhancing the stability of the siRNA double-strand complex and avoid degradation two overhanging bases at the 3'end, interfering the siRNA with the transcription and expression of duck RIG-I gene, using siDirect siRNA design software, based on the siRNA that satisfies the effective selection of siRNA by Ui-Tei, Reynolds, Amarzguioui and others as candidates after obtaining the cDNA sequence of the duck RIG-I gene, selecting GC content, after 35-50% siRNA, comparing the selected siRNA with the duck genome sequence included in the NCBI blast function database, excluding the homologous siRNA, designing following siRNA, in which the sense strand sequence is on the target gene designed target sequence is the same, where U replaces T, chemically synthesizing the siRNA sequence, adding two overhangs dTdT at the 3' end to enhance the stability of the siRNA, using ATCC MEM (containing 10% fetal bovine serum) complete medium for cell culture, subculturing ATCC DEF (immortalized duck embryo fibroblasts) in a 6-well plate the day before transfection, waiting for the second, when the cells grow to 40-60% density, changing the medium to ATCC MEM without antibiotics and serum, transferring the previously synthesized siRNA into the growing cells with the lipofectamine 2000, 12 hours after transfection, changing the medium to ATCC MEM containing 10% fetal bovine serum, culturing in a 37 degrees C and 6% carbon dioxide and humidity 90% for 24 hours in incubator, inoculating each well with duck short beak dwarf syndrome virus SBDSV M15300 mu l/well, extracting the total RNA and thoroughly digest the remaining genomic DNA with DNAase I, performing reverse transcription of purified RNA by Hifair III into cDNA, and diluting the reverse transcribed cDNA twice for qPCR reaction, where the reaction conditions are as follows: 20 mu l reaction system and 1 mu l cDNA template, and the qPCR reaction program: pre-denaturation at 94 degrees C for 30 seconds, denaturation at 94 degrees C for 5 seconds, annealing-extension at 60 degrees C for 30 seconds (40 cycles) and dissolution curve stage at 60-95 degrees C.
