PN CN111960445-A
TI Preparing battery level lithium carbonate by using            lithium sulfate crude ore and recycling side product            comprises e.g. crushing and screening lithium sulfate            crude ore and using primary pulp washing liquid for            primary pulp washing.
AU YAO T
   YUAN A
   LI W
AE CINF ENG CO LTD (CINF-Non-standard)
GA 2020B82588
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing battery level lithium carbonate by                using lithium sulfate crude ore and recycling side                product comprises e.g. (i) crushing and screening                the lithium sulfate crude ore, using a primary pulp                washing liquid for primary pulp washing at a                specific temperature, after finishing washing,                separating solid and liquid to obtain primary                lithium sulfate fine material S1 and first-stage                slurry washing filtrate L3, subjecting the primary                lithium sulfate concentrate S1 and the recycling                solution L4 to secondary slurry washing at a                specific temperature, and separating the secondary                slurry washing material from the solid and liquid                to obtain the secondary lithium sulfate concentrate                S2 and the secondary slurry washing filtrate L5 and                returning the secondary slurry washing filtrate L5                to the primary slurry washing process as a part of                the slurry washing liquid, the primary slurry                washing liquid is a mixed liquid composed of                lithium chloride mother liquid L1 and sodium                sulfate solution L2.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing battery                level lithium carbonate by using lithium sulfate                crude ore and recycling side product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can increase the yield of lithium                while reducing system outflow.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing battery level lithium carbonate by                using lithium sulfate crude ore and recycling side                product comprises (i) crushing and screening the                lithium sulfate crude ore, using a primary pulp                washing liquid for primary pulp washing at a                specific temperature, after finishing washing,                separating solid and liquid to obtain primary                lithium sulfate fine material S1 and first-stage                slurry washing filtrate L3, subjecting the primary                lithium sulfate concentrate S1 and the recycling                solution L4 to secondary slurry washing at a                specific temperature, and separating the secondary                slurry washing material from the solid and liquid                to obtain the secondary lithium sulfate concentrate                S2 and the secondary slurry washing filtrate L5 and                returning the secondary slurry washing filtrate L5                to the primary slurry washing process as a part of                the slurry washing liquid, the primary slurry                washing liquid is a mixed liquid composed of                lithium chloride mother liquid L1 and sodium                sulfate solution L2 or a mixed liquid composed of                lithium chloride mother liquid L1, sodium sulfate                solution L2 and secondary slurry washing filtrate                L5 and the primary slurry washing filtrate L3 is                used to prepare mixed salt of gypsum, bischofite                and borate; (ii) dissolving the secondary lithium                sulfate concentrate S2 prepared in step (i) in the                glauber's salt mother liquor L6 or sodium sulfate                solution L2 at a specific temperature, before                producing the solution L6, using the sodium sulfate                solution L2, precipitating sodium chloride during                the dissolution process, subjecting the mixed salt                to solid-liquid separation to obtain sodium                chloride mixed salt filter cake S3 and filtrate,                subjecting the filtrate to precision filtration to                obtain solution L8, ion exchanging the solution L8                to remove boron to obtain the solution L9, adding                solution L9 into the lithium precipitation reactor,                adding soda solution L10 for reacting to                precipitate lithium carbonate crystals and carrying                out solid-liquid separation of the reaction                solution to obtain lithium precipitation mother                liquor L11 and crude lithium carbonate S4 and using                lithium precipitation mother liquor L11 to prepare                Glauber's salt; (iii) subjecting the crude lithium                carbonate to slurry washing with the recycling                solution L4, washing the slurry, performing                solid-liquid separation to obtain the solution L12                and the wet lithium carbonate S6, adding wet                lithium carbonate S6 into the lithium carbonate                slurry tank, adding deionized water, carrying out                slurrying at a specific temperature, pumping the                slurry into the carbonization reactor, passing                carbon dioxide gas and recirculating solution L4 to                carry out carbonization reaction at a specific                temperature, reacting and subjecting the reaction                solution to precision filtration to obtain solution                L13.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic                representation of flow chart of preparing battery                level lithium carbonate by using lithium sulfate                crude ore and recycling side product. (Drawing                includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method of preparing                  battery level lithium carbonate by using lithium                  sulfate crude ore and recycling side product                  further comprises (iv) removing calcium and                  magnesium from solution L13 by ion exchange to                  obtain solution L14, adding the solution L14 into                  the decarburization reactor to carry out the                  decarburization reaction, carrying out                  solid-liquid separation to obtain the recycled                  solution L4 and the wet lithium carbonate S7,                  drying the wet lithium carbonate, dehydrating to                  obtain lithium carbonate S8, crushing the lithium                  carbonate S8 to a qualified particle size to                  obtain battery-grade lithium carbonate S9, where                  in the step (i), the primary slurry washing                  filtrate L3 is mainly composed of magnesium                  chloride, magnesium sulfate, lithium chloride and                  sodium Tetraborate, and the primary slurry                  washing filtrate L3 is used to prepare mixed salt                  of gypsum, bischofite and borate, adding the                  primary slurry washing filtrate L3 into the                  gypsum sedimentation reactor, and adding the                  mixed solution (calcium chloride solution and                  hydrochloric acid washing liquid) or a separate                  calcium chloride solution at the same time and at                  a specific temperature, reacting the magnesium                  sulfate in the primary slurry washing filtrate L3                  and calcium chloride solution to form calcium                  sulfate and magnesium chloride and the                  precipitating calcium sulfate dehydrate crystals                  to form gypsum slurry L20, carrying out                  solid-liquid separation of gypsum slurry to                  obtain solution L21 and gypsum S13, where the                  solution L21 mainly contains magnesium chloride,                  sending into the evaporation device to extract                  magnesium and boron which can also be partially                  opened to produce magnesium hydroxide, adding                  solution L21 into the evaporation device for                  evaporation and concentration to obtain solution                  L22, cooling the solution L22 discharged from the                  evaporator, crystallizing to obtain the                  bischofite suspension L23, carrying out                  solid-liquid separation to obtain the bischofite                  suspension L23, solution L24 and bischofite S14,                  adding solution L24 into the boron removal                  reactor, at the same time adding a small amount                  of flocculant, and reacting at a specific                  temperature to precipitate Sodium Tetraborate                  crystals to form a borate slurry L25, carrying                  out solid-liquid separation of borate slurry L25                  to obtain lithium chloride solution L1 and borate                  mixed salt S15 and returning Lithium chloride                  solution L1 to the primary slurry washing of                  coarse lithium sulfate ore as supplementary                  liquid, where in the step (iii), the method for                  preparing mirabilite from the mother liquor L11                  for lithium precipitation comprises adding the                  lithium precipitation mother liquor L11 into the                  evaporation device for evaporation and                  concentration, precipitating sodium chloride,                  sodium sulfate and lithium carbonate crystals to                  obtain a suspension L15, subjecting the                  suspension L15 discharged from the evaporation                  device to solid-liquid separation at a specific                  temperature to obtain a concentrated mother                  liquor L16 and mixed salt S10, returning the                  concentrated mother liquor L16 as a supplement to                  the mother liquor L6, adding the mixed salt S10                  into the dissolving tank, adding part of the                  mother liquor L19 at a specific temperature,                  using process water for slurrying before the                  mother liquor L19 is produced, separating the                  slurry from the solid and liquid to obtain the                  solution L17 and crude lithium carbonate S11,                  addinng the solution L17 into the cooling                  crystallization device, using a freezing liquid                  to cool, and cooling to crystallize to obtain a                  Glauber's salt suspension L18, carrying out                  solid-liquid separation of Glauber's salt                  suspension L18 to obtain Glauber's salt mother                  liquor L19 and product Glauber's salt S12, using                  Glauber's salt mother liquor L19 as a supplement                  to mother liquor L6 and distributing the product                  Glauber's salt S12 also be partly as a solution                  to supplement the sodium sulfate solution L2. In                  the step (i), the slurry washing is carried out                  in the slurry washing reactor, where the specific                  temperature of the primary slurry washing is                  60-70 degrees C, the concentration of the lithium                  chloride mother liquor L1 is 200-500 g/liter, the                  concentration of sodium sulfate in the sodium                  sulfate solution L2 is 200-450 g/liter, the                  slurry washing liquid it is a combination of                  lithium chloride mother solution L1 and sodium                  sulfate solution L2, the mixing volume ratio of                  the two is 0.9:1-1.2:1, when the slurry washing                  liquid is composed of lithium chloride mother                  liquid L1, sodium sulfate solution L2 and                  secondary slurry washing filtrate L5, the volume                  ratio of the mixture of L1 and L2 to L5 is                  2:1-4:1, where the solid-to-liquid ratio of the                  primary slurry washing process is 1:1-1.5:1 to                  obtain primary lithium sulfate concentrate S1 has                  a moisture content of 10-20% of which lithium                  sulfate content is 20-40%. In the step (i), the                  specific temperature of the secondary slurry                  washing is 60-70 degrees C, in the step (iv), the                  recycling solution L4 is produced before                  producing the recycling solution, where the                  recycling solution L4 is a 5-15 g/liter lithium                  carbonate solution and the solid-liquid ratio of                  the secondary slurry washing process is                  0.8:1-1.2:1. In the step (ii), the specific                  temperature is 60-70 degrees C, the concentration                  of sodium sulfate in sodium sulfate solution L2                  is 200-450 g/liter, mother liquor L6 comes from                  the step of preparing Glauber's salt, the                  concentration of sodium chloride in L6 is 200-350                  g/liter, the concentration of sodium sulfate is                  50-120 g/liter and the volume ratio of mother                  liquor L6 and sodium sulfate solution L2 is                  3:1-6:1, the solid-liquid ratio of the secondary                  lithium sulfate concentrate S2 and the mixed                  liquid (L6 and L2) is 1:3-1:6, carrying out                  precision filtration to separate particles                  smaller than 5 mu m from the solution, lithium                  sulfate content in solution L8 is 3-10%, carrying                  out ion exchange boron removal by using ion                  exchange resin to remove boron, the concentration                  of soda solution L10 is 200-400 g/liter and the                  volume ratio of the solution L9 to the soda                  solution L10 is 2:1-5:1. In the step (iii), the                  solid-liquid ratio of crude lithium carbonate S4                  to recycling solution L4 is 1:5-1:8, pulp washing                  temperature is 70-80 degrees C, where the pulp                  washing adopts the first-stage pulp washing or                  the multi-stage pulp washing purification method,                  wet lithium carbonate S6 has a moisture content                  of 4-20% of which lithium carbonate content is                  80-95%, the solid-liquid ratio of wet lithium                  carbonate S6 to deionized water is 1:8-1:12,                  setting the temperature to 5-15 degrees C, the                  volume ratio of the slurry to the recycling                  solution L4 is 1:2-1:3, before producing the                  solution L4, the recycling solution L4 in this                  step is 5-15 g/liter Lithium carbonate solution,                  the specific temperature of carbonization                  reaction is 15-25 degrees C and carrying out                  precision filtration to separate particles                  smaller than 5 mu m from the solution. In the                  step (iv), the ion exchange method is a resin ion                  exchange method, the resin can adsorb calcium and                  magnesium ions, and the resin can be ion-desorbed                  by sodium hydroxide solution, hydrochloric acid                  solution and deionized water to achieve resin                  regeneration, the decarburization temperature is                  90-95 degrees C, the moisture content of wet                  lithium carbonate S7 is 4.9-18%, and the content                  of lithium carbonate is 82-95%. In the step (iv),                  drying removes the water of wet lithium carbonate                  S7 to obtain lithium carbonate S8, and the drying                  equipment can be hot air drying or steam drying                  equipment, crushing to a particle size of less                  than 5 mu m, collecting lithium carbonate S8 to                  obtain battery-grade lithium carbonate S9,                  storing and packaging to obtain battery-grade                  lithium carbonate products. In the step (iii),                  the temperature is set to 60-70 degrees C, the                  concentration of calcium chloride solution is                  100-250 g/liter, reusing the hydrochloric acid                  lotion by ion exchange decalcification and                  magnesium step, containing 10-20 g/liter                  magnesium chloride, 30-70 g/liter lithium                  chloride and a little calcium chloride, when a                  mixed solution is used, the ratio of the volume                  of the hydrochloric acid lotion to the calcium                  chloride solution is 2-5%, the volume ratio of                  the primary slurry washing filtrate L3 to the                  mixed solution (calcium chloride solution and                  hydrochloric acid lotion) or single calcium                  chloride solution is 1:0.5-1:1.5, using a                  two-effect evaporator or an MVR evaporator for                  the evaporation and concentration, and the                  cooling crystallization device uses a freezing                  liquid as the refrigerant, and the                  crystallization temperature is -5 to 5 degrees C,                  set temperature for boron removal is 60-70                  degrees C, and the flocculant is Sodium                  Tetraborate crystals, where the added amount of a                  trace amount of flocculant is negligible relative                  to the solution L24 which is a bit similar to                  adding a trace amount of seed crystals to the                  evaporation crystallization to promote the                  precipitation of crystals. In the step (iii), the                  three-effect evaporation device is used for                  evaporation and concentration, and the specific                  temperature for solid-liquid separation of                  suspension L15 is set to 40-70 degrees C, the                  solid-liquid ratio of the mixed salt S10 to the                  mother liquor L19 or process water is 1:1-1:3,                  slurry temperature is 25-35 degrees C and the                  temperature for cooling and crystallization is -5                  to 5 degrees C.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN US2020308341-A1; WO2020198627-A1; IN202147042739-A; AU2020244873-A1; CA3134399-A1; KR2021148214-A; EP3946939-A1; CN114025957-A; BR112021019266-A2; JP2022526900-W; IL286659-A
TI New polymer for forming e.g. anion exchange            membrane, contains cation-functionalized            trifluoroketone monomer-derived structural unit, and            substituted aromatic monomer-derived structural unit or            crown ether monomer-derived structural unit.
AU YAN Y
   HU K
   WANG J
   WANG L
   ROJAS-CARBONELL S
   SETZLER B
   FU K
AE UNIV DELAWARE (UYDE-C)
   YAN Y (YANY-Individual)
   HU K (HUKK-Individual)
   WANG J (WANG-Individual)

   ROJAS-CARBONELL S (ROJA-Individual)
   SETZLER B (SETZ-Individual)
   UNIV DELAWARE (UYDE-C)
   UNIV DELAWARE (UYDE-C)

GA 2020970642
AB    NOVELTY - A polymer (a) comprising a                cation-functionalized trifluoroketone                monomer-derived structural unit (I), a substituted                aromatic monomer-derived structural unit (II) or a                crown ether monomer-derived structural unit (III),                and optionally trifluoromethyl ketone                monomer-derived structural unit (IV), is new.
   USE - New polymer is used for forming anion exchange                polymer for forming anion exchange membrane e.g.                hydroxide exchange membrane and reinforced                electrolyte membrane for fuel cell, electrolyzer,                electrodialyzer, solar hydrogen generator, flow                battery, desalinator, sensor, demineralization of                water, ultra-pure water production, wastewater                treatment, ion exchanger, or carbon dioxide                separator (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hydroxide exchange membrane formed using                the polymer has improved chemical stability,                hydroxide conductivity and mechanical property in                an ambient dry state, and decreased water uptake.                The polymer has improved solubility in selected                solvent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A polymer (a) comprising a                cation-functionalized trifluoroketone                monomer-derived structural unit of formula (I), a                substituted aromatic monomer-derived structural                unit of formula (II) or a crown ether                monomer-derived structural unit of formula (III),                and optionally trifluoromethyl ketone                monomer-derived structural unit of formula (IV), is                new.
   R11 = quaternary ammonium or phosphonium group                of formula (i), nitrogen-containing heterocyclyl or                their salts;
   nitrogen-containing heterocyclyl = pyrrolyl,                pyrrolinyl, pyrazolyl, pyrazolinyl, imidazolyl,                imidazolinyl, triazolyl, pyridinyl, triazinyl,                pyrazinyl, pyridazinyl, pyrimidinyl, azepinyl,                quinolinyl, piperidinyl, pyrrolidinyl,                pyrazolidinyl, imidazolidinyl, azepanyl,                isoxazolyl, isoxazolinyl, oxazolyl, oxazolinyl,                oxadiazolyl, oxatriazolyl, dioxazolyl, oxazinyl,                oxadiazinyl, isoxazolidinyl, morpholinyl,                thiazolyl, isothiazolyl, oxathiazolyl,                oxathiazinyl, or caprolactam (all optionally                substituted with group A);
   group A = alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, aryl, or                aralkyl;
                   R20,R30,R40,R50,R60,R70,R80,R90,R130,R140,R150,R160,R170                = H, halo, or alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, or aryl (all                optionally substituted with halo);
   or (R30+R60) = 5-membered ring (optionally                substituted with halo or alkyl);
   R100 = alkyl, alkenyl or alkynyl (all                optionally substituted with fluoro), or group of                formula (ii);
   R160,R240 = alkylene;
   R190,R200,R210,R220,R230 = alkyl, alkenyl, or                alkynyl;
   q = 0-20;
   m = 0-6;
   n = 0-3;
   a,b = 0-2;
   X- = anion;and
   Z' = P (when the unit (II) is present in the                polymer and the unit (III) is not present in the                polymer) or N or P (when the unit (III) is present                in the polymer).
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) anion exchange polymer, which comprises a                reaction product of a base and the polymer                (a);
   (2) hydroxide exchange polymer, which                comprises an imidazolium tethered-poly(aryl                alkylene) backbone or imidazolium                tethered-poly(aryl-crown ether-alkylene) backbone                free of ether linkages, and has water uptake of 47%                or less based on the dry weight of the polymer when                immersed in pure water at 80 degrees C, or                hydroxide conductivity of 31 mS/cm or more in pure                water at 20 degrees C. The hydroxide exchange                polymer is stable to degradation (as evidenced by                no change in proton nuclear magnetic resonance                spectra) when immersed in 1 M potassium hydroxide                at 130 degrees C for 1200 hours, or is stable to                degradation (as evidenced by no change in proton                nuclear magnetic resonance spectra) when immersed                in 10 M potassium hydroxide at 80 degrees C for 300                hours, or is stable to degradation (as evidenced by                no change in proton nuclear magnetic resonance                spectra) when kept under relative humidity of 23.3%                and 50.9% at 95 degrees C for 300 hours. The                hydroxide exchange polymer has a tensile strength                of 30 MPa or mire and/or elongation at break of                250% or more; and
   (3) manufacture of anion exchange polymer                membrane (16), which involves dissolving the                polymer (a) in the form of cation-functionalized                intermediate polymer in a solvent to form a polymer                solution, casting the polymer solution to form a                polymer membrane, and exchanging anions of the                polymer membrane with hydroxide ions, bicarbonate                ions and/or carbonate ions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                hydroxide exchange membrane fuel cell.
   Fuel cell (10)
   Anode (12)
   Cathode (14)
   Membrane (16)
   Catalyst layers (26,28)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The base comprises a                  hydroxide-, bicarbonate- or carbonate-containing                  base. The hydroxide-containing base comprises                  sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide. The                  bicarbonate-containing base comprises sodium                  bicarbonate or potassium bicarbonate. The                  carbonate-containing base comprises sodium                  carbonate or potassium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The monomer (II-1)                  comprises biphenyl, para-terphenyl,                  meta-terphenyl, para-quaterphenyl,                  9,9-dimethyl-9H-fluorene or benzene. The monomer                  (IV-1) comprises 2,2,2-trifluoroacetophenone or                  1,1,1-trifluoroacetone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): The polymer (a) is                  prepared by reacting a cation-functionalized                  trifluoroketone monomer of formula (I-1),                  optionally trifluoromethyl ketone monomer of                  formula (IV-1), and a substituted aromatic                  monomer of formula (II-1) or a crown ether                  monomer of formula (III-1) in the presence of an                  organic solvent and a polymerization catalyst to                  obtain cation-functionalized intermediate                  polymer, or reacting a halogenated                  trifluoroketone monomer of formula (I-2),                  optionally monomer (IV-1), and monomer (II-1) or                  monomer (III-1) in the presence of an organic                  solvent and a polymerization catalyst to obtain                  halogen-functionalized polymer.R10 = H, halo, or alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl,                  or aryl (all optionally substituted with                  halo);R12 = alkyl, alkenyl or alkynyl (all                  optionally substituted with fluoro), or group                  (ii);andR111 = quaternary ammonium or phosphonium                  group of formula (i), nitrogen-containing                  heterocyclyl or their salts.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  unit (I) with respect to total content of unit                  (II) and unit (III) is 0.01-1. The molar ratio of                  total content of unit (I) and unit (IV) with                  respect to total content of unit (II) and unit                  (III) is 0.95:1 to 1.4:1, preferably 1:1 to                  1.2:1. Preferred Properties: The hydroxide                  exchange polymer has a peak power density of 130                  mW/cm2 or more, when the hydroxide exchange                  polymer is used for hydroxide exchange membrane                  of hydroxide exchange membrane fuel cell (10)                  comprising an anode (12) comprising                  platinum-ruthenium/carbon, and is loaded at 20%                  as hydroxide exchange ionomer in cathodic and                  anodic catalyst layers (26,28) of the fuel cell                  under the test conditions of hydrogen flow rate                  of 1 L/minute, oxygen flow rate of 2 L/minute,                  cell temperature of 95 degrees C, anode                  humidifier temperature of 90 degrees C and                  cathode humidifier temperature of 97 degrees C.                  The hydroxide exchange polymer has a current                  density of 20 mA/cm2 or more at a voltage of 1.8                  V, when the polymer is used for hydroxide                  exchange membrane of hydroxide exchange membrane                  water electrolyzer comprising an cathode                  comprising platinum/carbon catalyst and an anode                  comprising iridium oxide catalyst, and is loaded                  at 30% as hydroxide exchange ionomer in cathodic                  and anodic catalyst layers of the electrolyzer                  under the test conditions of water flow rate of 3                  mL/minute, and electrolyzer temperature and water                  temperature of 80 degrees C. Preferred Process:                  Alternately, manufacture of anion exchange                  polymer membrane involves reacting the polymer                  (a) in the form of halogen-functionalized polymer                  with a quaternary phosphonium compound,                  nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compound or                  their salts in the presence of an organic solvent                  to form a cation-functionalized polymer,                  dissolving the cation-functionalized polymer in a                  solvent to form a polymer solution, casting the                  polymer solution to form a polymer membrane, and                  exchanging anions of the polymer membrane with                  hydroxide ions, bicarbonate ions and/or carbonate                  ions.
   Preferred Definitions:
   X- = halide, BF4-, PF6-, CO32-, or                HCO3-.
   EXAMPLE - In a 100 mL three-necked flask, 3 g                7-bromo-1,1,1-trifluoroheptan-2-one and 1.560 g                biphenyl were dissolved in 6.7 ml methylene                chloride. 6.7 ml Trifluoromethanesulfonic acid were                dripped into the flask over 30 minutes at 0 degrees                C. The reaction was continued at the same                temperature for 24 hours. The obtained viscous,                brown solution was poured slowly into ethanol. The                precipitated solid was filtered, washed with water                and immersed in 1 M potassium carbonate at room                temperature for 12 hours. The obtained product was                filtered, washed with water and dried completely at                60 degrees C under vacuum to obtain                bromide-functionalized polymer. In a 50 mL                one-necked flask equipped with magnetic bar, 2 g                bromide-functionalized polymer, 1.7 g imidazole and                37 ml N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone. The obtained solution                was stirred at 75 degrees C over 12 hours. The                obtained yellow solution was used to cast a                membrane. The membrane was washed consequently                three times with hydrochloride solution (pH of 1)                and deionized water, and dried completely at 60                degrees C under vacuum to obtain an                imidazolium-functionalized polymer.

PN KR2154493-B1
TI Eco-friendly packaging materials comprises base layer formed by laying aggregate group made of crushed coarse aggregate or natural aggregate, and auxiliary base layer mixture comprises mixed aggregates selected from crushed fine aggregate.
AU HEE L D
   KIM Y D
   GU S S
   KIM T S
AE ETOROAD CO LTD (ETOR-Non-standard)
GA 202088222D
AB    NOVELTY - Eco-friendly packaging materials comprises base layer formed by laying aggregate group made of crushed coarse aggregate or natural aggregate. The auxiliary base layer mixture comprises 95-98.5 wt.% mixed aggregates selected from crushed fine aggregate and natural sand, 1-4 wt.% clay coagulant and 0.5-3 wt.% alkali agent laid on upper portion of base layer to be laminated. The upper surface layer laminated by laying upper surface layer mixture comprising 2.5-7.5 wt.% binder mixture, and 92.5-97.5 wt.% aggregate on top of sub-base layer. The clay coagulant is selected from smectite, montmorillonite and hectorite. The alkali agent is used after mixing caustic soda in lime goto in ratio of 9:1, and diluted in water. The bio-flexible fiber is dietary fiber extracted from plantain and sweet potato. The pH adjusting agent is lime selected from quicklime, limestone, lime goto, slaked lime, busan lime, crushed fossil, light slaked lime and light dolomite.
   USE - Eco-friendly packaging materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The eco-friendly packaging materials is effective in creating close landscape, reduces air pollution by suppressing emission of carbon dioxide gas generated during cement production due to non-use of cement and cement toxicity, blocks occurrence, does not cause environmental pollution and leak harmful substances such as phosphorus and lead in water during rainfall, moves rainwater quickly to basement through excellent permeability during rainfall, and provides comfortable walkability to users with self-healing performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Eco-friendly packaging materials comprises base layer formed by laying aggregate group made of crushed coarse aggregate or natural aggregate. The auxiliary base layer mixture comprises 95-98.5 wt.% mixed aggregates selected from crushed fine aggregate and natural sand, 1-4 wt.% clay coagulant and 0.5-3 wt.% alkali agent laid on upper portion of base layer to be laminated. The upper surface layer laminated by laying upper surface layer mixture comprising 2.5-7.5 wt.% binder mixture, and 92.5-97.5 wt.% aggregate on top of sub-base layer. The clay coagulant is selected from smectite, montmorillonite and hectorite. The alkali agent is used after mixing caustic soda in lime goto in ratio of 9:1, and diluted in water. The bio-flexible fiber is dietary fiber extracted from plantain and sweet potato. The pH adjusting agent is lime selected from quicklime, limestone, lime goto, slaked lime, busan lime, crushed fossil, light slaked lime and light dolomite. The hydrophilic agent is selected from baking powder, baking soda and anhydrous sodium carbonate. The antibacterial and antifungal agent is self-healing bio-fiber binder. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for constructing eco-friendly packaging material using self-healing bio-fiber binder, which involves:
   (A) compacting road surface uniformly, and forming base layer by spreading and compacting aggregate having particle size of 13-25 mm;
   (B) spraying small amount of water on surface of mixed aggregate where fine aggregates of 5-10mm in size, passing Masato through 5 mesh sieve, removing natural sand with fine powder with 200 mesh sieve, and mixing clay coagulant with mixed aggregate containing moisture to obtain aggregate mixture;
   (C) preparing auxiliary base layer mixture by finely spraying alkaline agent prepared by dissolving lime goto and caustic soda in water to uniformly permeate aggregate mixture in which clay coagulant is mixed;
   (D) forming sub-base layer by uniformly laying sub-base mixture to thickness of 40-50 mm on upper portion of base layer;
   (E) preparing aggregate for upper surface layer by mixing Masato passed through 5 mesh sieve and natural sand from which fine powder is removed with 200 mesh sieve;
   (F) preparing binder mixture by mixing bio-flexible fiber, pH adjuster, hydrophilic agent and antibacterial and anti-fungal agent;
   (G) preparing upper surface layer mixture by uniformly mixing prepared upper surface aggregate and binder mixture, and adding appropriate amount of water; and
   (H) forming upper surface layer by uniformly laying upper surface layer mixture to thickness of 20-70 mm on upper portion of sub-base layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Compositions: The binder mixture 50-60 wt.% bio-flexible fiber, 15-25 wt.% pH adjuster, 15-25 wt.% hydrophilic agent and 0.1-1 wt.% antibacterial and antifungal agent. Preferred Conditions: The mulching material is further laid on upper surface layer.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Destructive distillation of polyethylene and polypropylene waste, comprises e.g. loading polyethylene and polypropylene waste preliminarily purified by flotation into first destructive distillation reactor and connecting and heating furnace.
AU SEIDAMETOV R I
   SETMANBETOV S N
   SEYDAMETOV R I
AE SEIDAMETOV R I (SEID-Individual)
   SETMANBETOV S N (SETM-Individual)
   SEYDAMETOV R I (SEYD-Individual)
   SETMANBETOV S N (SETM-Individual)
   SETMANBETOV S N (SETM-Individual)
   SEIDAMETOV R I (SEID-Individual)
   SETMANBETOV S N (SETM-Individual)
   SETMANBETOV S N (SETM-Individual)
   SEIDAMETOV R I (SEID-Individual)
GA 2020420408
AB    NOVELTY - Destructive distillation of polyethylene and polypropylene waste, comprises e.g. loading into first destructive distillation reactor, polyethylene and polypropylene waste preliminarily purified by flotation from impurities containing polyvinyl chloride, polyethylene terephthalate, cellulose, organic compounds and rubber; connecting and heating the furnace of the first destructive distillation reactor with a fuel burner; heating and maintaining the temperature in the cube-collector of hydrocarbons with exhaust gases, maintaining the temperature in the cube-collector of hydrocarbons of the dewaxing device carried out by turning off-connecting the supply of exhaust gases to the boiler; and regulating the outlet temperature of the vapor-gas mixture of hydrocarbons from the dewaxing unit by supplying cooling water to the dewaxing unit of the dewaxing unit, selection of paraffin fractions.
   USE - The method and device are useful for destructive distillation of polyethylene and polypropylene waste (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method and device: increase the productivity and efficiency of destructive distillation of waste polyethylene and polypropylene, thus improving the quality of distillation products, improving the safety of the device and destructive distillation, reducing harmful emissions into the environment and reducing energy consumption; save fuel and heating time; and expand the arsenal of structurally and technologically advanced technical means for the implementation of thermal destruction of polymer wastes with the production of destructive distillation products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Destructive distillation of polyethylene and polypropylene waste, comprises: loading into a first destructive distillation reactor, polyethylene and polypropylene waste preliminarily purified by flotation from impurities containing polyvinyl chloride, polyethylene terephthalate, cellulose, organic compounds and rubber; connecting and heating the furnace of the first destructive distillation reactor with a fuel burner; heating and maintaining the temperature in the cube-collector of hydrocarbons with exhaust gases, maintaining the temperature in the cube-collector of hydrocarbons of the dewaxing device carried out by turning off-connecting the supply of exhaust gases to the boiler; regulating the outlet temperature of the vapor-gas mixture of hydrocarbons from the dewaxing unit by supplying cooling water to the dewaxing unit of the dewaxing unit, selection of paraffin fractions; fractionating the remaining products of destructive distillation in a distillation column to obtain the vapor phase of the gasoline fraction and the liquid phase of the diesel fraction, controlling the temperature of the remaining vapor-gas mixture from the distillation column by supplying cooling water to the distillation column reflux condenser; cooling the remaining vapor-gas mixture in the column of a constantly working heat exchanger, separating it into gasoline and gas fractions; loading raw materials into the next destructive distillation reactor; shutting off the fuel supply to the burner of the first reactor; lowering the furnace of the first reactor and cooling it and connecting the furnace of the next reactor and heating it with a fuel burner; unloading solid carbonaceous precipitate from cooled destructive distillation reactors, before loading the raw materials, the bottom of each reactor is coated with a layer of non-stick lubricant; connecting reactor furnaces using air bags, vacuum loading of the reactors carried out in several stages according to the cycle of loading the reactor with raw materials, evacuating the loaded volume of the reactor, heating the reactor to 110-260 degrees C to dilute the loaded raw materials until the level of for liquefied raw materials, 7 from the height of the full load of the reactor; after the loaded first reactor enters the destructive distillation operating mode, loading the next reactor in several stages according to the same cycle loading the reactor with raw materials, evacuating the loaded volume of the reactor, heating the reactor to 110-26 degrees C to dilute the loaded raw materials until the level of 0 is reached for the liquefied raw materials, 7 from the full load height of the next reactor; reactor cooling carried out by supplying carbon dioxide; cleaning through the lower side hatch of the cooled reactor carried out with an industrial sandblasting machine or vacuum cleaner; and lowering the temperature of the exhaust gases discharged into the atmosphere carried out by passing them through the boilers. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device comprises at least two heating units that are connected in parallel, each of which contains a destructive distillation reactor configured to top load the polymer feed through the loading unit and to remove solid carbonaceous sediment through the bottom side hatch of the reactor, each of the reactors contains a furnace, which is configured to disconnect from and attach to the reactor and a fuel burner, a unit for separating the products of the destructive distillation of polymer raw materials into components comprising series-connected to a water-cooled heat exchanger with a downward flow of a steam-gas mixture of hydrocarbons, a hydrocarbon storage cube, made in the form of a cylindrical tank arranged horizontally, equipped with a boiler heated by exhaust gases, mounted on a dewaxing cube, consisting of a bottom with tubes and mass transfer nozzles filled with a catalyst, and from a reflux condenser made in the form of a shell-and-tube heat exchanger, installed in the upper part of the dewaxing device, distillation column for separating fractions of diesel fuel and gasoline, comprising a reflux condenser installed in the upper part of the column, a concentration section with mass transfer nozzles filled with a catalyst, power and stripping sections, a heat exchanger for cooling the diesel fraction, a tubular vertically mounted heat exchanger with a downward movement of gasoline vapor and hydrocarbon gas (gas fraction), a gas separator, a piping system for a steam-gas mixture of hydrocarbons, an exhaust gas piping system with gates installed before and after the hydrocarbon collector, cooling systems installed on the pipeline for a gas-vapor mixture of hydrocarbons of destructive distillation product collections having diesel fuel fraction, gasoline fraction, gas fraction, paraffin fraction collectors, where the circulating water tank is installed in the cooling system, carbon sediment collector, separated water collector, each furnace is lined with refractory high-temperature ceramic fibers and mounted on air cushions, loading unit of each reactor is made vacuum, the reactors are connected to the gas-vapor mixture piping system by pipeline sections with bypasses installed on them, a vacuum pump is installed in the piping system of the gas-vapor mixture with the possibility of its connection-disconnection to each of the reactors, in the system of pipelines of the gas-vapor mixture, a container with carbon dioxide is installed with the possibility of its connection-disconnection to each of the reactors, a water-cooled heat exchanger with a downward flow of a gas-vapor mixture of hydrocarbons is equipped with a steam discharge pipeline to the atmosphere with a branch pipe for extinguishing the fire steam, reactor connected to the pipelines of the gas-vapor mixture system in separate sections equipped with valves, before and after which the bypass pipes with pressure control valves in the system for dosed discharge of the gas-vapor mixture are cut, boilers installed in the exhaust gas piping system, in the upper part of the collection cube, under the water-cooled heat exchanger, there is a paraffin fraction trap made with the possibility of its selection and supplying the paraffin fraction through the refrigerator to the paraffin fraction collector, in the upper part of the collection cube, under the dewaxing device, and droplet eliminator installed in the form of a funnel with an elongated outlet tube to prevent foaming in the collection cube, at the end of the collection cube on the exhaust gas inlet side, a hatch is made for connecting the collection cube to a refrigerator for draining heavy paraffins into the heavy paraffin collector, after the dewaxing unit of the dewaxing unit, a vapor-gas mixture filter-damper is installed, connected tangentially to the outlet of the distillation column power section to create vortex motion of the vapor-gas mixture flow in a distillation column, in the distillation column distillation column, at least three distillation plates are installed, under the collection cube in front of the paraffin fraction refrigerator, after the stripping section in front of the diesel fraction cooling heat exchanger, automatic level controls with an exhaust valve are installed after the gas separator as a catalyst in mass transfer nozzles, titanium-free nickel-containing alloys with at least 20% nickel are used, device at the outlet of the gas separator is additionally equipped with a gas separator. A section of the pipeline with valves for inserting a vacuum pump on it with the possibility of its connection-disconnection to each reactor is cut into the gas-vapor mixture pipeline at the outlet of the loading unit of each reactor. The catalyst is made in the form of thin strips twisted in a spiral in the form of chips. The furnace is made with a lining of high-temperature soft ceramic cardboard (Hitermic NT). The furnace is made with a lining of refractory ceramic wool. The exhaust valves of the automatic level controller are arranged to open after the fractions reach the level 2/3-1/2 of the corresponding volumes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The vacuumization of the loaded volume of the reactor is carried out by a vacuum pump. The supply of carbon dioxide is carried out from tanks connected to the reactors with carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The refractory paraffin is used as a layer of non-stick lubricant. The resulting products of destructive distillation are used as burner fuel.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); ; A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.)

PN CN110802312-A; CN110802312-B
TI Welding flat butt joints of Q370qD and Q500qE            bridge steel comprises selecting mechanical properties            of base metal, joint forming, matching wire and            welding.
AU DU W
   WU J
   LIU Z
   LI Y
   TIAN L
AE CHINA RAILWAY BAOJI BRIDGE GROUP CO LTD (CREN-C)
   CHINA RAILWAY BAOJI BRIDGE YANGZHOU CO (CREN-C)
   CHINA RAILWAY BAOJI BRIDGE GROUP CO LTD (CREN-C)
GA 202016476E
AB    NOVELTY - Welding flat butt joints of Q370qD and Q500qE                bridge steel comprises (1) selecting mechanical                properties of base metal: tensile strength of                Q370qD bridge steel Rm greater than or equal to 415                MPa, yield strength Rel greater than or equal to                530 MPa, elongation A greater than or equal to 25%,                Kv2 impact energy-20J at -20 degrees C, tensile                strength Rm of Q500qE bridge steel greater than or                equal to 645 MPa, yield strength Rel greater than                or equal to 550 MPa, elongation A greater than or                equal to 23%, and Kv2 impact energy at -40 degrees                C greater than or equal to 140 J; (2) joint form:                the plate thickness of Q370qD and Q500qE bridge                steel at the joint are both 21-41 mm, the joint                groove is a V-shaped groove, the groove angle is                35-38?, the root gap is 6-30 mm, and groove angle                angle decreases with increasing root clearance; (3)                matching wire: tensile strength of solid wire Rm                greater than or equal to 600 MPa, yield strength                Rel greater than or equal to 700 MPa, elongation A                greater than or equal to 20%, Kv2 impact energy at                -40 degrees C is greater than or equal to 90 J;                tensile strength of flux-cored wire Rm greater than                or equal to 600 MPa, yield strength Rel greater                than or equal to 700 MPa, elongation A greater than                or equal to 20%, and Kv2 impact energy at -40                degrees C is greater than or equal to 70 J, and (4)                welding method.
   USE - The method is useful for welding flat butt                joints of Q370qD and Q500qE bridge steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: welds bridge steel with mechanical                properties of the weld meet the standard and                design, is required that both the strength of the                weld and the heat affected zone are higher than the                strength of the Q370qE base metal, does not break                at the stress concentration point of the butt joint                and the weak position of the weld, and has better                safety on Q370qD and Q500qE heterogeneous butt                joints.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Welding flat butt joints of Q370qD and Q500qE                bridge steel comprises (1) selecting mechanical                properties of base metal: tensile strength of                Q370qD bridge steel Rm greater than or equal to 415                MPa, yield strength Rel greater than or equal to                530 MPa, elongation A greater than or equal to 25%,                Kv2 impact energy-20J at -20 degrees C, tensile                strength Rm of Q500qE bridge steel greater than or                equal to 645 MPa, yield strength Rel greater than                or equal to 550 MPa, elongation A greater than or                equal to 23%, and Kv2 impact energy at -40 degrees                C greater than or equal to 140 J; (2) joint form:                the plate thickness of Q370qD and Q500qE bridge                steel at the joint are both 21-41 mm, the joint                groove is a V-shaped groove, the groove angle is                35-38?, the root gap is 6-30 mm, and groove angle                angle decreases with increasing root clearance; (3)                matching wire: tensile strength of solid wire Rm                greater than or equal to 600 MPa, yield strength                Rel greater than or equal to 700 MPa, elongation A                greater than or equal to 20%, Kv2 impact energy at                -40 degrees C is greater than or equal to 90 J;                tensile strength of flux-cored wire Rm greater than                or equal to 600 MPa, yield strength Rel greater                than or equal to 700 MPa, elongation A greater than                or equal to 20%, and Kv2 impact energy at -40                degrees C is greater than or equal to 70 J, and (4)                welding method: (4a) polishing the 30 mm range on                both sides of the groove to expose the metallic                luster; (4b) pasting ceramic blocks on the back of                the groove; (4c) preheating the steel plate at the                joint at 60-80 degrees C with an electromagnetic                induction heating pad; (4d) performing bottom                welding by using solid wire argon-rich gas shielded                welding process; (4e) using the carbon dioxide gas                shielded welding process of flux-cored wire for                filling and cover welding; and (4f) using                insulation cotton to cover the weld seam for slow                cooling.
DC X24 (Electric Welding)
MC X24-B01; X24-B06; X24-C

PT P
PN CN110639472-A; CN110639472-B
TI Preparation of composite adsorbent involves adding nanomineral crystal composite adsorbent powder and auxiliary material to tar, mixing, crushing, drying, soaking in saturated calcium chloride solution, and drying under nitrogen protection.
AU GONG X
   DONG Y
AE UNIV HARBIN COMMERCE (HAST-C)
GA 202004131V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of activated carbon/nanomineral crystal/graphene-based densified composite adsorbent comprises preparing dried impregnated material comprising anthracite powder, first auxiliary material and asphalt; carbonizing; crushing and grinding dried carbonized material, sieving, adding obtained carbonized material powder and nanomineral crystal powder to tar, thoroughly mixing, molding, and crushing; activating obtained mixed molded material to obtain nanomineral crystal composite adsorbent with activated carbon as framework; crushing and grinding nanomineral crystal composite adsorbent, sieving, adding composite adsorbent powder and second auxiliary material to tar, uniformly mixing, molding or crushing, and drying to obtain dried mixture; and soaking the dried mixture in saturated calcium chloride solution and drying under nitrogen protection.
   USE - The method is for preparing activated carbon/nanomineral crystal/graphene-based densified composite adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problems of low adsorption capacity and low heat and mass transfer performance of current adsorption material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of activated carbon/nanomineral crystal/graphene-based densified composite adsorbent comprises:
   (A) preprocessing anthracite to prepare anthracite powder, adding anthracite powder and first auxiliary material to asphalt, thoroughly and uniformly mixing, putting in dry molding device, pressing into block under 200-300 Pa, crushing into pieces, soaking in potassium hydroxide (KOH) solution for 4-6 hours, and drying at 105-125 degrees C to obtain dried impregnated material;
   (B) putting dried impregnated material in carbonization furnace, heating to 350-400 degrees C under carbon dioxide (CO2) protection, carbonizing at 350-400 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, continuously heating to 500-600 degrees C at 5 degrees C/minute under nitrogen protection, carbonizing at 500-600 degrees C for 40-60 minutes, soaking the carbonized material in acidic solution for 4-6 hours, and drying at 105-125 degrees C to obtain dried carbonized material;
   (C) crushing and grinding dried carbonized material, passing through 200-mesh sieve, adding obtained carbonized material powder and adsorption-type nanomineral crystal powder to tar, thoroughly and uniformly mixing, putting in dry molding device, pressing into block under 200-300 Pa, and crushing into 1-3 mm pieces to obtain mixed molded material;
   (D) putting the mixed molded material in activation furnace, heating to 800-950 degrees C while introducing water vapor at 1-3.5 mL/hour-g, and activating at 800-950 degrees C for 90-110 minutes to obtain nanomineral crystal composite adsorbent with activated carbon as framework;
   (E) crushing and grinding nanomineral crystal composite adsorbent, passing through 200-mesh sieve, adding composite adsorbent powder and second auxiliary material to tar and uniformly mixing, putting in molding device, molding into block or crushing into granules, and drying at 105-125 degrees C to obtain dried mixture; and
   (F) soaking the dried mixture in saturated calcium chloride (CaCl2) solution for 24 hours and drying at 105-150 degrees C under nitrogen protection.
   The weight-volume ratio (g:mL) of anthracite to asphalt is 11:2. The weight-volume (g:mL) ratio of first auxiliary material to asphalt is 7:2. The weight-volume ratio (g:L) of crushed pieces to potassium hydroxide solution is 100-250:1. The first auxiliary material is coconut shell powder or waste rubber chips. The weight-volume ratio (g:L) of carbonized material to acidic solution is 100-250:1. The weight ratio of carbonized material powder and adsorption-type nanomineral crystal powder is 2-3:1. The ratio of total weight of carbonized material powder and adsorption-type nanomineral crystal powder to volume of tar is 10:1. The weight ratio of composite adsorbent powder and second auxiliary material is 3-4:1. The ratio of total weight of composite adsorbent powder and second auxiliary material to volume of tar is 10:1. The second auxiliary material is mixture of graphene powder and copper nanopowder, mixture of graphene powder and iron nanopowder, graphene powder, or expanded graphite powder. The weight-volume ratio (g:L) of dried mixture to saturated calcium chloride solution is 100:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of KOH in KOH solution is 5-10 wt.%. The acidic solution is 5-10 wt.% phosphoric acid solution, or solution comprising 5-10 wt.% phosphoric acid and zinc chloride at volume ratio of 3:1. The nanomineral crystal powder is prepared by crushing and grinding sepiolite, thoroughly mixing with diatomite and attapulgite, and grinding. The weight ratio of diatomite and attapulgite is 3:2. The weight ratio of total amount of diatomite and attapulgite to sepiolite is 5:3. The graphite powder is prepared by grinding graphite particles and sieving through 200-mesh sieve. The weight ratio of graphene powder to copper nanopowder is 3:1. The weight ratio of graphene powder to iron nanopowder is 3:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Preprocessing of anthracite includes crushing anthracite and sieving through 200-mesh sieve. The coconut shell powder is obtained by crushing and grinding coconut shell, and sieving through 100-mesh sieve.

PN CN110514714-A; CN110514714-B
TI Polylactic acid/paper hybrid chip for online monitoring of nerve cells releasing dopamine comprises polylactic acid model which is used as supporting substrate for cell culture fluid, and filter paper or A4 paper is used as a carrier.
AU LIU A
   LIU M
   ZHONG Y
   LEI Y
   LIU H
   GUO Z
AE UNIV FUJIAN MEDICAL (UNFM-C)
   UNIV FUJIAN MEDICAL (UNFM-C)
GA 2019A1859W
AB    NOVELTY - Polylactic acid/paper hybrid chip for online monitoring of nerve cells releasing dopamine comprises a polylactic acid model which is used as a supporting substrate for cell culture fluid, and filter paper or A4 paper is used as a carrier for cell culture to realize three-dimensional neural cells to cultivate nerve cells.
   USE - Polylactic acid/paper hybrid chip for online monitoring of nerve cells releasing dopamine.
   ADVANTAGE - The polylactic acid/paper hybrid chip realizes the normal adrenal chromaffin cell tumor cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for obtaining polylactic acid/paper hybrid chip, which involves performing 3D printing method and a hot wax printing method, using the 123D Design software to build a three-dimensional model of the model, the size is 45mmx 45 mmx 11 mm, the diameter of the reservoir is 5 mm, and the height is 5mm, the rectangular channel width is 8mm and the depth is 2.5mm, using the Cura 15.02.1 software to slice the 3D model in layers, where the layer height is 0.1 mm, shell wall thickness is 0.8mm, bottom/top thickness is 1.2 mm, filling density is 80%, print temperature is 190 degrees C, connecting the computer with A8S type 3D printer, and then import the sliced 3D structure diagram into the 3D printer in gcode format, sending the wire of polymer material to a metal nozzle with a heating device through a gear mechanism, heating the polymer material above the glass transition temperature, and spraying the softened polymer material fuse from the nozzle to the bottom plate to cool down, connecting the power supply, turn on, level, feed, selecting the model to be printed, and waiting for the nozzle temperature to rise to 190 degrees C before starting printing, and after degassing the prepared PDMS solution applying it uniformly on the surface of the model, and then placing it in an 85 degrees C oven for 1 hour to cure, the patterned paper chip is made by using Abode Illustrator CS6 Vector drawing software designs paper chip patterns, where the length of the square cell culture area in the center of the paper base is 5mm and the width is 5mm, the diameter of the round cell culture area is 4mm, printing the pattern with a Xerox ColorQube 8870 thermal wax printer, and then baking in an oven at 95 degrees C until the paraffin on the surface of the paper base was completely melted and penetrated into the interior of the paper base, and forming closing hydrophilic areas to limit the cells; and
   (2) a method for detecting dopamine of a polylactic acid/paper hybrid chip for online monitoring of dopamine release from nerve cells, which involves the model of polylactic acid is a cross-shaped channel with four reservoirs at the end of the channel, injecting a hydrogel into the channel for the transfer of nutrients required for cell culture, using the liquid storage tank to store cell culture fluid, to ensure the substances and energy required for cell proliferation, dividing the patterned paper chip produced by the hot wax printing method is divided into two portions, a hydrophilic region and a hydrophobic region, the hydrophilic region is used for cell culture, the polylactic acid model and the paper hybrid chip are tightly fixed together with a round ticket holder, so the nutrients transported by the hydrogel can reach the cell culture area smoothly, taking cell-hydrogel suspension drops, and placing the device in a breathable box, putting it in a 5% carbon dioxide, at 37 degrees C incubator near the water, and changing the culture solution once a day.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Conditions: The successful hybridization chip is established on the polylactic acid/paper control group, model group(A beta 25-35), donepezil intervention group, stem cell is to be contacted with the liquid intervention group and stem cells intervening group, and is combined with the screen printed electrode, for detecting each group, five groups of nerve cell release dopamine dopamine levels with different expression.

AU DING X
AE HUIAN DAELIM SHOES CO LTD (HUIA-Non-standard)
GA 201999044B
AB    NOVELTY - A non-deformable three dimensional life-saving belt comprises a base fabric, where the base fabric is bottom layer, top layer and middle layer, where two free ends of the base fabric are connected with an adjustable length adjustment belt, the adjusting belt is provided with a connecting buckle for fastening the waist belt, and the base fabric is provided with an inflatable bottle and an inflation balloon, the inflation bottle is provided with an inflation switch for controlling the communication between the inflation bottle and the inflation bladder, the inflation balloon is provided with a one-way air blowing tube, the air pipe cover is provided on the air blowing pipe, and a fluorescent lifesaving logo is printed on the outer surface of the top surface, the base fabric and the top surface adopt a three dimensional warp knitting process, the base fabric is made of an antistatic polyester fiber material with a specification of 100-150D.
   USE - Non-deformable three dimensional life-saving belt.
   ADVANTAGE - The non-deformable three dimensional life-saving belt solves the problems that the existing inflatable life-saving waist belt is easily deformed, difficult to clean, not comfortable enough to wear, and has a single function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A non-deformable three dimensional life-saving belt comprises a base fabric, where the base fabric is bottom layer, top layer and middle layer, where two free ends of the base fabric are connected with an adjustable length adjustment belt, the adjusting belt is provided with a connecting buckle for fastening the waist belt, and the base fabric is provided with an inflatable bottle and an inflation balloon, the inflation bottle is provided with an inflation switch for controlling the communication between the inflation bottle and the inflation bladder, the inflation balloon is provided with a one-way air blowing tube, the air pipe cover is provided on the air blowing pipe, and a fluorescent lifesaving logo is printed on the outer surface of the top surface, the base fabric and the top surface adopt a three dimensional warp knitting process, the base fabric is made of an antistatic polyester fiber material with a specification of 100-150D, and is knitted according to the length and width of a stable knitting chain and a partial weft interlining organization, the base fabric and the top surface are connected in an air-through manner with a Spandex (RTM: Synthetic fiber) fiber of a size of 20-40D/1F, the top surface is woven with a Spandex (RTM: Synthetic fiber) elastic fiber material with a size of 20-40D/1F, the antistatic polyester is prepared by mixing 0.5-3 pts. wt. double-quinone polyaniline powder, 4-5 pts. wt. gas phase-grown carbon fiber powder, 0.5-2 pts. wt. maleic anhydride-grafted styrene-(ethylene-butene)-styrene, 90-95 pts. wt. polyethylene terephthalate chips by high speed stirrer, placing in a twin-screw extruder, uniformly melting and blending at a temperature of 220-280 degrees C, and performing melt spinning process to obtain the antistatic polyester fiber, and the specifications of the gas phase-grown carbon fiber powder are 50-200 mu m in length and 0.1-0.3 mu m in diameter, the first zone extrusion temperature of the twin-screw extruder is 220-230 degrees C, the second zone extrusion temperature of the twin-screw extruder is 230-240 degrees C, the third zone extrusion temperature of the twin-screw extruder is 240-250 degrees C, the fourth zone extrusion temperature of the twin-screw extruder is 250-260 degrees C, the fifth zone extrusion temperature of the twin-screw extruder is 260-270 degrees C, the sixth zone extrusion temperature of the twin-screw extruder is 270-280 degrees C, and the inflatable bottle is two high-pressure carbon dioxide inflatable steel bottles, which are respectively arranged at two ends of the inflation bladder.

PN CN110257327-A
TI Isolating and culturing umbilical cord mesenchymal            stem cells comprises e.g. collecting the umbilical cord            specimen, taking umbilical cord after umbilical cord,            adding umbilical cord tissue protection and sending it            to the laboratory.
AU PAN H
   GU M
   XU Z
   LI C
   GUO X
   JIA S
   YANG K
AE CHONGQING CELL BIOENGINEERING TECHNOLOGY (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201991779U
AB    NOVELTY - Isolating and culturing umbilical cord                mesenchymal stem cells comprises e.g. (i)                collecting the umbilical cord specimen, taking                umbilical cord after the umbilical cord of the                full-term delivery into the collection bottle,                adding the umbilical cord tissue protection                solution, and sending it to the laboratory, and                collecting the umbilical cord specimen, and                separating, (ii) cleaning the umbilical cord                specimen, first removing the umbilical cord                specimen from the collection bottle, immersing in                alcohol, disinfecting, rinsing umbilical cord                specimen with physiological saline to remove                alcohol residue, (iii) umbilical cord specimen                slicing, (iv) processing the umbilical cord tissue                sheet by placing the umbilical cord tissue sheet                cut into two pieces in physiological saline to                clean the blood stain on the umbilical tissue                sheet, (v) cultivating the umbilical tissue sheet                by uniformly affixing the cleaned umbilical tissue                sheet to the cell culture dish.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating and                culturing umbilical cord mesenchymal stem                cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: separate umbilical cord                mesenchymal stem cells, and tissue has high                probability of growing cells, and meet the needs of                clinical application and mass production of stem                cell products, and the separation culture has small                workload and high efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Isolating and culturing umbilical cord                mesenchymal stem cells comprises (i) collecting the                umbilical cord specimen, taking umbilical cord 10                cm (cm) after the umbilical cord of the full-term                delivery into the collection bottle, adding the                umbilical cord tissue protection solution, and                sending it to the laboratory at 4-8 degrees C, and                collecting the umbilical cord specimen, and                separating within 48 hours, (ii) cleaning the                umbilical cord specimen, first removing the                umbilical cord specimen from the collection bottle,                immersing it in 75% alcohol, disinfecting for 20-40                seconds, rinsing umbilical cord specimen with                physiological saline for 2-3 times to remove                alcohol residue, (iii) umbilical cord specimen                slicing, first cut 1cm at both ends of the                umbilical cord specimen, taking the middle 5cm                umbilical cord specimen, cutting the umbilical cord                tissue sheet of 1mm (mm) thickness along the                transverse direction of the umbilical cord                specimen, and slicing each umbilical cord tissue                into two pieces in half, (iv) processing the                umbilical cord tissue sheet by placing the                umbilical cord tissue sheet cut into two pieces in                physiological saline to clean the blood stain on                the umbilical tissue sheet, (v) cultivating the                umbilical tissue sheet by uniformly affixing the                cleaned umbilical tissue sheet to the cell culture                dish, and covering the umbilical tissue sheet with                a glass piece, adding complete culture solution,                and culturing in an incubator at 37 degrees C, 5%                carbon dioxide concentration, and saturated                humidity, changing the complete culture solution                every 4 days, and observeing the growth of cells                around the umbilical tissue strip on the bottom of                the culture dish and the glass piece, and (vi)                carrying out digestion treatment, when the bottom                of the culture dish and the glass piece around the                umbilical tissue sheet are covered with a large                number of cells, removing the umbilical cord tissue                sheet, and digesting the cells on the culture dish                and the glass piece with trypsin solution for 1-3                minutes, (vii) collecting the cells by                centrifugation, terminating the digestion by adding                2 ml of fetal bovine serum, collecting the                digestive juice in a 50 ml centrifuge tube, and                collecting the cells by centrifugation, (viii)                resuspending the collected cells in the whole                culture solution and inoculation in a culture                flask, where the cultured cells are P1 generation                cells, and (ix) digesting and subculturing, after                P1 cells are grown to 80%-95%, digesting with                trypsin and subculturing, where cells obtained by                subculture are P2 cells, method of subculture is to                pass on a bottle of 5 bottles, and (x) after P2                generation cells grow to 80-95% fusion, digesting                and collecting the cells for identification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  composition of the umbilical cord tissue                  protection solution described is DMEM/F12 medium.                  The volume ratio of D-Hanks solution,                  streptomycin, penicillin, DMEM/F12 medium and                  D-Hanks solution is 1:1. The concentration of                  penicillin is 100 U/ml and the concentration of                  streptomycin is 100 g/ml. In the step (iv), the                  physiological saline described is physiological                  saline for injection having a mass concentration                  of 0.9%. In the step (v), the complete culture                  solution described is DMEM/F12 medium containing                  5-15% by volume of fetal bovine serum. In the                  step (vi), the tryptic solution described has a                  mass concentration of 0.125-0.5%. In the step                  (vii), The cells were collected by centrifugation                  under 300-700G.
