TI Preparing photocatalyst copper oxide using copper cutting waste recycling useful for degrading organic dye comprises vacuum drying molten salt, dehydrating, oxidizing, carrying out high temperature molten salt reaction and centrifuging.
AU YIN H
   ZHOU F
   ZHAO H
   XIE H
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UYDB-C)
GA 2019818342
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing photocatalyst copper oxide using copper cutting waste recycling comprises (a) placing the molten salt raw material powder in muffle furnace, sealing, vacuum drying, dehydrating, cooling to obtain molten salt, (b) placing copper cutting waste in muffle furnace, heating, oxidizing, removing copper cutting and oil stains remaining on the surface to obtain copper oxide powder, (c) carrying out high temperature molten salt reaction by washing and drying dehydrated molten salt raw material in crucible, placing in a muffle furnace, heating under vacuum, introducing argon gas and raising the temperature, and adding copper oxide powder to molten salt system, incubating and cooling to obtain a molten salt system of copper oxide, and (iv) removing crucible, adding deionized water to molten salt system, stirring to obtain suspension of copper oxide, and (d) centrifuging to obtain an orange-yellow powder, and washing the orange-yellow powder, drying and packaging to obtain the product.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful for the degradation of organic dyes.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst: has a good degradation effect on methyl orange or methylene blue. The method: is economical, simple, has simple process flow, environmentally friendly, and contains the process product of photodegradation organic dye to be only carbon dioxide and oxygen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing photocatalyst copper oxide using copper cutting waste recycling comprises (a) placing molten salt raw material powder in muffle furnace, sealing, vacuum drying, dehydrating, cooling to room temperature with a furnace to obtain a molten salt, where the molten salt is chloride salt, carbonate salt and/or nitrate salt, (b) placing the copper cutting waste in muffle furnace, heating to 900-1200 degrees C in air, oxidizing at a constant temperature until the copper cutting waste is fully oxidized, removing the copper cutting, removing oil stains remaining on the surface of copper cutting waste to obtain copper oxide powder, (c) carrying out high temperature molten salt reaction by (i) washing and drying the dehydrated molten salt raw material in crucible and sealing, (ii) placing the sealed reactor in a muffle furnace, heating to 200-500 degrees C under vacuum for 3-5 hours, introducing argon gas as a shielding gas, raising the temperature to the corresponding molten salt melting temperature to obtain molten salt system in a molten state, and (iii) adding the copper oxide powder to molten salt system in a molten state, allowing to stand at a constant temperature for 1-3 hours at room temperature and cooling to room temperature to obtain a molten salt system of copper oxide, where the amount of the copper oxide powder is a molten salt is 1-10 wt.%, and the experimental temperature is higher than the temperature of molten salt system in the molten state by 0-400 degrees C, and (iv) removing crucible, adding deionized water to the molten salt system of copper oxide, stirring until the molten salt is completely dissolved to obtain suspension of copper oxide, and (d) centrifuging the suspension of copper oxide to obtain an orange-yellow powder, and (ii) washing the orange-yellow powder, drying and packaging to obtain the product. INDEPNDENT CLAIM is also included for a photocatalyst copper oxide prepared by recycling copper cutting waste to a particle size of less than 2 mu m which is obtained by the above mentioned method; #use method of photocatalyst copper oxide prepared by the copper cutting waste recycling process for degrading an organic dye comprising adding the photocatalyst copper oxide prepared by recycling copper cutting waste into the sewage containing organic dye to maintain desorption-desorption equilibrium, allowing to degrade the organic dye under the condition of light and stirring until the sewage is clarified, where the concentration is 5-30 mg/l, and the amount of copper oxide photocatalyst is 0.01-2 mg/ml.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The vacuum drying and dehydration process in step (a) comprises raising the temperature to 200-500 degrees C for 10-15 hours at a heating rate of 2-5 degrees C/minute. The cleaning and drying in step (a) comprises repeatedly cleaning after washing with fresh water, naturally drying in a muffle furnace, heating at 100-120 degrees C at a rate of 2-3 degrees C/minutes, and a constant temperature of 20-60 minutes. The argon gas in step (b) is used as a shielding gas at a gas flow rate of 80-150 ml/minute. The experimental temperature in step (c) is 750-950 degrees C, where the molten salt system is a mixed salt of calcium chloride and sodium chloride, and the experimental temperature is 580-900 degrees C. The centrifugal speed in step (a) is 4000-12000 revolutions/minute for 5-20 minutes. The step (b) further comprises carrying out washing by adding deionized water and centrifuging at 4000-12000 revolutions/minute for 5-20 minutes and removing the washing liquid. The degradation of organic dye comprises (i) uniformly dispersing the copper oxide in the dark in an aqueous solution of methyl orange, methylene blue or organic dye to form a suspension, where the concentration of methyl orange, methylene blue or other organic dye is 5 mg-30 mg/l, and the amount of photocatalyst is 0.01-2 mg/ml, (iii) stirring in the dark for 0-0.5 hours to ensure that the photocatalyst reaches the adsorption-desorption equilibrium in the suspension to obtain the adsorption-desorption equilibrium suspension, subjecting to light irradiation degradation, stirring, irradiating the xenon lamp source for a period of time, taking out the sample and centrifuging, subjecting the supernatant to spectral scanning to measure the absorbance, (iii) calculating the degradation rate of organic dye according to formula D=(C0-C)/C0/asterisk100%, where D is the degradation of organic dye by copper oxide photocatalyst, C0 is the concentration of the original methyl orange, methylene blue or other organic dye, and C is the concentration of methyl orange, methylene blue or other organic dye after the photocatalyst treatment. The use method further comprises the light intensity achieved by the xenon lamp source to 300-800 mW/cm2, where the sampling is 4-6 ml per sample, the suspension to the organic dye becomes clear and transparent after 0.1-10 hours. Preferred Components: The molten salt system in step (c) is calcium chloride. Preferred Composition: The particle diameter of the molten salt raw material powder in step (a) is less than or equal to 2 mm. The molten salt is a calcium chloride salt or a mixed salt of calcium chloride and sodium chloride having a ratio of (0.4-0.5):(0.5-0.6). The ratio of the molten salt system to water of the suspension in step (d) is (101-110)g:500ml.

PT P
PN WO2019158827-A1
TI Liquefying natural gas in combination with process for producing synthesis gas, comprises e.g. pretreating feed natural gas to remove impurities that may freeze during liquefaction process and extracting stream enriched in e.g. hydrocarbons.
AU COSTA DE BEAUREGARD P
   MARTY P
   MOREL T
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 201973269R
AB    NOVELTY - Liquefying natural gas in combination with a process for producing synthesis gas, comprises: (a) pretreatment of feed natural gas to remove impurities that may freeze during liquefaction process; (b) extracting, from the gas stream from step (a), of a stream enriched in hydrocarbons having more than two carbon atoms and a depleted stream of hydrocarbons having more than two carbon atoms; (c) liquefying of gaseous stream depleted in hydrocarbons having more than two carbon atoms from step (b); and (d) storage, optionally after expansion, of the stream from step (c) in storage means followed by possible loading in transport means, at least portion of heat source required for synthesis gas production process is produced by at least a portion of steam generated in step (d) by expansion of natural gas from step (c) before storage and/or by steam generated in storage means of liquefied natural gas and/or by steam generated during loading of liquefied natural gas in transport means.
   USE - The process is useful for liquefying natural gas in combination with a process for producing synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: utilizes the storage pressure that is greater than the pressure of the fuel network, possible to do without compressors/rotating machinery, thus representing a significant saving on the cost of the liquefaction unit of natural gas; utilizes the simple of operation; avoids a number of utilities (the amine or demineralized water supplement); is for small-scale natural gas liquefaction units (producing e.g. less than 50000 tonnes of liquefied natural gas per year); and utilizes the pretreatment of natural gas that is provided by an adsorption process, providing the regeneration gas returned to the fuel network that represents 15% of the fuel balance and utilizes the heavy hydrocarbons extracted from the natural gas liquefier and the natural gas vapors generated at the liquefied natural gas storage and/or the loading bay will be of less importance in the fuel balance (less than 1%) and this integration drastically reduces the number of dedicated equipment on secondary streams of the natural gas liquefaction unit and avoids an incinerator and/or an expensive heavy hydrocarbon extraction system for small units.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Liquefying natural gas in combination with a process for producing synthesis gas, comprises: (a) pretreatment of a feed natural gas to remove impurities that may freeze during the liquefaction process; (b) extracting, from the gas stream from step (a), of a stream enriched in hydrocarbons having more than two carbon atoms and a depleted stream of hydrocarbons having more than two carbon atoms; (c) liquefying of gaseous stream depleted in hydrocarbons having more than two carbon atoms from step (b); and (d) storage, optionally after expansion, of the stream from step (c) in a storage means followed by a possible loading in a transport means, where the process for producing synthesis gas comprises (a1) desulfurization at a temperature above 350 degrees C of a natural gas feed stream, (b1) optional pre-reforming, at a temperature above 500 degrees C to convert the hydrocarbon chains containing at least two carbon atoms of the gas stream from step (a1) to methane, and (c1) reforming consisting in reacting the gaseous stream from step (a1) or (b1) with water vapor to produce hydrogen, carbon dioxide, at a temperature above 800 degrees C carbon and carbon monoxide, at least a portion of the heat source required for the synthesis gas production process is produced by at least a portion of the steam generated in step (d) by expansion of the natural gas from the step (c) before storage and/or by the steam generated in the storage means of the liquefied natural gas and/or by the steam generated during the loading of the liquefied natural gas in the transport means.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The pretreatment step (a) is implemented by means of an adsorption separation system implementing a regeneration current. The step (a) comprises an adsorption pretreatment by means of an adsorption system comprising between two and five containers of at least one adsorbent layer and at least one filtering device, heating and/or cooling an adsorption and/or regeneration current circulating in the adsorption system. The water vapor resulting from the synthesis gas production process is used to heat the regeneration stream. During step (a1), all the sulfur derivatives contained in the feed gas are converted into hydrogen sulfide by catalysis in a reactor. The product hydrogen sulfide is catalytically extracted. The impurities liable to freeze during the liquefaction process removed during step (a) comprise water, carbon dioxide and sulfurous derivatives contained in the gas. During step (c), the stream of hydrocarbon-depleted natural gas having more than two carbon atoms from step (b) is liquefied at a temperature less than -140 degrees C by means of natural gas liquefaction unit comprising at least one main heat exchanger and frigory production system. The natural gas feed stream implemented in step (a) and the natural gas feed stream implemented in step (a1) originate from of the same natural gas supply stream. The synthesis gas production unit is a steam reforming hydrogen production unit having a hydrogen production capacity of at least 20000 Nm3/h. The process comprises 5-35%, preferably 10-20% of the quantity of fuel of the heat source necessary for the synthesis gas production process is produced by at least a portion of the generated steam during step (d) by an expansion of the natural gas from step (c) before storage and/or by the steam generated in the storage means of the liquefied natural gas and/or by the steam generated during the charging of the liquefied natural gas in the means of transport.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   USE - Chemical oxygen demand electrochemical rapid                online monitoring method.
   ADVANTAGE - The chemical oxygen demand electrochemical                rapid online monitoring method has realized online                monitoring instrument.

            sample cell prepared by an insulating material                having a volume of 50-100 ml, placing a magnetic                stirrer having a length of less than 2 cm at the                bottom of the sample cell, and placing on a                magnetic stirrer to complete the agitation task                during sample determination, connecting the working                electrode to a solar cell, which supplies current                having a voltage value of 2.5 V, and completing a                daily charging of the solar cell by a solar panel,                connecting the sample inlet of the sample cell to a                peristaltic pump by a catheter to complete an                injection work during the online monitoring of the                sample, connecting output end of the working                electrode to a current conversion chemical oxygen                demand chip, calculating and measuring the current                consumption during the measurement of the working                electrode are completed and providing the current                required for the operation of the current                conversion chemical oxygen demand chip by the solar                battery, transmitting a chemical oxygen demand data                obtained by the current conversion chemical oxygen                demand chip to an user computer through a wireless                transmission transmitting end, receiving by the                wireless transmission receiving end to the user                computer, and providing the wireless transmission                transmitting end with the current required for                operation by the solar battery, adopting                nano-modified silica as a carrier by the working                electrode, using a multi-carbon waste in petroleum                processing as a raw material, preparing and                modifying graphene by a thermal cracking method,                and forming by compounding with a silica                nano-modified material carrier, pumping sewage into                an oxidation tank through a peristaltic pump with a                flow rate of 20 mL/min, generating a hydroxide                hydroxyl radical by an electrochemical working                electrode completely oxidizes an organic matter in                the sewage into water and carbon dioxide in the                range of 1-5000 Mg/L, where the working voltage of                the working electrode is 2.4 V, and a working                current is 15 mu A, an oxidation time 5-30 minutes,                a power supply is 6 V, 1.6 A, 10 W, and converting                the oxidation current value into a chemical oxygen                demand value by a current-converting chemical                oxygen demand chip, converting the current value                into a chemical oxygen demand value by a                correspondence between the current of the complete                oxidation process and the oxygen consumption. PT P
PN CN110012921-A
TI Method for preparing soft bun, involves preparing high gluten flour, low gluten flour, whipped cream, butter, edible salt, yeast, honey, milk, micro-bubble water and eggs followed by removing eggshell and putting in suitable container.
AU YE Y
AE HORSH FUJIAN FOOD CO LTD (HORS-Non-standard)
GA 201964166Q
AB    NOVELTY - A soft bun preparing method involves preparing high-gluten flour, low-gluten flour, whipped cream, butter, edible salt, yeast, honey, milk, micro-bubble water and eggs, removing the eggshell and putting it in a container. Honey, milk, and micro-bubble water are added to the container and mixed. The dough is mixed, the high-gluten flour and the low-gluten flour are stirred and the mixed liquid is added, and the micro-bubbles and the yeast are added. The foundation is proofed, the dough is placed in the proofing room of the proofing machine and fermented. The dough is placed into the dough mixer and the surface is vented and placed in the proofing room. A small dough is formed into a small bread using a molding machine and placed in the proofing room. The fermented bread is placed in a rotary furnace and baked. The bread is cooled, sent to the packaging workshop and packaged. Ozone sterilization is required, the production date is printed and the packaging is completed.
   USE - Method for preparing soft bun.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing oxygen for basic proofing, improving the efficiency of basic proofing, efficiency of bread making, quality of proofing and quality of the finished product, ensuring bactericidal effect when it is noodles and reducing the bacteria in the bread.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A soft bun preparing method involves preparing 36 % high-gluten flour, 4 % low-gluten flour, 16 % whipped cream, 4 % butter, 0.4 % edible salt, 0.6 % yeast, 7 % honey, 6 % milk, 16 % micro-bubble water and 10 % eggs, removing the eggshell and put it in a suitable container for use, after cleaning and disinfecting the eggs. Honey, milk, and micro-bubble water are added to the container and mixed well. The dough is mixed, the high-gluten flour and the low-gluten flour are evenly stirred and the mixed liquid is gradually added under the stirring condition, in the process, the micro-bubbles play a certain bactericidal effect on the raw material, and at the same time the dough is rich in oxygen-enriched air. The yeast is added and mixed again, after the mixture is added. The foundation is proofed, the dough is placed in the proofing room of the proofing machine and the fermentation is carried out under the conditions of a temperature of 27 degrees C and a relative humidity of 75 % and the oxygen-enriched air in the dough promotes the fermentation. The dough is placed into the dough mixer in a low pressure environment and the surface is vented. The carbon dioxide gas generated during the fermentation process is discharged, the low pressure promoted the discharge of the gas, and the dough is divided into small pieces for use. The small dough is put into the proofing room of the proofing machine for fermentation, producing a new gas into the dough, restoring the softness and extensibility of the dough and forming it. A small dough is formed into a small bread using a molding machine. The small bread is placed in the proofing room of the proofing machine, fermented at a temperature of 35-38 degrees C and a relative humidity of 80-85 % till the small bread is twice the size. The bread is placed in a high pressure environment with a certain pressure and taken out after five minutes, during which the internal gas becomes softer during the shrinking process. The fermented bread is placed in a rotary furnace and baked at a temperature of 185- 210 degrees C for 10 minutes. The bread is cooled after baking, sent to the packaging workshop after cooling and packaged by automatic packaging machine. Ozone sterilization is required for 10 minutes before packaging, the packaging temperature is controlled at 25 degrees C during the packaging, the production date is printed and the packaging is completed.
DC D11 (Baking - including bakery products, flour, doughs, bakery ovens, dough transporting and/or handling equipment, pies and pasta, but not flour milling (A21).)
MC D01-A04; D01-B02A
IP A21D-013/06; A21D-002/02; A21D-002/34

AU ZHANG Y

   ZHANG W

   WANG L
AE SHAANXI PETROLEUM & CHEM IND RES DESIGN (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 201946863H
AB    NOVELTY - A composite forward osmosis membrane preparing method involves mixing polyvinyl alcohol and polyethersulfone to obtain a casting solution, pouring the casting solution into a mold, solidifying and removing the mold to obtain polyethersulfone base film, adding dopamine to tris-HCl buffer to obtain a dopamine buffer, mixing sodium chloride and distilled water to obtain a polycationic electrolyte solution, mixing sodium chloride and distilled water to obtain a polyanionic electrolyte solution, mixing toluene and distilled water to obtain an aqueous phase monomer solution, mixing trimesic acid chloride and toluene to obtain an oil phase monomer solution, reacting polyethersulfone-based membrane, and immersing in deionized water to obtain a polyethersulfone-cationic polymer/anionic polymer support layer base film, interfacially polymerizing the polyethersulfone-cationic polymer/anionic polymer support layer base film and placing the base film in an oven to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing composite forward osmosis membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing composite forward osmosis membrane with high mechanical strength, better hydrophilicity, strong anti-pollution ability, high pure water flux and high stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A composite forward osmosis membrane preparing method involves mixing 1.5-5 mass% polyvinyl alcohol, 13.5-20 mass% polyethersulfone and 75-85 mass% N-methylpyrrolidone under nitrogen atmosphere and heating to 50-70 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, dissolving polyethersulfone, and stirring at a constant temperature for 1.5-3.5 hours and allowing to stand at room temperature for 24-48 hours to obtain a casting solution, pouring the casting solution into a mold which has been preheated to 50-70 degrees C, and cooling at room temperature, solidifying the casting liquid and phase-separating, removing the mold and extracting in water at 10-50 degrees C for 10-30 minutes to obtain polyethersulfone base film, adding dopamine to tris-HCl buffer at pH 8-9 to prepare a dopamine buffer with a concentration of 0.2-0.6%, coating it on a polyethersulfone-based membrane for a coating time for 2-4 hours to obtain a polyethersulfone-based film loaded with a polydopamine layer, mixing 0.05-0.2 mass% polycationic electrolyte, 0.05-0.2 mass% sodium chloride and 99.6-99.9 mass% distilled water, adjusting the pH of the solution to 10.6 plus minus 1 with a 0.5 mol/L sodium hydroxide aqueous solution to obtain a polycationic electrolyte solution, mixing 0.05-0.2 mass% polyanionic electrolyte, 0.05-0.2 mass% sodium chloride and 99.6-99.9 mass% distilled water, and then adjusting the pH of the solution to 3.51 with 0.5 mol/L sodium hydroxide aqueous solution to obtain a polyanionic electrolyte solution, mixing 0.25-2 mass% m-phenylenediamine, 0.5-2.5 mass% toluene and 95.5-99.2 mass% distilled water to obtain an aqueous phase monomer solution, mixing 0.05-0.2% trimesic acid chloride and 99.8-99.95% toluene to obtain an oil phase monomer solution, reacting the polyethersulfone-based membrane loaded with polydopamine layer in a polycationic electrolyte solution for 15-20 minutes, and immersing in deionized water for 5-8 minutes, placing in a polyanionic electrolyte solution for 10-15 minutes, and immersing in deionized water for 5-8 minutes to obtain a polyethersulfone-cationic polymer/anionic polymer support layer base film, interfacially polymerizing the polyethersulfone-cationic polymer/anionic polymer support layer base film on the surface of the smooth glass, buckling with a mold, pouring an aqueous phase monomer solution into the mold, and carrying out the interface polymerization for 1-5 minutes, and blowing the base film to the surface of the membrane with nitrogen or carbon dioxide without liquid, and pouring into the oil phase monomer solution, and carrying out the interface polymerization reaction for 0.5-2 minutes, pouring out an excess of the oil phase monomer solution, placing the base film in an oven at 50-100 degrees C and performing heat treatment for 10-60 minutes to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyanionic electrolyte comprises polyacrylic acid, sodium alginate and sodium polystyrene sulfonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polycationic electrolyte comprises polyethyleneimine, polyacrylamine hydrochloride, chitosan and polydiallyldimethylammonium chloride.

PN CN109293136-A
TI Treating rural domestic sewage comprises e.g. adding sewage to pool, adding treatment agent to sewage, stirring, soaking, exposing, heating, adding maize straw powder, culturing and pouring waste water to hollow lotus seed cultivation field.
AU MU X
   CHEN D
   WANG G
AE GUIZHOU TAIJIN AGRIC TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2019146983
AB    NOVELTY - Treating rural domestic sewage comprises (a) adding sewage into a pool, adding a treatment agent to the sewage according to a mass ratio of 100:9, stirring at 90-110 revolutions/minute for 50-70 minutes, soaking for 3-5 days and exposing again for 2-4 days; (b) heating wastewater treated in the step (a) to 30-35 degrees C, adding maize straw powder with granularity of 0.3-0.6 cm according to the mass ratio of 100:0.3, stirring for 30-50 minutes after adding the mixed bacteria liquid, stirring for 50 minutes for every 4 hours, culturing for 3-5 days at normal temperature; and (c) pouring waste water stream treated in the previous step into a hollow lotus seed cultivation field, is absorbed by the hollow lotus seed grass for 1-2 weeks, where the planting density of hollow lotus seeds is 80-300 plants/m2 and plant height is not less than 25 cm.
   USE - The method is useful for treating rural domestic sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes removal of contaminants e.g. nitrogen and phosphorus causing eutrophication of water, can inhibit pathogenic microorganisms in water, utilizing antibacterial raw material can not only adsorb the fine particles in the water, but also provides a comprehensive mineral component to promote the growth of the probiotic flora, optimizes the water environment, promotes the growth of the hollow lotus seed grass, and improves sewage purification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating rural domestic sewage comprises (a) adding sewage into a pool, adding a treatment agent to the sewage according to a mass ratio of 100:9, stirring at 90-110 revolutions/minute for 50-70 minutes, soaking for 3-5 days and exposing again for 2-4 days where the treatment agent is prepared by uniformly mixing 10-20 pts. wt. perlite, 10-20 pts. wt. medical stone, 30-50 pts. wt. vermiculite, crushing into 0.3-0.6 cm particles, placing particles into a container, keeping the air pressure in the container is 0.1-0.3 times of standard atmospheric pressure, heating to 700-800 degrees C by microwave, keeping the temperature for 20-30 minutes, stopping heating, the particle drop temperature to 30-40 degrees C, filling carbon dioxide into the container until the container pressure is 1.5-2 times of standard atmospheric pressure, sealing the container, allowing to stand for 30-50 minutes , reducing the temperature to 3-5 degrees C, introducing 10-20 pts. wt. 100-120 degrees C water vapor into container, sealing the container for 50-80 minutes to obtain mixture A, and crushing 50-80 pts. wt. garlic straw, 30-50 pts. wt. leaf of Chinese ilex, 30-50 pts. wt. Perilla and 100-200 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum straw mixing into powder of 0.5-1.2 cm to obtain mixture B and mixing mixture A and mixture B at a mass ratio of 3:13; (b) heating wastewater treated in the step (a) to 30-35 degrees C, adding maize straw powder with granularity of 0.3-0.6 cm according to the mass ratio of 100:0.3, stirring for 30-50 minutes after adding the mixed bacteria liquid, stirring for 50 minutes for every 4 hours, culturing for 3-5 days at normal temperature, where the mixed bacteria liquid is Saccharomycetes, photosynthetic bacteria, Lactobacillus acidophilus, sulfurized bacteria and fecal cocci in weight ratio of 3:4:2:6:1; and (c) pouring waste water stream treated in the previous step into a hollow lotus seed cultivation field, is absorbed by the hollow lotus seed grass for 1-2 weeks, where the planting density of hollow lotus seeds is 80-300 plants/m2 and plant height is not less than 25 cm.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN CN108850517-A
TI Leuconostoc mesenteroides feed additive useful for promoting animal metabolism, prepared by mixing e.g. Leuconostoc mesenteroides, seabuckthorn freeze-dried powder, Rosa roxburghii freeze-dried powder and mixing carbonized eggshell powder.
AU ZHANG K
   ZHANG Z
AE JIANGSU QIANYAOTANG NAT PHYSICIAN CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018959750
AB    NOVELTY - Leuconostoc mesenteroides feed additive (1000 g) is prepared by mixing 30000-150000 billion, 50000-150000 billion, 70000-150000 or 90000-150000 billion L.mesenteroides, 5-50 g, 10-50 g, 15-50 g or 20-50 g seabuckthorn freeze-dried powder, 5-50 g, 8-50 g, 12-50 g or 15-50 g Rosa roxburghii freeze-dried powder, 3-30 g, 5-30 g, 8-30 g or 10-30 g Chinese lantern freeze-dried powder, 3-30 g, 5-30 g, 8-30 g or 10-30 g blackcurrant freeze-dried powder and 50-150 g, 70-150 g, 85-150 g or 100-150 g cactus freeze-dried powder, adding remainder of carbonized eggshell powder, mixing uniformly and carrying out complex packaging.
   USE - The L.mesenteroides feed additive is useful for promoting animal metabolism and improving disease resistance.
   ADVANTAGE - The feed additive improves stability, nutrition and adsorption of bacteria, and promotes animal growth.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Leuconostoc mesenteroides feed additive (1000 g) is prepared by mixing 30000-150000 billion, 50000-150000 billion, 70000-150000 or 90000-150000 billion L.mesenteroides, 5-50 g, 10-50 g, 15-50 g or 20-50 g seabuckthorn freeze-dried powder, 5-50 g, 8-50 g, 12-50 g or 15-50 g Rosa roxburghii freeze-dried powder, 3-30 g, 5-30 g, 8-30 g or 10-30 g Chinese lantern freeze-dried powder, 3-30 g, 5-30 g, 8-30 g or 10-30 g blackcurrant freeze-dried powder and 50-150 g, 70-150 g, 85-150 g or 100-150 g cactus freeze-dried powder, adding remainder of carbonized eggshell powder, mixing uniformly and carrying out complex packaging. The seabuckthorn freeze-dried powder, R.roxburghii freeze-dried powder, Chinese lantern freeze-dried powder, blackcurrant freeze-dried powder and cactus freeze-dried powder are prepared by taking fresh seabuckthorn, R.roxburghii, Chinese lantern, blackcurrant and cactus, removing fruit stems, rot fruits and other impurities, rinsing, freezing by rapidly cooling material to less than -45 degrees C, carrying out sublimation drying at -22 to -32 degrees C and 120-150 pa until the moisture content is reaches to 6 weight/weight%, crushing at low temperature, peeling and removing seed. The rinsing is performed by adding 0.1 wt.% citric acid in 40 degrees C water, rinsing material for 15 minutes, stirring constantly, removing and rinsing in pure water for 3 times. The carbonized eggshell powder is prepared by (a) cleaning the eggshell, draining the water, drying for 1-2 hours, crushing into 20-30 mesh coarse powder, spraying prepared 25 wt.% salt (sodium chloride) solution on eggshell coarse powder in a weight ratio of 1:0.5, mixing while spraying, leaving for 6-7 hours, evenly mixing once and leaving for 6-7 hours to allow the salt to effectively penetrate into inner space of the eggshell tissue, (b) placing the impregnated eggshell powder raw material in a rotary carbonization pot, and carbonizing at 420-450 degrees C for 70-80 minutes to observe that raw material is completely blackened, (c) quickly transferring the carbonized raw material to activation furnace, closing the furnace door, introducing carbon dioxide gas, exhausting air and performing anodic activation at 720-800 degrees C for 55-85 minutes, (d) cooling the activated material to room temperature, rinsing the activated material in washing tank with water to remove sodium chloride, soaking the material in 12 %mass hydrochloric acid for 2.5-3 hours, washing with 95 degrees C pure water until the particles are no longer white, leaving at room temperature, neutralizing material by rinsing with water, filtering, and rinsing activated material and (e) drying the rinsed activated material at 115-120 degrees C until the moisture content is less than 6 weight/weight%, passing through 60 mesh sieve, crushing the sieved coarse particles and passing through 60 mesh sieve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Microorganism: The L.mesenteroides is a dry powder product containing not less than 250 billion live bacteria/gram. Preferred Method: The low temperature crushing is performed by crushing material when the material temperature is below 0 degrees C. The complex packaging adopts inner and outer bag complex packaging, the inner bag is made of polypropylene plastic bag, the outer package is made of aluminum foil bag, and the inner and outer bags are filled with a silica gel desiccant bag. The drying is performed by hot air drying or far infrared drying at 180-300 degrees C. Preferred Components: The eggshell is one or more of chicken eggshell, duck eggshell, goose eggshell and quail eggshell.
   ACTIVITY - Metabolic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. TI Spray useful in petrochemicals, textile, cement, rubber, vaccine, electrical and electronics, blood sachets, alcohol, food industry and agro-products, comprises chemical solution filled in polypropylene plastic spray container.
AU UMADEVI U
   UMAKANTHAN T
AE U U (UUUU-Individual)
   T U (TUUU-Individual)
GA 2018411710
AB    NOVELTY - Spray (universal potentiator) comprises chemical solution filled in polypropylene plastic spray container having dimensions of 45x 26x 29 mm size, 20 ml volume and more, where the container has ejection pressure of 2500-5000 K Pascal, and ejection volume of 0.02-0.18 ml.
   USE - The spray is useful in petrochemicals, liquefied petroleum gas, traditional lamps using kerosene, textile (for fabric production), cement, rubber, vaccine (human and veterinary medicine), electrical and electronics (cell phone, computers, laptops, television and radio), blood (for blood transfusion) sachets, tobacco and allied products, alcohol (hangover reduction), agro-products, edible oil, therapeutics, drugs, pharmaceuticals, cosmetics, and food industry (for preparing foodstuff e.g. snacks, drinks, meat product, raw and cooked eggs, vegetables, fruits, food grains, pulses, cereals, flour items, milk and milk products, soft drinks and dried and moist foodstuff and used as food preservative, additive and chemical residues) (claimed).

The blood sachet has extended shelf-life of 1-2 years, and reduces wastage of banked blood due to its natural short expiry (4-6 weeks). The tobacco and allied products are non-toxic, smooth and soft to inhale without annoying and affecting passive, non-smokers and children. The spray vanishes mouth and finger odor of the tobacco and allied products within 5-10 minutes after usage, and no discoloration in lips and fingers. The spray does preserve satiety of smoking and reduces upto 75% nicotine in cigarette simultaneously. The alcohol is cost-effective and reduces hangover of 100% and shivering, and the consumer can feel the enhanced taste. The spray when included in agroproducts improves yield, reduces agrochemical requirement upto 65%, and agrochemical residue, and improves soil fertility. The edible oil can be reused for 4 times and remains same consistency, avoids oil borne diseases, and improves aroma, texture and shelf life of recipe. The therapeutics cures most of the diseases in 7 seconds to 30 days, and is cost-effective. The spray when included in drugs, pharmaceuticals and cosmetics, increases its potency of 3-153%, and is cost-effective, enhanced shelf life, reduces drug resistance and drug quantity requirement, and does not cause drug adverse reactions even upon spraying into mouth, ears, eyes, skin of humans, animals and plants. In food industry, easily prepares perishable foodstuff with extended shelf life of 6 months to 3 years, taste and aroma and increases denatured nutritive value.

PN CN107736542-A
TI Tablet comprises blueberry anthocyanin extract powder, calcium powder, seaweed powder, peppermint oil, Scaphium scaphigerum powder, Chrysanthemum powder, Litchi juice, soy protein solution, and water.
AU QI H
AE GUANGXI NANNING GUIERCHUANG ENVIRONMENTA (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201818396K
AB    NOVELTY - Tablet comprises 40-60 pts. wt. blueberry anthocyanin extract powder, 6-10 pts. wt. calcium powder, 15-25 pts. wt. seaweed powder, 5-15 pts. wt. peppermint oil, 1-5 pts. wt. Scaphium scaphigerum powder, 1-5 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum powder, 1-5 pts. wt. Litchi juice, 50-70 pts. wt. soy protein solution, and water (suitable quantity).
   USE - As blueberry anthocyanin extract containing tablet.
   ADVANTAGE - The tablet is prepared in a cost-effective manner, has sweet, sour and refreshing taste, exhibits eye-catching effect, and provides balanced nutrition with a reasonable mix of nutrients. The tablet is rich in anthocyanin, flavonoid, vitamin and microelement contents, produces a large amount of carbon dioxide gas and rapidly dissolves into water. The tablet has rapid onset of action and high bioavailability, and is convenient to carry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the tablet, which involves (a) taking 60-80 pts. wt. high-quality rice, washing with clean water, adding water to soak the rice until hard core removes, putting the soaked rice into the rice cooker and cooking the rice until the cooked rice is not sticky, dispensing the cooked rice into container, inoculating with Aspergillus oryzae and mixing uniformly, culturing at a temperature of 35 degrees C with humidity of 90%, and fermenting for 4-6 days, (b) taking the fermented rice into the container, adding 10-20% phosphate buffer solution, homogenizing for 3 minutes, centrifuging at 4200 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 5 minutes, and taking supernatant, (c) washing fresh blueberry fruit, freezing the blueberry fruits with liquid nitrogen, crushing the fruits quickly, adding 5-10 %mass obtained supernatant and mixing uniformly, freezing for 10-30 minutes, and drying using freeze dryer at a temperature of -80 degrees C to obtain blueberry dry powder, (d) mixing the obtained blueberry dry powder and acetic acid solution in a ratio 1 (g):10-30 (ml), adjusting pH to 2-4 using citric acid, performing microwave processing at 40-60 degrees C at a power of 420w for 20 seconds, extracting for 60-130 minutes to obtain anthocyanin crude extract, (e) adding 5-8 vol.% citric acid and 5-8 vol.% obtained supernatant in the obtained anthocyanin crude extract, extracting 3 times using ethyl acetate, adding non-ester layer to D101 macroporous resin column, primary washing with ultra-pure water, eluting using ethanol water system, collecting 50% ethanol eluate, concentrating under reduced pressure, freeze-drying to obtain the purified anthocyanin powder, (f) mixing 40-60 pts. wt. blueberry anthocyanin extract powder with calcium powder, seaweed powder, peppermint oil, S.scaphigerum powder, Litchi juice, 1-5 pts. wt. medlar powder, and 16-32 pts. wt. potassium bicarbonate powder, adding 5% anhydrous ethanol to wet soft material, drying at a temperature of 40-50 degrees C in a blow dryer for 2-3 hours, and sieving through 100 mesh sieve to obtain the granules, (g) mixing soy protein solution with the granules uniformly and stirring at 500 rpm, adding 10-20 pts. wt. transglutaminase, and quickly vortexing to make the mixture evenly dispersed, mixing the obtained mixture at 900 rpm at 40 degrees C using magnetic stirrer for 120-150 minutes, centrifuging at 500 rpm for 1 minute, removing the oil phase and washing with saline for 2 times, and centrifuging at 500 rpm for 1 minute to collect microcapsule particles, (h) dissolving polyethylene glycol at 70-75 degrees C, adding anhydrous citric acid powder and mixing uniformly, cooling and grinding the mixture and sieving through 80 mesh sieve to obtain acid source, and (i) mixing the obtained microcapsule particles with the acid source in a ratio of 1:1 uniformly, adding 2-3 %mass L-leucine and 3-4 %mass polyethylene glycol 6000 powder, and mixing them in a rotary tablet machine to obtain tablet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves inoculating 2-4% A.oryzae in cooked rice. The step (c) involves using the liquid nitrogen temperature at a temperature of -30 to -50 degrees C having flow rate of 4-5 m/s. Preferred Components: The pH of the phosphate buffer solution is 6.5-7.5. The acetic acid concentration is 3-4 mol/l. The water content in the blueberry dry powder is 5% or less. The citric acid has a pH of 3-5 and a concentration of 0.3-0.5 g/ml. The ratio of the calcium powder to the potassium bicarbonate powder is (20-25):80.

      CN101292736-A   NORTH GREEN FOOD CO LTD (NGRE-Non-standard)   JIN L

TI System for capturing and utilizing carbon dioxide            of gases from plants, comprises carbon dioxide capture            unit, flow controllers, and automated control system            which determine parameters of gas through the system            gas flow inlet channel.
AU MUKHERJEE A
AE DASTUR ENERGY INC (DAST-Non-standard)
   DASTUR ENERGY INC (DAST-Non-standard)
GA 2022436334
AB    NOVELTY - A system for capturing and utilizing carbon                dioxide (CO2) of one or more gases from one or more                plants, comprises a system gas flow inlet channel                which receives one or more gases from one or more                plants, where one or more gases comprise at least                carbon monoxide (CO) and water vapor; a plug flow                reactor (PFR) which receives a first fraction of                one or more gases and convert one or more gases to                CO2 and hydrogen (H2); a bypass channel which                receives a second fraction of one or more gases so                that the second fraction of one or more gases                bypasses the PFR and combines the second fraction                of one or more gases with the CO2 and H2 from the                PFR to form CO2 and syngas; a CO2 capture unit                which receives the CO2 and syngas and separate the                CO2 from the syngas; one or more flow controllers                which modulate the fractions; and an automated                control system (ACS) which determine one or more                parameters of at least one gas of the gases through                the system gas flow inlet channel.
   USE - A system for capturing and utilizing CO2 of                one or more gases from one or more plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses optimization algorithms to                determine the system configuration to attain                optimal CO2 emission goals, while ensuring the                maximum economic return. The system optimization                algorithms may maximize the economic value and may                be used to determine tailor-made energy-efficient,                sustainable, and profitable carbon capture                solutions for integrated steel plants (ISPs).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system for capturing and utilizing carbon                dioxide (CO2) of one or more gases from one or more                plants, comprises a system gas flow inlet channel                which receives one or more gases from one or more                plants, where one or more gases comprise at least                carbon monoxide (CO) and water vapor; a plug flow                reactor (PFR) which receives a first fraction of                one or more gases and convert one or more gases to                CO2 and hydrogen (H2); a bypass channel which                receives a second fraction of one or more gases so                that the second fraction of one or more gases                bypasses the PFR and combines the second fraction                of one or more gases with the CO2 and H2 from the                PFR to form CO2 and syngas; a CO2 capture unit                which receives the CO2 and syngas and separate the                CO2 from the syngas for compression, distribution,                storage, and other uses of the CO2; one or more                flow controllers which modulate the first fraction                and the second fraction; and an automated control                system (ACS) which determine one or more parameters                of at least one gas of the gases through the system                gas flow inlet channel, the first fraction of one                or more gases, the second fraction of one or more                gases, the CO2 flowing into the CO2 capture unit,                or the syngas flowing into the CO2 capture unit,                and command the one or more flow controllers to                modulate at least one of first fraction or the                second fraction based on the one or more                parameters.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) ACS for capturing and utilizing CO2 of one                or more gases from one or more plants, comprising                multiple sensors; a memory (104); and a processor                configured to receive, from at least one sensor,                one or more parameters of at least one gas through                a system gas flow inlet channel, a first volumetric                flow rate of one or more gases through PFR, a                second volumetric flow rate of one or more gases                through a bypass channel that bypasses the PFR, CO2                flowing into a CO2 capture unit, or syngas flowing                into the CO2 capture unit, and command one or more                flow controllers to modulate at least one of the                first volumetric flow rate of one or more gases                through PFR or the second volumetric flow rate of                one or more gases through the bypass channel based                on the one or more parameters; and
   (2) method implemented by an automatic control                system (ACS) for capturing and utilizing CO2 of one                or more gases from one or more plants, which                involves receiving, from at least one sensor, one                or more parameters of at least one gas of one or                more gases through a system gas flow inlet channel,                a first volumetric flow rate of one or more gases                through PFR, a second volumetric flow rate of the                one or more gases through a bypass channel that                bypasses the PFR, CO2 flowing into a CO2 capture                unit, or syngas flowing into the CO2 capture unit,                and commanding one or more flow controllers to                modulate at least one of the first volumetric flow                rate of one or more gases through PFR or second                volumetric flow rate of one or more gases through                the bypass channel based on one or more                parameters.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                data processing components used to implement data                processing carbon capture, utilization, and storage                (CCUS) methods.
   Memory (104)
   Operating system (106)
   System design optimization model (108)
   Network (118)
   Processing control interface (120)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Process: The ACS commands the one                  or more flow controllers to modulate at least one                  of the first fraction or second fraction based on                  at least one of a predetermined amount of CO2 or                  a predetermined amount of syngas. The ACS further                  commands the one or more flow controllers to                  modulate the first fraction and second fraction                  based on a cost model that utilizes a cost                  function to derive a cost based on a total system                  cost loss. The CO2 separated by CO2 capture unit                  is compressed and transported for at least one of                  CO2 storage, enhanced oil recovery (EOR),                  methanol, polycarbonates, concrete curing,                  mineral aggregates, or beverage                  carbonation.Preferred Devices: The PFR comprises at                  least one water gas shift reactor (WGSR). The one                  or more plants comprise at least one of a blast                  furnace, a basic oxygen furnace, or a coke oven.                  The system further comprises one or more sensors,                  where at least one sensor measures at least one                  parameter of one or more parameters associated                  with at least one of the system gas flow inlet                  channel, the PFR, the bypass channel, one or more                  flow controllers, or CO2 capture unit; and where                  determining one or more parameters comprises                  receiving, from at least one sensor, at least one                  parameter of the one or more parameters.Preferred Parameters: The one or more                  parameters comprises at least one of flow rate,                  temperature, pressure, fuel utilization, or                  chemical composition. At least one of the first                  fraction or the second fraction comprises a                  volumetric flow rate of one or more gases.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The one or more gases                  comprise at least one of blast furnace gas (BFG),                  basic oxygen furnace gas (BOFG), or coke oven gas                  (COG). The syngas comprises at least one of rich                  carbon monoxide (CO) and H2 or nitrogen gas (N2).                  The syngas is separated by CO2 capture unit to                  produce at least one of methanol, ethanol,                  hydrogen, ammonia, urea, or power.

TI Apparatus for phototrophic organism wastewater treatment process comprises inlet flow path, first stage pre-treatment system, containing tank, rotatable log assembly, air supply and outlet.
AU FAGAN J D
AE FAGAN J D (FAGA-Individual)
   FAGAN J D (FAGA-Individual)
GA 2021C4820T
AB    NOVELTY - A phototrophic organism wastewater treatment apparatus (1) comprises inlet flow path (2) for receiving and pre-treatment of wastewater from a source; first stage pre-treatment system comprising primary clarification device (3), equalization and pre-conditioning tank (4), and pre-conditioning chemical feed (5); greater than or equal to 1 containing tank (60) for containing wastewater received through inlet flow path and first stage pre-treatment system; division of greater than or equal to 1 containing tank into four compartments (6, 7, 8, 9); rotatable log assembly with extended surface area of longitudinal fins; air supply arranged within greater than or equal to 1 containing tank compartments and having outlets directed at a corresponding one of rotatable log assembly to rotate rotatable log assembly within the wastewater; and outlet for discharge of effluent treated by treatment system after contact with the phototrophic bacteria and algae attached to rotatable log assembly.
   USE - An apparatus for phototrophic organism wastewater treatment process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus allows the treatment of wastewater with minimized electrical energy input; minimizes wastewater treatment carbon footprint and related environmental impact; the treatment of wastewater with minimal chemical addition; maximizes light availability to the phototrophic organisms; optimizes light/dark sequencing for best performance; the excess biomass to freely move within the reactors; the rotation of the logs to move the excess biomass to settling zones for effective removal; wastewater treatment with no mains electricity supply; optimizes performance with artificial light where necessary; production of treated wastewater suitable for a range of reuse applications; and maximizes chemical energy within the waste solids for energy recovery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A phototrophic organism wastewater treatment apparatus (1) comprises inlet flow path (2) for receiving and pre-treatment of wastewater from a source; first stage pre-treatment system comprising primary clarification device (3), equalization and pre-conditioning tank (4), and pre-conditioning chemical feed (5); greater than or equal to 1 containing tank (60) for containing wastewater received through inlet flow path and first stage pre-treatment system; division of greater than or equal to 1 containing tank into four compartments (6, 7, 8, 9); rotatable log assembly with extended surface area of longitudinal fins, angled in such a manner as to trap air to facilitate buoyant rotation, and circumferential fins, mounted in greater than or equal to 1 containing tank for rotation within the wastewater and each containing a phototrophic heterotrophic bacterial colony capable of digesting organic carbon in the wastewater and respiring carbon dioxide (CO2), and an algal colony that uses the carbon dioxide (CO2) and respires oxygen (O2) and each rotatable tube comprising surfaces for supporting phototrophic bacteria and algal growth, where the surfaces arranged to be alternately immersed into and out of the wastewater and alternatively exposed and not exposed to a light source of either or both of sunlight or artificial light; air supply arranged within greater than or equal to 1 containing tank compartments and having outlets directed at a corresponding one of rotatable log assembly to rotate rotatable log assembly within the wastewater, and is capable of being intermittently operable at a higher air flow rate to agitate the rotatable log assembly and the wastewater, and outlet for discharge of effluent treated by treatment system after contact with the phototrophic bacteria and algae attached to rotatable log assembly. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for phototrophic organism wastewater treatment process which involves receiving and pre-treating wastewater from a source through an inlet flow path, firstly pre-treating through a pre-treatment system, containing wastewater received through inlet flow path and first stage pre-treatment system in greater than or equal to 1 containing tank; dividing greater than or equal to 1 containing tank into four compartments, providing rotatable log assembly with extended surface area of longitudinal fins, angled in such a manner as to trap air to facilitate buoyant rotation, and circumferential fins, mounted in greater than or equal to 1 containing tank for rotation within the wastewater and each containing a phototrophic heterotrophic bacterial colony capable of digesting organic carbon in the wastewater and respiring carbon dioxide (CO2), and algal colony that uses the carbon dioxide (CO2) and respires oxygen (O2) and each rotatable log assembly comprising surfaces for supporting phototrophic bacteria and algal growth, surfaces arranged to be alternately immersed into and out of the wastewater and be alternatively exposed and not exposed to a light source of either or both of sunlight or artificial light; providing an air supply arranged within greater than or equal to 1 containing tank and having outlets directed at a corresponding one of rotatable log assembly to rotate rotatable log assembly within the wastewater, providing an outlet for discharge of effluent treated by treatment system after contact with the phototrophic bacteria and algae attached to the rotatable log assembly.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the treatment system.
   Phototrophic organism wastewater treatment apparatus (1)
   Inlet flow path (2)
   Primary clarification device (3)
   Equalization and pre-conditioning tank (4)
   Pre-conditioning chemical feed (5)
   Compartments (6, 7, 8, 9)
   Containing tank (60)

PN WO2020097256-A1; US2020224121-A1; US10975328-B2; EP3877355-A1; EP3877355-A4
TI Generation, isolation or purification of free            fatty acids from mixed lipid feedstock in manufacture            of e.g. biodiesel, involves acidifying mixture            containing triglyceride-comprising material, and            separating e.g. lipid component from mixture.
AU LONG R
   KILLINGSWORTH N
   BLANCHARD C O
   LONG R A
   BLANCHARD C O A N
AE INVENTURE RENEWABLES INC (INVE-Non-standard)
   INVENTURE RENEWABLES INC (INVE-Non-standard)
   INVENTURE RENEWABLES INC (INVE-Non-standard)
   INVENTURE RENEWABLES INC (INVE-Non-standard)
   INVENTURE INT PTE LTD (INVE-Non-standard)
GA 202042030Y
AB    NOVELTY - Generation, isolation or purification of free                fatty acids from mixed lipid feedstock involves                providing a mixture (m1) containing                triglyceride-comprising material, saponifiable                material, and tall oil, acidifying (or                acidification of) the mixed lipid feedstock to                generate free fatty acids from soaps by providing                an acid or an acid solution or a gas comprising                carbon dioxide or a stack gas, capable of forming                an acid when mixed with water, combining or mixing                the mixture with the acid or acid solution or the                gas, optionally carbon dioxide or a stack gas in                the presence of a solvent, to perform acidulation                reaction and to generate a mixture (a) comprising                free fatty acids, and having a pH of 1-6, in which                the amount of gas is appropriate to increase the                pressure of the reaction mixture optionally in a                reaction vessel, and separating a lipid phase or a                lipid component from the reaction mixture.
   USE - Generation, isolation or purification of free                fatty acids from mixed lipid feedstock in                manufacture of renewable diesel and/or biodiesel                and/or oleochemical (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and efficient                generation, isolation or purification of free fatty                acids from mixed lipid feedstock with high                yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Generation, isolation or purification of free                fatty acids from mixed lipid feedstock involves                providing a mixture (m1) comprising an aqueous                solution or mixture comprising a mixed lipid                feedstock comprising a soapstock, a                triglyceride-comprising material, a saponifiable                material, optionally glyceride or phospholipid, a                tall oil, optionally liquid rosin tall oil, a gum                product, optionally chemically or enzymatically                derived gums product, a crude biodiesel, a fatty                acid optionally from a distillation bottom, and a                fat splitter emulsion, optionally purged from fat                splitter due to accumulation when recycled, or a                mixed lipid feedstock comprising a soapstock, and                wash-water comprising soaps, optionally generated                during chemical refining of crude natural oil, or a                mixed lipid feedstock comprising a mixture of fatty                acid soaps, fatty acids or fatty acid soaps and                fatty acids, optionally generated via fermentation                process or reaction, or a mixed lipid feedstock                comprising a biological source, optionally                including plants (including agricultural products                or waste- products) and microorganisms, such as,                algae (such as kelp) or a yeast, or a mixed lipid                feedstock derived from a biomass, a crude natural                oil, or a plant or an animal source, optionally a                tallow, or a mixed lipid feedstock derived from                enzymatic degumming of edible and inedible oils, or                pretreated mixed lipid feedstock, acidifying the                mixed lipid feedstock to generate free fatty acids                from soaps by providing an acid or an acid solution                or a gas comprising carbon dioxide or a stack gas,                capable of forming an acid when mixed with water,                combining or mixing the mixture with the acid or                acid solution or the gas, optionally carbon dioxide                or a stack gas in the presence of a solvent, in                which the ratio of water-to-total dissolved solids                with respect to mixed lipid feedstock is 100:1 or                less, preferably 10:1 or less, to perform                acidulation reaction and to generate a mixture (a)                comprising free fatty acids, and having a pH of                1-6, in which the amount of gas is appropriate to                increase the pressure of the reaction mixture                optionally in a reaction vessel, in which the                acidulation reaction is performed at 0-5000 psig,                and separating a lipid phase or a lipid component                from the reaction mixture. The lipid phase or the                lipid component is formed by allowing the reaction                products of the acidulation reaction to settle or                partition away from the non-lipid component of the                reaction products of the acidulation reaction. The                separated lipid phase or a lipid component                comprises 0.01-15%, preferably 5% or more unreacted                soaps. The unreacted soaps comprise soaps which are                not acidulated to generate free fatty acids                comprising 0.01-70 wt.%, preferably 10-45 wt.%                lipid phase or lipid component. Once the majority                of fatty acids are removed, the soap-containing                residue is recycled back in the process upstream of                separation so as to re-split certain components                and/or to acidulate the soaps to fatty acids, and                to isolate or purify free fatty acids from a mixed                lipid feedstock. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included                for the following:
   (1) use of generated fatty acid or fatty acid                derivative in manufacture of renewable diesel                and/or biodiesel and/or oleochemical. The fatty                acid or fatty acid derivative is converted to                glyceride and/or its ester prior to the production                of the renewable diesel and/or the biodiesel and/or                oleochemical. The fatty acid or fatty acid                derivative is pretreated to remove impurities;                and
   (2) manufacture of renewable diesel, and/or                biodiesel and/or oleochemical, which involves using                the generated fatty acid or a fatty acid                derivative.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the                generation, isolation or purification of free fatty                acids from mixed lipid feedstock.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The acid or acid                  solution comprises an organic and/or an inorganic                  acid or mineral acid, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric                  acid, formic acid and/or sodium bisulfate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further                  involves performing thermal hydrolysis reaction                  for hydrolyzing the mixed lipid feedstock before                  the acidulation reaction using reagents                  comprising saccharide, capable of generating                  acids, performing lipase reaction for hydrolyzing                  the mixed lipid feedstock before the acidulation                  reaction, performing saponification reaction                  using a saponifiable material comprising                  glycerides and/or phospholipids, under conditions                  of a temperature of 10-600 degrees C, preferably                  75-120 degrees C, and a pressure of 0-2000 psig,                  preferably 3-250 psig, separating the free fatty                  acids from the separated lipid phase or lipid                  component by utilizing an apparatus or device                  capable of separating the free fatty acids from                  the separated lipid phase or lipid component,                  preferably distillation unit, performing                  fractional distillation, crystallization,                  adsorption or chromatography, and recycling                  0.5-30% soap-containing lipid phase generated                  after the recovery of fatty acids from the                  separated lipid phase so as to increase the                  overall conversion of soaps to fatty acids. The                  thermal hydrolysis reaction involves heating and                  pressurizing the aqueous solution or mixture                  comprising the mixed lipid feedstock under                  conditions of a temperature of 10-700 degrees C,                  preferably 50-500 degrees C, and a pressure of                  300-2000 psig, preferably 0-2500 psig for 1                  second to 3000 minutes, preferably 5-200 minutes                  to generate a reaction mixture comprising a free                  fatty acid and/or a soap (fatty acid salt),                  and/or a glyceride. The lipase reaction involves                  heating and pressurizing the aqueous solution or                  mixture comprising the mixed lipid feedstock                  under conditions of a temperature of 10-700                  degrees C, preferably 50-500 degrees C, and a                  pressure of 300-2000 psig, preferably 0-2500 psig                  for 1 second to 24 days, preferably 5 minutes to                  1 day to generate a reaction mixture comprising a                  free fatty acid and/or a soap (fatty acid salt),                  and/or a glyceride. The process further involves                  reacting the fatty acids generated from any                  portion of the process with an alcohol or                  alcohols using organic and/or inorganic catalysts                  used to aid in the formation of fatty acid alkyl                  esters and/or glycerides, and optionally enzymes                  used to aid in the formation of (or to catalyze                  the formation of) fatty acid alkyl esters and/or                  glycerides, to form a reaction mixture comprising                  fatly acid alkyl esters and/or glycerides, under                  the conditions of a temperature of 20-350 degrees                  C, preferably 20-400 degrees C, and a pressure of                  0-3000 psig, preferably 1500-2000 psig,                  esterifying the free fatty acids to generate                  fatty acid alkyl esters and/or glycerides,                  optionally, fatty acid methyl esters or                  tripalmitin, and/or optionally the alcohol                  comprises methanol, ethanol, and separating,                  isolating, and/or purifying the free fatty acids                  and/or the fatty acid alkyl esters into separate                  fractions, by distillation, crystallization or                  chromatography. The mixed lipid feedstock is                  pretreated by reagents comprising salts, bases,                  oxidizers, and/or reductants. The process is                  batch process, semi-batch process and/or                  continuous process. Preferred Composition: The                  glyceride comprises monoacylglycerol (MAG),                  diacylglycerol (DAG), and/or triacylglycerol                  (TAG). The ratio of water-to-total dissolved                  solids with respect to mixed lipid feedstock is                  15:1 or less, preferably 1:1. The ratio of                  solvent-to-total dissolved solids with respect to                  mixed lipid feedstock is 100:1 or less,                  preferably 10:1 or less. The natural oil                  comprises a vegetable oil, preferably soybean                  oil, canola oil, rapeseed oil, com oil, rice oil,                  sunflower oil, peanut oil, sesame oil, palm oil,                  algae oil, Jatropha oil, castor oil, safflower                  oil, and/or grape seed oil. The natural oil or                  crude natural oil composes castor oil, and                  optionally free fatty acid generated is                  ricinoleic acid (12-hydroxy-9-cis-octadecenoie                  acid). The mixed lipid feedstock further                  comprises additional water, a phospholipid and/or                  a non-saponifiable material. The acid or acid                  solution comprises carbonic acid, and optionally                  the carbonic acid is generated by adding carbon                  dioxide to the mixture (a), thereby causing the                  carbon dioxide to react with water in the mixture                  (a) to form carbonic acid. The source of the                  carbon dioxide comprises a stack gas or a flue                  gas, or a gaseous carbon dioxide emitted from an                  industrial process or an oven, a furnace, a                  boiler, a steam generator, a coal fired power                  plant, an ethanol plant, a brewery, or an                  industrial process, where a gaseous waste stream                  comprising carbon dioxide is emitted. The                  compounds produced as byproduct from the                  water-washing of crude biodiesel comprise                  soapstock, monoglyceride, diglyceride,                  triglyceride and/or their fatty acid alkyl ester.                  The lipid layer or lipid phase comprises free                  fatty acids and any non-acidulated soaps, and the                  aqueous layer comprises water, glycerol,                  phosphate salts, bicarbonate, carbonate or other                  equivalent salts, non-saponifiable material,                  optionally waxes and sterols, and dissolved                  carbonic acid. The solvent added to the thermal                  hydrolysis reaction is a polar solvent,                  optionally methanol, or non-polar solvent,                  optionally hexane. The lipid layer or lipid phase                  optionally comprising unreacted soaps is                  transferred to an electrolysis unit comprising a                  hydrogen evolving cathode electrolysis unit, an                  anode comprising mixed metal oxide layer coated                  onto a stable metal substrate, optionally                  titanium, an anolyte comprising sodium or                  potassium sulfate, sodium or potassium nitrate,                  or sodium or potassium chloride, and optionally                  an anode vessel, and a vessel or other suitable                  container comprising cathode comprising titanium                  or Monel (RTM: nickel-copper alloy), optionally a                  cathode vessel, separated by selective filtration                  membrane, optionally polytetrafluoroethylene                  membrane, and the lipid layer or lipid phase or                  component is reacted with an anolyte, such that                  the unreacted soaps generate free fatty acids,                  and optionally the electrolysis step converts 98%                  or more of the unreacted soaps to free fatty                  acids.
