PT P
PN CN110386980-A
TI Preparing monoclonal antibody against aflatoxin-G1 by performing hybridoma cell fusion by mixing myeloma cell with B lymphocyte, screening hybridoma cell, sub-cloning and screening of hybridoma cells, and extracting monoclonal antibody.
AU JIA M
   MENG F
AE HENAN JIANTONG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 201992477K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a monoclonal antibody against aflatoxin-G1 (AFG1) involves (a) culturing myeloma cell, (b) preparing B lymphocytes, (c) performing hybridoma cell fusion, (d) screening fused-hybridoma cell, (e) screening hybridoma cell, (f) sub-cloning and screening the hybridoma cells, and (g) extracting monoclonal antibody.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing monoclonal antibody against AFG1.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares monoclonal antibody in a simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing a monoclonal antibody against aflatoxin-G1 (AFG1) involves (a) culturing myeloma cell, (b) preparing B lymphocytes, (c) performing hybridoma cell fusion, (d) screening fused-hybridoma cell, (e) screening hybridoma cell, (f) sub-cloning and screening the hybridoma cells, and (g) extracting monoclonal antibody. The step (a) is performed by culturing the myeloma cells in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide incubator to adjust cell density, and allowing it leave the culturing when the cell density reaches to 80%. The step (b) is performed by intravenously injecting into mice, injecting 200 ul saline containing antigen into the mice for 2-4 days, removing the spleen of the mouse by surgery, gently grinding the spleen, dispersing the spleen tissue into spleen cells, washing with 3-5 ml of SF medium with a 5 ml pipette, letting above cells flow through sieve to lower culture dish, mixing the obtained cell liquid, and centrifuging to obtain B lymphocytes. The step (c) is performed by mixing the myeloma cells with B lymphocytes, centrifuging mixed cell liquid at 1200 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 5 minutes, discarding the supernatant, resuspending in 30 ml of SF medium, centrifuging at 1200 rpm for 5 minutes, discarding the supernatant, pipetting 1 ml polyethylene glycol (PEG) with a blue tip, and adding it in a dropwise manner to the mixed myeloma cells and B lymphocytes, gently blowing and mixing the mixture, using a blue tip to circle on the liquid surface, allowing it to stand, adding stop solution, gently agitating the cells with a pipette to prevent the cells from sinking to bottom, performing termination reaction, centrifuging the cells at 1200 rpm for 5 minutes, and discarding the supernatant to obtain hybridoma cell mixture. The step (d) is performed by transferring the mixed hybridoma cell mixture to a medium supplemented with hypoxanthine-aminopterin-thymidine (HAT) medium, mixing the mixture, adding to a culture dish, plating into a 96-well plate, placing the plate in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide incubator for culturing, the HAT medium is replaced with hypoxanthine-thymidine (HT) medium to continue the culture, and screening the successfully hybridized hybridoma cells. The step (e) is performed by screening the hybridoma cells after 5-7 days of fusion, observing the growth state of the cells in the microplates under a microscope, and detecting the positive cell lines by ELISA, labeling the positively-high plate sequentially, sampling and detecting titer, specificity, and fork reactivity when the positive cell growth accounts for one quarter -1/3 of the bottom area of the hole, measuring antibody specificity and cross-reactivity based on the titer results, and screening the optimal hybridoma cells. The step (f) is performed by adding screened hybridoma cells to HT medium containing 16% serum, mixing, plating in 96-well plates, adding feeder cells before plating, placing it in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide incubator, performing sub-cloning of hybridoma cell, screening sub-cloned cells within one week, sampling and screening it when a clone has 20-60 cells or more, selecting the supernatant of 100 ul positive, trying to choose a single hole, waiting until the cell rises well, transferring it to 24 wells when the cell grows one quarter -1/3 of the bottom area of the hole, placing the cells in a 24-well plate before transforation, carrying out the tittering for 24-72 hours after the transformation, selecting the well with high titer and subclone again until 100% of the wells are positive, selecting several strains of high titer and monoclonal cells for expanded culture, finally selecting the best cell for ascites preparation, and selecting the cells for culture. The step (g) is performed by intraperitoneally injecting hybridoma cells into mice, leaving for 5-8 days, bulging the belly of the mice, taking ascites, preparing 50 ml centrifuge tube, inserting a 20 ml syringe needle into abdomen of the mouse, placing a 50 ml centrifuge tube below, twitching the needle back and forth, and flowing the ascites into the centrifuge tube, taking 3 ml each time for 3-5 times until the end of the extraction, storing the ascites at -20 degrees C, purifying ascites to obtain monoclonal antibodies, and performing an in vitro culture by adding the hybridoma cells to 990 ul of HT medium comprising 16% serum, mixing the mixture, taking the corresponding number of cells in 10 ml of HT medium comprising 16% serum, mixing the mixture, plating it in a 96-well plate, placing it at 37 degrees C, culturing the medium in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator, and extracting the monoclonal antibody from the culture solution at a later stage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Medium: The HAT screening medium comprises RPMI-1640 medium, 1X HAT, 16% imported fetal bovine serum (FBS), 1X double antibody, where the medium is stored at 4 degrees C. The HT screening medium comprises RPMI-1640 medium, 1X HT, 16% imported FBS, 1X double antibody, where the medium is stored at 4 degrees C. Preferred Method: The method involves obtaining PEG mixture taking 30 g of PEG4000 powder, autoclaving in a bottle, sterilizing placing it in a water bath at 60 degrees C to prevent PEG from solidifying, adding an equal volume of preheated 1640 medium, adjusting pH to 7.5-8 with 1 ml of 7.5% sodium bicarbonate, measuring with a pH test paper, dispensing 1 ml per tube into the cryotube, and storing it at 4 degrees C. The method involves purifying the ascites by protein A affinity column. Preferred Component: The antigen is AFG1-BSA, and the dose of the injected antigen is the same as the dose of the basal immunization.
DC A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PT P
PN IN201941015698-A
TI Reducing formation and discharge of nitrogen oxide in flue gas leaving boilers involves e.g. recirculating cleaned flue gas leaving induced draft fan to stack, admitting recirculated flue gas, and varying admittance of recirculated flue gas.
AU RATHO L
   RAMAN T G S
AE REOTECH PULP & PAPER PROJECTS P LTD (REOT-Non-standard)
GA 202144969D
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing formation and discharge of nitrogen oxide in flue gas leaving the boilers involves e.g. (i) recirculating 10-15% cleaned flue gas leaving the induced draft fan to stack through gas recirculation fan, (ii) admitting the recirculated flue gas, (iii) admitting recirculated flue gas equivalent, (iv) varying the admittance of recirculated flue gas and (v) reducing the combustion temperature in the reducing atmosphere in the primary level of the combustion chamber.
   USE - The process is useful for reducing formation and discharge of nitrogen oxide in flue gas leaving the boilers.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: reduces consumption of excess air by 3-5%, thus ensuring energy conservation on a sustained basis by reducing low pressure and medium pressure steam consumption by ~0.65 TPH for preheating reduced primary air; reduces greenhouse gas emissions; reduces steam consumption for primary air flow preheating in steam coil air preheater by 15%; reduces gross greenhouse emission by ~1800 ton carbon dioxide emission/annum, thus increasing green energy by generating ~ 1 TPH LP/HP additional green steam available for process; is ecofriendly, sustainable energy conservation, economically viable solution, easy to use, capable of being applied to new as well as existing boilers; and minimizes air ingress in the flue gas path.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reducing formation and discharge of nitrogen oxide in flue gas leaving the boilers comprises (i) recirculating 10-15% the cleaned flue gas leaving the induced draft fan to stack through a gas recirculation fan, (ii) admitting the recirculated flue gas equivalent to 4-5% the total air into the primary air in the combustion chamber by admitting the flue gas into the primary air duct through a rectangular externally insulated metallic duct, the flue gas being suctioned into primary air duct by gas recirculation fan, alternatively adjusting the damper at the entry duct to vary the flow of the flue gas being admitted, displacing 10-15 % primary air at 160 degrees C, reducing low pressure and medium pressure steam consumption for heating primary air (2) by 10-15%, (iii) admitting recirculated flue gas equivalent to 5-7% of the total air into the tertiary/quaternary air in the combustion chamber by admitting the flue gas into the tertiary/ quaternary air entry duct at least one point through the rectangular externally insulated metallic duct, adjusting the damper at the entry duct to vary the flow of the flue gas being admitted, mixing the air and ensuring total combustion, (iv) varying the admittance of recirculated flue gas from the tertiary/quaternary ports entry located in the front and the rear portion of the combustion chamber, in order to correct the flue gas energy imbalance between the left hand side and right-hand side of the combustion chamber, the admittance of flue gas from ports on the front and rear walls of the combustion chamber ensuring total buffeting of flue gas curtain at the top elevation filling up the entire cross-sectional area of the chamber, the varied admittance ensuring uniform lowered flue gas temperatures at electrostatic precipitators inlet to the extent possible and increasing thermal efficiency of the Boiler by 0.2%, increasing HP steam generation by ~0.2% on a continuous basis, (v) reducing the combustion temperature in the reducing atmosphere in the primary level of the combustion chamber by 100 degrees C, reducing combustion temperature by 50-60 degrees C at the tertiary/quaternary level, admitting the heated inert recirculated flue gas thus lowering temperature, reducing formation of nitrogen oxide at the primary level resulting from ~2% water vapor of the mixed primary air including recirculated flue gas being admitted, lowering combustion temperature resulting from the inert in the flue gas, the lower combustion temperature resulting in reduced nitrogen oxide formation by 20%, (vi) reducing consumption of excess air by 3-5%, thus ensuring energy conservation on a sustained basis by reducing low pressure and medium pressure steam consumption by ~0.65 TPH for preheating reduced primary air, (vii) reducing greenhouse gas emissions by reducing steam consumption for primary air flow preheating in steam coil air preheater by 15%, resulting in increased low-pressure steam available for process use, reducing greenhouse emissions by 940 ton carbon dioxide emission/annum through carbon offset, increasing total air intake temperature by 4-5 degrees C by mixing hot flue gas at 160 degrees C with tertiary air, (viii) increasing HP steam generation by 0.2% by increasing boiler thermal efficiency by 0.2%, resulting in carbon emission reduction of 860 t carbon dioxide/annum, (ix) reducing gross greenhouse emission by ~1800 ton carbon dioxide emission/annum and increasing green energy by generating ~ 1.0 TPH LP/HP additional Green steam and (x) is an ecofriendly, sustainable energy conservation, economically viable solution being easy to use and capable of being applied to new as well as existing boilers. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for processing Flue gas recirculation in Rotary Lime kilns for reducing nitrogen oxide reduction in flue gas discharge through integrated stack to the surrounding environment comprising recirculating 5-10% total combustion air into the combustion air fan inlet, effecting a reduction in flame temperature in the combustion zone and reducing nitrogen oxide formation by lowering combustion and burning zone temperatures.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The recirculated flue gas comprises water vapor and inert, the water vapor in flue gas recirculated being 12-14 wt.%, admitting 15 wt.% flue gas into the primary air entry duct, 2% the water vapor content mixed primary air including recirculated flue gas and 85% flue gas inert and the total quantity of water vapor added to the combustion chamber being 1.5% the combination of water vapor and inert lowering the combustion temperature in the primary level of the Combustion Chamber by ~100 degrees C, reducing nitrogen oxide formation in reducing atmosphere of the primary air zone, the flue gas recirculated into the tertiary/quaternary level lowering combustion at the tertiary/quaternary level by 50-60 degrees C. The step of recirculating flue gas admittance into the combustion chamber along with primary air by admitting flue gas at the primary air fan suction.

TI System used for e.g. high rate acidification,            organic solids solubilization and biohydrogen            production, comprises high rate acidifier having            completely mixed bioreactor, membrane unit having            microfiltration membrane, and storage tank.
AU HAFEZ H M
   SAYEED A
   BRADT C B
   SALT D A
AE GREENFIELD GLOBAL INC (GREE-Non-standard)
   GREENFIELD GLOBAL INC (GREE-Non-standard)
   GREENFIELD GLOBAL INC (GREE-Non-standard)
   GREENFIELD GLOBAL INC (GREE-Non-standard)
GA 202023839B
AB    NOVELTY - System (10) comprises: (a) a high rate                acidifier including a completely mixed bioreactor                (12) comprising an input (14) for receiving organic                stream into the completely mixed bioreactor and an                output (18) for discharging an output stream; (b) a                membrane unit (16) located downstream of the                completely mixed bioreactor comprising at least one                microfiltration membrane and comprising a first                side and a second side; and (c) a storage tank                comprising a storage tank input and a storage tank                output, where the membrane concentrate output of                the membrane unit is hydraulically connected to the                storage tank input for receiving concentrated                hydrolyzing, acidifying, and hydrogen producing                microorganisms from the first side of the membrane                unit.
   USE - The system and method are useful for high rate                acidification, organic solids solubilization and                biohydrogen production and continuously producing                hydrogen gas from biomass (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system and method: achieve improved                control of the SRT for feedstock containing up to                15% TSS to achieve superior solubilization of                suspended solids of 15-65% %TSS in the feedstock                and significant productivity in VFAs of 0.1 mg                VFAs, 1 mg TSS to 0.4 mg VFA5/mg TSS feed increase                over the VFAs contained in the feedstock; utilize                the both a membrane unit and a cake/sludge                storage/acidification tank in combination, resulted                in a surprisingly high solubilization rate in                addition to hydrogen production compared to the                gravity settler technology that did not offer any                noticeable solubilization; and utilize the                cake/sludge storage/acidification tank that is                required for process control and unexpected and                positive benefit of increased solubilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - System (10) comprises: (a) a high rate                acidifier including a completely mixed bioreactor                (12) comprising an input (14) for receiving organic                stream into the completely mixed bioreactor and an                output (18) for discharging an output stream, where                the organic stream enters the completely mixed                bioreactor is broken down microbiologically by                hydrolyzing, acidifying, and hydrogen producing                microorganisms to predominantly produce hydrogen                gas and carbon dioxide, and a mixture of VFAs and                primary alcohols, hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide                are emitted from the completely mixed bioreactor,                the output stream contains the volatile fatty acids                (VFAs), primary alcohols and hydrolyzing,                acidifying, and hydrogen producing microorganisms                is discharged from the completely mixed bioreactor;                (b) a membrane unit (16) located downstream of the                completely mixed bioreactor comprising at least one                microfiltration membrane and comprising a first                side and a second side, where the first side                comprises a membrane input, a recirculation input,                and a membrane concentrate output, the second side                comprises a permeate output, the membrane input on                the membrane unit is hydraulically connected with                the output of the completely mixed bioreactor for                receiving the output stream from the completely                mixed bioreactor, permeate containing predominantly                the VFAs and the primary alcohols flow through the                microfiltration membrane and is discharged through                the membrane permeate output, and the                microorganisms in the membrane concentrate output                stream are concentrated on the first side of the                membrane unit; and (c) a storage tank comprising a                storage tank input and a storage tank output, where                the membrane concentrate output of the membrane                unit is hydraulically connected to the storage tank                input for receiving concentrated hydrolyzing,                acidifying, and hydrogen producing microorganisms                from the first side of the membrane unit, the                storage tank output is hydraulically connected to                the completely mixed bioreactor for recirculating                desired quantities of biomass from the storage tank                to the completely mixed bioreactor, and to an                output conduit from the storage tank for                discharging of excess biomass. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM                is also included for continuously producing                hydrogen gas from a biomass, comprising (a) seeding                a completely mixed bioreactor containing a mixture                of microorganisms, where the mixture of                microorganisms include hydrogen producing                microorganisms, (b) continuously flowing an organic                stream into the completely mixed bioreactor, (c)                using the hydrogen producing microorganisms to                continuously break down the biomass in the                completely mixed bioreactor and produce hydrogen                gas, carbon dioxide gas, and a liquid effluent                containing a mixture of volatile fatty acids,                primary alcohols, and the mixture of                microorganisms, (d) continuously emitting the                hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas from the                completely mixed bioreactor, (e) decoupling a solid                retention time from a hydraulic retention time and                controlling the volumetric concentration factor                (VCF) of output stream by flowing the output                containing the mixture of volatile fatty acids, the                primary alcohols, and the mixture of microorganisms                to a microfiltration membrane located downstream of                the completely mixed bioreactor, and concentrating                the hydrogen producing microorganisms and/or                biomass on a first side of the membrane and flowing                liquid permeate through the membrane to a second                side of the membrane, (f) flowing the concentrated                hydrogen producing microorganisms and/or biomass on                a first side of the membrane to a storage tank, and                recirculating a portion of the microorganisms                and/or biomass to the completely mixed bioreactor,                and discharging a remaining portion of the biomass                from the storage tank in an excess waste stream,                and (g) discharging the permeate from the second                side of the membrane to a subsequent downstream                process and/or partially recycling it to the                completely mixed bioreactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows block diagram of the                system.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The nutrients comprise                  nitrogen containing compounds, phosphorus                  containing compounds, trace metals including                  iron, manganese, magnesium, calcium, cobalt,                  zinc, nickel or copper. The hydrogen producing                  microorganisms comprise Clostridium                  acetobutylicum, Bacillus thuringiensis or                  Clostridium butyricum. Preferred Method: The                  method comprises: controlling a temperature of                  completely mixed bioreactor; and dispensing                  nutrients and pH adjustment compounds into the                  completely mixed bioreactor, where the pH                  adjustment compounds comprise soda ash, sodium                  bicarbonate, sodium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide,                  magnesium hydroxide, nitric acid or hydrochloric                  acid. The temperature of the completely mixed                  bioreactor is maintained in a temperature of                  20-70 degrees C. The organic stream comprises up                  to 15% total suspended solids (TSS). The solids                  residence time (SRT) is 1.6-4.5 days. The                  hydraulic retention time (HRT) is 6-18 hours. The                  VCF is 1.5-2.2.

TI Preventing terminal crack for submerged arc welding of Q420 high-strength steel flux copper backing method comprises e.g. processing welded joints of steel plates with Y-shaped welding grooves, cleaning the oxide layer and welding.
AU ZHANG J
   LEI B
   SHAO D
   CHEN L
   PAN Q
   YANG C
   WU W
   ZHANG Y
AE GUANGZHOU HUANGCHUAN OCEANOGRAPHIC ENG (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202004112A
AB    NOVELTY - Preventing terminal crack for submerged arc welding of Q420 high-strength steel flux copper backing method comprises e.g. (i) processing welded joints of steel plates with Y-shaped welding grooves, where the thickness of the steel plate is different, corresponding parameters of the Y-shaped welding groove are different, processing the Y-shaped welding groove, cleaning the oxide layer and impurities within 20 mm around the Y-shaped welding groove surface and the Y-shaped welding groove surface, (ii) assembling the bevel of the steel plate, and welding, then using semi-automatic carbon dioxide gas shielded welding to perform tack welding on the front of the Y-shaped welding groove, and where the thickness of the tack weld is 4-5 mm, and(iii) preparing the arc starting plate and the arc extinguishing plate, and using the gouging method on the arc starting plate, and cutting from the center of the width of the arc quenching plate.
   USE - The method is useful for preventing terminal crack prevention process for submerged arc welding of Q420 high-strength steel flux copper backing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: prevents the terminal welding crack problem of submerged arc welding of Q420 high strength steel flux copper liner method; and reduces the workload of repairing cracks at the weld terminal after welding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preventing terminal crack prevention process for submerged arc welding of Q420 high-strength steel flux copper backing method comprises (i) processing welded joints of steel plates with Y-shaped welding grooves, where the thickness of the steel plate is different, corresponding parameters of the Y-shaped welding groove are different, and steel plate having a thickness of 10 mm less than or equal to tless-than17 mm, opening angle is 57-63 degrees , blunt edge is 2-4 mm, and root clearance is 0-1 mm, 17mm less than or equal to t less than 23 mm steel plate, opening angle is 47-53 degrees , the blunt side is 2-4 mm, and the root gap is 0-1 mm, steel plates with a thickness of 23mm less than or equal to t lees than 31 mm, the opening angle is 42-48 degrees , blunt edge is 4-6 mm, root clearance is 0-1 mm, thickness of steel plate 31mm less than or equal to t less than or equal to 40mm, opening angle is 42-48 degrees , blunt edge is 5-7 mm, root clearance is 0-1 mm, processing the Y-shaped welding groove, cleaning the oxide layer and impurities within 20 mm around the Y-shaped welding groove surface and the Y-shaped welding groove surface, (ii) assembling the bevel of the steel plate, welding, then using semi-automatic carbon dioxide gas shielded welding to perform tack welding on the front of the Y-shaped welding groove, and where the thickness of the tack weld is 4-5 mm, (iii) preparing the arc starting plate and the arc extinguishing plate, and using the gouging method on the arc starting plate, arc gouging groove with length of 150-200 mm, cutting from the center of the width of the arc quenching plate along the length direction from the center to one side edge, where the material, thickness, groove size, blunt side and root clearance of the arc striking plate, arc quenching plate and welding plate are the same, (iv) assembling the gouging groove of the arc starting plate and the arc quenching plate to the Y-shaped welding groove of the steel plate, ensuring that the ignition and quenching plates are flush with the steel plate, then using semi-automatic carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, and installing the arc quenching plate at both ends of the welding groove, (v) using semi-automatic carbon dioxide gas shielded welding method, starting the Y-shaped welding groove is 200-250 mm, Y-type welding groove terminal is 450-500 mm for bottom welding, and controlling the thickness of the bottom bead at 4-5 mm, (vi) using flux-cored wire with a diameter of 4.8mm and a grade of Kobe steel US-36LS as the first electrode wire, flux-cored wire with a diameter of 4.8 mm and a grade of Kobe steel US-36T as second electrode wire, and flux-cored wire with diameter of 6.4 mm and grade of Kobe steel US-36T as the third electrode wire, FCB submerged arc welding with surface flux of Kobe steel PF-I55E and back flux of Kobe steel PF-I50R, (vii) heating the terminal trapezoidal area, making sure that the temperature of this area reaches 600-650 degrees C before the welding head welds this area, and then welding the terminal trapezoidal area, and(viii) cooling the weld, cutting the arc starting plate and the quenching plate at both ends of the weld, using flame cutting or plasma cutting to cut off the arc-extinguishing plates at both ends of the weld.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), (iv) and (v), when welding, the flux-cored wire used meets the requirements of the E81T1-K2CJ model in the AWSA5.29 standard. In the steps (iv) and (v), the flux cored wire used is Tiantai TWE-81K2 flux cored wire. In the steps (viii), performing cutting by flame cutting or plasma cutting.

TI System useful for snail and weed control comprises e.g. automated embedded robotic unit, biosensing components, sensors, modules, data input utility, processor utility, memory unit, communication utility, camera sensors, and power supply.
AU KAKULTE V R
   MAMLAYYA A B
   MAMLAYYA V B
AE KAKULTE V R (KAKU-Individual)
   MAMLAYYA A B (MAML-Individual)
   MAMLAYYA V B (MAML-Individual)
GA 202287108D
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises: automated embedded robotic unit; biosensing components; sensors capable to detect the soil parameters; sensors capable to detect the water quantity and quality; module with a mechanism to detect snail infestation; module to detect weed; module to analyse and provide on spot treatment of herbicide and pesticide; a data input utility; a processor utility; a memory unit; communication utility; data collection unit; camera sensors; global positioning system; container tank; electrodes; the collection chamber comprising sampling reservoirs, at least one pump, a power supply, a microprocessor control board and data delivery system, at least one external sensor connection, at least one solute or deionized reservoir and at least one ion selective electrode to provide real-time nutrient monitoring of soil and plant; alarm; notification unit; LED; and power supply.
   USE - The system is useful for: providing complete monitoring and working of the farm with the minimum workforce to obtain maximum yield by detecting soil parameters, water quantity and quality, snail infestation, and weeds and to provide on spot treatment to reduce the spraying of herbicides and pesticides harming the soil and crops; and tracking the color of the plant that indicates a deficiency in at least one minerals or vitamins and identify environmental conditions including illumination (intensity or and/or spectrum), temperature, humidity, and air composition indicative of carbon dioxide contents and odor along with meteorological data that captures wind speed and direction, lighting, temperature, humidity and rainfall to analyze and predict the crop yield.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: detects snail infestation, weeds and others using the robotic mobile unit and provides on spot treatment and reducing the spraying of herbicides and pesticides harming the soil and crops; and has nano-sensors designed to track the soil parameters including pH and nutrients, residual pesticides in crop and soil, and soil humidity and further detect the pathogens and estimate nitrogen uptake to predict the crop yield; adopts robotic unit, which (a) provide on spot treatment to the pest without spraying to kill the pest and to reduce soil contamination and overfeeding of the pesticide, (b) provide real-time nutrient monitoring, and (c) sense nearby motion and the robotic unit moves in the direction of the motion and possibly turns on its weed cutter and the camera subsystem incorporated into the robot assists with navigation to take images or videos of the plants and pest, especially snails to alert the farm keeper informed of the action; detects the water requirement and provides nano zeolites into the soil to enhance the soil's water-holding capacity; controls the water supply and the fertilizer mixing unit using intelligence by analysing the requirement of additives based on the plant and providing on spot herbicide treatment to reduce spraying and contamination of soil and plants and it has arms attached to the front of the machine that engages in uprooting the weed in real time; and track the color of the plant that indicates a deficiency in at least one mineral or vitamin and identify environmental conditions including illumination (intensity or and/or spectrum), temperature, humidity, and air composition indicative of carbon dioxide contents and odor along with meteorological data that captures wind speed and direction, lighting, temperature, humidity and rainfall to analyze and predict the crop yield.

TI Cultivation bag assembly for culturing microbes,            fungi and other organisms, has inner bag having first            and second walls being constructed of layer of            water-vapor permeable material, and outer bag having            opening with gas filter patch.
AU HSU W K
AE HSU W K (HSUW-Individual)
   HSU W K (HSUW-Individual)
GA 2021D3633D
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivation bag assembly (10) for culturing                microbes, fungi and other organisms comprises an                inner bag (12) having first and second walls (24,                26) that are joined together along side edges to                define an inner bag interior for containing a food                substrate and an organism to be cultivated, at                least one of the first and second walls being                constructed of a layer of water-vapor permeable                material to allow the passage of water vapor; and                an outer bag (22) having first and second walls                joined along side edges to define an outer bag                interior. The first and second walls of outer bag                are formed from gas impermeable layers, at least                one of the first and second walls of the outer bag                having an opening (36) with a gas filter patch (38)                covering the opening to allow the passage of oxygen                and carbon dioxide gases through the gas filter                patch to and from ambient air and into and out of                the interior of the inner bag to facilitate                incubation of the organism within the interior of                the inner bag.
   USE - The cultivation bag assembly is useful for                culturing microbes, fungi and other                organisms.
   ADVANTAGE - The improved cultivation bag assembly has some                advantages over the present art to reduce the steps                in preparation and manufacture of the agents and to                reduce risks of competing or contaminating matter                introduced into to the process. It stimulates                maximum growth of filamentous fungi spores by                allowing wet substrate contained in a bag and                providing a breathing unit through a filter. The                biomass becomes the product to be dried and reduced                in size so that it can be easily sprayed into                fields or areas where the pests to be destroyed                live.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for forming a                cultivation bag assembly for culturing microbes,                fungi and other organisms which involves forming an                inner bag having first and second walls that are                joined together along side edges to define an inner                bag interior for containing a food substrate and an                organism to be cultivated, at least one of the                first and second walls being constructed of a layer                of water-vapor permeable material to allow the                passage of water vapor, at least one of the first                and second walls of the inner bag is                non-transparent or opaque; providing a window in at                least one of the first and second non-transparent                or opaque walls of the inner bag to allow one to                view the interior of the inner bag through the                window; forming an outer bag having first and                second walls joined along side edges to define an                outer bag interior, the inner bag being contained                within the interior of the outer bag, the first and                second walls of the outer bag being formed from gas                impermeable layers, at least one of the first and                second walls of the outer bag having an opening                with a gas filter patch covering the opening to                allow the passage of oxygen and carbon dioxide                gases through the gas filter patch to and from                ambient air and into and out of the interior of the                inner bag to facilitate incubation of the organism                within the interior of the inner bag; and coupling                the inner bag and outer bag together at a first end                of each of the inner bag and outer bag to close the                first end of each of the inner bag and outer                bag.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded perspective                view of the cultivation bag assembly.

AB    NOVELTY - Nitrile-group-containing copolymer rubber                comprises 15-28 wt.% units (a) of an alpha , beta                -ethylenically unsaturated nitrile monomer, 10-50                wt.% units (b) of an alpha , beta -ethylenically                unsaturated monocarboxylic acid ester monomer,                22-74 wt.% units (c) of a conjugated diene monomer,                and 1-10 wt.% units (d) of a                nitrile/carboxyl-group-containing monomer and which                has an iodine value of 120 or less, where at least                some of the units (c) of a conjugated-diene monomer                have been hydrogenated and the units (c) of a                conjugated-diene monomer have a content of isoprene                units of 5-33 wt.%.
   USE - The nitrile group-containing copolymer rubber                is used in a crosslinkable rubber composition for                rubber crosslinked product (all claimed). The                rubber crosslinked product is used in various                applications such as: various sealing materials                such as O-rings, packings, diaphragms, oil seals,                shaft seals, bearing seals, well head seals, shock                absorber seals, seals for pneumatic equipment,                seals for chlorofluorocarbons or fluorocarbons or                carbon dioxide used in air conditioner cooling                devices and air conditioner refrigerator                compressors, sealing seals for supercritical or                subcritical carbon dioxide used in precision                cleaning media, seals for rolling devices (rolling                bearings, automobile hub units, automobile water                pumps, linear guide devices and ball screws),                valves and valve seats, blow out presenters, and                platters; various gaskets such as intake manifold                gasket installed at the connection between the                intake manifold and the cylinder head, cylinder                head gasket installed at the connection between the                cylinder block and the cylinder head, a rocker                cover gasket attached to the joint between the                rocker cover and the cylinder head, an oil pan                gasket attached to the joint between the oil pan                and the cylinder block or transmission case, fuel                cell separator gaskets and hard disk drive top                cover gaskets that are mounted between a pair of                housings that sandwich a unit cell with a positive                electrode, electrolyte plate, and negative                electrode; various rolls such as printing rolls,                iron making rolls, paper making rolls, industrial                rolls, and office machine rolls; various belts such                as flat belts (film core flat belts, cord flat                belts, laminated flat belts, and stand-alone flat                belts), V-belts (wrapped V-belts, and low-edge                V-belts), V-ribbed belt (single V-ribbed belt,                double V-ribbed belt, wrapped V-ribbed belt, back                rubber V-ribbed belt, and upper cog V-ribbed belt),                continuously variable transmission (CVT) belts,                timing belts, toothed belts, and conveyor belts;                various hoses such as fuel hose, turbo air hose,                oil hose, radiator hose, heater hose, water hose,                vacuum brake hoses, control hoses, air conditioner                hoses, brake hoses, power steering hoses, air                hoses, marine hoses, risers, and flow lines;                various boots such as constant velocity joint CVJ                boots, propeller shaft boots, constant velocity                joint boots, rack and pinion boots; damping                material rubber components such as cushion                material, dynamic damper, rubber coupling, air                spring, vibration isolator, and clutch facing                material; and dust covers, automobile interior                members, friction materials, tires, coated cables,                soles, electromagnetic wave shields, adhesives for                flexible printed substrates, fuel cell separators,                and the electronics field.
   ADVANTAGE - The rubber crosslinked product has excellent                in cold resistance, swelling resistance in oil                (small volume change in oil), and curability in oil                (small change in hardness in oil containing                condensed aromatic compound). The composition has                excellent balance between curability in oil                resistance and cold resistance, and has excellent                productivity, and excellent heat resistance                (excellent heat resistance by lowering the iodine                value).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a crosslinkable rubber composition                containing the nitrile group-containing copolymer                rubber, and a crosslinking agent; and
   (2) a rubber crosslinked product obtained by                crosslinking the crosslinkable rubber                composition.

PN CN212325144-U; CN113892455-A
TI Aquatic culture container for breeding aquatic organism, has air pressure layer sealing plate whose length does not reach bottom of container, where pressure generated by height of water level is pressure difference between water body and opening.
AU SHE W
AE GUANGZHOU JIELIU ECOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021087844
AB    (CN212325144-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims an aquaculture container with air pressure layer and pressure relief opening, the beneficial effect is that when establishing the aquiculture container, the water body in the container is divided into a high pressure area and a low pressure area; the high pressure area is easy to dissolve the added gas into the water body; and the low pressure area is easier to discharge the gas in the water; when the container is used for breeding the aquatic organism, the gas in the water is easy to be replaced by the gas of the air pressure layer; although the aquatic organism in the container continuously consumes the oxygen and releases the carbon dioxide, the gas and the air pressure layer can have strong exchange; Therefore, the gas component in the water tends to the gas component of the air pressure layer, so as to reach the purpose of keeping a relatively high oxygen dissolving amount.
EA    NOVELTY - The aquaculture container has container whose top is provided with pressurizing cover plate. The pressurization cover plate does not seal top part of whole container, but leaves pressure relief opening. The pressure relief openings are provided with water storage surrounding plates. The water level in the water storage surrounds plate is lifted from the overflow of the water level of pressure relief. The air bubbles are increased. The bubbles finally pass over the lowest point of the air pressure sheet sealing plate, and discharges the container from the water layer of the storage surrounding plate in the pressure release port, where the water in the pressure relief opening has a water level difference relative to the lowest point of the air pressure layer sealing plate, and the pressure generated by the height of water level is the pressure difference between the water body corresponding to the water pressure layer, and the water bodies corresponding to pressure relief opens.
   USE - Aquaculture container i.e. breeding container, for use in aquatic product activity or long time cultivation process, and can also be used for fish pond or fish tank.
   ADVANTAGE - The water body in the container is divided into a high pressure area and a low pressure area when establishing the aquaculture container, so that the high pressure area is more easy to dissolve the external gas into the water body, and the low pressure area is easier to discharge the gas in the water, the gas in the water is easy to be replaced by the gas of the air pressure layer when the container is used for breeding aquatic organism, no matter the aquatic organism in the container continuously consumes oxygen and releases the carbon dioxide, it will tend to the gas component of the air pressure layer. The container allows the water in the pressure relief opening and the water storage surrounding plate to be divided into high and low pressure areas such that the gas dissolving amount of the water can be reduced and the pressure of the pressure layer is not changed, thus reducing the saturation value of the part of water.

PN CN111635888-A
TI Inducing apoptosis of human acute T lymphocyte            leukemia cells, such as Jurkat cell and Nalm-6 cells,            involves resuscitating human acute T lymphocyte            leukemia cells, and suspending them in RPMI 1640 medium            with fetal bovine serum.
AU LIU W
   MA W
   YANG Y
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST MEDICAL AFFILIATED HOSPIT (USWM-C)
GA 2020895543
AB    NOVELTY - Inducing apoptosis of human acute T lymphocyte                leukemia cells involves resuscitating human acute T                lymphocyte leukemia cells suspending them in                Roswell Park Memorial Institute (RPMI) 1640 medium                with 10% fetal bovine serum, and placing them in a                saturated humidity incubator at 37 degrees C. The                volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the incubator                is 5%. The human acute T lymphocyte leukemia cells                in the logarithmic growth phase afe collected and                the cell density is adjusted to 1.5x 105-2x 105                cells/ml to obtain a cell suspension. The cell                suspension in an amount of 90 mu l is added to each                well of a 96-well plate followed by the addition of                10 mu L shuanghuanglian (SHL) with a concentration                greater than 70 mu g/ml to each well and added 10                mu L of serum-free RPMI 1640 medium to the negative                control group. The blank is set to zero group,                where each group is provided with 3 multiple                holes.
   USE - Method for inducing apoptosis of human acute T                lymphocyte leukemia cells, such as Jurkat cell and                Nalm-6 cells (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can realize the use of                shuanghuanglian to inhibit the proliferation of                leukemia cells and promote the apoptosis of                leukemia cells which is further useful for later                clinical research on drugs optimizing the treatment                plan for patients with leukemia.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Inducing apoptosis of human acute T lymphocyte                leukemia cells involves resuscitating human acute T                lymphocyte leukemia cells suspending them in                Roswell Park Memorial Institute (RPMI) 1640 medium                with 10% fetal bovine serum, and placing them in a                saturated humidity incubator at 37 degrees C. The                volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the incubator                is 5%. The human acute T lymphocyte leukemia cells                in the logarithmic growth phase afe collected and                the cell density is adjusted to 1.5x 105-2x 105                cells/ml to obtain a cell suspension. The cell                suspension in an amount of 90 mu l is added to each                well of a 96-well plate followed by the addition of                10 mu L shuanghuanglian (SHL) with a concentration                greater than 70 mu g/ml to each well and added 10                mu L of serum-free RPMI 1640 medium to the negative                control group. The blank is set to zero group,                where each group is provided with 3 multiple holes.                The experiment is repeated 3 times independently,                and 100 mu L sterile phosphate-buffered saline                (PBS) is added to the edge holes. After culturing                in the saturated humidity incubator for 10-12                hours, Cell Counting Kit-8 solution in an amount of                10 mu L is added to each well and incubation is                continued for 4 hours. The optical density (OD) of                each well is detected at 450 nm using a                full-wavelength spectrophotometer. The acerage OV                value of each group is calculated and the cell                proliferation inhibition rate is calculated by                using relationship: Proliferation inhibition rate                (%)=(control group OD value-experimental group OD                value)/(control group OD value-blank group OD                value)x 100. The cells in the logarithmic growth                phase are inoculated into a 12-well culture plate                in an amount of 2x 105 cells per well. The cells                are washed twice with PBS, then resuspended, fixed                with 400 mu L of 1x binding buffer, added 3 mu L of                AnnexinV-FITC and incubated for 15 minutes in dark                followed by addition of 3 mu L propidium iodide                (PI) dye for dyeing and finally tested on the flow                cytometer. The experiment is repeated 3 times                independently. The result is analyzed by FlowJo                analysis.
TF ACTIVITY - Apoptotic; Cytostatic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN111616249-A
TI Roasting coffee beans by preheating coffee roaster boiler, feeding coffee beans, heating and evaporating so that sugar in undergo caramelization and carbohydrates undergo Maillard browning and cooling roaster.
AU SHI X
AE NANJING RUOZHAO COMMERCE & TRADE CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202091606U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for roasting coffee beans involves (a) preheating coffee roaster boiler, feeding the coffee beans, which immediately absorb heat, removing most of the moisture in the raw beans, (b) heating, sampling and observing, and heating until the whole bean had expanded, the color of the bean surface changed from grayish green to yellow, and the aroma of the coffee beans changed from grassy and grainy into toasty, (c) continuing heating and evaporating so that the sugar in the cells starts to undergo caramelization and browning, and the amino acids and carbohydrates undergo Maillard browning to produce a large amount of carbon dioxide and water vapor, (d) heating until beans pop and (e) cooling the roaster.
   USE - The method is useful for roasting coffee beans.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances aroma of coffee, makes the coffee more swollen and the surface is smoother and promotes development of the uniform color and taste of the beans.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for roasting coffee beans involves (a) preheating coffee roaster boiler at 200 degrees C with heating power of 35%, feeding the coffee beans, which immediately absorb heat, where the boiler temperature is rapidly reduced, and removing most of the moisture in the raw beans to prepare for the caramel and Maillard reaction, (b) heating to the temperature point within 1 minute and 40 seconds, increasing heating to 50%, when the temperature of the boiler reaches a certain time point after the temperature drops, the temperature rises and reaches an equilibrium point, which is called the temperature return point, sampling and observing in first 1-4 minutes, where the color change of the beans cannot be seen by naked eyes, sampling and observing in 5-6 minutes, when the coffee beans had been partially dehydrated and heating at 150-160 degrees C until the whole bean had expanded, the color of the bean surface changed from grayish green to yellow, and the aroma of the coffee beans changed from grassy and grainy into toasty, (c) continuing heating at 205 degrees C with heating power of 40%, sampling in 7-9 minutes, where it is found that the bean hulls began to appear ugly wrinkles and black markings and the toasted taste changes to coffee taste, continuously evaporating the water in the coffee cells into water vapor, heating at 180 degrees C so that the sugar in the cells starts to undergo caramelization and browning, and the amino acids and carbohydrates undergo Maillard browning to produce a large amount of carbon dioxide and water vapor and makes a popping sound, which is called a burst that lasts about 1-2 minutes, where the volume of the beans will increase, the density will drop sharply, and the weight loss will continue to increase, heating at 205 degrees C with heating power of 40% to allow the beans to pass the heat release stage, (d) heating at 215 degrees C with heating power of 30% for first popping and leaving for 1-2 minutes, where the coffee turns from exothermic to endothermic, heating at 210 degrees C for second popping sound, where the second popping sound is smaller and quicker than the first popping sound, giving out a unique rich fragrance and develop the uniform color and taste of the beans and (e) cooking the beans at 225 degrees C for 15 minutes, and cooling the roaster to less than 70 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (e) involves cooling the beans rapidly to lock the flavor, if the high temperature in the beans continues to work, it will burn off the aromatic substances and make coffee have a smoky and burnt taste.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN110697752-A
TI Modifying nano calcium carbonate, comprises using mixed solution A, mixed solution B and mixed solution C as raw materials, and modifying device comprising e.g. heating kettle, vacuum reactor, high-speed mixer, dryer and irradiation device.
AU CHEN Z
AE FOSHAN ZHANHUA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 2020086381
AB    NOVELTY - Modifying nano calcium carbonate, comprises using a mixed solution A comprising quicklime, nano calcium carbonate, carbon dioxide and distilled water, a mixed solution B comprising butyl titanate solution, absolute ethanol, dispersant and polyacrylate, and a mixed solution C comprising the mixed solution A and the mixed solution B as raw materials, and a modifying device comprising a heating kettle, a vacuum reactor, a high-speed mixer, a mortar, a dryer and an irradiation device.
   USE - The method is useful for modifying nano calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes wet modification technology to avoid the problem of hard agglomeration during the dry modification process, and radiation treatment process to solve the problem of poor binding ability of nanometer calcium carbonate; reduces the performance of the material; and produces product that has excellent thermal stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing modified nano calcium carbonate, comprising (a) adding 100 pts. wt. quicklime and 2000 pts. wt. distilled water into a high-speed mixer, simultaneously introducing while carbon dioxide, simultaneously heating the high-speed mixer at 60 degrees C to obtain an internal mixed liquid, and stirring the mixed solution at a constant temperature by using a height mixer at 3000 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 1 hour to obtain A mixed solution, (b) adding 20 pts. wt. butyl titanate solution, 70 pts. wt. absolute ethanol and 20 pts. wt. polyacrylate into a high-speed mixer, stirring at 1000 rpm for 0.5 hours and mixing, adding 20 pts. wt. dispersant to the high-speed mixer, and continuously stirring the internal solution for 1.5 hours by using the high-speed mixer to obtain a mixed solution B, (c) adding the mixed solution A and mixed solution B into a vacuum reactor, heating the mixed liquid in the vacuum reactor at 80 degrees C, then stopping heating of the solution inside the vacuum reactor, maintaining the solution inside the vacuum reactor at 80 degrees C, simultaneously stirring and mixing the solution in the vacuum reactor at 2000 rpm, and continuously stirring for 0.5 hours to obtain a mixed solution C, (d) placing the mixed solution C in a dryer, dehydrating and drying the mixed solution C by using a dryer for 3 hours to obtain a dried powder of the mixed solution , adding the powder into a mortar, grinding to obtain a powdered material of the mixed solution, conveying into a heating kettle, heating the powder material in heating kettle to 500 degrees C for 2 hours, allowing the powder stand, and cooling to obtain a nano-calcium carbonate powder with high whiteness, and (e) adding the high-whiteness nano-calcium carbonate powder into an irradiation device, performing high-energy irradiation treatment on the powder by using high-energy ultraviolet light, adding 30 pts. wt. monomer olefin and 30 pts. wt. sulfonate with the treated powder into a high speed mixer, mixing by using a high speed mixer at 1000 rpm for 2 hours, collecting the powder mixed inside the high-speed mixer, adding the mixed powder into the irradiation device, irradiating, reacting monomer olefin and high whiteness nanometer calcium carbonate powder to form an organic coating to obtain a high activity, tightly binding high whiteness nano calcium carbonate powder on the surface of sulfonate and nanometer calcium carbonate powder in the form of multi-point anchoring, where the sulfonate has a large molecular weight and high thermal stability to obtain obtaining nano calcium carbonate powder with high thermal stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (i) preparing nano calcium carbonate, quicklime and distilled water, (ii) preparing butyl titanate solution, absolute ethanol, and dispersant polyacrylate, and (iii) preparing monomeric olefins and sulfonates. Preferred Composition: The nano calcium carbonate comprises 100-200 pts. wt. nano calcium carbonate, 90-110 pts. wt. quicklime, 2000-5000 pts. wt. distilled water, 10-30 pts. wt. butyl titanate solution, 40-100 pts. wt. absolute ethanol, 10-30 pts. wt. polyacrylate, 20-50 pts. wt. dispersant, 20-50 pts. wt. monomer olefin, and 20-50 pts. wt. sulfonate. Preferred Components: The dispersant comprises isocetyl stearate and polyoxyethylene glyceryl ether monostearate.

PN IN201821019166-A
TI Synergistic insecticidal composition used e.g. to control insect-pests in the crops of cotton and wheat, comprises chlorantraniliprole, one insecticide of e.g. fipronil, further insecticide of e.g. bifenthrin, and other inactive excipient.
AU GUJRAL A S
   SHAH K V
   SHAH B V
   SINGH A
   DIPAKKUMAR P
AE GSP CROP SCI PVT LTD (GSPC-Non-standard)
GA 2019A36534
AB    NOVELTY - Synergistic insecticidal composition comprises: (a) chlorantraniliprole; (b) one insecticide of fipronil, bifenthrin and chlorpyrifos; (c) further at least one insecticide of bifenthrin, lambda cyhalothrin, chlorpyrifos, cartap hydrochloride or thiocyclam hydrogen oxalate or its salts; and (d) at least one other inactive excipient.
   USE - The composition is useful for managing or controlling insect-pests in the crops comprising genetically modified organism (GMO) and non-GMO varieties of e.g. cotton (Gossypium spp.), paddy (Oryza sativa), wheat (Triticum aestivum), Maize (Zea mays), sugarcane (Saccharum officinarum), sugar beet (Beta vulgaris), soybean (Glycin max), peanut (Arachis hypogaea), green gram (Vigna radiata) and black gram (Vigna mungo) and controlling pests and insects from the order of the Lepidoptera e.g. Agrotis ipsilon, Chilo infuscatellus, Chilo partellus and Chilo suppressalis, beetles (coleoptera) e.g. Diabrotica longicornis, Diabrotica semipunctata, Diabrotica punctata, Diabrotica speciosa and Diabrotica virgifera, thrips (thysanoptera) e.g. Frankliniella occidentalis, Frankliniella tritici, Scirtothrips citri, Scirtothrips dorsalis and Thrips oryzae, termites (isoptera) e.g. Kalotermes flavicollis, Coptotermes formosanus, Heterotermes aureus, Leucotermes flavipes and Microtermes obesi (all claimed). The ability of the composition to control insect-pests was tested against early shoot borer (Chilo infuscatellus) in sugarcane by measuring % early shoot borer control by using biological assays. The results showed that application of 10 kg composition (comprised of 0.6% chlorantraniliprole+0.6% fipronil+0.5 bifenthrin granule) exhibited 98.8% control of early shoot borer after 45 days.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: can be prepared easily without much complex manufacturing process; increases efficacy against target insect-pests, broadens the activity means to control more than one insect-pests at a time, reduces the total loading of insecticides in to the environment, provides safety to the applicators and crop ecosystem and but not least delay or to manage resistance development; provides, with a view to effective resistance management and effective control of harmful insects, at application rates which are as low as possible, at a reduced total quantity of active compounds applied, improved activity against the harmful pests and a broadened activity spectrum; provides improved vitality of the plant, improved plant growth, improved plant development, improved visual appearance, improved plant stand (less plant verse/lodging), improved emergence, enhanced root growth and/or more developed root system, enhanced nodulation, preferably rhizoidal nodulation, bigger leaf blade, bigger size, increased plant weight, increased plant height, increased tiller number, increased number of side shoots, increased number of flowers per plant, increased shoot growth, increased root growth (extensive root system), increased yield when grown on poor soils or unfavorable climate, enhanced photosynthetic activity (e.g. based on increased stomatal conductance and/or increased carbon dioxide assimilation rate), increased stomatal conductance, increased carbon dioxide assimilation rate, enhanced pigment content (e.g. chlorophyll content), earlier flowering, earlier fruiting, earlier and improved germination, earlier grain maturity, improved self-defense mechanisms, improved stress tolerance and resistance of the plants against biotic and abiotic stress factors e.g. fungi, bacteria, viruses, insects, heat stress, cold stress, drought stress, UV stress and/or salt stress, less non-productive tillers, less dead basal leaves, less input needed (e.g. fertilizers or water), greener leaves, complete maturation under shortened vegetation periods, less fertilizers needed, less seeds needed, easier harvesting, faster and more uniform ripening, longer shelf-life, longer panicles, delay of senescence, stronger and/or more productive tillers, better extractability of ingredients, improved quality of seeds (for being seeded in the following seasons for seed production), better nitrogen uptake, improved reproduction, reduced production of ethylene and/or the inhibition of its reception by the plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The formulations comprises of granules (GR) or Zeon concentrate (ZC) formulation. The GR formulation comprises (a)-(c) as per se, (e) polyaryl phenol ethoxylate and calcium dodecyl benzene sulfonate blend, (f) phosphoric acid, (g) azo dye, (h) diacetone alcohol, (i) china clay, and (j) river sand. The ZC formulation comprises either (a)-(c) as per se, (e1) acrylic graft polymer, (f1) fatty alcohol ethoxylated, (g1) monoethylene glycol, (h1) sodium salt of naphthalene sulfonate condensate, (i1) 1,2-benzisothiazolin-3-one, (j1) toluene diisocyanate, (k) polymethyl polyphenyl isocyanate, (l) mixture of heavy aromatic hydrocarbons, (m) silicon dioxide, (n) polyaryl phenol ethoxylate, (o) modified styrene acrylic polymer, (p) xanthan powder and (q) silicone antifoam. Preferred Components: The component (a) is in ratio of 0.1-20%, component (b) insecticide is in ratio of 0.1-30% and component (c) is in ratio of 0.1-50%. The inactive excipient comprises dispersant, antifreezing agent, anti-foam agent, wetting agent, suspension aid, antimicrobial agent, thickener, quick coating agent or sticking agents (stickers or binders) or buffering agent, or (a)-(c) as per se, (e2) sodium dioctyl sulfosuccinate, (f2) modified styrene acrylic polymer, (g2) 1,2- benzisothiazolin-3-one, (h1) toluene diisocyanate, (i2) polymethyl polyphenyl isocyanate, (j2) silicon dioxide, (k1) ethylene oxide/propylene oxide block copolymer, (l1) xanthan powder, (m1) polyamines, (n1) silicone antifoam, and (o1) polyethylene glycol. Preferred Components: The component (a) is in ratio of 0.1-20%, component (b) insecticide is in ratio of 0.1-30% and component (c) is in ratio of 0.1-50%. The inactive excipient comprises dispersant, antifreezing agent, anti-foam agent, wetting agent, suspension aid, antimicrobial agent, thickener, quick coating agent or sticking agents (stickers or binders) or buffering agent.
   ACTIVITY - Insecticide; Plant Protectant.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PN CN109673508-A
TI Making rice resistant to herbicides by mutation breeding comprises e.g. selecting rice varieties to be improved that are more sensitive to acetolactate synthase inhibitor herbicides, and soaking the rice seeds in water for overnight.
AU ZHANG B
   CUI J
   SONG Y
AE ANHUI LVYI SEEDS CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201940834U
AB    NOVELTY - Making rice resistant to herbicides by mutation breeding comprises e.g. (i) selecting rice varieties to be improved that are more sensitive to acetolactate synthase inhibitor herbicides, soaking the rice seeds in water for overnight, and re-immersing in a composite mutagen consisting of sodium azide, nitrosylmethyl urea, and 5-bromouracil, (ii) washing the rice seeds with clean water for 2-4 times, irradiating with carbon dioxide, (iii) removing the treated rice hulls, sterilizing the mature embryos and excising, inoculating into the induction medium, using the mature embryos as explants to induce embryogenic callus in the dark condition, and inducing to culture, (iv) under aseptic conditions, subjecting the rice embryogenic callus to plasma radiation mutagenesis treatment, (v) performing tissue expansion and cultivation of post-treatment embryogenic callus, transplanting the seedlings after rooting to the seedbed.
   USE - The method is useful for making rice resistant to herbicides by mutation breeding.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases the frequency of mutations; improves missense mutation in the conserved region of the rice ALS genome; changes in the structure and/or spatial conformation of the enzyme; and reduces the affinity of rice for ALS-inhibiting herbicides.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Making rice resistant to herbicides by mutation breeding comprises (i) selecting rice varieties to be improved that are more sensitive to acetolactate synthase (ALS) inhibitor herbicides, soaking the rice seeds in water for overnight, and re-immersing in a composite mutagen consisting of 4 mmol/l sodium azide, 0.3% nitrosylmethyl urea, and 0.2% 5-bromouracil for 4-8 hours, (ii) washing the rice seeds with clean water for 2-4 times, irradiating with carbon dioxide at the radiation intensity of 20-25 W/cm2 for 15-20 seconds, (iii) removing the treated rice hulls, sterilizing the mature embryos and excising, inoculating into the induction medium, using the mature embryos as explants to induce embryogenic callus in the dark condition at 28 degrees C, and inducing to culture for 24-28 days, (iv) under aseptic conditions, subjecting the rice embryogenic callus to plasma radiation mutagenesis treatment for 90-120 seconds, where the radiant power is 20-30 W/cm2, and the discharge spacing is 40-50 mm, (v) performing tissue expansion and cultivation of post-treatment embryogenic callus, transplanting the seedlings after rooting to the seedbed, when the rice seedlings grow to 3-4 true leaves, spraying the rice stems and leaves with 400-450 dilution of the ALS-inhibiting herbicidal composition, and at every 4 weeks, spraying again to screen out the M1 generation resistant mutant rice, (vi) harvesting rice seeds from M1 generation resistant mutant rice plants, planting again, when the rice seedlings grow to 3-4 true leaves, subjecting the rice to stem and leaf spray treatment using a 350-400 dilution of the ALS-inhibiting herbicidal composition, and at every 4 weeks, spraying again to screen out M2 generation resistant mutant rice with excellent agronomic traits, and completing the accumulation of resistance genes, (vii) harvesting rice seeds from M2 generation resistant mutant rice plants, sowing again, when the rice seedlings grow to 3-4 true leaves, spraying the rice stems and leaves with 320-350 dilution of the ALS-inhibiting herbicidal composition, then at every 4 weeks, spraying again to screen out M3 resistant mutant rice with excellent agronomic traits, completing the accumulation of resistance genes, and harvesting the rice material with herbicide resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The induction medium composition comprises nutrient broth medium, 2.5 mg/l 2,4-dichlorophenoxyacetic acid, 0.2 mg/l 6-benzylaminopurine and 15 g/l agar. The ALS-inhibiting herbicidal composition comprises 3.8 g/l diflufenacil or 2.5 g/l thioxasulfuron.

Polymers and proteins.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PT P
PN WO2019078746-A1; PL423177-A1; EP3697892-A1
TI Silage pre-treatment process, comprises directly taking silage from tray and feeing to macerator, in which it is mixed with liquid material and mechanically shredded, and resulting mixture is forwarded to the biotechnological accelerator.
AU CZEKALA W
   DACH J
   JANCZAK D
   LEWICKI A
   MICHALAK M
AE DYNAMIC BIOGAS SPOLKA ZOO SPOLKA KOMANDY (DYNA-Non-standard)
   DYNAMIC BIOGAS SPOLKA ZOO SPOLKA KOMANDY (DYNA-Non-standard)
   DYNAMIC BIOGAS SPOLKA ZOO SPOLKA KOMANDY (DYNA-Non-standard)
GA 201937108Y
AB    NOVELTY - Silage pre-treatment process, preferably maize silage, comprises directly taking the silage from the tray and feeing to the macerator, where it is mixed with liquid material and mechanically shredded, resulting mixture is forwarded to the biotechnological accelerator, preferably in portions, the temperature is maintained at the assumed level, the acidic environment in the accelerator is maintained, supporting the occurrence of biochemical changes of fermentation process, preferably hydrolysis and acidogenesis processes, and preferably also acetanogenesis, the acidification process in the accelerator is obtained by overloading the accelerator chamber with a dry organic mass of the substrate and maintaining the dry matter content in a batch on the assumed level, moreover carbon dioxide and hydrogen evolved in the accelerator during biochemical transformations of the hydrolysis process are administered to the main fermentation tank.
   USE - The process is useful for pre-treatment of silage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Silage pre-treatment process, preferably maize silage, comprises directly taking the silage from the tray and feeing to the macerator, where it is mixed with liquid material and mechanically shredded, resulting mixture is forwarded to the biotechnological accelerator, preferably in portions, the temperature is maintained at the assumed level, the acidic environment in the accelerator is maintained, supporting the occurrence of biochemical changes of fermentation process, preferably hydrolysis and acidogenesis processes, and preferably also acetanogenesis, the acidification process in the accelerator is obtained by overloading the accelerator chamber with a dry organic mass of the substrate and maintaining the dry matter content in a batch on the assumed level, moreover carbon dioxide and hydrogen evolved in the accelerator during biochemical transformations of the hydrolysis process are administered to the main fermentation tank, and the biochemical processes carried out by specific consortia of microorganisms are strengthened by a favorable mixing that is low in intensity and symmetrically equable in the entire volume, implemented in the accelerator tank, in that the temperature in the biotechnological accelerator, is maintained at 20-65 degrees C, preferably 35-42 degrees C, moreover, the acidic environment in the accelerator is maintained at a pH of 3-5, preferably 3.5-4.6, where the overloading of the accelerator chamber with the dry organic substrate mass is regulated in 30-150 kg/m3/d1 maintaining the dry matter content in the charge at the level of 12-20%, preferably 14-16%, the selection of individual parameters values of silage pre-treatment method is taken as optimal when, in the pulp from the accelerator, the specific consortia of microorganisms are kept, whose structure at the type level is expressed by following percentage of individual microorganisms including 50-65% Firmicutes, 5-25% Actinobacteria, 2-20% Proteobacteria and less than 30% Bacterioidetes, and a consortium of microorganisms has a structure at the class level expressed the following percentage of individual microorganisms including less than 30% Clostridia, less than 30% Bacteroidia, 5-50% Bacilli, 2-10% Alphaproteobacteria, 2-8% Gammaproteobacteria, 2-5% Betaprotobacteria, 5-25% Acinobacteria, 1-3% Flavobacteria, less than 1% Cloacamonae and less tan 1% Verrucomicrobia, and a specific microorganism consortium has a structure at the level of the order expressed as the percentage of each individual microorganisms including 15-45% Lactobacillales, 5-25% Bifidobacteriales, 2-8% Rhodspiriales, 1-5% Bacillales, 1-5% Actinomycetales, 1-8% Byrkholderiales, less than 25% Clostridiales, less than 30% Bacteriodales, and less than 2% Cloacamonales. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the microorganisms consortium intended for silage pre-treatment process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: In the process: as a liquid material which is fed into the macerator, where it is mixed with the silage, and the obtained mixture is mechanically broken, slurry is used; as a liquid materials which is fed into the macerator, where it is mixed with the silage, and the obtained mixture is mechanically broken, liquid fraction of digestate pulp is used; or as a liquid materials which is fed into the macerator, where it is mixed with the silage, and the obtained mixture is mechanically broken, water is used. In the process, the advantageous mixing is carried out by means of a jet agitator centrally located in the propeller.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
