PT P
PN CN109266563-A
TI Producing whole yeast rice comprises producing tower kettle water by adopting pump cooling, crushing e.g. rice shells and brown rice, adding amylase, heating, freezing, adding saccharifying enzyme and acid protease, stirring and fermenting.
AU TONG Y
   LUO H
   SUN Z
   SU Y
   XU W
   FENG H
   GUO C
   LI Y
   HUANG Y
AE GUANGXI COFCO BIO-ENERGY CO LTD (COFG-C)
GA 201911881W
AB    NOVELTY - Producing whole yeast rice comprises (i) producing tower kettle water by adopting pump cooling water of the production system, producing tower water by distillation, recycling carbon dioxide washing water with water, (ii) mixing rice shells with brown rice in a hammer mill and crushing to obtain whole rice powder, mixing whole rice powder with water to obtain the powder slurry, (iii) adding amylase and whole rice powder to powder slurry, heating, pumping into an injector, passing to a laminar flow tank, controlling the liquefying temperature, cooling by circulating water and freezing to obtain a liquefied mash and (iv) adding saccharifying enzyme and whole rice powder to obtain saccharifying liquefied mash, passing into a barmpot, adding acid protease and whole rice powder to yeast wine in a cultured fermentation tank, transferring unfiltered saccharifying liquefied mash in to cultured fermentation tank, fermenting, controlling temperature, stirring and continuously fermenting.
   USE - The method is useful for producing whole yeast rice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the inhibition of yeast population growth during yeast culture process; meets the requirements of the whole rice fermentation production process; improves distillers dried grains with solubles (DDGS) yield, increases production efficiency; and uses production of fuel ethanol, which has good effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing whole yeast rice comprises (i) producing tower kettle water by adopting pump cooling water of the production system, producing tower water by distillation, recycling carbon dioxide washing water with water for producing the slurry mixing process, (ii) mixing 30-100% rice shells with 0-50% brown rice in a hammer mill and crushing to obtain whole rice powder, passing through a 20 mesh sieve at a passing rate of more than 90%, mixing whole rice powder with pulping process water to obtain the powder slurry having solid content of 28-30% and adjusting the pH of powder slurry with sulfuric acid to 5.4-5.8, where the ratio of whole rice powder and water of the pulping process is 1:2 plus minus 0.1-2.2, (iii) adding 140,000 u/ml amylase and 0.11-0.13 kg/t whole rice powder to the powder slurry, performing secondary heating by vacuum flashing, heating at 82-86 degrees C for 30 minutes, pumping into an injector, steam spray liquefying at 95-97 degrees C, passing a laminar flow tank for 6 minutes, cooling vacuum flashing, controlling the liquefying temperature at 86-89 degrees C for 2.5hours, reducing the flash temperature to 67 degrees C, taking a plate heat exchanger, cooling to 28-30 degrees C by circulating water and freezing to obtain a liquefied mash and (iv) adjusting the pH of the liquefied mash by adding concentrated sulfuric acid to 4.6-4.9, adding 260000 u/ml saccharifying enzyme and 0.65-0.7 kg/t whole rice powder to obtain saccharifying liquefied mash, pumping ito a barmpot, adding acid protease and 0.05-0.08 kg/t whole rice powder to 20-30% yeast wine in a cultured fermentation tank , where the wine has a yeast number of greater than or equal to 250 million/ml, mortality rate of less than or equal to 3%, transferring Unfiltered saccharifying liquefied mash in to cultured fermentation tank, fermenting for 8 hours, controlling the temperature at 32 plus minus 0.5 degrees C, stirring at low-speed, continuously fermenting for 60 hours to obtain the mature mash solution for subsequent ethanol production.

PN CN109226679-A
TI Casting mold rapid forming manufacturing equipment, comprises a workbench installed by welding with supporting and one side equipped with control box set with dust board and dust-proof box connected between dust board by fixing screw.
AU WANG Q
AE GUANGDONG HAIXUEYUN EDUCATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201912616U
AB    NOVELTY - Casting mold rapid forming manufacturing equipment, comprises a workbench (1), where the workbench bottom corner is installed by welding with supporting foot (2). The one side of the workbench is equipped with control box (4). The control box is set with dust board (5). The dust board is set with a dustproof component. The dustproof component comprises a dust-proof box (601), roller, a bearing, a crank, a cloth and fixing rod. The dust-proof box is fixedly connected between dust board through a fixing screw. The anti-dust box is equipped with a releasing roller. The laying roller is rotatably connected between the dustproof boxes by a bearing. The dust-proof box one end is installed with a rocking handle. The outside of laying roller is wound with cloth. The cloth one end is provided with a fixing rod. The two sides of the bottom of control box are equipped with a fixing plate. The workbench is installed with a feeding box. The discharging box side provided with a first magnet.
   USE - Used as casting mold rapid forming manufacturing equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - The equipment is scientific, economical, safe and convenient; can avoid working with the device while tilting, prevent the support bottom plate from slipping and guarantee the stability of the whole equipment during the working process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Casting mold rapid forming manufacturing equipment, comprises a workbench (1), where the workbench bottom corner is installed by welding with supporting foot (2). The one side of the workbench is equipped with control box (4). The control box is set with dust board (5). The dust board is set with a dustproof component. The dustproof component comprises a dust-proof box (601), roller, a bearing, a crank, a cloth and fixing rod. The dust-proof box is fixedly connected between the dust board through a fixing screw. The anti-dust box is equipped with a releasing roller. The laying roller is rotatably connected between the dustproof boxes by a bearing. The dust-proof box one end is installed with a rocking handle. The outside of the laying roller is wound with cloth. The cloth one end is provided with a fixing rod. The two sides of the bottom of the control box are equipped with a fixing plate. The workbench at one side is installed with a feeding box. The discharging box side part is provided with a first magnet. The feeding box at one side is equipped with a sealing cover. The feeding box and the sealing cover are fixedly connected by the first magnet and the second magnet. The sealing cover is equipped with a heating pipe. The feeding box bottom is provided with a placing plate. The workbench is set with a cylinder. The cylinder is installed on the top of the molding table. The workbench the other side is installed on the top of box. The workbench each corner is equipped with supporting column. The supporting column is set with a top plate. The top plate is installed with a carbon dioxide laser. The top plate is equipped with a forming head. The output end electric property of control box connecting electric telescopic rod, a heating pipe and a carbon dioxide laser of input end. The control box of input end is electrically connected with the power connection.

PT P
PN CN109081950-A
TI Mesoporous and microporous mesh halloysite-silicon dioxide complex aerogel material is prepared by adding halloysite nanotube to mixed solution of de-ionized water and ethanol, vacuumizing, polymerizing and super-critically drying.
AU LI H
   SONG L
   LIU H
   ZHOU J
   ZHANG B
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 201901712C
AB    NOVELTY - Mesoporous and microporous mesh halloysite-silicon dioxide complex aerogel material, is claimed. The average specific surface area of halloysite- silicon dioxide complex aerogel material is 600-604 m2/g, where the halloysite-silicon dioxide complex aerogel material contains both nano-scale and micron-scale pores at the same time, the average nano-scale pore is 10-11 nm and the average micron-scale pore is 20-21 mu m. The preparation comprises (i) adding halloysite nanotube to mixed solution of de-ionized water and ethanol, ultrasonically dispersing, adding sodium p-styrene sulfonate, polyvinyl siloxane, initiator and decabromodiphenyl oxide into mixed solution, uniformly stirring, vacuumizing, dispersing, polymerizing, (ii) washing product, dispersing in water, adding tetraethoxysilane, hydrochloric acid, initiator and ammonium polyphosphate, uniformly stirring, heating, polymerizing, and (iii) adding methyl dodecyl trimethyl ammonium chloride, super-critically drying.
   USE - Used as mesoporous and microporous mesh halloysite-silicon dioxide complex aerogel material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Mesoporous and microporous mesh halloysite-silicon dioxide complex aerogel material, is claimed. The average specific surface area of halloysite- silicon dioxide complex aerogel material is 600-604 m2g-1, where the halloysite-silicon dioxide complex aerogel material contains both nano-scale and micron-scale pores at the same time, the average nano-scale pore is 10-11 nm and the average micron-scale pore is 20-21 mu m. The preparation comprises (i) adding 0.6-16 pts. wt. halloysite nanotube to mixed solution of 50 pts. wt. de-ionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, ultrasonically dispersing evenly to obtain halloysite dispersion, adding 0.6-16 pts. wt. sodium p-styrene sulfonate, 0.06-4 pts. wt. polyvinyl siloxane, 0.01-0.4 pts. wt. initiator and 0.05-12 pts. wt. decabromodiphenyl oxide into mixed solution of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, uniformly stirring, adding solution to halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing, vacuumizing and maintaining vacuum, returning to normal pressure, repeatedly vacuumizing for three times, washing product, dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water, heating to 60-120 degrees C in water bath to initiate polymerization, polymerizing for at least 48 hours to obtain product, where the number average molecular weight of polyvinyl siloxane is 500-5000, preferably 1000-3000, the molar percentage of vinyl content is (which is the ratio of molar number of vinyl and amino terminated polydimethylsiloxane divinyl siloxane) is 0.1-5% of amino mono-terminated polydimethylsiloxane divinyl siloxane or amino double terminated dimethyl vinyl siloxane, (ii) washing product, dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water, adding 74-154 pts. wt. tetraethoxysilane, 0.07-1.5 pts. wt. 12 mol/l hydrochloric acid, 0.01-1.8 pts. wt. initiator and 0.08-16 pts. wt. ammonium polyphosphate, uniformly stirring, heating to 30-75 degrees C in water bath to initiate polymerization, adding 0.01-0.4 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, stirring to completely dissolve, polymerizing for at least 48 hours to obtain mesoporous and microporous mesh halloysite-silicon dioxide complex wet gel, and (iii) adding 0.08-1.2 pts. wt. methyl dodecyl trimethyl ammonium chloride to mesoporous and microporous mesh halloysite-silicon dioxide complex wet gel, allowing to stand, placing product in a carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extracting device, super-critically drying at 32-58 degrees C under the pressure of 6-12 MPa for at least 1 hour using carbon dioxide as medium. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for Mesoporous and microporous mesh halloysite-silicon dioxide complex aerogel material prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The initiator is dibenzoyl peroxide (BPO) or azobisisobutyronitrile (ABIN). Preferred Method: The step 1 comprises adding 1-15 pts. wt. halloysite nanotube to mixed liquid of de-ionized water and ethanol, ultrasonically dispersing for 1-2 hours, adding 1-15 pts. wt. sodium p-styrene sulfonate, 0.1-2 pts. wt. poly-vinyl siloxane, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. initiator, 0.1-10 pts. wt. decabromodiphenyl ether to mixed solution of de-ionized water and ethanol, stirring for 15-65 minutes, adding halloysite dispersion, ultrasonically dispersing for 20-40 minutes, cooling mixed liquid, vacuumizing for 1-2 hours, polymerizing at 60-120 degrees C for 10-60 minutes, cooling to 15-50 degrees C for polymerizing at 80 degrees C for 12-24 hours, polymerizing at 90-100 degrees C for 2-8 hours. The step (ii) comprises adding 80-150 pts. wt. tetraethoxysilane, 0.1-1 pts. wt. 12 mol/l hydrochloric acid, 0.01-1 pts. wt. initiator and 0.1-15 pts. wt. ammonium polyphosphate into dispersion, stirring for 10-70 minutes, polymerizing at 30-75 degrees C for 10-40 minutes, adding 0.01-0.2 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, stirring to completely dissolve, cooling to 20-50 degrees C in constant temperature water bath to polymerize for 20-35 hours, separately polymerizing at 80 degrees C and 90-100 degrees C for 2-4 hours. The step (iii) comprises adding 0.1-1 pts. wt. methyl dodecyl trimethyl ammonium chloride, allowing to stand for 1-6 hours, super critically drying at 33-50 degrees C under the pressure of 7-10MPa for 2-4 hours, preferably 3 hours.

PN CN108619416-A
TI Medicinal composition useful for treating liver cirrhosis, liver ascites and liver cancer comprises e.g. kelp, Centella asiatica, corn silk, tangerine peel, rhizoma arisaematis, cortex moutan, semen lepidii, and cortex lycii radicis.
AU ZHENG F
AE ZHENG F (ZHEN-Individual)
GA 2018800183
AB    NOVELTY - Medicinal composition comprises 75-225 pts. wt. kelp, 60-180 pts. wt. Centella asiatica, 50-100 pts. wt. corn silk, 60-180 pts. wt. tangerine peel, 50-100 pts. wt. rhizoma arisaematis, 40-120 pts. wt. black ants, 50-100 pts. wt. cortex moutan, 50-100 pts. wt. Bischofia javanica, 15-45 pts. wt. Lobelia Chinesis, 10-30 pts. wt. semen lepidii, 10-30 pts. wt. cortex lycii radicis, 10-30 pts. wt. leatherleaf milletia, 5-15 pts. wt. ginseng, 5-15 pts. wt. safflower, 5-15 pts. wt. pseudo-ginseng and 5-15 pts. wt. agilawood.
   USE - The composition is useful for treating liver cirrhosis, liver ascites and liver cancer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: removes the organ congestion; enhances the blood flow and microcirculation of the liver main body, and immunity of the body; improves the resistance of the liver cell membrane to the toxin and quality of life of liver cancer patients; repairs the liver cells; and softens the liver.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the composition, comprising taking 1/2 kelp, 1/2 C. asiatica, 1/2 tangerine peel, 1/2 rhizoma arisaematis and all black ants for ultra-micro crushing to obtain superfine powder, and spraying 20-60 pts. wt. dry ice during the super-micro crushing process as a coolant, where at least 85% of the fine powder in ultrafine pulverization does not exceed 6 microns, washing remaining 1/2 kelp, 1/2 C. asiatica, 1/2 tangerine peel, 1/2 rhizoma arisaematis, adding 6-10 times amount of water to soak for 15-45 minutes, decocting for 1-3 hours for the first time, and filtering to obtain filtrate, further boiling the dregs with 2-6 times the amount of water for 1-2 hours, filtering to obtain filtrate, combining the both filtrates, filtering, centrifuging the filtrate at the speed of 3000 rotations/minute (rpm) for 2-6 minutes, separating the supernatant, evaporating and drying to extract A of 1.25-1.30 in an water bath, soaking corn silk, cortex moutan and B. javanica with 4-12 times of water for 15-45 minutes, adding 20-40 pts. wt. of the complex enzyme comprises papain and cellulase at the weight ratio of 461, primarily performing enzymolysis at 20-60 degrees C for 15-45 minutes, then decocting for two times, each time for 0.5-1.5 hours, combining the both decoctions, filtering, concentrating the filtrate at 25-75 degrees C to measure the relative density of 1.35-1.40 extract B, crushing the remaining half powders L. Chinesis, semen lepidii, cortex lycii radicis, leatherleaf milletia, ginseng, safflower, pseudo-ginseng and agilawood and passing through 20-60 meshes sieve, adding ultra-micro powder into the carbon dioxide extraction kettle for 1.5-4.5 hours, and controlling the pressure in the extraction kettle at 20-30 MPa, separating the soaked ultra-micro powder in a separation kettle, controlling the pressure of the separating kettle at 3-9 MPa, heating to 20-60 degrees C for 2.5-7.5 hours, and maintaining flow rate of the carbon dioxide at 20-40 Kg/hour, drying the separated extract to obtain extract D, uniformly stirring the extract A, extract B and extract D with a high speed stirrer, adding kelp, C. asiatica, tangerine peel, rhizoma arisaematis and black ants powder and stirring uniformly, adding sodium carboxymethyl starch and 12-36 pts. wt. dicalcium phosphate at the weight ratio of 561, mixing and stirring uniformly to prepare granule, tablet or capsule.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 150 pts. wt. kelp, 120 pts. wt. C. asiatica, 100 pts. wt. corn silk, 120 pts. wt. tangerine peel, 100 pts. wt. rhizoma arisaematis, 80 pts. wt. black ants, 100 pts. wt. cortex moutan, 100 pts. wt. B. javanica, 30 pts. wt. L. chinesis, 20 pts. wt. semen lepidii, 20 pts. wt. cortex lycii radicis, 20 pts. wt. leatherleaf milletia, 10 pts. wt. ginseng, 10 pts. wt. safflower, 10 pts. wt. pseudo-ginseng and 10 pts. wt. agilawood. The composition is added to a conventionally accepted tablet, capsule, granule, pill or oral solution by conventional excipients according to a conventional procedure. Preferred Method: The preparation method of the composition comprises taking 1/2 kelp, 1/2 C. asiatica, 1/2 tangerine peel, 1/2 rhizoma arisaematis and all black ants for ultra-micro crushing to obtain superfine powder, and spraying 40 pts. wt. dry ice during the super-micro crushing process as a coolant, where at least 85% of the fine powder in ultrafine pulverization does not exceed 6 microns, washing remaining 1/2 kelp, 1/2 C. asiatica, 1/2 tangerine peel, 1/2 rhizoma arisaematis, adding 8 times amount of water to soak for 8 minutes, decocting for 2 hours for the first time, and filtering to obtain filtrate, further boiling the dregs with 4 times the amount of water for 1.5 hours, filtering to obtain filtrate, combining the both filtrates, filtering, centrifuging the filtrate at the speed of 3000 rotations/minute (rpm) for 4 minutes, separating the supernatant, evaporating and drying to extract A of 1.25-1.30 in an water bath, soaking corn silk, cortex moutan and B. javanica with 8 times of water for 30 minutes, adding 30 pts. wt. of the complex enzyme comprises papain and cellulase at the weight ratio of 461, primarily performing enzymolysis at 40 degrees C for 30 minutes, then decocting for two times, each time for 1 hours, combining the both decoctions, filtering, concentrating the filtrate at 50 degrees C to measure the relative density of 1.35-1.40 extract B, crushing the remaining half powders L. Chinesis, semen lepidii, cortex lycii radicis, leatherleaf milletia, ginseng, safflower, pseudo-ginseng and agilawood and passing through 40 meshes sieve, adding ultra-micro powder into the carbon dioxide extraction kettle for 3 hours, and controlling the pressure in the extraction kettle at 25 MPa, separating the soaked ultra-micro powder in a separation kettle, controlling the pressure of the separating kettle at 6 MPa, heating to 40 degrees C for 5 hours, and maintaining flow rate of the carbon dioxide at 30 Kg/hour, drying the separated extract to obtain extract D, uniformly stirring the extract A, extract B and extract D with a high speed stirrer, adding kelp, C. asiatica, tangerine peel, rhizoma arisaematis and black ants powder and stirring uniformly, adding sodium carboxymethyl starch and 24 pts. wt. dicalcium phosphate at the weight ratio of 561, mixing and stirring uniformly to prepare granule, tablet or capsule.
   ACTIVITY - Hepatotropic; Cytostatic.

PN CN108489968-A; CN108489968-B
TI Method for conveniently detecting dithiocarbamate pesticides, involves preparing dithiocarbamate pesticide with EDTA solution, measuring standard solution of dithiocarbamate pesticide and carrier gas is helium or argon.
AU HAN B
   HE Y
   LI Y
   QIAN B
   PENG L
AE ANALYSIS & TESTING CENT CATAS (ANAL-Non-standard)
GA 201871178V
AB    NOVELTY - A dithiocarbamate pesticides detecting method involves preparing dithiocarbamate pesticide with an EDTA solution, measuring the standard solution of the dithiocarbamate pesticide, form a mixed solution with the carrier liquid and separating by a liquid chromatography column for recording the retention time, separating the mixed solution by the liquid chromatography column is mixed with the reducing solution at a volume ratio of 2-1:1-2 and subjecting to microwave irradiation with a power to prepare a dithiocarbamate pesticide, it is reduced to carbon disulfide, mixed with carrier gas, cooling gas-liquid separated and finally carbon dioxide is sent to the dielectric barrier discharge device to excite carbon disulfide emission spectrum by carrier gas, the internal and external electrode voltage of dielectric barrier discharge device is selected as carbon disulfide, characteristic emission line and using a charge coupled spectrometer for detecting emission intensity.
   USE - Method for conveniently detecting dithiocarbamate pesticides.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables detecting dithiocarbamate pesticides with better reduction effect, little disturbance, high sensitivity, quick analysis, in simple, cost effective and convenient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A dithiocarbamate pesticides detecting method involves preparing dithiocarbamate pesticide with an EDTA solution, measuring the standard solution of the dithiocarbamate pesticide, form a mixed solution with the carrier liquid at a volume ratio of 0.1-2:1 to 5 and separating by a liquid chromatography column for recording the retention time, separating the mixed solution by the liquid chromatography column is mixed with the reducing solution at a volume ratio of 2-1:1-2 and subjecting to microwave irradiation with a power of 150-800 W to prepare a dithiocarbamate pesticide, it is reduced to carbon disulfide, mixed with carrier gas, cooling gas-liquid separated and finally carbon dioxide is sent to the dielectric barrier discharge device to excite carbon disulfide emission spectrum by carrier gas, the internal and external electrode voltage of dielectric barrier discharge device is controlled at 1.5-3.3kV and 257.94 nm is selected as carbon disulfide, characteristic emission line, using a charge coupled spectrometer for detecting emission intensity, reducing solution is a tin chloride/hydrochloric acid solution in which the concentration of tin chloride is 5-30 g L-1 and the concentration of hydrochloric acid is 1.5-4 mol L-1 and the carrier gas is helium or argon, preparing different concentrations of standard solution, converting the dithiocarbamate pesticide converted to tin chloride at different concentrations and linearize with the concentration of the dithiocarbamate pesticide, after homogenizing the test substance, adding L-cysteine at a mass ratio of 40-50:1 and adding EDTA extract at a mass to volume ratio of 1:4-5 at a high speed, centrifuging the slurry and taking the supernatant as a sample solution, taking the sample solution and mixing with the carrier liquid to obtain the detection solution, input into the liquid chromatography column for separation and characterizing the pesticide species in the sample solution according to the retention time, mixing the detection liquid and the reducing liquid separated by the liquid chromatography column at a volume ratio of 2-1:1-2 and using microwave radiation having a power of 150-800 W to make the dithiocarbamic acid in the detection liquid, reducing the salt pesticide to carbon disulfide, then mixed with the carrier gas, cooled, gas-liquid separated and finally the carbon disulfide is carried out using the carrier gas, comparing the emission intensity measured with the standard curve to obtain the concentration of the dithiocarbamate pesticide in the sample solution.

TI Organic waste treatment system comprises a primary agitation apparatus and a secondary agitation apparatus, and apparatus is used for decomposing organic waste into aerobic microorganisms and reducing the amount of organic wastes.
AU YOO M S
AE YOO M S (YOOM-Individual)
   KUNJANGCON CO LTD (KUNJ-Non-standard)
GA 201853732U
AB    NOVELTY - Organic waste treatment system comprises a primary agitation apparatus (100) and a secondary agitation apparatus (100a). The apparatus is used for decomposing organic waste into aerobic microorganisms and reducing the amount of organic wastes remaining after the reduction in the primary agitation apparatus. The amount of the organic waste remaining after the weight loss is reduced and the organic waste and the microbial biochip mixed with the microbial biochip and the organic waste and decomposed with the aerobic microorganisms which are grown when the growth environment of the aerobic microorganisms deteriorates. The primary agitation apparatus includes an injection device (120) for injecting organic waste into a tank having a conical upper unit and a lower inverted conical unit, and a microbial biochip and organic waste upward from the lower unit to the upper unit, and a recycling device (130) for repeating a process of gradually lowering to a lower portion.
   USE - Used as organic waste treatment system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system shortens decomposition time and drying time of the organic waste, rapidly decomposes organic wastes, and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Organic waste treatment system comprises a primary agitation apparatus (100) and a secondary agitation apparatus (100a). The apparatus is used for decomposing organic waste into aerobic microorganisms and reducing the amount of organic wastes remaining after the reduction in the primary agitation apparatus. The amount of the organic waste remaining after the weight loss is reduced and the organic waste and the microbial biochip mixed with the microbial biochip and the organic waste and decomposed with the aerobic microorganisms which are grown when the growth environment of the aerobic microorganisms deteriorates. The primary agitation apparatus includes an injection device (120) for injecting organic waste into a tank having a conical upper unit and a lower inverted conical unit, and a microbial biochip and organic waste upward from the lower unit to the upper unit, a recycling device (130) for repeating a process of gradually lowering to a lower portion, a cleaning blade for spraying the air with the spray nozzle while the knife-like blade rotates to prevent the microbial bio-chip and the organic waste from sticking to bottom of the inverted cone, the water vapor generated from the decomposition process of the organic waste and the carbon dioxide, and a drain device for discharging and discharging energy (heat). The secondary agitation apparatus is provided with an apparatus for introducing microbial biochips and organic wastes mixed together with organic wastes by the amount of organic wastes remaining after the weight reduction in the primary agitation apparatus, a recycling device for repeating the steps of raising and lowering the microbial biochip and the organic waste from the lower unit to the upper unit, an upper agitator for spraying air or hot air to the spray nozzle while agitating the organic waste to prevent the microbial biochip and the organic waste from sticking to bottom of the lower inverted cone, a cleaning blade for spraying the air with the spray nozzle during rotation of the knife-shaped blade to prevent adhesion of microbial biochips and organic waste on the floor of the lower inverted cone, an upper agitator for spraying air or hot air to the spray nozzle while agitating the organic waste, and gradually descending microbial biochip from the upper unit to the lower unit by the recycling device a lower stirrer, and a discharge device for discharging water vapor, carbon dioxide and energy (heat) generated during decomposition and drying of the microbial biochip and the organic waste.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the organic waste treatment system.
   Primary agitation apparatus (100)
   Secondary agitation apparatus (100a)
   Tank (110)
   Injection device (120)
   Recycling device (130)

PN CN108103384-A
TI Making laser additive amorphous alloy by polishing front and back surfaces and periphery of carbon steel plate, treating plate by quartz sand blasting, scrubbing and cleaning non-crystalline thin strip surface with anhydrous ethanol.
AU WANG W
   CHANG Z
   GE Y
   YAN Z
   CUI Z
   DUAN J
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201844970A
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing laser additive amorphous alloy involves (a) taking a carbon steel plate as a substrate, placing the carbon steel plate on the steel plate, polishing front and back surfaces and periphery of the carbon steel plate to determine the working surface, treating the working surface of the carbon steel plate by quartz sand blasting and scrubbing working surface and periphery of the carbon steel by anhydrous ethanol so that the carbon steel is clean, scrubbed and aired, (b) cleaning non-crystalline thin strip surface with anhydrous ethanol so that the amorphous thin strip surface is clean, scrubbed and aired, and (c) placing the carbon steel plate on a working table of a laser machine with working surface, adjusting X, Y and Z distance of the laser machine, laying a layer (i) of iron-based amorphous ribbon on a working surface of the carbon steel plate, fixing it on both end faces and adjusting the argon tube angle.
   USE - Method is useful for preparing laser additive amorphous alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of the laser additive amorphous alloy with high quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - In the step (a), the quartz sand blasting is carried out at a surface roughness of 0.16-0.32 mu m. The step (c) further involves adjusting focus lens laser beam tip vertically aligned with working surface of carbon steel plate and adjusting the focal length of the surface of the amorphous thin strip that is preset by the laser beam and the carbon steel plate to be 70 mm. The method involves (d) adjusting the laser mirror, mirror, focusing mirror angle of the laser machine and the focusing mirror vertically aligned with the upper portion of the amorphous ribbon, installing and adjusting the position and angle of the argon cylinder and argon tube, where angle between the argon tube and the focused beam is 30 degrees , opening carbon dioxide gas bottle, nitrogen gas bottle and argon gas bottle, introducing mixed gas of carbon dioxide, nitrogen and argon into a laser generator in a ratio of 1:8:7 at a speed of 300 cm3/minute, turning on a cooling water tank, introducing cooling water into the laser generator and cooling, turning on laser controller, emitting the laser light using laser generator, irradiating laser beam onto the upper surface of the amorphous ribbon through the laser mirror, the mirror and the focusing mirror, carrying out laser cladding at a laser wavelength of 10.6 mu m, a laser beam output power of 1-2 kW, a spot diameter of 2 mm and a scanning speed of 450 mm/minute for 0.1 second, opening argon gas cylinder and carrying out laser cladding under argon gas protection at a speed of 200 cm3/minute, (e) subjecting the iron-based amorphous ribbon to layer-by-layer deposited on the working surface of the carbon steel sheet to form an amorphous alloy bulk material, and (f) detecting, analyzing and characterizing surface morphology, phase analysis and characterization of the surface of iron-based amorphous alloys formed by laser additive forming, observing and analyzing surface morphology of iron-based amorphous alloy blocks by scanning electron microscopy, carrying out phase analysis of the surface of iron-based amorphous alloy block by X-ray diffractometer.

TI Preparing sintered brick involves preparing            non-sintered brick using dyeing and printing wastewater            treatment plant sludge, transferring and drying            non-sintered brick in drying chamber, transferring and            roasting non-sintered brick in kiln.
AU ZHENG M
AE SHISHI HONGHUI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION BUILDING              MATERIALS C (SHIS-Non-standard)
GA 201814882E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing sintered brick involves                preparing insulation brick using dyeing and                printing wastewater treatment plant sludge,                transferring well-shaped insulation brick using                automatic robot code to the kiln car and leaving                brick, transferring and curing brick on the carbon                dioxide conservation box, then collecting and                transferring non-sintered brick into drying chamber                through drying truck and drying non-sintered brick                for 65-85 minutes at 150-190 degrees C to a                moisture content of 2-3% and transferring                non-sintered brick to a tunnel kiln and roasting                non-sintered brick.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sintered                brick.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared sintered brick is                environmentally-friendly, has high content of                sludge organic matter and reduces thermal                insulation sound absorption and noise                reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing sintered brick involves                (a) adding dyeing and printing wastewater treatment                plant sludge having water content of 85% into the                tank, then adding 97% water content sludge water                and diluting content to obtain 93% moisture content                of sludge, then adding agent and mixing sludge,                pumping sludge using high-pressure screw pump                having moisture content of 93% to frame diaphragm                filter press, the high-pressure feeding, diaphragm                squeezing and automatically discharge mud cake,                then transferring mud cake using conveyor belt into                burning brick tunnel kiln waste heat drying kiln,                drying mud cake to moisture content of 35% and                storing dried mud cake, (b) loading 30-40 parts                dried printing and dyeing sludge, 15-20 parts fly                ash, 28-38 parts construction waste and 1-2 parts                polymer adhesive respectively into a plate feeder                in accordance to chemical composition, the                electronic metering through the PLC for automatic                control of ingredients, coarse crushing content                using hammer coarse broken machine, and then fine                crushing content using broken hammer and then                sieving content through a sieve to obtain sieved                content, (c) coarse and fine crushing construction                waste soil using roller, then adding a mixture of                polymer adhesive and water to crushing content,                then controlling mixture to a moisture content of                20% and aging mixture for 3 days, (d) continue                aging the mixture of dry printing and dyeing                sludge, fly ash, construction waste and polymer                binder obtained in the step (c) for more than 72                hours, after the multi-bucket excavator reclaimer,                transferring content through conveyor belt into                roller and rolling content, crushing and refining                content, then transferring content through the                conveyor belt into the wheel mill, increasing the                fineness and plasticity of the content and then                transferring content through conveyor belt into                mixer, adjusting the moisture content of the                content and increase the plasticity and then                transferring content into the 75-type double-roll                vacuum hard plastic extruder using conveyor belt to                obtain insulation brick, (e) transferring                well-shaped insulation brick using automatic robot                code to the kiln car, (f) transferring insulation                brick through the tractor into a closed brick with                a confined space and leaving for 24 hours, where                the air does not flow, drying and mixing printing                and dyeing sludge, fly ash and construction waste,                then maintaining uniform water content of mixture,                thus the surface of the brick does not crack to                achieve the purpose of improving the strength of                thermal insulation brick, (g) transferring and                placing content obtained in the step (f) on the                carbon dioxide conservation box and curing content                for 2-3 days, removing and then collecting                non-sintered brick, (h) transferring non-sintered                brick into drying chamber through drying truck and                drying non-sintered brick for 65-85 minutes at                150-190 degrees C to a moisture content of 2-3% and                (i) transferring non-sintered brick to a tunnel                kiln and roasting non-sintered brick at 850-1050                degrees C for 65-85 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: In the step (a), the agent                  is polyaluminum chloride sludge conditioner,                  cationic polyacrylamide sludge conditioner or                  their combination. In the step (i), roasting                  non-sintered brick five time, (i) roasting                  non-sintered brick at 80 degrees C and rate of                  100 degrees C/hour for 30-40 minutes, (ii)                  roasting non-sintered brick at 80-450 degrees C                  and rate of 120 degrees C/hour for 35-40 minutes,                  (iii) roasting non-sintered brick at 450-850                  degrees C and rate of 130 degrees C/hour for                  40-50 minutes, (iv) roasting non-sintered brick                  at 850-1050 degrees C and rate of 150 degrees                  C/hour for 3-4 hours to form a brick and (v)                  roasting non-sintered brick at 1050 degrees C,                  then cooling non-sintered brick temperature to                  100 degrees C and rate of 200 degrees C/hour and                  removing brick from kiln.

PN EP4056531-A1; US2022289651-A1; CN115073267-A
TI Producing methanol from synthesis gas by            producing, in partial oxidation chamber, synthesis gas            by performing partial oxidation process with            hydrocarbon and oxygen stream, and passing, in            water-gas shift reactor, synthesis gas over water-gas            shift catalyst to convert carbon monoxide and            water.
AU KOPETSCH H
   WILLIAMS B
   COVELLA K
   AOUN J
   KOPECKY H
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2022B64531
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing methanol from a synthesis                gas, involves (a) producing, in a partial oxidation                chamber, the synthesis gas having a stoichiometric                number of less than substantially 1.8 by performing                a partial oxidation process with a hydrocarbon                stream and an oxygen stream, (b) passing, in a                water-gas shift reactor, the synthesis gas over a                water-gas shift catalyst to convert at least a                portion of carbon monoxide and water into hydrogen                and carbon dioxide for obtaining a shifted                synthesis gas, (c) producing a dry, shifted                synthesis gas by separating liquid condensate from                the shifted synthesis gas, (d) combining, in a                recycle compressor, the dry, shifted synthesis gas                with a recycle synthesis gas comprised of unreacted                synthesis gas and a hydrogen rich product to form a                mixed synthesis gas stream, and (e) converting, in                a cooled methanol synthesis reactor, at least a                portion of the mixed synthesis gas stream into                methanol.
   USE - The method is useful for producing methanol                from a synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces methanol from a synthesis                gas that is generated by a non-catalytic partial                oxidation with simplified apparatus, and low                investment cost and low production cost, and does                not require use of a complicated multi-tube steam                reformer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for producing methanol from a synthesis                gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The method involves                  cooling the synthesis gas to a first temperature                  without condensation of water vapor occurring,                  before passing the synthesis gas over the                  water-gas shift catalyst. The step of producing                  the dry, shifted synthesis gas involves cooling,                  in a first cooling unit, the shifted synthesis                  gas to a point of condensation at a second                  temperature to form a liquid condensate, and                  separating, in a condensate separator, the liquid                  condensate from the shifted synthesis gas to                  produce the dry, shifted synthesis gas. The                  method involves cooling, in a second cooling                  unit, an effluent from the cooled methanol                  synthesis reactor to a point of condensation at a                  third temperature to produce a condensate that                  comprises crude methanol and water, separating,                  in a methanol separator, the crude methanol from                  unreacted synthesis gas, routing a first portion                  of the unreacted synthesis gas from the methanol                  separator as the recycle synthesis gas to the                  recycle compressor, routing a second portion of                  the unreacted synthesis gas from the methanol                  separator, to a hydrogen recovery unit,                  producing, in the hydrogen recovery unit, the                  hydrogen rich product to be mixed with the                  recycle synthesis gas stream to the recycle                  compressor to form a mixed synthesis gas stream,                  compressing, in the recycle compressor, the mixed                  synthesis gas stream by increasing the pressure                  of the mixed synthesis gas stream, mixing both                  the hydrocarbon stream and the oxygen stream with                  steam, and/or preheating the hydrocarbon stream                  and/or the oxygen stream before supplying to the                  partial oxidation chamber for performing the                  partial oxidation process. The method involves                  operating the partial oxidation chamber and the                  water-gas shift reactor under a pressure of 30-90                  bar. Preferred Conditions: The step (i) involves                  cooling of the shifted synthesis gas to a point                  of condensation and separating the liquid                  condensate from the shifted synthesis gas, and                  (ii) cooling methanol synthesis reactant effluent                  to a point of condensation and separating the                  crude methanol from unreacted synthesis gas, are                  performed in an equipment that is common to (i)                  and (ii). The water-gas shift reactor and the                  cooled methanol synthesis reactor share a                  mutually common coolant and steam generation                  system. The water-gas shift reactor and cooled                  methanol synthesis reactor are mutually contained                  in the same vessel.

PN CN114672847-A
TI Controllable preparation of high performance            electric catalyst used as working electode, involves            placing foamed copper sheet in precursor solution and            soaking for period of time, taking out soaked copper            foam and drying in a vacuum oven, and calcining soaked            copper foam in argon atmosphere.
AU CHENG M
   ZHANG H
   ZHANG G
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 202293574Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves dissolving sodium                persulfate and sodium hydroxide in water to obtain                a precursor solution. The foamed copper sheet is                placed in the precursor solution and soaked for a                period of time at 25degrees Celsius. The soaked                copper foam is taken out and dried in a vacuum oven                at 60degrees Celsius. The soaked copper foam is                calcined in an argon atmosphere after the material                is dried. The heating rate of calcination is                2degrees Celsius/min, the temperature is raised to                500degrees Celsius, and the temperature is kept for                4 hours to fully decompose the hydroxide. The                chemical formula of the obtained high-performance                electro catalyst is copper/copper dioxide. The                morphology of the catalyst is nanowires with a                diameter of 200-300 nm.
   USE - Controllable preparation method of                high-performance electro-catalyst used as working                electrode for electrocatalytic coupling of carbon                dioxide and nitrate to prepare urea (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The high-performance electro-catalyst has                excellent urea synthesis rate and Faraday                efficiency, at the same time, the research reaction                product has higher urea conversion rate and                stability. The performance of the material with                higher electrocatalysis coupling by comparing the                performance of commercial material with the nano                material has universality.

TI Special-shaped flame-retardant composite needled felt for automobile cushion, heat preservation heat insulation product and air filtering, has splicing component that is arranged between substrate and flame retardant component.

AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a special-shaped flame-retardant composite needled felt, comprising a needled felt base body, the needled felt base body is uniformly distributed and the distance interval them is equal, the needled felt base body is fixedly connected with uniformly distributed inflaming retarding component, the needled felt base body each six forms a whole body, the whole outer side surface composed of the needled felt substrate is fixedly connected with a splicing component, the needled felt substrate comprises a group sheet, the material of the group sheet is polyester fiber, the inner part of the group sheet is provided with uniformly distributed connection groove, the inner part of the connection groove provided by the group sheet is fixedly connected with a thin tube, the material of the thin tube is rubber, the number of the thin pipe in the same group is four, the inner part of the thin pipe is filled with carbon dioxide gas, the flame retardant component comprises a flame retardant sheet, the irregular flame retardant composite punched felt solves the problem of low flame retardance of the traditional punched felt, The deformed flame-retardant composite needled felt solves the problem that the size of the traditional composite needled-punched felt cannot be freely spliced to change the size.
